// DBML Schema for Business: Life Insurance
// Version: 1
// Generated on: 2026-05-04 07:01:02

Project {
  database_type: 'Databricks Unity Catalog'
  Note: 'Lakehouse data model for Life Insurance - Version 1'
}

Table policyholder.insured {
  insured_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the insured-life role record in the Silver layer. Primary key for the insured entity; globally unique across all policies and group certificates.']
  benefit_structure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.benefit_structure. Business justification: The benefit structure assigned to an insured defines their death benefit type, LTC acceleration eligibility, waiver provisions, and nonforfeiture options — directly corresponding to insured attributes']
  cohort_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.cohort_definition. Business justification: LDTI (ASC 944) and IFRS17 require each insured to be assigned to a cohort for GAAP/IFRS reserve measurement. Cohort assignment drives locked-in discount rates, CSM/LFPB calculations, and profitability']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance policy or annuity contract under which this insured-life role exists. Links the insured to the Policy Administration System (e.g., Sapiens LifePro, FAST) policy record.']
  insured_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party supertype record that holds demographic identity (legal name, date of birth, gender, SSN/TIN, address). The insured entity is a role record; all identity attributes are owned by the party record.']
  mortality_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: The mortality table applied to each insured drives their net amount at risk, reserve calculation, and reinsurance cession rate. Underwriters and actuaries must know which approved mortality table gove']
  premium_rate_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.premium_rate_table. Business justification: The premium rate table used at underwriting is determined by the insured\'s issue_age, gender, tobacco_status, risk_class, and face_amount band. Regulatory and actuarial audit requirements mandate trac']
  primary_insured_party_id varchar [note: 'FK to policyholder.party.party_id — The insured role record must link to the party supertype for demographic resolution. This is the foundational in-domain FK enabling the party-role pattern that the policyholder domain is built on.']
  risk_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: The insured\'s assigned risk class drives premium calculation, reinsurance cession class, and policy administration. insured.risk_class is a plain text denormalized value that should be a proper FK to ']
  underwriting_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: Underwriting class assignment is a core life insurance process: the insured\'s risk profile (age, tobacco, occupation, table_rating) is evaluated to assign an underwriting_class_id. This drives premium']
  adb_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the insured is eligible for the ADB (Accidental Death Benefit) rider, which pays an additional death benefit if death results from a covered accident. Eligibility is determined during underwriting based on age, occupation, and health status.']
  adl_impairment_count varchar [note: 'Number of ADLs (Activities of Daily Living) with which the insured requires assistance, as assessed for LTC rider benefit eligibility. Standard ADLs include bathing, dressing, eating, toileting, transferring, and continence. Benefit triggers typically require impairment in 2 or more ADLs.']
  aps_received_date date [note: 'Date on which the APS (Attending Physician Statement) was received from the insured\'s attending physician. Used to track underwriting cycle time and NIGO resolution. Null if APS was not required or not yet received.']
  aps_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an APS (Attending Physician Statement) was required from the insured\'s treating physician as part of the underwriting process. Triggered by age, face amount thresholds, or medical history disclosures. Sourced from the Underwriting Workbench.']
  attained_age varchar [note: 'Current age of the insured as of the most recent policy anniversary or valuation date, expressed in whole years. Used in COI (Cost of Insurance) calculations for UL/IUL products, NAR (Net Amount at Risk) determination, and ongoing actuarial reserving.']
  aviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the insured has disclosed aviation activity (private pilot, commercial pilot, or frequent flyer in non-commercial aircraft) that constitutes a hazardous avocation. Triggers aviation exclusion riders or flat extra premium charges in underwriting.']
  citizenship_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the insured\'s country of citizenship. Used in AML/KYC compliance screening, OFAC sanctions checks, foreign national underwriting guidelines, and FATCA/FBAR reporting obligations.']
  coverage_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which coverage for this insured-life role became effective (policy issue date or certificate effective date for group). Used in actuarial valuation, reserve calculations, and claims adjudication to determine coverage period.']
  coverage_termination_date date [note: 'Date on which coverage for this insured-life role ended or is scheduled to end. Null for in-force policies. Populated upon lapse, surrender, maturity, or death claim settlement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this insured-life role record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements under SOX, and ETL processing tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the insured individual. Critical for issue age and attained age calculations, MEC (Modified Endowment Contract) testing under IRC Section 7702, RMD (Required Minimum Distribution) tracking for annuities, and mortality risk assessment.']
  exclusion_rider_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the exclusion rider(s) attached for this insured, specifying the excluded condition, activity, or body part. Used by claims adjusters during adjudication to determine whether a claim falls within an excluded category.']
  exclusion_rider_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more exclusion riders have been attached to the policy for this insured, limiting coverage for specific pre-existing conditions, hazardous activities, or body parts. Relevant for claims adjudication to determine benefit eligibility.']
  face_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The death benefit (DB) face amount of coverage for this insured-life role, expressed in USD. Represents the base sum insured excluding riders. Used in NAR (Net Amount at Risk) calculation, reinsurance cession determination, and actuarial reserve calculations.']
  flat_extra_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the flat extra premium surcharge expires for the insured. After this date, the insured reverts to the base table rating without the temporary extra charge. Null if no flat extra applies or if the flat extra is permanent.']
  flat_extra_premium decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional per-thousand or per-unit premium charged to substandard insured lives for a defined period, typically reflecting temporary extra mortality risk (e.g., post-surgery recovery, hazardous occupation). Expressed as a dollar amount per $1,000 of face amount per year.']
  foreign_travel_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the insured has disclosed travel to or residence in countries with elevated mortality or political risk, as assessed during underwriting. May result in exclusion riders, flat extra premiums, or declination depending on destination and duration.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Gender of the insured as recorded for underwriting and actuarial purposes. M=Male, F=Female, X=Non-binary/Other, U=Unknown. Used in gender-distinct mortality table selection where permitted by state regulation. Classified as restricted PII.']
  group_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number assigned to an insured under a group life insurance master policy. Uniquely identifies the individual certificate within the group. Null for individual policies. Used in group policy administration, billing, and claims processing.']
  insurable_interest_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a valid insurable interest relationship has been verified between the policy owner and the insured at the time of application. Required by state insurance law to prevent wagering contracts and STOLI (Stranger-Originated Life Insurance) arrangements.']
  insurable_interest_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the insurable interest relationship between the policy owner and the insured. Self indicates the owner and insured are the same person. Business_partner and key_person apply to business-owned life insurance (BOLI). Trust applies to ILIT (Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust) arrangements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: self|spouse|child|business_partner|employer|key_person|trust|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  insured_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned by the Policy Administration System to uniquely identify this insured-life role on a policy. Used in ACORD transactions, bordereaux reporting, and reinsurance cession records.']
  insured_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the insured-life role record. Active indicates coverage is in force; deceased triggers claims adjudication; lapsed/surrendered/terminated reflect policy end states; pending indicates underwriting not yet complete.']
  issue_age varchar [note: 'Age of the insured at the time the policy was issued, expressed in whole years. Fundamental actuarial input for mortality table selection, premium rate determination, and reserve calculations under PBR and LDTI.']
  ltc_rider_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an LTC (Long-Term Care) rider is attached to the policy for this insured, providing accelerated death benefits upon inability to perform ADLs (Activities of Daily Living). Triggers LTC benefit administration and state LTC regulatory reporting.']
  mib_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the MIB (Medical Information Bureau) inquiry conducted during underwriting for this insured. A \'hit\' indicates a prior medical disclosure that requires further investigation. Used in underwriting risk assessment and NIGO (Not In Good Order) tracking.']
  military_status varchar [note: 'Military service status of the insured as disclosed on the application. Active duty military may trigger war exclusion clauses or special underwriting considerations. Relevant for SGLI (Servicemembers\' Group Life Insurance) coordination and exclusion rider application.']
  nar_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'NAR (Net Amount at Risk) for this insured life, calculated as the death benefit minus the policy account value or reserve. Represents the insurer\'s net mortality exposure. Used in COI charge calculation for UL/IUL products, reinsurance cession amounts, and actuarial reserve modeling.']
  occupation_class varchar [note: 'Occupational risk classification assigned to the insured based on their profession and associated hazard level. Used in DI (Disability Income) underwriting and life underwriting for hazardous occupations. Typically coded as Class 1 (professional) through Class 4+ (hazardous). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: class_1|class_2|class_3|class_4|class_5|unclassifiable — promote to reference product]']
  occupation_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the insured\'s occupation as stated on the application. Used in underwriting risk assessment, hazardous occupation exclusion determination, and DI benefit eligibility. Sourced from the Underwriting Workbench application data.']
  reinsurance_cession_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any portion of the risk on this insured life has been ceded to a reinsurer under an automatic or facultative treaty. Triggers reinsurance bordereaux reporting and cession processing in the Reinsurance Management System (Swiss Re IRIS; RGA AURA).']
  residency_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code representing the insured\'s state of residency at the time of policy issue. Determines applicable state insurance regulations, mandated benefit provisions, and state-specific exclusion limitations. Used in regulatory reporting to State Departments of Insurance.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the insured\'s role on the policy. Primary insured is the primary life covered; secondary/joint applies to survivorship or joint-life products; group_certificate identifies an insured under a group master policy; dependent covers riders; key_person identifies business key-person coverage.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this insured record was sourced (e.g., FAST, SAPIENS_LIFEPRO, ORACLE_IPAS). Supports data lineage tracking, multi-system reconciliation, and Silver layer ETL audit in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  table_rating varchar [note: 'Numerical or alphabetical table rating applied to substandard insured lives (e.g., Table 2, Table D). Each table increment typically represents an additional 25% of standard mortality. Used in premium surcharge calculation and reinsurance cession pricing.']
  tobacco_status varchar [note: 'Tobacco use classification of the insured as determined during underwriting. Directly impacts mortality risk class, premium rates, and COI charges. Non-tobacco and preferred non-tobacco classes receive lower rates. Status may be re-evaluated at policy anniversary for certain products.']
  underwriting_decision varchar [note: 'Final underwriting decision rendered for this insured-life role. Approved indicates standard or preferred issuance; approved_rated indicates substandard issuance with table rating or flat extra; approved_modified indicates policy issued with exclusion riders; postponed indicates deferred pending additional information; declined indicates coverage refused.']
  underwriting_decision_date date [note: 'Date on which the final underwriting decision was rendered for this insured-life role. Used to measure underwriting cycle time (NB processing KPI), track NIGO resolution, and support regulatory reporting on new business processing timelines.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this insured-life role record was last modified in the Silver layer data product. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail compliance under SOX. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  waiver_of_premium_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the AWL (Automatic Waiver of Premium) rider is active for this insured, waiving premium payments during a qualifying disability period. Relevant for billing system integration to suspend premium billing and for claims adjudication of disability claims.']

  Note: 'Role record for every insured individual covered under a life insurance policy or annuity contract. Captures the person whose life is the basis of coverage — distinct from the contract owner or annuitant. Stores role-specific attributes: tobacco/health status, occupation class, insurable interest flags, and coverage-specific risk indicators not on the party supertype. References the party record via FK for demographic identity (name, DOB, gender, SSN/TIN). SSOT for the insured-life role across underwriting, claims, and policy domains. Supports both individual and group certificate insured records.'
}

Table policyholder.contract_owner {
  contract_owner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for contract_owner']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance policy or annuity contract for which this party holds the owner role. A single policy may have one active owner at a time; history is tracked via ownership transfer flags.']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: The contract_owner table has an is_also_insured boolean flag indicating when the owner is also the insured on the policy. However, there is no FK to the actual insured role record. Adding insured_id c']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Contract owners hold policies issued by a specific licensed legal entity. SAP/GAAP financial reporting, RBC filings, and state regulatory submissions require attributing contract ownership to the issu']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party supertype record that holds identity attributes (legal name, SSN/TIN, date of birth, contact details) for this contract owner. Supports individual, trust, corporate, and ILIT ownership structures.']
  servicing_producer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.producer. Business justification: The servicing producer of record for a contract owner drives policy conservation campaigns, owner-directed service request routing, and producer commission statements. Life insurance operations requir']
  suitability_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation requires a suitability review for the contract owner (purchaser) before policy issuance. The contract owner is the regulated buyer; this link ']
  trust_id varchar [note: 'FK to policyholder.trust']
  uw_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Contract owners are established during application underwriting. Linking owner to originating application tracks ownership from inception, supports STOLI investigations (ownership patterns from applic']
  absolute_assignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an absolute (irrevocable) assignment of ownership has occurred, transferring all ownership rights to a new party. When True, the original owner loses all policy rights and the new owner record becomes active.']
  aml_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Anti-Money Laundering (AML) risk classification assigned to the contract owner based on KYC due diligence, transaction patterns, and OFAC/sanctions screening. Drives enhanced due diligence (EDD) requirements and transaction monitoring thresholds.']
  beneficiary_designation_authority varchar [note: 'Level of authority the contract owner holds to change beneficiary designations on the policy. \'full\' allows unrestricted changes; \'restricted\' requires co-owner or irrevocable beneficiary consent; \'none\' applies when ownership rights have been assigned.']
  collateral_assignee_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the lender or creditor to whom the policy has been collaterally assigned. Required for death benefit payment processing and CSV surrender authorization when collateral_assignment_flag is True.']
  collateral_assignment_date date [note: 'Date on which the collateral assignment of the policy became effective. Used to determine priority of claims against policy proceeds and to track assignment duration for servicing restrictions.']
  collateral_assignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy has been assigned as collateral to a lender or creditor by the contract owner. When True, the assignee has priority rights over the cash surrender value (CSV) and death benefit proceeds up to the loan balance.']
  correspondence_preference varchar [note: 'Contract owner\'s preferred method for receiving policy correspondence, statements, and regulatory notices. Drives document delivery routing in the Policy Administration System and CRM platform.']
  electronic_consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract owner provided consent for electronic document delivery. Required for regulatory audit trails under the NAIC Electronic Transactions Model Act and state e-delivery regulations.']
  electronic_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract owner has provided affirmative consent to receive policy documents and regulatory notices electronically, as required by the NAIC Electronic Transactions Model Act and applicable state e-delivery laws.']
  entity_state_of_formation varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code where the trust, corporation, partnership, or other non-individual entity was legally formed or domiciled. Used for state regulatory compliance and premium tax situs determination.']
  exchange_1035_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ownership record is associated with a tax-free 1035 exchange transaction, where the policy was funded by proceeds from a prior life insurance policy or annuity contract. Triggers specific IRS cost-basis tracking requirements.']
  insurable_interest_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the legal insurable interest relationship between the contract owner and the insured life. Required at policy issuance to validate that the owner has a legitimate financial interest in the continued life of the insured. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: self|spouse|dependent|business_partner|employer|creditor|trust — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_also_annuitant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract owner is also the annuitant on an annuity contract. Relevant for annuity tax treatment, RMD calculations, and death benefit trigger rules under IRC Section 72.']
  is_also_insured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract owner is also the insured life on the policy. When True, the owner-insured relationship triggers specific tax treatment under IRC Section 7702 and MEC testing rules.']
  is_primary_owner boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this party is the primary (first-named) owner of the policy or annuity contract. The primary owner holds full beneficiary designation authority and receives all policy correspondence.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current Know Your Customer (KYC) identity verification status for the contract owner. Drives AML compliance workflows and may restrict policy servicing transactions until verification is complete.']
  kyc_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract owner\'s identity was most recently verified and KYC status was set to \'verified\'. Used for AML periodic review scheduling and regulatory audit trails.']
  ofac_last_screened_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract owner was most recently screened against OFAC sanctions lists. Used to ensure periodic re-screening compliance and to trigger alerts when screening is overdue.']
  ofac_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) and sanctions list screening for the contract owner. \'blocked\' status prevents policy issuance and servicing transactions per regulatory requirements.']
  owner_role_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier assigned by the Policy Administration System to uniquely identify this ownership role record. Used in correspondence, 1035 exchange documentation, and collateral assignment notices.']
  ownership_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this party\'s ownership role became legally effective for the policy or annuity contract. For original owners, this aligns with the policy issue date. For transferred ownership, this is the assignment effective date.']
  ownership_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this party\'s ownership role ended, due to absolute assignment, policy surrender, death of owner, or contract termination. Null for currently active ownership records.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage share of ownership held by this party when multiple co-owners exist on a single policy or annuity contract. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 50.00 for 50%). Must sum to 100.00 across all active owners for a given policy.']
  ownership_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential ordinal indicating the order of ownership for a given policy, used to distinguish primary owner (1) from co-owners (2, 3, etc.) and to order historical ownership records chronologically.']
  ownership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract owner role. Transitions from pending (application stage) to active (policy issued), and may move to transferred (absolute assignment) or terminated (policy lapse/surrender/maturity).']
  ownership_transfer_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the transfer of policy ownership from a prior owner to this contract owner. Supports 1035 exchange tracking, gift tax reporting, and estate planning audit trails. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: absolute_assignment|divorce_decree|court_order|gift|sale|ilit_transfer|estate_transfer|bankruptcy — promote to reference product]']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the legal entity type holding ownership of the policy or annuity contract. Drives KYC/AML requirements, beneficiary designation authority, and tax reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: individual|trust|corporation|ilit|partnership|estate|government|non_profit — promote to reference product]']
  prior_contract_number varchar [note: 'Policy or contract number of the prior life insurance policy or annuity from which a 1035 exchange was executed. Required for IRS cost-basis carryover calculations and exchange documentation.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract owner role record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and SOX compliance for financial data governance.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract owner role record was most recently modified in the Silver Layer data product. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this contract owner record was sourced (e.g., FAST, SAPIENS_LIFEPRO, ORACLE_OIPA). Used for data lineage tracking and ETL reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  source_system_owner_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier for this contract owner record in the originating Policy Administration System (e.g., FAST owner role ID, Sapiens LifePro owner record key). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit.']
  stoli_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract owner relationship has been flagged for Stranger-Originated Life Insurance (STOLI) review. STOLI arrangements, where a third party with no insurable interest finances a policy, are prohibited in most US states.']
  tin varchar [note: 'Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) for the contract owner, which may be a Social Security Number (SSN) for individuals or an Employer Identification Number (EIN) for trusts, corporations, and other entities. Required for IRS Form 1099 reporting and AML/KYC compliance.']
  tin_certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of IRS TIN certification (W-9 or W-8 form) for the contract owner. Drives backup withholding obligations under IRS regulations. \'backup_withholding\' indicates the owner is subject to 28% withholding on taxable distributions.']
  tin_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of TIN provided by the contract owner: SSN (Social Security Number for individuals), EIN (Employer Identification Number for entities), ITIN (Individual Taxpayer Identification Number for non-resident aliens), or foreign (non-US tax identifier).']

  Note: 'Role record for the legal owner of a life insurance policy or annuity contract. The owner may be an individual, a trust (ILIT), a corporation, or another legal entity — and may differ from the insured or annuitant. Stores ownership type (individual, trust, corporate, ILIT), ownership effective date, ownership transfer history flags, and role-specific attributes not on the party supertype. References the party record via FK for identity attributes (legal name, TIN/SSN, entity details). SSOT for policy ownership role identity, supporting 1035 exchange tracking, beneficiary designation authority, and collateral assignment governance.'
}

Table policyholder.annuitant {
  annuitant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the annuitant role record in the Annuity Administration Platform. Primary key for the annuitant entity within the policyholder domain Silver layer.']
  benefit_structure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.benefit_structure. Business justification: The annuitant\'s gmab_indicator, gmdb_indicator, gmib_indicator, gmwb_indicator directly correspond to benefit_structure guaranteed benefit flags. The benefit structure defines the formula parameters f']
  cohort_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.cohort_definition. Business justification: LDTI/IFRS17 require annuitants to be grouped into cohorts by issue year and product for GAAP reserve measurement. Each annuitant\'s cohort determines the locked-in discount rate and initial CSM/LFPB am']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuity contract record for which this individual serves as the annuitant. Drives benefit calculations, payout phase administration, and RMD tracking in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party supertype record that holds the annuitant\'s demographic identity (full name, date of birth, gender, SSN/TIN, address). The annuitant role record is a specialization of the party entity.']
  suitability_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: Annuity suitability reviews assess product appropriateness for the annuitant (age, income needs, liquidity). Compliance teams link reviews to specific annuitants for state insurance department exam de']
  underwriting_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: Annuitant mortality classification drives payout calculations, reserve requirements, and COI charges. The annuitant\'s issue_age, gender, tobacco_status, and mortality_table_code are inputs to underwri']
  uw_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Annuity contracts originate from underwriting applications. Linking annuitant to application tracks which underwriting case created the annuitant role, essential for suitability reviews, annuity-speci']
  annuitant_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier assigned by the Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity / FAST Annuity) to uniquely identify the annuitant role on a contract. Used in bordereaux reporting and customer correspondence.']
  annuity_start_date date [note: 'The date on which annuity benefit payments commence (annuitization date). Governs the payout phase schedule, RMD calculation start, and GMIB/GMWB benefit base lock-in. Also known as the income start date or annuitization effective date in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  annuity_type varchar [note: 'The type of annuity product associated with this annuitant role. \'FIA\' = Fixed Indexed Annuity; \'VA\' = Variable Annuity; \'SPIA\' = Single Premium Immediate Annuity; \'MYGA\' = Multi-Year Guaranteed Annuity; \'DIA\' = Deferred Income Annuity; \'QLAC\' = Qualifying Longevity Annuity Contract. Drives product-specific benefit calculation logic and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FIA|VA|SPIA|MYGA|DIA|QLAC — promote to reference product]']
  benefit_base_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current guaranteed benefit base amount used for GMIB, GMWB, or GMAB calculations. Represents the notional value upon which guaranteed benefit payments or withdrawal amounts are calculated, distinct from the actual account value. Updated annually or upon step-up elections per the rider terms.']
  contract_qualification_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the annuity contract is held in a qualified (tax-deferred) or non-qualified (after-tax) account. \'qualified\' includes traditional IRA, 401(k), 403(b); \'non_qualified\' is funded with after-tax dollars; \'roth_ira\' has distinct RMD and distribution rules; \'inherited_ira\' applies to beneficiary-owned contracts. Drives RMD applicability, tax withholding, and 1099-R reporting.']
  current_age varchar [note: 'The annuitant\'s age in whole years as of the most recent valuation or processing date. Refreshed periodically by the Annuity Administration Platform for RMD factor lookups, benefit recalculations, and payout phase administration. Stored to avoid repeated real-time computation in batch actuarial runs.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'The annuitant\'s date of birth as recorded on the annuity contract application. Used directly by the Annuity Administration Platform for mortality table lookups, age-based benefit calculations, RMD required beginning date determination, and GMDB/GMIB/GMWB payout factor selection. Stored here as a role-level denormalization for performance-critical actuarial calculations.']
  death_benefit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current death benefit amount payable upon the annuitant\'s death, reflecting the greater of the account value or the GMDB benefit base (if applicable). Updated at each valuation cycle. Used by the Claims Management System for death claim adjudication and by the Actuarial Valuation System for NAR calculations.']
  death_benefit_option varchar [note: 'The distribution option elected by the beneficiary for the death benefit payable upon the annuitant\'s death. \'lump_sum\' is a single payment; \'annuitize\' converts to an income stream; \'five_year_rule\' distributes within 5 years per IRC Section 72(s); \'stretch_ira\' uses life expectancy distributions for eligible designated beneficiaries under SECURE Act rules.']
  death_date date [note: 'The date of death of the annuitant as reported and verified by the Annuity Administration Platform. Triggers death benefit processing, payout cessation or continuation (for joint annuitants), beneficiary notification, and claim adjudication workflows. Null for living annuitants.']
  death_verified_date date [note: 'The date on which the annuitant\'s death was officially verified by the insurer through receipt of a death certificate or equivalent documentation. Distinct from the reported death date. Initiates the formal death claim adjudication process and triggers statutory interest accrual on death benefits.']
  gender varchar [note: 'The annuitant\'s gender as recorded on the contract application. Used in mortality table selection and payout factor calculations where gender-distinct tables are applied. Some states mandate unisex tables; the mortality_table_code field captures the applicable table assignment.']
  gmab_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB) rider is active on this annuitant\'s contract. When True, the contract guarantees the account value will be at least equal to the GMAB benefit base at the end of the accumulation period, regardless of market performance.']
  gmdb_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit (GMDB) rider is active on this annuitant\'s contract. When True, the death benefit payable upon the annuitant\'s death is the greater of the account value or the GMDB benefit base. Drives death benefit calculation logic in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  gmib_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) rider is active on this annuitant\'s contract. When True, the annuitant may annuitize at a guaranteed minimum benefit base regardless of account value performance. Drives GMIB benefit base tracking and annuitization calculations.']
  gmwb_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) rider is active on this annuitant\'s contract. When True, the annuitant is guaranteed a minimum annual withdrawal amount regardless of account value depletion. Drives GMWB benefit base and maximum annual withdrawal calculations.']
  is_joint_annuitant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this annuitant record represents a joint annuitant on a joint-and-survivor annuity contract. When True, the payout continues to the surviving annuitant at the designated continuation percentage after the primary annuitant\'s death. Drives joint-life payout factor selection in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  issue_age varchar [note: 'The annuitant\'s age in whole years at the time the annuity contract was issued. Captured as a point-in-time snapshot used for benefit base calculations, payout factor selection, and actuarial reserving. Derived from date_of_birth and contract issue date at time of issuance and stored for audit stability.']
  joint_survivor_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the primary annuitant\'s benefit payment that continues to the joint annuitant (survivor) after the primary annuitant\'s death. Common values are 50%, 66.67%, 75%, and 100%. Null if is_joint_annuitant is False. Used in joint-life payout factor calculations.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'The current Know Your Customer (KYC) identity verification status for this annuitant. \'verified\' indicates all identity checks have passed; \'pending\' indicates verification is in progress; \'failed\' indicates a verification failure requiring remediation; \'exempt\' applies to certain institutional or trust-owned contracts. Required for AML/BSA compliance.']
  kyc_verified_date date [note: 'The date on which the annuitant\'s KYC identity verification was most recently completed and confirmed. Used for AML compliance tracking and periodic re-verification scheduling.']
  mortality_table_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the actuarial mortality table assigned to this annuitant for payout factor calculations and reserve valuation (e.g., \'2012 IAR\', \'2000 CSO\', \'UP-1984\', \'RP-2014\'). Assigned at contract issue and may be updated upon annuitant change. Used by the Actuarial Valuation System (MoSes/AXIS/Prophet) for PBR reserve calculations.']
  payout_option_code varchar [note: 'The annuity payout option elected for this annuitant\'s contract. Governs the income stream structure: \'life_only\' pays for the annuitant\'s lifetime only; \'life_period_certain\' guarantees payments for a minimum period; \'joint_survivor\' continues to a joint annuitant; \'period_certain\' pays for a fixed term; \'lump_sum\' is a single payment; \'systematic_withdrawal\' is a non-annuitized withdrawal schedule. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: life_only|life_period_certain|joint_survivor|period_certain|lump_sum|systematic_withdrawal — promote to reference product]']
  period_certain_years varchar [note: 'The number of years guaranteed under a life-with-period-certain or period-certain-only payout option. Common values are 5, 10, 15, or 20 years. Null if the payout option does not include a period-certain guarantee. Used to calculate the minimum benefit obligation and reserve.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this annuitant role record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports audit trail requirements, data lineage tracking, and SOX compliance. Follows the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this annuitant role record was most recently updated in the Silver layer data product. Used for incremental load detection, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance. Follows the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  rmd_calculation_method varchar [note: 'The IRS life expectancy table method used to calculate the annuitant\'s annual RMD amount. \'uniform_lifetime\' uses the Uniform Lifetime Table (most common); \'joint_life_expectancy\' applies when the sole beneficiary is a spouse more than 10 years younger; \'single_life_expectancy\' applies in certain inherited IRA scenarios. Null for non-qualified contracts.']
  rmd_required_begin_date date [note: 'The date by which the annuitant must begin taking Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs) from a qualified annuity contract (IRA, 403(b), etc.). Determined by IRS rules based on the annuitant\'s date of birth and the applicable SECURE Act age threshold (age 73 under SECURE 2.0). Null for non-qualified contracts.']
  role_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this annuitant role became effective on the contract. May differ from the contract issue date if the annuitant was added or changed via a contract endorsement or owner-driven change.']
  role_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this annuitant role was terminated, replaced, or became inactive. Null for currently active annuitant roles. Populated upon death, contract surrender, or annuitant change endorsement.']
  role_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the annuitant role record. \'active\' indicates the annuitant is the current measuring life; \'deceased\' triggers death benefit processing and payout recalculation; \'replaced\' indicates a successor annuitant has been designated; \'cancelled\' applies to voided or rescinded contracts.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the annuitant\'s role on the contract. \'primary\' is the measuring life for payout calculations; \'joint\' triggers joint-and-survivor benefit rules; \'contingent\' activates if the primary annuitant predeceases; \'successor\' applies under certain IRA/qualified plan continuation rules.']
  source_system_annuitant_code varchar [note: 'The native annuitant identifier as assigned by the originating Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity or FAST Annuity). Preserved for cross-system reconciliation, ETL lineage, and audit traceability back to the system of record.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational source system from which this annuitant record was ingested (e.g., \'SAPIENS_ANNUITY\', \'FAST_ANNUITY\'). Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the Silver layer of the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  ssn_tin varchar [note: 'The annuitant\'s Social Security Number (SSN) or Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) as recorded on the annuity contract. Required for IRS 1099-R tax reporting, RMD compliance, and AML/KYC identity verification. Stored at the role level to support tax reporting workflows independent of the party supertype.']
  state_of_issue varchar [note: 'The two-letter US state code of the state in which the annuity contract was issued. Determines applicable state insurance regulations, unisex mortality table requirements, free-look period, surrender charge rules, and state premium tax obligations.']
  suitability_review_status varchar [note: 'The status of the annuity suitability review conducted for this annuitant at the time of contract issuance or material change. Required under NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation (#275) and applicable state regulations. \'approved\' indicates the product was deemed suitable; \'rejected\' blocks issuance; \'waived\' applies to certain replacement or exchange transactions.']
  tax_withholding_election varchar [note: 'The annuitant\'s federal income tax withholding election for annuity payments and distributions. \'withhold\' elects mandatory withholding; \'do_not_withhold\' opts out where permitted; \'state_only\' applies state withholding only. Governs 1099-R reporting and payment processing in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  withholding_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The elected federal income tax withholding percentage applied to annuity payments and distributions. Applicable when tax_withholding_election is \'withhold\'. Null if the annuitant has elected no withholding or if a flat dollar amount is used instead.']

  Note: 'Role record for the individual whose life expectancy governs annuity benefit calculations and payout duration. Distinct from the contract owner and insured in annuity contracts. Stores role-specific attributes: annuity start date, joint annuitant indicator, mortality table assignment, and payout-phase parameters not on the party supertype. References the party record via FK for demographic identity (name, DOB, gender, SSN/TIN). SSOT for the annuitant-life role used by the Annuity Administration Platform for RMD tracking, GMDB/GMIB/GMWB benefit calculations, and payout phase administration.'
}

Table policyholder.policyholder_beneficiary {
  policyholder_beneficiary_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each beneficiary designation record in the policyholder domain. Serves as the primary key for the policyholder_beneficiary data product. Entity role: MASTER_PARTY — represents a named party designated to receive death benefit (DB) or contract proceeds.']
  aml_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_case. Business justification: Beneficiary AML screening at claim payout is a named compliance process. policyholder_beneficiary already carries aml_screening_status, confirming AML review occurs at the beneficiary role level. An A']
  annuitant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.annuitant. Business justification: The policyholder_beneficiary master record serves both life insurance policies and annuity contracts (evidenced by the annuity_contract_id FK). For annuity contracts, the beneficiary is designated wit']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Annuity contracts require beneficiary records for death benefit distribution (primary, contingent, per stirpes designations). Current model only links beneficiaries to life insurance policies. Annuity']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance policy or annuity contract under which this beneficiary designation applies. Enables cross-domain linkage to the policy or annuity contract record.']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: The policyholder_beneficiary master record represents a named beneficiary designated to receive death benefit proceeds upon the insured\'s death. The relationship_to_insured string field on policyholde']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder (contract owner or insured) who made this beneficiary designation. Links the beneficiary record to the owning party in the policyholder master.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: Beneficiary designation changes are processed as service requests in policy administration. Linking the resulting beneficiary record to the originating service request supports audit trails, NIGO trac']
  trust_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.trust. Business justification: Beneficiaries can be trust entities. Currently trust_formation_date and trust_ein are stored in policyholder_beneficiary. Normalizing to trust.trust_id FK allows joining to the authoritative trust ent']
  uw_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Beneficiaries are designated during application. Linking beneficiary to application tracks initial designation, supports STOLI investigations (unusual beneficiary patterns from application), and enabl']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the beneficiary\'s mailing address. Used for claims correspondence, benefit payment delivery, and regulatory reporting. Required for state-mandated unclaimed property (escheatment) compliance when a beneficiary cannot be located.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the beneficiary (apartment, suite, unit, floor, PO Box). Supplements address_line1 for complete mailing address capture.']
  aml_screening_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the beneficiary\'s AML (Anti-Money Laundering) screening against OFAC sanctions lists, PEP (Politically Exposed Person) databases, and adverse media. \'cleared\' indicates no matches found; \'flagged\' indicates a potential match requiring review; \'pending\' indicates screening is in progress; \'escalated\' indicates the case has been referred to the AML compliance team.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number for EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer) disbursement of death benefit proceeds to the beneficiary. Stored in encrypted/tokenized form per PCI DSS and data security standards. Populated only when payment_method is \'eft\'.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'ABA routing transit number (RTN) for the beneficiary\'s bank, used for EFT disbursement of death benefit proceeds. Nine-digit code identifying the financial institution. Populated only when payment_method is \'eft\'.']
  beneficiary_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the beneficiary is a natural person (individual), a legal trust (including ILIT — Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust), the insured\'s estate, a charitable organization, or another legal entity such as a corporation or partnership. Drives KYC/AML identity verification requirements and tax reporting obligations.']
  beneficiary_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the beneficiary\'s priority tier in the designation hierarchy. Primary beneficiaries receive proceeds first; contingent beneficiaries receive proceeds if all primary beneficiaries predecease the insured; tertiary beneficiaries are the third-tier fallback. Governs the order of benefit distribution upon a death benefit (DB) claim.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the beneficiary\'s mailing address. Used for claims correspondence, state jurisdiction determination, and unclaimed property escheatment filings.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the beneficiary\'s country of residence (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Required for FATCA/FBAR foreign beneficiary reporting, AML/KYC country risk assessment, and international claims payment processing.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the individual beneficiary. Used to verify identity during claims adjudication, determine minor beneficiary status (requiring guardian or trust appointment), calculate required minimum distributions (RMD) for inherited annuity contracts, and comply with HIPAA and state insurance regulations.']
  designation_form_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier of the signed beneficiary designation or change form submitted by the policyholder. Provides an audit trail linking the data record to the physical or digital source document stored in the Document Management system. Supports compliance and legal dispute resolution.']
  designation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage share of the death benefit (DB) or contract proceeds allocated to this beneficiary within their priority tier (primary, contingent, or tertiary). All beneficiaries within the same tier must sum to 100.00%. Governs pro-rata distribution of proceeds at claim settlement.']
  designation_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal sequence number used to order multiple beneficiaries within the same priority tier (primary, contingent, or tertiary) on a given policy. Supports display ordering on policy documents and beneficiary designation forms. Does not imply priority — all beneficiaries in the same tier share proceeds per their designation_percentage.']
  designation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the beneficiary designation record. \'active\' indicates the designation is in force; \'revoked\' indicates the policyholder has removed this beneficiary; \'superseded\' indicates a newer designation has replaced this record; \'pending\' indicates the change request is awaiting processing or approval; \'expired\' indicates the designation has passed its effective end date.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this beneficiary designation became legally effective. Typically corresponds to the date the signed beneficiary change form was received and processed by the insurer. Used to determine which designation was in force at the time of the insured\'s death for claims adjudication.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the beneficiary. Used for digital claims notification, document delivery, and beneficiary portal access. Supports CRM-driven customer engagement workflows for beneficiary outreach.']
  entity_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the trust, estate, charity, or corporate entity designated as beneficiary. Populated when beneficiary_category is \'trust\', \'estate\', \'charity\', or \'entity\'. Supports ILIT (Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust) and other non-individual beneficiary structures. Required for AML entity due diligence.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this beneficiary designation ceases to be effective, if applicable. Null for open-ended designations. Used for time-limited beneficiary arrangements such as collateral assignments or court-ordered designations with defined end dates.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal given name of the individual beneficiary as recorded on the beneficiary designation form. Populated only when beneficiary_category is \'individual\'. Required for identity verification, KYC compliance, and death benefit (DB) claim processing.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Gender of the individual beneficiary as recorded on the designation form. Used for identity verification during claims processing and for actuarial life expectancy calculations on inherited annuity contracts subject to RMD rules.']
  guardian_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the court-appointed or designated guardian or custodian for a minor beneficiary. Populated only when minor_flag is True. Required for claims payment processing to ensure proceeds are directed to the legal guardian rather than the minor directly.']
  is_irrevocable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this beneficiary designation is irrevocable. When True, the policyholder cannot change or revoke the beneficiary without the beneficiary\'s written consent. Common in ILIT (Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust) structures, collateral assignments, and divorce decree requirements. Restricts policy servicing actions such as loans, surrenders, and assignment changes.']
  kyc_verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the beneficiary\'s KYC identity verification was completed and approved. Used to track the currency of identity verification and trigger re-verification workflows when the verification has aged beyond the insurer\'s AML program refresh threshold.']
  kyc_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the beneficiary\'s identity has been verified through the insurer\'s KYC (Know Your Customer) process. When True, identity documents have been reviewed and validated per AML/BSA program requirements. Required for high-value death benefit (DB) payments and trust/entity beneficiaries.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal surname of the individual beneficiary as recorded on the beneficiary designation form. Populated only when beneficiary_category is \'individual\'. Required for identity verification and death benefit (DB) claim processing.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Legal middle name or initial of the individual beneficiary. Used to disambiguate beneficiaries with common first and last names during claims adjudication and identity verification.']
  minor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the individual beneficiary is a legal minor (under age 18 or applicable state age of majority) at the time of designation. When True, a guardian, custodian, or trust must be appointed to receive and manage death benefit proceeds on behalf of the minor, triggering special claims handling workflows.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for disbursing death benefit (DB) or contract proceeds to this beneficiary. \'eft\' indicates electronic funds transfer to a bank account; \'check\' indicates paper check; \'wire\' indicates domestic or international wire transfer; \'annuity_settlement\' indicates proceeds are to be paid as an annuity settlement option rather than a lump sum.']
  per_stirpes_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the beneficiary\'s share is designated per stirpes (by representation). When True, if this beneficiary predeceases the insured, the beneficiary\'s share passes to their lineal descendants rather than being redistributed among surviving beneficiaries. Governs death benefit (DB) distribution logic in claims adjudication.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the beneficiary. Used by the claims department to notify and communicate with beneficiaries upon the insured\'s death. Supports outbound claims notification workflows in the Claims Management System.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code for the beneficiary\'s mailing address. Used for claims correspondence routing, state jurisdiction determination, and unclaimed property escheatment compliance.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this beneficiary designation record was first created in the system of record. Provides the audit trail creation marker required for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this beneficiary designation record was most recently modified in the system of record. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  relationship_to_insured varchar [note: 'Describes the legal or familial relationship between the beneficiary and the insured (e.g., spouse, child, parent, sibling, domestic partner, business partner, trust, estate, charity). Used in insurable interest determinations, claims adjudication, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spouse|child|parent|sibling|domestic_partner|business_partner|grandchild|grandparent|other_relative|trust|estate|charity|employer|other — promote to reference product]']
  settlement_option varchar [note: 'The elected settlement option governing how death benefit (DB) proceeds will be paid to this beneficiary. \'lump_sum\' pays the full amount at once; \'life_income\' provides lifetime annuity payments; \'fixed_period\' pays over a specified number of years; \'interest_only\' retains principal and pays interest; \'joint_survivor\' provides income for two lives. Drives annuity contract administration workflows.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this beneficiary designation record was sourced (e.g., FAST, SAPIENS_LIFEPRO, ORACLE_IPAS). Supports data lineage tracking, ETL reconciliation, and multi-system integration in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or territory code for the beneficiary\'s mailing address (e.g., CA, NY, TX). Used to determine applicable state insurance regulations, unclaimed property escheatment jurisdiction, and state income tax withholding on death benefit proceeds.']
  tin varchar [note: 'The IRS-issued Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) for the beneficiary, which may be a Social Security Number (SSN) for individuals or an Employer Identification Number (EIN) for trusts and entities. Required for IRS Form 1099-R tax reporting on death benefit proceeds and for AML/KYC identity verification. Stored in encrypted form per data security standards.']

  Note: 'Master record for each named beneficiary designated to receive death benefit (DB) or contract proceeds upon the insured\'s or annuitant\'s death. Stores beneficiary name, relationship to insured, beneficiary type (primary, contingent, tertiary), designation percentage, TIN/SSN, trust/entity name for non-individual beneficiaries, irrevocable flag, and effective/expiry dates. Supports ILIT and trust beneficiary structures. SSOT for beneficiary identity and designation data across policy and annuity domains.'
}

Table policyholder.kyc_verification {
  kyc_verification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for kyc_verification']
  aml_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_case. Business justification: KYC verification that flags OFAC matches, PEP status, or suspicious activity directly escalates to an AML case — a named BSA/AML operational process. kyc_verification carries ofac_match_flag, sar_flag']
  identity_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: BSA/AML and USA PATRIOT Act compliance require the actual identity document (passport, driver\'s license) to be retrievable from the KYC record. kyc_verification stores identity_document_number, identi']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.in_force_policy. Business justification: Regulatory AML/KYC compliance requires ongoing KYC monitoring tied to specific in-force policies. Regulators (FinCEN, state DOI) require KYC records traceable to active policies. policy_number is a de']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: KYC/AML programs are administered at the legal entity level per BSA/AML regulatory requirements. Each KYC verification is conducted under a specific legal entity\'s compliance program. FinCEN and state']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder party (insured, owner, annuitant, or beneficiary) who is the subject of this KYC/AML verification event.']
  remediation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.remediation_plan. Business justification: KYC deficiencies requiring corrective action link to remediation plans. Operations track which KYC gaps are being addressed through formal remediation for regulatory exam responses and ongoing monitor']
  sar_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sar_filing. Business justification: BSA requires SAR filing when KYC verification uncovers suspicious activity. kyc_verification has sar_flag and sar_reference_number attributes confirming a direct SAR filing relationship. This link sup']
  uw_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: KYC verifications are triggered by underwriting applications to comply with AML/KYC regulations. Linking verification to application tracks compliance at underwriting, supports STOLI/AML risk scoring ']
  aml_program_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the AML/BSA compliance program ruleset or screening configuration in effect at the time of this verification. Enables audit traceability when program rules are updated.']
  beneficial_owner_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the beneficial owner(s) of the policy or annuity contract (e.g., trust beneficiaries, ILIT trustees) were identified and verified as part of this KYC event, as required under FinCEN\'s Beneficial Ownership Rule for legal entity customers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this KYC/AML verification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance record retention tracking.']
  enhanced_due_diligence_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) is required for this party based on the outcome of this verification event. EDD is mandated for high-risk parties, PEPs, and parties from high-risk jurisdictions under FATF and FinCEN guidelines.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this KYC/AML verification record expires and re-verification is required. Distinct from next_review_date in that expiry_date represents the hard compliance deadline, while next_review_date may be an earlier scheduled review.']
  identity_document_expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the identity document used for verification. Expired documents may trigger re-verification requirements under CIP rules.']
  identity_document_issuing_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the government authority that issued the identity document. Used for OFAC screening and cross-border AML risk assessment.']
  identity_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of government-issued identity document presented or verified during the identity proofing process. Required under FinCEN Customer Identification Program rules.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or territory code (ISO 3166-2 US subdivision) of the regulatory jurisdiction governing this verification event. Determines applicable state Department of Insurance (DOI) requirements and reporting obligations.']
  kyc_program_type varchar [note: 'The specific KYC program tier under which this verification was conducted. CIP (Customer Identification Program) is the baseline; CDD (Customer Due Diligence) is the standard ongoing program; EDD (Enhanced Due Diligence) applies to high-risk parties; ongoing_monitoring covers periodic re-screening.']
  mib_check_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Medical Information Bureau (MIB) inquiry was performed as part of this verification event. MIB checks are used in life insurance underwriting to detect undisclosed medical conditions or prior application activity.']
  mib_match_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MIB inquiry returned a coded record match for the party, suggesting prior insurance application activity or a reportable medical condition. Triggers underwriting review when True.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic KYC/AML re-verification of this party, determined by the assigned risk rating and AML program policy. High-risk parties require more frequent review cycles.']
  ofac_match_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party\'s name or identifiers returned a potential match against the OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list or other OFAC sanctions lists during this verification event.']
  ofac_match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric confidence score (0.00–100.00) returned by the OFAC screening engine indicating the degree of similarity between the party\'s identity data and a potential SDN list match. Used to distinguish true positives from false positives.']
  party_role varchar [note: 'The role of the policyholder party being verified in relation to the insurance contract. Determines the applicable KYC/AML requirements and risk thresholds for the verification.']
  pep_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party has been identified as a Politically Exposed Person (PEP) — a current or former senior government official, military officer, or their close associate — requiring enhanced due diligence under FATF and FinCEN guidelines.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'AML/KYC risk rating assigned to the policyholder party as a result of this verification event. Drives ongoing monitoring frequency, enhanced due diligence requirements, and SAR filing thresholds per FinCEN and NAIC guidelines.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score (typically 0–100) assigned by the verification system or third-party bureau to quantify the AML/KYC risk level of the party. Complements the categorical risk_rating for fine-grained risk stratification and analytics.']
  sar_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this KYC/AML verification event has triggered or is associated with a Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) filing with FinCEN. Drives escalation workflows and compliance reporting obligations.']
  sar_reference_number varchar [note: 'The FinCEN-assigned or internal reference number of the Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) filed in connection with this verification event. Populated only when sar_flag is True. Classified as confidential due to legal sensitivity of SAR disclosures.']
  source_of_funds_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the verified source of funds for premium payments or annuity contributions (e.g., \'Employment income\', \'Proceeds from real estate sale\', \'Inheritance\', \'1035 Exchange from prior policy\'). Classified as confidential due to financial sensitivity.']
  source_of_funds_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the source of funds for premium payments or annuity contributions was verified during this KYC/AML event. Required for high-risk parties and large premium transactions under AML/BSA program requirements.']
  ssn_tin_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party\'s SSN or TIN was successfully verified against IRS or Social Security Administration records during this verification event. Critical for tax compliance and identity proofing.']
  stoli_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this verification event identified characteristics consistent with a Stranger-Originated Life Insurance (STOLI) arrangement, where a policy is originated by a third party with no insurable interest. Triggers enhanced review and potential policy rescission.']
  trigger_event varchar [note: 'The business event that initiated this KYC/AML verification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_business|policy_renewal|beneficiary_change|ownership_change|large_transaction|periodic_review|regulatory_request|reinstatement|1035_exchange|annuity_payout — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this KYC/AML verification record was last modified. Tracks updates to verification status, risk rating, or SAR flag following analyst review or system re-screening.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the KYC/AML verification event was initiated or performed. Used for regulatory timeliness tracking and compliance reporting.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The operational method used to perform the verification. Manual indicates analyst-driven review; automated indicates rules-engine or system-driven check; third_party_bureau indicates use of an external data provider (e.g., LexisNexis, Experian); document_review indicates physical or digital document inspection; biometric indicates fingerprint, facial recognition, or other biometric confirmation.']
  verification_outcome_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded explanation of the reason for the verification outcome, particularly for fail, escalated, or inconclusive statuses. Documents the specific finding (e.g., \'Name mismatch on OFAC SDN list\', \'Expired identity document\', \'Insufficient documentation provided\').']
  verification_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this verification event by the verifying system or third-party bureau, used for audit trail and cross-system reconciliation.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the KYC/AML verification event. Pass indicates successful identity confirmation; fail indicates identity could not be confirmed or a match was found on a watchlist; pending indicates verification is in progress; escalated indicates the case has been referred for manual review or SAR filing; inconclusive indicates insufficient data to render a determination.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone offset) when the KYC/AML verification event was executed by the system or analyst. Distinct from verification_date; used for audit trail and SLA measurement.']
  verification_type varchar [note: 'Category of KYC/AML verification performed. Values: identity (standard identity proofing), aml_bsa (Anti-Money Laundering/Bank Secrecy Act screening), ofac (Office of Foreign Assets Control sanctions screening), mib (Medical Information Bureau check), enhanced_due_diligence (EDD for high-risk parties), pep_screening (Politically Exposed Person screening).']
  verifying_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the system, platform, or third-party bureau that performed or facilitated the verification (e.g., \'LexisNexis Risk Solutions\', \'Experian IdentityWorks\', \'Salesforce FSC KYC Module\', \'Manual Review\'). Supports audit trail and vendor management.']
  watchlist_screened varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or coded identifier of the watchlists and sanctions databases screened during this verification event (e.g., \'OFAC_SDN, EU_SANCTIONS, UN_SANCTIONS, FBI_MOST_WANTED\'). Documents the scope of screening for regulatory audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each KYC (Know Your Customer) and AML (Anti-Money Laundering) identity verification event performed on a policyholder party (insured, owner, annuitant, or beneficiary). Stores verification type (identity, AML/BSA, OFAC, MIB), verification date, verification method (manual, automated, third-party bureau), verification status (pass, fail, pending, escalated), risk rating assigned, FinCEN/SAR flag, and the identity of the verifying system or analyst. Supports NAIC, FinCEN, and state DOI compliance obligations.'
}

Table policyholder.party_address {
  party_address_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each party address record in the policyholder domain. Serves as the primary key for the party_address data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder party (insured, owner, annuitant, beneficiary, or trust entity) to whom this address belongs. Links the address record to the master party record in the policyholder domain.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: Address changes for policyholders are processed as service requests in life insurance policy administration. Linking party_address to the originating service_request supports audit trails for address ']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line including house/building number, street name, and street type (e.g., \'123 Main St\'). Used for USPS/CASS validation, regulatory notice delivery, and premium tax situs determination.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for apartment, suite, unit, floor, PO Box, or care-of designations. Supplements address_line_1 for complete deliverability. May be null when not applicable.']
  address_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating business channel or system through which this address was captured or updated. Used for data quality auditing and tracing address provenance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: application|policy_service|crm|agent_submission|ncoa|self_service|document — promote to reference product]']
  address_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the address record. \'active\' indicates the address is current and in use; \'inactive\' indicates the address is no longer current but retained for history; \'undeliverable\' indicates USPS or carrier returned mail as undeliverable; \'pending_validation\' indicates the address has been entered but not yet CASS-validated.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the functional purpose of the address record. \'home\' is the primary residential address; \'mailing\' is the preferred correspondence address; \'business\' is a commercial address; \'legal_domicile\' is the address used for state DOI jurisdiction and premium tax situs determination; \'seasonal\' is a secondary residence; \'temporary\' is a short-term address. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: home|mailing|business|legal_domicile|seasonal|temporary — promote to reference product]']
  address_verified_by varchar [note: 'Identifies the method or service used to verify the accuracy and deliverability of this address. \'usps_cass\' indicates USPS CASS certification; \'third_party\' indicates a commercial address verification vendor; \'manual\' indicates manual review by operations staff; \'self_reported\' indicates unverified party-provided data; \'ncoa\' indicates updated via USPS NCOA linkage.']
  address_verified_date date [note: 'The date on which the address was most recently verified through the method identified in address_verified_by. Used to assess address data currency and trigger re-verification workflows when the verification age exceeds policy thresholds.']
  cass_return_code varchar [note: 'The standardized USPS CASS return/footnote code indicating the result of the address validation process (e.g., \'A\' for address corrected, \'B\' for PO Box match, \'N\' for no match found). Used to assess address quality and deliverability risk for regulatory notices.']
  cass_validated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the address has been validated and standardized through the USPS Coding Accuracy Support System (CASS) certification process. CASS validation ensures deliverability and is required for bulk mailing compliance and regulatory notice delivery.']
  cass_validation_date date [note: 'The date on which the address was last validated through the USPS CASS certification process. CASS certifications expire periodically; this date is used to determine whether re-validation is required for regulatory notice mailings.']
  census_tract varchar [note: 'US Census Bureau census tract code for the address location. Used for demographic analytics, Community Reinvestment Act (CRA) compliance reporting, actuarial territory studies, and market penetration analysis.']
  city varchar [note: 'Municipality or city name of the address. Used in conjunction with state and ZIP code for USPS validation, state DOI jurisdiction determination, and regulatory notice delivery.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code (e.g., \'USA\', \'CAN\', \'GBR\'). Required for international policyholders, ILIT trust addresses, and AML/KYC country-of-residence determination. Defaults to \'USA\' for domestic policies.']
  county varchar [note: 'County or parish name within the state. Used for local tax jurisdiction determination, actuarial territory assignments, and certain state-level regulatory reporting requirements. May be null for non-US addresses.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this address record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  do_not_mail boolean [note: 'Indicates that the party has requested suppression of physical mail to this address, or that the address has been flagged as undeliverable for mailing purposes. When True, physical correspondence should be suppressed and alternative delivery methods (e.g., electronic) should be used where permitted.']
  doi_jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'The two-letter state code identifying the state Department of Insurance (DOI) with regulatory jurisdiction over this party based on their address of record. Used to determine applicable state insurance regulations, required policy forms, mandated notices, and consumer protection rules.']
  dpv_confirmation_code varchar [note: 'USPS Delivery Point Validation (DPV) confirmation code indicating the deliverability status of the address. \'Y\' = confirmed deliverable; \'S\' = deliverable to primary number only (secondary missing); \'D\' = deliverable to primary number only (secondary not needed); \'N\' = not deliverable. Critical for ensuring regulatory notices reach policyholders.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this address became the active address of record for the party. Used to establish the timeline of address history, determine applicable state jurisdiction for regulatory notices, and support premium tax situs audits.']
