  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this address record ceased to be the active address of record. Null for currently active addresses. Used to maintain a complete address history for regulatory compliance, audit trails, and KYC/AML reviews.']
  fips_county_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit FIPS county code (2-digit state FIPS + 3-digit county FIPS) for the address location. Used for actuarial territory assignments, catastrophe risk zone mapping, regulatory reporting, and geographic analytics aligned with US Census Bureau standards.']
  foreign_address boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address is located outside the United States. Foreign addresses trigger additional AML/KYC review requirements, FATCA reporting obligations, and may affect state DOI jurisdiction determination and premium tax situs.']
  geocode_quality varchar [note: 'Indicates the precision level of the geocoded coordinates for this address. \'rooftop\' is the highest precision (exact building location); \'parcel\' is parcel-level; \'street\' is street-level interpolation; \'zip_centroid\' is ZIP code centroid; \'city_centroid\' is city centroid; \'failed\' indicates geocoding was unsuccessful. Used to assess reliability of geographic analytics.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address is the designated primary address of record for the party. Only one address per party should have this flag set to True at any given time. The primary address is used for all official regulatory notices, policy documents, and premium billing correspondence.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Populated through geocoding services. Used for actuarial territory rating, catastrophe risk modeling, geographic segmentation analytics, and proximity-based agent assignment.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Populated through geocoding services. Used in conjunction with latitude for actuarial territory rating, catastrophe risk modeling, and geographic analytics.']
  msa_code varchar [note: 'US Office of Management and Budget (OMB) Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA) code for the address location. Used for market segmentation, distribution analytics, actuarial experience studies, and geographic reporting aligned with OMB statistical area definitions.']
  ncoa_move_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent address update identified through the USPS National Change of Address (NCOA) linkage process. Populated when an NCOA match is found, indicating the party has filed a change-of-address with USPS. Used to proactively update policyholder address records.']
  ncoa_move_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of USPS National Change of Address (NCOA) move record matched to this party. \'individual\' indicates a single person move; \'family\' indicates a household move; \'business\' indicates a business entity move. Used to determine the scope of address update actions required.']
  postal_code_foreign varchar [note: 'Postal or postcode for non-US international addresses where the USPS ZIP code format does not apply (e.g., Canadian postal codes, UK postcodes). Used for international policyholder correspondence and AML/KYC country-of-residence verification.']
  province_region varchar [note: 'Province, region, or administrative division for non-US addresses where the state_code field is not applicable. Used for international policyholder address records, ILIT trust addresses, and foreign beneficiary addresses. Null for domestic US addresses.']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing integer version number for this address record, incremented on each update. Used for optimistic concurrency control, change history tracking, and ensuring data consistency across distributed Silver Layer processing in Databricks.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier for this address record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Policy Administration System such as Sapiens LifePro, FAST, or CRM such as Salesforce Financial Services Cloud). Used for data lineage tracing, ETL reconciliation, and cross-system address matching.']
  source_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this address record was sourced (e.g., \'Sapiens LifePro\', \'FAST\', \'Salesforce FSC\', \'Majesco Billing\'). Used for data lineage, ETL audit trails, and multi-system address deduplication.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state or territory abbreviation (e.g., \'CA\', \'NY\', \'TX\'). Critical for determining state Department of Insurance (DOI) jurisdiction, premium tax situs, regulatory filing requirements, and applicable state insurance law. Used in NAIC regulatory reporting.']
  tax_situs_state varchar [note: 'The two-letter state code designating the state of premium tax situs for this address. Determined from the legal domicile or home address and used by the Finance and Actuarial teams for state premium tax calculations, SAP/GAAP reporting, and NAIC regulatory filings. May differ from state_code when the legal domicile address differs from the mailing address.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this address record was most recently modified in the Silver Layer data product. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance.']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit USPS ZIP code for the address. Used for mail deliverability, geographic segmentation, premium tax situs, and actuarial territory rating. Combined with zip_plus4 for full ZIP+4 precision.']
  zip_plus4 varchar [note: 'Four-digit ZIP+4 extension code assigned by USPS to narrow delivery to a specific block face or building. Enhances mail deliverability precision and is required for CASS-certified address validation. Null when not yet validated or not available.']

  Note: 'Master record for all physical and mailing addresses associated with a policyholder party (insured, owner, annuitant, beneficiary). Stores address type (home, mailing, business, legal domicile), street, city, state, ZIP+4, county, country, address effective date, and USPS/CASS validation status. Supports state DOI jurisdiction determination, premium tax situs, and regulatory notice delivery. A party may have multiple addresses with one designated as the primary address of record.'
}

Table policyholder.party_contact {
  party_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each contact point record associated with a policyholder party. Serves as the primary key for the party_contact entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder party (insured, contract owner, annuitant, or beneficiary) to whom this contact point belongs. One party may have multiple contact records.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: Contact information updates (email, phone, TCPA consent changes) are processed as service requests in policy administration. Linking party_contact to service_request supports audit trails for contact ']
  best_contact_time_end varchar [note: 'Latest time of day (HH:MM in 24-hour format, local to contact time_zone) at which the party prefers to be contacted via this channel. Paired with best_contact_time_start to define the preferred outreach window.']
  best_contact_time_start varchar [note: 'Earliest time of day (HH:MM in 24-hour format, local to contact time_zone) at which the party prefers to be contacted via this channel. Used by CRM outbound scheduling to respect party preferences and TCPA calling hour restrictions.']
  bounce_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of delivery failures (bounces) recorded for this contact point. For email contacts, tracks hard and soft bounces. For phone contacts, tracks failed delivery attempts. Used to trigger automatic deactivation after a configurable threshold is exceeded.']
  contact_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank indicating the priority order for attempting contact via this channel when multiple contacts of the same type exist for a party. Lower values indicate higher priority (e.g., rank 1 is attempted before rank 2). Supports fallback outreach sequencing in CRM and claims notification workflows.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact record. \'bounced\' applies to email addresses that have returned delivery failures. \'opted_out\' reflects a party\'s explicit withdrawal from contact via this channel. Drives suppression logic in outreach campaigns.']
  contact_subtype varchar [note: 'Further qualifies the contact type to distinguish personal contacts from business, trust (ILIT), or estate-related contacts. Supports customer relationship hierarchy management for complex ownership structures.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the communication channel for this contact record. Drives routing logic for CRM outreach, regulatory notices, billing alerts, and claims correspondence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: home_phone|mobile_phone|work_phone|email|fax|digital_id|pager|social_handle — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  contact_use_purpose varchar [note: 'Specifies the business purpose(s) for which this contact point may be used. Enables granular consent and suppression management — for example, a party may allow billing notifications but opt out of marketing. Aligns with GDPR purpose limitation principle.']
  contact_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual contact point data — phone number, email address, fax number, or digital identifier — stored as a string. Classification tag applies to phone and email values; downstream masking rules are applied based on contact_type. For email values, format follows RFC 5321. For phone values, format follows E.164 international standard.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code associated with this contact point. Used for international dialing prefix resolution, regulatory jurisdiction determination, and GDPR/CCPA applicability assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contact record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record-keeping requirements.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the assessed quality and reliability of this contact record, derived from verification status, bounce history, and last successful contact date. Used by data stewards and CRM teams to prioritize contact data cleansing efforts.']
  do_not_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates that the party has explicitly requested no outreach via this contact point. When true, all non-mandatory communications (marketing, service campaigns) are suppressed. Mandatory regulatory notices (e.g., lapse notices, policy anniversary statements) may still be sent per state insurance regulations.']
  do_not_contact_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the do_not_contact flag is set. Supports compliance audit trails and ensures appropriate handling of suppressed contacts. \'legal_hold\' indicates a litigation or regulatory hold. \'fraud_flag\' indicates a suspected fraudulent contact.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this contact record is considered valid and active for use in outreach and regulatory notice delivery. Supports bi-temporal data modeling for contact history tracking.']
  email_opt_in_date date [note: 'Date on which the party provided or last updated their email opt-in consent. Required for CAN-SPAM and GDPR audit trails. Null if email_opt_in_status is opted_out or unsubscribed.']
  email_opt_in_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the party\'s email marketing and communication opt-in status for this email contact. Applies only when contact_type is email. Governs CAN-SPAM and CASL compliance for outbound email campaigns and service notifications.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this contact record is no longer considered valid. Null for open-ended active contacts. Used in bi-temporal history management and to enforce contact data retention policies.']
  is_preferred boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the party\'s overall preferred method of communication across all channels. Distinct from is_primary, which is channel-scoped. Used for CRM campaign targeting and service notification routing.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact record is the designated primary contact for its channel type. Only one contact per party per contact_type may be flagged as primary. Used by CRM and policy administration systems to determine the default outreach channel.']
  is_regulatory_notice_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact point is approved for delivery of mandatory regulatory notices (e.g., lapse notices, grace period warnings, annual statements, policy anniversary notices) as required by state insurance regulations. A contact may be suppressed for marketing but still eligible for regulatory notices.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'BCP 47 language tag (e.g., en-US, es-MX, zh-CN) indicating the party\'s preferred language for communications delivered via this contact channel. Supports multilingual regulatory notice delivery and CRM localization.']
  last_bounce_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent delivery failure for this contact point. Used alongside bounce_count to assess contact data quality and determine whether re-verification or deactivation is warranted.']
  last_successful_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent confirmed successful outreach via this contact point (e.g., email delivered without bounce, call answered, SMS delivered). Used to assess contact data quality and trigger re-verification or update workflows.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context about this contact record, such as special handling instructions, agent-entered remarks, or explanations for non-standard contact configurations. Not used for PII storage.']
  phone_country_dial_code varchar [note: 'International dialing prefix (e.g., +1 for USA, +44 for UK) for phone and fax contact types. Populated only when contact_type is home_phone, mobile_phone, work_phone, or fax. Supports E.164 number formatting for outbound dialing systems.']
  phone_extension varchar [note: 'Optional telephone extension number for work phone contact types, typically used for corporate or group policyholder contacts. Appended after the main phone number during outbound dialing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this contact record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when the same contact appears in multiple source systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: policy_admin|crm|underwriting|claims|billing|annuity|agent_portal|self_service|reinsurance — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_contact_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier for this contact record in the originating operational system (e.g., Salesforce Contact ID, Policy Administration System contact reference). Enables cross-system reconciliation and ETL lineage tracing.']
  tcpa_consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the party provided or last updated their TCPA consent for this contact number. Required for audit and regulatory compliance documentation. Null if tcpa_consent_status is not_consented.']
  tcpa_consent_method varchar [note: 'Method by which TCPA consent was obtained for this contact. Supports audit trail requirements and legal defensibility in the event of a TCPA complaint or regulatory inquiry.']
  tcpa_consent_status varchar [note: 'Records the party\'s consent status under the Telephone Consumer Protection Act (TCPA) for automated calls, robocalls, and text messages to this contact number. Mandatory for mobile_phone contact types. Drives suppression of automated outreach to non-consented numbers.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier (e.g., America/New_York, America/Chicago) associated with this contact point. Used by CRM and outbound dialing systems to schedule calls and messages within permissible calling hours per TCPA regulations.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Business user ID or system process identifier that last modified this contact record. Supports audit trail and change accountability requirements under SOX and NAIC market conduct standards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this contact record. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the contact point was most recently verified as reachable and accurate. Used to assess contact data freshness and trigger re-verification workflows when contacts exceed the maximum staleness threshold.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify this contact point. Supports audit trail for KYC/AML compliance and regulatory notice delivery confirmation. \'third_party\' indicates verification via an external data provider such as LexisNexis or Experian.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this contact point has been validated through an outreach verification process (e.g., email confirmation link, SMS OTP, phone call confirmation). Supports KYC/AML compliance and ensures regulatory notices reach the intended party.']

  Note: 'Master record for all communication contact points for a policyholder party — phone numbers, email addresses, and digital identifiers. Stores contact type (home phone, mobile, work, email, fax), contact value, preferred contact indicator, TCPA consent status, contact verification date, and active/inactive status. One party may have multiple contacts with one designated as primary per channel. Supports CRM outreach, service notifications, and regulatory notice delivery. Contact preferences and restrictions are governed by the communication_preference entity.'
}

Table policyholder.relationship {
  relationship_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the inter-party relationship record within the policyholder domain. Primary key for the relationship data product.']
  legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: POA, guardianship, and insurable interest relationships require the authorizing legal document to be directly retrievable for compliance and claims processing. relationship stores legal_doc_reference ']
  relationship_party_b_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the second party (object/grantee side) in the relationship. For a power-of-attorney record, this is the attorney-in-fact. For a guardianship record, this is the guardian/custodian. For a spousal relationship, this is the spouse.']
  relationship_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the first party (subject/grantor side) in the relationship. For a power-of-attorney record, this is the principal granting authority. For a guardianship record, this is the ward/minor. For a spousal relationship, this is the policyholder.']
  trust_id varchar [note: 'FK to policyholder.trust']
  aml_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this relationship record has been flagged for AML review due to complex ownership structures (e.g., ILIT, corporate ownership, foreign trust), high-risk jurisdictions, or unusual transaction patterns. Triggers referral to the AML compliance team.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date on which consent was formally obtained from the relevant parties for the establishment and use of this relationship record. Supports HIPAA and state privacy law compliance audit trails.']
  consent_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit consent has been obtained from the relevant parties for the establishment and use of this relationship record, as required by HIPAA, state privacy laws, and GDPR for international policyholders. Particularly relevant for healthcare POA and medical information sharing relationships.']
  court_appointment_date date [note: 'Date on which the court formally appointed the guardian or conservator for the ward. Establishes the legal commencement of guardianship authority for claims payment and beneficiary management purposes. Null for non-court-appointed custodial arrangements (e.g., UTMA/UGMA).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this relationship record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Establishes the audit trail for record origination and supports SOX and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the relationship became legally effective and operative for policy servicing, claims, and financial transaction authorization purposes.']
  guardianship_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the guardianship or custodianship legally expires, typically corresponding to the ward\'s age of majority (18 or 21 depending on jurisdiction) or a court-specified termination date. After this date, the ward assumes direct control of policy proceeds and beneficiary designations.']
  guardianship_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'US state or territory in which the guardianship or custodianship was legally established and is governed. Two-letter USPS state code (e.g., CA, NY, TX). Determines applicable state law for guardianship authority, age of majority, and UTMA/UGMA account rules.']
  guardianship_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the guardianship or custodial arrangement when relationship_subtype is legal_guardian, utma_custodian, or ugma_custodian. Determines the legal framework governing the guardian\'s authority over the ward\'s insurance proceeds and beneficiary designations.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level within the customer or ownership hierarchy at which this relationship exists. Level 1 = direct relationship to the policyholder; higher levels represent indirect relationships (e.g., trustee of an ILIT that owns a policy). Supports ILIT ownership chain modeling and group policy member-to-sponsor hierarchy analytics.']
  insurable_interest_basis varchar [note: 'Legal basis establishing insurable interest between the parties, required for policy issuance and anti-STOLI (Stranger-Originated Life Insurance) compliance. Documents the economic or familial nexus that justifies the insurance relationship. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: familial|financial_dependency|business_partner|creditor_debtor|employer_employee|trust_beneficiary|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  insurable_interest_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the insurable interest basis has been formally verified and documented during the underwriting or new business application process. Required for STOLI compliance and regulatory audit.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary or controlling relationship of its type for the party pair. For example, a policyholder may have multiple beneficiary relationships but only one primary beneficiary. Used in beneficiary designation management and claims adjudication workflows.']
  kyc_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the KYC identity verification process has been completed for the relationship parties, particularly for trust ownership, POA, and ILIT structures subject to AML/BSA compliance requirements.']
  legal_doc_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the legal instrument that establishes or evidences this relationship. Drives document verification workflows and compliance audit requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: marriage_certificate|birth_certificate|court_order|poa_document|trust_agreement|corporate_resolution|divorce_decree|adoption_order — promote to reference product]']
  legal_doc_verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the supporting legal documentation was verified and accepted by the company. Establishes the audit trail for compliance with KYC/AML documentation requirements and policy servicing authorization controls.']
  legal_doc_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supporting legal documentation for this relationship has been received, reviewed, and verified by the policy servicing or new business team. Unverified relationships may be restricted from certain transaction types pending document receipt.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information about this relationship record entered by policy servicing staff. Examples include documentation of unusual ownership structures, pending legal proceedings, or special handling instructions for claims.']
  party_a_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of Party A within this specific relationship. Provides semantic context for the relationship direction and governs which policy servicing transactions Party A may initiate. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: owner|insured|annuitant|beneficiary|principal|ward|grantor|employer|sponsor — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  party_b_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of Party B within this specific relationship. Governs the scope of authority, transaction eligibility, and legal standing of Party B in relation to Party A\'s policies and accounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spouse|dependent|trustee|attorney_in_fact|guardian|custodian|corporate_officer|key_person|employee|member — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  poa_authorization_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of authority granted under the power-of-attorney instrument. Full scope permits all policy servicing and financial transactions; limited scope restricts the attorney-in-fact to specific enumerated transactions as documented in the authorization_scope_description field. Null for non-POA relationships.']
  poa_authorization_scope_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific transactions or authorities granted when poa_authorization_scope is limited. Documents the enumerated powers (e.g., premium payments only, surrender requests, beneficiary changes) for policy servicing and compliance audit purposes.']
  poa_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the power-of-attorney instrument expires and the attorney-in-fact\'s authority ceases. Null for durable or open-ended POA instruments. System must enforce this date to prevent unauthorized transactions after expiry.']
  poa_revocation_date date [note: 'Date on which the principal formally revoked the power-of-attorney instrument, terminating the attorney-in-fact\'s authority. Null if the POA has not been revoked. Triggers status change to revoked and blocks further transaction authorization.']
  poa_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the power-of-attorney instrument when relationship_subtype is power_of_attorney. Durable POA remains effective upon incapacity; limited POA is restricted to specific transactions; healthcare POA covers medical decisions; springing POA activates upon a triggering event. Null for non-POA relationships.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the relationship record. Drives eligibility for policy servicing transactions, claims authorization, and beneficiary payouts. A terminated or revoked status prevents further use of the relationship for financial transaction authorization.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the inter-party relationship. Drives downstream processing rules for policy servicing, claims authorization, and beneficiary management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: familial|legal_authorization|fiduciary|business|trust_ownership|group_sponsorship — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this relationship record originated. PAS = Policy Administration System; CRM = Customer Engagement Platform; UW = Underwriting Workbench; CLAIMS = Claims Management System; ANNUITY = Annuity Administration Platform; MANUAL = manually entered by operations staff.']
  source_system_relationship_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this relationship record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Salesforce Financial Services Cloud relationship ID, FAST Policy Administration relationship key). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Granular classification within the relationship type. Examples include: spouse, domestic_partner, parent, child, sibling, grandparent, trustee, ilit_trustee, corporate_officer, power_of_attorney, legal_guardian, utma_custodian, ugma_custodian, employer_sponsor, key_person. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spouse|domestic_partner|parent|child|sibling|grandparent|trustee|ilit_trustee|corporate_officer|power_of_attorney|legal_guardian|utma_custodian|ugma_custodian|employer_sponsor|key_person — promote to reference product]']
  termination_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the relationship was legally terminated, dissolved, or expired. Null for open-ended active relationships. For guardianship, typically the ward\'s age of majority date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this relationship record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change data capture, audit trail maintenance, and regulatory compliance reporting.']

  Note: 'Association record capturing formal legal, familial, or business relationships between two parties in the policyholder domain. Stores relationship type (spouse, parent, child, trustee, ILIT trustee, corporate officer, power of attorney, legal guardian, UTMA custodian), relationship subtype, effective date, termination date, insurable interest basis, and legal documentation reference. For power-of-attorney relationships: POA type (durable, limited, healthcare), attorney-in-fact identity, authorization scope (full or limited to specific transactions), effective date, expiration date, and revocation date — supporting policy servicing, claims, and financial transaction authorization workflows. For guardianship relationships: guardianship type (legal guardian, custodian, UTMA custodian), court appointment date, jurisdiction of appointment, and guardianship expiration date (typically at age of majority) — supporting claims payment to minors, beneficiary designation management, and UTMA/UGMA account coordination. Supports customer hierarchy modeling, ILIT trust ownership chains, group policy member-to-sponsor relationships, and all inter-party legal authorizations. SSOT for all inter-party associations, legal authorizations, power-of-attorney records, and guardianship records within the policyholder domain.'
}

Table policyholder.beneficiary_designation {
  beneficiary_designation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for beneficiary_designation']
  aml_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_case. Business justification: Beneficiary designation changes are a known STOLI and fraud vector requiring AML case tracking. beneficiary_designation carries stoli_review_flag, aml_screening_date, and aml_screening_status confirmi']
  annuitant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.annuitant. Business justification: For annuity contracts, beneficiary designations are made with respect to the annuitant — the annuitant\'s death triggers the death benefit payout to the designated beneficiary. While beneficiary_design']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Annuity contracts have death benefit beneficiaries (GMDB, standard death benefit). Current model only links beneficiary designations to life insurance policies. Annuity death benefit claims processing']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the document management system record containing the scanned or electronic copy of the beneficiary designation form and any supporting legal documents (e.g., court order, divorce decree, trust agreement). Supports regulatory examination and claims adjudication.']
  form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.form. Business justification: Beneficiary designation forms are specific product forms filed with regulators and tracked in product.form. The beneficiary_designation carries denormalized form_number and form_version attributes. Re']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance policy or annuity contract on which this beneficiary designation event was recorded. Links the designation transaction to the governing policy master record.']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: A beneficiary designation is made with respect to a specific insured individual — the insured\'s death is the triggering event for the benefit payout. While beneficiary_designation already links to in_']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder or contract owner who submitted or authorized this beneficiary designation change. Establishes the party reference required for the TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  policyholder_beneficiary_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the beneficiary master record identifying the individual, trust, estate, or entity being designated. Distinct from the designation event itself — the master record holds the beneficiary\'s identity and contact details.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: Beneficiary designation forms are submitted and processed as service requests in policy admin systems. Linking beneficiary_designation to service_request enables NIGO resolution tracking, processing S']
  trust_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.trust. Business justification: Beneficiary designations can name trusts as beneficiaries. Currently trust_name and trust_date are stored as strings in beneficiary_designation. Normalizing to trust.trust_id FK allows joining to the ']
  uw_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Beneficiary designations occur at application time. Linking designation to application tracks initial setup, supports underwriting review of insurable interest, and enables audit of designation change']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'The legal distribution method governing how proceeds are allocated among beneficiaries. \'per_capita\' distributes equally among surviving beneficiaries; \'per_stirpes\' passes a deceased beneficiary\'s share to their descendants; \'specific_dollar\' designates a fixed dollar amount; \'specific_percentage\' designates a fixed percentage share.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage share of the death benefit, annuity proceeds, or account value allocated to this specific beneficiary under this designation. All active primary beneficiary allocations for a given policy must sum to 100.00%. Stored per designation record to support per-beneficiary audit history.']
  aml_screening_date date [note: 'The date on which the AML/KYC screening was performed for this beneficiary designation. Required for regulatory audit trail under FinCEN AML/BSA compliance. Null if screening was not required.']
  aml_screening_status varchar [note: 'The AML/KYC screening status of the designated beneficiary at the time of this designation event. Insurers are required under FinCEN AML/BSA rules to screen beneficiaries, particularly for large face amount policies and irrevocable designations. \'flagged\' triggers a Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) review.']
  beneficiary_entity_type varchar [note: 'Classifies whether the beneficiary is a natural person, a legal entity, or a special designation. Drives downstream tax withholding rules (e.g., trusts and estates have different IRC Section 72 treatment), KYC requirements, and claims payment processing workflows.']
  beneficiary_relationship varchar [note: 'The legal or familial relationship of the designated beneficiary to the insured or contract owner. Used for AML/KYC screening, insurable interest validation, tax reporting (spousal rollover eligibility under IRC Section 72), and claims adjudication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spouse|child|parent|sibling|grandchild|domestic_partner|trust|estate|charity|employer|other — promote to reference product]']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason or triggering life event that prompted the beneficiary designation change. Used for actuarial experience studies, compliance monitoring, and customer lifecycle analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_policy|marriage|divorce|death_of_beneficiary|birth_of_child|court_order|owner_request|policy_assignment|adoption|other — promote to reference product]']
  contingent_condition varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any special conditions or contingencies attached to this beneficiary designation (e.g., \'if beneficiary survives insured by 30 days\', \'subject to divorce decree dated MM/DD/YYYY\'). Supports complex designation structures and court-ordered conditions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this beneficiary designation record was first created in the data platform. Represents the audit record creation time in the silver layer, distinct from the business effective date and the received date. Required for SOX audit trail and data lineage.']
  designation_method varchar [note: 'The channel or legal instrument through which the beneficiary designation was submitted. \'paper_form\' is a wet-ink signed ACORD or proprietary form; \'e_signature\' is a digitally signed electronic form; \'court_order\' is a legally mandated designation (e.g., divorce decree, QDRO); \'will_reference\' designates the estate; \'online_portal\' is a self-service digital submission; \'phone_request\' is a voice-recorded request. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paper_form|e_signature|court_order|will_reference|online_portal|phone_request — promote to reference product]']
  designation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier assigned by the Policy Administration System (e.g., Sapiens LifePro, FAST) to uniquely reference this designation transaction. Used in correspondence, audit trails, and claims adjudication workflows.']
  designation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this designation transaction. \'pending\' indicates the form has been received but not yet processed; \'active\' indicates the designation is in force; \'superseded\' indicates a newer designation has replaced this one; \'revoked\' indicates the owner formally withdrew the designation; \'rejected\' indicates the form was rejected (e.g., NIGO); \'cancelled\' indicates administrative cancellation.']
  designation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the beneficiary\'s priority tier and revocability. \'primary\' beneficiaries receive proceeds first; \'contingent\' receive proceeds if all primary beneficiaries predecease the insured; \'tertiary\' is a third-tier designation; \'irrevocable\' designations cannot be changed without the beneficiary\'s written consent; \'revocable\' designations may be changed by the owner at any time.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this beneficiary designation becomes legally binding and in force on the policy. Typically the date the completed, good-order form is received and accepted by the insurer. Critical for claims adjudication to determine which designation was active at the time of the insured event.']
  guardian_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the court-appointed or designated guardian or custodian for a minor beneficiary. Required when minor_beneficiary_flag is True to ensure proceeds can be legally disbursed. Populated only for minor beneficiaries.']
  is_irrevocable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this beneficiary designation is irrevocable, meaning the policy owner cannot change or remove the beneficiary without the beneficiary\'s written consent. Irrevocable designations are common in divorce settlements, ILIT structures, and collateral assignments. Drives policy servicing workflow restrictions.']
  is_nigo boolean [note: 'Flags whether the beneficiary designation form was received in a Not In Good Order (NIGO) condition — i.e., missing required information, unsigned, or otherwise deficient. NIGO forms require follow-up with the policy owner before the designation can be processed and made effective.']
  jurisdiction_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code of the jurisdiction governing this beneficiary designation, typically the state where the policy was issued or the insured resides. Determines applicable state insurance law requirements for designation validity, notarization, and beneficiary rights.']
  minor_beneficiary_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the designated beneficiary is a minor (under age 18 or applicable state age of majority). Minor beneficiaries require a guardian or custodian to receive proceeds, triggering special claims payment workflows and UTMA/UGMA account considerations.']
  nigo_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded description of the specific deficiency that caused the designation form to be flagged as NIGO. Examples include missing beneficiary SSN/TIN, missing owner signature, illegible form, or missing notarization. Populated only when is_nigo is True.']
  nigo_resolved_date date [note: 'The date on which all NIGO deficiencies were remediated and the designation form was accepted as complete and in good order. Null if the form has not yet been resolved or was not NIGO. Used to measure NIGO resolution cycle time for operational reporting.']
  notarization_date date [note: 'The date on which the beneficiary designation form was notarized, if notarization was required. Null if notarization was not required or has not yet been completed. Used to validate the legal completeness of the designation.']
  notarization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the designation form required notarization under applicable state law, policy contract terms, or court order. Notarization is commonly required for irrevocable designations and court-ordered changes.']
  prior_beneficiary_snapshot varchar [note: 'A serialized summary of the beneficiary designation(s) that were in force immediately before this designation change was processed. Stored as a structured text snapshot (e.g., JSON-encoded summary) to support audit trail requirements and claims adjudication without requiring a full historical join. Captures prior beneficiary name, type, allocation percentage, and effective date.']
  processed_date date [note: 'The date the designation change was fully processed and recorded in the Policy Administration System. Represents the completion of the administrative workflow, distinct from the received date and the effective date.']
  received_date date [note: 'The date the beneficiary designation form or request was physically or electronically received by the insurer or its TPA. May differ from effective_date if the form required NIGO remediation or administrative processing time.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or administrative comments entered by the processing representative regarding this beneficiary designation event. Examples include notes on court order interpretation, special payment instructions, or follow-up actions required.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this designation record originated. \'PAS\' = Policy Administration System (e.g., FAST, Sapiens LifePro); \'CRM\' = CRM and Customer Engagement Platform; \'PORTAL\' = online self-service portal; \'TPA\' = Third Party Administrator submission; \'PAPER\' = paper form manually keyed; \'ANNUITY\' = Annuity Administration Platform.']
  stoli_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this beneficiary designation triggered a STOLI (Stranger-Originated Life Insurance) review. STOLI arrangements involve designating a stranger or investor as beneficiary to circumvent insurable interest requirements. Flagged designations require compliance review and may result in policy rescission.']
  superseded_date date [note: 'The date on which this designation was superseded by a subsequent designation change. Null if the designation is currently active or was revoked/rejected. Enables point-in-time reconstruction of the beneficiary designation history for audit and claims adjudication.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this beneficiary designation record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks the most recent update to the record for change data capture, audit trail, and data quality monitoring purposes.']
  witness_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the designation form required a witness signature under applicable state law or policy contract terms. Certain states and irrevocable designations mandate witnessed signatures for legal validity.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each formal beneficiary designation event — the act of naming, changing, or revoking a beneficiary on a policy or annuity contract. Stores designation effective date, prior beneficiary snapshot, new beneficiary allocation percentages, designation method (written form, e-signature, court order), NIGO flag if the form was not in good order, and processing status. Distinct from the beneficiary master record — this captures the lifecycle of designation changes over time for audit and claims adjudication purposes.'
}

Table policyholder.ownership_transfer {
  ownership_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each policy or annuity ownership transfer event record in the Silver layer lakehouse.']
  aml_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_case. Business justification: Ownership transfers for value are AML red flags (life settlements, STOLI). AML teams investigate suspicious transfers requiring case linkage for transaction monitoring alerts, SAR filings, and regulat']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Annuity ownership transfers (absolute assignments, collateral assignments, 1035 exchanges, gifts, sales for value) must be tracked per contract for tax reporting, STOLI review, and insurable interest ']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance policy or annuity contract on which the ownership transfer is being executed.']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: Ownership transfers on life insurance policies require verification of insurable interest between the new owner and the insured. The ownership_transfer table has insurable_interest_verified and insura']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Transfer-for-value transactions (IRC 101(a)(2)) require specific journal entries for gain/loss recognition and tax reporting. Ownership transfers also trigger GAAP/SAP accounting entries for considera']
  legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Ownership transfer processing requires the supporting legal document (assignment form, court order, trust instrument) to be directly retrievable. ownership_transfer stores legal_doc_reference as a den']
  primary_ownership_prior_owner_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder party record representing the outgoing owner before the transfer event.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: Ownership transfers are initiated and tracked as service requests in policy administration. This link connects the ownership transfer record to the service request that processed it, supporting transf']
  servicing_producer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.producer. Business justification: Ownership transfers are processed by the servicing producer who submits paperwork and ensures compliance documentation. This FK supports producer accountability reporting, commission adjustments on tr']
  suitability_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: Annuity ownership transfers trigger a new suitability review under NAIC replacement and suitability regulations. ownership_transfer has stoli_review_flag and transfer_for_value_flag indicating regulat']
  trust_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.trust. Business justification: Ownership transfers can transfer policy ownership to a trust (e.g., ILIT). Currently trust_name and trust_tax_id are stored as strings in ownership_transfer. Normalizing to trust.trust_id FK allows jo']
  aml_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ownership transfer requires Anti-Money Laundering (AML) review due to risk indicators such as high consideration amounts, unusual transfer patterns, or new owner risk profile.']
  aml_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the AML review for this ownership transfer. A SAR (Suspicious Activity Report) filed status indicates the transfer was reported to FinCEN.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the insurer formally approved the ownership transfer request after completing all required reviews and documentation verification.']
  collateral_assignee_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lending institution or creditor to whom the policy is collaterally assigned as loan security. Applicable only when transfer_type is collateral_assignment.']
  collateral_assignment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of the policy death benefit or cash value pledged as collateral under a collateral assignment transfer type. Applicable only when transfer_type is collateral_assignment.']
  collateral_assignment_release_date date [note: 'The date on which the collateral assignment was released by the assignee upon satisfaction of the underlying debt obligation, restoring full ownership rights to the policy owner.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The date on which all administrative processing was finalized, policy records were updated, and confirmation was issued to all parties.']
  consideration_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary consideration (purchase price or value exchanged) paid by the new owner in connection with the ownership transfer. A non-zero value triggers transfer-for-value analysis under IRC Section 7702.']
  consideration_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the consideration amount paid in connection with the ownership transfer (e.g., USD).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ownership transfer record was first created in the Silver layer lakehouse, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the ownership transfer becomes legally effective and the new owner assumes all policy rights and obligations. This is the principal business event date for IRC Section 7702 transfer-for-value compliance.']
  ilit_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trust receiving ownership is an Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust (ILIT), which has specific estate planning and tax implications under IRC Section 2042.']
  insurable_interest_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the new owner\'s insurable interest in the insured life has been verified as required by state insurance law prior to completing the ownership transfer.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or territory code representing the governing jurisdiction for this ownership transfer, which determines applicable state insurance law requirements for assignment and transfer.']
  legal_doc_type varchar [note: 'Type of legal documentation submitted to support and authorize the ownership transfer. Required for compliance validation and NIGO determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: absolute_assignment_form|collateral_assignment_form|court_order|divorce_decree|trust_agreement|power_of_attorney|corporate_resolution — promote to reference product]']
  legal_doc_verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the legal documentation was verified as authentic and complete by the authorized reviewer.']
  legal_doc_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the submitted legal documentation has been reviewed and verified as authentic and complete by the policy servicing team prior to processing the transfer.']
  new_owner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the new owner entity type, distinguishing between individual persons, trusts, corporations, partnerships, estates, and charitable organizations. Drives tax reporting and compliance requirements.']
  nigo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ownership transfer request was flagged as Not In Good Order (NIGO) due to missing, incomplete, or incorrect documentation. A NIGO flag suspends processing until deficiencies are resolved.']
  nigo_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded description of the specific deficiency that caused the ownership transfer request to be flagged as NIGO, such as missing signature, incomplete assignment form, or unverified notarization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or additional context recorded by the processing team regarding this ownership transfer event.']
  policy_csv_at_transfer decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Cash Surrender Value (CSV) of the policy or annuity contract at the time of the ownership transfer. Used for IRC Section 7702 transfer-for-value basis calculations and actuarial reporting.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known alphanumeric policy or annuity contract number as assigned by the policy administration system. Provides a human-readable business identifier for the contract subject to the ownership transfer.']
  request_date date [note: 'The date on which the ownership transfer request was formally submitted to the insurer by the policy owner or authorized representative.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the policy administration system of record from which this ownership transfer record was sourced (e.g., FAST, Sapiens LifePro, Oracle IPAS).']
  source_system_transfer_code varchar [note: 'The native transaction identifier assigned by the originating policy administration system for this ownership transfer event. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail back to the system of record.']
  stoli_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ownership transfer has been flagged for Stranger-Originated Life Insurance (STOLI) review due to patterns suggesting the policy was originated with the intent to transfer to a non-insurable-interest party.']
  transfer_for_value_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ownership transfer constitutes a transfer-for-value under IRC Section 7702, which may affect the income tax treatment of death benefit proceeds. Critical for tax compliance reporting.']
  transfer_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or motivation for the ownership transfer event. Used for actuarial analysis, compliance reporting, and customer segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: estate_planning|divorce_settlement|business_succession|loan_collateral|gift|court_order|ilit_funding|corporate_restructuring — promote to reference product]']
  transfer_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier assigned by the policy administration system (FAST, Sapiens LifePro) to uniquely identify this ownership change transaction for correspondence, audit, and regulatory reporting.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the ownership transfer request within the policy administration system. Drives processing queues and compliance holds.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ownership transfer event indicating the legal mechanism used. Absolute assignment conveys full ownership rights; collateral assignment pledges the policy as loan security; trust transfer moves ownership to a trust entity (e.g., ILIT); corporate ownership change reflects business entity ownership; divorce decree and court order are legally mandated transfers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: absolute_assignment|collateral_assignment|trust_transfer|corporate_ownership_change|divorce_decree|court_order — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ownership transfer record was most recently modified in the Silver layer lakehouse, supporting audit trail and change tracking requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each policy or annuity ownership transfer event — absolute assignment, collateral assignment, trust transfer, or corporate ownership change. Stores transfer type, prior owner reference, new owner reference, transfer effective date, transfer reason, legal documentation type (absolute assignment form, court order, divorce decree), NIGO flag, and processing status. Supports policy administration system (FAST, Sapiens LifePro) ownership change workflows and IRC Section 7702 transfer-for-value rule compliance.'
}

Table policyholder.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system identifier for the party record. Primary key. Serves as the single source of truth for any individual or legal entity participating in the policyholder domain across all roles (insured, owner, annuitant, beneficiary, trustee, etc.).']
  guardian_party_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on party (guardian_party_id)']
  aml_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Anti-Money Laundering (AML) risk classification for the party based on customer due diligence (CDD) assessment. Low indicates standard risk. Medium indicates elevated risk requiring enhanced monitoring. High indicates significant risk requiring enhanced due diligence (EDD). Prohibited indicates party cannot be onboarded due to AML concerns.']
  citizenship_country varchar [note: 'Country of citizenship for the party. Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). For individuals with dual citizenship, primary citizenship for tax and regulatory purposes. Used for FATCA/CRS reporting, OFAC screening, and foreign national underwriting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this party record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of the individual party. Null for non-individual party types. Critical for age-based underwriting, mortality calculations, annuity payout schedules, Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) calculations, and identity verification. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  deceased_date date [note: 'Date of death for the party. Populated when party_status transitions to deceased. Triggers death benefit processing, annuity death benefit calculations, and beneficiary claim adjudication. Null for living parties. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  deceased_verified_date date [note: 'Date the party death was officially verified through death certificate, Social Security Death Master File (DMF), or other authoritative source. Used for death benefit claim processing and audit trail. Null for living parties. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  entity_formation_date date [note: 'Date the legal entity (corporation, partnership, association) was formed or registered with the state. Null for individual and trust party types. Used for entity age verification and legal entity validation. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  entity_state_of_formation varchar [note: 'U.S. state or jurisdiction where the legal entity (trust, corporation, partnership) was formed or registered. Two-letter state code. Null for individual party types. Determines governing law, regulatory jurisdiction, and legal entity validation.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the individual party. Null for non-individual party types (trust, corporation, etc.). Used for personalized communications and identity verification.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Gender of the individual party. Null for non-individual party types. Used for mortality table selection, underwriting risk classification, and actuarial valuation. Non_binary and unknown options support inclusive data capture and regulatory compliance in jurisdictions requiring gender diversity recognition.']
  grantor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the grantor (settlor) who established the trust. Populated only when party_type is trust. Used for grantor trust tax rules, insurable interest verification, and estate planning documentation.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of Know Your Customer (KYC) identity verification for the party. Verified indicates successful identity verification. Pending indicates verification in progress. Failed indicates verification failure requiring manual review. Expired indicates verification has lapsed and requires renewal. Exempt indicates party is exempt from KYC (e.g., institutional entities).']
  kyc_verified_date date [note: 'Date the party successfully completed KYC identity verification. Used for KYC expiration tracking and periodic re-verification scheduling per AML program requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Surname or family name of the individual party. Null for non-individual party types. Primary sorting and indexing field for individual parties.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the party as it appears on government-issued identification or legal formation documents. For individuals: full name including middle name/initial. For entities: registered legal name as filed with state or jurisdiction of formation. Used for policy issuance, tax reporting (1099, 1042-S), and regulatory filings.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or middle initial of the individual party. Null for non-individual party types. Supports full name matching for identity verification and MIB (Medical Information Bureau) checks.']
  ofac_last_screened_date date [note: 'Date the party was last screened against OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list and other sanctions lists. Used for periodic re-screening scheduling per AML program requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  ofac_screening_status varchar [note: 'Status of Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctions screening for the party. Clear indicates no match to OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list. Match indicates potential match requiring investigation. Pending_review indicates match under investigation. Blocked indicates confirmed OFAC match prohibiting business relationship.']
  party_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the party. Human-readable reference number used in correspondence, customer service, and external communications.']
  party_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the party. Active indicates normal operational state. Deceased triggers death benefit processing. Incapacitated may require guardian or power of attorney. Sanctioned indicates OFAC or regulatory restriction. Fraud_flagged indicates suspected fraudulent activity requiring investigation.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the party entity. Distinguishes between natural persons (individual) and legal entities (trust, corporation, partnership, association, estate). Drives downstream processing rules for identity verification, tax reporting, and regulatory compliance.']
  pep_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party is a Politically Exposed Person (PEP) - an individual who holds or has held a prominent public position (government official, senior executive of state-owned enterprise, etc.). True indicates PEP status requiring enhanced due diligence (EDD). False indicates non-PEP status.']
  residency_country varchar [note: 'Country of tax residency for the party. Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. Determines tax reporting obligations, withholding requirements, and regulatory jurisdiction. May differ from citizenship_country for expatriates and foreign residents.']
  residency_state varchar [note: 'U.S. state or territory of primary residence for the party. Two-letter state code (e.g., CA, NY, TX). Determines state insurance department jurisdiction, premium tax situs, and state-specific regulatory requirements. Null for non-U.S. residents.']
  sar_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) has been filed for this party with FinCEN. True indicates SAR filing history requiring ongoing monitoring. False indicates no SAR filing history. SAR details are maintained in separate secure system per regulatory confidentiality requirements.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this party record (e.g., FAST, EXL, Sapiens, RGAX, Guidewire, Salesforce). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and source system traceability in multi-system environments.']
  source_system_party_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the party in the source operational system. Used for cross-system reconciliation, data lineage tracking, and bidirectional synchronization between lakehouse and operational systems.']
  ssn varchar [note: 'Social Security Number for U.S. individual parties. Masked or tokenized in storage per PII compliance requirements. Used for tax reporting (1099-R, 1042-S), IRS TIN matching, and identity verification. Null for non-U.S. parties or entities using EIN/TIN.']
  stoli_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party has been flagged for potential Stranger-Originated Life Insurance (STOLI) or investor-owned life insurance activity. True indicates STOLI concern requiring enhanced underwriting review and insurable interest verification. False indicates no STOLI concern.']
  suffix varchar [note: 'Generational or professional suffix appended to the individual party name (e.g., Jr., Sr., II, III, MD, Esq.). Null for non-individual party types. Supports accurate name matching and identity verification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Jr|Sr|II|III|IV|V|MD|PhD|Esq — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tin varchar [note: 'Taxpayer Identification Number for the party. For individuals: SSN or ITIN. For entities: EIN (Employer Identification Number). Masked or tokenized in storage. Used for IRS tax reporting, 1099 issuance, FATCA/CRS compliance, and TIN matching validation.']
  tin_certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of IRS TIN certification and validation. Certified indicates successful IRS TIN matching. Pending indicates awaiting validation. Failed indicates TIN mismatch requiring B-notice processing. Exempt indicates party is exempt from TIN certification (e.g., foreign entities under treaty).']
  tin_type varchar [note: 'Type of taxpayer identification number. SSN for U.S. individuals, EIN for entities, ITIN for non-resident aliens, foreign_tax_id for non-U.S. tax identifiers. Drives tax reporting format and regulatory compliance requirements.']
  trust_ein varchar [note: 'Employer Identification Number (EIN) assigned to the trust by the IRS. Populated only when party_type is trust. Used for tax reporting (1099-R, 1042-S) and IRS TIN matching. Masked or tokenized in storage per PII compliance.']
  trust_establishment_date date [note: 'Date the trust was legally established or executed. Populated only when party_type is trust. Used for trust age calculations, grantor trust rules, and estate planning compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  trust_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the trust entity. Populated only when party_type is trust. Used for policy ownership records, beneficiary designations, and trust-owned life insurance (TOLI) or irrevocable life insurance trust (ILIT) administration.']
  trust_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trust entity. ILIT (Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust) for life insurance ownership. Revocable_living for revocable living trusts. CRT (Charitable Remainder Trust), GRAT (Grantor Retained Annuity Trust), QPRT (Qualified Personal Residence Trust) for specialized estate planning trusts. Null for non-trust party types.']
  trustee_name varchar [note: 'Name of the current trustee(s) of the trust. Populated only when party_type is trust. May be individual or corporate trustee. Used for trust administration, correspondence, and fiduciary responsibility tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this party record was last updated. Updated on every modification to track data freshness and change frequency. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Supertype master record representing any individual or legal entity that participates in the policyholder domain in any role (insured, owner, annuitant, beneficiary, guardian, attorney-in-fact, trustee, group sponsor). Stores core identity attributes shared across all roles: legal name, DOB (individuals), gender, SSN/TIN/EIN, citizenship, residency status, party type (individual, trust, corporation, association), and party status (active, deceased, incapacitated, sanctioned). For trust/legal entity parties: trust type (ILIT, revocable living trust, CRT, corporate entity), trustee names, grantor information, establishment date, state of formation, and termination provisions. Maintains identity credentials (SSN, TIN, EIN, passport, drivers license, state ID, ITIN, foreign tax IDs) with issuing authority, issue/expiry dates, document type, and masked/tokenized values for PII compliance. Tracks full party status history as embedded audit trail: prior status, new status, effective date, reason code, source system, and triggering analyst/process — covering transitions through active, deceased, incapacitated, legally incompetent, minor, expatriate, sanctioned/OFAC, and fraud-flagged states. All role-specific records (insured, contract_owner, annuitant, beneficiary) reference this party record via FK. SSOT for the unique person/entity identity — enabling de-duplication across roles where the same individual serves as both insured and owner. Supports IRS TIN matching, FATCA/CRS foreign account reporting, OFAC screening, HIPAA-compliant identity management, and ACORD Party model alignment.'
}

Table policyholder.trust {
  assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the trust entity record. Primary key.']
  trust_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for trust']
  aml_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_case. Business justification: FinCEN beneficial ownership rules require AML case tracking for trust-owned policies. The trust product has aml_risk_rating, kyc_status, and ofac_screening_status, confirming AML processes apply at th']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: ILIT and trust ownership verification requires the trust agreement document to be directly retrievable from the trust record. trust stores document_reference as a denormalized string; a FK to document']
  primary_trustee_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party record for the current trustee of the trust.']
  successor_trust_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on trust (successor_trust_id)']
  trust_grantor_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party record for the grantor of the trust.']
  trust_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party master record representing this trust entity in the party system.']
  uw_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Trusts as owners/beneficiaries are verified during underwriting. Linking trust to application tracks trust underwriting (ILIT verification, insurable interest, STOLI review), supports financial underw']
  aml_risk_rating varchar [note: 'The assessed AML risk level for the trust entity based on beneficial ownership, jurisdiction, and transaction patterns.']
  beneficial_owner_disclosed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the beneficial owners of the trust (individuals with 25% or greater ownership or control) have been identified and disclosed per CDD requirements.']
  beneficial_owner_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the identity of disclosed beneficial owners has been verified through documentary or non-documentary methods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this trust record was first created in the data platform.']
  document_verification_date date [note: 'The date the trust document was verified and approved for policy ownership.']
  document_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trust document has been reviewed and verified by underwriting or legal for policy ownership purposes.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date the trust became effective and operational for policy ownership and beneficiary purposes.']
  ein varchar [note: 'The IRS-issued Employer Identification Number (Tax ID) for the trust entity, required for tax reporting and policy ownership.']
  establishment_date date [note: 'The date the trust was legally established or executed, as documented in the trust instrument.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The legal jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and administration of the trust, which may differ from the state of formation.']
  ilit_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this trust is specifically an Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust designed to own life insurance policies and exclude proceeds from the insured\'s taxable estate.']
  insurable_interest_basis varchar [note: 'The documented basis for the trust\'s insurable interest in the insured, such as grantor-insured relationship, beneficiary relationship, or business interest.']
  insurable_interest_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trust\'s insurable interest in the insured life has been verified and documented per underwriting requirements.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of KYC verification for the trust entity and its beneficial owners.']
  kyc_verified_date date [note: 'The date KYC verification was completed for the trust entity.']
  ofac_last_screened_date date [note: 'The most recent date OFAC sanctions screening was performed for this trust entity.']
  ofac_screening_status varchar [note: 'Status of OFAC sanctions screening for the trust entity, trustees, and beneficial owners.']
  revocable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trust is revocable (can be modified or terminated by the grantor) or irrevocable (cannot be changed without beneficiary consent).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this trust record originated.']
  source_system_trust_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this trust entity in the source operational system.']
  state_of_formation varchar [note: 'The U.S. state or jurisdiction under whose laws the trust was established and is governed.']
  stoli_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this trust ownership arrangement has been flagged for STOLI review due to potential stranger-originated life insurance concerns.']
  stoli_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of STOLI compliance review for this trust ownership.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Additional classification detail for the trust, such as Crummey trust, generation-skipping trust, or other specialized trust structures.']
  successor_trustee_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the designated successor trustee who will assume trustee responsibilities upon the current trustee\'s resignation, incapacity, or death.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date the trust was terminated, dissolved, or ceased to exist as a legal entity.']
  tin_certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of IRS TIN certification for backup withholding compliance.']
  tin_type varchar [note: 'Type of tax identification number used for the trust: EIN for trust entities or SSN for grantor trusts treated as disregarded entities.']
  trust_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the trust as registered in the trust document or declaration.']
  trust_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the trust for external reference and correspondence.']
  trust_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the trust entity in the system.']
  trust_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trust structure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: irrevocable_life_insurance_trust|revocable_living_trust|testamentary_trust|charitable_remainder_trust|special_needs_trust|grantor_retained_annuity_trust|qualified_personal_residence_trust|dynasty_trust|spendthrift_trust — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this trust record was last modified in the data platform.']

  Note: 'Master record for trust entities that own life insurance policies — Irrevocable Life Insurance Trusts (ILITs), revocable living trusts, and testamentary trusts. Captures trust name, type, trustee, grantor, tax ID, and trust date.'
}

Table policyholder.death_verification {
  death_verification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the death verification record. Primary key.']
  aml_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_case. Business justification: Death claims are high-risk for fraud and money laundering. AML teams investigate suspicious death verifications (e.g., rapid policy changes before death, beneficiary red flags) requiring formal case l']
  annuitant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.annuitant. Business justification: Death verification is equally applicable to annuitants as to insureds — an annuitant\'s death triggers annuity payout processing. The death_verification table already has insured_id for life insurance ']
  claim_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the death benefit claim record created from this verification, if applicable.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Annuity death benefit processing requires linking the verified death event directly to the annuity contract to initiate death benefit calculation, payout phase transition, and state unclaimed property']
  death_certificate_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Claims processing and regulatory compliance require direct retrieval of the death certificate document from the death_verification record. death_verification tracks death_certificate_received_flag and']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policy associated with the deceased insured.']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: Death verification tracks the death of insured individuals. Currently death_verification.party_id links to the party entity, but does not link to the specific insured role record. Adding insured_id FK']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party (insured individual) whose death is being verified.']
  reverification_death_verification_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on death_verification (reverification_death_verification_id)']
  aps_received_date date [note: 'Date when the APS was received by the insurance company.']
  aps_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an Attending Physician Statement was obtained to verify death and cause.']
  beneficiary_notification_date date [note: 'Date when beneficiaries were notified of the insured death and their right to file a claim.']
