  beneficiary_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the designated beneficiaries have been notified of the death and claim process.']
  cause_of_death varchar [note: 'Primary cause of death as documented on the death certificate or medical records.']
  claim_initiated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a death benefit claim has been initiated as a result of this verification.']
  coroner_report_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a coroner or medical examiner report was obtained, typically for accidental or suspicious deaths.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this death verification record was first created in the system.']
  death_certificate_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the death certificate was officially issued by the vital records authority.']
  death_certificate_issuing_state varchar [note: 'US state or jurisdiction that issued the death certificate.']
  death_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official certificate number from the issuing vital records authority.']
  death_certificate_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an official death certificate has been received and reviewed.']
  death_date date [note: 'Reported or verified date of death of the insured individual.']
  death_date_source varchar [note: 'Source document or system that provided the death date information. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: death_certificate|dmf|obituary|family_report|physician|coroner|hospital|funeral_home — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dmf_match_date date [note: 'Date when the DMF match was identified during proactive screening.']
  dmf_match_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the insured was found in the Social Security Death Master File.']
  dmf_match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence score of the DMF match based on SSN, name, and date of birth matching algorithms, expressed as a percentage.']
  manner_of_death varchar [note: 'Classification of how the death occurred, used for underwriting and claims adjudication purposes.']
  obituary_publication_date date [note: 'Date when the obituary was published or posted.']
  obituary_source varchar [note: 'Publication or online source where the obituary was found (e.g., newspaper name, funeral home website).']
  obituary_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an obituary notice was found and verified as part of the death verification process.']
  place_of_death varchar [note: 'Location where the death occurred (e.g., hospital, home, nursing facility, hospice).']
  place_of_death_city varchar [note: 'City where the death occurred.']
  place_of_death_country varchar [note: 'Country where the death occurred, using 3-letter ISO country code.']
  place_of_death_state varchar [note: 'State or jurisdiction where the death occurred.']
  proactive_search_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this death was discovered through proactive DMF screening rather than beneficiary notification.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system that originated this death verification record.']
  ssn varchar [note: 'Social Security Number of the deceased insured, used for DMF matching and verification.']
  third_party_verification_service varchar [note: 'Name of the external service provider used for death verification (e.g., Lexis Nexis, Accurint, Veris).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this death verification record was last modified.']
  verification_confidence_level varchar [note: 'Overall confidence level in the death verification based on quality and quantity of evidence obtained.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the death was officially verified by the insurance company.']
  verification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the verification process, evidence reviewed, and any special circumstances.']
  verification_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the death verification case, used for tracking and correspondence.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the death verification process in its lifecycle.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the death verification was completed, including time zone information.']
  verification_type varchar [note: 'Primary method or source used to verify the death of the insured individual. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dmf_match|death_certificate|obituary|physician_statement|coroner_report|family_notification|third_party_service — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Tracks death verification processes for insured individuals including Social Security Death Master File (DMF) matches, death certificate verification, and obituary confirmation. Required for proactive death benefit claims processing.'
}

Table policy.in_force_policy {
  in_force_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system identifier for the in-force policy record. Primary key for the in-force policy master table.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing account that manages premium collection for this policy. Multiple policies may share a single billing account (list billing).']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Every issued policy originates from an underwriting application. Policy admin systems must trace back to the originating application for contestability period tracking, replacement regulation complian']
  cohort_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.cohort_definition. Business justification: LDTI (ASC 944) and IFRS17 require every in-force policy to be assigned to a measurement cohort for LFPB/CSM calculation and DAC amortization. Actuaries and finance teams depend on this link for quarte']
  coi_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.coi_rate_schedule. Business justification: UL/VUL/IUL in-force policies require a direct reference to the COI rate schedule applied for monthly deduction calculations. This link enables COI charge audit trails, rate action impact analysis on t']
  form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.form. Business justification: in_force_policy carries policy_form_number as a plain text column — a denormalized reference to product.form. Linking to form enables regulatory compliance verification that the correct approved form ']
  hierarchy_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.hierarchy_node. Business justification: Life insurance carriers assign each policy to a distribution hierarchy node (territory/region/branch) for override commission roll-up, production reporting, and market conduct analysis. A domain exper']
  ifrs17_contract_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ifrs17_contract_group. Business justification: IFRS 17 requires each insurance contract to be assigned to a contract group for measurement (GMM, PAA, or VFA). Direct FK from in_force_policy to ifrs17_contract_group is a fundamental IFRS 17 operati']
  jurisdiction_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_license. Business justification: Life insurance policies may only be issued in jurisdictions where the company holds a valid license. Market conduct exams and regulatory audits verify that each policy was issued in a licensed jurisdi']
  pbr_model_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.pbr_model_segment. Business justification: NAIC VM-20/VM-21 Principle-Based Reserving requires each policy to be assigned to a PBR model segment that governs reserve methodology, credibility weighting, and stochastic scenario application. Actu']
  policy_form_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval. Business justification: Every life insurance policy must be issued on a DOI-approved form. Market conduct exams verify that each in-force policy was issued on an approved form for its jurisdiction. policy_form_number is a de']
  pricing_basis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Every policy is issued under a specific pricing basis defining mortality tables, expense loadings, and profit margins. Underwriting and product teams reference pricing_basis for rate guarantees and re']
  primary_in_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the insurance product definition that governs this policy contract.']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed agent or producer who sold this policy and is entitled to first-year and renewal commissions.']
  risk_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: The in_force_policy carries denormalized `risk_class` and `underwriting_class` text columns that are authoritative data owned by underwriting.risk_class. A FK enforces referential integrity, drives CO']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.schedule. Business justification: At policy issue, carriers assign a specific commission schedule to lock in renewal commission rates and chargeback periods for the policy\'s lifetime. Producer compensation audits and renewal commissio']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Every life insurance policy is governed by the state regulation of its issuing jurisdiction. Market conduct exams and compliance audits require tracing each policy to its governing state regulation fo']
  underwriting_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: in_force_policy carries underwriting_class as a plain text column. Linking to the underwriting_class entity enables experience studies by class, reserve adequacy reporting, and reinsurance cession dec']
  variable_separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable life and variable annuity policies are legally and regulatorily required to identify their backing separate account (SEC registration, prospectus delivery, NAIC separate account reporting). R']
  writing_agency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.agency. Business justification: Policies are placed through agencies, not just individual producers. Carriers track the writing agency for agency-level commission statements, E&O coverage verification at issue, agency production rep']
  writing_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.appointment. Business justification: State DOI regulations require carriers to verify the writing producer held an active appointment in the state of issue at policy issuance. Linking the policy to the specific appointment record support']
  account_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current accumulated value in the policy account, representing premiums paid plus interest credited minus cost of insurance and expense charges. Applicable to universal life and annuity products.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this policy record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  csv_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount the policy owner would receive if the policy were surrendered today, net of surrender charges and policy loans. This is the guaranteed minimum non-forfeiture value.']
  death_benefit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current total death benefit payable upon death of the insured, including face amount plus any accumulated account value, riders, and adjustments. This is the actual amount payable to beneficiaries.']
  death_benefit_option varchar [note: 'The death benefit structure elected by the policy owner. OPTION_A=level death benefit equal to face amount, OPTION_B=face amount plus account value, OPTION_C=increasing death benefit corridor, ROP=return of premium.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date coverage begins under the policy contract. May differ from issue date due to backdating or delayed effective date elections.']
  face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The base death benefit amount stated on the policy face page, before any adjustments for riders, policy loans, or accumulated value. Also known as sum insured or coverage amount.']
  gpt_cvat_election varchar [note: 'The IRC Section 7702 test elected at policy issue to determine life insurance qualification. GPT=Guideline Premium Test with guideline single and level premium limits, CVAT=Cash Value Accumulation Test with cash value corridor requirements.']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party whose life is insured under this policy contract. Links to the policyholder domain party master.']
  irc_7702_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy meets IRC Section 7702 definition of life insurance for federal tax purposes. True=compliant and death benefit is tax-free, False=non-compliant and may lose tax advantages.']
  issue_age varchar [note: 'The age of the insured at the time the policy was issued, calculated using the insurance age method (age nearest birthday or age last birthday per company practice). Used for premium rating and underwriting class assignment.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date the policy was officially issued and became a binding contract. This is the principal business event timestamp for the policy lifecycle.']
  jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'The two-letter US state code where the policy was issued and is subject to regulatory oversight. Determines applicable insurance laws and regulations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this policy record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking and incremental processing.']
  maturity_date date [note: 'The date the policy contract reaches maturity and the death benefit becomes payable as an endowment. Nullable for term policies and annuities without fixed maturity.']
  mec_status_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy has been classified as a Modified Endowment Contract under IRC Section 7702A due to premium payments exceeding the 7-pay test. True=MEC status, distributions subject to LIFO tax treatment and 10% penalty.']
  modal_premium decimal(18,2) [note: 'The scheduled premium amount due per billing mode (monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual). Does not include additional premium payments or adjustments.']
  paid_to_date date [note: 'The date through which premiums have been paid and coverage is guaranteed. Used to determine grace period expiration and lapse date.']
  policy_currency varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all policy values, premiums, and benefits are denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).']
  policy_loan_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current outstanding principal balance of all policy loans taken against the cash value. Reduces the death benefit and cash surrender value. Null or zero if no loans outstanding.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier assigned to the policy contract at issuance. This is the externally-known policy number used across all customer communications, billing statements, and claims processing.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the policy contract. ACTIVE=in-force and premium-paying, LAPSED=terminated due to non-payment, SURRENDERED=voluntarily terminated by owner, MATURED=reached maturity date, DEATH_CLAIM=claim filed on insured death, FREE_LOOK=within free-look cancellation period, GRACE_PERIOD=premium overdue but within grace period. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ACTIVE|LAPSED|SURRENDERED|MATURED|DEATH_CLAIM|FREE_LOOK|GRACE_PERIOD — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  premium_mode varchar [note: 'The frequency at which premiums are scheduled to be paid. SINGLE indicates a single premium paid at issue with no recurring payments.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the insurance product. WL=Whole Life, UL=Universal Life, IUL=Indexed Universal Life, VUL=Variable Universal Life, YRT=Yearly Renewable Term, TERM=Term Life, FIA=Fixed Indexed Annuity, SPIA=Single Premium Immediate Annuity, MYGA=Multi-Year Guaranteed Annuity, DIA=Deferred Income Annuity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WL|UL|IUL|VUL|YRT|TERM|FIA|SPIA|MYGA|DIA|VARIABLE_ANNUITY — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reinsurance_cession_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any portion of the policy risk has been ceded to a reinsurer under a reinsurance treaty. True=reinsurance in place, False=100% retained risk.']
  table_rating varchar [note: 'Additional mortality rating applied to substandard risks, expressed as a letter (A-P) or numeric percentage (e.g., 150 for 150% of standard mortality). Null for standard and preferred risks.']
  tax_qualification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the policy is held within a tax-qualified retirement plan. QUALIFIED=held in employer-sponsored or individual retirement account, NON_QUALIFIED=personal after-tax policy. Specific plan types include IRA, Roth IRA, 401(k), 403(b), 457, TSP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: QUALIFIED|NON_QUALIFIED|IRA|ROTH_IRA|401K|403B|457|TSP — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date the policy contract was terminated, lapsed, surrendered, or otherwise ceased to be in force. Null for active policies.']

  Note: 'Core master record and SSOT for every life insurance and annuity contract administered in the Policy Administration System. Stores policy number (unique business key), product type (WL, UL, IUL, VUL, YRT, FIA, SPIA, MYGA, DIA), issue date, effective date, policy status (active, lapsed, surrendered, matured, death claim, free-look, grace period, pending, rescinded, converted), face amount, death benefit (DB) type (Option A/B/C) and amount, issue age, risk class (preferred plus, preferred, standard, substandard with table rating), underwriting class, modal premium, policy currency, jurisdiction state, IRC 7702 compliance flag, MEC status flag, GPT/CVAT election, tax qualification status (qualified, non-qualified), policy form number, ACORD product code, reinsurance cession flag, and system-of-record identifiers. Links to policyholder domain for insured/owner/annuitant party references. All other policy domain entities reference this master record.'
}

Table policy.value {
  value_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the policy value record. Primary key for the policy value snapshot.']
  crediting_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.crediting_strategy. Business justification: Policy value records track index_credit_amount and interest_credited_amount computed per crediting strategy. Actuarial valuation, in-force illustration, and regulatory reporting require an audit trail']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent policy contract for which these values are calculated.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Variable product valuations require portfolio reference to calculate account value, death benefit, and surrender charges. Daily valuation runs apply portfolio unit values to policy units. Essential fo']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable policy account value (av_amount, fund_allocation_total, index_credit_amount) is directly derived from the separate account\'s NAV and unit values. Daily policy valuation, statutory reserve cal']
  unit_value_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.unit_value. Business justification: Variable policy account value (av_amount), interest_credited_amount, and index_credit_amount are computed directly from unit values. Daily policy valuation processing, policyholder statements, and FIN']
  valuation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.valuation. Business justification: Variable product policy values are calculated by applying investment valuations (unit values) from separate accounts. Links policy accounting to investment accounting for daily unit value application,']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the actuarial valuation run that produced these policy values. Links to the actuarial valuation system for audit and reconciliation.']
  accumulated_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative sum of all premiums paid into the policy from inception through the valuation date. Used for MEC testing and tax compliance.']
  admin_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The administrative or policy fee deducted from the account value during the most recent billing cycle prior to the valuation date.']
  av_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross account value of the policy before any surrender charges or loan deductions. Represents accumulated premiums plus interest credits less cost of insurance and fees.']
  coi_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost of insurance charge deducted from the account value during the most recent billing cycle prior to the valuation date.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this policy value record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  csv_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cash surrender value available to the policyholder if the policy is surrendered on the valuation date. Represents account value less surrender charges and outstanding loans.']
  cvat_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy is in compliance with the Cash Value Accumulation Test under IRC Section 7702. True if compliant, False if failed. Alternative to GPT for tax qualification.']
  dac_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The unamortized deferred acquisition cost balance allocated to this policy as of the valuation date. Represents capitalized sales and underwriting expenses under GAAP.']
  death_benefit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total death benefit payable to beneficiaries if the insured dies on the valuation date. Includes base face amount plus any riders and accumulated values per policy design.']
  dividend_accumulation_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The balance of policyholder dividends left on deposit with the insurer, earning interest. Available for withdrawal or to pay premiums.']
  fund_allocation_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total value of all sub-account or fund allocations for variable or indexed products. Should reconcile to account value for variable universal life (VUL) policies.']
  gpt_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy is in compliance with the Guideline Premium Test under IRC Section 7702. True if compliant, False if failed. Critical for tax-qualified life insurance status.']
  guaranteed_minimum_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The guaranteed minimum cash value or account value as of the valuation date, per policy guarantees. Cannot fall below this floor regardless of performance.']
  index_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The index-linked interest credit applied to indexed universal life (IUL) or fixed indexed annuity (FIA) accounts during the most recent crediting cycle.']
  interest_credited_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The interest credited to the account value during the most recent crediting cycle prior to the valuation date. May include guaranteed and excess interest.']
  last_premium_payment_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent premium payment received prior to or on the valuation date. Used for lapse monitoring and grace period calculations.']
  loan_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The outstanding principal balance of all policy loans as of the valuation date. Reduces cash surrender value and may affect death benefit.']
  loan_interest_accrued_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The accrued but unpaid interest on outstanding policy loans as of the valuation date. Typically capitalized into loan balance if unpaid.']
  mec_status_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy has been classified as a Modified Endowment Contract under IRC Section 7702A. True if MEC, False if not. Affects tax treatment of withdrawals.']
  nar_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount at risk to the insurer, calculated as death benefit minus account value. Critical for risk-based capital calculations and reinsurance cession.']
  next_billing_date date [note: 'The next scheduled date on which premium is due or policy charges will be deducted. Critical for billing system integration and lapse prevention.']
  paid_up_additions_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The accumulated value of paid-up additional insurance purchased with dividends or excess premiums. Increases both cash value and death benefit.']
  policy_anniversary_date date [note: 'The annual anniversary date of the policy, used for age changes, rate adjustments, and anniversary-based policy actions.']
  premium_load_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The sales load or premium charge deducted from premiums received during the most recent billing cycle. Represents acquisition cost recovery.']
  premium_ytd_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative amount of premiums received during the current calendar year through the valuation date. Used for MEC testing and premium tracking.']
  reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actuarial reserve held by the insurer for this policy as of the valuation date. Calculated per statutory or GAAP requirements depending on valuation basis.']
  rider_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total charges for all active policy riders (ADB, AWL, LTC, etc.) deducted during the most recent billing cycle prior to the valuation date.']
  surrender_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The surrender charge that would be assessed if the policy is surrendered on the valuation date. Typically declines over time per the policy schedule.']
  surrender_charge_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the surrender charge period expires and no further surrender charges will be assessed. Null if surrender charges have already expired.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this policy value record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  valuation_basis_code varchar [note: 'The accounting or regulatory basis under which these policy values were calculated. STAT=Statutory (SAP), GAAP=US GAAP, IFRS=IFRS 17, TAX=Tax basis.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The as-of date for which all policy values in this record are calculated. Critical for point-in-time financial reporting and actuarial analysis.']
  withdrawal_ytd_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative amount of partial withdrawals taken from the policy during the current calendar year through the valuation date. Used for tax reporting and withdrawal limit tracking.']

  Note: 'Tracks all policy-level financial values as of a given valuation date. Owns CSV (Cash Surrender Value), AV (Account Value), net death benefit, NAR (Net Amount at Risk), accumulated premiums, loan balance, loan interest accrued, surrender charge amount, surrender charge period end date, paid-up additions value, dividend accumulation balance, and policy fund allocation totals. Updated on each premium posting, withdrawal, loan, or monthly deduction cycle. Critical for ALM, actuarial reserving, and policyholder self-service.'
}

Table policy.rider {
  rider_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system identifier for the rider record. Primary key.']
  endorsement_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Rider additions generate endorsement documents that are legal amendments to the base policy contract, required for state DOI filing and policyholder delivery. Direct FK links the rider record to its e']
  mortality_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: Mortality-sensitive riders (term, ADB, guaranteed insurability) use rider-specific mortality tables that may differ from the base policy table. Actuarial reserve calculations and COI rate setting for ']
  policy_form_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval. Business justification: Riders must be issued on DOI-approved forms, just like base policies. Market conduct exams review whether riders were issued on approved forms in each jurisdiction. acord_form_number on policy_rider i']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Variable riders (GMDB, GMWB, GMIB) reference separate account portfolios for benefit base calculations, guaranteed benefit valuations, hedge effectiveness testing, and actuarial reserve calculations. ']
  pricing_basis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Riders (LTC, ADB, waiver of premium, term) have distinct pricing bases with separate COI rates, benefit pricing, and expense loadings. Actuarial pricing and rider reserve calculations require the ride']
  primary_policy_in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the base policy contract to which this rider is attached. A policy may have multiple riders.']
  rider_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.rider_definition. Business justification: Riders on in-force policies must reference their product definition (rider_definition) for benefit structure, premium rates, underwriting class rules, form numbers, and compliance parameters. Essentia']
  risk_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: Riders (waiver of premium, LTC, disability income) carry their own underwriting risk classification separate from the base policy. The policy_rider has a denormalized `underwriting_class` text column.']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable riders (GMDB, GMWB, GMIB) calculate charges and guarantee bases against the separate account\'s NAV. Rider reserve calculations, hedge effectiveness testing, and NAIC AG43/VM-21 reserve report']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: Rider additions, modifications, and terminations are post-issue servicing transactions processed through service requests (request_type=\'RIDER_ADD\', \'RIDER_TERMINATE\', \'RIDER_MODIFY\'). The service_req']
  treaty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance treaty under which this rider is ceded. Null if rider is not reinsured.']
  underwriting_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: Riders (DI, LTC) carry independent underwriting classes separate from the base policy. Linking policy_rider to underwriting_class enables rider-specific experience studies, independent reinsurance ces']
  attained_age varchar [note: 'The current age of the insured. Used for age-based premium adjustments and benefit calculations. Expressed in years.']
  benefit_period_months varchar [note: 'The maximum duration (in months) for which benefits will be paid once triggered. Common for disability income and long-term care riders. Null if benefit is a lump sum or lifetime benefit.']
  benefit_trigger_condition varchar [note: 'The specific condition or event that must occur for the rider benefit to be payable. Examples: accidental death, disability, critical illness diagnosis, inability to perform ADLs (Activities of Daily Living), account value depletion, etc.']
  cash_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The accumulated cash value or account value associated with this rider, if applicable. Some riders (e.g., paid-up additions riders) accumulate cash value. Null for term riders or pure protection riders. Expressed in policy currency.']
  cost_of_insurance_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The per-unit cost of insurance rate applied to the rider face amount to calculate the rider charge. Common for universal life and variable universal life riders. Expressed as a rate per thousand of coverage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rider record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when this specific rider coverage became effective on the policy. May differ from policy issue date if rider was added post-issue.']
  elimination_period_days varchar [note: 'The waiting period (in days) from the date of a qualifying event until benefits begin to be paid. Common for disability income and long-term care riders. Zero or null if no elimination period applies.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the rider coverage ends or matures. Nullable for riders with lifetime coverage or those tied to base policy duration.']
  face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The benefit amount for this specific rider attachment. For ADB riders, this is the accidental death benefit amount. For term riders, this is the additional death benefit. Amount is specific to this policy-rider combination.']
  guaranteed_insurability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rider includes a guaranteed insurability option, allowing the policyholder to increase coverage at specified intervals without additional underwriting. True if option is included, False otherwise.']
  issue_age varchar [note: 'The age of the insured at the time the rider was issued. Used for premium calculation and benefit determination. Expressed in years.']
  loan_outstanding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The outstanding loan amount taken against this riders cash value, if loans are permitted. Expressed in policy currency. Null or zero if no loan exists.']
  premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The premium charged for this specific rider on this policy, calculated based on rider premium_basis, insured age, risk class, and face amount. This is the actual premium for this policy-rider instance.']
  premium_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the rider premium is charged. Annual: once per year. Semi-annual: twice per year. Quarterly: four times per year. Monthly: twelve times per year. Single: one-time premium payment.']
  premium_mode varchar [note: 'Pattern of premium payments over the rider term. Level: constant premium. Increasing: premium increases over time. Decreasing: premium decreases over time. Flexible: premium varies based on policyholder elections or account performance.']
  rating_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional premium percentage applied for substandard underwriting risk. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 25.00 means 25% additional premium). Null or zero for standard and preferred classes.']
  reinsurance_ceded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the risk for this rider has been ceded to a reinsurer. True if reinsured, False if retained. Used for risk management and financial reporting.']
  rider_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rider. Active: rider is in force and providing coverage. Suspended: temporarily not in force. Terminated: rider has been cancelled. Lapsed: rider has lapsed due to non-payment. Pending: rider application is under review. Matured: rider has reached its maturity date.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the operational system from which this rider record originated (e.g., FAST, Sapiens LifePro, Oracle Insurance Policy Administration). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  state_of_issue varchar [note: 'The two-letter US state code where the rider was issued. Determines applicable state insurance regulations and tax treatment.']
  surrender_charge decimal(18,2) [note: 'The penalty or charge applied if the rider is surrendered or terminated early. Common for riders with cash value or guaranteed benefits. Expressed in policy currency. Null if no surrender charge applies.']
  tax_qualified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rider is part of a tax-qualified plan (e.g., 401k, IRA, 403b). True if tax-qualified, False otherwise. Affects tax treatment of premiums and benefits.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date when this rider coverage was terminated, either due to rider expiry age, policy termination, or voluntary removal by policy owner.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rider record was last modified in the data platform. Used for audit trail and change tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  version varchar [note: 'The version or edition of the rider form. Used to track changes in rider terms, benefits, or pricing over time. Important for compliance and benefit administration.']
  waiver_of_premium_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rider includes a waiver of premium benefit, meaning rider premiums are waived if the insured becomes disabled or meets other qualifying conditions. True if waiver applies, False otherwise.']
  withdrawal_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum annual withdrawal percentage allowed under a GMWB (Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit) rider without penalty. Expressed as a percentage of the benefit base (e.g., 5.00 means 5% per year).']

  Note: 'Master record for every rider attached to a base policy. Covers all rider types: ADB (Accidental Death Benefit), AWL (Automatic Waiver of Premium), CAR (Company Action Rider), LTC (Long-Term Care), GMDB, GMIB, GMWB, GMAB, DI (Disability Income), child term, spouse term, and return-of-premium riders. Stores rider code, rider description, rider face amount, rider premium, rider effective date, rider expiry date, rider status, benefit trigger conditions, elimination period, benefit period, and ACORD rider form number. One policy may have multiple riders.'
}

Table policy.status_history {
  status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each policy status transition record. Primary key for the policy status history audit log.']
  grace_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.grace_period. Business justification: State insurance regulators require traceability from a policy status transition (e.g., grace, lapse) to the specific billing grace period event that triggered it. This FK enables the regulatory audit ']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the policy contract that experienced this status transition. Links to the policy master record.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Grace period notices, lapse notifications, and reinstatement eligibility communications are mandated by specific state regulatory obligations. status_history has regulatory_reporting_flag indicating r']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Grace period lengths, free-look cancellation windows, and lapse notification requirements are state-regulated. status_history tracks these events and has state_jurisdiction_code as a denormalized refe']
  status_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Policy status changes (lapse, termination) trigger reserve release journal entries. Finance teams reconcile reserve releases to specific lapse events for statutory reserve adequacy reporting and LDTI ']
  status_notice_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Policy status changes (lapse, grace period, reinstatement eligibility) trigger mandatory regulatory notices (grace period notices, lapse notices) required by state insurance codes. Direct FK links the']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers to document special circumstances, regulatory considerations, or audit notes related to this status transition.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The business-effective date when the new status became active. This is the date used for policy valuation, billing, and regulatory reporting. May differ from processed timestamp.']
  free_look_cancellation_request_date date [note: 'Date when the policyholder requested cancellation during the free-look period. Null if no free-look cancellation occurred.']
  free_look_period_end_date date [note: 'Date when the free-look period expires. Typically 10-30 days after start date per state DOI requirements. Policyholder may cancel without penalty before this date.']
  free_look_period_start_date date [note: 'Date when the free-look period began. Typically starts on policy delivery or issue date. Null if not applicable to this transition.']
  free_look_refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount refunded to the policyholder upon free-look cancellation. Typically includes all premiums paid minus any policy charges per state regulations.']
  free_look_refund_disbursement_date date [note: 'Date when the free-look refund was actually disbursed to the policyholder. Null if no refund occurred.']
  free_look_refund_method varchar [note: 'Method used to refund the premium during free-look cancellation. Null if no refund occurred.']
  lapse_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the policy officially lapsed due to non-payment after grace period expiry. Null if policy did not lapse.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The policy status after this transition. Captures the to-state in the status lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|pending|in_force|lapsed|grace_period|suspended|terminated|surrendered|matured|death_claim|free_look|rescinded|converted|reinstated|not_taken — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  overdue_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total premium amount that is overdue and triggered the grace period or lapse. Null if transition is not payment-related.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier of the policy contract. Denormalized for reporting convenience and audit trail completeness.']
  prior_status varchar [note: 'The policy status immediately before this transition occurred. Captures the from-state in the status lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|pending|in_force|lapsed|grace_period|suspended|terminated|surrendered|matured|death_claim|free_look|rescinded|converted|reinstated|not_taken — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the status transition was recorded in the policy administration system. Captures the exact moment of system processing.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition must be included in regulatory reporting to state DOI or NAIC. True if reportable, False otherwise.']
  reinstatement_eligibility_expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the policy is no longer eligible for reinstatement. Typically 3-5 years after lapse date per policy terms and state regulations.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition was later reversed or corrected. True if reversed, False otherwise. Used for audit trail integrity.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why this status transition was reversed. Null if no reversal occurred.']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this status transition was reversed. Null if no reversal occurred.']
  source_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the upstream transaction that triggered this status transition. Examples: billing transaction ID, claim ID, underwriting decision ID. Enables traceability to originating event.']
  source_transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of transaction that triggered this status transition. Provides context for the originating business event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: premium_payment|claim_submission|underwriting_decision|policy_amendment|reinstatement_application|surrender_request|maturity_event|death_notification|free_look_cancellation|system_batch — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transition_approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition required supervisory or underwriting approval. True if approval was required, False otherwise.']
  transition_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the status transition was approved. Null if no approval was required or if pending approval.']
  transition_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the status transition occurred. Examples: lapse for non-payment, reinstatement, surrender, death claim, maturity, conversion, rescission, free-look cancellation, grace period expiry, premium payment received, underwriting approval, policy issued.']
  transition_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the reason for the status transition. Provides business context beyond the reason code.']
  transition_triggered_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system that triggered the status transition. Examples: Policy Administration System, Billing System, Claims System, Underwriting Workbench, Manual Entry.']
  transition_triggered_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who initiated the status transition. Denormalized for audit trail completeness. Null if transition was system-automated.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit log of every policy status transition throughout the full policy lifecycle. SSOT for grace period tracking, free-look period management, and all status-driven compliance events. Records prior status, new status, transition reason code (lapse for non-payment, reinstatement, surrender, death claim, maturity, conversion, rescission, free-look cancellation, grace period expiry), effective date, processed timestamp, grace period start date, grace period end date, grace period length in days, overdue premium amount, grace period notice sent date, grace period status (active, expired, cured), lapse effective date (if grace expired without payment), free-look period start date, free-look period end date, free-look cancellation request date, free-look refund amount, free-look refund method, free-look refund disbursement date, reinstatement eligibility expiry date, and the system or user that triggered the transition. Supports regulatory reporting, lapse studies, state DOI grace period compliance (30-61 day minimums), free-look cancellation processing (10-30 day state DOI requirements), and actuarial experience analysis.'
}

Table policy.policy_beneficiary {
  policy_beneficiary_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the policy beneficiary record. Primary key for the policy beneficiary entity.']
  designation_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Beneficiary designation changes require executed designation forms as legal documents governing death benefit distribution. Direct FK links the beneficiary record to its governing designation document']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance policy or annuity contract to which this beneficiary designation applies.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policy servicing change request that created or modified this beneficiary designation. Links to the policy change history for audit trail purposes. Null for original designations at policy issue.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the beneficiary. Used for mailing death benefit checks, tax forms (1099-R), and beneficiary correspondence.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for apartment, suite, or unit number. Optional field for additional address details.']
  beneficiary_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the beneficiary designation indicating the order of precedence and revocability. Primary beneficiaries receive proceeds first; contingent beneficiaries receive proceeds if primary beneficiaries predecease the insured; tertiary beneficiaries are third in line; irrevocable beneficiaries cannot be changed without their consent; final beneficiaries receive any remaining proceeds.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the beneficiary address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the beneficiary address (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Used for international beneficiary management and tax treaty compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this beneficiary record was first created in the policy administration system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the individual beneficiary. Used for age verification, minor beneficiary identification, and Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) calculations for inherited annuities. Null for trust or entity beneficiaries.']
  designation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount designated to this beneficiary, used when the beneficiary receives a specific amount rather than a percentage. Null if percentage-based designation is used.']
  designation_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this beneficiary designation became effective. Corresponds to the policy issue date for original designations or the change request approval date for subsequent changes.']
  designation_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this beneficiary designation was terminated or superseded by a new designation. Null for currently active beneficiary designations.']
  designation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the death benefit (DB) or annuity proceeds allocated to this beneficiary. Must sum to 100.00 within each beneficiary type class for a given policy. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 50.00 for 50%).']
  designation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the beneficiary designation. Active designations are in force; superseded designations have been replaced by newer designations; deceased indicates the beneficiary predeceased the insured; removed indicates the beneficiary was explicitly removed; pending indicates a change request is in process.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Email address for electronic communication with the beneficiary. Used for beneficiary notifications, digital document delivery, and claims correspondence.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the beneficiary as an individual person, trust, estate, charitable organization, or business entity. Determines tax treatment and distribution rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: individual|trust|estate|charity|corporation|partnership|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  first_name varchar [note: 'First or given name of the individual beneficiary. Null for trust or entity beneficiaries.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'The complete legal name of the beneficiary as it should appear on death benefit or annuity payout documentation. For trust beneficiaries, this is the full legal name of the trust.']
  guardian_name varchar [note: 'Name of the legal guardian or custodian designated to manage death benefit proceeds on behalf of a minor beneficiary until the minor reaches the age of majority. Required when is_minor is True.']
  guardian_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the guardian or custodian to the minor beneficiary. Used for legal verification and compliance with state minor beneficiary laws.']
  is_minor boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the beneficiary is a minor (under age 18 or state-specific age of majority). True if minor, False if adult. Determines whether guardian or custodian designation is required.']
  is_revocable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the beneficiary designation can be changed by the policy owner without the beneficiary consent. True if revocable (can be changed), False if irrevocable (requires beneficiary consent to change).']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Last name or surname of the individual beneficiary. Null for trust or entity beneficiaries.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the individual beneficiary. Null for trust or entity beneficiaries.']
  per_stirpes_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the beneficiary share should be distributed per stirpes (by branch) if the beneficiary predeceases the insured. True means the beneficiary share passes to their descendants; False means the share is redistributed among surviving beneficiaries of the same class.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the beneficiary. Used for beneficiary outreach during claims processing and death benefit distribution.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the beneficiary address. Used for mailing and geographic analysis.']
  relationship_to_insured varchar [note: 'The familial, legal, or business relationship between the beneficiary and the insured or annuitant. Used for underwriting, tax reporting, and regulatory compliance purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spouse|child|parent|sibling|grandchild|other_relative|trust|estate|charity|business_entity|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Ordering number within the beneficiary type class to establish precedence when multiple beneficiaries of the same type exist.']
  settlement_option varchar [note: 'The method by which the death benefit or annuity proceeds will be paid to the beneficiary. Lump sum is a single payment; installment is periodic payments over time; life income provides payments for the beneficiary lifetime; interest only holds proceeds and pays interest.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the policy administration system or source application from which this beneficiary record originated (e.g., FAST, Sapiens LifePro, Oracle Insurance Policy Administration). Used for data lineage and integration tracking.']
  ssn varchar [note: 'Social Security Number of the individual beneficiary. Stored in masked or encrypted format. Required for tax reporting (Form 1099-R) upon distribution of death benefits or annuity proceeds. Null for non-US beneficiaries.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the beneficiary address. Two-letter state code for US addresses (e.g., CA, NY, TX).']
  suffix varchar [note: 'Generational or professional suffix appended to the beneficiary name (e.g., Jr., Sr., II, III).']
  tin varchar [note: 'Taxpayer Identification Number for trust, estate, or entity beneficiaries. Used for IRS tax reporting when the beneficiary is not an individual. Stored in masked or encrypted format.']
  trust_date_established date [note: 'Date the trust was legally established. Used for trust qualification verification and estate planning documentation. Null for non-trust beneficiaries.']
  trust_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the trust when the beneficiary is an Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust (ILIT) or other trust entity. Null for individual beneficiaries.']
  trustee_name varchar [note: 'Name of the trustee responsible for administering the trust and receiving death benefit proceeds on behalf of trust beneficiaries. Null for non-trust beneficiaries.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this beneficiary record was last modified in the policy administration system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record for all primary and contingent beneficiaries designated on a life insurance policy or annuity contract. Stores beneficiary name, relationship to insured, beneficiary type (primary, contingent, tertiary, irrevocable), designation percentage, TIN/SSN (masked), date of birth, address, effective date of designation, revocable flag, trust name (for ILIT designations), minor guardian details, and beneficiary change history reference. Owned by the policy domain as a direct contractual attribute of the policy.'
}

Table policy.owner {
  owner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the policy ownership record. Primary key for the policy owner association entity.']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance or annuity policy contract that is owned. Links to the policy master record.']
  owner_collateral_assignee_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party (typically a lender or financial institution) who holds the collateral assignment interest in the policy. Null if collateral_assignment_flag is False.']
  owner_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal owner party (individual, trust, corporation, or other entity) who holds ownership rights to the policy. Links to the party master record.']
  owner_poa_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party who holds power of attorney authority to act on behalf of the owner. Null if power_of_attorney_flag is False.']
  ownership_transfer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policy servicing transaction that created or modified this ownership record. Links to the transaction audit trail for ownership changes.']
  party_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary mailing address for the policy owner. Used for correspondence, premium notices, and regulatory communications. Links to the address master record.']
  absolute_assignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy has been absolutely assigned (full transfer of all ownership rights) versus collateral assignment (partial rights for security). True = absolute assignment; False = not absolutely assigned. Absolute assignment typically results in ownership transfer.']
  collateral_assignment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount of the loan or obligation secured by the collateral assignment. Used to determine assignee priority claim on death benefit and CSV. Null if collateral_assignment_flag is False.']
  collateral_assignment_date date [note: 'Date when the collateral assignment became effective. Null if collateral_assignment_flag is False.']
  collateral_assignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy has been assigned as collateral for a loan or other financial obligation. True = policy is assigned as collateral; False = no collateral assignment. Affects death benefit and CSV distribution rights.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date when the owner provided written consent to the ownership arrangement. Required for certain ownership structures (e.g., employer-owned life insurance requires employee consent per IRC Section 101(j)).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this policy ownership record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this ownership relationship became legally effective. For original policy issuance, this is the policy issue date. For ownership transfers, this is the transfer effective date.']
  insurable_interest_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether insurable interest between the owner and insured was verified at policy issuance or ownership transfer. True = insurable interest documented and verified; False = not verified. Required for STOLI prevention and regulatory compliance.']
  kyc_verification_date date [note: 'Date when KYC and AML verification was completed for the policy owner. Null if kyc_verification_status is pending or not_required.']
  kyc_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of KYC and AML verification for the policy owner. Verified = identity and AML checks passed; Pending = verification in progress; Failed = verification failed; Not Required = KYC not required for this owner type. Required for AML compliance and FinCEN reporting.']
  notice_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred method for delivering policy notices, statements, and communications to the owner. Mail = postal mail; Email = electronic delivery; Portal = online customer portal; Agent = through agent/producer.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ownership interest held by this owner in the policy. For sole ownership = 100.00; for co-ownership scenarios, sum of all owner percentages must equal 100.00. Used for CSV and AV distribution calculations.']
  ownership_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source policy administration system or platform that originated this ownership record (e.g., FAST, Sapiens LifePro, Oracle Insurance). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  ownership_source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this ownership record in the source policy administration system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and bidirectional synchronization.']
  ownership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ownership relationship. Active = ownership is in force; Terminated = ownership has ended; Suspended = ownership temporarily inactive; Pending = ownership change awaiting approval.']
  ownership_transfer_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for ownership change or termination. Sale = ownership sold to another party; Gift = ownership gifted; Divorce = ownership transferred due to divorce settlement; Death = ownership transferred due to owner death; Trust Transfer = ownership moved to/from trust; 1035 Exchange = tax-free policy exchange per IRC Section 1035.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the legal ownership structure. Individual = single person owner; Joint = multiple co-owners; Trust = trust entity; Corporation = corporate owner; Partnership = partnership entity; ILIT = Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust.']
  pep_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy owner is identified as a Politically Exposed Person per AML regulations. True = owner is PEP; False = not PEP. PEP status requires enhanced due diligence and monitoring.']
  power_of_attorney_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a power of attorney arrangement is in place allowing another party to act on behalf of the owner for policy servicing transactions. True = POA is active; False = no POA.']
  relationship_to_insured varchar [note: 'Relationship of the policy owner to the insured life. Self = owner is the insured; Spouse = owner is spouse of insured; Employer = employer-owned life insurance; Trust = trust owns policy on insured; Other = other relationship. Used for underwriting, compliance, and STOLI detection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: self|spouse|parent|child|sibling|employer|trust|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sanctions_screening_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent sanctions screening was performed for the policy owner. Null if sanctions_screening_status is not_screened.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of screening the policy owner against OFAC and other sanctions lists. Clear = no match found; Match = potential match requires review; Pending = screening in progress; Not Screened = screening not yet performed.']
  tax_reporting_owner_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this owner is the designated party for IRS tax reporting purposes (1099-R, 1099-INT). True = this owner receives tax forms; False = not the tax reporting owner. Critical for IRC Section 7702 and 72 compliance.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when this ownership relationship ended. Null for active ownership. Populated upon ownership transfer, policy surrender, or policy maturity.']
  tin varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (SSN or EIN) of the policy owner for IRS reporting. Required for tax reporting and 1099 issuance. May be SSN for individual owners or EIN for entity owners.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this policy ownership record was last modified in the data platform. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking a policy to its legal owner(s), capturing ownership type (individual, trust, corporation, ILIT, employer), ownership percentage for co-ownership scenarios, ownership effective date, ownership termination date, ownership transfer reason, power-of-attorney flag, and collateral assignment flag. Distinct from the insured (policyholder domain) and from the beneficiary. Supports ownership change transactions, collateral assignment tracking, and 1035 exchange origination.'
}

Table policy.policy_reinstatement {
  policy_reinstatement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the policy reinstatement record. Primary key for the policy reinstatement entity.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Reinstatement requiring evidence of insurability generates a new underwriting application. The reinstatement record must reference this application to track the underwriting submission, decision, and ']
  assumption_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.assumption_set. Business justification: Reinstatement underwriting uses actuarial assumption sets to determine evidence of insurability thresholds, back-premium interest rates, and contestability period restart rules. Actuarial and complian']
  cession_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.reinsurance_cession. Business justification: Policy reinstatement requires reinstating the associated reinsurance cession, which may have lapsed or been terminated. Reinsurance operations must coordinate cession reinstatement with the reinsurer.']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Reinstatement applications require health statements, evidence of insurability forms, and back-premium receipts as regulatory documents. Insurers must retain these for contestability period compliance']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policy being reinstated. Links to the master policy record in the policy administration system.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Reinstatements collect back premiums and fees, generating journal entries for premium income and fee revenue recognition. Finance reconciliation of reinstatement collections to GL postings is required']
  lapse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.lapse_event. Business justification: Reinstatement applications must reference the specific lapse event to verify lapse reason, duration, outstanding amounts, and reinstatement eligibility window. Critical for underwriting decision workf']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent who assisted with or submitted the reinstatement application. Supports commission tracking and agent performance metrics.']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.underwriting_risk_assessment. Business justification: Reinstatement underwriting produces a formal risk assessment. The reinstatement record carries denormalized `underwriting_decision` and `underwriting_decision_date` fields that belong on the risk asse']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: A policy reinstatement application is initiated through a service request (request_type=\'REINSTATEMENT\'). Linking policy_reinstatement to the originating service_request provides full traceability fro']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Reinstatement eligibility windows, evidence of insurability requirements, and back-premium interest rules are state-regulated. Compliance reporting on reinstatement practices during market conduct exa']
  adverse_action_notice_sent_date date [note: 'Date the adverse action notice was sent to the applicant following a denial or rated decision. Required for regulatory compliance.']
  amount_collected decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount collected from the policyholder toward reinstatement. Tracks partial payments and full settlement.']
  application_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the reinstatement application was submitted. Supports channel performance analysis and customer experience tracking.']
  application_date date [note: 'Date the policyholder submitted the reinstatement application. Key date for calculating time-sensitive requirements and deadlines.']
  aps_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an Attending Physician Statement is required as part of the reinstatement underwriting. Typically required for longer lapse periods or health-related concerns.']
  back_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of unpaid premiums owed from the lapse date to reinstatement date. Must be paid for reinstatement approval.']
  back_premium_interest_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Interest charged on unpaid back premiums calculated per state regulation and company policy. Added to total reinstatement cost.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-form notes and comments from underwriters, processors, or customer service regarding the reinstatement case. Supports case management and audit trail.']
  contestable_period_restart_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the two-year contestable period restarts from the reinstatement effective date. Impacts claims adjudication and fraud investigation rights.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reinstatement record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  csv_at_lapse_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cash surrender value of the policy at the time of lapse. May be applied toward back premiums or returned to policyholder based on policy provisions.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the primary reason for denial of the reinstatement application. Required for regulatory reporting and adverse action notices.']
  denial_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the reinstatement application was denied. Supports adverse action letter generation and appeals process.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the policy coverage is restored if reinstatement is approved. May be retroactive to lapse date or prospective based on company policy and state regulation.']
  evidence_of_insurability_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the applicant must provide evidence of continued insurability for reinstatement. Typically required if lapse exceeded a threshold period.']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Administrative fee charged for processing the reinstatement application. May vary by policy type and jurisdiction.']
  health_statement_attestation_date date [note: 'Date the applicant signed and attested to the accuracy of the health statement. Legal date for representation and warranty purposes.']
  health_statement_received_date date [note: 'Date the completed health statement was received from the applicant. Tracks compliance with evidence of insurability requirements.']
  health_statement_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the applicant must complete a health statement questionnaire. Standard requirement for reinstatements beyond the grace period.']
  lapse_date date [note: 'Date the policy originally lapsed due to non-payment of premium or other reason. Determines reinstatement eligibility window and requirements.']
  lapse_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason the policy lapsed. Influences reinstatement requirements and underwriting criteria.']
  loan_balance_at_lapse_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Outstanding policy loan balance at the time of lapse. Must be repaid or reinstated along with the policy.']
  nigo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the application was initially submitted incomplete or with errors requiring rework. Tracks application quality and processing efficiency.']
  nigo_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the specific deficiency that caused the application to be marked as not in good order. Supports root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used by the policyholder to pay reinstatement amounts. Influences processing time and reconciliation procedures.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date the reinstatement payment was received by the company. Key date for determining when reinstatement can be processed.']
  processing_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from application receipt to final decision. Key performance indicator for operational efficiency and customer experience.']
  reinstatement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the reinstatement application in the workflow. Tracks progression from initial application through final decision.']
  reinstatement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reinstatement based on requirements and conditions. Automatic requires only payment; conditional requires underwriting; unconditional is within grace period.']
  suicide_exclusion_restart_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the suicide exclusion period restarts from the reinstatement effective date. Impacts death benefit eligibility for suicide claims.']
  total_amount_due decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount required for reinstatement including back premiums, interest, and fees. Must be collected before policy is reinstated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reinstatement record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking for compliance and operational monitoring.']
  window_expiry_date date [note: 'Last date the policy is eligible for reinstatement per policy terms and state regulation. Typically 3-5 years from lapse date.']

  Note: 'Tracks all policy reinstatement applications and decisions for lapsed policies. Captures reinstatement application date, lapse date, reinstatement effective date, reinstatement status (pending, approved, declined, withdrawn), evidence of insurability requirement flag, APS required flag, back-premium amount due, reinstatement premium collected, interest on back-premiums, health statement attestation date, underwriting decision reference, and reinstatement denial reason code. Sourced from the Policy Administration System reinstatement module.'
}

Table policy.surrender {
  surrender_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the surrender transaction record. Primary key.']
  cession_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.reinsurance_cession. Business justification: Surrender processing requires terminating the associated reinsurance cession and notifying the reinsurer. Operations teams must identify which cession to close when a policy surrenders. A reinsurance ']
  charge_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.charge_schedule. Business justification: Surrender transactions apply surrender charges, MVA, and free withdrawal amounts defined in charge_schedule. Linking surrender to charge_schedule provides the audit trail of which schedule version was']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Surrender transactions generate IRS Form 1099-R, surrender request forms, and disbursement confirmations required for tax reporting and regulatory compliance. Direct FK enables tax document retrieval,']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the life insurance policy or annuity contract against which this surrender transaction was executed.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Surrender transactions generate specific GL postings (CSV disbursement, surrender charge revenue, taxable gain recognition). Finance reconciliation of surrender proceeds to GL requires tracing each su']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Variable product surrenders require portfolio valuation to calculate net proceeds, market value adjustments based on underlying fund performance, and surrender charges. Essential for accurate surrende']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.producer. Business justification: Surrenders are often producer-assisted transactions requiring conservation efforts. Business needs producer attribution for commission clawback calculations, persistency metrics by producer, conservat']
  resulting_annuity_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Surrendered life policies frequently roll into annuities via 1035 tax-free exchanges. Surrender processing tracks the resulting annuity contract for tax reporting (Form 1099-R coordination), customer ']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Surrendering a variable life/annuity policy requires unit redemption from the specific separate account. The surrender processing system must reference the separate account to calculate MVA, process u']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: A surrender or partial withdrawal transaction is initiated through a service request (request_type=\'SURRENDER\' or \'PARTIAL_WITHDRAWAL\'). Linking surrender to the originating service_request provides e']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Nonforfeiture options, surrender charge schedules, and minimum cash surrender value requirements are state-regulated. The surrender table tracks naic_compliance_flag and nonforfeiture_option_type. A F']
  valuation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.valuation. Business justification: Surrender value (gross_amount, csv_amount_used, net_proceeds) is computed from the current market valuation of the policy\'s investment holdings. The specific valuation snapshot used at surrender must ']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the surrender request was approved by underwriting, policy services, or automated workflow. Marks transition from pending to approved status.']
  charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Surrender charge assessed against the gross amount per the policy or contract schedule. Recovers deferred acquisition costs (DAC) and early termination expenses.']
  cost_basis_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Policyholder\'s cost basis in the contract at the time of surrender, representing total premiums paid less any prior non-taxable distributions. Used to calculate taxable gain per IRC Section 72.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this surrender transaction record was first created in the policy administration system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  csv_amount_used decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cash Surrender Value amount applied to fund the nonforfeiture benefit (extended term or reduced paid-up insurance) or automatic premium loan. Represents the policy equity consumed by the nonforfeiture transaction.']
  disbursement_date date [note: 'Date the net surrender proceeds were disbursed to the policyholder, beneficiary, or receiving financial institution.']
  disbursement_method varchar [note: 'Method by which net surrender proceeds were disbursed to the policyholder or receiving institution: check (mailed check), EFT (electronic funds transfer), wire transfer (same-day wire), 1035 direct transfer (trustee-to-trustee transfer for tax-free exchange), ACH (automated clearing house transfer).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the surrender transaction became effective for policy value calculations, coverage termination, and accounting purposes.']
  extended_term_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the extended term insurance coverage when the extended term nonforfeiture option is elected. Coverage terminates on this date unless the policy is reinstated.']
  form_1099r_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this surrender transaction requires IRS Form 1099-R reporting for taxable distributions from life insurance, annuities, pensions, or retirement plans. True if 1099-R reporting is required; false otherwise.']
  free_withdrawal_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of the withdrawal that falls within the contract\'s free withdrawal corridor (typically 10% of account value annually) and is not subject to surrender charges.']
