  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total gross value available for surrender before application of surrender charges, market value adjustments, or tax withholding. Represents the Cash Surrender Value (CSV) or Account Value (AV) at the time of surrender.']
  mva_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Market Value Adjustment applied to fixed indexed annuities (FIA) or fixed annuities with MVA riders. Positive value indicates upward adjustment; negative value indicates downward adjustment based on interest rate movements since contract issue.']
  naic_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this surrender transaction complies with NAIC Standard Nonforfeiture Law requirements for minimum cash values, nonforfeiture options, and grace period provisions. True if compliant; false if exception or waiver applies.']
  net_proceeds decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net amount payable to the policyholder or contract owner after deducting surrender charges, market value adjustments, outstanding policy loans, and tax withholding from the gross amount.']
  nonforfeiture_election_date date [note: 'Date the policyholder elected or the insurer applied the default nonforfeiture option. Applicable when policy lapses without premium payment and nonforfeiture provisions are triggered.']
  nonforfeiture_option_type varchar [note: 'Nonforfeiture benefit option elected or defaulted upon policy lapse or surrender: cash surrender (CSV payout), extended term insurance (CSV purchases term coverage for original face amount), reduced paid-up insurance (CSV purchases reduced permanent coverage), automatic premium loan (CSV funds overdue premiums), or none (no nonforfeiture benefit applicable).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments captured by policy service representatives or underwriters regarding special circumstances, exceptions, or processing details for this surrender transaction.']
  outstanding_loan_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Outstanding policy loan principal and accrued interest deducted from the gross surrender value at the time of surrender. Applies to life insurance policies with loan provisions.']
  processing_date date [note: 'Date the surrender transaction was processed and posted to the policy administration system, updating policy status, values, and accounting ledgers.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason for the surrender or withdrawal: financial hardship (policyholder financial need), 1035 exchange (tax-free transfer to another policy per IRC Section 1035), annuitization (conversion to income stream), death (beneficiary surrender post-claim), lapse nonforfeiture (automatic nonforfeiture election upon lapse), policy maturity (endowment maturity payout).']
  receiving_carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the receiving insurance carrier or financial institution for 1035 exchange transactions. Populated when the surrender is part of a tax-free policy exchange.']
  receiving_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy or contract number at the receiving carrier for 1035 exchange transactions. Links the surrendered policy to the replacement contract.']
  reduced_paid_up_face_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Face amount (death benefit) of the reduced paid-up insurance coverage when the reduced paid-up nonforfeiture option is elected. Calculated based on the CSV and attained age of the insured.']
  reduced_paid_up_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the reduced paid-up policy resulting from the nonforfeiture election: active (coverage in force), lapsed (coverage terminated), matured (endowment matured), surrendered (subsequently surrendered for remaining CSV).']
  request_date date [note: 'Date the policyholder or contract owner submitted the surrender or withdrawal request to the insurer.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the policy administration system or platform that originated this surrender transaction record (e.g., FAST, Sapiens LifePro, Oracle Insurance Policy Administration).']
  source_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier from the source policy administration system. Enables traceability and reconciliation between lakehouse and operational systems.']
  tax_withholding_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Federal and state income tax withholding amount deducted from the surrender proceeds per IRS regulations and policyholder election. Applies to taxable gain portion of the distribution.']
  taxable_gain_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Taxable gain portion of the surrender distribution calculated as the excess of the amount received over the cost basis (premiums paid less prior distributions). Reported on IRS Form 1099-R.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique transaction number assigned to this surrender event for customer and agent reference.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the surrender transaction: pending (request received, under review), approved (authorized for processing), processed (transaction completed in policy admin system), disbursed (funds paid out), rejected (request denied), reversed (transaction voided or corrected).']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of surrender or nonforfeiture transaction: full surrender (complete termination with CSV payout), partial surrender (partial withdrawal retaining policy), systematic withdrawal (scheduled recurring withdrawals), extended term insurance election (nonforfeiture option converting CSV to term coverage), reduced paid-up insurance election (nonforfeiture option converting CSV to reduced permanent coverage), or automatic premium loan election (nonforfeiture option using CSV to pay overdue premiums).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this surrender transaction record was last modified in the policy administration system. Tracks data currency and change history.']

  Note: 'Records all surrender, partial surrender, systematic withdrawal, and nonforfeiture benefit transactions against life insurance policies and annuity contracts. SSOT for nonforfeiture option elections and outcomes. Stores transaction type (full surrender, partial surrender, systematic withdrawal, extended term insurance election, reduced paid-up insurance election, automatic premium loan election, cash surrender value nonforfeiture), request date, effective date, gross amount, surrender charge applied, market value adjustment (MVA) applied, net proceeds, tax withholding amount, 1099-R indicator, free withdrawal amount utilized, reason code (financial hardship, 1035 exchange, annuitization, death, lapse nonforfeiture), nonforfeiture option type elected or defaulted, nonforfeiture election date, extended term insurance expiry date, reduced paid-up face amount, reduced paid-up policy status, CSV amount used to fund the nonforfeiture benefit, disbursement method, and compliance with NAIC Standard Nonforfeiture Law. Sourced from the Policy Administration System surrender, lapse administration, and nonforfeiture processing modules.'
}

Table policy.loan {
  loan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the policy loan record. Primary key.']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Policy loans require executed loan agreement documents and disclosure forms as legal instruments. Regulators and auditors require direct traceability from the loan record to its governing document. Di']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying life insurance policy against which this loan is issued.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Policy loan disbursements, interest accruals, and repayments generate journal entries for interest income recognition and loan balance reconciliation. Finance teams must trace each loan transaction to']
  origination_valuation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.valuation. Business justification: Maximum policy loan amount is determined by the CSV, which is derived from the investment valuation at loan origination. Recording the specific valuation snapshot used supports loan origination audit ']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Variable product loans are collateralized by separate account portfolio value. Required for loan-to-CSV ratio monitoring, automatic premium loan (APL) trigger detection, collateral adequacy assessment']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent or service representative responsible for administering this loan and handling borrower inquiries.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: A policy loan origination is initiated through a service request (request_type=\'LOAN_REQUEST\'). Linking loan to the originating service_request provides traceability from the policyholder\'s loan appli']
  accrued_interest_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total interest accrued on the loan that has not been paid or capitalized into principal.']
  apl_trigger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this loan was automatically triggered due to insufficient funds to pay a premium. True if the loan was initiated by the APL provision; False if borrower-initiated.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date the loan was fully closed (either through full repayment, offset at death, offset at surrender, or foreclosure). Null if loan is still active.']
  closure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason the loan was closed. Full_repayment indicates borrower paid off loan; offset_death_claim indicates loan was deducted from death benefit; offset_surrender indicates loan was deducted from surrender proceeds; policy_lapse indicates policy lapsed due to loan exceeding CSV; loan_forgiveness indicates loan was forgiven (rare, typically taxable event).']
  collateral_csv_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cash Surrender Value (CSV) of the policy at the time of loan origination, representing the maximum collateral available to secure the loan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this policy loan record was first created in the system.']
  disbursement_date date [note: 'Date on which loan proceeds were disbursed to the policyholder or applied to premium/loan interest.']
  disbursement_method varchar [note: 'Method by which loan proceeds were disbursed. Check indicates mailed check; EFT indicates electronic funds transfer to bank account; wire indicates wire transfer; applied_to_premium indicates loan was used to pay premium (APL); applied_to_loan indicates loan was used to pay interest on existing loan.']
  interest_capitalization_date date [note: 'Date on which accrued interest was capitalized (added to principal balance), typically occurring annually on the policy anniversary if interest remains unpaid.']
  interest_crediting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which interest is calculated and added to the accrued interest balance.']
  interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual interest rate applied to the policy loan, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0500 for 5%). Rate may be fixed at origination or variable based on an index.']
  interest_rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the loan interest rate is determined. Fixed rates remain constant; variable rates adjust periodically based on an index such as Moody\'s Corporate Bond Yield Average, US Treasury rates, or LIBOR; guaranteed_max indicates a rate with a contractual ceiling.']
  last_interest_posting_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which interest was calculated and posted to the accrued interest balance.']
  last_repayment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of the most recent repayment transaction. Null if no repayments have been made.']
  last_repayment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent repayment transaction applied to this loan. Null if no repayments have been made.']
  loan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the policy loan, typically displayed on customer statements and correspondence.']
  loan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the policy loan. Active indicates outstanding balance; repaid indicates full repayment; foreclosed indicates policy lapsed due to loan exceeding CSV; offset_at_death indicates loan was deducted from death benefit at claim; offset_at_surrender indicates loan was deducted from surrender proceeds.']
  loan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the policy loan. Standard loans are borrower-initiated; Automatic Premium Loan (APL) loans are triggered when premium payment fails and policy has sufficient CSV; systematic loans are recurring scheduled loans; emergency loans are expedited loans for urgent needs.']
  maximum_loan_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount that can be borrowed against the policy, typically a percentage of CSV (e.g., 90% of CSV) as defined in the policy contract.']
  mec_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this loan was taken on a policy classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC), which has different tax treatment for loans and withdrawals. True if policy is a MEC; False otherwise.']
  next_scheduled_repayment_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled loan repayment, if a repayment schedule is in place. Null if no repayment schedule exists or loan is fully repaid.']
  offset_amount_at_claim decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total loan balance (principal plus accrued interest) that was deducted from the death benefit when a claim was paid. Null if policy has not resulted in a death claim.']
  offset_amount_at_surrender decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total loan balance (principal plus accrued interest) that was deducted from the cash surrender value when the policy was surrendered. Null if policy has not been surrendered.']
  original_loan_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Principal amount of the loan at origination, before any interest accrual or repayments.']
  origination_date date [note: 'Date the policy loan was issued and funds were disbursed to the policyholder or applied to premium.']
  outstanding_principal_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current outstanding principal balance of the loan after partial repayments, excluding accrued interest.']
  repayment_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Type of repayment schedule established for the loan. None indicates no scheduled repayments (loan repaid at death or surrender); monthly/quarterly/annual indicate regular scheduled payments; custom indicates a non-standard repayment plan.']
  scheduled_repayment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of each scheduled repayment installment, if a repayment schedule is in place. Null if no repayment schedule exists.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source policy administration system from which this loan record originated (e.g., FAST, Sapiens LifePro, Oracle Insurance Policy Administration).']
  tax_reporting_year varchar [note: 'Tax year in which a taxable event related to this loan must be reported to the IRS (e.g., when loan causes policy to become a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) or when loan is forgiven at lapse).']
  taxable_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount subject to income tax reporting related to this loan (e.g., gain recognized on MEC loan distribution or loan forgiveness at lapse). Null if no taxable event has occurred.']
  taxable_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this loan has triggered a taxable event requiring IRS reporting (e.g., loan on a MEC policy, loan forgiveness at lapse). True if taxable event occurred; False otherwise.']
  to_csv_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of total outstanding loan balance to current policy CSV, expressed as a decimal. Used to monitor loan collateralization and lapse risk. A ratio approaching 1.0 indicates high lapse risk.']
  total_outstanding_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Combined outstanding principal and accrued interest balance. This is the amount that would be deducted from death benefit or surrender value.']
  total_repayments_to_date decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount of all repayments (principal and interest) made on this loan since origination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this policy loan record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master record for all policy loans issued against the cash surrender value (CSV) of permanent life insurance policies. Captures loan number, loan type (standard, automatic premium loan — APL, systematic loan), loan origination date, original loan amount, outstanding loan balance, accrued interest balance, loan interest rate, interest rate type (fixed, variable, Moody\'s Corporate Bond Yield Average), loan status (active, repaid, foreclosed, offset at death), last interest posting date, interest capitalization date, collateral CSV amount at origination, automatic premium loan trigger flag, loan repayment schedule, partial repayment amount, and loan offset amount applied at claim or surrender. Critical for policy value calculations, death benefit reduction, and IRS reporting of loan-related taxable events.'
}

Table policy.dividend {
  dividend_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dividend record. Primary key for the dividend product.']
  assumption_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.assumption_set. Business justification: Dividend scales are built from actuarial assumption sets containing mortality, interest, and expense factors. Actuaries use assumption_set to calculate declared_amount and apportionment factors. Real ']
  experience_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.experience_study. Business justification: Dividend factors (mortality_factor, interest_factor, expense_factor on dividend record) are directly derived from experience study results. Actuarial teams set annual dividend scales based on experien']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the participating whole life policy to which this dividend applies. Links to the policy master record.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Dividend declarations and payments generate journal entries for policyholder dividend expense, cash disbursements, and PUA purchases. Finance requires direct traceability from dividend records to GL p']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Participating whole life dividend calculations are driven by general account portfolio performance (mortality, interest, expense factors). Required for dividend scale setting, apportionment methodolog']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: Participating policy dividend declarations are based on annual actuarial valuation results (mortality, interest, expense experience). Linking dividend to valuation_run enables actuarial teams to trace']
  accumulated_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'The current balance of dividends left on deposit with the company when the accumulate-at-interest option is elected. Includes principal and accrued interest.']
  accumulated_interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The annual interest rate credited to accumulated dividends left on deposit. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0350 for 3.50%). Rate may be guaranteed or declared annually.']
  accumulated_interest_ytd decimal(15,2) [note: 'The interest credited to accumulated dividends during the current policy year. Used for tax reporting and policy value reconciliation.']
  apportionment_method varchar [note: 'The actuarial method used to allocate divisible surplus to this policy. CONTRIBUTION = contribution method; THREE_FACTOR = mortality, interest, expense factors; EXPERIENCE = experience rating; ASSET_SHARE = asset share method.']
  cash_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount paid in cash to the policyholder when the cash dividend option is elected. May differ from declared amount if partial amounts are applied to other options.']
  cash_payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the cash dividend payment was issued or electronically transferred to the policyholder. Used for cash flow tracking and reconciliation.']
  cash_payment_method varchar [note: 'The method used to disburse the cash dividend payment. CHECK = paper check; ACH = automated clearing house; WIRE = wire transfer; EFT = electronic funds transfer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this dividend record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  declaration_date date [note: 'The date on which the board of directors or authorized committee formally declared the dividend for the policy year. Represents the official authorization date.']
  declared_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total dividend amount declared for the policy for the dividend year, expressed in the policy currency. Represents the policyholder\'s share of divisible surplus before any option election.']
  dividend_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the dividend. DECLARED = authorized but not yet processed; PENDING = awaiting option election; PAID = cash payment issued; APPLIED = applied per elected option; FORFEITED = lost due to lapse; REVERSED = reversed due to policy adjustment.']
  expense_factor decimal(15,2) [note: 'The expense component of the dividend calculation. Represents the policyholder\'s share of favorable expense experience relative to pricing assumptions.']
  illustration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dividend amount was used in a policy illustration. True if included in an illustration provided to the policyholder; false otherwise. Used for compliance tracking.']
  interest_factor decimal(15,2) [note: 'The investment earnings component of the dividend calculation. Represents the policyholder\'s share of investment income exceeding guaranteed rates.']
  loan_reduction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount of the dividend applied to reduce the outstanding policy loan balance when the loan reduction option is elected. Reduces principal and/or accrued loan interest.']
  mortality_factor decimal(15,2) [note: 'The mortality component of the dividend calculation. Represents the policyholder\'s share of favorable mortality experience for the dividend year.']
  option_code varchar [note: 'The dividend option elected by the policyholder. CASH = cash payment; PREM_RED = premium reduction; PUA = paid-up additions; ACCUM = accumulate at interest; OYT = one-year term insurance; LOAN_RED = loan reduction.']
  option_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the elected dividend option becomes effective. May differ from declaration date if the policyholder changes their election within the allowed window.']
  oyt_coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The face amount of one-year term insurance purchased with the dividend when the OYT option is elected. Provides temporary additional death benefit for one policy year.']
  oyt_premium_paid decimal(15,2) [note: 'The portion of the dividend used to purchase the one-year term coverage. Represents the cost of the temporary insurance benefit.']
  premium_reduction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount of the dividend applied to reduce the next premium due when the premium reduction option is elected. Reduces the net premium payable by the policyholder.']
  processing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the dividend record was processed by the policy administration system. Represents the system processing event, distinct from business effective dates.']
  pua_cash_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The cash surrender value of the paid-up additions purchased with this dividend. Contributes to the total policy cash value and is available for loans or surrender.']
  pua_face_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The face amount of paid-up additional insurance purchased with the dividend when the PUA option is elected. Represents incremental death benefit added to the base policy.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The date on which a previously declared or paid dividend was reversed. Used for audit trail and financial reconciliation.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'The reason for reversing a previously declared or paid dividend. POLICY_LAPSE = policy lapsed before dividend paid; POLICY_RESCISSION = policy rescinded; DATA_CORRECTION = data error correction; CALCULATION_ERROR = actuarial calculation error; OPTION_CHANGE = policyholder changed option election.']
  scale_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the dividend scale used to calculate this dividend. Links to the actuarial dividend scale table and supports audit trail for rate changes.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The identifier of the source policy administration system from which this dividend record originated. Supports multi-system environments and data lineage tracking.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this dividend record in the source policy administration system. Enables traceability back to the system of record.']
  special_dividend_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a special or extraordinary dividend declared outside the normal annual dividend cycle. True for special dividends; false for regular annual dividends.']
  tax_reporting_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which the dividend is reportable for tax purposes. May differ from dividend year due to timing of payment or option application.']
  taxable_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The portion of the dividend that is taxable to the policyholder. Generally zero for participating whole life unless dividends exceed premiums paid, but tracked for IRS reporting compliance.']
  terminal_dividend_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a terminal dividend paid upon policy maturity, death claim, or surrender. True for terminal dividends; false for annual dividends.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this dividend record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  year varchar [note: 'The policy year or calendar year for which the dividend was declared. Represents the experience period underlying the dividend calculation.']

  Note: 'Tracks annual policyholder dividend declarations and elections for participating whole life policies. Stores dividend year, declared dividend amount, dividend option elected (cash, premium reduction, paid-up additions — PUA, accumulate at interest, one-year term), dividend option effective date, paid-up additions face amount purchased, accumulated dividend balance, dividend interest rate, and dividend payment date. Owned by the policy domain as a contractual feature of participating policies.'
}

Table policy.tax_compliance_test {
  tax_compliance_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for tax_compliance_test']
  assumption_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.assumption_set. Business justification: IRC 7702 guideline premium and cash value accumulation test calculations require specific actuarial assumptions (interest rates, mortality). The assumption_set governs these inputs beyond just the mor']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance or annuity policy for which this MEC test was performed.']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: IRC 7702 and MEC testing requires insured age, risk class, and mortality data directly from the insured record. insured_age_at_test is a denormalized copy of insured data. This FK enables actuarial co']
  irc7702_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.irc7702_parameter. Business justification: IRC 7702 CVAT/GPT compliance tests use guideline_single_premium, guideline_level_premium, seven_pay_premium_limit, and corridor_percentage defined in irc7702_parameter. Linking tax_compliance_test to ']
  mortality_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: MEC and CVAT tests require mortality assumptions to calculate guideline premiums and corridor percentages. IRS compliance testing mandates specific mortality tables. Real regulatory process actuaries ']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: IRC 7702 compliance testing and MEC 7-pay testing are specific regulatory obligations under the Internal Revenue Code. Linking each tax_compliance_test to its governing regulatory_obligation enables c']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: IRC 7702/7702A compliance tests for variable life require the separate account\'s unit values and NAV to compute CVAT/GPT corridor tests and account value. Regulators and auditors require traceability ']
  tax_compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: IRC 7702 and MEC test results require mandatory policyholder notification documents (MEC notices, corridor test disclosures) under IRS regulations. Direct FK links the compliance test record to its ge']
  tax_compliance_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: MEC reclassification events (IRC 7702 test failures) generate journal entries for deferred tax adjustments and IRS Form 1099-R reporting. Finance must trace MEC determination events to their GL impact']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: IRC 7702 and MEC compliance tests are executed within actuarial valuation cycles. Linking tax_compliance_test to valuation_run enables audit traceability of which valuation run produced the guideline ']
  value_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.value. Business justification: IRC Section 7702A MEC testing is performed against a specific policy value snapshot as of the test date. Linking tax_compliance_test to the policy.value record used in the test provides authoritative ']
  account_value_at_test decimal(15,2) [note: 'The policy account value (gross cash value before surrender charges) at the time the MEC test was performed. Relevant for universal life and variable universal life products.']
  actuarial_system_source varchar [note: 'The name and version of the actuarial valuation system that performed the MEC test calculation (e.g., MoSes, AXIS, Prophet). Critical for audit trail and calculation reproducibility.']
  cash_value_accumulation_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum cash value the policy can accumulate under the Cash Value Accumulation Test (CVAT) without violating IRC 7702 life insurance definition. Only applicable if CVAT election was made.']
  cash_value_at_test decimal(15,2) [note: 'The policy cash surrender value at the time the MEC test was performed. Used in corridor test calculations and CVAT limit assessments.']
  corridor_test_result varchar [note: 'Result of the corridor test which ensures the death benefit maintains a minimum ratio to the cash value. Required for policies to maintain life insurance tax treatment under IRC 7702.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this MEC test result record was first created in the system.']
  cumulative_premiums_paid decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total amount of premiums paid into the policy from issuance through the test date. Used to compare against the 7-pay premium limit.']
  death_benefit_at_test decimal(15,2) [note: 'The policy death benefit amount in effect at the time the MEC test was performed. Death benefit is a key input to the 7-pay premium limit calculation.']
  definition_election varchar [note: 'The IRC Section 7702 definition of life insurance elected by the policyholder at issuance. GPT uses guideline premiums and cash value corridor; CVAT uses cash value accumulation limits. This election is irrevocable.']
  exchange_1035_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy was acquired through a Section 1035 tax-free exchange from another life insurance or annuity contract. 1035 exchanges carry forward the MEC status from the original contract.']
  exchanged_policy_mec_status varchar [note: 'If this policy was acquired through a 1035 exchange, this field captures the MEC status of the original exchanged policy. MEC status is preserved in 1035 exchanges.']
  form_1099r_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether distributions from this policy are reportable on IRS Form 1099-R due to MEC status. MEC distributions are subject to income tax and potential early withdrawal penalties.']
  guideline_level_premium decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum level annual premium that can be paid into the policy under the Guideline Premium Test (GPT) without violating IRC 7702 life insurance definition. Only applicable if GPT election was made.']
  guideline_single_premium decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum single premium that can be paid into the policy under the Guideline Premium Test (GPT) without violating IRC 7702 life insurance definition. Only applicable if GPT election was made.']
  irs_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this MEC test result requires reporting to the IRS. True if the policy became a MEC or if a material change occurred that affects tax treatment.']
  material_change_date date [note: 'The date on which a material change event occurred, triggering a new 7-pay test period. Null if no material change has occurred.']
  material_change_event_type varchar [note: 'The type of material change event that triggered a new MEC test. Material changes reset the 7-pay test period. If no material change occurred, this field is set to \'none\'.']
  mec_status varchar [note: 'The result of the MEC test indicating whether the policy is classified as a Modified Endowment Contract. MEC status affects the tax treatment of policy distributions and loans.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'If the MEC test result was manually overridden by an underwriter or compliance officer, this field captures the business justification for the override. Null if no override occurred.']
  override_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the MEC test result was manually overridden. Null if no override occurred.']
  policy_issue_date date [note: 'The original issue date of the policy. Used to determine policy year and the start of the 7-pay test period.']
  policy_year_at_test varchar [note: 'The policy year (anniversary year) in which the MEC test was performed. Relevant for tracking the 7-pay test period which spans the first seven policy years or until a material change.']
  prior_mec_status varchar [note: 'The MEC status of the policy before this test was performed. Used to track status changes and identify when a policy first became a MEC. Set to \'not applicable\' for initial issuance tests.']
  seven_pay_premium_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum cumulative premium that can be paid into the policy during the first seven years (or until a material change) without causing the policy to become a MEC. Calculated based on actuarial assumptions and policy death benefit.']
  test_calculation_method varchar [note: 'The actuarial methodology and assumptions used to calculate the 7-pay premium limit and other test thresholds. May reference specific assumption sets or calculation standards.']
  test_date date [note: 'The date on which the MEC test was performed. This may be at policy issuance or upon a material change event.']
  test_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes and comments regarding the MEC test, including any special circumstances, assumptions, or actuarial judgments applied during the calculation.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'The processing status of the MEC test. Indicates whether the test calculation has been completed, is pending actuarial review, failed due to data issues, or was manually overridden.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'The type of MEC test performed. The 7-pay test is the primary test under IRC 7702A to determine if cumulative premiums paid exceed the 7-pay premium limit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this MEC test result record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Stores the results of IRC Section 7702A Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) testing performed on each policy at issuance and upon material change. Captures test date, test type (7-pay test), 7-pay premium limit, cumulative premiums paid to date, MEC status flag (MEC / non-MEC), material change event type, material change date, corridor test result, GPT/CVAT election, guideline single premium, guideline level premium, and the actuarial system that performed the calculation. Critical for IRS compliance and tax reporting.'
}

Table policy.fund_allocation {
  fund_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each policy fund allocation record. Primary key for the policy fund allocation entity.']
  crediting_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.crediting_strategy. Business justification: Each IUL/FIA fund allocation segment is governed by a specific crediting strategy defining cap, floor, participation, and spread rates. Linking fund_allocation to crediting_strategy is essential for s']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent policy contract (UL, IUL, VUL, or FIA) to which this fund allocation applies.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Fixed allocation options in hybrid variable products reference general account portfolios. Required for blended portfolio management, ALM analysis, and unified performance reporting across fixed and v']
  scenario_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.scenario_set. Business justification: Indexed and variable fund allocations (IUL, VA) are stress-tested against economic scenario sets for AG43/VM-21 stochastic reserve requirements. Linking fund_allocation to scenario_set enables actuari']
  separate_account_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.separate_account_fund. Business justification: Variable and indexed product fund allocations must link to the separate account fund master (separate_account_fund) for NAV calculation, performance reporting, prospectus compliance, expense ratio app']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable life/annuity fund allocations must reference the investment.separate_account for daily unit-value-based crediting, NAV computation, and SEC/FINRA regulatory reporting. The existing separate_a']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: Fund allocation changes (rebalancing, strategy changes, index segment elections) for universal life and variable annuity policies are initiated through service requests (request_type=\'FUND_REALLOCATIO']
  suitability_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: Variable and indexed product fund allocation changes require suitability review under FINRA Reg BI and state variable product regulations. Linking fund_allocation to the suitability_review that approv']
  valuation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.valuation. Business justification: Each fund allocation\'s dollar amount (allocation_amount) is computed from the current market valuation of the underlying investment. Linking to the specific valuation snapshot used at allocation_effec']
  allocation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For dollar-based allocations (e.g., DCA programs), the fixed dollar amount allocated to this fund per period. Null for percentage-based allocations.']
  allocation_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the allocation instruction was approved and accepted by the policy administration system, completing validation and compliance checks.']
  allocation_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this fund allocation instruction became effective and began applying to premium payments or account value rebalancing.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current percentage of premium or account value allocated to this fund. Must sum to 100% across all allocations for a given policy and allocation type. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 25.50 for 25.5%).']
  allocation_request_date date [note: 'Date when the allocation change was requested by the policyholder or advisor. May differ from effective date due to processing time or future-dated instructions.']
  allocation_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering number for multiple fund allocations within the same policy and allocation type. Used for processing priority and display ordering.']
  allocation_source varchar [note: 'Origin of this allocation instruction: policyholder-elected, system default, advisor-recommended, or automated program-driven.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this fund allocation instruction. Active allocations are applied to transactions; inactive/terminated allocations are historical records.']
  allocation_termination_date date [note: 'Date when this allocation instruction was terminated or superseded by a new allocation. Null for currently active allocations.']
  allocation_termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why this allocation instruction was terminated. Null for active allocations.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Type of allocation instruction: premium_allocation (applies to new premium payments), account_value_rebalance (periodic rebalancing of existing AV), transfer (one-time fund transfer), systematic_rebalance (automated periodic rebalancing), dollar_cost_averaging (DCA program allocation).']
  cap_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum interest rate that can be credited to the policy account value for this indexed fund allocation, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 12.00 for 12%). Applicable to IUL and FIA products with capped index strategies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this allocation record was first created in the policy administration system.']
  dollar_cost_averaging_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation is part of a dollar cost averaging program where premium is systematically transferred from a fixed account to investment funds over time.']
  floor_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed interest rate for this fund allocation, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 0.00 for 0% floor). Protects against negative index performance in IUL and FIA products.']
  fund_category varchar [note: 'High-level investment category classification of the fund for reporting and risk management purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equity|fixed_income|balanced|money_market|index|specialty|guaranteed|alternative — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fund_expense_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual expense ratio charged by the investment fund, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0075 for 0.75%). Used for cost disclosure and performance reporting.']
  fund_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk profile classification of the investment fund used for suitability assessment and portfolio construction.']
  index_segment_end_date date [note: 'For IUL and FIA products, the end date of the current index crediting segment or term when index gains/losses are calculated and credited.']
  index_segment_start_date date [note: 'For IUL and FIA products, the start date of the current index crediting segment or term. Used to track index performance measurement periods.']
  index_segment_term_years varchar [note: 'Length of the index crediting term in years (e.g., 1, 2, 5, 7) for IUL and FIA products. Determines the measurement period for index performance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this allocation record was most recently updated or modified.']
  last_rebalance_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent automatic or manual rebalancing of account value to target allocation percentages was executed.']
  maximum_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage that can be allocated to this fund per product rules or regulatory requirements. Used for compliance validation during allocation changes.']
  minimum_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage that must be maintained in this fund per product rules or regulatory requirements. Used for compliance validation during allocation changes.']
  mortality_and_expense_risk_charge decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual M&E risk charge assessed against this fund allocation for variable products, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0125 for 1.25%). Covers insurance guarantees and administrative expenses.']
  next_rebalance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next automatic rebalancing transaction based on the rebalancing frequency. Null if no automatic rebalancing is configured.']
  participation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of index gain that is credited to the policy account value, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 85.00 for 85% participation). Applicable to IUL and FIA products.']
  rebalancing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the policy account value is automatically rebalanced to match the target allocation percentages. \'none\' indicates no automatic rebalancing.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the policy administration system or platform that originated this allocation record (e.g., FAST, SAPIENS, ORACLE_IPAS).']
  spread_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage deducted from index gains before crediting to the policy account value, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 2.50 for 2.5% spread). Alternative to cap rate in some indexed product designs.']

  Note: 'Tracks investment fund allocation instructions and current allocation percentages for universal life (UL, IUL, VUL) and fixed indexed annuity (FIA) policies with investment options. Stores fund code, fund name, allocation percentage, allocation effective date, allocation type (premium allocation, account value rebalance, transfer), rebalancing frequency, dollar cost averaging flag, index strategy code (for IUL/FIA: point-to-point, monthly average, annual reset), cap rate, participation rate, floor rate, spread, and last rebalance date. Sourced from the Policy Administration System investment allocation module.'
}

Table policy.assignment {
  assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the policy or annuity contract assignment record.']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scanned or stored legal assignment document in the document management system.']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance policy or annuity contract being assigned.']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.producer. Business justification: Collateral assignments (especially premium financing arrangements) typically involve producer facilitation and documentation. Business tracks producer involvement for compliance verification, commissi']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: An absolute or collateral assignment of a life insurance policy is a post-issue servicing transaction initiated through a service request (request_type=\'ASSIGNMENT\'). Linking assignment to the origina']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the assignment was approved by the insurer.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Internal approval status of the assignment request: approved (accepted by insurer), pending (awaiting review), rejected (denied), or under_review (in evaluation).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the underwriter or operations staff who approved the assignment.']
  assigned_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For collateral assignments, the maximum dollar amount of the loan or debt secured by the policy. Null for absolute assignments.']
  assigned_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of policy benefits assigned to the assignee. Typically 100% for absolute assignments, may be partial for collateral assignments.']
  assignee_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the assignee, used for legal notices and correspondence.']
  assignee_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (suite, apartment, floor) for the assignee.']
  assignee_city varchar [note: 'City of the assignee\'s address.']
  assignee_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the assignee\'s address.']
  assignee_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for assignee correspondence and notifications.']
  assignee_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual, financial institution, employer, or trust receiving the assignment rights.']
  assignee_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the assignee.']
  assignee_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the assignee\'s address.']
  assignee_state varchar [note: 'State or province code of the assignee\'s address.']
  assignee_tin varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (SSN or EIN) of the assignee for IRS reporting and identity verification.']
  assignee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assignee entity: individual person, financial institution (bank, lender), employer (for group policy assignments), trust (ILIT or other), or other entity type.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the assignment, often used in correspondence and legal documents.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assignment: active (in force), released (terminated by assignee), foreclosed (lender exercised rights), pending (awaiting approval), or cancelled (voided before activation).']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Type of assignment: absolute (full transfer of ownership rights) or collateral (security interest for a loan).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the assignment, including special instructions, exceptions, or clarifications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the assignment becomes legally effective and the assignee\'s rights commence.']
  irrevocable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assignment is irrevocable (true) or revocable (false). Irrevocable assignments cannot be changed without assignee consent.']
  lender_loan_reference varchar [note: 'External loan or account number from the lending institution, used to correlate the assignment with the underlying debt obligation.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified the assignment record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment record was last modified.']
  notarization_date date [note: 'Date on which the assignment document was notarized.']
  notary_public_name varchar [note: 'Name of the notary public who notarized the assignment document, if applicable.']
  notification_sent_date date [note: 'Date on which the insurer sent formal notification of the assignment to the policy owner and assignee.']
  premium_payment_responsibility varchar [note: 'Party responsible for paying policy premiums under the assignment: owner (policy owner retains responsibility), assignee (assignee assumes premium payments), or shared (split responsibility).']
  reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or purpose for the assignment, such as premium financing, estate planning, loan collateral, or gift.']
  recording_date date [note: 'Date on which the assignment was officially recorded in the insurer\'s policy administration system.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the assignment request was rejected, if applicable.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which the assignment was released or terminated, returning full rights to the policy owner. Null if assignment is still active.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created the assignment record.']

  Note: 'Records all absolute and collateral assignments of life insurance policies and annuity contracts. Captures assignment type (absolute, collateral), assignee name, assignee TIN, assignee type (individual, financial institution, employer, trust), assignment effective date, assignment release date, assigned amount (for collateral assignments), assignment status (active, released, foreclosed), lender loan reference number, assignment recording date, and notification sent date. Supports lender notification workflows, collateral release processing, premium financing arrangements, and ownership rights management under UCC Article 9.'
}

Table policy.service_request {
  service_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the policy service request transaction. Primary key for all post-issue policy servicing transactions, contractual endorsements, administrative changes, policy conversions, and IRC Section 1035 tax-free exchanges.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Service requests for coverage increases, reinstatements, or conversion exercises that require evidence of insurability spawn a new underwriting application. Linking service_request to the resulting un']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Service requests (beneficiary changes, address updates, endorsements) generate primary confirmation documents required for audit trails and regulatory compliance. Operations teams need direct linkage ']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance or annuity policy contract being serviced. Links to the master policy record in the Policy Administration System.']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.producer. Business justification: Service requests (conversions, exchanges, beneficiary changes) are frequently producer-facilitated. Business tracks producer involvement for commission attribution, service quality metrics, compliance']
  resulting_annuity_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Service requests for 1035 exchanges and policy-to-annuity conversions track the resulting annuity contract for workflow completion verification, customer notifications, and regulatory documentation. E']
  suitability_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: Service requests involving 1035 exchanges, replacements, and annuity conversions trigger mandatory suitability reviews under NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation and state rules. ']
  workflow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.workflow. Business justification: Service requests initiate document processing workflows (imaging, indexing, NIGO resolution, approval routing). Direct FK from service_request to workflow enables SLA tracking, work queue management, ']
  collateral_assignment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For collateral assignment requests, the maximum amount of the death benefit assigned to the lender as collateral. Limits the lenders claim on policy proceeds.']
  collateral_assignment_lender_name varchar [note: 'For collateral assignment requests, the name of the lending institution or creditor to whom the policy death benefit is assigned as collateral security. Business-confidential financial relationship data.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments from the policyholder, agent, or processing administrator regarding the service request. Captures additional context not structured in other fields.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date the service request was fully processed and completed in the Policy Administration System. Used for SLA compliance measurement and audit trail.']
  conversion_credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For conversion requests, the monetary credit or value transferred from the original policy to the new converted policy. Represents accumulated value or conversion privilege benefit.']
  conversion_privilege_expiry_date date [note: 'For conversion requests, the last date the policyholder can exercise their contractual conversion privilege without evidence of insurability. Critical for conversion window tracking.']
  conversion_source_policy_type varchar [note: 'For conversion requests, the original policy type being converted from. Typically YRT (Yearly Renewable Term) or term life insurance converting to permanent coverage.']
  conversion_target_policy_type varchar [note: 'For conversion requests, the new policy type being converted to. Typically permanent life insurance products (WL, UL, IUL, VUL).']
  cost_basis_transferred decimal(15,2) [note: 'For 1035 exchange requests, the tax cost basis (investment in the contract) transferred from the surrendering policy. Critical for future tax calculations on distributions and surrenders.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service request record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail for data lineage and regulatory compliance.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the service request change becomes effective on the policy contract. For endorsements, this is the amendment effective date. May differ from request date for future-dated changes.']
  endorsement_number varchar [note: 'Sequential endorsement identifier for formal contractual amendments to the policy. Null for administrative changes that do not constitute contractual modifications.']
  evidence_of_insurability_waived_flag boolean [note: 'For conversion requests, indicates whether medical underwriting and evidence of insurability requirements were waived under the conversion privilege. True if waived, False if evidence required.']
  exchange_1035_type varchar [note: 'For 1035 exchange requests, the type of tax-free exchange under IRC Section 1035. Determines permissible exchange paths and tax treatment.']
  exchange_amount_transferred decimal(15,2) [note: 'For 1035 exchange requests, the total cash surrender value or account value transferred from the surrendering policy to the new policy. Represents the gross exchange proceeds.']
  gain_deferred decimal(15,2) [note: 'For 1035 exchange requests, the amount of taxable gain deferred through the tax-free exchange. Calculated as exchange_amount_transferred minus cost_basis_transferred. Deferred gain remains taxable upon future surrender or distribution.']
  irs_form_1035_attestation_flag boolean [note: 'For 1035 exchange requests, indicates whether the policyholder has signed and submitted IRS Form 1035 attesting to the tax-free exchange. Required for regulatory compliance and tax reporting.']
  new_policy_reference varchar [note: 'For conversion requests, the policy number or reference of the newly issued converted policy. Links the conversion service request to the resulting new policy contract.']
  new_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value of the policy attribute after the service request change. Combined with prior_value, creates complete before-and-after audit trail.']
  nigo_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the service request was marked NIGO (Not In Good Order). Identifies missing documents, incomplete information, or deficiencies requiring correction. Critical for NIGO reduction initiatives.']
  prior_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value of the policy attribute before the service request change. Provides full audit trail of all contractual and administrative modifications for regulatory compliance.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason or trigger for the service request. Supports root cause analysis and process improvement initiatives.']
  reinstatement_evidence_required_flag boolean [note: 'For reinstatement requests, indicates whether evidence of insurability (medical underwriting) is required to reinstate the lapsed policy. Depends on lapse duration and policy provisions.']
  reinstatement_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For reinstatement requests, the total premium amount (including back premiums, interest, and fees) required to reinstate a lapsed policy. Must be paid to restore coverage.']
  request_channel varchar [note: 'Channel or interface through which the service request was submitted. Supports channel analytics and customer experience optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phone|web_portal|agent|mail|email|fax|in_person — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  request_date date [note: 'Date the service request was received or initiated by the policyholder, agent, or administrator. Represents the business event timestamp for SLA tracking.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the service request, endorsement, or administrative change. Used for customer and agent communication and tracking.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service request. NIGO (Not In Good Order) indicates incomplete or deficient submission requiring additional information or correction. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|in_review|pending_documents|completed|nigo|cancelled|rejected — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the policy service request or endorsement type. Includes administrative changes (address, beneficiary, ownership, premium mode, face amount, dividend option, name, duplicate contract), contractual amendments (rider addition/removal, collateral assignment), policy reinstatements, term-to-permanent conversions, and IRC Section 1035 tax-free exchanges. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: address_change|beneficiary_change|ownership_change|premium_mode_change|face_amount_change|dividend_option_change|name_change|duplicate_policy_contract|rider_addition|rider_removal|collateral_assignment|reinstatement|conversion|1035_exchange — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rider_code varchar [note: 'For rider addition or removal requests, the standardized code identifying the specific rider (ADB - Accidental Death Benefit, AWL - Automatic Waiver of Premium, CAR - Company Action Rider, LTC - Long-Term Care, etc.).']
  sla_due_date date [note: 'Target completion date based on service level agreement commitments for the request type. Used for SLA monitoring, NIGO reduction initiatives, and operational performance tracking.']
  source_system_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Original transaction identifier from the Policy Administration System (e.g., FAST, Sapiens LifePro, Oracle Insurance). Enables traceability back to source system for reconciliation and audit.']
  surrendering_carrier_name varchar [note: 'For 1035 exchange requests, the name of the insurance carrier whose policy is being surrendered and exchanged. Required for IRS Form 1035 reporting.']
  surrendering_policy_number varchar [note: 'For 1035 exchange requests, the policy number of the contract being surrendered at the external carrier. Required for exchange processing and IRS reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service request record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'SSOT for all post-issue policy servicing transactions, contractual endorsements, administrative changes, policy conversions, and IRC Section 1035 tax-free exchanges processed through the Policy Administration System. Encompasses administrative service requests, formal contractual amendments (endorsements), term-to-permanent conversions, and 1035 exchange transactions. Captures request/endorsement type (address change, beneficiary change, ownership change, premium mode change, face amount change, dividend option change, name change, duplicate policy contract request, rider addition/removal, collateral assignment, reinstatement, conversion, 1035 exchange), endorsement number (for contractual amendments), amendment effective date, request channel (phone, web portal, agent, mail), request date, effective date, request status (received, in-review, completed, NIGO — Not In Good Order, cancelled), prior value, new value, reason code, NIGO reason code, SLA due date, completion date, processing agent ID, source system transaction ID. For conversions: source policy type (YRT, term), target policy type (WL, UL, IUL), conversion credit amount, conversion privilege expiry date, evidence of insurability waived flag, new policy reference. For 1035 exchanges: exchange type (life-to-life, annuity-to-annuity, life-to-annuity), surrendering carrier name, surrendering policy number, exchange amount transferred, cost basis transferred, gain deferred, IRS Form 1035 attestation flag. Provides full audit trail of all contractual modifications. Supports policy servicing SLA monitoring, NIGO reduction initiatives, and regulatory audit trail requirements.'
}

Table policy.conversion {
  conversion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the policy conversion transaction. Primary key for the policy conversion record.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Conversions requiring evidence of insurability (e.g., term-to-perm beyond guaranteed conversion window) generate a new underwriting application. Linking conversion to the underwriting application supp']
  conversion_in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original term life policy being converted. Links to the source policy record in the in-force policy table.']
  conversion_new_policy_in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newly issued permanent policy resulting from the conversion. Links to the target policy record in the in-force policy table.']
  conversion_processing_agent_producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user or system agent who processed the conversion transaction. Used for audit and workflow tracking.']
  conversion_producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent or producer who facilitated the conversion. Links to the producer record in the agent management system.']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Term-to-permanent conversions generate conversion application forms, new policy contracts, and evidence-of-insurability waiver documents required for regulatory filing and new policy issuance. Direct ']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Policy conversions (term-to-permanent) trigger journal entries for reserve transfers, DAC reclassification, and premium income reclassification. Finance must trace conversion events to GL impact for G']
  original_conversion_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on conversion (original_conversion_id)']
  policy_form_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval. Business justification: Policy conversions result in a new policy issued on a new approved form. Compliance requires verifying the converted-to product form was approved in the issuing jurisdiction at conversion time. No exi']
  pricing_basis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Policy conversions require pricing basis to calculate new premium rates, face amounts, and underwriting class adjustments. Underwriting teams reference pricing basis for conversion privilege calculati']
  resulting_annuity_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Policy conversion to annuity (e.g., term-to-annuity or whole life conversion privilege) is a named life insurance business process requiring direct traceability to the resulting annuity contract for c']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.service_request. Business justification: A term-to-permanent policy conversion is a post-issue servicing transaction initiated through a service request (request_type=\'CONVERSION\'). Linking conversion to the originating service_request provi']
  suitability_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: Policy conversions, especially to annuity or variable products, require suitability review under NAIC model regulations and state rules. Linking conversion to suitability_review enables compliance tra']
  target_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan. Business justification: Conversion records the target plan a policy converts to. Linking conversion to the target plan_id enables conversion privilege validation against plan eligibility rules, new policy issuance workflow, ']
  underwriting_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.decision. Business justification: Conversions with evidence of insurability requirements produce a formal underwriting decision (approve/decline/rate). The conversion table has denormalized decision context but no FK to the authoritat']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the conversion request was approved by underwriting or policy services. Indicates when the conversion was authorized to proceed.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether managerial or underwriting approval is required for this conversion. True if the conversion requires special authorization beyond standard processing.']
  attained_age_at_conversion varchar [note: 'The insureds age at the time of conversion. Used for underwriting and pricing calculations for the new permanent policy.']
  basis varchar [note: 'The age basis used for pricing the new permanent policy. Determines whether the new policy is priced at the insureds current attained age or original issue age.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The distribution channel through which the conversion request was initiated. Indicates how the policyholder submitted the conversion request.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special instructions, or context related to the conversion transaction. Used for documenting unique circumstances or processing notes.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the conversion transaction was fully completed and the new policy was issued. Marks the end of the conversion lifecycle.']
  conversion_number varchar [note: 'Unique business transaction number assigned to this conversion event. Used for tracking and reference in policy administration systems.']
  conversion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the conversion transaction. Tracks the conversion through its workflow from initial request to final completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|in_progress|completed|cancelled|rejected|suspended — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  conversion_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the conversion based on the contractual provision being exercised. Indicates whether this is a standard contractual right or a special conversion offer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this conversion record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any credit or value transferred from the original term policy to the new permanent policy. May include accumulated dividends, paid-up additions, or other policy values.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the conversion becomes effective and the new permanent policy is in force. The original term policy terminates on this date.']
  evidence_of_insurability_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether medical evidence of insurability is required for the conversion. True if underwriting evidence is needed; false if conversion is guaranteed without evidence.']
  evidence_of_insurability_waived_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the evidence of insurability requirement was waived for this conversion. True if the conversion privilege allows conversion without medical underwriting.']
  new_face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Death benefit face amount of the newly issued permanent policy. May differ from the original face amount based on conversion terms and policyholder election.']
  new_modal_premium decimal(18,2) [note: 'The premium amount for the new permanent policy based on the selected payment frequency. Represents the recurring premium obligation for the converted policy.']
  new_policy_number varchar [note: 'The policy number of the newly issued permanent policy. Business identifier for the converted policy.']
  new_premium_mode varchar [note: 'The payment frequency selected for the new permanent policy. Indicates how often premiums will be paid on the converted policy.']
  new_table_rating varchar [note: 'Any table rating or substandard rating applied to the new permanent policy. Expressed as a percentage or table letter indicating additional mortality risk.']
  new_underwriting_class varchar [note: 'The underwriting risk class assigned to the new permanent policy. Determines the premium rate structure for the converted policy.']
  nigo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the conversion application was flagged as Not In Good Order due to missing or incomplete information. True if the application requires additional documentation or corrections.']
  nigo_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the specific reason the conversion application was flagged as Not In Good Order. Used for tracking application quality and processing efficiency.']
  original_face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Death benefit face amount of the original term policy at the time of conversion. Represents the coverage amount being converted.']
  original_issue_age varchar [note: 'The insureds age when the original term policy was issued. May be used for pricing if the conversion is on an original-age basis.']
  original_policy_number varchar [note: 'The policy number of the original term life policy being converted. Business identifier for the source policy.']
  original_product_type varchar [note: 'Type of term life insurance product being converted. Identifies the specific term product category of the source policy.']
  privilege_expiry_date date [note: 'The last date on which the policyholder can exercise the conversion privilege under the original term policy. After this date, the conversion right expires.']
  processing_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from conversion request to completion. Used for service level agreement monitoring and process efficiency analysis.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason or motivation for the conversion. Used for tracking conversion patterns and policyholder behavior analysis.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for conversion rejection if the request was denied. Used for tracking denial patterns and compliance reporting.']
  rejection_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the conversion request was rejected. Provides context for the rejection decision and supports customer communication.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the policyholder submitted the conversion request. Marks the beginning of the conversion transaction lifecycle.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source policy administration system from which the conversion record originated. Used for data lineage and system integration tracking.']
  source_transaction_code varchar [note: 'The unique transaction identifier from the source policy administration system. Used for reconciliation and traceability back to the originating system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this conversion record was last modified. Used for tracking changes and maintaining data currency.']

  Note: 'Tracks term-to-permanent policy conversion transactions where a term life policyholder exercises the contractual conversion privilege. Captures conversion date, original term policy, new permanent policy, conversion basis, and evidence requirements.'
}

Table underwriting.application {
  application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for application']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Application intake captures payer and billing setup preferences that establish the billing account for premium collection post-issue. Essential for new business processing workflow linking underwritin']
  acord_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.acord_form. Business justification: Every life insurance application is submitted on a specific ACORD form version (e.g., ACORD 103, 104). This FK is required for NIGO resolution (wrong form version), compliance audits, and state-specif']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agency, Brokerage General Agency (BGA), or Managing General Agent (MGA) through which the application was submitted.']
  automatic_binding_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.automatic_binding_limit. Business justification: During new business intake, the application is evaluated against the automatic binding limit to determine if it qualifies for STP processing. Referencing the specific binding limit record supports tre']
  hierarchy_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.hierarchy_node. Business justification: Commission override calculation and production reporting require the hierarchy node at time of application submission. Producers can move between nodes post-submission; capturing hierarchy_node_id at ']
  jurisdiction_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_license. Business justification: Applications can only be accepted in jurisdictions where the company holds a valid license. Direct FK enables pre-issuance compliance validation that the company is licensed to write business in the a']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Applications are written by specific legal entities (insurance carriers). Essential for statutory reporting, RBC calculations, consolidation, and regulatory filings. NAIC annual statements require app']
  owner_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the proposed policy owner if different from the applicant. Nullable for owner-insured applications.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life or annuity product applied for (e.g., Whole Life, Universal Life, Indexed Universal Life, Fixed Indexed Annuity, Single Premium Immediate Annuity).']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Applications must reference the exact plan version in effect at submission to apply correct underwriting class structure, rate tables, and regulatory forms. Plan versions have different underwriting e']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the writing agent, broker, or producer who submitted the application.']
  producer_product_auth_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.producer_product_auth. Business justification: Regulatory compliance and E&O audit trails require capturing which producer_product_auth record was active at application submission. Producer authorizations can be revoked or modified post-submission']
  reinsurer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance company assuming risk for this application under facultative or automatic reinsurance arrangements. Null if fully retained.']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.commission_schedule. Business justification: Applications must reference the commission schedule in effect at submission to calculate producer compensation. Critical for commission calculation, producer statements, and regulatory compliance (ens']
