[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: policy_face_amount|account_value|benefit_base|income_base|adl_daily_benefit|flat_benefit|percentage_of_premium — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  benefit_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the benefit payment amount (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Aligns with policy currency.']
  benefit_duration_months varchar [note: 'The maximum duration (in months) for which benefits are payable under this claim, as defined by the policy contract. Common durations include 24, 60, or to age 65 for DI; 24, 36, 60, or lifetime for LTC. Null indicates lifetime or indefinite duration.']
  benefit_end_date date [note: 'The date on which benefit payments ended or are scheduled to end. May be due to benefit duration expiration, recovery, death, policy termination, or other termination event. Null indicates benefits are ongoing.']
  benefit_start_date date [note: 'The date on which benefit payments commenced or are scheduled to commence. Typically follows the elimination period end date and claim approval.']
  care_setting varchar [note: 'The care setting in which Long-Term Care (LTC) services are being provided. Options include home health care, assisted living facility, nursing facility (skilled nursing), adult day care, hospice, or not applicable (for non-LTC claims). Determines benefit eligibility and payment rates.']
  claim_subtype varchar [note: 'Specific type of living benefit claim being processed. Distinguishes between Disability Income (DI), Long-Term Care (LTC), Accelerated Death Benefit (ADB), chronic illness, critical illness, terminal illness, Waiver of Premium (WP), Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB), Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB), Activities of Daily Living (ADL) benefit, and cognitive impairment claims. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: disability_income|long_term_care|accelerated_death_benefit|chronic_illness|critical_illness|terminal_illness|waiver_of_premium|gmib|gmwb|adl_benefit|cognitive_impairment — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cognitive_impairment_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the insured has been diagnosed with cognitive impairment requiring substantial supervision to protect health and safety. Cognitive impairment (e.g., Alzheimer\'s, dementia) is an alternative trigger for LTC benefits even without ADL impairment. Protected Health Information (PHI).']
  coordination_of_benefits_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the coordination of benefits arrangement, including the other payer(s) and the coordination method (e.g., Medicare primary, policy secondary; Medicaid coordination for LTC).']
  coordination_of_benefits_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether coordination of benefits (COB) with other insurance, Medicare, Medicaid, or government programs applies to this claim. True indicates benefits are coordinated to prevent duplicate payment; False indicates no coordination required.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this living benefit claim record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'ICD-10 or ICD-11 diagnosis code documenting the medical condition underlying the claim. Used for medical underwriting, claim validation, and regulatory reporting. Protected Health Information (PHI).']
  diagnosis_description varchar [note: 'Plain-language description of the medical diagnosis or condition that triggered the living benefit claim. Protected Health Information (PHI).']
  elimination_period_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the elimination period was satisfied and benefit payments became eligible to commence. Calculated as elimination_period_start_date plus elimination_period_days.']
  elimination_period_satisfied_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the elimination period has been fully satisfied. True indicates the waiting period is complete and benefits are eligible for payment; False indicates the elimination period is still in progress.']
  elimination_period_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the elimination period began, typically the triggering event date. Used to calculate when benefit payments should commence.']
  functional_capacity_evaluation_date date [note: 'The date on which a functional capacity evaluation (FCE) was performed to assess the insured\'s ability to perform work duties (for DI claims) or activities of daily living (for LTC claims). Used to support disability determination.']
  inflation_protection_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The annual compound inflation protection rate applied to Long-Term Care (LTC) benefits to adjust for cost-of-living increases. Common rates are 3% or 5% compound. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.03 for 3%). Zero or null indicates no inflation protection.']
  monthly_benefit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The calculated monthly benefit payment amount for this living benefit claim. Expressed in the claim currency. For non-monthly payment frequencies, this represents the normalized monthly equivalent.']
  next_eligibility_verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the next periodic eligibility verification (re-certification) is required to confirm ongoing qualification for living benefits. Common for DI and LTC claims to ensure continued impairment. Null if no periodic verification is required.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which living benefit payments are disbursed to the claimant. Common frequencies include monthly (most common for DI and LTC), quarterly, annual, lump sum (for ADB or critical illness), or as incurred (for reimbursement-based LTC).']
  recovery_date date [note: 'The date on which the insured recovered from the disability or condition, resulting in termination of living benefit payments. Null if no recovery has occurred or if claim terminated for other reasons.']
  reinsurance_recoverable_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount recoverable from reinsurers for this living benefit claim under applicable reinsurance treaties. Represents the ceded portion of claim payments. Zero if no reinsurance applies.']
  reserve_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The actuarial reserve amount established for this living benefit claim to cover estimated future benefit payments. Updated periodically based on claim experience and actuarial assumptions. Used for statutory and GAAP financial reporting.']
  social_security_offset_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monthly amount by which the disability income benefit is reduced due to Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI) benefits received by the insured. Many DI policies include SSDI offset provisions to prevent over-insurance. Zero if no offset applies.']
  termination_reason_code varchar [note: 'The reason why living benefit payments were terminated. Options include recovery from disability/illness, death of insured, benefit maximum reached, policy lapse, fraud determination, voluntary termination by claimant, or not terminated (benefits ongoing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: recovery|death|benefit_maximum_reached|policy_lapse|fraud|voluntary_termination|not_terminated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  total_benefit_paid_to_date decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative total of all benefit payments made on this living benefit claim from inception to the current date. Expressed in the claim currency. Used for benefit maximum tracking and financial reporting.']
  triggering_event varchar [note: 'The specific event or condition that triggered eligibility for this living benefit claim. Examples include disability onset, terminal illness diagnosis, chronic illness diagnosis, critical illness diagnosis, Activities of Daily Living (ADL) impairment, cognitive impairment, premium waiver trigger condition, or annuity guarantee trigger event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: disability_onset|terminal_diagnosis|chronic_diagnosis|critical_diagnosis|adl_impairment|cognitive_impairment|premium_waiver_trigger|annuity_guarantee_trigger — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  triggering_event_date date [note: 'The date on which the triggering event occurred (e.g., date of disability onset, date of terminal diagnosis, date ADL impairment began). Used to determine benefit eligibility and elimination period start.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this living benefit claim record was last modified. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance. Updated on any change to claim status, benefit amounts, or other attributes.']
  waiver_of_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monthly or annual premium amount being waived under a Waiver of Premium (WP) rider due to the insured\'s disability or qualifying condition. Represents the premium obligation that the insurer is absorbing. Zero for non-WP claims.']

  Note: 'Consolidated claim detail record for all living-insured benefit claims including Disability Income (DI), Long-Term Care (LTC), Accelerated Death Benefit (ADB), chronic/critical illness riders, GMIB/GMWB annuity guarantees, and Waiver-of-Premium (WP) claims. Captures claim subtype, triggering event (disability onset, terminal/chronic/critical illness diagnosis, ADL impairment, cognitive impairment), elimination period tracking, benefit calculation basis, payment frequency and duration, ongoing eligibility verification requirements, medical documentation (ICD-10 codes, APS, functional capacity evaluations), disability definition applied (own-occ, any-occ, modified own-occ), care setting (for LTC: home health, assisted living, nursing facility), Social Security offset (for DI), coordination of benefits with Medicare/Medicaid (for LTC), tax treatment (IRC 101(g) for ADB), waiver trigger and premium amount waived (for WP), inflation protection adjustment (for LTC), and termination/recovery events. Extends the core claim record with type-specific attributes while sharing the common claim lifecycle. SSOT for all non-death-benefit claim detail within the claims domain — replaces standalone DI, LTC, and waiver-of-premium claim records.'
}

Table claims.claim_payment {
  claim_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the claim payment transaction. Primary key for the claim payment record.']
  adjudication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claims.adjudication. Business justification: Each claim payment should reference the adjudication that authorized it. The adjudication record contains the benefit_amount_approved and settlement_method, and the payment executes that decision. Thi']
  cession_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.cession. Business justification: When claims are paid, finance teams must calculate reinsurance recoverables against specific cessions to determine net claim cost. Payment processing systems require cession identification for recover']
  claim_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent claim for which this payment is being made. Links payment to the adjudicated claim record.']
  claimant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claims.claimant. Business justification: Payment should directly reference the claimant receiving the payment for in-domain tracking. Currently claim_payment has payee_id → commission.payee (cross-domain for payment processing), but should a']
  db_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claims.db_calculation. Business justification: Death benefit payments must be directly traceable to the specific DB calculation record that determined the net_db_payable amount. While adjudication.db_calculation_id provides an indirect path, a dir']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the insurance policy under which this claim payment is made. Enables direct policy-to-payment traceability.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Every claim payment must be recorded in the general ledger for financial reporting, statutory compliance, and audit trail. This links payment execution to the specific GL entry that books cash disburs']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Claim payments must be attributed to a legal entity for statutory cash flow reporting, intercompany settlement, and prompt payment compliance tracking by domicile state. Insurers operating across mult']
  living_benefit_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claims.living_benefit_claim. Business justification: claim_payment disburses funds for all claim types including living benefit claims (DI, LTC, GMIB, waiver-of-premium). A direct FK from claim_payment to living_benefit_claim enables direct traceability']
  original_payment_claim_payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original payment record if this is a reissue. Null for initial payment issuance.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Social Security Number (SSN) or Employer Identification Number (EIN) of the payee. Required for IRS Form 1099 reporting.']
  payment_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Claim payments generate formal tax documents (1099-R) and settlement agreements stored in the document domain. Linking claim_payment to its payment/tax document supports IRS 1099 reporting workflows, ']
  payout_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.payout_schedule. Business justification: When a death benefit claim is settled via installment/annuity option, the claim_payment stream is governed by a payout_schedule. Claims operations must reference the payout_schedule to validate paymen']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Payment processing requires product-specific settlement options, payment frequency rules, tax treatment codes, and 1099 reporting requirements. Product plan defines available payment methods and tax q']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: State prompt payment laws are regulatory obligations that govern claim payment timing and interest penalties. claim_payment tracks payment_date and payment_effective_date against these deadlines. This']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable annuity death benefit and living benefit payments require liquidating separate account units to fund the payment. Claims operations must reference the specific separate account to initiate un']
  surrender_charge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.surrender_charge. Business justification: Annuity death and terminal illness claims trigger surrender charge waivers. claim_payment must reference the surrender_charge record to document which waiver provision applied and the waived amount, s']
  beneficiary_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total claim benefit allocated to this specific payee in split-benefit scenarios. Sum across all payments for a claim equals 100%.']
  cleared_date date [note: 'Date the payment cleared the banking system and funds were successfully transferred to payee account. Null if not yet cleared.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  direct_deposit_authorization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payee has provided written authorization for direct deposit. Required for ACH compliance.']
  escheatment_referral_date date [note: 'Date the unclaimed payment was referred to state escheatment process. Null if payment has not reached escheatment status.']
  escheatment_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code to which unclaimed funds were escheated. Determined by payee last known address or policy domicile.']
  federal_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of federal income tax withheld from the payment per payee election or mandatory withholding rules. Reported on Form 1099-R.']
  form_1099_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment is reportable on IRS Form 1099-R or 1099-MISC. Drives year-end tax reporting workflow.']
  form_1099_type varchar [note: 'Specific IRS Form 1099 type applicable to this payment. 1099-R for distributions from retirement accounts, 1099-MISC for other payments.']
  gross_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total payment amount before any withholdings or deductions. Represents the full benefit amount approved for disbursement.']
  net_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount disbursed to payee after all withholdings and deductions. This is the actual amount received by the beneficiary.']
  payee_bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number for electronic funds transfer (EFT) or ACH payment. Encrypted at rest per PCI-DSS requirements.']
  payee_bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution holding the payee\'s account. Used for payment verification and audit trail.']
  payee_bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit ABA routing transit number for the payee\'s financial institution. Required for ACH and wire transfers.']
  payment_approval_date date [note: 'Date the payment was approved by claims examiner or automated adjudication system. Precedes payment issuance.']
  payment_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment. Typically USD for US-domiciled policies but supports multi-currency scenarios.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date the payment was issued or disbursed to the payee. Used for accounting recognition and regulatory reporting.']
  payment_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the payment is effective for benefit calculation purposes. May differ from payment date for backdated settlements.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency of recurring payments for installment or periodic benefit disbursements. Null for lump sum payments.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument or mechanism used to disburse the payment. Determines processing workflow and settlement timeline.']
  payment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the payment transaction. Captures special handling instructions or payment-specific context.']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the payment transaction. Used for customer communication and payment tracking.']
  payment_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of this payment within the claim. Supports tracking of multiple partial payments or installment disbursements for a single claim.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction. Tracks payment from issuance through final disposition including returned or escheated funds.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment based on the benefit type being disbursed. Aligns with claim type and benefit structure.']
  reissue_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment is a reissue of a previous payment. True if replacing a voided, returned, or stopped payment.']
  settlement_option varchar [note: 'Executed settlement election chosen by beneficiary or claimant. Records how the benefit is being paid out over time.']
  state_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of state income tax withheld from the payment per payee election or state withholding requirements.']
  stop_payment_date date [note: 'Date the stop payment order was placed. Null if no stop payment has been requested.']
  stop_payment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a stop payment order has been placed on this payment. Typically used for lost or stolen checks.']
  stop_payment_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for placing stop payment order. Common reasons include lost check, incorrect payee, or duplicate payment.']
  tax_withholding_election varchar [note: 'Payee\'s election for federal and state income tax withholding. Determines withholding calculation method.']
  tax_withholding_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Custom withholding percentage elected by payee if custom withholding option was selected. Null if not applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment record was last modified. Tracks all changes to payment status, amounts, or payee information.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for every monetary disbursement made in settlement or partial settlement of a claim. Captures payment amount, payment date, payment method (check, EFT/ACH, wire transfer), payee identity and banking details, tax withholding amount (federal and state), tax withholding election, 1099-R or 1099-MISC reporting flag, payment status (issued, cleared, voided, returned, escheatment-referred), stop-payment flag, reissue reference, and direct deposit authorization. For settlement execution: records the executed settlement option (lump sum, installment, life income, retained asset account), executed payment frequency, and beneficiary payment allocation for split-benefit scenarios. Supports periodic DI/LTC benefit payments with payment schedule tracking and annuity domain handoff for life income settlement options requiring annuitization. SSOT for all claim disbursement transactions and payment execution within the claims domain. Note: the adjudication record captures the decision-time settlement election; this product records the actual payment execution.'
}

Table claims.claim_reserve {
  claim_reserve_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the claim reserve record. Primary key for the claim reserve entity.']
  adjudication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claims.adjudication. Business justification: Claim reserves are directly impacted by adjudication decisions — an approval triggers reserve release, a denial triggers reserve closure, and a partial approval triggers reserve adjustment. A direct F']
  cession_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.reinsurance_cession. Business justification: Actuarial reserve reporting and RBC credit calculations require linking each claim reserve to the specific cession (not just the treaty) to compute the ceded reserve amount and reinsurance recoverable']
  claim_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the claim for which this reserve is established. Links to the parent claim record.']
  cohort_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.cohort_definition. Business justification: Under LDTI (ASC 944) and IFRS17, claim reserves must be measured and reported at the cohort level. Linking claim_reserve to cohort_definition enables period-end GAAP reporting that reconciles claim re']
  contract_valuation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.contract_valuation. Business justification: Claim reserves for annuity contracts must be reconciled against contract_valuation (statutory_reserve, gaap_reserve, death_benefit_amount, cash_surrender_value). Actuarial reserve adequacy reviews and']
  gaap_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gaap_reserve. Business justification: LDTI (ASC 944) requires reconciliation of claim-level reserves to GAAP reserve line items. Direct FK supports GAAP reserve adequacy testing, auditor traceability from claims reserve to finance GAAP re']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policy under which the claim and reserve are recorded.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Claim reserves must be recorded in the general ledger via journal entries. The claim_reserve product tracks actuarial reserve estimates, but the actual GL posting is a separate finance domain event. A']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Claim reserves must be attributed to a legal entity for statutory reserve filings (SAP Exhibit 11), RBC C-2 insurance risk calculations, and state DOI reporting. Multi-entity insurers require legal en']
  living_benefit_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claims.living_benefit_claim. Business justification: Living benefit claims (DI, LTC) require long-duration case reserves that must be managed over the entire benefit period, which can span years or decades. A direct FK from claim_reserve to living_benef']
  pbr_model_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.pbr_model_segment. Business justification: PBR (VM-20/VM-21) requires claim reserves to be associated with the model segment that governs their reserve basis, credibility weighting, and mortality assumptions. Linking claim_reserve to pbr_model']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Reserve calculations require product-specific mortality tables, interest rate assumptions, reserve basis, and valuation methods. Product performance monitoring and reserve adequacy testing aggregate r']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Claim reserves are backed by investment portfolios for ALM duration matching and RBC C3 interest rate risk calculations. Statutory reserve adequacy testing requires linking reserves to supporting asse']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Statutory reserve requirements are regulatory obligations governing reserve adequacy, methodology, and actuarial sign-off. claim_reserve.reserve_basis and actuarial_signoff_status are directly governe']
  reserve_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.reserve_calculation. Business justification: Claims reserves are inputs to actuarial reserve calculations for statutory/GAAP reporting, reserve adequacy testing, and regulatory filings. Actuaries reconcile case reserves to valuation reserves qua']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable annuity and variable life claim reserves are held in and valued against separate accounts. Actuarial reserve adequacy testing, SAP/GAAP reserve calculations, and RBC C-3 charges require direc']
  treaty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.treaty. Business justification: Actuaries calculating claim reserves must identify applicable reinsurance treaties to properly account for ceded reserves and reinsurance recoverables in net reserve amounts. Required for Schedule S s']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: Statutory/GAAP reporting requires claim reserves to be reconciled directly to the actuarial valuation run that produced them. Actuaries and finance teams run period-end reserve reconciliation reports ']
  actuarial_signoff_date date [note: 'The date on which the appointed actuary formally approved the reserve estimate, certifying its adequacy and compliance with applicable standards.']
  actuarial_signoff_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the reserve estimate by the appointed actuary or actuarial team. PENDING: awaiting actuarial review; APPROVED: actuary has signed off on the reserve; REJECTED: reserve estimate rejected and requires recalculation; CONDITIONAL: approved with conditions or qualifications.']
  catastrophic_event_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific catastrophic event (e.g., COVID-19, Hurricane Katrina, 9/11) for bulk reserve tracking and reporting. Null for non-catastrophic reserves.']
  catastrophic_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reserve is associated with a catastrophic event (e.g., pandemic, natural disaster, terrorism) requiring bulk or supplemental reserves. True if catastrophic; False otherwise.']
  confidence_interval_lower decimal(18,2) [note: 'The lower bound of the confidence interval (typically 75th or 80th percentile) for the IBNR estimate, representing a more conservative reserve level. Used for risk assessment and capital adequacy analysis.']
  confidence_interval_upper decimal(18,2) [note: 'The upper bound of the confidence interval (typically 90th or 95th percentile) for the IBNR estimate, representing a more adverse reserve scenario. Used for stress testing and capital planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this reserve record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  current_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current outstanding reserve amount after all adjustments, payments, and releases. Represents the best estimate of remaining liability as of the valuation date.']
  discount_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The interest rate used to discount future claim payments to present value for certain reserve calculations, particularly under PBR and IFRS 17. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.035 for 3.5%).']
  estimation_methodology varchar [note: 'The actuarial or statistical method used to calculate the reserve. Examples: chain-ladder development, Bornhuetter-Ferguson, frequency-severity, expected loss ratio, case-based estimation. Applicable primarily to IBNR and bulk reserves. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: chain_ladder|bornhuetter_ferguson|frequency_severity|expected_loss_ratio|case_based|cape_cod|benktander|grossing_up — promote to reference product]']
  ibnr_development_variance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The difference between the current period IBNR estimate and the prior period estimate, adjusted for claims emerged and paid. Positive variance indicates adverse development; negative indicates favorable development.']
  initial_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original reserve amount established when the claim was first reported or the IBNR estimate was first recorded. Represents the initial actuarial or claims examiner estimate of liability.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the reserve adequacy was reviewed and confirmed or adjusted by a claims examiner or actuary.']
  loss_development_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'The age-to-age development factor applied in the reserve calculation, representing the expected ratio of ultimate losses to reported losses at the current development stage. Used in chain-ladder and related IBNR methodologies.']
  net_amount_at_risk decimal(18,2) [note: 'The difference between the death benefit and the account value or cash surrender value at the time of reserve establishment. Represents the insurance company\'s pure mortality risk exposure net of policyholder funds.']
  net_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The reserve amount net of reinsurance recoverables, representing the company\'s retained liability after reinsurance. Calculated as current_reserve_amount minus reinsurance_recoverable_amount.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next reserve adequacy review. Used for workflow management and compliance with periodic review requirements.']
  prior_period_ibnr_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The IBNR reserve amount from the previous valuation period, used for comparison and development analysis. Enables tracking of reserve adequacy and emergence patterns over time.']
  reinsurance_recoverable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the reserve that is expected to be recovered from reinsurers under ceded reinsurance treaties. Represents the reinsurer\'s share of the claim liability.']
  reserve_adequacy_indicator varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the current reserve is sufficient to cover expected claim payments. ADEQUATE: reserve meets actuarial best estimate; DEFICIENT: reserve is insufficient and requires strengthening; REDUNDANT: reserve exceeds expected liability; UNDER_REVIEW: adequacy assessment in progress.']
  reserve_basis varchar [note: 'Accounting or regulatory basis under which the reserve is calculated. SAP: Statutory Accounting Principles; GAAP: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (ASC 944, LDTI); IFRS: International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS 17); TAX: tax basis reserves; PBR: Principle-Based Reserving (VM-20/VM-21).']
  reserve_change_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net change in reserve amount since the last valuation or adjustment. Positive values indicate reserve strengthening; negative values indicate reserve release.']
  reserve_change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the most recent reserve adjustment. NEW_INFORMATION: new medical or investigative findings; PAYMENT: partial payment reducing reserve; SETTLEMENT: claim settled; ADEQUACY_REVIEW: periodic review adjustment; ACTUARIAL_UPDATE: updated loss development factors or methodology; CLAIM_CLOSURE: claim closed and reserve released; REOPENING: claim reopened requiring reserve re-establishment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_information|payment|settlement|adequacy_review|actuarial_update|claim_closure|reopening — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reserve_change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative explaining the business rationale for the reserve change, providing additional context beyond the reason code.']
  reserve_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the reserve amounts (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP).']
  reserve_establishment_date date [note: 'The date on which the reserve was first established in the system. For case reserves, typically the FNOL or claim intake date; for IBNR, the estimation period end date.']
  reserve_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, assumptions, or qualifications related to the reserve calculation or adequacy assessment. Used for documentation and audit trail purposes.']
  reserve_release_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of reserve released upon claim closure or settlement. Represents the difference between the final reserve and actual claim payments, contributing to reserve development gains or losses.']
  reserve_release_date date [note: 'The date on which the reserve was released back to surplus, typically upon final claim settlement or closure. Null for open reserves.']
  reserve_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reserve. OPEN: active reserve; CLOSED: reserve closed upon claim settlement; RELEASED: reserve released back to surplus; ADJUSTED: reserve amount modified; UNDER_REVIEW: reserve under adequacy review.']
  reserve_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reserve. CASE: individual claim case reserve; IBNR: Incurred But Not Reported portfolio reserve; BULK: supplemental reserves for catastrophic events or emerging trends; ALAE: Allocated Loss Adjustment Expense; ULAE: Unallocated Loss Adjustment Expense; SUPPLEMENTAL: additional reserves for specific risk exposures.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this reserve record was last modified. Audit field for tracking reserve adjustments and changes over time.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The as-of date for which the reserve amount is calculated and reported. Typically month-end, quarter-end, or year-end for IBNR; claim event date or review date for case reserves.']

  Note: 'Actuarial reserve record tracking claim-level case reserves and portfolio-level IBNR (Incurred But Not Reported) estimates for claims liability management. CASE RESERVES: captures initial and current reserve amounts per open claim, reserve change history with reason codes, reserve adequacy review dates, reserving examiner, and reserve release upon claim closure. IBNR ESTIMATES: captures estimation period (month-end, quarter-end, year-end), estimation methodology (chain-ladder development, Bornhuetter-Ferguson, frequency-severity), estimated IBNR amount by claim type (DB, DI, LTC, living benefit), confidence intervals, loss development factors, prior period IBNR comparison, estimating actuary ID, and actuarial sign-off/approval status. BULK RESERVES: captures supplemental reserves for catastrophic events or emerging trends. Tracks SAP and GAAP reserve basis and supports LDTI (ASC 944), PBR (VM-20/VM-21), and ORSA reserve adequacy assessments. Feeds actuarial and finance domains for statutory reporting (Annual Statement Schedule P equivalent for life). SSOT for all claim-level case reserves, portfolio-level IBNR estimates, and bulk reserve balances within the claims domain.'
}

  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level of the document content. Determines access controls and handling procedures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this document record was first created in the system. Supports audit trails and data lineage tracking.']

  document_source varchar [note: 'Origin or provider of the document. Identifies the party who submitted or generated the document for claims processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: claimant|beneficiary|physician|hospital|funeral_home|attorney|agent|third_party_administrator|reinsurer — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  document_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the document within the claims adjudication workflow. Tracks document lifecycle from receipt through final disposition.']

  fraud_indicator_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the fraud indicators detected in the document. Supports fraud investigation and special investigation unit (SIU) referral.']

  pii_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document contains Personally Identifiable Information. True if PII is present, requiring enhanced data protection measures.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date the document was rejected. Triggers claimant notification and resubmission workflow.']
  required_for_adjudication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document is mandatory for claim adjudication. True if the claim cannot be processed without this document.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date the document was reviewed by the claims examiner or adjudicator. Tracks processing timeline and SLA compliance.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'Method or channel through which the document was submitted. Supports channel analytics and digital transformation tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mail|fax|email|web_portal|mobile_app|agent_portal|api — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this document record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date the document was successfully verified. Marks completion of the verification workflow.']
  verification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document requires independent verification from the source. True if verification is needed before acceptance.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the document verification process. Tracks whether independent verification has been completed and the outcome.']

  Note: 'Registry of all documents associated with a claim throughout its lifecycle. Tracks document type (death certificate, APS, autopsy report, police report, proof of identity, beneficiary designation form, claimant statement, medical records, court order, letters testamentary, HIPAA authorization, tax withholding forms), document receipt date, document source, document status (received, reviewed, accepted, rejected, pending), NIGO reason if rejected, document completeness checklist status, and document storage reference linking to the document management domain. Manages the document completeness requirements for adjudication readiness. SSOT for all claim-related document tracking within the claims domain.'
}

Table claims.claim_status_history {
  claim_status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each claim status history record. Primary key for the immutable audit trail of claim lifecycle events.']
  adjudication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claims.adjudication. Business justification: Status history events related to adjudication (adjudication completed, decision rendered, approval issued) should reference the specific adjudication record. This provides granular audit trail linking']
  appeal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claims.appeal. Business justification: Status history events related to appeal (appeal filed, appeal under review, appeal decision rendered) should reference the specific appeal record. This provides audit trail of appeal lifecycle events.']
  claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent claim record. Links this status history event to the specific claim being tracked.']
  insurance_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this activity to a document in the document management system. Enables retrieval of supporting documentation for audit and dispute resolution.']
  activity_note_text varchar [note: 'Free-form text note entered by the examiner or system describing the activity, decision rationale, or communication summary. Critical for claim file narrative and regulatory exam defense.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Type of activity or event recorded in this history entry. Distinguishes between status transitions, examiner notes, communications, diary entries, Service Level Agreement (SLA) milestones, and document receipt events.']
  attorney_client_privilege_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this activity record is protected by attorney-client privilege. Used to restrict disclosure during regulatory examinations and litigation discovery processes.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the status change or activity. Maps to internal reason code reference tables for consistent categorization.']
  change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable explanation of the reason for the status change or activity. Provides business context for audit review and dispute resolution.']
  changed_by_role varchar [note: 'Business role or job function of the user at the time of the event (e.g., Claims Examiner, Senior Adjudicator, Special Investigations Unit (SIU) Investigator, System Administrator). Supports role-based audit analysis.']
  changed_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who performed the status change or activity. Denormalized for audit reporting and dispute resolution without requiring user table joins.']
  communication_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the communication occurred (phone, email, postal mail, fax, customer portal, in-person). Supports channel effectiveness analysis and regulatory documentation requirements.']
  communication_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external party involved in the communication. Supports complete claim file documentation for regulatory exams and litigation defense.']
  communication_party_type varchar [note: 'Type of external party involved in the communication (claimant, beneficiary, agent, attorney, attending physician, investigator, reinsurer, other). Enables stakeholder engagement tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: claimant|beneficiary|agent|attorney|physician|investigator|reinsurer|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  communication_type varchar [note: 'Direction of communication activity: inbound (received from claimant, beneficiary, agent, or third party) or outbound (sent to external party). Applicable when activity_type is communication.']
  confidential_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this activity record contains attorney-client privileged information or Special Investigations Unit (SIU) sensitive content. Controls access and disclosure in regulatory exams and litigation discovery.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this status history record was inserted into the data warehouse. Distinct from status_change_timestamp which reflects the business event time. Supports data lineage and Extract Transform Load (ETL) auditing.']
  diary_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the diary entry has been completed or resolved. False indicates an open diary item requiring follow-up action.']
  diary_due_date date [note: 'Follow-up date for diary entries. Indicates when the examiner should review the claim for pending items or next actions. Supports workflow management and Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of document received or generated (e.g., Attending Physician Statement (APS), death certificate, beneficiary identification, investigative report). Applicable when activity_type is document_received.']
  event_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering of status change and activity events within a claim lifecycle. Enables chronological reconstruction of claim history.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The claim status after this event. For non-status-change activities, this field contains the current status at the time of the activity. Supports state-at-time queries.']
  prior_status varchar [note: 'The claim status immediately before this event. Null for the initial claim submission event. Enables tracking of status transitions and workflow progression.']
  siu_sensitive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this activity is part of a fraud investigation by the Special Investigations Unit (SIU). Restricts access to authorized fraud investigators and legal counsel.']
  sla_actual_date date [note: 'Actual date when this Service Level Agreement (SLA) milestone was completed. Compared against target date to measure compliance and identify process bottlenecks.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this Service Level Agreement (SLA) milestone was met within the target timeframe. False indicates a breach of service level commitments or regulatory prompt payment requirements.']
  sla_milestone_type varchar [note: 'Type of Service Level Agreement (SLA) milestone reached (e.g., initial contact, acknowledgment sent, decision rendered, payment issued). Supports compliance tracking for state prompt payment laws.']
  sla_target_date date [note: 'Target date by which this Service Level Agreement (SLA) milestone should be completed. Used to calculate compliance with state Department of Insurance (DOI) prompt payment requirements (typically 30-day adjudication windows).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that created this status history record (e.g., Guidewire ClaimCenter, Majesco Claims). Supports data lineage tracking in multi-system environments.']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier from the source operational system. Enables traceability back to the originating system for reconciliation and troubleshooting.']
  status_change_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the status change or activity event. Supports day-level reporting and aggregation for state Department of Insurance (DOI) regulatory reporting.']
  status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the status change or activity event occurred. Critical for Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance monitoring and regulatory prompt payment law tracking.']
  system_generated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this activity was automatically generated by the claims system (e.g., automated Service Level Agreement (SLA) milestone tracking, workflow triggers) versus manually entered by a user.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit trail of every status transition and significant activity event for a claim throughout its lifecycle. Records status change events (submitted, under review, pended, investigation, approved, denied, payment issued, closed, reopened), prior and new status, change timestamp, changed-by user ID, change reason code, activity type (status change, examiner note, communication, diary entry, SLA milestone), note text for activity entries, confidentiality flag (attorney-client privilege, SIU sensitive), and associated references. Enables SLA compliance monitoring, regulatory reporting of claim processing timelines, dispute resolution, and complete claim file narrative for audits. Supports state DOI prompt payment law compliance tracking (typically 30-day adjudication windows). SSOT for claim lifecycle audit trail, activity log, and examiner notes within the claims domain.'
}

Table claims.appeal {
  appeal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the claim appeal record. Primary key.']

Currently appeal has claim_id but not the specific adjudication_id. A claim may have multiple adjudications (initial, reconsideration)']
  claim_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying claim that is being appealed.']
  claimant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the claimant who filed the appeal.']
  contestability_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claims.contestability_review. Business justification: Appeals frequently arise directly from contestability review outcomes — when a contestability review results in a rescission recommendation or claim denial based on material misrepresentation, the cla']
  exam_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.exam_finding. Business justification: Market conduct exam findings often identify improper claim denials, triggering appeals. Insurance operations track which appeals resulted from exam findings for remediation tracking and regulatory rep']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policy under which the appealed claim was filed.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Appeal reviews require product plan specifications to evaluate whether original adjudication correctly applied policy provisions, benefit calculations, and settlement options. Essential for appeal dec']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: External review mandates and appeal rights disclosure requirements are state regulatory obligations. appeal.doi_complaint_filed_flag and exhaustion_status reference these requirements. This FK links a']
  appeal_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number assigned to the appeal for tracking and correspondence.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the appeal: filed, under review, pending additional evidence, decision rendered, closed, withdrawn, or escalated to higher authority. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: filed|under_review|pending_evidence|decision_rendered|closed|withdrawn|escalated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  appeal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the appeal mechanism: internal administrative review, external independent review, Department of Insurance (DOI) complaint, litigation, arbitration, or mediation.']
  arbitration_case_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the arbitration body or forum.']
  arbitration_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claimant has initiated binding arbitration proceedings as an alternative dispute resolution mechanism.']
  arbitration_filing_date date [note: 'Date on which the arbitration claim was filed.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The date on which this employee was formally assigned to the appeal review committee. Used to track committee formation timeline and workload distribution.']
  basis varchar [note: 'Claimant\'s stated reason or grounds for contesting the original claim decision, including factual, legal, or procedural objections.']
  claimant_representative_contact varchar [note: 'Contact phone number or email address for the claimant\'s representative.']

  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the appeal was formally closed, either after decision communication, withdrawal, or exhaustion of all remedies.']
  committee_position varchar [note: 'The formal position held by this employee on the appeal review committee. Determines decision-making authority and procedural responsibilities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the appeal record was first created in the claims management system.']
  deadline_date date [note: 'Regulatory or contractual deadline by which the appeal decision must be rendered to remain compliant with state DOI or ERISA timelines.']
  decision varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the appeal: upheld (original decision stands), overturned (claim approved), modified (partial adjustment), remanded (sent back for re-review), or withdrawn by claimant.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date on which the final appeal decision was rendered and communicated to the claimant.']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation and justification for the appeal decision, including reference to policy terms, evidence reviewed, and regulatory standards applied.']
  doi_complaint_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claimant filed a formal complaint with the state Department of Insurance regarding the claim or appeal.']
  doi_complaint_filing_date date [note: 'Date on which the claimant filed the complaint with the state Department of Insurance.']
  doi_complaint_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the state DOI to the complaint filed by the claimant.']
  exhaustion_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the claimant has exhausted all internal and external appeal remedies as required before pursuing litigation or regulatory complaint.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date on which the claimant formally filed the appeal with the insurer or regulatory body.']
  filing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the appeal was received and logged into the system, used for regulatory timeline compliance.']
  hours_spent decimal(6,2) [note: 'The number of hours this employee spent reviewing the appeal case, evidence, and documentation. Used for workload tracking and cost allocation.']
  litigation_case_number varchar [note: 'Court-assigned case number for the lawsuit filed by the claimant.']
  litigation_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claimant has initiated legal proceedings (lawsuit) against the insurer related to the claim or appeal.']
  litigation_filing_date date [note: 'Date on which the lawsuit was filed in court.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes, observations, and case management details related to the appeal process.']
  recusal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this employee recused themselves from the appeal review due to conflict of interest, prior involvement in the claim, or other ethical considerations. Critical for regulatory compliance and audit defense.']
  recusal_reason varchar [note: 'Documented explanation for why the employee recused themselves from this appeal review. Required for compliance and governance audit trail when recusal_flag is true.']
  review_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the appeal review was completed and a decision was reached.']
  review_date date [note: 'The date on which this employee conducted their review of the appeal. Used for regulatory timeline compliance and audit trail.']
  review_role varchar [note: 'The specific role or capacity in which this employee is participating in the appeal review committee. Determines voting authority and area of expertise contribution.']
  review_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal review of the appeal commenced.']
  reviewing_committee_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal committee or external independent review organization (IRO) responsible for the appeal decision.']
  revised_claim_amount_approved decimal(18,2) [note: 'Updated claim benefit amount approved as a result of the appeal decision, if the appeal was overturned or modified.']
  revised_claim_amount_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual additional payment disbursed to the claimant following a successful appeal.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the appeal record was last modified, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  vote_outcome varchar [note: 'This committee member\'s individual vote on the appeal decision. Captured for regulatory compliance and internal governance audit trail.']

  Note: 'Tracks the formal appeal process when a claimant contests a claim denial or partial payment decision. Captures appeal type (internal administrative review, external independent review, DOI complaint, litigation, arbitration), appeal filing date, appeal deadline, appeal basis stated by claimant, additional evidence submitted, reviewing examiner or committee, appeal decision, appeal decision date, outcome (upheld, overturned, modified), and appeal exhaustion status. Manages the regulatory appeal timelines mandated by state DOI regulations and ERISA for group products. Feeds compliance domain for DOI complaint reporting. SSOT for all claim appeal and dispute resolution activity within the claims domain.'
}

  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_assessment. Business justification: Contestability reviews evaluate whether misrepresentation impacted underwriting decision. Reviewers need access to complete risk assessment including medical risk classification, APS review outcomes, ']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: State regulations define contestability period lengths (state_regulation.contestability_period_months). contestability_review.contestability_period_days is directly governed by the applicable state re']
  underwriting_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: Contestability reviews examine whether the insured was correctly classified at issue. The underwriting_class defines eligibility_criteria_summary, mortality_loading_factor, and table_rating directly r']
  uw_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Contestability reviews are the core business process comparing claim facts against original application question responses, disclosed medical conditions, and applicant statements. Rescission decisions']
  uw_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.decision. Business justification: Contestability adjudication requires the original underwriting decision record to evaluate what rating, rationale, and exclusions were applied. Reviewers must determine if material misrepresentation a']
  actual_fact_discovered varchar [note: 'The true fact or condition discovered during investigation that contradicts the applicant response.']
  appeal_deadline_date date [note: 'Final date by which the beneficiary may file an appeal of the contestability review decision.']
  appeal_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the beneficiary or policyholder has filed an appeal of the rescission decision.']
  applicant_response varchar [note: 'The answer provided by the applicant on the insurance application that is under contestability review.']
  application_question_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific question number on the insurance application where misrepresentation occurred.']
  aps_reviewed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether attending physician statements were obtained and reviewed as part of the contestability investigation.']
  beneficiary_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the beneficiary or claimant was formally notified of the contestability review outcome and rescission decision.']
  claim_denial_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Death benefit or claim amount that is being denied as a result of the rescission decision.']
  contestability_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days elapsed between policy issue date and date of loss, used to confirm contestability eligibility (typically within 730 days).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the contestability review record was first created in the claims management system.']
  date_of_loss date [note: 'Date of the insured event (death or disability) that triggered the claim under review.']
  evidence_source varchar [note: 'Primary source of evidence that substantiated the material misrepresentation finding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: aps|mib|prescription_database|dmv_records|criminal_records|financial_records|social_media|witness_statement|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  legal_review_date date [note: 'Date when legal counsel completed their review of the contestability case.']
  legal_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contestability case requires review and approval by the legal department before final decision.']
  material_misrepresentation_found_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigation identified material misrepresentation or concealment of facts in the application that would have affected underwriting.']
  medical_director_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case was escalated to the medical director for clinical assessment of the misrepresentation materiality.']
  mib_check_performed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Medical Information Bureau database check was conducted during the review.']
  mib_discrepancy_found_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MIB check revealed information inconsistent with the application.']
  misrepresentation_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of material misrepresentation discovered during the contestability review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical_history|tobacco_use|occupation|hazardous_activities|financial_information|criminal_history|other_insurance|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  misrepresentation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the specific facts, omissions, or false statements identified as material misrepresentation.']
  policy_issue_date date [note: 'Original issue date of the policy, used to determine if claim falls within the two-year contestability period.']
  premium_refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of premiums paid that will be refunded to the policyholder or estate if rescission is approved.']
  premium_refund_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the premium refund amount.']
  rescission_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision rendered by the company regarding policy rescission following contestability review.']
  rescission_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the policy is deemed void ab initio (from the beginning) if rescission is approved, typically the original policy issue date.']
  rescission_recommended_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigator recommends rescinding the policy contract based on material misrepresentation findings.']
  review_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the contestability review was finalized and a decision was rendered.']
  review_initiated_date date [note: 'Date when the contestability review was formally opened and investigation began.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field capturing investigator notes, findings summary, and key decision rationale for the contestability review.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the contestability review case, used for tracking and reference.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contestability review process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initiated|in_progress|pending_legal_review|pending_medical_review|completed|rescission_approved|rescission_denied|closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  review_trigger_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason or business rule that triggered the contestability review investigation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automatic_contestability|fraud_indicator|mib_discrepancy|aps_discrepancy|underwriting_red_flag|siu_referral|large_claim_amount|early_death — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  state_doi_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the state insurance department was notified of the rescission decision.']
  state_doi_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether state insurance department notification is required for this rescission action per regulatory requirements.']
  underwriting_decision_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of how the discovered misrepresentation would have affected the original underwriting decision had the true facts been known.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the contestability review record was last modified, used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  within_contestability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claim occurred within the statutory two-year contestability period from policy issue date.']

  Note: 'Tracks contestability period reviews for claims filed within the two-year contestability period. Captures material misrepresentation findings, rescission decisions, and premium refund calculations.'
}

Table billing.premium_schedule {
  premium_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the premium schedule record. Primary key for this entity in the Billing and Premium Collection System (Sapiens BillingPro / Majesco Billing).']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Premium schedules define billing arrangements that are managed by billing_account entities. The billing_account aggregates policies and manages payment collection. Currently premium_schedule has denor']
  annuitant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.annuitant. Business justification: For annuity contracts, the contribution/premium schedule is driven by the annuitant\'s age, RMD required begin date, payout option, and benefit base. Annuity billing operations require direct annuitant']
  coi_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.coi_rate_schedule. Business justification: For UL and variable products, the premium schedule must reference the COI rate schedule to compute cost-of-insurance deductions. Actuarial billing reconciliation and in-force management reports requir']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: A premium_schedule governs billing cadence for flexible premium deferred annuities. Currently premium_schedule references in_force_policy_id for life policies but has no annuity_contract_id. Direct li']
  crediting_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.crediting_strategy. Business justification: For indexed and fixed annuity products, the premium schedule must reference the crediting strategy (cap rate, participation rate, index type) to validate minimum premium requirements and support illus']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Premium schedules determine which GL account receives premium revenue postings. Essential for revenue recognition and financial reporting under GAAP/SAP. Links billing setup to chart of accounts struc']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: Premium schedule amounts, waiver-of-premium activation, flat extra premiums, and limited-pay periods are all insured-specific underwriting outcomes. Billing operations and waiver-of-premium processing']
  irc7702_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.irc7702_parameter. Business justification: Premium schedules for life insurance must comply with IRC 7702 (guideline annual premium, seven-pay limits). The irc7702_parameter defines these limits per plan version. This link enables automated ME']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Premium schedules are issued under a specific legal entity (insurer of record), essential for SAP/GAAP segmentation, multi-entity carrier statutory reporting, and state-of-domicile regulatory filings.']
  list_bill_group_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the list billing group when billing_method is \'list_bill\'. Identifies the employer, association, or group sponsor responsible for consolidated premium remittance on behalf of multiple policyholders.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employer entity when billing_method is \'payroll_deduction\'. Identifies the employer responsible for deducting and remitting premiums from employee payroll. Applicable to employer-sponsored life insurance plans subject to ERISA.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Premium schedules are configured according to product plan specifications (modal frequencies, grace periods, APL eligibility, billing methods). Essential for billing system configuration, product prof']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Premium schedules are governed by a specific plan version, not just the plan, because rate tables and IRC 7702 parameters are version-specific. In-force policies continue on their original version whe']
  policy_form_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval. Business justification: Premium schedules implement rates from state-approved policy forms. Billing configuration must reference the approved form number to ensure premiums charged match DOI-approved rates. Required for rate']
  premium_rate_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.premium_rate_table. Business justification: Premium schedule generation requires the approved rate table to validate modal premium amounts, face amount bands, and COI rates. Actuarial billing reconciliation and regulatory audits require traceab']
  primary_premium_in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the insurance policy for which this premium schedule is defined. Links the billing schedule to the master policy record in the Policy Administration System.']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.producer. Business justification: Premium schedules need writing producer attribution for commission calculations, persistency tracking, and producer book-of-business reporting. Standard requirement for producer compensation systems a']
  rate_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.rate_filing. Business justification: Premium schedules are built from DOI-approved rates. When a rate filing is approved, affected premium schedules must be updated and traceable to the authorizing filing. Actuarial and compliance audit ']
  risk_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: The risk class assigned during underwriting determines the premium rate applied in the premium schedule (COI rates, table ratings). Post-issue rating changes require updating the premium schedule. Act']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Premium schedules must comply with state-mandated grace periods, nonforfeiture rules, and billing frequency requirements. Insurers validate schedule parameters (grace_period_days, limited_pay_years) a']
  trust_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.trust. Business justification: ILIT (Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust) owned policies require Crummey notice compliance tied to the trust\'s premium schedule. Trust EIN drives 1099 tax reporting on premium payments. ILIT billing ope']
  underwriting_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: Premium schedules are set based on the underwriting class assigned at issue (preferred, standard, substandard), which determines base premium rates. Billing must reference the underwriting class to va']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded description of the reason for the most recent amendment to this premium schedule (e.g., \'modal frequency change\', \'face amount increase\', \'rider addition\', \'reinstatement\'). Populated when schedule_version > 1. Supports audit trail and policyholder service history.']
  ape_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) representing the annualized value of the modal premium. Calculated as the modal premium multiplied by the modal frequency factor (e.g., monthly × 12). Used for new business reporting, sales performance measurement, and actuarial valuation inputs per industry APE conventions.']
  apl_activated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) feature is currently active on this schedule. When True, the billing system has been configured to execute APL draws upon premium non-payment. Distinct from apl_eligible which reflects product eligibility.']
  apl_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy is eligible for Automatic Premium Loan (APL) processing. When True, the billing system will automatically trigger a policy loan to cover an unpaid premium before the policy lapses, provided sufficient cash surrender value (CSV) exists. Applicable to whole life and universal life products.']
  base_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annualized premium attributable to the base policy coverage only, excluding rider premiums. Used for premium allocation across policy components (base vs. riders) and for Cost of Insurance (COI) calculations in universal life products.']
  billing_day_of_month varchar [note: 'The day of the month on which the premium billing notice is generated and/or the EFT/ACH debit is initiated. Valid values are 1 through 28 (capped at 28 to avoid month-end variability). Stored in the billing system as the recurring debit/invoice trigger date.']
  billing_method varchar [note: 'The method by which premiums are collected from the policyholder. \'direct_bill\' sends invoices directly to the insured; \'eft_ach\' uses Electronic Funds Transfer / Automated Clearing House (EFT/ACH) for automatic bank debits; \'payroll_deduction\' applies to employer-sponsored plans; \'list_bill\' is used for group or association billing; \'bank_draft\' is a pre-authorized bank withdrawal. Drives payment processing workflow in Sapiens BillingPro / Majesco Billing.']
  billing_notice_preference varchar [note: 'The policyholder\'s preferred channel for receiving premium billing notices and payment reminders. \'paper\' sends physical mail; \'email\' sends electronic notices; \'sms\' sends text message reminders; \'portal\' delivers notices via the insurer\'s online self-service portal; \'none\' suppresses notices (e.g., for EFT/ACH auto-pay enrollees).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this premium schedule record was first created in the billing system. Represents the business event time of schedule establishment, which typically coincides with policy issuance or a subsequent schedule amendment. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this premium schedule (e.g., USD). Required for multi-currency policy administration and regulatory reporting.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this premium schedule becomes binding and the first premium is due. Aligns with the policy issue date or the date of a schedule amendment (e.g., modal frequency change, face amount change).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this premium schedule ends. Null for open-ended schedules (e.g., whole life). Populated for term policies at the end of the premium-paying period or upon policy maturity.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the premium due date during which the policy remains in force without payment. Standard grace period is 30 or 31 days per NAIC model regulations. After expiry of the grace period without payment, the policy enters lapse administration processing.']
  guideline_annual_premium decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guideline Annual Premium (GAP) used in the Guideline Premium Test (GPT) under IRC Section 7702 to determine whether the policy qualifies as life insurance for tax purposes. Calculated by the actuarial system. Premiums paid must not exceed the greater of the GAP or the Guideline Single Premium (GSP).']
  lapse_date date [note: 'The date on which the policy lapsed due to non-payment of premium beyond the grace period. Null for active schedules. Populated by the lapse administration module of the billing system. Critical for lapse experience studies and regulatory reporting.']
  last_paid_date date [note: 'The date through which the policy is paid up based on the most recent premium payment received. Used to determine the paid-to date for policy servicing, lapse calculations, and reinstatement eligibility.']
  limited_pay_years varchar [note: 'Number of years over which premiums are payable for limited-pay policies (e.g., 10-pay, 20-pay, paid-up at 65). Null when premium_paying_period is not \'limited_pay\'. Used to calculate the schedule expiry date and for MEC testing under IRC Section 7702A.']
  mec_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the policy has been classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) under IRC Section 7702A. \'non_mec\' policies retain favorable tax treatment; \'mec\' policies are subject to LIFO tax treatment on distributions; \'pending_test\' indicates the 7-pay test is in progress. Critical for tax compliance reporting to the IRS.']
  modal_factor decimal(8,6) [note: 'The multiplier applied to the annual premium to derive the modal premium amount for the selected billing frequency. Reflects the cost of installment billing (e.g., monthly factor ≈ 0.0875, quarterly ≈ 0.26, semi-annual ≈ 0.51, annual = 1.0). Stored for audit and recalculation purposes.']
  modal_frequency varchar [note: 'The payment frequency selected by the policyholder for premium remittance. Determines the billing cycle and the modal premium amount applied. Standard life insurance modal frequencies per ACORD and NAIC conventions.']
  modal_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The scheduled premium amount due at each billing interval as determined by the selected modal frequency. Reflects the base policy premium plus any rider premiums and modal loading factor. This is the gross amount billed to the policyholder per billing cycle.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'The date on which the next scheduled premium payment is due from the policyholder. Updated by the billing engine after each successful payment or following a reinstatement. Critical for grace period tracking and lapse administration.']
  nsf_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF) / returned payment events on this premium schedule. Used to assess payment reliability, trigger EFT/ACH suspension, and support fraud detection and AML monitoring per FinCEN BSA requirements.']
  nsf_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The fee charged to the policyholder per Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF) / returned payment event, as defined in the policy contract and state regulations. Used for billing reconciliation and policyholder account management.']
  paid_to_date date [note: 'The date through which the policy coverage is fully funded by premiums received. Distinct from last_paid_date in that it reflects the coverage period end rather than the transaction date. Used in policy servicing, reinstatement, and lapse administration workflows.']
  premium_paying_period varchar [note: 'Defines the duration over which premiums are payable under the policy contract. \'whole_life\' requires premiums for the life of the insured; \'limited_pay\' specifies a fixed number of years; \'single_premium\' is a one-time payment; \'term_to_age\' requires premiums until a specified attained age. Drives schedule expiry and Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) testing.']
