  reinstatement_date date [note: 'The date on which a lapsed policy\'s premium schedule was reinstated following payment of overdue premiums and satisfaction of reinstatement requirements. Null for schedules that have never lapsed. Used in reinstatement processing and regulatory reporting.']
  rider_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annualized premium attributable to all policy riders combined (e.g., Accidental Death Benefit (ADB), Waiver of Premium, Long-Term Care (LTC) rider). Supports premium allocation reporting and rider-level profitability analysis.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this premium schedule, as assigned by the Billing and Premium Collection System (e.g., Sapiens BillingPro). Used in policyholder correspondence, billing notices, and reconciliation with payment records.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the premium schedule. Drives billing engine behavior: \'active\' schedules generate billing notices; \'suspended\' schedules are on hold (e.g., waiver of premium); \'lapsed\' indicates non-payment past grace period; \'reinstated\' follows successful reinstatement processing; \'cancelled\' or \'terminated\' are end-states. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|suspended|lapsed|reinstated|cancelled|terminated — promote to reference product]']
  schedule_version varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version number for this premium schedule record. Incremented each time the schedule is amended (e.g., modal frequency change, face amount change, rider addition/removal). Supports audit trail and temporal data management in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  seven_pay_premium_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum cumulative premium that can be paid in the first seven policy years without triggering Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) classification under IRC Section 7702A. Calculated by the actuarial system and stored here for billing compliance monitoring.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this premium schedule record was sourced (e.g., Sapiens BillingPro, Majesco Billing). Used for data lineage tracking, ETL reconciliation, and Silver Layer data quality management in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this premium schedule record in the billing system. Updated on any field change including status transitions, payment date updates, and schedule amendments. Used for incremental ETL processing and audit trail in the Silver Layer.']
  waiver_of_premium boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Waiver of Premium (WP) benefit is currently active on this schedule, typically triggered by the insured\'s disability. When True, scheduled premiums are waived and the billing engine suspends premium collection while keeping the policy in force.']
  waiver_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the Waiver of Premium (WP) benefit became effective for this schedule. Null when waiver_of_premium is False. Used to track the duration of the waiver period and for actuarial experience studies.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the premium payment schedule for a policy, including modal frequency (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly), scheduled premium amounts, APE calculations, billing method (direct bill, EFT/ACH, payroll deduction, list bill), and billing day-of-month. Serves as the authoritative source for what is owed and when for each policy. Owned by the Billing and Premium Collection System (Sapiens BillingPro / Majesco Billing).'
}

Table billing.premium_bill {
  premium_bill_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each premium billing demand record in the Sapiens BillingPro / Majesco Billing system. Serves as the primary key for the premium_bill data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Premium bills are issued against billing accounts. Each bill represents a demand for payment from a billing relationship entity. While premium_bill already links to policy and party, the billing_accou']
  billing_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Premium bills generate regulatory billing notices, lapse warnings, and payment statements that must be retained for compliance and customer service. Real business process: state insurance law complian']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Premium bills are issued for premium-paying annuity contracts. Currently premium_bill only has in_force_policy_id for life policies. A direct annuity_contract_id FK supports annuity billing statement ']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance policy for which this billing demand is generated. Links the bill to the master policy record in the Policy Administration System.']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: State DOI regulations require premium bills to identify the insured. Waiver-of-premium billing notices and disability waiver activation are insured-specific. Customer service bill lookup by insured is']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Each premium bill generates journal entries for premium revenue recognition and accounts receivable. Core accounting process linking billing operations to general ledger. Required for financial statem']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Premium bills are issued by a specific legal entity, required for multi-entity carrier financial reporting, intercompany billing reconciliation, and state insurance department regulatory filings. Insu']
  list_bill_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.list_bill_group. Business justification: For group list billing, a premium_bill is issued to the list_bill_group (employer/group administrator) rather than to individual policyholders. premium_bill has group_bill_indicator (boolean) but no d']
  party_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mailing or billing address record used for physical notice delivery for this billing demand. May differ from the policyholder\'s residential address when a separate billing address has been designated.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder (owner) responsible for premium payment. Supports billing address resolution, communication preferences, and regulatory notice delivery requirements.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Bills are generated per product plan billing rules (modal premium, grace period, notice requirements). Required for billing accuracy validation, product-level revenue reporting, regulatory compliance ']
  premium_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_schedule. Business justification: Premium bills are generated from premium schedules. Each bill should reference the master schedule that defines the payment plan, modal frequency, and billing rules. This enables tracing bills back to']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.producer. Business justification: Premium bills require producer attribution for commission allocation, servicing responsibility tracking, and producer performance reporting. Essential for commission processing and producer book-of-bu']
  rate_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.rate_filing. Business justification: Bills generated after a rate change must reference the authorizing rate filing for regulatory audit and consumer notice compliance. Market conduct exams routinely verify that billed amounts align with']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Premium bills must comply with state-mandated notice language, grace period disclosures, and billing format requirements. state_of_issue is a denormalized state reference; replacing with FK to state_r']
  ape_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) for this billing demand, calculated as the annualized value of the modal premium (e.g., monthly premium × 12). Used for new business production reporting, agent commission calculations, and actuarial valuation inputs.']
  apl_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) advanced against the policy\'s cash surrender value (CSV) to cover the unpaid premium for this billing cycle. Null or zero when APL was not triggered.']
  apl_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was triggered for this billing cycle (True) because the premium was not paid and the policy has sufficient cash surrender value (CSV) to fund the loan. APL prevents lapse by borrowing against the policy\'s cash value.']
  base_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the modal premium attributable to the base policy coverage, excluding rider premiums and Cost of Insurance (COI) charges. Supports premium allocation reporting across policy components.']
  bill_date date [note: 'The date on which the billing demand was generated and issued by the billing system. Represents the business event timestamp for the billing cycle demand. Used for billing cycle reconciliation and regulatory notice timeline tracking.']
  bill_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable billing demand reference number assigned by the billing system (e.g., BL-2024-00012345). Printed on billing notices and used by policyholders and agents to reference a specific billing cycle demand.']
  bill_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the billing demand. \'issued\' = bill generated and sent; \'paid\' = full payment received; \'partial\' = partial payment received; \'lapsed\' = policy lapsed due to non-payment; \'cancelled\' = bill voided; \'reinstated\' = policy reinstated after lapse.']
  billed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount demanded in this billing notice, which may differ from modal_premium_amount when outstanding balances, NSF fees, reinstatement arrears, or rider adjustments are included. This is the amount printed on the billing notice sent to the policyholder.']
  billing_cycle_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying the billing cycle within the policy year (e.g., 1 for the first monthly bill, 12 for the last). Used for billing cycle reconciliation, APE calculations, and identifying the position of this demand within the annual premium schedule.']
  billing_mode varchar [note: 'The modal billing frequency elected by the policyholder, determining how often premium demands are generated. Drives Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) calculations (e.g., monthly modal factor = 1/12 of annual premium).']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the coverage period for which this billing demand applies. Defines the end of the modal billing interval and is used for pro-rata premium calculations on mid-term changes.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the coverage period for which this billing demand applies. Together with billing_period_end_date, defines the modal billing interval (monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual).']
  coi_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Cost of Insurance (COI) charge component within the billed amount, applicable to Universal Life (UL), Indexed Universal Life (IUL), and Variable Universal Life (VUL) policies. Represents the mortality charge deducted from the account value for the billing period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this premium bill record was first created in the billing system or ingested into the lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA monitoring for billing cycle processing.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this billing record (e.g., USD). Required for multi-currency policy support and regulatory reporting.']
  delivery_confirmation_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or tracking identifier confirming successful delivery of the billing notice (e.g., USPS tracking number, email message ID, ACH trace number). Serves as the audit trail for regulatory proof-of-notice compliance.']
  delivery_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether delivery of the billing notice has been confirmed (True) or is unconfirmed/failed (False). For mail, confirmation may be via USPS tracking; for email, via read receipt or delivery receipt; for EFT, via ACH acknowledgment.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which the billing notice was dispatched to the policyholder via the selected delivery method. Used to establish the regulatory notice timeline (e.g., state DOI mandated days-before-lapse notice requirements).']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The channel through which the billing notice was delivered to the policyholder or group administrator (e.g., mail, email, EFT notice, SMS, agent portal). Supports delivery preference management and regulatory proof-of-notice requirements.']
  due_date date [note: 'The contractual date by which premium payment must be received to keep the policy in force without entering the grace period. Derived from the policy anniversary date and modal billing frequency.']
  eft_account_masked varchar [note: 'Masked bank account number used for EFT/ACH premium collection, retaining only the last four digits for identification and audit purposes (e.g., XXXX1234). Full account number is stored in the secure payment vault and never persisted in the lakehouse.']
  grace_period_expiry_date date [note: 'The last date by which premium payment can be received before the policy lapses. Calculated as the due date plus the state-mandated or contractual grace period (typically 30 or 31 days for life insurance). Drives lapse administration and regulatory notice obligations.']
  group_bill_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this billing demand is part of a group billing arrangement (True), where the bill is sent to a group administrator or employer rather than an individual policyholder (False). Drives group vs. individual billing workflow routing.']
  modal_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The scheduled premium amount due for the current billing mode (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, or monthly). Represents the gross base premium before any adjustments, credits, or APL offsets. Core component of Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) calculations.']
  notice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing communication type associated with this record. Drives regulatory notice compliance tracking per state DOI mandated timelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initial_bill|reminder|grace_warning|final_notice|lapse_notice|reinstatement_offer|nsf_notification|apl_notification — promote to reference product]']
  nsf_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee assessed on this billing demand as a result of a prior Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF) / returned payment event. Included in the billed_amount when applicable. Zero or null when no NSF event has occurred for this billing cycle.']
  nsf_occurrence_count varchar [note: 'The number of Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF) / returned payment events recorded against this billing demand. Used to determine NSF fee escalation, EFT suspension thresholds, and fraud risk scoring.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative unpaid premium balance carried forward from prior billing cycles at the time this bill was generated. A non-zero value indicates the policyholder has arrears. Used in lapse determination and reinstatement calculations.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or mechanism elected for this billing demand (e.g., EFT/ACH, check, credit card, direct bill, payroll deduction). Drives EFT/ACH processing workflows and NSF risk assessment.']
  policy_year varchar [note: 'The policy year (1, 2, 3, ...) in which this billing demand falls, measured from the policy issue date. Used for DAC amortization, persistency analysis, and first-year vs. renewal premium classification in GAAP (ASC 944) and SAP reporting.']
  regulatory_notice_ref varchar [note: 'Reference code or citation identifying the specific state Department of Insurance (DOI) regulation or NAIC model regulation that mandates the notice type and timeline for this billing communication (e.g., \'CA-INS-10113\', \'NAIC-MOD-615\'). Supports regulatory audit and compliance reporting.']
  reinstatement_arrears_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total past-due premium amount required to reinstate a lapsed policy, representing the sum of all unpaid modal premiums from the lapse date to the reinstatement date. Included in billed_amount when reinstatement_required is True.']
  reinstatement_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this billing demand is associated with a reinstatement offer following a policy lapse (True). When True, the billed_amount includes arrears premiums and any applicable reinstatement fees required to restore the policy to in-force status.']
  rider_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate premium amount attributable to all policy riders (e.g., Accidental Death Benefit (ADB), Waiver of Premium, Long-Term Care (LTC) rider) included in this billing demand. Supports rider-level premium allocation and profitability analysis.']
  source_system_bill_code varchar [note: 'The native bill identifier assigned by the originating billing system (Sapiens BillingPro or Majesco Billing) before lakehouse ingestion. Preserved for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent update to this premium bill record, capturing status changes (e.g., issued → paid, issued → lapsed), payment postings, or notice delivery confirmations. Essential for change data capture (CDC) and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each billing cycle demand and all associated notices sent to policyholders or group administrators. Captures bill date, due date, grace period expiry date, billed amount, modal premium, outstanding balance, notice type (initial bill, reminder, grace warning, final notice, lapse notice, reinstatement offer, NSF notification, APL notification), delivery method (mail, email, EFT notice, SMS), delivery date, delivery confirmation, bill status (issued, paid, partial, lapsed, cancelled), and regulatory notice requirement reference including state DOI mandated notice timelines. Serves as SSOT for all premium billing demands and billing-related communications. Analogous to an invoice plus its delivery confirmation and notice audit trail in the insurance billing context. Source system: Sapiens BillingPro / Majesco Billing.'
}

Table billing.billing_premium_payment {
  billing_premium_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each premium payment or refund transaction record within the billing domain. Primary key for the billing_premium_payment data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing account or premium schedule record that owns the billing obligation being satisfied by this payment.']
  eft_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.eft_authorization. Business justification: A premium payment made via EFT/ACH directly references the EFT authorization that authorized the debit. billing_premium_payment has eft_account_last4 as a denormalized field, but the authoritative EFT']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the insurance policy against which this premium payment or refund is applied. Links the payment transaction to the master policy record.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Premium payments trigger cash receipt journal entries debiting cash and crediting receivables. Fundamental accounting link for cash management, reconciliation, and financial reporting. Required for SO']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Premium payments are received and posted to a specific legal entity\'s books. Critical for intercompany settlement, statutory premium income reporting by entity, and RBC calculations. Insurance finance']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder (party) who owns the policy associated with this payment. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  payment_receipt_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Life insurance carriers must generate and retain payment receipt/confirmation documents for regulatory audit and customer service. State insurance regulations require traceable payment receipts. No ex']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Payments apply to policies sold under specific product plans. Essential for product-level revenue recognition, persistency analysis by product, commission calculations tied to product type, and produc']
  premium_bill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_bill. Business justification: Payments are applied against specific bills. This FK links each payment transaction to the bill it satisfies. Nullable because advance payments may be received before a bill is generated (is_suspense ']
  ape_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual Premium Equivalent amount derived from this payment, calculated by annualizing the modal premium. APE = annual premium + 10% of single premiums. Used for new business reporting, agent commission calculations, and management KPI tracking per industry standard.']
  apl_trigger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment event triggered an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) to prevent policy lapse. When True, the policy\'s cash surrender value (CSV) was used to fund the premium obligation in lieu of a cash payment from the policyholder.']
  applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the payment amount that has been successfully applied to the policy premium obligation. May differ from payment_amount when a partial application occurs or when a portion is held in suspense.']
  bank_reference_number varchar [note: 'Bank-assigned reference or trace number for the payment transaction (e.g., ACH trace number, wire confirmation number, check number). Used for bank reconciliation and payment dispute resolution.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the premium billing period to which this payment is applied. Together with billing_period_start_date, defines the coverage interval funded by this payment.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the premium billing period to which this payment is applied. Defines the coverage period being funded by this payment transaction.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Check number printed on the paper check instrument when payment_method is \'check\'. Null for non-check payment methods. Used for check reconciliation and return item processing.']
  clearance_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment instrument was confirmed cleared by the bank. Null for payments not yet cleared or returned. Used for cash reconciliation and float management.']
  clearance_status varchar [note: 'Bank clearance status of the payment instrument. Pending: awaiting bank confirmation. Cleared: funds confirmed received by bank. Returned: check or ACH returned by bank (NSF, account closed). Stopped: stop payment issued. Voided: payment voided before presentment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing_premium_payment record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the payment amount (e.g., USD, CAD). Supports multi-currency policy administration for international life and annuity products.']
  eft_account_last4 varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the bank account number used for EFT/ACH payment. Stored as a masked reference for reconciliation and payor identification without retaining full account details. Null for non-EFT payment methods.']
  grace_period_payment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment was received during the policy grace period (typically 30-31 days after the premium due date per state regulation). Grace period payments prevent lapse but may affect policy values and reinstatement requirements.']
  is_suspense boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any portion of this payment is currently held in a suspense account pending identification, resolution, or instruction. True when suspense_amount > 0 and the payment cannot be fully applied.']
  modal_premium_mode varchar [note: 'Billing frequency mode associated with this payment, indicating whether the premium was collected on an annual, semi-annual, quarterly, or monthly basis. Drives APE (Annual Premium Equivalent) calculation and billing schedule alignment.']
  nsf_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee amount assessed to the policyholder as a result of a returned NSF payment. Zero or null when nsf_indicator is False. Governed by state-specific fee limits.']
  nsf_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment was returned by the bank due to non-sufficient funds (NSF) in the payor\'s account. True triggers NSF fee assessment, grace period re-evaluation, and potential lapse administration workflow in Sapiens BillingPro.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount of the premium payment received or refund issued, expressed in the policy currency. For receipts, this is the total cash collected from the payor. For refunds, this is the total cash disbursed to the payee. This is the base monetary amount before any allocation or adjustment.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The real-world business date on which the premium payment was received from the payor or the refund was issued to the payee. This is the principal business event date used for premium accounting, grace period calculation, and billing period application.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument or mechanism used to tender the premium payment. Check: paper check. EFT/ACH: electronic funds transfer via Automated Clearing House. Credit card: card payment. Wire: bank wire transfer. Payroll deduction: employer-sponsored payroll withholding. ACH reversal: return of a prior ACH receipt (NSF or stop payment).']
  payment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by billing operations staff regarding this payment transaction. May include exception handling details, payor instructions, or reconciliation remarks.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable transaction reference number assigned by Sapiens BillingPro at the time of payment receipt or refund issuance. Used for cash application, reconciliation, and customer-facing correspondence.']
  payment_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating party or mechanism that funded the premium payment. Policyholder: direct payment by the insured. Employer: group or payroll-deducted payment. Bank draft: pre-authorized bank account draft. Third party: payment by a non-policyholder payor. Trust: payment from an ILIT or other trust arrangement.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment transaction within the billing workflow. Pending: received but not yet cleared. Cleared: bank confirmation received. Applied: posted to policy premium account. Reversed: cancelled due to NSF or error. Suspended: held in suspense pending resolution. Failed: payment attempt unsuccessful.']
  premium_allocation_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the policy component to which this payment is allocated. Base policy: core policy premium. Rider: supplemental benefit rider premium (e.g., ADB, LTC, DI). COI: Cost of Insurance charge for universal life products. Load: expense loading. Other: miscellaneous allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: base_policy|rider|coi|load|waiver|other — promote to reference product]']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the payment transaction was recorded in the Sapiens BillingPro system. Used for intraday cash application sequencing, audit trails, and same-day processing cutoff determination.']
  refund_approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status for premium refund transactions. Pending: awaiting authorization. Approved: authorized for disbursement. Rejected: refund request denied. Issued: refund disbursed to payee. Null for non-refund transactions.']
  refund_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the unearned premium refund amount. Pro-rata: refund proportional to unused coverage days. Short-rate: refund less a penalty for policyholder-initiated cancellation. Flat: full premium refund (e.g., free-look period). Null for non-refund transactions.']
  refund_issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the premium refund was disbursed to the payee. Null for non-refund transactions or refunds not yet issued. Used for refund aging and regulatory compliance tracking.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method by which a premium refund is disbursed to the payee. Check: paper check mailed. ACH credit: electronic credit to bank account. Policy credit: applied as a credit to the policy account balance. Wire: bank wire transfer. Null for non-refund transactions.']
  refund_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for premium refund transactions. Policy cancellation: refund upon policy surrender or lapse. Overpayment: excess premium collected. Rate correction: refund due to premium rate adjustment. Death claim return: unearned premium returned upon insured death. Free-look cancellation: refund within the free-look period per state regulation. Null for non-refund transactions.']
  reinstatement_payment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment is associated with a policy reinstatement event, where a lapsed policy is being restored. Reinstatement payments typically include all past-due premiums plus interest.']
  source_system_payment_code varchar [note: 'Native payment transaction identifier assigned by the Sapiens BillingPro source system. Enables traceability back to the operational system of record for reconciliation, audit, and issue resolution.']
  suspense_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the payment amount held in a suspense account pending resolution (e.g., unidentified payor, NIGO application, overpayment pending instruction). Zero when the full payment is applied.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the direction and nature of the premium cash movement: receipt (inbound premium collected), refund (outbound return of premium), reversal (cancellation of a prior receipt, e.g., NSF), adjustment (correction entry), or transfer (reallocation between policies).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this billing_premium_payment record. Used for change data capture, reconciliation, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each premium payment received or refund issued against a policy billing obligation. Captures transaction direction (receipt/refund), payment date, amount, payment method (check, EFT/ACH, credit card, wire, payroll deduction, ACH reversal), payment source (policyholder, employer, bank draft), applied billing period, suspense flag, clearance status, bank reference number. For refunds: refund reason (policy cancellation, overpayment, rate correction, death claim premium return, free-look cancellation), refund amount, refund method (check, ACH reversal, credit), pro-rata/short-rate calculation method, refund approval status, and refund issuance date. SSOT for all inbound premium cash receipts and outbound premium refunds within the billing domain. Supports cash application, reconciliation, aging, refund processing, and premium accounting workflows. Source system: Sapiens BillingPro payment processing module.'
}

Table billing.premium_allocation {
  premium_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each premium allocation transaction record in the billing system. Primary key for the premium_allocation data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing account under which the premium payment was received and from which this allocation is derived.']
  billing_premium_payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent premium payment transaction from which this allocation is derived. Links the allocation detail back to the received payment header.']
  coi_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.coi_rate_schedule. Business justification: Premium allocation splits payments into COI, expense load, and savings components. The COI component is computed from the COI rate schedule. This link enables actuarial validation of per-policy COI de']
  crediting_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.crediting_strategy. Business justification: For indexed and fixed annuity products, premium allocations are directed to specific crediting strategies (index strategies, fixed account). This link is required for accurate interest crediting, acco']
  ifrs17_contract_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ifrs17_contract_group. Business justification: IFRS 17 insurance revenue recognition requires premium allocations to be mapped to contract groups for CSM release and insurance revenue calculation. Finance teams reconcile allocated premium by IFRS ']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance policy to which this premium allocation applies. Establishes the policy context for the allocation record.']
  irc7702_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.irc7702_parameter. Business justification: Premium allocation performs MEC testing using seven-pay premium limits defined in irc7702_parameter. Direct link enables automated MEC compliance validation at allocation time. seven_pay_premium_limit']
  original_allocation_premium_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original premium_allocation_id that this record reverses or adjusts. Populated only when allocation_status is REVERSED or ADJUSTED, enabling a complete audit chain from original allocation through all corrections.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder (owner) associated with the policy for which this premium is being allocated. Supports customer-level billing analytics and PARTY_REFERENCE requirement.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Premium allocations follow product plan component structure (base premium, rider premiums, COI rates, expense loads). Critical for accurate accounting, DAC calculations, statutory reserve computations']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Variable life/annuity premium allocations are directed to specific investment portfolios (general account segments or separate account sub-portfolios). Portfolio-level premium inflow reporting, ALM an']
  separate_account_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.separate_account_fund. Business justification: For variable life/annuity products, premium allocations are directed to specific separate account funds. This link validates that allocations target only plan-approved fund options, supports SEC-requi']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable life and annuity products require tracking which separate account fund each premium allocation is invested into. This is the core operational link for policyholder-directed investment electio']
  account_value_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net dollar amount credited to the policy Account Value (AV) after deducting COI charges and expense loads from the gross premium payment. Applicable for UL/IUL/VUL products. Populated when component_type is ACCOUNT_VALUE_FUNDING.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross dollar amount of the premium payment allocated to this specific policy component. This is the primary monetary fact for the allocation line. For UL/IUL/VUL products, this represents the portion directed to COI, expense load, rider premium, or account value funding as indicated by component_type.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'The business effective date on which the premium amount was allocated to the policy component. This is the principal real-world event date for the allocation, used for policy accounting, reserve calculations, and billing period reconciliation.']
  allocation_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for this premium allocation record, used in billing statements, reconciliation reports, and policyholder correspondence. Follows the format ALLOC-{YYYYMMDD}-{sequence}.']
  allocation_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential line number ordering the allocation components within a single premium payment. Enables ordered reconstruction of the full allocation breakdown for a given payment_id, supporting reconciliation and audit.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this premium allocation record. PENDING = allocation calculated but not yet posted to policy; APPLIED = allocation successfully posted to the policy component; REVERSED = allocation reversed due to payment return (NSF) or correction; ADJUSTED = allocation modified due to billing correction or reallocation; FAILED = allocation processing encountered an error.']
  allocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this allocation record was created and posted in the billing system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED timestamp and supports intraday reconciliation and audit trail requirements.']
  annualized_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annualized equivalent of the allocated component premium, calculated by applying the modal factor to the allocated_amount. Used for Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) reporting, new business value calculations, and actuarial reserving.']
  apl_balance_after decimal(18,2) [note: 'The outstanding Automatic Premium Loan (APL) balance on the policy immediately after this allocation is applied. Populated when component_type is APL_REPAYMENT. The difference between apl_balance_before and apl_balance_after equals the allocated_amount for APL repayment lines.']
  apl_balance_before decimal(18,2) [note: 'The outstanding Automatic Premium Loan (APL) balance on the policy immediately before this allocation is applied. Populated when component_type is APL_REPAYMENT to track loan reduction. APL is triggered when a premium is unpaid and the policy has sufficient cash value.']
  billing_mode varchar [note: 'The modal billing frequency under which the premium payment was collected and this allocation was generated. Determines the billing period duration and the modal premium factor applied to the annualized premium.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the billing period to which this premium allocation is applied. Together with billing_period_start_date, defines the modal billing interval covered by this allocation.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the billing period to which this premium allocation is applied. Used for modal billing reconciliation (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly) and premium accounting.']
  coi_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The Cost of Insurance (COI) rate per thousand dollars of Net Amount at Risk (NAR) applied to calculate the COI charge for this allocation period. Applicable only when component_type is COI. Derived from the policy\'s mortality table, age, risk class, and policy year.']
  component_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the policy component to which this allocation amount is applied. BASE_PREMIUM covers the core policy premium; COI (Cost of Insurance) is the mortality charge for UL/IUL/VUL products; RIDER_PREMIUM covers supplemental benefit riders (ADB, AWL, CAR, LTC); EXPENSE_LOAD is the administrative expense charge; APL_REPAYMENT is repayment of an Automatic Premium Loan; ACCOUNT_VALUE_FUNDING is the net amount credited to the policyholder\'s account value for UL/IUL/VUL.']
  coverage_effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the premium allocation provides coverage or account value credit for the associated policy component. Used to align premium allocation with the billing period and policy anniversary calculations.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'The date through which the premium allocation provides coverage for the associated policy component. Defines the end of the billing period covered by this allocation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the allocated amount. Typically USD for domestic US life insurance policies. Required for multi-currency policy support and regulatory reporting.']
  dac_eligible_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this premium allocation is eligible for Deferred Acquisition Cost (DAC) capitalization under GAAP accounting. DAC-eligible premiums contribute to the deferral of policy acquisition costs amortized over the expected policy life. Relevant for FASB ASC 944 and LDTI compliance.']
  expense_load_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The administrative expense charge deducted from the premium payment before crediting to account value for UL/IUL/VUL products. Represents the per-premium or per-policy expense load as defined in the policy contract. Populated when component_type is EXPENSE_LOAD.']
  grace_period_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this premium allocation was applied during the policy\'s grace period. When True, the allocation is a late payment that prevented lapse. Critical for grace period management, lapse administration, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this allocation record was most recently modified, such as during a reversal, adjustment, or status change. Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  mec_test_status varchar [note: 'The result of the Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) 7-pay test for this premium allocation. PASS = premium does not cause the policy to become a MEC; FAIL = premium causes or contributes to MEC classification; NOT_APPLICABLE = MEC testing not required for this product type. MEC classification has significant tax implications under IRC Section 7702A.']
  modal_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The scheduled modal premium amount for the policy at the time of this allocation, representing the total premium due for the billing mode (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly). Used to validate that the total allocation equals the expected modal premium.']
  nar_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Net Amount at Risk (NAR) used in the COI charge calculation for this allocation period, representing the difference between the death benefit and the policy account value. Applicable for UL/IUL/VUL products when component_type is COI.']
  nsf_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premium payment associated with this allocation was returned due to Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF). When True, the allocation is typically reversed and the policy may enter a grace period. Supports NSF handling and lapse administration workflows.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or method used to remit the premium payment from which this allocation was derived. EFT_ACH = Electronic Funds Transfer via ACH; CHECK = paper check; CREDIT_CARD = credit/debit card; WIRE = wire transfer; DIRECT_BILL = direct billing to policyholder; LIST_BILL = employer or group list billing.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known policy contract number associated with this allocation. Denormalized for operational reporting and reconciliation without requiring a join to the policy table.']
  policy_year varchar [note: 'The policy year (1-based integer) in which this premium allocation falls, calculated from the policy issue date. Used for COI rate determination, MEC 7-pay test tracking, DAC amortization, and actuarial experience studies.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the insurance product type associated with the policy for this allocation. Determines applicable allocation logic: UL/IUL/VUL products require COI and expense load splits; WL and TERM products allocate entirely to base premium. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WL|UL|IUL|VUL|YRT|TERM|SPIA|FIA|ANNUITY — promote to reference product]']
  reinstatement_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this premium allocation is associated with a policy reinstatement payment, where a lapsed policy is being restored to in-force status. Reinstatement allocations may include back-premium and interest charges.']
  reinsurance_ceded_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a portion of the premium associated with this allocation has been ceded to a reinsurer under an active reinsurance treaty. When True, the corresponding reinsurance premium cession will be recorded in the reinsurance domain.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason this allocation was reversed when allocation_status is REVERSED. NSF = Non-Sufficient Funds return; PAYMENT_ERROR = incorrect payment amount; POLICY_CANCEL = policy cancelled retroactively; BILLING_CORRECTION = billing system correction; DUPLICATE = duplicate payment reversal; REINSTATEMENT_REVERSAL = reinstatement declined. Null when allocation has not been reversed.']
  rider_code varchar [note: 'Industry-standard code identifying the specific rider to which this allocation applies when component_type is RIDER_PREMIUM. ADB = Accidental Death Benefit; AWL = Automatic Waiver of Premium; CAR = Company Action Rider; LTC = Long-Term Care; DI = Disability Income; GMDB/GMWB/GMIB/GMAB = Guaranteed Minimum benefit riders; WP = Waiver of Premium. Null when component_type is not RIDER_PREMIUM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ADB|AWL|CAR|LTC|DI|GMDB|GMWB|GMIB|GMAB|WP — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational source system that originated this premium allocation record (e.g., SAPIENS_BILLING, FAST_PAS, MAJESCO_BILLING). Supports data lineage, reconciliation between systems, and ETL audit trails in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  tax_qualified_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this premium allocation is associated with a tax-qualified plan (e.g., 401(k), IRA, pension plan). Tax-qualified allocations may have different contribution limits, tax treatment, and regulatory reporting requirements under ERISA and IRS regulations.']

  Note: 'Transactional record detailing how a received premium payment is allocated across policy components — base policy premium, rider premiums (ADB, AWL, CAR, LTC), COI charges for UL/IUL/VUL products, and administrative expense loads. Captures allocation amount per component, component type, and effective date. Essential for UL/IUL/VUL products where premium is split between COI, expense charges, and account value funding.'
}

Table billing.grace_period {
  grace_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the grace period record. Primary key for the grace_period data product in the billing domain.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Premium-paying annuity contracts (e.g., flexible premium deferred annuities) enter grace periods when premiums are missed. The grace_period record must reference the annuity_contract to trigger contra']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance policy for which this grace period has been initiated. Links the grace period record to the master policy entity.']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: Grace period notices must identify the insured per state DOI requirements. Waiver-of-premium eligibility during grace period (disability waiver) is insured-specific. Regulatory grace period reporting ']
  notice_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Grace periods trigger mandatory regulatory lapse warning notices required by state insurance laws. Real business process: consumer protection compliance, third-party notification requirements, proof o']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder (owner) associated with the policy under grace period. Supports customer-level grace period reporting and lapse prevention outreach.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Grace period rules (duration, APL eligibility, notice requirements) are product-specific design features. Required for lapse management, conservation efforts, product design compliance validation, and']
  premium_bill_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the premium bill or billing notice that triggered the grace period due to a missed or insufficient payment. Establishes the causal link between the billing event and the grace period.']
  premium_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_schedule. Business justification: Grace periods are triggered by missed payments on a premium schedule. While grace_period references premium_bill, it should also reference the schedule to enable schedule-level grace period tracking a']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Grace period management for variable products requires real-time separate account value monitoring to determine APL eligibility and CSV sufficiency to cover overdue premiums. The grace_period stores c']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Grace period duration (typically 30-31 days) and notice requirements are mandated by state regulation and vary by jurisdiction. Each grace period event must reference the applicable state regulation t']
  apl_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) applied to cover the outstanding premium, if APL was triggered as the resolution outcome. Null if APL was not triggered.']
  apl_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy is eligible for an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) to cover the outstanding premium during the grace period. Eligibility is based on sufficient policy cash surrender value (CSV) and the presence of an APL provision in the policy contract.']
  billing_mode varchar [note: 'The modal billing frequency of the policy at the time the grace period was triggered. Determines the premium amount due and the billing cycle context for the grace period. Supports Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this grace period record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Represents the audit creation time for data lineage and regulatory compliance purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this grace period record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency policy administration for international or expatriate life insurance products.']
  days_remaining varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days remaining in the grace period window as of the last record update. Supports lapse prevention workflow prioritization and regulatory notice timeline management. Null if the grace period is closed.']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'The total number of calendar days in the grace period window, as defined by the applicable state regulation or policy contract. Typically 30 or 31 days for life insurance per NAIC model regulation. Stored explicitly to support regulatory compliance validation without requiring date arithmetic.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the grace period expires. Typically 30 or 31 days from the start date per NAIC model regulation, but may vary by state DOI requirement, product type, or contractual terms. After this date, the policy lapses if no cure action has been taken.']
  first_notice_date date [note: 'The date on which the first regulatory grace period notice was sent to the policyholder, informing them of the missed payment and the outstanding premium required to cure. Required for state DOI regulatory notice timeline compliance.']
  lapse_notice_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal lapse notice was sent to the policyholder, notifying them that the policy has lapsed or is imminent to lapse due to non-payment at the end of the grace period. Required by most state DOI regulations.']
  modal_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The scheduled premium amount due for the billing mode (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, or monthly) that was missed, triggering the grace period. Represents the full modal premium before any partial payment credit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, exception handling comments, or special circumstances related to this grace period record (e.g., customer hardship accommodation, regulatory exception, manual override reason). Used by billing operations staff.']
  notice_channel varchar [note: 'The primary channel through which grace period notices were delivered to the policyholder (e.g., postal mail, email, SMS, phone call, online portal). Supports regulatory compliance documentation and lapse prevention workflow tracking.']
  nsf_count varchar [note: 'The number of Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF) payment returns associated with this grace period event. Multiple NSF returns may indicate a pattern requiring escalated lapse prevention action or payment method change.']
  outstanding_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net premium amount still required to cure the grace period and restore the policy to active status. Equals the modal premium amount less any partial payments received. This is the cure amount communicated in regulatory notices.']
  partial_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of any partial premium payment received during the grace period that has been credited toward the outstanding premium. Zero if no partial payment was received.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment method on file for the policy at the time the grace period was triggered. Identifies whether the failure was an EFT/ACH failure, a returned check (NSF), or a direct bill non-response. Supports root cause analysis for lapse prevention.']
  period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the grace period record. \'open\' indicates the grace period is active and the policy is still in force. \'cured\' indicates the outstanding premium was paid within the grace period. \'lapsed\' indicates the policy lapsed due to non-payment. \'apl_triggered\' indicates an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was applied. \'reinstated\' indicates the policy was reinstated after lapse. \'expired\' indicates the grace period window closed without resolution.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the grace period based on its governing basis. \'standard\' reflects the default NAIC model regulation grace period (30-31 days). \'extended\' reflects a grace period longer than standard, granted by company policy or state mandate. \'state_mandated\' reflects a grace period required by a specific state DOI regulation. \'contractual\' reflects a grace period defined within the policy contract terms.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the policy under grace period, as assigned by the Policy Administration System. Used for regulatory notices, customer correspondence, and cross-system reconciliation.']
  premium_due_date date [note: 'The original scheduled premium due date that was missed or insufficiently paid, triggering the grace period. Used to reconcile the grace period against the premium billing schedule.']
  prior_grace_count varchar [note: 'The number of prior grace period events on this policy before the current grace period. A high count indicates a pattern of payment difficulty and may trigger enhanced lapse prevention workflows or underwriting review.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'The type of life insurance or annuity product associated with the policy under grace period. Determines applicable grace period rules, state regulatory requirements, and resolution options (e.g., APL eligibility). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_life|term_life|universal_life|variable_universal_life|indexed_universal_life|annuity|disability_income|long_term_care — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_notice_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory grace period or lapse notice is required to be sent to the policyholder under applicable state DOI regulations for this policy. Drives the notice generation workflow in the billing system.']
  reinstatement_deadline date [note: 'The last date by which the policyholder may apply for reinstatement of a lapsed policy, as defined by the policy contract and applicable state regulations. Typically 3-5 years from the lapse date. Null if the policy has not lapsed.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the grace period was resolved, either through payment, lapse, APL trigger, reinstatement, or waiver. Null if the grace period is still open.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'The final resolution outcome of the grace period. \'paid\' indicates the outstanding premium was paid in full within the grace period. \'lapsed\' indicates the policy lapsed due to non-payment. \'apl_triggered\' indicates an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was applied to cover the outstanding premium. \'reinstated\' indicates the policy was reinstated after lapse. \'waived\' indicates the premium was waived (e.g., due to disability waiver rider).']
  second_notice_date date [note: 'The date on which the second or follow-up grace period notice was sent to the policyholder. Some states require multiple notices during the grace period window. Null if only one notice is required or sent.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational billing or policy administration system from which this grace period record originated. Supports data lineage, ETL reconciliation, and multi-system integration in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  source_system_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier or reference number assigned to this grace period record in the originating operational system (e.g., Sapiens BillingPro internal grace period ID). Supports cross-system reconciliation and ETL traceability.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the grace period commenced, typically the day after the premium due date when payment was not received. This is the principal business event date for the grace period lifecycle.']
  state_of_issue varchar [note: 'The two-letter US state code of the state in which the policy was issued. Determines the applicable state DOI grace period regulations, required notice timelines, and lapse rules that govern this grace period record.']
  third_party_notice_date date [note: 'The date on which the lapse notice was sent to the designated third party (e.g., assignee, ILIT trustee). Null if no third party notice is required or if it has not yet been sent.']
  third_party_notice_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a lapse notice must also be sent to a designated third party (e.g., assignee, collateral assignee, or irrevocable beneficiary) per state DOI regulations or policy contract terms. Relevant for ILIT (Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust) and collateral assignment scenarios.']
  trigger_reason varchar [note: 'The specific reason that initiated the grace period. \'missed_payment\' indicates no payment was received by the due date. \'insufficient_payment\' indicates a partial payment was received but did not meet the required premium amount. \'nsf_return\' indicates a Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF) check was returned. \'eft_failure\' indicates an Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT)/ACH payment failed. \'check_return\' indicates a physical check was returned unpaid.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this grace period record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks the most recent status change, notice sent, or resolution update for audit trail purposes.']
  waiver_of_premium_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Waiver of Premium rider (e.g., Automatic Waiver of Premium — AWL, or disability waiver) is active on the policy at the time of the grace period. If true, the outstanding premium may be waived, affecting the resolution outcome.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking the grace period status for a policy following a missed or insufficient premium payment. Captures grace period start date, grace period end date (typically 30-31 days per NAIC model regulation, varies by state), grace period type, triggering bill reference, outstanding premium amount required to cure, notices sent (with dates), and resolution outcome (paid, lapsed, APL triggered, reinstated). SSOT for grace period administration per state DOI requirements. Supports regulatory notice timeline compliance and lapse prevention workflows.'
}

Table billing.lapse_event {
  lapse_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each policy lapse event record in the billing domain. Primary key for the lapse_event data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing account associated with the lapsed policy. Links the lapse event to the premium billing and collection record in Sapiens BillingPro.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Premium-paying annuity contracts can lapse due to missed premiums. The lapse_event must reference the annuity_contract to record CSV at lapse, non-forfeiture elections, and reinstatement deadlines. Cu']
  grace_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.grace_period. Business justification: Lapse events occur after grace period expiration. This FK captures the direct causal relationship between the grace period and the resulting lapse. Currently lapse_event has denormalized grace period ']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policy that has lapsed due to non-payment of premium after expiration of the grace period. Core linkage to the policy master record.']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: Non-forfeiture option calculations (reduced paid-up, extended term) at lapse are insured-age-dependent. Lapse processing requires insured attained age and risk class to compute extended term insurance']
  lapse_notice_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Lapse events generate mandatory regulatory notifications and non-forfeiture option election forms required by state law. Real business process: state DOI compliance, policyholder rights documentation,']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder (insured owner) whose policy has lapsed. Supports customer-level lapse analytics and regulatory notification workflows.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Lapse behavior varies significantly by product design (term vs permanent, premium structure, surrender charges). Essential for product experience studies, pricing assumption validation, product profit']
  premium_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_schedule. Business justification: Lapse occurs when a premium schedule\'s payment obligations aren\'t met after grace period expiry. Linking lapse to schedule enables schedule-level lapse analysis and supports identification of high-ris']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable life/annuity lapse processing requires identifying the separate account holding policy value at lapse for liquidation, non-forfeiture option calculation, and regulatory reporting. The lapse_e']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Lapse processing must comply with state-specific nonforfeiture requirements (extended term insurance, reduced paid-up options, cash surrender value calculations). Each lapse event must reference the g']
  triggering_premium_bill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_bill. Business justification: A lapse event is triggered by non-payment of a specific premium bill after the grace period expires. lapse_event has missed_premium_due_date but no direct reference to the specific premium_bill that t']
  apl_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of the Automatic Premium Loan (APL) applied against the policy\'s Cash Surrender Value (CSV) to fund the outstanding premium. Null if APL was not applied.']
  apl_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) was actually applied to prevent or reverse the lapse. True if APL was triggered and the premium was funded via policy loan. Distinct from apl_eligible which reflects eligibility only.']
  apl_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy is eligible for an Automatic Premium Loan (APL) to prevent lapse. True if the policy has sufficient Cash Surrender Value (CSV) to cover the outstanding premium and the APL provision is active in the policy contract.']
  billing_mode varchar [note: 'The premium payment frequency mode in effect at the time of lapse. Determines the modal premium amount and grace period calculation. Values: annual, semi_annual, quarterly, monthly.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this lapse event record was first created in the data platform (Silver Layer). Audit trail timestamp for data governance and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this lapse event record. Typically USD for domestic US life insurance policies.']
  extended_term_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the extended term insurance coverage expires when the extended_term non-forfeiture option is elected. Calculated based on the CSV at lapse and the net single premium for term coverage. Null if extended term was not elected.']
  face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original face amount (death benefit) of the lapsed policy as of the lapse effective date. Used for reinsurance cession adjustments, actuarial reserving, and regulatory reporting.']
  lapse_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the policy officially lapsed, defined as the day following the expiration of the grace period without receipt of the required premium. This is the principal business event date for the lapse transaction.']
  lapse_event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the lapse event, assigned by the Sapiens BillingPro lapse administration module. Used for cross-system reference and regulatory correspondence.']
  lapse_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for the policy lapse as recorded in Sapiens BillingPro. \'NSF\' = non-sufficient funds; \'EFT_FAIL\' = electronic funds transfer failure; \'VOLUNTARY\' = policyholder elected to lapse; \'PREMIUM_UNAFFORDABLE\' = policyholder cited affordability; \'ADDRESS_UNKNOWN\' = undeliverable billing notices; \'POLICY_SURRENDER\' = policyholder surrendered; \'OTHER\' = other reason. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NSF|EFT_FAIL|VOLUNTARY|PREMIUM_UNAFFORDABLE|ADDRESS_UNKNOWN|POLICY_SURRENDER|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  lapse_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context for the lapse reason, supplementing the lapse_reason_code. May include notes from the billing administrator or automated system messages from Sapiens BillingPro.']
  lapse_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the lapse event. \'pending\' indicates grace period has expired but lapse is not yet finalized; \'confirmed\' indicates lapse is fully processed; \'reinstated\' indicates the policy was reinstated after lapse; \'reversed\' indicates the lapse was reversed due to payment received; \'written_off\' indicates the policy is permanently lapsed with no reinstatement.']
  missed_premium_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled premium due date for which payment was not received, triggering the grace period and subsequent lapse. Corresponds to the billing cycle date in the premium schedule.']
  modal_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The scheduled periodic premium amount for the billing mode (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly) applicable at the time of lapse. Represents the single missed payment that triggered the grace period.']
  nar_at_lapse decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Net Amount at Risk (NAR) — the difference between the death benefit and the policy reserve or account value — as of the lapse effective date. Used for reinsurance cession termination calculations and actuarial risk analysis.']
  non_forfeiture_election_date date [note: 'The date on which the non-forfeiture option was elected by the policyholder or automatically applied by the system. Null if no non-forfeiture option was applicable or elected.']
  non_forfeiture_option varchar [note: 'The non-forfeiture option elected or automatically applied upon lapse. \'extended_term\' = policy continues as term insurance for the original face amount for a limited period; \'reduced_paid_up\' = policy continues as paid-up whole life at a reduced face amount; \'cash_surrender\' = policyholder receives CSV; \'none\' = no non-forfeiture option applicable (e.g., term policy with no CSV).']
  outstanding_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total unpaid premium balance outstanding at the time of lapse, including the missed modal premium and any arrears. Expressed in USD. Used for reinstatement calculations and regulatory reporting.']
  policy_duration_months varchar [note: 'The number of complete months the policy was in force from issue date to lapse effective date. Used in actuarial lapse rate studies, persistency analysis, and DAC (Deferred Acquisition Cost) amortization modeling.']
  policy_issue_date date [note: 'The original issue date of the lapsed policy. Used to calculate policy duration at lapse, which is a key metric for actuarial experience studies and lapse rate analysis.']
  policy_loan_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The outstanding policy loan balance (including accrued interest) at the time of lapse. Reduces the net CSV available for non-forfeiture options and APL eligibility. Relevant for IRC Section 7702 MEC (Modified Endowment Contract) compliance.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known policy contract number that has lapsed. Denormalized from the policy master for operational reporting and regulatory notification without requiring a join.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the lapse event was formally processed and confirmed in the billing system. Represents the business event timestamp when the lapse transaction was executed in Sapiens BillingPro.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'The life insurance product type of the lapsed policy. Drives non-forfeiture option availability, grace period rules, and regulatory notification requirements. Values: WL (Whole Life), UL (Universal Life), IUL (Indexed Universal Life), VUL (Variable Universal Life), term, annuity, DI (Disability Income), LTC (Long-Term Care). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WL|UL|IUL|VUL|term|annuity|DI|LTC — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reduced_paid_up_face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The reduced face amount of the paid-up insurance when the reduced_paid_up non-forfeiture option is elected. Calculated based on the CSV at lapse and the net single premium for paid-up coverage. Null if reduced paid-up was not elected.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory lapse notification must be sent to the policyholder and/or state insurance department. Driven by state-specific requirements for lapse notices, particularly for policies with CSV or for senior policyholders.']
  regulatory_notification_sent_date date [note: 'The date on which the required regulatory lapse notification was sent to the policyholder and/or state insurance department. Null if notification has not yet been sent or is not required.']
  reinstatement_deadline_date date [note: 'The last date by which the policyholder may apply for reinstatement of the lapsed policy under the policy contract terms. Typically 3-5 years from the lapse effective date per state regulations.']
  reinstatement_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lapsed policy is eligible for reinstatement under the policy contract terms and applicable state regulations. Eligibility typically requires reinstatement within 3-5 years of lapse and evidence of insurability.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this lapse event record was sourced. Primarily Sapiens BillingPro lapse administration module. Used for data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  source_system_lapse_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this lapse event record in the source operational system (e.g., Sapiens BillingPro internal lapse transaction ID). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and back-reference to the system of record.']
  state_of_issue varchar [note: 'The two-letter US state code where the policy was issued. Determines applicable state insurance regulations, grace period requirements, non-forfeiture rules, and regulatory notification obligations for the lapse event.']
  third_party_notice_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a lapse notice was sent to a designated third party (e.g., assignee, irrevocable beneficiary, ILIT trustee) as required by the policy contract or state regulation. Relevant for ILIT (Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust) and collateral assignment scenarios.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this lapse event record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks updates such as status changes (e.g., lapse reversed upon payment receipt, reinstatement processed). Audit trail for SOX compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the lapse of a policy due to non-payment of premium after expiration of the grace period. Records lapse effective date, lapse reason code, outstanding premium at lapse, cash surrender value at lapse, automatic premium loan (APL) eligibility flag, non-forfeiture option elected (extended term, reduced paid-up), and any regulatory notification requirements. Source: Sapiens BillingPro lapse administration module.'
}

Table billing.billing_reinstatement {
  billing_reinstatement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the policy reinstatement record in the billing domain. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.account. Business justification: Reinstatements are processed through billing accounts. Back premiums and reinstatement fees are collected via the account. This FK enables account-level reinstatement tracking and supports account bal']
  aml_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_case. Business justification: Reinstatements are a known money-laundering vector (lapsed policy reinstated with illicit funds). billing_reinstatement has aml_review_required (plain attr confirming this business process). Direct FK']
  application_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Reinstatement requires signed application forms, evidence of insurability documents, attending physician statements. Real business process: underwriting review, policy administration workflow, regulat']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: A billing reinstatement requires a new underwriting application for evidence of insurability. The billing reinstatement process is initiated by and dependent on the underwriting application. State ins']
  billing_premium_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_premium_payment. Business justification: A billing reinstatement requires collection of back premiums and fees, which results in a payment record in billing_premium_payment. billing_reinstatement has amount_collected, payment_date, payment_m']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Premium-paying annuity contracts that have lapsed require reinstatement processing. The billing_reinstatement record must reference the annuity_contract to restore contract status, recalculate account']
  gaap_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gaap_reserve. Business justification: Reinstatement restores GAAP reserves released at lapse under LDTI/ASC 944. Finance teams must link reinstatement to the specific GAAP reserve cohort being restored for LDTI roll-forward reporting, ext']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance policy being reinstated. Links this reinstatement transaction to the master policy record.']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: Reinstatement requires evidence of insurability from the insured — APS, MIB check, and health statement requirements are driven by the insured\'s risk profile and underwriting decision. The reinstateme']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Reinstatements require journal entries to restore reserves, recognize back premiums, and record interest. Essential for financial reporting accuracy and audit trail. Links policy administration events']
  kyc_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.kyc_verification. Business justification: Reinstatement triggers AML/KYC re-verification requirements — billing_reinstatement has aml_review_required flag but no direct link to the kyc_verification record. Compliance and AML audit processes r']
  lapse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.lapse_event. Business justification: Reinstatements reverse lapse events. This FK establishes the direct link between a reinstatement transaction and the lapse event it is reversing. Currently reinstatement has a denormalized lapse_date ']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder (owner) who submitted the reinstatement application. Used for customer-level reinstatement analytics.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Reinstatement rules (evidence requirements, windows, interest calculations) are product-specific. Required for underwriting decision support, product administration compliance, reinstatement rate anal']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Reinstatement may occur under a different plan version than original issue if the plan was updated during the lapse period. The billing reinstatement must reference the applicable plan version for cor']
  policy_form_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval.
