  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Group billing arrangements with ERISA plans, employer groups, and association groups carry specific regulatory obligations (ERISA, DOL, state group insurance laws). list_bill_group has erisa_plan_indi']
  administrator_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary billing contact at the group administrator organization. Used for electronic invoice delivery, billing notifications, and remittance reconciliation correspondence.']
  administrator_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the group administrator organization responsible for billing inquiries, remittance, and enrollment changes.']
  administrator_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the group administrator\'s billing contact. Used for billing inquiries, NSF resolution, and remittance reconciliation communications.']
  aml_review_status varchar [note: 'Current Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Know Your Customer (KYC) review status for the group administrator entity. Required for FinCEN BSA compliance and NAIC AML Model Regulation adherence.']
  ape_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) for the list bill group, calculated as the annualized total of all member policy premiums. Used for new business production reporting, sales performance measurement, and actuarial analysis.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number of the group administrator used for ACH/EFT premium remittance. Stored in tokenized or masked form per PCI-DSS requirements. Required for automated debit arrangements.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'ABA routing transit number of the group administrator\'s financial institution used for ACH/EFT premium remittance. Nine-digit identifier assigned by the American Bankers Association.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the group administrator\'s billing address where invoices and billing correspondence are sent.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (suite, floor, department, attention line) for the group administrator\'s billing address.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City of the group administrator\'s billing address.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the group administrator\'s billing address. Typically USA for domestic group life insurance arrangements.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Modal billing frequency at which a consolidated list bill invoice is issued to the group administrator. Determines the billing schedule and premium due dates for all member policies within the group.']
  billing_day_of_month varchar [note: 'Day of the month (1–28) on which the list bill invoice is generated and sent to the group administrator. Aligns with payroll deduction cycles for employer-sponsored plans.']
  billing_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or territory code for the group administrator\'s billing address. Determines applicable state insurance regulations and premium tax jurisdiction.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the list bill group billing arrangement. Controls whether invoices are generated and whether member policies remain in force. Values: active (billing current), suspended (billing paused), lapsed (premium not received past grace period), terminated (group arrangement closed), pending_setup (new group being onboarded), grace_period (payment overdue but within grace window).']
  billing_zip_code varchar [note: 'US ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the group administrator\'s billing address.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this list bill group record. Typically USD for domestic US life insurance group billing.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the list bill group billing arrangement became or becomes effective. Marks the start of the billing relationship and the first premium due date cycle.']
  employer_contribution_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employer contributes to the premium cost for member policies (employer-paid or contributory plan). True = employer contributes; False = fully employee-paid (non-contributory from employer perspective).']
  employer_contribution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total group premium contributed by the employer. Ranges from 0.00 (fully employee-paid) to 100.00 (fully employer-paid). Used for premium allocation, payroll reporting, and IRC Section 79 imputed income calculations.']
  erisa_plan_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the group billing arrangement is associated with an ERISA-governed employer-sponsored benefit plan. True = ERISA plan subject to DOL fiduciary and reporting requirements; False = non-ERISA arrangement.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days after the premium due date during which the group administrator may remit payment without triggering lapse or suspension of member policies. Typically 31 days per NAIC model regulation.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the employer, association, or Brokerage General Agency (BGA) acting as the group administrator responsible for remitting consolidated premium payments on behalf of member policyholders.']
  group_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the list bill group by the billing system. Used on remittance statements, invoices, and correspondence with the group administrator. Maps to the group billing account number in Sapiens BillingPro.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the group administrator entity. Determines billing workflow, regulatory treatment, and commission routing. Values: employer (payroll deduction sponsor), association (member organization), bga (Brokerage General Agency), mga (Managing General Agent), tpa (Third Party Administrator), trust (ILIT or employer trust). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: employer|association|bga|mga|tpa|trust — promote to reference product]']
  invoice_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the consolidated list bill invoice is delivered to the group administrator. Values: email (electronic delivery), mail (paper mail), portal (self-service web portal), edi (Electronic Data Interchange), fax.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the most recent consolidated premium payment received from the group administrator. Used for remittance reconciliation and underpayment/overpayment detection.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent consolidated premium payment was received from the group administrator. Used for grace period tracking, lapse administration, and billing status determination.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the list bill group record, including status changes, billing parameter updates, or administrator contact changes. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  member_policy_count varchar [note: 'Current number of individual employee or member policies enrolled under this list bill group arrangement. Used for billing validation, group size reporting, and experience rating.']
  next_bill_date date [note: 'Scheduled date on which the next consolidated list bill invoice will be generated and sent to the group administrator. Derived from billing cycle and billing day of month but stored for operational scheduling.']
  nsf_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of Non-Sufficient Funds (NSF) or returned payment events for this list bill group. Used for risk monitoring, payment method review, and potential suspension triggers.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the group administrator remits consolidated premium payments. Drives EFT/ACH setup, lockbox routing, and NSF handling workflows. Values: ach (Automated Clearing House), eft (Electronic Funds Transfer), check, wire (wire transfer), lockbox (bank lockbox remittance).']
  payroll_deduction_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether member premiums are collected via employer payroll deduction and remitted as part of the consolidated list bill. True = payroll deduction arrangement; False = direct employer-paid or other remittance method.']
  remittance_reconciliation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to reconcile the group administrator\'s remittance against individual member policy premiums. Self-administered: employer submits detailed enrollment roster; carrier-administered: insurer maintains member records; tpa-administered: Third Party Administrator (TPA) manages reconciliation.']
  setup_date date [note: 'Date on which the list bill group arrangement was initially established and configured in the billing system. Represents the record creation date from a business perspective.']
  sic_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) code identifying the industry of the employer or group administrator. Used for experience rating, underwriting risk segmentation, and group life insurance pricing.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational billing system of record from which this list bill group record was sourced (e.g., SAPIENS_BILLING, MAJESCO_BILLING). Supports data lineage and ETL reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) or Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) of the group administrator entity. Required for IRS reporting, AML/KYC compliance, and premium tax filings.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the list bill group billing arrangement was or is scheduled to be terminated. Null for active, open-ended arrangements. Triggers conversion or lapse processing for member policies.']
  total_group_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total consolidated premium amount billed to the group administrator for the current billing cycle, aggregating all member policy premiums within the group. Expressed in USD. Used for remittance reconciliation and financial reporting.']

  Note: 'Master record for employer or group list billing arrangements where a single bill is issued to a group administrator (employer, association, BGA) covering multiple employee/member policies. Captures group administrator identity, group billing address, billing cycle, total group premium, remittance reconciliation method, and group billing status. Supports payroll deduction and employer-sponsored life insurance billing workflows.'
}

Table billing.premium_adjustment {
  premium_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each premium adjustment transaction record on a policy billing schedule. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.account. Business justification: Premium adjustments affect account-level balances and billing. While adjustments are policy-specific, they must also be tracked at the account level for account reconciliation and balance integrity. T']
  adjustment_notice_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Premium adjustments require supporting documentation: rate change notices, rider amendment forms, COLA calculation disclosures. Real business process: policy administration, regulatory filing requirem']
  charge_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.charge_schedule. Business justification: Premium adjustments for annuity products involving surrender charge recalculations or M&E fee changes must reference the applicable charge schedule. This link enables billing to correctly compute adju']
  coi_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.coi_rate_schedule. Business justification: COI rate actions trigger premium adjustments for UL in-force policies. The adjustment must reference the new COI rate schedule to document the rate basis for the change, supporting actuarial audit of ']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance policy whose billing schedule is being adjusted. Links the adjustment to the master policy record.']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: Premium adjustments are driven by insured risk changes — tobacco status change, occupation reclassification, table rating removal, or attained-age recalculation. Regulatory rate change filings and pre']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Premium adjustments from rate changes, rider additions, or corrections require correcting journal entries. Essential for revenue accuracy, SOX controls, and audit trail. Links billing changes to finan']
  new_risk_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: Premium adjustments triggered by post-issue risk class changes (rating improvements, reconsiderations) must reference the new underwriting risk_class. premium_adjustment.new_risk_class and prior_risk_']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Premium adjustments must comply with product plan rules (COLA provisions, face amount change limits, rate adjustment triggers). Required for billing accuracy validation, regulatory compliance audits, ']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Premium adjustments triggered by plan version changes must reference the new plan version to document what changed. This supports regulatory reporting on version-driven billing changes, actuarial audi']
  premium_bill_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific premium bill record on which this adjustment is applied, linking the adjustment to the billing cycle in which it takes effect.']
  premium_rate_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.premium_rate_table. Business justification: Premium adjustments for attained-age recalculations, face amount changes, and rate revisions are directly driven by a specific premium rate table version. Regulatory filings and actuarial audit trails']
  premium_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_schedule. Business justification: Premium adjustments modify premium schedules (rate changes, face amount changes, rider additions/removals, COLA adjustments). This FK links the adjustment transaction to the premium schedule being mod']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.producer. Business justification: Premium adjustments impact commission calculations and require producer notification for servicing. Tracking the servicing producer enables proper commission adjustments, chargebacks, and producer com']
  rate_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.rate_action. Business justification: Rate actions (COI increases, crediting rate changes) directly trigger premium adjustments for in-force policies. This link provides end-to-end traceability from product rate decision to billing impact']
  rate_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.rate_filing. Business justification: Premium adjustments implementing approved rate changes must reference the DOI-approved rate filing. Required for rate compliance verification, audit trail, and ensuring adjustments match approved rate']
  reversed_adjustment_premium_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original premium_adjustment_id that this record reverses, when reversal_indicator is True. Enables bidirectional audit trail linking between original and reversal adjustment records.']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Premium adjustments must comply with state-mandated consumer notice requirements, effective date rules, and rate change limitations. state_of_issue is a denormalized reference; replacing with FK to st']
  statutory_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.statutory_reserve. Business justification: Premium adjustments (face amount changes, rate revisions, COLA) directly affect statutory reserve levels. Actuarial and finance teams must link adjustments to the impacted reserve for SAP reserve chan']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net monetary change in modal premium resulting from this adjustment (new_modal_premium minus prior_modal_premium). A positive value indicates a premium increase; a negative value indicates a decrease. Used for financial reporting and actuarial analysis.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for this premium adjustment transaction, assigned by the Policy Administration System or Billing System (e.g., ADJ-2024-00123456). Used for audit trail, correspondence, and reconciliation.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the business reason for the premium adjustment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ANNIV_RATE_CHG|ATTAINED_AGE|RIDER_ADD|RIDER_REMOVE|FACE_AMT_CHG|RISK_RECLASS|COLA|REG_RATE_REV|UW_REVISION|BILLING_CORR — promote to reference product]']
  adjustment_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the reason for the premium adjustment, providing additional context beyond the standardized reason code. May include details such as the specific rider added, the new risk class assigned, or the regulatory revision reference.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the premium adjustment record. \'pending\' indicates awaiting approval; \'approved\' indicates authorized but not yet applied to billing; \'applied\' indicates posted to the billing schedule; \'reversed\' indicates a previously applied adjustment that has been backed out; \'rejected\' indicates the adjustment was denied.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the direction and nature of the premium adjustment. \'increase\' indicates the new premium is higher than the prior; \'decrease\' indicates the new premium is lower; \'correction\' indicates a billing error fix with no net change in intended premium; \'reversal\' indicates the cancellation of a previously applied adjustment.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this premium adjustment required and received formal authorization. \'not_required\' for system-generated adjustments within automated rules; \'pending\' for adjustments awaiting human review; \'approved\' for authorized adjustments; \'rejected\' for denied adjustments. Supports SOX and market conduct compliance.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this premium adjustment was formally approved by the authorizing user or automated system. Null when approval_status is \'not_required\' or \'pending\'. Supports SOX audit trail and turnaround time reporting.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier of the individual or automated process that approved this premium adjustment. Null when approval_status is \'not_required\'. Required for SOX audit trail on manual or exception adjustments.']
  attained_age varchar [note: 'The insured\'s attained age (in whole years) at the time of this premium adjustment. Relevant for attained-age recalculation adjustments on Yearly Renewable Term (YRT), Universal Life (UL), and Indexed Universal Life (IUL) products where COI rates are age-banded.']
  billing_mode varchar [note: 'The modal billing frequency of the policy at the time of this adjustment (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, or monthly). Determines the modal factor applied to convert annual premium to the scheduled payment amount.']
  cola_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The Cost of Living Adjustment (COLA) percentage rate applied to the premium when the adjustment_reason_code is COLA. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0300 for 3%). Null for non-COLA adjustments.']
  component_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the specific premium component affected by this adjustment: \'base\' for the base policy premium, \'rider\' for a policy rider premium, \'coi\' for Cost of Insurance (COI) charges on universal life products, or \'apl\' for Automatic Premium Loan (APL) repayment component.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this premium adjustment record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency policy administration for international or expatriate life insurance products.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the premium adjustment becomes effective on the policy billing schedule. This is the business event date — the date from which the new premium amount applies, which may differ from the transaction processing date.']
  mec_test_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this premium adjustment triggered a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) test review under IRC Section 7702A. True when the adjustment requires validation that the new premium level does not cause the policy to fail the 7-pay test and become classified as a MEC.']
  new_annual_premium decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annualized premium amount (Annual Premium Equivalent) that will apply after this adjustment, calculated by annualizing the new modal premium based on the billing mode. Supports APE reporting, actuarial reserving, and regulatory filings.']
  new_face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The policy face amount (death benefit) that applies after this adjustment. Captured when the adjustment is triggered by a face amount change. Used to verify 7702 compliance and recalculate guideline premium limits.']
  new_modal_premium decimal(18,2) [note: 'The modal premium amount (per billing frequency) that will apply to the policy billing schedule after this adjustment is applied. This becomes the new scheduled premium for future billing cycles.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, exception handling comments, or supplementary context recorded by the billing operations team regarding this premium adjustment. Not used for structured data; intended for human-readable commentary.']
  policy_year varchar [note: 'The policy year (1-based count from policy issue date) in which this premium adjustment is effective. Used for anniversary-based rate change processing, attained age recalculations, and actuarial experience studies.']
  prior_annual_premium decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annualized premium amount (Annual Premium Equivalent) in effect before this adjustment, calculated by annualizing the prior modal premium based on the billing mode. Supports APE reporting and actuarial valuation.']
  prior_face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The policy face amount (death benefit) in effect before this adjustment. Captured when the adjustment is triggered by a face amount change, as face amount directly drives base premium calculations.']
  prior_modal_premium decimal(18,2) [note: 'The modal premium amount (per billing frequency) in effect on the policy billing schedule immediately before this adjustment was applied. Provides the baseline for calculating the adjustment delta and supports audit trail requirements.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the premium adjustment was fully processed and posted to the billing schedule in the Policy Administration or Billing System. Supports audit trail and SLA monitoring for billing operations.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'The internal product code identifying the life insurance or annuity product type associated with the policy (e.g., WL, UL, IUL, VUL, YRT, SPIA, FIA). Supports product-level premium adjustment analytics and actuarial segmentation.']
  regulatory_revision_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the regulatory filing, state department of insurance bulletin, or NAIC model regulation revision that mandated this premium rate adjustment. Populated when adjustment_reason_code is REG_RATE_REV. Supports regulatory compliance audit trail.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this adjustment record is a reversal of a previously applied adjustment. True when this record cancels and backs out a prior adjustment; False for original adjustments. Used in financial reconciliation to net out reversed transactions.']
  rider_code varchar [note: 'The standardized code identifying the specific policy rider whose premium is being adjusted, when the component_type is \'rider\'. Examples include ADB (Accidental Death Benefit), AWL (Automatic Waiver of Premium), LTC (Long-Term Care), or DI (Disability Income) riders. Null when adjustment applies to base premium or COI.']
  seven_pay_premium decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum annual premium allowed under the IRC Section 7702A seven-pay test at the time of this adjustment. Captured when mec_test_flag is True to document the compliance threshold against which the new premium was evaluated.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this premium adjustment transaction. \'PAS\' = Policy Administration System (e.g., Sapiens LifePro, FAST); \'BILLING\' = Billing and Premium Collection System (e.g., Sapiens BillingPro); \'UW_WORKBENCH\' = Underwriting Workbench; \'ACTUARIAL\' = Actuarial Valuation System; \'MANUAL\' = manual override by authorized user.']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'The transaction identifier from the originating source system (Policy Administration System or Billing System) that authorized and triggered this premium adjustment. Provides end-to-end traceability from the billing data product back to the system of record.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the premium adjustment transaction was created and recorded in the source system. Represents the system-of-record capture time, distinct from the business effective date.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of premium adjustments applied to a policy\'s billing schedule, including rate changes due to policy anniversary, attained age recalculation, rider addition or removal, face amount change, risk class reclassification, cost of living adjustment (COLA), or regulatory rate revision. Captures adjustment effective date, prior premium amount, new premium amount, adjustment reason code, authorizing source system transaction, and approval status. Provides audit trail for all premium amount changes on the billing schedule.'
}

This is a direct regulatory accounting requirement; eve']

Business justification: Fixed and indexed annuity general account liabilities are backed by investment portfolios.   ifrs17_contract_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ifrs17_contract_group. Business justification: Annuity contracts are grouped into IFRS 17 cohorts by issue year and profitability for CSM calculation, risk adjustment, and international financial reporting.   jurisdiction_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_license. Business justification: Contracts can only be issued in states where carrier holds active license. Links contract state_of_issue to jurisdiction license for validation at issue and ongoing compliance monitoring. Prevents una']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Annuity contracts are issued by specific legal entities for NAIC company code reporting, statutory reserve allocation, RBC calculations, and consolidated financial statements.   owner_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party record representing the contract owner — the individual, joint owner, trust, or entity that holds legal ownership of the annuity contract and has rights to make changes, withdrawals, and surrender. Maps to the owner record in the CRM or policyholder master.']
  pbr_model_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.pbr_model_segment. Business justification: VM-21 principle-based reserving requires each annuity contract to be assigned to a PBR model segment for stochastic and deterministic reserve calculations. This is a direct NAIC regulatory requirement']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuity product definition record in the product master, identifying the specific product series, plan code, and feature set (e.g., crediting strategy options, rider availability, surrender charge schedule template) associated with this contract.']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Contracts issued under specific plan versions require version-level rate guarantees, benefit structures, and regulatory compliance parameters. Critical for rate action processing, benefit calculations']
  policy_form_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval. Business justification: Contracts must use state-approved policy forms at issue. Links contract to approved form filing for state_of_issue validation. Essential for market conduct exam defense and form usage audits. Prevents']
  primary_annuity_annuitant_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party record representing the annuitant — the individual whose life expectancy governs the payout calculations and upon whose death the death benefit or survivor benefit is triggered. May differ from the contract owner in trust-owned or entity-owned contracts.']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent or producer record in the Agent and Commission Management System who sold and submitted this annuity contract. Used for commission calculation, suitability review attribution, and distribution analytics.']
  rate_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.rate_action. Business justification: Rate actions drive renewal cap/participation rate changes on FIA contracts. Policyholder notification, opt-out processing, and actuarial impact reporting require linking each contract to the rate_acti']
  risk_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: Life-contingent annuity contracts (life-only payout, GMDB riders) are issued under a specific underwriting risk class governing mortality assumptions, COI charges, and statutory reserve calculations. ']
  selling_agency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.agency. Business justification: Agency-level commission statements, production reports, and state appointment verification at contract issuance require linking each annuity contract to its selling agency. The existing producer_id ca']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable annuity contracts are issued under a registered separate account (SEC/NAIC requirement). This link supports contract administration, SEC registration reporting, and NAIC Schedule D filing. A ']
  state_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.state_approval. Business justification: State-specific contract terms (free look period, grace period, nonforfeiture options) derive from the state_approval record for the plan/state combination. DOI compliance and state-specific benefit ad']
  suitability_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.suitability_rule. Business justification: Regulatory compliance (FINRA Rule 2330, state suitability laws) requires tracing which suitability_rule version governed each contract at issuance. Compliance audits and DOI examinations depend on thi']
  surrender_charge_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the surrender charge schedule definition record that governs this contract\'s declining surrender charge percentages by contract year, free withdrawal allowance percentage, MVA applicability, and waiver conditions (nursing home, terminal illness, unemployment, disability). Determined at issuance based on product and state.']
  uw_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Annuity contracts often originate from underwriting applications (deferred annuities sold with life insurance, standalone annuity applications requiring underwriting for large cases or impaired risk a']
  uw_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.decision. Business justification: New business processing and audit trails require tracing which underwriting decision authorized contract issuance. Compliance and new business operations teams use this link to verify approved face am']
  account_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current Account Value (AV) of the annuity contract as of the last valuation date — the total accumulated value including all premiums paid, interest/index credits earned, and minus all withdrawals, fees, and charges. The AV is the primary valuation metric for deferred annuity contracts and drives CSV, death benefit, and GMWB benefit base calculations.']
  account_value_date date [note: 'The valuation date as of which the account_value field was last calculated and recorded. Essential for point-in-time reporting, actuarial reserving, and reconciliation with the Actuarial Valuation System (e.g., AXIS, Prophet).']
  annuitization_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract transitioned from the accumulation phase to the payout (annuitization) phase. Null for contracts still in accumulation. Populated when contract_status = ANNUITIZED. Drives payout benefit calculations and IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio computations.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable contract number assigned by the Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity, FAST Annuity) at issuance. Used in all owner correspondence, statements, and regulatory filings. Maps to the contract_id field in the source system.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the annuity contract as maintained in the Annuity Administration Platform. ACTIVE = in-force accumulation or payout; SURRENDERED = full surrender processed; ANNUITIZED = converted to payout phase; LAPSED = terminated for non-payment; PENDING = application received, not yet issued; DEATH_CLAIM = death benefit claim in progress.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the annuity product type: FIXED (traditional fixed annuity), FIA (Fixed Indexed Annuity), VARIABLE (Variable Annuity / VUL-linked), SPIA (Single Premium Immediate Annuity), or MYGA (Multi-Year Guaranteed Annuity). Drives product-specific processing rules, crediting methods, and regulatory treatment.']
  cost_basis_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The owner\'s investment in the contract (cost basis) for IRC Section 72 tax purposes — the total after-tax premiums paid minus any previously recovered basis through tax-free distributions. Used to calculate the taxable portion of withdrawals and the exclusion ratio upon annuitization. Carried forward from prior contract in 1035 exchanges.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this annuity contract record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Represents the data ingestion audit trail timestamp, not the contract issue date. Required for SOX audit trail and data lineage compliance.']
  csv_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Cash Surrender Value (CSV) — the net amount the contract owner would receive upon full surrender, calculated as the Account Value minus applicable surrender charges and market value adjustment (MVA). Represents the owner\'s actual liquidity value at any point in time.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the annuity contract is denominated (e.g., USD). All monetary values on this contract record are expressed in this currency. Supports multi-currency operations for international annuity products.']
  current_surrender_charge_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The surrender charge percentage currently applicable to this contract based on the current contract year within the surrender charge schedule (e.g., 0.0700 = 7%). Updated annually on the contract anniversary. Used to calculate CSV and net surrender proceeds.']
  exchange_prior_contract_number varchar [note: 'The contract or policy number of the prior insurance or annuity contract that was exchanged into this contract via a 1035 Exchange. Populated only when is_1035_exchange = True. Required for IRS cost basis continuity and tax reporting compliance.']
  fia_index_strategy varchar [note: 'For FIA (Fixed Indexed Annuity) contracts, the selected index crediting strategy (e.g., S&P 500 Annual Point-to-Point with Cap, S&P 500 Monthly Sum with Cap, Bloomberg US Dynamic Balance Index). Determines how index-linked interest credits are calculated. Null for non-FIA contract types. May reference a crediting strategy code from the product definition.']
  free_look_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the owner\'s free-look (right to return) period expires. During the free-look period, the owner may cancel the contract and receive a full refund of premiums paid. Typically 10–30 days from contract delivery, as mandated by state insurance regulations.']
  free_withdrawal_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The percentage of the account value (or premium) that the owner may withdraw each contract year without incurring surrender charges, as defined in the contract (e.g., 0.1000 = 10% free withdrawal allowance). Unused free withdrawal amounts typically do not carry forward.']
  initial_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The first premium payment received at contract issuance, in contract currency. For single-premium products (SPIA, MYGA), this is the total purchase payment. For flexible-premium products, this is the first deposit. Used as the baseline for surrender charge calculations and cost basis tracking.']
  is_1035_exchange boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this annuity contract was funded via a 1035 Exchange (tax-free exchange of an existing life insurance policy, endowment, or annuity contract under IRC Section 1035). True = funded by 1035 exchange; False = direct premium payment. Affects cost basis tracking and tax reporting.']
  is_mva_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Market Value Adjustment (MVA) applies to surrenders or withdrawals in excess of the free withdrawal allowance during the surrender charge period. True = MVA applies (adjusts surrender proceeds up or down based on interest rate movements); False = no MVA. Relevant primarily for MYGA and fixed annuity products.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the annuity contract was formally issued and became effective. Serves as the anchor date for surrender charge schedule calculations, free-look period, and contract year determinations. Corresponds to the policy_effective_date in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  maturity_date date [note: 'The date on which the annuity contract reaches its scheduled maturity, typically triggering mandatory annuitization or lump-sum distribution. Relevant for MYGA and deferred annuity products. Null for SPIA contracts which begin payout immediately.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the legal ownership structure of the annuity contract: INDIVIDUAL (single natural person), JOINT (two natural persons, typically spouses), TRUST (e.g., ILIT — Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust), or ENTITY (corporation, partnership, or other non-natural person). Affects tax treatment, RMD rules, and death benefit distribution.']
  payout_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which annuity income payments are made to the annuitant or payee during the payout phase (MONTHLY, QUARTERLY, SEMI_ANNUAL, ANNUAL). Null for contracts in accumulation phase or with LUMP_SUM payout option.']
  payout_option varchar [note: 'The elected payout option governing how annuity income is distributed during the payout phase: LIFE_ONLY (payments for annuitant\'s lifetime), LIFE_PERIOD_CERTAIN (lifetime with guaranteed minimum period), JOINT_SURVIVOR (payments continue to survivor), PERIOD_CERTAIN (fixed period regardless of survival), LUMP_SUM (single payment). Null for contracts still in accumulation phase.']
  phase varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the annuity contract is currently in the ACCUMULATION phase (premiums growing tax-deferred) or the PAYOUT phase (periodic income distributions being made to the annuitant). Drives which processing modules are active in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  rmd_amount_current_year decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) amount that must be distributed from this contract in the current calendar year, based on the prior year-end account value and the IRS Uniform Lifetime Table. Null if rmd_required = False. Tracked by the RMD module of the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  rmd_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract is subject to IRS Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) rules. True = qualified contract (IRA, 403b, etc.) where the owner has reached RMD age and annual distributions are mandated; False = not subject to RMD (non-qualified or owner below RMD age). Drives RMD tracking module in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  state_of_issue varchar [note: 'The two-letter US state code of the state in which the annuity contract was issued and delivered. Determines applicable state insurance regulations, surrender charge requirements, free-look period, suitability rules, and tax withholding requirements. Aligns with NAIC state-specific filing requirements.']
  surrender_charge_period_years varchar [note: 'The total number of contract years during which surrender charges apply, as defined by the product\'s surrender charge schedule (e.g., 7-year, 10-year surrender charge period). After this period, the contract is fully liquid without surrender charges.']
  tax_qualification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the tax qualification status of the annuity contract under IRS rules. NON_QUALIFIED = funded with after-tax dollars, IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio applies; TRADITIONAL_IRA / ROTH_IRA / 403B / 457B / SEP_IRA / SIMPLE_IRA = qualified plan funding with specific RMD and distribution rules. Drives tax reporting (1099-R) and RMD calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NON_QUALIFIED|TRADITIONAL_IRA|ROTH_IRA|403B|457B|SEP_IRA|SIMPLE_IRA — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this annuity contract record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated on every change event sourced from the Annuity Administration Platform. Required for SOX audit trail, change data capture, and data lineage.']

  Note: 'Master record for each annuity contract issued by the company, serving as the SSOT for all annuity contract administration. Captures contract type (Fixed, Fixed Indexed, Variable, SPIA, MYGA), issue date, contract status, annuitant identity, owner identity, contract phase (accumulation vs payout), surrender charge schedule (declining charge percentages by contract year, free withdrawal allowance, MVA applicability, and waiver conditions for nursing home, terminal illness, unemployment, disability), market value adjustment (MVA) flag, tax qualification status (qualified IRA, Roth IRA, 403b, 457, non-qualified), IRC Section 72 tax treatment, 1035 exchange indicator, ownership type (individual, joint, trust, entity), and contract currency. Serves as the parent entity for all annuity domain transactions and valuations.'
}

Table annuity.account_value {
  account_value_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each periodic account value snapshot record in the annuity valuation ledger. Primary key for this table. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete periodic valuation event for an annuity contract.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuity contract for which this account value snapshot is recorded. Links to the annuity contract master record in the Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity, FAST Annuity).']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Daily account value changes (credited interest, rider charges, index credits, COI deductions) generate GAAP/SAP journal entries. Finance teams reconcile account value movements to GL postings each val']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Account values must be attributed to issuing legal entity for consolidated balance sheet reporting, statutory reserve calculations, and regulatory capital adequacy (RBC) filings. Required for multi-en']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuity contract owner (policyholder/annuitant) associated with this valuation record. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for policyholder annual statement generation and KYC/AML compliance.']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: Account value snapshots are produced within actuarial valuation cycles. Linking to valuation_run enables reconciliation between contract-level account values and aggregate reserve totals, a standard a']
  bov_account_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Account Value (AV) at the beginning of the valuation period (BOY/BOM/BOQ/BOD depending on cycle). Represents the opening balance before any transactions, credits, or charges are applied during the period. Sourced from the Annuity Administration Platform contract administration module.']
  coi_charge decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost of Insurance (COI) charge deducted from the account value during the valuation period. Applicable primarily to variable annuities and indexed products with mortality and expense risk charges. Calculated based on the Net Amount at Risk (NAR) and applicable COI rates.']
  contract_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the annuity contract lifecycle at the time of this valuation: accumulation (premium growth phase), annuitization (conversion to income stream), payout (systematic income payments), surrender (contract terminated by owner), or lapse (contract terminated for non-payment). Critical for determining applicable valuation rules.']
  contract_year varchar [note: 'The contract year number at the valuation date, calculated from the contract issue date. Used to determine applicable surrender charge rates, free withdrawal corridors, and benefit base roll-up periods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this account value record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and SOX compliance.']
  credited_interest_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total interest credited to the contract account value during the valuation period for fixed and fixed-indexed annuity products. Includes declared interest rates for fixed products and guaranteed minimum interest for FIA products. Excludes index credits which are tracked separately.']
  csv decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cash Surrender Value (CSV) of the annuity contract at the valuation date, representing the net amount the contract owner would receive upon full surrender after deducting applicable surrender charges, market value adjustments (MVA), and outstanding loans. Key metric for policyholder statements and regulatory reporting.']
  death_benefit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current death benefit (DB) payable upon the death of the annuitant or insured at the valuation date. May equal the account value, a return-of-premium amount, or a stepped-up value depending on the contract terms and any applicable GMDB rider.']
  eov_account_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Account Value (AV) at the end of the valuation period (EOY/EOM/EOQ/EOD depending on cycle). Represents the closing balance after all transactions, credits, and charges have been applied. This is the primary AV figure used for policyholder statements, ALM, and actuarial reserving.']
  fia_cap_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The maximum index credit rate (cap) applied to the linked index return for FIA products during the crediting period (as a decimal, e.g., 0.06 = 6%). Null for non-FIA products or strategies without a cap.']
  fia_floor_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The minimum index credit rate (floor) guaranteed for FIA products during the crediting period (as a decimal, e.g., 0.00 = 0%). Protects the contract owner from negative index returns. Null for non-FIA products.']
  fia_index_strategy varchar [note: 'The index crediting strategy elected by the contract owner for FIA products (e.g., S&P 500 Annual Point-to-Point with Cap, S&P 500 Monthly Sum, MSCI EAFE Participation Rate). Determines how index credits are calculated. Null for non-FIA products.']
  fia_participation_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The participation rate (as a decimal, e.g., 0.80 = 80%) applied to the linked index return when calculating index credits for FIA products. Null for non-FIA products. Sourced from the Annuity Administration Platform crediting parameters.']
  free_withdrawal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount that can be withdrawn from the contract during the current contract year without incurring surrender charges, typically 10% of the account value or premium. Used for withdrawal processing and policyholder servicing.']
  gaap_reserve decimal(18,2) [note: 'GAAP reserve for this annuity contract at the valuation date, calculated per FASB ASC 944 Long Duration Targeted Improvements (LDTI). Represents the present value of future benefit payments less the present value of future gross premiums under GAAP accounting.']
  gmab_benefit_base decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB) benefit base at the valuation date. Represents the guaranteed minimum account value at the end of the accumulation period. Null if no GMAB rider is attached.']
  gmdb_benefit_base decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit (GMDB) benefit base at the valuation date. Represents the guaranteed floor for the death benefit, which may be higher than the current account value due to step-up provisions or roll-up features. Used for NAR calculation and actuarial reserving under LDTI.']
  gmib_benefit_base decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) benefit base at the valuation date. Represents the accumulation value used to calculate the guaranteed minimum annuitization income. Null if no GMIB rider is attached.']
  gmwb_benefit_base decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) benefit base at the valuation date. Represents the principal amount from which guaranteed withdrawal amounts are calculated. May grow through roll-up credits or step-ups. Null if no GMWB rider is attached.']
  gross_withdrawal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross amount withdrawn from the contract account value during the valuation period, including both systematic withdrawals and ad-hoc partial surrenders. Used to calculate surrender charges, tax reporting under IRC Section 72, and GMWB benefit base adjustments.']
  index_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Index-linked interest credit applied to the contract account value during the valuation period for Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA) products. Calculated based on the performance of the linked index (e.g., S&P 500), subject to applicable cap, floor, and participation rate. Zero for non-FIA products.']
  is_1035_exchange boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this annuity contract was funded in whole or in part through a tax-free 1035 Exchange (IRC Section 1035). Affects cost basis tracking, tax reporting, and gain/loss calculations for IRC Section 72 purposes.']
  is_mec boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the annuity contract has been classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) under IRC Section 7702A. MEC status affects the tax treatment of withdrawals and loans, triggering LIFO tax treatment and potential 10% penalty.']
  is_qualified_contract boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the annuity contract is a qualified contract (held within a tax-qualified retirement plan such as IRA, 401(k), 403(b)) or a non-qualified contract. Determines applicable tax treatment under IRC Section 72 and RMD requirements.']
  nar_gmdb decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Amount at Risk (NAR) for the GMDB guarantee, calculated as the excess of the GMDB benefit base over the current account value (max of zero). Used for COI charge calculation, reinsurance cession, and actuarial reserving under LDTI/IFRS 17.']
  nar_living_benefit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Amount at Risk (NAR) for living benefit guarantees (GMWB/GMIB/GMAB), representing the present value of the excess of guaranteed benefit payments over the projected account value. Used for actuarial reserving under LDTI and IFRS 17 and reinsurance reporting.']
  net_premium_applied decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total net premium (after applicable premium loads and taxes) credited to the contract account value during the valuation period. Includes initial premiums, subsequent premiums, and 1035 exchange proceeds applied during the period.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Internal product code identifying the annuity product type (e.g., FIA, VUL, SPIA, GMWB annuity). Used to apply product-specific valuation rules, crediting methods, and benefit base calculations.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the annuity product: Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA), Variable Annuity (VA), Single Premium Immediate Annuity (SPIA), Multi-Year Guaranteed Annuity (MYGA), Fixed Premium Deferred Annuity (FPDA), or Fixed Premium Immediate Annuity (FPIA). Drives crediting method and benefit calculation logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FIA|VA|SPIA|MYGA|FPDA|FPIA|RILA|DIA — promote to reference product]']
  rider_charge_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total of all rider charges (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB, GMDB, LTC, etc.) deducted from the account value during the valuation period. Represents the aggregate cost of all optional benefit riders attached to the contract.']
  rmd_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) amount calculated for this contract for the current tax year, applicable to qualified annuity contracts held in IRAs or employer-sponsored retirement plans. Tracked per IRS regulations to ensure compliance with IRC Section 401(a)(9).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity, FAST Annuity) for this account value snapshot. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and ETL traceability between the operational system and the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  statutory_reserve decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated statutory reserve for this annuity contract at the valuation date, calculated per NAIC Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP) and Principle-Based Reserving (PBR) requirements. Sourced from or reconciled with the Actuarial Valuation System (e.g., MoSes, AXIS, Prophet).']
  sub_account_gain_loss decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net investment gain or loss from variable annuity sub-account performance during the valuation period. Applicable only to Variable Annuity (VA) and Variable Universal Life (VUL) products. Reflects market value changes in the separate account sub-accounts.']
  surrender_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Applicable surrender charge that would be assessed if the contract were fully surrendered at the valuation date. Calculated based on the surrender charge schedule and contract duration. Used for CSV calculation and policyholder disclosure.']
  surrender_charge_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Applicable surrender charge rate (as a decimal, e.g., 0.07 = 7%) at the valuation date based on the contract\'s surrender charge schedule and contract year. Used to calculate the surrender charge amount and free withdrawal corridor.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this account value record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Populated on restatements, corrections, or status changes.']
  valuation_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency classification of this valuation snapshot indicating whether it is a daily, monthly, quarterly, or annual valuation record. Drives downstream actuarial and ALM processing cadence.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The as-of date for this account value snapshot. Represents the principal real-world business event date (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category). May be daily, month-end, quarter-end, or year-end depending on the valuation cycle. Used for actuarial reserving, ALM, and regulatory reporting.']
  valuation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this account value record indicating whether the valuation is preliminary (in-process), final (approved), restated (corrected after finalization), or voided (cancelled). Supports LIFECYCLE_STATUS requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']

  Note: 'Authoritative periodic valuation ledger and contract valuation snapshot for each annuity contract, capturing the full financial position at each valuation date (daily, monthly, quarterly, annually). Records beginning-of-period and ending-of-period Account Value (AV), net premiums applied, withdrawals deducted, COI charges, rider charges, credited interest, index credits (FIA), sub-account performance (variable), surrender value (CSV), death benefit amount, GMDB value, GMWB/GMIB/GMAB benefit base snapshots, net amount at risk (NAR) for each guarantee, accrued rider charges, and statutory reserve estimate. Serves as the single source of truth for ALL annuity contract valuations and periodic valuation snapshots (subsumes contract_valuation), supporting policyholder annual statements, ALM, actuarial reserving (LDTI/IFRS 17), valuation cycle feeds, and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table annuity.benefit_base {
  benefit_base_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the guaranteed benefit base record associated with a living benefit rider on an annuity contract. Primary key for this entity.']
  annuitant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.annuitant. Business justification: GMWB/GMIB/GMAB living benefit administration requires the annuitant\'s age for guaranteed withdrawal rate determination, step-up eligibility, and roll-up period calculations. benefit_base tracks owner_']
  assumption_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.assumption_set. Business justification: Living benefit (GMWB/GMIB/GMAB) benefit base net amount at risk drives rider reserve calculations. The assumption set (lapse, utilization, mortality) used for those reserves must be traceable from the']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuity contract to which this benefit base and living benefit rider are attached. Links the benefit base to the parent annuity contract record.']
  gaap_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gaap_reserve. Business justification: Benefit base values (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB, GMDB) are the primary inputs to LDTI/GAAP MRB fair value reserve calculations. Actuaries and finance teams must link each benefit base record to its GAAP reserve']
  hedge_portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Living benefit riders (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB) create market risk hedged via derivative portfolios. Actuarial teams calculate net amount at risk, hedge effectiveness (ASC 815), and RBC C3 charges by linking']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Living benefit riders (GMWB/GMIB/GMAB) have version-specific roll-up rates, step-up provisions, and guaranteed withdrawal rates. Essential for benefit base calculations, rider charge assessments, and ']
  rider_benefit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.rider_benefit. Business justification: benefit_base manages the guaranteed benefit base amounts for living benefit riders (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB). rider_benefit is the master record for each optional rider attached to an annuity contract. A ben']
  rider_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific living benefit rider (GMWB, GMIB, or GMAB) attached to the annuity contract that governs this benefit base record.']
  statutory_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.statutory_reserve. Business justification: Benefit base amounts drive CARVM/PBR statutory reserve calculations for living benefit riders. State regulators require insurers to demonstrate reserve adequacy at the benefit base level; statutory ac']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: Benefit base values (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB) are calculated during actuarial valuation runs. Regulatory reserve reporting requires tracing benefit base amounts to the specific valuation run that produced th']
  annual_guaranteed_withdrawal decimal(18,2) [note: 'For GMWB riders, the maximum dollar amount the contract owner may withdraw annually while maintaining the guaranteed benefit base. Calculated as the benefit base multiplied by the guaranteed withdrawal rate. Recalculated upon step-up events.']
  benefit_base_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the benefit base record, used in policyholder statements, actuarial reports, and regulatory filings. Typically derived from the contract number with a rider suffix.']
  benefit_base_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the benefit base. Active = benefit base is in force and accumulating or being drawn down; Suspended = temporarily inactive due to excess withdrawal or contract lapse; Exhausted = benefit base has been fully depleted; Terminated = rider has been surrendered or cancelled; Annuitized = contract has entered payout phase under GMIB election.']
  benefit_election_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the contract owner has formally elected to begin receiving guaranteed benefits under the rider. Not Elected = owner has not yet begun taking guaranteed withdrawals or income; Elected = owner is actively receiving guaranteed withdrawals (GMWB) or income (GMIB); Annuitized = contract has been annuitized under GMIB; Exhausted = benefit base depleted.']
  contract_anniversary_date date [note: 'The most recent contract anniversary date, used as the reference point for automatic step-up evaluation, roll-up credit application, and annual rider charge assessment. Derived from the contract issue date but stored here for operational processing efficiency.']
  cumulative_withdrawals decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total dollar amount of withdrawals that have been charged against the benefit base since rider inception. Used to track benefit base depletion and determine remaining guaranteed withdrawal capacity. Excess withdrawals beyond the annual guaranteed amount reduce the benefit base proportionally.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all benefit base monetary amounts are denominated (e.g., USD). Required for multi-currency annuity platforms and regulatory reporting.']
  current_benefit_base decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current guaranteed benefit base balance as of the most recent valuation date, reflecting all roll-up credits, step-up elections, and withdrawal reductions applied since inception. This is the principal amount used to calculate the guaranteed withdrawal or income benefit. Critical for actuarial reserving under LDTI and IFRS 17.']
  excess_withdrawal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract owner has taken a withdrawal in excess of the annual guaranteed withdrawal amount in the current or any prior contract year. An excess withdrawal triggers a proportional reduction of the benefit base rather than a dollar-for-dollar reduction, and may reset or suspend certain rider provisions.']
  first_withdrawal_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract owner first took a withdrawal against the benefit base, triggering the transition from the accumulation phase to the distribution phase. Null if no withdrawals have been taken. Marks the end of the roll-up credit period for most product designs.']
  gmab_guaranteed_floor decimal(18,2) [note: 'For GMAB riders, the minimum Account Value guaranteed at the GMAB maturity date. If the actual AV at maturity is below this floor, the insurer credits the difference to the contract. Null for GMWB and GMIB riders. Key input for LDTI and IFRS 17 reserve calculations.']
  gmab_maturity_date date [note: 'For GMAB riders, the contractually defined date on which the guaranteed minimum accumulation amount becomes payable if the contract Account Value is below the guaranteed floor. Null for GMWB and GMIB riders. Triggers actuarial reserve strengthening as the maturity date approaches.']
  gmib_annuitization_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'For GMIB riders, the guaranteed annuitization rate (expressed as a decimal income factor per dollar of benefit base) used to convert the benefit base into a guaranteed lifetime income stream upon GMIB election. Null for GMWB and GMAB riders. Locked in at rider inception regardless of prevailing interest rates at annuitization.']
  gmib_eligible_date date [note: 'For GMIB riders, the earliest date on which the contract owner may elect to annuitize under the GMIB guarantee. Derived from inception date plus the GMIB waiting period. Null for GMWB and GMAB riders. Used in benefit election processing and policyholder communications.']
  gmib_waiting_period_years varchar [note: 'For GMIB riders, the minimum number of contract years that must elapse before the contract owner is eligible to elect the GMIB annuitization option. Typically 10 years. Null for GMWB and GMAB riders.']
  guaranteed_withdrawal_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'For GMWB riders, the contractually guaranteed annual withdrawal percentage applied to the benefit base to determine the maximum guaranteed annual withdrawal amount. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.050000 for 5%). May vary by age band at first withdrawal election.']
  in_the_money_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the living benefit guarantee is currently in-the-money, meaning the benefit base exceeds the contract Account Value (AV). True = guarantee is in-the-money (NAR > 0); False = guarantee is out-of-the-money (AV >= benefit base). Used for actuarial triage, reserve prioritization, and risk management reporting.']
  inception_date date [note: 'The date on which the living benefit rider and its associated benefit base became effective on the annuity contract. Marks the start of the benefit base accumulation or guarantee period. Used as the anchor date for roll-up credit calculations.']
  inception_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The initial guaranteed benefit base amount established at rider inception, typically equal to the contract Account Value (AV) at the time the rider was issued or the premium amount for a single-premium contract. Serves as the floor for step-up and roll-up calculations.']
  last_step_up_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the benefit base was stepped up to the contract Account Value under the step-up provision. Null if no step-up has occurred since inception. Used to track step-up history and determine eligibility for future step-ups.']
  last_step_up_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The benefit base amount recorded at the time of the most recent step-up event. Provides an audit trail of the step-up history and supports actuarial analysis of step-up frequency and magnitude.']
  max_anniversary_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The highest contract Account Value (AV) recorded on any contract anniversary date since rider inception. Used as the step-up candidate value in automatic anniversary step-up provisions. The benefit base is stepped up to the MAV if it exceeds the current benefit base.']
  net_amount_at_risk decimal(18,2) [note: 'The excess of the current benefit base over the contract Account Value (AV), representing the insurer\'s current financial exposure on the living benefit guarantee. A positive NAR indicates the guarantee is in-the-money. Critical input for actuarial reserving under LDTI, IFRS 17, and RBC calculations.']
  owner_age_at_first_withdrawal varchar [note: 'The age of the contract owner (in whole years) at the time of the first withdrawal from the benefit base. Determines the applicable guaranteed withdrawal rate tier for age-banded GMWB products. Null if no withdrawals have been taken.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this benefit base record was first created in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and state insurance department data retention regulations.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this benefit base record was most recently updated in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for incremental ETL processing and change data capture in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  rider_charge_basis varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the annual rider charge is assessed as a percentage of the benefit base or the contract Account Value (AV). Benefit Base = charge applied to the guaranteed benefit base amount; Account Value = charge applied to the current AV. Affects the charge amount when AV diverges from the benefit base.']
  rider_charge_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The annual fee rate charged for the living benefit rider, expressed as a decimal applied to the benefit base (e.g., 0.010000 for 1.00% per annum). Deducted from the contract Account Value periodically. Used in product profitability analysis and policyholder cost disclosure.']
  rider_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the living benefit rider and its associated benefit base guarantee period ends, if applicable. Null for riders with no fixed expiry. Relevant for GMAB riders with a defined accumulation period end date.']
  rider_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the living benefit rider governing this benefit base. GMWB = Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit; GMIB = Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit; GMAB = Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit; GMDB = Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit. Determines the benefit calculation methodology applied.']
  roll_up_credits_inception_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total cumulative dollar amount of guaranteed roll-up credits applied to the benefit base from rider inception through the current valuation date. Key metric for actuarial cost-of-guarantee analysis and LDTI/IFRS 17 reserve adequacy assessment.']
  roll_up_credits_ytd decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative dollar amount of guaranteed roll-up credits applied to the benefit base in the current contract year. Resets at each contract anniversary. Used for in-force management reporting and actuarial experience studies.']
  roll_up_end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the guaranteed roll-up credit period expires for this benefit base. Derived from the inception date plus the roll-up period in years, but stored explicitly for operational processing and actuarial projection.']
  roll_up_period_years varchar [note: 'The maximum number of contract years during which the guaranteed roll-up rate is applied to grow the benefit base. After this period expires, the benefit base no longer accrues roll-up credits. Common values are 10 or 20 years depending on product design.']
  roll_up_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The contractually guaranteed annual percentage rate at which the benefit base grows during the accumulation phase, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.060000 for 6%). Applied to the benefit base each year regardless of actual contract Account Value performance. Ceases upon first withdrawal or at the end of the roll-up period.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational annuity administration system from which this benefit base record was sourced (e.g., SAPIENS_ANNUITY, FAST_ANNUITY). Supports data lineage tracking and ETL reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  step_up_count varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of step-up events that have occurred on this benefit base since rider inception. Used in actuarial experience studies and product profitability analysis.']
  step_up_provision varchar [note: 'Indicates the type of step-up provision available under the rider. Automatic Anniversary = benefit base is automatically stepped up to the contract AV on each anniversary if AV exceeds current benefit base; Owner Elected = policyholder must actively elect a step-up; None = no step-up provision available.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The most recent date as of which the benefit base values (current benefit base, cumulative withdrawals, roll-up credits) were calculated and recorded. Aligns with the actuarial valuation cycle for LDTI and IFRS 17 reserving.']

  Note: 'Manages the guaranteed benefit base amounts for living benefit riders attached to annuity contracts, including GMWB (Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit), GMIB (Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit), and GMAB (Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit). Tracks benefit base inception value, step-up events, roll-up credits, maximum anniversary value, current benefit base balance, rider charge rate, and benefit base type. Critical for actuarial reserving under LDTI and IFRS 17 and for benefit calculation at claim time.'
}

Table annuity.withdrawal_transaction {
  withdrawal_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each withdrawal transaction or systematic withdrawal plan (SWP) instruction record. Primary key for the withdrawal_transaction data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Withdrawal disbursements are processed through billing accounts containing bank routing and account information. Real business process: payment disbursement, ACH/wire processing, 1099-R tax reporting ']
  aml_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_case. Business justification: Large or structured withdrawals trigger AML monitoring per BSA requirements. Links suspicious withdrawal patterns to AML case investigations. Required for SAR filing and transaction monitoring rule va']
  benefit_base_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.benefit_base. Business justification: withdrawal_transaction captures gmwb_benefit_base_before and gmwb_benefit_base_after, indicating the transaction directly affects a GMWB benefit_base record. Linking withdrawal_transaction to the spec']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuity contract against which this withdrawal transaction or SWP instruction is recorded. Links to the annuity contract master record.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Withdrawals generate journal entries for cash disbursement (credit), surrender charge revenue (credit), tax withholding liability (credit), and account value reduction (debit). Direct GL posting requi']
  owner_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuity contract owner (policyholder) who authorized or is associated with this withdrawal transaction. Required for tax reporting and KYC compliance.']
  rmd_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.rmd_schedule. Business justification: withdrawal_transaction has is_rmd_coordinated and rmd_amount fields indicating RMD-coordinated withdrawals. When a withdrawal satisfies an RMD obligation, it must reference the rmd_schedule that gover']
  sar_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sar_filing. Business justification: BSA/AML regulations require SAR filings for suspicious withdrawal transactions. withdrawal_transaction needs a direct sar_filing_id to support the SAR reporting workflow — distinct from benefit_paymen']
  surrender_charge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.surrender_charge. Business justification: withdrawal_transaction captures surrender_charge_amount and surrender_charge_rate for withdrawals that incur surrender charges. surrender_charge holds the schedule and rate table governing those charg']
  withdrawal_request_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Withdrawal requests require a signed withdrawal request form retained per transaction for audit and regulatory compliance. Operations must link the executed document to the transaction record to satis']
  account_value_after decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annuity contract\'s Account Value (AV) immediately after this withdrawal transaction has been applied. Confirms the post-withdrawal contract value for owner statements, benefit base recalculation, and subsequent transaction processing.']
  account_value_before decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annuity contract\'s Account Value (AV) immediately prior to this withdrawal transaction being applied. Used for surrender charge calculation, benefit base adjustments, and reconciliation of AV movement.']
  bank_account_last4 varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the owner\'s bank account number used for ACH or direct deposit disbursement of withdrawal proceeds. Stored in masked format for security compliance. Full account details are maintained in the EFT authorization record.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'The 9-digit ABA routing transit number of the financial institution receiving the withdrawal proceeds via ACH or wire transfer. Required for electronic disbursement processing.']
  cost_basis_recovered_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The non-taxable portion of the withdrawal representing return of the owner\'s after-tax investment (cost basis) in the annuity contract under IRC Section 72. Reduces the remaining cost basis tracked on the contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this withdrawal transaction record was first created in the annuity administration system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this withdrawal transaction. Typically \'USD\' for domestic US annuity contracts. Required for the MONETARY_TRIPLET and multi-currency reporting.']
  distribution_code varchar [note: 'IRS-defined distribution code reported in Box 7 of Form 1099-R indicating the nature of the distribution for tax purposes. Common codes: \'1\'=early distribution no exception; \'2\'=early distribution exception applies; \'3\'=disability; \'4\'=death; \'7\'=normal distribution; \'D\'=annuity payments from non-qualified annuity; \'Q\'=qualified distribution from Roth IRA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|7|D|Q|G|L|M|N|P|R|S|T|U|W — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'The contractual effective date on which the withdrawal is applied to the annuity contract\'s Account Value (AV). This is the valuation date used for surrender charge calculation, MVA adjustment, and 1099-R tax year determination. May differ from request_date due to processing cycles or market close timing.']
  federal_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of federal income tax withheld from this withdrawal per the owner\'s withholding election or mandatory withholding rules under IRC Section 3405. Reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 4.']
  federal_withholding_election varchar [note: 'The contract owner\'s federal income tax withholding election for this withdrawal. \'withhold\' = owner elected voluntary withholding; \'do_not_withhold\' = owner opted out of withholding; \'mandatory\' = mandatory withholding applies (e.g., eligible rollover distributions).
