Governs IRS Form W-4P compliance.']
  free_withdrawal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The contractual free withdrawal allowance amount available for the current contract year, typically 10% of Account Value (AV) or accumulated premiums, within which no surrender charge applies. Used to determine the surrender-charge-free portion of this withdrawal.']
  gmwb_benefit_base_after decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) benefit base amount immediately after this withdrawal has been applied. Reflects any proportional reduction due to excess withdrawal above the contractual GMWB annual withdrawal amount.']
  gmwb_benefit_base_before decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) benefit base amount immediately prior to this withdrawal. Excess withdrawals above the GMWB annual withdrawal amount reduce the benefit base proportionally. Required for variable and indexed annuity contracts with GMWB riders.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross dollar amount of the withdrawal before deduction of surrender charges, Market Value Adjustment (MVA), and tax withholding. Represents the amount deducted from the contract\'s Account Value (AV). Core monetary field for the MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  is_1035_exchange boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this withdrawal is part of a tax-free 1035 Exchange (IRC Section 1035) where proceeds are being transferred directly to another annuity or life insurance contract. True = 1035 exchange; proceeds are not taxable to the owner at time of withdrawal.']
  is_excess_withdrawal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this withdrawal exceeds the contractual GMWB or GMIB annual withdrawal amount, triggering a proportional reduction of the guaranteed benefit base. True = excess withdrawal occurred; False = withdrawal is within the guaranteed annual withdrawal amount.']
  is_rmd_coordinated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this withdrawal is coordinated with the contract\'s Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) calculation under IRC Section 401(a)(9). True = RMD coordination is active and this withdrawal satisfies or partially satisfies the annual RMD obligation. Applicable to qualified annuity contracts.']
  is_within_free_withdrawal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entire gross withdrawal amount falls within the contractual free withdrawal allowance corridor and therefore no surrender charge was applied. True = fully within free withdrawal; False = surrender charge applies to some or all of the withdrawal.']
  mva_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the Market Value Adjustment (MVA) applied to this withdrawal. Can be positive (in owner\'s favor when interest rates have fallen) or negative (charge when rates have risen). Applicable only to fixed annuity contracts with MVA provisions. Zero if not applicable.']
  net_proceeds_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net dollar amount disbursed to the contract owner after deducting surrender charges, MVA adjustment, and federal/state tax withholding from the gross withdrawal amount. Represents the actual cash received by the owner. Core monetary field for the MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The disbursement method used to deliver net withdrawal proceeds to the contract owner. \'ach\' = Automated Clearing House electronic funds transfer; \'check\' = paper check mailed to owner; \'wire\' = wire transfer; \'direct_deposit\' = direct deposit to owner\'s bank account; \'1035_transfer\' = direct transfer to receiving carrier for 1035 exchange.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the withdrawal transaction was executed and posted to the annuity contract in the administration system. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for audit, reconciliation, and regulatory reporting.']
  request_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the withdrawal request was received from the contract owner or their authorized representative. Establishes the business event initiation date for processing, valuation, and regulatory timing purposes.']
  rmd_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) amount for the current tax year applicable to this annuity contract under IRC Section 401(a)(9). Populated when is_rmd_coordinated is True. Used to ensure the withdrawal satisfies the minimum distribution requirement and avoid IRS excise tax penalties.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational annuity administration system that originated this withdrawal transaction record. \'sapiens_annuity\' = Sapiens Annuity platform; \'fast_annuity\' = FAST Annuity platform. Required for data lineage, reconciliation, and ETL audit in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  state_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of state income tax withheld from this withdrawal per the owner\'s state withholding election or mandatory state withholding requirements. Reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 14.']
  state_withholding_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code identifying the state for which income tax withholding was applied on this withdrawal. Determines applicable state withholding rates and mandatory withholding rules. Aligns with the owner\'s state of residence.']
  surrender_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the contingent deferred sales charge (CDSC) or surrender charge applied to this withdrawal. Calculated based on the contract\'s surrender charge schedule and the amount withdrawn in excess of the free withdrawal allowance. Zero if within free withdrawal corridor.']
  surrender_charge_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The applicable surrender charge rate (as a decimal, e.g., 0.07 = 7%) applied to the excess withdrawal amount above the free withdrawal allowance. Derived from the contract\'s contingent deferred sales charge (CDSC) schedule for the current surrender charge year.']
  surrender_charge_year varchar [note: 'The contract year number within the surrender charge schedule at the time of this withdrawal. Determines the applicable surrender charge rate percentage per the contract\'s CDSC schedule. For example, year 1 may carry a 7% charge, declining to 0% by year 8.']
  swp_amount_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the SWP disbursement is a fixed dollar amount, a percentage of the current Account Value (AV), or a system-calculated RMD amount. Drives the withdrawal amount calculation logic in the annuity administration platform.']
  swp_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the Systematic Withdrawal Plan (SWP) instruction is scheduled to terminate or was terminated. Null if the SWP is open-ended (e.g., lifetime income or until contract exhaustion). Populated for fixed-duration SWP plans.']
  swp_frequency varchar [note: 'The payment frequency of the Systematic Withdrawal Plan (SWP) instruction. Applicable when withdrawal_type is \'systematic\'. Determines the schedule on which recurring withdrawals are processed from the annuity contract.']
  swp_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the Systematic Withdrawal Plan (SWP) instruction became effective and the first scheduled withdrawal was processed. Establishes the SWP plan inception date for scheduling and anniversary calculations.']
  swp_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Systematic Withdrawal Plan (SWP) instruction associated with this transaction. \'active\' = SWP is running per schedule; \'suspended\' = temporarily halted by owner request; \'terminated\' = SWP permanently stopped; \'pending_setup\' = SWP instruction received but not yet activated.']
  taxable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the gross withdrawal amount that is taxable as ordinary income under IRC Section 72, reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 2a. Equals gross amount minus the non-taxable cost basis (investment in contract) recovered in this withdrawal.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable transaction reference number assigned by the annuity administration platform (Sapiens Annuity or FAST Annuity) at the time of withdrawal processing. Used for customer correspondence, audit trails, and reconciliation.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the withdrawal transaction or SWP instruction. \'pending\' = request received but not yet processed; \'in_process\' = funds movement initiated; \'completed\' = disbursement confirmed; \'reversed\' = transaction unwound; \'cancelled\' = request voided before processing; \'suspended\' = SWP plan temporarily halted.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this withdrawal transaction record in the annuity administration system. Supports change tracking, reconciliation, and audit compliance.']
  withdrawal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the withdrawal event. \'systematic\' = scheduled SWP disbursement; \'ad_hoc\' = owner-initiated one-time withdrawal; \'rmd\' = Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) mandated under IRC Section 401(a)(9); \'full_surrender\' = complete contract liquidation; \'partial_surrender\' = partial contract liquidation with surrender charge; \'free_withdrawal\' = withdrawal within the contractual free withdrawal allowance.']

  Note: 'Records all withdrawal events and manages standing systematic withdrawal plan (SWP) instructions against annuity contracts — serving as the single source of truth for ALL withdrawal activity, both ad-hoc and systematic (subsumes systematic_withdrawal_plan). For individual withdrawals: captures withdrawal request date, effective date, gross amount, surrender charge applied, MVA adjustment, net proceeds, withdrawal type (systematic, ad-hoc, RMD, full surrender), tax withholding, 1099-R reportable amount, and free withdrawal allowance status. For SWP plans: captures plan start date, frequency, amount or percentage, end date or duration, tax withholding elections (federal and state), payment method and banking instructions, RMD coordination flag, plan status, and SWP suspension/reinstatement history. Sourced from Sapiens Annuity and FAST Annuity withdrawal processing modules.'
}

Table annuity.rmd_schedule {
  rmd_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each RMD schedule record within the annuity administration platform. Primary key for the rmd_schedule data product.']
  annuitant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.annuitant. Business justification: IRS RMD rules require the annuitant\'s date of birth and life expectancy for distribution period calculation. rmd_schedule already has party_id but annuitant holds annuity-specific demographics (issue_']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualified annuity contract for which this RMD schedule is maintained. Links the RMD obligation to the underlying annuity contract in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: RMD distributions generate journal entries for federal/state tax withholding remittances and distribution accounting. Finance teams reconcile RMD payment amounts to GL postings for IRS Form 945 withho']
  mortality_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: RMD calculations use IRS life expectancy tables (Uniform Lifetime, Single Life, Joint Life) which are mortality tables. Required for accurate RMD calculation, IRS compliance, and audit trail of which ']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuity contract owner (policyholder) who is subject to the RMD obligation. The owner\'s age and applicable IRS life expectancy table are derived from this party record.']
  rmd_notice_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: IRC 401(a)(9) requires annual RMD notices be sent to contract owners. The rmd_schedule drives notice generation; linking the issued notice document to the schedule enables regulatory proof of timely n']
  aggregation_group_code varchar [note: 'Identifier grouping multiple IRA or annuity contracts owned by the same individual for RMD aggregation purposes. IRS rules permit owners to aggregate RMDs across multiple IRAs and satisfy the total from one or more accounts. Used for cross-contract RMD satisfaction tracking and IRS Form 5498 reporting.']
  april_1_deferral_elected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract owner has elected to defer the first-year RMD to April 1 of the following calendar year as permitted by IRS rules. Applicable only when first_rmd_year_flag is True. When True, the rmd_due_date is set to April 1 of the year following the first RMD year.']
  beneficiary_age_at_rmd_year varchar [note: 'Age of the designated beneficiary (typically spouse) as of their birthday in the RMD calculation year. Required only when the Joint and Last Survivor Table is applicable (sole beneficiary is a spouse more than 10 years younger than the owner). Null when Uniform Lifetime or Single Life tables are used.']
  calculated_rmd_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross RMD amount calculated for the calendar year, derived by dividing the prior year-end Account Value (AV) by the applicable IRS distribution period factor. This is the minimum amount the contract owner must withdraw to satisfy IRS RMD requirements and avoid the IRC Section 4974 excise tax.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this RMD schedule record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trails, data lineage, and Silver Layer record management. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  distribution_period decimal(6,2) [note: 'The life expectancy factor (in years) retrieved from the applicable IRS life expectancy table for the owner\'s age (and beneficiary\'s age if Joint table applies) in the RMD year. This divisor is applied to the prior year-end account value to compute the RMD amount. Updated per SECURE Act 2.0 tables effective 2022.']
  excise_tax_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of the potential IRS excise tax exposure under IRC Section 4974 if the RMD is not fully satisfied by the due date. Calculated as 25% of the remaining_rmd_balance (reduced to 10% if corrected within the correction window under SECURE Act 2.0). Supports proactive owner outreach and compliance risk management.']
  first_rmd_year_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the first calendar year in which the contract owner is subject to RMD requirements. When True, the owner may elect to defer the first RMD to April 1 of the following year per IRS rules, which may result in two RMDs being taken in the same calendar year.']
  form_1099r_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IRS Form 1099-R (Distributions From Pensions, Annuities, Retirement or Profit-Sharing Plans) has been issued to the contract owner for distributions taken toward the RMD in the calendar year. Supports tax reporting compliance and owner communication.']
  form_5498_report_date date [note: 'The date on which the IRS Form 5498 reporting the RMD obligation was filed or transmitted to the IRS. Null if form_5498_reported_flag is False. Supports tax reporting audit trails.']
  form_5498_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RMD obligation for this calendar year has been reported to the IRS on Form 5498 (IRA Contribution Information), which requires insurers to report the RMD amount and indicate that an RMD is required for the year. Supports IRS tax reporting compliance.']
  inherited_contract_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the annuity contract is an inherited (beneficiary) contract subject to the IRS 10-year rule or life expectancy payout rules under SECURE Act 2.0. When True, the Single Life Expectancy Table and beneficiary-specific RMD rules apply.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'The channel through which the RMD notification was delivered to the contract owner. \'suppressed\' indicates the owner opted out of notifications or the notification was withheld for a documented reason.']
  original_owner_dod date [note: 'The date of death of the original annuity contract owner, applicable when inherited_contract_flag is True. Determines the commencement of the beneficiary\'s RMD obligation and the applicable distribution rules under SECURE Act 2.0 (10-year rule vs. life expectancy payout).']
  owner_age_at_rmd_year varchar [note: 'The age of the annuity contract owner as of their birthday in the RMD calculation year. Used to look up the applicable distribution period from the IRS life expectancy table. Per IRS rules, age is determined as of the owner\'s birthday in the distribution year.']
  owner_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract owner was notified of their RMD obligation and calculated amount for the calendar year. IRS regulations require insurers to notify IRA owners of their RMD amount by January 31 of the distribution year. Supports regulatory compliance tracking.']
  prior_calculated_rmd_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The RMD amount from the most recent prior calculation before the current recalculation. Populated only when recalculation_flag is True. Supports audit trails and change management for IRS reporting.']
  prior_year_end_av decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Account Value (AV) of the annuity contract as of December 31 of the year immediately preceding the RMD calculation year. This is the IRS-mandated valuation date for RMD calculation purposes. Sourced from the Annuity Administration Platform\'s end-of-year account value snapshot.']
  prior_year_end_av_date date [note: 'The specific date on which the prior year-end Account Value (AV) was captured for RMD calculation purposes. Should be December 31 of the prior year. Recorded explicitly to support audit trails and IRS Form 5498 reporting.']
  qualified_plan_type varchar [note: 'The type of qualified retirement plan or IRA under which the annuity contract is held, which determines the applicable RMD rules, tax treatment, and reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: traditional_ira|sep_ira|simple_ira|401k|403b|457b|inherited_ira — promote to reference product]']
  recalculation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RMD amount for this calendar year has been recalculated after the initial calculation, typically due to a correction in the prior year-end account value, a change in the applicable life expectancy table, or a data correction. When True, recalculation_reason should be populated.']
  recalculation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason the RMD was recalculated for the calendar year. Applicable only when recalculation_flag is True. Examples include account value restatement, beneficiary designation change, table correction, or data quality remediation.']
  remaining_rmd_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The outstanding RMD amount still required to be distributed to satisfy the IRS obligation for the calendar year. Computed as calculated_rmd_amount minus ytd_distributions_applied. A zero or negative value indicates the RMD has been satisfied. Used for proactive owner notifications and IRS compliance monitoring.']
  rmd_calculation_date date [note: 'The date on which the RMD amount was calculated by the Annuity Administration Platform for the given calendar year. Supports audit trails and recalculation tracking when account values are restated or corrected.']
  rmd_due_date date [note: 'The IRS deadline by which the RMD must be fully distributed to avoid the IRC Section 4974 excise tax. For most owners, this is December 31 of the RMD year. For the first RMD year (the year the owner reaches the applicable RMD age), the deadline may be extended to April 1 of the following year per IRS rules.']
  rmd_satisfied_date date [note: 'The date on which the RMD obligation for the calendar year was fully satisfied (i.e., cumulative distributions reached or exceeded the calculated RMD amount). Null if the RMD has not yet been satisfied. Used for IRS Form 1099-R reporting and compliance monitoring.']
  rmd_start_age varchar [note: 'The IRS-mandated age at which the contract owner is required to begin taking RMDs from the qualified annuity. Reflects the applicable RMD age under current law (e.g., age 73 under SECURE Act 2.0 for owners born 1951–1959; age 75 for owners born 1960 or later). Stored to support historical accuracy as legislation changes.']
  rmd_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RMD obligation for the given calendar year. \'pending\' = RMD calculated but no distributions taken; \'in_progress\' = partial distributions applied; \'satisfied\' = full RMD amount distributed; \'waived\' = IRS-approved waiver applied (e.g., first-year RMD deferral); \'overdue\' = due date passed without satisfaction; \'cancelled\' = contract no longer subject to RMD (e.g., annuitized, surrendered).']
  rmd_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year (YYYY) for which this RMD obligation applies. Each qualified annuity contract generates one RMD schedule record per applicable calendar year. Determines the December 31 prior-year account value snapshot used in the RMD calculation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The identifier of the operational source system record from which this RMD schedule was originated or last updated (e.g., Sapiens Annuity contract ID, FAST Annuity record key). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and ETL audit trails in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  systematic_rmd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract owner is enrolled in the annuity platform\'s automatic systematic RMD distribution program, which automatically calculates and distributes the RMD amount on a scheduled basis (monthly, quarterly, or annually) to ensure timely satisfaction.']
  systematic_rmd_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which systematic RMD distributions are scheduled when the owner is enrolled in the automatic RMD program (systematic_rmd_flag = True). Null when systematic RMD is not elected.']
  systematic_rmd_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract owner\'s enrollment in the systematic RMD distribution program became effective. Null when systematic RMD is not elected.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this RMD schedule record was most recently modified in the data platform. Updated whenever distributions are applied, recalculations occur, or status changes. Supports audit trails and change tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code explaining why the RMD obligation has been waived for the calendar year. Applicable only when rmd_status is \'waived\'. Examples include first-year deferral election, IRS-approved waiver, contract annuitization, full surrender, or regulatory relief (e.g., COVID-19 CARES Act waiver).']
  ytd_distributions_applied decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative amount of distributions taken from the annuity contract during the RMD calendar year that have been applied toward satisfying the RMD obligation. Updated each time a qualifying withdrawal is processed. Used to compute the remaining RMD balance.']

  Note: 'Manages Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) schedules for qualified annuity contracts subject to IRS RMD rules under IRC Section 72 and IRS Publication 590-B. Tracks RMD calculation year, applicable IRS life expectancy table (Uniform, Joint, Single), account value as of December 31 prior year, calculated RMD amount, distributions already taken toward RMD, remaining RMD balance, RMD due date, and RMD satisfaction status. Supports IRS Form 5498 and 1099-R tax reporting obligations.'
}

Table annuity.index_crediting_strategy {
  index_crediting_strategy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each index crediting strategy or sub-account allocation record associated with an annuity contract. Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuity contract to which this index crediting strategy or sub-account allocation belongs. Links the strategy to its parent contract in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  crediting_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.crediting_strategy. Business justification: Contract-level strategy elections reference product crediting strategy definitions for cap/participation rates, index types, crediting methods, and allocation limits. Essential for index credit calcul']
  derivative_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.derivative_contract. Business justification: Each FIA index crediting strategy term is hedged by a specific index option (derivative contract). This link supports GAAP ASC 815 hedge accounting, NAIC Schedule DB derivative reporting, and hedge ef']
  index_security_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.security. Business justification: FIA index strategies reference specific market indices (S&P 500, NASDAQ-100) that are securities in the investment master. Crediting calculations, cap/participation rate setting, and option pricing re']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: FIA crediting strategies with cap rates, participation rates, and index options are version-specific and change with product filings. Required for rate renewal processing, strategy availability valida']
  policy_form_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval. Business justification: FIA crediting strategies (cap, participation, spread rates) must match approved policy forms and illustrations. Links strategy elections to approved form language for regulatory compliance validation.']
  prior_strategy_index_crediting_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the predecessor index crediting strategy record that this record replaced or was renewed from. Enables transfer and rebalance history tracking and supports audit trail requirements for policyholder allocation changes.']
  rate_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.rate_action. Business justification: FIA renewal rate-setting is driven by rate_action records. index_crediting_strategy has renewal_election and renewal_rate_flag indicating rate renewal events. Linking to rate_action enables renewal ra']
  rate_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.rate_filing. Business justification: FIA index crediting strategy parameters (cap rates, participation rates, spreads) are subject to state rate filings. The index_crediting_strategy must reference the approved rate filing to validate th']
  scenario_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.scenario_set. Business justification: FIA index crediting strategies are priced and hedged using economic scenario sets for option budget calculations and hedge effectiveness testing. Actuaries use this link to validate that credited rate']
  allocation_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of new premium and/or account value directed to this specific strategy or sub-account. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 25.0000 = 25%). All active strategy allocations for a contract must sum to 100%. Governs how premium deposits and rebalancing flows are distributed.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this strategy election on the contract. \'active\' = currently receiving allocations and crediting; \'pending\' = elected but not yet effective; \'terminated\' = no longer active; \'suspended\' = temporarily halted; \'transferred\' = funds moved to another strategy.']
  av_at_term_end decimal(18,2) [note: 'Account Value (AV) allocated to this FIA strategy at the end of the crediting term after interest has been credited, in USD. Reflects the starting AV plus credited interest minus any withdrawals during the term. Null for open terms and VA sub-account strategies.']
  av_at_term_start decimal(18,2) [note: 'Account Value (AV) allocated to this FIA strategy at the beginning of the crediting term, in USD. Serves as the basis for interest crediting calculations at term end. Null for VA sub-account strategies where unit-based valuation applies.']
  buffer_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of index loss absorbed by the carrier before the policyholder bears downside risk, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 10.0000 = 10%). Used in buffered or RILA-style FIA strategies as an alternative to a floor. Null for floor-based strategies and VA sub-accounts.']
  cap_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Maximum index-linked interest rate that can be credited to the FIA strategy in a given crediting term, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 8.5000 = 8.5%). Limits the upside participation in index gains. Null for uncapped strategies and VA sub-accounts. Set by the carrier and subject to contractual minimums filed with state regulators.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this index crediting strategy record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  credited_interest_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Actual index-linked interest rate credited to the FIA strategy at the end of the crediting term, expressed as a decimal percentage. Reflects the application of cap, participation rate, spread, floor, and buffer mechanics to the raw index change. Populated only after term completion. Null for open terms and VA sub-accounts.']
  crediting_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the FIA index crediting term in months (e.g., 12 for annual, 24 for two-year). Defines the measurement period over which index performance is evaluated and interest is credited. Null for VA sub-account strategies.']
  declared_interest_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Fixed interest rate declared by the carrier for a fixed declared rate strategy bucket, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 3.5000 = 3.5%). Applicable only to FIXED_DECLARED and DECLARED_RATE strategy types. Null for index-linked and variable sub-account strategies.']
  dollar_cost_averaging_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this strategy is part of a Dollar Cost Averaging (DCA) program, where premium is systematically transferred from a fixed account into this strategy over a defined period. Common in variable annuity new business processing.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this strategy election became or becomes effective for the contract. For FIA strategies, this typically aligns with the start of the crediting term. For VA sub-accounts, this is the date the allocation instruction took effect.']
  election_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the business event that created or last modified this strategy election. \'new_business\' = elected at contract issue; \'policyholder_change\' = changed via service request; \'renewal\' = auto-renewed at term end; \'rebalance\' = created by rebalancing event; \'transfer\' = result of a fund transfer; \'dca_program\' = created by DCA program.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this strategy election expires or is scheduled to terminate. For FIA strategies, this is the end of the crediting term. Null for open-ended variable annuity sub-account allocations. Used to trigger renewal or reallocation workflows.']
  floor_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Minimum index-linked interest rate guaranteed to be credited to the FIA strategy in a given crediting term, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.0000 = 0%). Protects the policyholder from negative index returns. Typically 0% for most FIA products. Null for VA sub-accounts.']
  fund_cusip varchar [note: 'Committee on Uniform Securities Identification Procedures (CUSIP) identifier for the variable annuity sub-account fund. Uniquely identifies the registered investment fund underlying the sub-account. Null for FIA index crediting strategies and fixed declared rate strategies. Required for SEC-registered variable products.']
  fund_ticker varchar [note: 'Exchange ticker symbol for the underlying investment fund of a variable annuity sub-account (e.g., \'VFINX\', \'AGTHX\'). Used for market data feeds and NAV lookups. Null for FIA index crediting strategies.']
  index_end_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The closing value of the underlying market index on the crediting term end date. Used in conjunction with the index start value to calculate the index change percentage for interest crediting. Populated at term maturity. Null for VA sub-account strategies and open terms.']
  index_start_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The closing value of the underlying market index on the crediting term start date. Used as the baseline for calculating index-linked interest at term end. Captured at term inception and locked for the duration of the crediting term. Null for VA sub-account strategies.']
  market_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current market value of the policyholder\'s holding in the variable annuity sub-account, in USD. Calculated as units held multiplied by the current unit value. Updated daily. Null for FIA index crediting strategies where AV-based valuation applies.']
  maximum_allocation_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Maximum allocation percentage permitted for this strategy as specified in the product filing, expressed as a decimal percentage. Enforced during allocation change processing to prevent over-concentration in a single strategy.']
  minimum_allocation_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Minimum allocation percentage required for this strategy as specified in the product filing, expressed as a decimal percentage. Enforced during allocation change processing to ensure compliance with product design constraints.']
  participation_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of the index gain credited to the FIA strategy, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 80.0000 = 80%). Determines the proportion of index performance passed through to the policyholder\'s account value. Null for VA sub-accounts. Subject to contractual minimums per state-filed product specifications.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the annuity product type to which this strategy applies. FIA = Fixed Indexed Annuity; VA = Variable Annuity; SPIA = Single Premium Immediate Annuity; FIXED = Traditional Fixed Annuity; IUL = Indexed Universal Life. Determines which crediting mechanics and regulatory frameworks apply.']
  rebalancing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic periodic rebalancing is enabled for this strategy allocation. When True, the contract\'s account value is periodically rebalanced back to the target allocation percentage. Applicable primarily to variable annuity sub-account strategies.']
  rebalancing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which automatic rebalancing is performed for this strategy allocation. Applicable when rebalancing_flag is True. Null or \'none\' when automatic rebalancing is not elected.']
  renewal_election varchar [note: 'Policyholder\'s standing instruction for what to do with the FIA strategy allocation at the end of the crediting term. \'renew_same\' = automatically renew into the same strategy; \'reallocate\' = redistribute per new allocation instructions; \'annuitize\' = begin annuity payout; \'surrender\' = surrender the contract. Drives automated renewal processing.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this strategy record originated (e.g., \'SAPIENS_ANNUITY\', \'FAST_ANNUITY\'). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and ETL reconciliation.']
  spread_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Margin or asset fee deducted from the index gain before crediting to the FIA strategy, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 2.0000 = 2%). Also known as the \'margin\' or \'asset fee\'. Reduces the effective credited rate. Null for strategies using cap or participation rate mechanics and for VA sub-accounts.']
  strategy_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this strategy is currently available for new elections or reallocation on the contract. A strategy may become unavailable if the carrier discontinues it, if it is closed to new money, or if the policyholder\'s contract vintage does not support it.']
  term_end_date date [note: 'End date of the current FIA index crediting term. The index value on this date serves as the ending point for point-to-point crediting calculations. Interest is credited to the account value at term end. Null for VA sub-account strategies.']
  term_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the current FIA index crediting term. The index value on this date serves as the starting point for point-to-point and other crediting method calculations. Null for VA sub-account strategies.']
  transfer_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transfers out of this strategy are currently restricted (e.g., due to market timing restrictions, surrender charge periods, or contractual lock-in provisions). When True, transfer requests from this strategy will be pended for compliance review.']
  unit_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Current Net Asset Value (NAV) per accumulation unit for the variable annuity sub-account, in USD. Updated daily based on the underlying fund\'s NAV. Used to calculate the current market value of the policyholder\'s sub-account holding. Null for FIA index crediting strategies.']
  units_held decimal(18,6) [note: 'Number of accumulation units held in the variable annuity sub-account as of the most recent valuation date. Multiplied by the unit value to derive the current sub-account market value. Null for FIA index crediting strategies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this index crediting strategy record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated on every change to the record. Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'Date as of which the current unit value, units held, and market value figures are reported. For VA sub-accounts, this is the most recent business day NAV date. For FIA strategies, this is the most recent account value calculation date.']

  Note: 'Defines and manages all investment allocation strategies elected for annuity contracts, covering both FIA index crediting strategies and variable annuity sub-account fund allocations — serving as the single source of truth for ALL annuity investment allocation management (subsumes sub_account_allocation). For FIA: captures index name (S&P 500, Russell 2000, MSCI EAFE), crediting method (annual point-to-point, monthly average, monthly cap), cap rate, participation rate, spread/margin, floor rate, buffer rate, and crediting term dates. For variable annuity: captures sub-account fund name, fund CUSIP, allocation percentage, current unit value, number of units held, current market value, allocation effective date, and rebalancing instructions. Common fields: allocation percentage, allocation effective date, strategy/fund election status, and transfer/rebalance history. Supports FIA index credit calculations, VA NAV-based AV calculations, and policyholder allocation reporting.'
}

Table annuity.index_credit_event {
  index_credit_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA) index crediting event record in the Silver layer. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the FIA annuity contract to which this index crediting event applies. Links the crediting event to the owning contract in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  crediting_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.crediting_strategy. Business justification: Index credit events reference product crediting strategy definitions to validate cap/participation rates, crediting methods, and index calculation rules. Required for credit calculation validation, he']
  index_crediting_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.index_crediting_strategy. Business justification: index_credit_event records each FIA crediting event applied at the end of a crediting term. index_crediting_strategy defines the strategy (cap rate, participation rate, floor, buffer, term) that gover']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: FIA index crediting events generate journal entries for interest credited to policyholder accounts (GAAP interest expense, SAP reserve increase). Finance teams reconcile index credit amounts to GL pos']
  reversed_event_index_credit_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original index credit event record that this record reverses, when reversal_flag is True. Enables audit trail linkage between reversal and original crediting events. Null for original (non-reversal) events.']
  sub_account_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific index allocation segment within the FIA contract that this crediting event applies to. A contract may have multiple index allocations across different indices and crediting strategies.']
  actuarial_basis_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the actuarial valuation basis under which this index credit event is recognized for reserve and liability calculations (e.g., GAAP, SAP, IFRS). Supports multi-basis reporting in the Actuarial Valuation System (AXIS, Prophet, MoSes) and LDTI compliance.']
  allocation_av_after_credit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Account Value (AV) of this specific index allocation segment immediately after the index credit has been applied. Equals allocation_av_start plus credited_amount, adjusted for any withdrawals processed at term end.']
  allocation_av_start decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Account Value (AV) of this specific index allocation segment at the beginning of the crediting term, before any withdrawals or adjustments during the term. Used as the base for calculating the credited dollar amount.']
  buffer_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The percentage of index loss absorbed by the insurance company before any loss is passed to the policyholder in a buffer-based FIA crediting strategy. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.100000 = 10% buffer). Null if a floor applies instead of a buffer.']
  cap_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The maximum index-linked interest rate that can be credited to the FIA contract for this crediting term, as specified in the contract terms. Limits the upside participation in index gains. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.060000 = 6.0%). Null if no cap applies to this crediting strategy.']
  contract_av_after_credit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total Account Value (AV) across all allocation segments of the FIA contract immediately after this index credit has been applied. Reflects the contract-level AV used for policyholder statements and benefit base calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this index credit event record was first created in the source Annuity Administration Platform system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credited_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of index-linked interest credited to the FIA contract allocation for this term, calculated by applying the credited_interest_rate to the allocation_av_start. This is the actual monetary value added to the Account Value (AV).']
  credited_interest_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The final index-linked interest rate credited to the FIA contract allocation for this term, after applying cap, participation rate, floor, buffer, and spread constraints to the raw index return. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.060000 = 6.0%). This is the rate that drives the credited dollar amount.']
  crediting_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world business event timestamp representing when the index credit was officially applied to the FIA contract Account Value (AV) in the Annuity Administration Platform. Distinct from record audit timestamps.']
  declared_rate_date date [note: 'The date on which the insurance company declared the cap rate, participation rate, or spread applicable to this crediting term. Required for regulatory disclosure and policyholder notification compliance.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for this index crediting event, as assigned by the Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity or FAST Annuity). Used on policyholder statements and audit trails.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the index crediting event within the Annuity Administration Platform workflow. \'pending\' = term ended but credit not yet calculated; \'calculated\' = credit computed awaiting posting; \'applied\' = credit posted to Account Value (AV); \'reversed\' = previously applied credit reversed; \'voided\' = event cancelled before application.']
  floor_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The minimum index-linked interest rate that will be credited to the FIA contract for this crediting term, protecting the policyholder from negative index returns. Typically 0% (zero floor). Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.000000 = 0%). Null if a buffer applies instead of a floor.']
  gmab_benefit_base_after_credit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB) benefit base value immediately after this index crediting event, if a GMAB rider is attached to the contract. Tracks whether the AV meets or exceeds the guaranteed accumulation target. Null if no GMAB rider is present.']
  gmib_benefit_base_after_credit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) benefit base value immediately after this index crediting event, if a GMIB rider is attached to the contract. Updated when the credited AV triggers a step-up. Null if no GMIB rider is present.']
  gmwb_benefit_base_after_credit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) benefit base value immediately after this index crediting event, if a GMWB rider is attached to the contract. Updated when the credited AV exceeds the prior benefit base (step-up). Null if no GMWB rider is present.']
  index_end_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'The closing value of the external market index on the crediting term end date. Used in conjunction with the index start value to calculate the raw index return percentage.']
  index_option_type varchar [note: 'The type of derivative option instrument used by the insurance company to hedge the index crediting obligation for this allocation segment. Relevant for actuarial valuation, ALM (Asset Liability Management), and investment portfolio management.']
  index_return_pct decimal(10,6) [note: 'The raw percentage change in the external market index over the crediting term, calculated as (index_end_value - index_start_value) / index_start_value. This is the pre-cap, pre-floor, pre-buffer gross index performance. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.085000 = 8.5%).']
  index_start_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'The closing value of the external market index on the crediting term start date. Used as the baseline for calculating the index return percentage at the end of the term.']
  participation_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The percentage of the index gain that is credited to the FIA contract for this crediting term. Applied to the index return before cap and floor constraints. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.800000 = 80%). Null if no participation rate applies to this crediting strategy.']
  renewal_rate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cap rate, participation rate, or spread applied in this crediting term was a renewal rate (True) or the initial declared rate at contract issue (False). Renewal rates may differ from initial rates and are subject to NAIC disclosure requirements.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this index credit event record represents a reversal of a previously applied credit (True) or an original crediting event (False). Reversals occur due to data corrections, index value restatements, or system errors.']
  source_event_reference varchar [note: 'The native event or transaction identifier from the originating Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity transaction ID or FAST Annuity event reference). Used for reconciliation between the Silver layer and the source system.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The identifier of the Annuity Administration Platform source system that originated this index credit event record (e.g., \'SAPIENS_ANNUITY\', \'FAST_ANNUITY\'). Supports data lineage and multi-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  spread_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'A fixed percentage deducted from the index return before crediting in a spread-based FIA crediting strategy. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.020000 = 2.0% spread). Null if cap/participation rate applies instead of a spread.']
  step_up_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this index crediting event triggered a benefit base step-up on any attached guaranteed living benefit rider (GMWB, GMIB, or GMAB). True when the post-credit AV exceeds the prior benefit base, resulting in a ratchet to the higher value.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The calendar tax year in which this index crediting event falls, relevant for IRC Section 72 tax reporting on annuity contract earnings. Used for IRS Form 1099-R preparation and tax deferred growth tracking.']
  term_duration_months varchar [note: 'The length of the index crediting term expressed in months (e.g., 12 for a one-year term, 24 for a two-year term). Determines the measurement window for index performance.']
  term_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the index crediting term for this allocation segment. The index end value is typically captured on this date. Defines the end of the measurement period for index return calculation.']
  term_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the index crediting term for this allocation segment. The index start value is typically captured on this date. Defines the beginning of the measurement period for index return calculation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this index credit event record was last modified in the source Annuity Administration Platform system. Tracks reversals, corrections, and recalculations.']
  withdrawal_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of withdrawals processed during the crediting term that reduced the allocation base used for interest crediting. Reflects the impact of systematic or ad-hoc withdrawals on the credited amount calculation.']

  Note: 'Records each index crediting event applied to a FIA contract at the end of a crediting term. Captures crediting term start and end dates, index start value, index end value, calculated index return, cap applied, participation rate applied, floor applied, buffer applied, credited interest rate, credited dollar amount, and resulting AV after credit. This is the transactional record of FIA index performance and credit application, supporting policyholder statements and actuarial experience studies.'
}

Table annuity.annuitization_election {
  annuitization_election_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the annuitization election record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  benefit_base_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.benefit_base. Business justification: annuitization_election has gmib_benefit_base_amount and gmib_rider_applied fields. When a GMIB rider is applied at annuitization, the election uses the guaranteed income benefit base to determine the ']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuity contract undergoing annuitization. Links this election to the parent contract record in the annuity domain.']
  contract_owner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder or contract owner who submitted the annuitization election. Used for party-level reporting and KYC compliance.']
  election_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Annuitization elections require signed election forms per state insurance regulations. Links election record to executed document for regulatory audit trail, rescission period tracking, and irrevocabl']
  funding_portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Annuitization converts accumulation value to payout stream backed by specific portfolio assets. ALM requires duration-matching payout obligations to portfolio holdings. Investment teams need to reserv']
  gaap_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gaap_reserve. Business justification: Annuitization elections reclassify contracts from accumulation to payout phase, triggering GAAP reserve reclassification under LDTI. Finance teams link elections to GAAP reserve records to measure the']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Annuitization elections trigger accounting entries: transfer from accumulation to payout reserve, DAC acceleration/write-off, and deferred gain recognition. Finance teams require a direct link from th']
  mortality_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: Annuitization rates are calculated using specific mortality tables (e.g., 2012 IAM, Annuity 2000). Critical for pricing annuity payouts, regulatory compliance, and ensuring consistent rate application']
  payout_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.payout_schedule. Business justification: When a contract owner elects to annuitize (annuitization_election), the system creates a payout_schedule that defines the ongoing payment stream. The election captures the DECISION and election-time p']
  pricing_basis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Annuity option rates applied at annuitization election are derived from actuarial pricing_basis assumptions (interest rate basis, mortality table). This link supports GMIB exercise pricing validation ']
  primary_annuitization_annuitant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuitant whose life expectancy governs the payout calculations. May differ from the contract owner in owner-driven annuity structures.']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed agent or financial professional who facilitated the annuitization election. Used for commission impact tracking and suitability compliance documentation.']
  risk_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: Annuitization elections determine payout rates using the annuitant\'s risk class (standard, preferred, rated). The risk class directly drives the annuity option rate applied at election. Annuity operat']
  suitability_review_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the suitability review record completed prior to or at the time of the annuitization election, as required under NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation and DOL fiduciary standards.']
  surrender_charge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.surrender_charge. Business justification: annuitization_election captures surrender_charge_amount and surrender_charge_waived for contracts that annuitize during the surrender charge period. surrender_charge holds the schedule governing those']
  annuitant_age_at_election varchar [note: 'The age of the primary annuitant (in whole years) at the time of the annuitization election date. Used in actuarial factor selection, mortality table lookup, and IRC Section 72 expected return calculations.']
  annuitization_basis varchar [note: 'The value basis used to calculate the annuity income stream. \'account_value\' (AV) = current contract account value; \'guaranteed_annuitization_value\' = contractually guaranteed minimum value per rider (e.g., GMIB benefit base); \'greater_of\' = higher of AV or guaranteed value. Determines the starting principal for payout calculations.']
  annuitization_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of the annuitization basis (AV or guaranteed annuitization value) used to calculate the periodic income payment at the time of election. Captured as a point-in-time snapshot at the effective date.']
  annuity_option_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The annuity factor or option rate (per $1,000 of annuitization value) used to convert the annuitization basis into the periodic payment amount. Derived from the applicable mortality table and interest rate assumption at the time of annuitization.']
  annuity_type varchar [note: 'The type of annuity contract being annuitized. \'fixed\' = guaranteed fixed payment; \'variable\' = payments vary with separate account performance relative to AIR; \'fixed_indexed\' = FIA with index-linked crediting. Governs payout calculation methodology and SEC/FINRA regulatory requirements.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the annuitization election was formally approved by the insurance company\'s new business or contract servicing team. Null if election is still pending or was cancelled.']
  assumed_interest_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'The Assumed Interest Rate (AIR) used in the annuity factor calculation at the time of annuitization. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0350 = 3.50%). Relevant for variable annuity payout calculations where actual performance is compared to AIR.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this election record. Typically USD for domestic US annuity contracts.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract officially transitions from the accumulation phase to the payout phase. Also known as the Annuity Start Date (ASD) or Annuity Commencement Date. Governs IRC Section 72 tax treatment.']
  election_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the contract owner formally submitted the annuitization election request. Serves as the principal business event date for this transaction.']
  election_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier assigned to this annuitization election by the Annuity Administration Platform. Used in correspondence, confirmations, and regulatory filings.']
  election_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the annuitization election request was received and logged in the Annuity Administration Platform. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED timestamp for this transaction.']
  election_source_channel varchar [note: 'The distribution or service channel through which the annuitization election was submitted. Used for operational analytics and agent commission impact assessment.']
  election_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the annuitization election. \'pending\' = submitted but not yet approved; \'approved\' = approved but payout not yet started; \'in_force\' = payout phase active; \'cancelled\' = withdrawn before effective date; \'rescinded\' = reversed after effective date per applicable free-look or regulatory provision.']
  exclusion_ratio decimal(6,4) [note: 'The IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio representing the proportion of each annuity payment that is a tax-free return of the owner\'s cost basis. Calculated as investment in contract divided by expected return. Used for 1099-R tax reporting.']
  expected_return decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total expected return from the annuity contract as calculated under IRC Section 72 tables, used as the denominator in the exclusion ratio calculation. For life annuities, derived from IRS actuarial tables based on annuitant age and payout option.']
  first_payment_date date [note: 'The scheduled date of the first annuity income payment to the payee following annuitization. May differ from the effective date depending on payout frequency and payment timing convention (immediate vs. deferred first payment).']
  gmib_benefit_base_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) benefit base amount at the time of annuitization election, when the GMIB rider is exercised. Represents the guaranteed minimum value used to calculate the income stream, which may exceed the current account value.']
  gmib_rider_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) rider was exercised as the basis for this annuitization election. True = GMIB benefit base used; False = standard account value annuitization.']
  investment_in_contract decimal(18,2) [note: 'The owner\'s total after-tax cost basis in the annuity contract at the time of annuitization, as defined under IRC Section 72. Used to calculate the exclusion ratio for tax-free recovery of basis in annuity payments.']
  is_1035_exchange boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the annuitization election involves a tax-free 1035 Exchange (IRC Section 1035) from another insurance or annuity contract. True = the annuitization basis includes proceeds from a 1035 exchange; False = no exchange involved.']
  is_irrevocable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the annuitization election is irrevocable once the effective date is reached. True = the owner cannot reverse or modify the election after the annuity start date; False = election may be modified within applicable free-look or rescission period.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this annuitization election record was last modified in the source system or Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  rescission_deadline_date date [note: 'The last date by which the contract owner may rescind or cancel the annuitization election under applicable state free-look or rescission provisions. Null for irrevocable elections or after the deadline has passed.']
  source_system_election_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier assigned to this annuitization election record in the originating Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity). Supports data lineage tracing from Silver Layer back to the operational system of record.']
  surrender_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The surrender charge amount that would have applied at the time of annuitization, prior to any waiver. Captured for audit and actuarial analysis even when waived. Null if no surrender charge schedule applies.']
  surrender_charge_waived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the surrender charge was waived upon annuitization. Many annuity contracts waive surrender charges when the owner elects to annuitize, as an incentive for lifetime income election.']
  tax_qualified_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the annuity contract is tax-qualified (e.g., IRA, 403(b), 401(a)) or non-qualified. Governs IRC Section 72 tax treatment, RMD applicability, and 1099-R reporting codes.']

  Note: 'Captures the annuitization election details when a contract transitions from the accumulation phase to the payout phase. Records election date, annuitization effective date, payout option selected (life only, life with period certain, joint and survivor, fixed period), annuity start date, annuitization basis (AV or guaranteed annuitization value), payout frequency, first payment date, and whether the election is irrevocable. Sourced from Sapiens Annuity benefit calculation and annuitization modules.'
}

Table annuity.payout_schedule {
  payout_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the annuity payout schedule record in the Annuity Administration Platform. Primary key for this entity.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent annuity contract for which this payout schedule is defined. Links the payout schedule to the annuity contract administration record.']
  mortality_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: Life-contingent payout schedules (life only, joint-and-survivor, period certain and life) require mortality tables for payment calculation. Essential for accurate benefit payments, reserve adequacy, a']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Annuitization payout options, mortality tables, and annuity option rates are locked to plan version at election date. Critical for payout calculations, mortality table selection, and regulatory compli']
  pricing_basis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Payout schedule payment amounts are calculated using actuarial pricing_basis assumptions (assumed interest rate, mortality). This link enables SPIA/annuity payment rate validation against filed pricin']
  primary_payout_annuitant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary annuitant (measuring life) whose life expectancy governs the payout duration. Required for life-contingent payout options.']
  risk_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: Life-contingent payout schedules (life-only, life with period certain) use the annuitant\'s underwriting risk class to apply appropriate mortality assumptions for scheduled payment amounts and reserve ']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable annuity payout schedules calculate periodic payments using annuity unit values from a specific separate account. This link is required for annuity unit value lookups during payment calculatio']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Payout schedules must comply with state-specific nonforfeiture regulations, minimum annuity payout options, and withholding rules. Payout administrators reference the applicable state regulation to va']
  statutory_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.statutory_reserve. Business justification: Payout annuity schedules drive statutory reserve calculations (CARVM payout phase, Commissioners Annuity Reserve Valuation Method). Appointed actuaries link payout schedules to statutory reserve recor']
  account_value_at_annuitization decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Account Value (AV) of the deferred annuity contract at the time of annuitization, used as the premium basis for calculating the annuity income payment amount. Null for SPIA contracts.']
  annuitization_date date [note: 'Date on which a deferred annuity contract was formally annuitized, converting the accumulated account value into a stream of annuity income payments. Null for SPIA contracts where annuitization occurs at contract issue.']
  annuity_start_date date [note: 'The annuity commencement date (ACD) — the date on which the annuity contract formally enters the payout phase and benefit calculations are locked. Distinct from payment_start_date, which is the first actual disbursement date. Governs IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio calculations.']
  cola_anniversary_date date [note: 'Annual date on which the Cost-of-Living Adjustment (COLA) is applied to increase the scheduled annuity income payment amount. Typically the contract anniversary date. Null if no COLA applies.']
  cola_index_type varchar [note: 'Type of inflation index used to determine the Cost-of-Living Adjustment (COLA) applied to annuity income payments. Fixed indicates a contractually specified fixed rate; CPI-U and CPI-W reference U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Consumer Price Index measures; none indicates no COLA applies.']
  cola_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Annual Cost-of-Living Adjustment (COLA) rate applied to the scheduled payment amount to increase annuity income payments over time to offset inflation. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.03 for 3%). Zero or null if no COLA applies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payout schedule record was first created in the system of record. Used for audit trail and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this payout schedule (e.g., USD). Required for multi-currency annuity contracts.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the payout schedule is in effect. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) tracking for schedule amendments such as COLA adjustments, payment amount changes, or payout option modifications.']
  exclusion_ratio decimal(8,6) [note: 'The IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio applied to each annuity income payment to determine the non-taxable return-of-investment portion. Calculated as the investment in the contract divided by the expected return. Used for IRS Form 1099-R tax reporting.']
  expected_return decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount expected to be received under the annuity contract over its payout period, as calculated under IRC Section 72 using IRS actuarial tables. Used as the denominator in the exclusion ratio calculation.']
  federal_withholding_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Federal income tax withholding rate applied to the taxable portion of each annuity income payment, as elected by the annuitant or as mandated by IRS default withholding rules. Used for IRS Form 1099-R reporting and payment net calculation.']
  interest_rate_basis decimal(8,6) [note: 'The assumed interest rate used in the actuarial calculation of the annuity income payment amount at the time of annuitization or SPIA pricing. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.035 for 3.5%). Used in actuarial reserve calculations and FASB ASC 944 LDTI discount rate determinations.']
  investment_in_contract decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annuitant\'s after-tax cost basis in the annuity contract at the annuity start date, as defined under IRC Section 72. Used to calculate the exclusion ratio and the non-taxable portion of each annuity income payment for IRS Form 1099-R reporting.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount of the most recently issued annuity income payment. May differ from scheduled_payment_amount due to COLA adjustments, partial-period proration, or manual adjustments.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent annuity income payment was issued under this payout schedule. Used to confirm payment continuity and detect missed payments.']
  next_payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the next scheduled annuity income payment is due to be issued. Updated after each payment cycle by the Annuity Administration Platform. Used in benefit payment processing and cash flow management.']
  payment_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the last scheduled annuity income payment is due. Populated for period-certain and fixed-term payout options; null for life-only payout options where payments continue until the annuitant\'s death.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which annuity income payments are disbursed to the annuitant. Determines the payment schedule cadence and is used in benefit payment processing and actuarial liability calculations.']
  payment_mode_code varchar [note: 'Method by which annuity income payments are disbursed to the annuitant (e.g., Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT), check, wire transfer). Drives payment processing routing in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  payment_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the first annuity income payment is scheduled to be issued. For Single Premium Immediate Annuities (SPIA), this is typically one payment period after the contract issue date. Governs the commencement of the payout phase.']
  payments_made_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of annuity income payments that have been issued to date under this payout schedule. Used to track progress against total expected payments and to determine remaining guaranteed payments.']
  payout_option_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the annuity payout form elected by the annuitant (e.g., life-only, life with period certain, joint-and-survivor, period certain only, installment refund, cash refund). Drives payment duration logic and survivor benefit calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: life_only|life_period_certain|joint_survivor|period_certain_only|installment_refund|cash_refund|life_with_refund — promote to reference product]']
  payout_option_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the elected annuity payout option as it appears on the annuity contract and benefit statements (e.g., \'Life with 10-Year Period Certain\', \'Joint and 50% Survivor\'). Used in annuitant communications and regulatory disclosures.']
  period_certain_years varchar [note: 'Number of years for which annuity income payments are guaranteed regardless of the annuitant\'s survival (e.g., 5, 10, 20 years). Applicable to life-with-period-certain and period-certain-only payout options. Null for life-only options.']
  pop_up_provision boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payout schedule includes a pop-up provision, whereby the annuity income payment increases (pops up) to the single-life amount if the joint annuitant predeceases the primary annuitant. Applicable to joint-and-survivor payout options only.']
  qualified_plan_type varchar [note: 'Type of tax-qualified retirement plan under which the annuity contract is held, governing Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) rules and tax reporting requirements. Non-qualified for contracts not held in a qualified plan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IRA|Roth_IRA|403b|401a|457b|457f|SEP_IRA|SIMPLE_IRA|non_qualified — promote to reference product]']
  refund_feature_type varchar [note: 'Type of refund guarantee attached to the payout option. Cash refund pays the beneficiary a lump sum equal to the premium minus payments received; installment refund continues payments to the beneficiary until the total premium is recovered. None indicates no refund feature.']
  rmd_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the annuity payout schedule satisfies the Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) rules under IRC Section 401(a)(9). True for tax-qualified contracts where the annuitant has reached the applicable RMD age.']
  rmd_start_date date [note: 'Date by which Required Minimum Distributions (RMD) must commence under IRC Section 401(a)(9), based on the annuitant\'s required beginning date. Null for non-qualified contracts or contracts not subject to RMD rules.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric identifier for the payout schedule, as assigned by the Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity or FAST Annuity). Used in benefit payment processing and annuitant correspondence.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payout schedule. Active indicates payments are being issued; suspended indicates a temporary hold; completed indicates all scheduled payments have been made; cancelled indicates the schedule was terminated before completion; pending indicates the schedule is set up but the first payment date has not yet arrived.']
  scheduled_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross periodic income payment amount due to the annuitant per payment frequency, before any applicable withholding taxes or deductions. Expressed in the contract currency. Core field for benefit payment processing and actuarial liability calculations.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational Annuity Administration Platform from which this payout schedule record was sourced (e.g., SAPIENS_ANNUITY, FAST_ANNUITY). Used for data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  spia_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payout schedule is associated with a Single Premium Immediate Annuity (SPIA) contract, as opposed to an annuitized deferred annuity. Drives product-specific processing rules in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  state_withholding_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'State income tax withholding rate applied to the taxable portion of each annuity income payment, as elected by the annuitant or as required by the applicable state tax authority. Varies by state of residence.']
  survivor_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of the primary annuity income payment that continues to the joint annuitant upon the death of the primary annuitant, for joint-and-survivor payout options (e.g., 0.50 for 50%, 0.667 for 66.7%, 1.00 for 100%). Null for single-life payout options.']
  tax_qualified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the annuity contract funding this payout schedule is a tax-qualified contract (e.g., IRA, 403(b), 401(a)) or a non-qualified contract. Determines IRC Section 72 tax treatment and exclusion ratio applicability.']
  total_expected_payments varchar [note: 'Total number of periodic income payments expected over the guaranteed period of the payout schedule (e.g., 120 for a 10-year period certain with monthly payments). Used in actuarial liability calculations and benefit payment processing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payout schedule record was most recently modified in the system of record. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  withholding_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state code for the state to which income tax withholding on annuity payments is remitted. Determined by the annuitant\'s state of residence at the time of payout commencement.']

  Note: 'Defines the periodic income payment schedule for annuity contracts in the payout phase, including SPIA (Single Premium Immediate Annuity) and annuitized deferred contracts. Captures scheduled payment amount, payment frequency, payment start date, payment end date (if period certain), cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) rate, inflation index linkage, joint annuitant survivor percentage, and total expected payments. Supports benefit payment processing and actuarial liability calculations.'
}

Table annuity.benefit_payment {
  benefit_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each benefit payment record disbursed under an annuity payout schedule or living benefit claim. Primary key for the benefit_payment data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Annuitization benefit payments are disbursed through billing accounts. Real business process: annuity payout processing, recurring payment execution, tax withholding coordination. Critical for post-an']
  annuitant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuitant or beneficiary receiving this benefit payment. Supports party-level reporting and 1099-R tax reporting under IRC Section 72.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuity contract under which this benefit payment is disbursed. Links the payment to the governing contract in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  exclusion_rider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.exclusion_rider. Business justification: Benefit payment adjudication requires checking applicable exclusion riders to determine if the triggering cause (cause of death, disability type) is excluded. Claims and benefit operations teams use t']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Annuity benefit payments require journal entries for benefit expense (debit), tax withholding liability (credit), and cash disbursement (credit). Direct GL posting for every annuitization payment and ']
  mortality_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: Life-contingent annuity benefit payments (period certain + life, joint life) require mortality table reference for reserve release calculations and expected payment duration. Actuaries use this link f']
  original_payment_benefit_payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original benefit_payment_id that this record reverses or corrects. Populated only when payment_reversal_indicator is True. Enables tracing of correction chains for audit and reconciliation.']
