  payout_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.payout_schedule. Business justification: benefit_payment records each periodic income payment disbursed under an annuity payout. payout_schedule defines the payment schedule (frequency, amount, option) that governs those payments. A benefit_']
  rider_benefit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.rider_benefit. Business justification: benefit_payment records periodic income payments including rider-based payments (GMWB income, GMIB annuitization, GMAB maturity). rider_benefit is the master record for each optional rider. When a pay']
  rmd_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.rmd_schedule. Business justification: benefit_payment has is_rmd_payment and rmd_tax_year fields indicating RMD-satisfying payments. When a benefit_payment satisfies an RMD obligation (common for qualified annuities in payout phase), it m']
  sar_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sar_filing. Business justification: Annuity payments to suspicious parties or unusual payment patterns may trigger SAR filing per BSA requirements. Links benefit payments to SAR filings for AML transaction monitoring and regulatory repo']
  surrender_charge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.surrender_charge. Business justification: benefit_payment captures surrender_charge_amount for payments that incur surrender charges (e.g., early annuitization or partial surrenders processed as benefit payments). surrender_charge holds the s']
  tax_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Benefit payments generate 1099-R tax documents that must be retained and linked to the originating payment for IRS reporting, owner tax inquiries, and state tax authority audits. The benefit_payment a']
  account_value_at_payment decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annuity contract\'s Account Value (AV) as of the payment date, prior to the disbursement of this payment. Used to assess the relationship between the benefit base and AV, determine if the contract is \'in the money\' on living benefit riders, and support actuarial reserving.']
  benefit_base_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The guaranteed benefit base (rider base) value at the time of this payment, applicable for GMWB, GMIB, or GMAB riders. The benefit base is the notional amount used to calculate guaranteed income or withdrawal amounts and may differ from the contract Account Value (AV).']
  check_number varchar [note: 'The sequential check number assigned to a paper check disbursement. Populated only when payment_method is \'check\'. Used for check reconciliation, stop-payment processing, and audit trail.']
  contract_qualification_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the annuity contract is qualified (funded with pre-tax dollars under ERISA/IRS qualified plans such as IRA, 401(k), 403(b)) or non-qualified (funded with after-tax dollars). Determines tax treatment, exclusion ratio applicability, and RMD obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this benefit payment record was first created in the Sapiens Annuity payment processing system. Serves as the record audit creation timestamp for data lineage and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this payment record (e.g., \'USD\'). Supports multi-currency operations for international annuity contracts.']
  distribution_code varchar [note: 'IRS-defined distribution code reported in Box 7 of Form 1099-R that characterizes the nature of the distribution for tax purposes (e.g., \'1\'=early distribution no exception, \'2\'=early distribution exception applies, \'3\'=disability, \'4\'=death, \'7\'=normal distribution, \'D\'=annuity payments from non-qualified annuity). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|7|D|G|L|P|Q|R|S|T|U|W — promote to reference product]']
  early_withdrawal_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the 10% federal early withdrawal penalty tax assessed on this distribution under IRC Section 72(t) when the annuitant is under age 59½ and no exception applies. Reported on IRS Form 5329.']
  exclusion_ratio_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The non-taxable portion of the gross payment for non-qualified annuity contracts, calculated by applying the IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio (investment in contract divided by expected return) to the gross payment. Represents recovery of the annuitant\'s after-tax investment in the contract.']
  exclusion_ratio_pct decimal(8,6) [note: 'The IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.312500 = 31.25%) applied to this payment to determine the non-taxable recovery of investment in contract. Calculated as investment in contract divided by expected return at annuity start date.']
  federal_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of federal income tax withheld from the gross benefit payment per the annuitant\'s W-4P election or mandatory withholding rules under IRC Section 3405. Reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 4.']
  gross_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total scheduled benefit payment amount before any federal or state tax withholding deductions. Represents the full contractual income entitlement for the payment period. Used as the base for 1099-R gross distribution reporting.']
  investment_in_contract_recovered decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative after-tax investment in the annuity contract that has been recovered (returned tax-free) through all benefit payments up to and including this payment. Tracks the running total of basis recovered under IRC Section 72 to determine when the full investment has been recouped.']
  is_mec_distribution boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment is a distribution from a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) as defined under IRC Section 7702A. MEC distributions are subject to income-first taxation and potential 10% penalty. Reported in IRS Form 1099-R Box 7 with code \'D\'.']
  is_rmd_payment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit payment satisfies all or part of the annuitant\'s Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) obligation for the tax year under IRC Section 401(a)(9). True = this payment counts toward the RMD; False = not an RMD payment.']
  net_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual dollar amount remitted to the annuitant or beneficiary after deducting federal and state tax withholding from the gross payment amount. This is the amount deposited via ACH, issued by check, or wired.']
  payee_bank_account_last4 varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the payee\'s bank account number used for ACH or direct deposit disbursement. Stored as a masked reference for reconciliation and payment verification without retaining full account details. Full account data is stored in the EFT authorization record.']
  payee_routing_number varchar [note: 'ABA routing transit number of the payee\'s financial institution used for ACH or wire transfer disbursement. Nine-digit identifier assigned by the American Bankers Association.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the benefit payment was disbursed or issued to the annuitant or beneficiary. This is the principal real-world event date used for tax reporting, reconciliation, and regulatory compliance.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'The contractual frequency at which benefit payments are scheduled to be disbursed. Drives the payment schedule generation in the Sapiens Annuity platform and is used for actuarial cash flow projections.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The disbursement instrument used to remit the net payment to the annuitant or beneficiary. \'ach\' = Automated Clearing House electronic transfer; \'check\' = paper check mailed to payee; \'wire\' = domestic or international wire transfer; \'direct_deposit\' = direct deposit to a designated financial account.']
  payment_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the benefit period covered by this payment. Together with payment_period_start_date, defines the income interval for tax and actuarial reporting.']
  payment_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the benefit period covered by this payment. Used for income period attribution, tax year allocation, and actuarial reporting.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned by the Sapiens Annuity payment processing module to uniquely identify this disbursement for reconciliation, customer service, and audit purposes.']
  payment_reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment record represents a reversal or correction of a previously issued payment. True = this is a reversal/correction entry; False = original payment. Used for reconciliation and audit trail integrity.']
  payment_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordinal number of this payment within the annuity contract\'s payout schedule (e.g., 1 = first payment, 24 = 24th monthly payment). Used for payment tracking, duration analysis, and identifying the position within the payout phase.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the benefit payment. \'scheduled\' = payment queued for future disbursement; \'issued\' = payment sent to payee; \'cleared\' = confirmed received by payee\'s financial institution; \'returned\' = payment rejected or returned by bank; \'stopped\' = payment halted before issuance; \'voided\' = payment cancelled and reversed.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the benefit payment by its contractual or regulatory basis. Distinguishes annuitization income, Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) payments, Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) income, systematic withdrawals, Required Minimum Distributions (RMD), death benefit payouts, partial surrenders, and lump-sum settlements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annuitization|gmwb_withdrawal|gmib_income|systematic_withdrawal|rmd_distribution|death_benefit|surrender_partial|lump_sum — promote to reference product]']
  payout_phase varchar [note: 'Indicates the contractual phase of the annuity contract at the time of this payment. \'accumulation\' = contract still in growth phase (e.g., systematic withdrawal); \'annuitization\' = contract has been annuitized and is in income phase; \'payout\' = living benefit payout phase; \'surrender\' = payment associated with full or partial surrender.']
  reportable_amount_1099r decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross distribution amount to be reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 1 for this payment. For most payments equals gross_payment_amount; may differ for corrected or adjusted payments. Drives annual 1099-R tax form generation for the annuitant.']
  rmd_tax_year varchar [note: 'The IRS tax year (four-digit year) to which this RMD payment is attributed. Populated only when is_rmd_payment is True. Used for RMD compliance tracking and IRS reporting under IRC Section 401(a)(9).']
  scheduled_payment_date date [note: 'The contractually scheduled date on which the benefit payment was originally planned to be disbursed per the annuity payout schedule. Used to identify late payments and measure payment timeliness against contractual obligations.']
  state_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of state income tax withheld from the gross benefit payment per the annuitant\'s state withholding election or applicable state mandatory withholding rules. Reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 14.']
  state_withholding_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state code identifying the state for which state income tax was withheld on this payment. Reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 15. Required for multi-state tax reporting compliance.']
  surrender_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual deferred sales charge (surrender charge) deducted from the payment amount when a withdrawal or surrender occurs within the surrender charge period defined in the annuity contract. Reduces the net payment to the annuitant.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The IRS tax year (four-digit calendar year) to which this benefit payment is attributed for 1099-R reporting purposes. Typically the year in which the payment_date falls, but may differ for year-end payments processed in January.']
  taxable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the gross payment amount that is subject to ordinary income tax. For non-qualified contracts, this equals gross payment minus the exclusion ratio amount. For qualified contracts, typically equals the full gross payment. Reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 2a.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this benefit payment record was last modified in the source system. Used for incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail maintenance.']
  withholding_election_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the basis for federal tax withholding on this payment. \'elected\' = annuitant submitted a W-4P withholding election; \'mandatory\' = mandatory withholding applies (e.g., eligible rollover distributions); \'waived\' = annuitant elected to waive withholding where permitted under IRC Section 3405.']

  Note: 'Records each periodic income payment disbursed to an annuitant or beneficiary under an annuity payout schedule or living benefit claim. Captures payment date, scheduled payment amount, actual payment amount, federal and state tax withholding, net payment amount, payment method (ACH, check, wire), payment status, 1099-R reportable amount, exclusion ratio applied (for non-qualified contracts under IRC Section 72), and cumulative investment in contract recovered. Sourced from Sapiens Annuity payment processing module.'
}

Table annuity.tax_free_exchange {
  tax_free_exchange_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for tax_free_exchange']
  aml_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_case. Business justification: 1035 exchanges require AML review per BSA source-of-funds verification requirements. High-value exchanges from unknown carriers trigger enhanced due diligence. Links exchange transactions to AML inves']
  annuity_premium_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_premium_payment. Business justification: A 1035 tax-free exchange transfers funds from a source contract to a new annuity contract, resulting in a premium payment on the new contract. The tax_free_exchange record tracks the exchange transact']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the new (receiving) annuity contract issued as a result of this 1035 exchange. Links to the annuity contract master record.']
  exchange_authorization_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: 1035 tax-free exchanges require signed authorization forms and carrier acknowledgment letters per IRS regulations. Links exchange transaction to supporting documents for tax reporting, suitability rev']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: 1035 exchanges have specific accounting treatment requiring journal entries for cost basis transfer, gain deferral, and new contract establishment. Required for GAAP compliance and tax reporting (Form']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder or contract owner initiating the 1035 exchange. Used for KYC, AML, and tax reporting linkage.']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed agent or broker who facilitated the 1035 exchange transaction. Used for commission and suitability tracking.']
  source_in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.in_force_policy. Business justification: IRC Section 1035 tax-free exchange from a life policy to an annuity is a core regulatory compliance process requiring direct FK traceability to the originating life policy for cost basis transfer, IRS']
  source_uw_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: 1035 exchanges INTO annuities from life insurance policies need to reference the original underwriting application for the surrendered life policy. Real business process: replacement documentation, su']
  suitability_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: NAIC Replacement Regulation requires a suitability review specific to 1035 exchange/replacement transactions. This is distinct from the premium payment suitability review — it evaluates whether the re']
  workflow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.workflow. Business justification: 1035 exchanges require multi-step document workflows: replacement notices, suitability reviews, carrier transfer forms, and NIGO resolution. Linking tax_free_exchange to its workflow enables SLA track']
  aml_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Anti-Money Laundering (AML) review conducted for this 1035 exchange transaction. Large or unusual exchanges may trigger enhanced due diligence under BSA/AML requirements.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The reason for cancellation if the 1035 exchange was cancelled before completion. Captures owner-initiated cancellations, carrier rejections, or regulatory holds.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The date on which all funds were received from the source carrier and the exchange was fully processed and posted to the new annuity contract.']
  cost_basis_transferred decimal(18,2) [note: 'The investment in contract (cost basis) transferred from the source contract to the new annuity contract. Preserves the owner\'s after-tax basis for future distribution tax calculations under IRC Section 72.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this 1035 exchange record was first created in the annuity administration system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this exchange record. Typically USD for domestic US annuity contracts.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the 1035 exchange is legally effective — i.e., when the transferred funds are applied to the new annuity contract and the tax-free treatment is established.']
  exchange_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business reference number assigned to this 1035 exchange transaction, used in correspondence with the source carrier and the contract owner.']
  exchange_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the 1035 exchange transaction. \'nigo\' indicates the exchange is Not In Good Order and requires remediation before processing can continue. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_progress|completed|cancelled|nigo|rejected — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]']
  exchange_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the 1035 exchange based on the source and target contract types as permitted under IRC Section 1035. Annuity-to-annuity and life-to-annuity are the most common types in the annuity domain.']
  form_1099r_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IRS Form 1099-R has been issued to the contract owner and filed with the IRS for this 1035 exchange transaction.']
  form_1099r_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IRS Form 1099-R must be issued for this exchange. A 1099-R is required even for tax-free 1035 exchanges to report the distribution from the source contract, using distribution code 6.']
  form_5498_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IRS Form 5498 has been issued to the contract owner and filed with the IRS for this 1035 exchange transaction.']
  form_5498_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IRS Form 5498 must be issued by the receiving carrier to report the rollover or exchange contribution to the new annuity contract.']
  free_look_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the free-look (right to return) period expires for the new annuity contract issued as a result of this exchange. State-mandated free-look periods typically range from 10 to 30 days.']
  funds_received_date date [note: 'The date on which the transferred funds were actually received from the source carrier by the receiving company. Used to track processing timelines and determine the contract\'s account value start date.']
  gain_deferred decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of taxable gain deferred as a result of the 1035 exchange (transferred amount minus cost basis transferred). This gain is not recognized at the time of exchange but will be subject to ordinary income tax upon future distributions.']
  is_mec_new_contract boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the new annuity contract issued as a result of this exchange is classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC). Determined based on MEC carryover from source and new contract funding.']
  is_mec_source boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the source contract was classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) under IRC Section 7702A. MEC status carries over to the new contract in a 1035 exchange, affecting tax treatment of distributions.']
  nigo_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded description of the reason(s) the exchange application was flagged as Not In Good Order (NIGO). Documents the specific deficiency requiring remediation.']
  nigo_resolved_date date [note: 'The date on which all NIGO deficiencies were resolved and the exchange application was cleared for processing.']
  nigo_status varchar [note: 'Tracks the Not In Good Order (NIGO) resolution status for this exchange application. NIGO occurs when required documentation or information is missing or incorrect, preventing processing.']
  partial_exchange_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a partial 1035 exchange (only a portion of the source contract\'s value is transferred) as opposed to a full exchange. Partial exchanges have specific IRS rules regarding basis allocation.']
  replacement_notice_date date [note: 'The date on which the replacement notice was sent to the contract owner as required by NAIC Model Regulation #613 and applicable state replacement regulations.']
  replacement_notice_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required replacement notice (NAIC Model Regulation on Replacement of Life Insurance and Annuities) was sent to the contract owner prior to processing the exchange.']
  request_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract owner formally submitted the 1035 exchange request. Used as the business event initiation date for processing timelines and regulatory reporting.']
  source_account_value_at_exchange decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total account value (AV) of the source contract at the time of the exchange request. Used for partial exchange basis allocation calculations and suitability documentation.']
  source_carrier_naic_code varchar [note: 'The five-digit NAIC company code assigned to the source carrier. Used for regulatory identification and carrier verification during the exchange process.']
  source_carrier_name varchar [note: 'The name of the insurance company or financial institution from which the existing contract is being exchanged. Required for coordination of transfer and IRS Form 1099-R reporting.']
  source_contract_issue_date date [note: 'The original issue date of the source contract being exchanged. Used to determine the contract\'s age, surrender charge schedule applicability, and MEC status carryover.']
  source_contract_type varchar [note: 'The product type of the source contract being exchanged. Determines eligibility under IRC Section 1035 and influences tax treatment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fixed_annuity|variable_annuity|fia|whole_life|universal_life|term_life — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  source_surrender_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The surrender charge or contingent deferred sales charge (CDSC) assessed by the source carrier upon surrender of the existing contract as part of the exchange. Reduces the net transferred amount.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The calendar tax year in which the 1035 exchange is reportable for IRS purposes. Determines the tax reporting period for Form 1099-R and Form 5498 filings.']
  transfer_method varchar [note: 'The method by which funds were transferred from the source carrier to the receiving company. Affects processing timelines and reconciliation procedures.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this 1035 exchange record was last modified in the annuity administration system. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Manages tax-free 1035 exchange transactions where an existing annuity or life insurance contract is exchanged for a new annuity contract without triggering a taxable event under IRC Section 1035. Captures source contract details, source carrier, exchange request date, exchange effective date, transferred amount, cost basis transferred, gain deferred, IRS Form 1099-R and 5498 reporting flags, exchange type (annuity-to-annuity, life-to-annuity), and NIGO (Not In Good Order) resolution status. Critical for tax compliance and new business processing.'
}

Table annuity.rider_benefit {
  rider_benefit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each rider or optional benefit record attached to an annuity contract. Primary key of this entity. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a contractual benefit attachment with its own lifecycle, charge structure, and eligibility rules.']
  annuitant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.annuitant. Business justification: Rider eligibility (LTC, joint-life, disability waiver, nursing home waiver) requires annuitant age, health status, and ADL impairment count. rider_benefit.annuitant_age_at_election is denormalized fro']
  assumption_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.assumption_set. Business justification: Living benefit rider reserve calculations (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB, GMDB) depend on actuarial assumption sets for lapse, utilization, and mortality. This link supports rider reserve adequacy review, charge r']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent annuity contract to which this rider or benefit is attached. Links the rider to its owning contract record in the annuity contract administration system.']
  gaap_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gaap_reserve. Business justification: Rider benefits (GMDB, GMWB, GMIB, GMAB) are classified as Market Risk Benefits under LDTI, requiring fair value measurement in GAAP reserves. Finance teams link each rider benefit election to its GAAP']
  hedge_derivative_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.derivative_contract. Business justification: GMWB/GMDB/GMIB riders are hedged with specific derivative contracts (equity options, variance swaps). This link supports GAAP ASC 815 hedge effectiveness testing, rider-level NAR hedge reporting, and ']
  hedge_portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Optional riders (death benefit enhancements, income riders) create guaranteed liabilities hedged via derivative portfolios. Risk management calculates net amount at risk, hedge effectiveness, and RBC ']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Rider availability, charge rates, benefit structures, and eligibility rules vary by plan version. Essential for rider charge calculations, benefit eligibility validation, and tracking version-specific']
  policy_form_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval. Business justification: Rider forms require separate state approval; rider_form_number must match approved filing. Links rider elections to approved form filings for compliance validation. Essential for market conduct exams ']
  pricing_basis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Living benefit rider terms (charge_rate, rollup_rate, withdrawal_rate) are derived from actuarial pricing_basis assumptions. This link supports product profitability analysis, charge rate adequacy rev']
  rate_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.rate_filing. Business justification: Rider charges (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB charge rates) are subject to state rate filings and DOI approval. rider_benefit must reference the approved rate filing to validate that charge_rate complies with filed']
  rider_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.rider_definition. Business justification: Contract rider elections reference product rider definitions for benefit structure, charge rate schedules, eligibility rules, and underwriting requirements. Critical for rider charge calculations, ben']
  risk_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.risk_class. Business justification: Living benefit riders (GMIB, GMWB, GMDB, LTC) are priced and reserved using the insured\'s underwriting risk class. Rider charge rates and benefit eligibility thresholds are risk-class-dependent. Actua']
  statutory_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.statutory_reserve. Business justification: Living benefit riders require dedicated statutory reserve components under CARVM and PBR. Appointed actuaries must link rider benefit records to statutory reserve calculations for annual statement Exh']
  suitability_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: Living benefit rider elections (GMWB, GMIB, GMAB) require suitability reviews under NAIC Senior Protection regulations, particularly for annuitants over 65. The rider_benefit must reference its suitab']
  adl_trigger_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of Activities of Daily Living (ADL) impairments required to trigger benefit eligibility under an LTC rider. Typically 2 out of 6 standard ADLs per HIPAA Section 7702B. Null for non-LTC riders.']
  benefit_base_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current dollar value of the guaranteed benefit base associated with this rider. For GMWB/GMIB/GMAB riders, this is the protected value used to calculate guaranteed withdrawals or income. Distinct from account value (AV); may be higher than AV after market downturns. Tracked separately in benefit_base product but captured here as the rider\'s principal financial fact.']
  benefit_election_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the contract owner has formally elected to begin receiving benefits under this rider. Not_elected = rider is in force but benefits not yet activated; Elected = owner has triggered benefit commencement; In_payout = benefit payments are actively being made; Exhausted = benefit pool fully depleted; Waived = owner waived benefit election rights.']
  benefit_eligible_date date [note: 'Earliest calendar date on which the contract owner becomes eligible to elect or receive benefits under this rider, calculated from the effective_date plus any applicable waiting period. Used in customer service, benefit election processing, and proactive outreach campaigns.']
  charge_basis varchar [note: 'The financial base against which the rider charge rate is applied. Benefit_base = charge applied to the guaranteed benefit base; Account_value = charge applied to the current account value (AV); Premium_base = charge applied to cumulative premiums paid; NAV = charge applied to net asset value for variable products.']
  charge_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the rider charge is assessed and deducted from the contract. Drives billing cycle integration and account value deduction scheduling in the annuity administration platform.']
  charge_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Annual rider charge rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.009500 = 0.95% per annum) assessed against the benefit base or account value. This is the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity. Used in premium billing, account value deduction calculations, and profitability analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rider benefit record was first created in the data platform (Silver layer). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions.']
  death_benefit_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of death benefit provided by this rider for GMDB or enhanced death benefit riders. Return_of_premium = beneficiary receives at least cumulative premiums paid; Highest_anniversary = DB equals highest contract anniversary value; Earnings_enhancement = DB includes a percentage of earnings; Standard = base contract DB with no enhancement; None = rider does not provide a death benefit.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the rider becomes contractually effective and benefit guarantees begin to accrue. Marks the start of the rider\'s binding period. Used in benefit base calculations, waiting period tracking, and actuarial reserving.']
  election_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract owner formally elected or added this rider to the annuity contract. May differ from effective_date if there is a processing lag or waiting period. Relevant for suitability review and new business processing audit trails.']
  inflation_protection_type varchar [note: 'Type of inflation protection feature included in the rider, primarily relevant for LTC riders. None = no inflation protection; Simple = benefit increases by a fixed percentage of the original benefit; Compound = benefit increases by a fixed percentage of the current benefit; CPI_linked = benefit increases tied to the Consumer Price Index.']
  is_irrevocable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rider, once elected, cannot be removed by the contract owner during the contract\'s life (True = irrevocable; False = owner may remove the rider). Relevant for LTC riders and certain GMIB riders that become irrevocable upon benefit election.']
  is_optional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rider was optionally elected by the contract owner (True) or is a mandatory benefit included in the base contract without separate election (False). Drives charge assessment logic and suitability review requirements.']
  joint_life_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rider covers both the primary annuitant and a joint annuitant (True = joint life coverage; False = single life coverage). Joint life riders typically carry a higher charge rate and provide continued benefits upon the death of the first annuitant.']
  ltc_benefit_period_months varchar [note: 'Maximum number of months for which long-term care benefits are payable under an LTC rider. Applicable only to LTC rider types. Null for non-LTC riders. Used in LTC benefit pool calculations and NAIC Long-Term Care Insurance Model Regulation compliance reporting.']
  max_benefit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual ceiling on the total benefit payable under this rider over its lifetime. Applicable to riders with aggregate benefit caps (e.g., LTC riders with lifetime maximum benefit pools, enhanced death benefit riders with maximum DB multiples). Null if no contractual maximum applies.']
  max_charge_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Contractually guaranteed maximum annual charge rate that the insurer may assess for this rider, expressed as a decimal. Protects the contract owner from unlimited charge increases. Required disclosure under NAIC Annuity Disclosure Model Regulation.']
  max_issue_age varchar [note: 'Maximum age of the annuitant at which this rider may be elected, as defined in the rider\'s contractual terms. Used in new business eligibility validation and underwriting rules engine checks.']
  min_issue_age varchar [note: 'Minimum age of the annuitant at which this rider may be elected, as defined in the rider\'s contractual terms. Used in new business eligibility validation.']
  reinsurance_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the risk associated with this rider is subject to a reinsurance treaty (True = reinsured; False = retained). Relevant for GMDB and LTC riders which are commonly reinsured. Drives cession processing in the reinsurance management system.']
  reserve_basis varchar [note: 'Accounting basis under which actuarial reserves for this rider are calculated. GAAP = Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (FASB ASC 944 / LDTI); SAP = Statutory Accounting Principles (NAIC); PBR = Principle-Based Reserving; IFRS17 = International Financial Reporting Standards 17. Used in actuarial valuation system configuration.']
  rider_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rider or benefit attachment. Active = rider is in force and charges are being assessed; Suspended = rider temporarily not in force (e.g., during free-look period); Terminated = rider has been removed or contract surrendered; Pending = rider elected but not yet effective; Lapsed = rider lapsed due to non-payment of rider charge; Matured = rider has reached its contractual maturity date.']
  rider_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the rider product design, corresponding to a specific generation of benefit terms, charge rates, and eligibility rules. Insurers periodically update rider versions; this field distinguishes legacy riders from current offerings and is critical for actuarial valuation and reserving under LDTI.']
  rollup_period_years varchar [note: 'Maximum number of years during which the guaranteed rollup rate is applied to grow the benefit base. After this period, the benefit base stops rolling up but the rider may remain in force. Null if rollup continues indefinitely until benefit election.']
  rollup_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Annual guaranteed growth rate applied to the benefit base during the accumulation (deferral) phase, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.060000 = 6.00% per annum). Applicable to GMWB, GMIB, and GMAB riders. Null for riders without a rollup feature. Critical input for actuarial reserving and LDTI cash flow projections.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this rider benefit record was sourced (e.g., \'SAPIENS_ANNUITY\', \'FAST_ANNUITY\'). Used for data lineage tracking, ETL reconciliation, and Silver layer audit in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  source_system_rider_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this rider record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Sapiens Annuity internal rider ID). Enables back-referencing to the source system for reconciliation, issue resolution, and ETL lineage tracing.']
  state_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of US state codes (2-letter USPS abbreviations) in which this rider form has been approved and is available for sale. Null or \'ALL\' if available in all states. Used in new business eligibility validation and regulatory compliance tracking.']
  step_up_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the automatic step-up (ratchet) evaluation occurs for the benefit base. Annual = evaluated each contract anniversary; Triennial = every 3 years; Quinquennial = every 5 years; On_demand = owner may request step-up at any time subject to conditions; None = no step-up provision. Null if step_up_provision is False.']
  step_up_provision boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rider includes an automatic or optional step-up (ratchet) provision that locks in market gains by resetting the benefit base to the current account value if the account value exceeds the benefit base on a specified anniversary date. True = step-up provision exists; False = no step-up.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the rider was or is scheduled to be terminated. Null if the rider is still in force. Populated upon rider lapse, voluntary removal, contract surrender, annuitization, or death claim settlement.']
  termination_trigger varchar [note: 'The contractual condition or event that caused or will cause this rider to terminate. Surrender = full contract surrender; Death = death of the annuitant or owner; Annuitization = contract converted to income stream; Account_value_zero = account value depleted to zero; Owner_request = voluntary rider removal by owner; Non_payment = rider charge could not be deducted. Null if rider is still active.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rider benefit record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail compliance under SOX. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions.']
  waiting_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of contract years that must elapse before the contract owner is eligible to elect benefits under this rider. Common for GMIB riders which typically require a 10-year deferral period before annuitization. Zero if no waiting period applies.']
  withdrawal_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Annual guaranteed withdrawal percentage of the benefit base that the contract owner may withdraw each year without reducing the guaranteed benefit, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.050000 = 5.00%). Applicable to GMWB riders. May vary by age band at election. Null for non-GMWB riders.']

  Note: 'Master record for each optional rider or benefit attached to an annuity contract, including GMWB, GMIB, GMAB, GMDB (Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit), enhanced death benefit, long-term care (LTC) riders, return-of-premium riders, and earnings enhancement riders. Captures rider type, rider effective date, rider charge rate, benefit election status, benefit trigger conditions, maximum benefit amount, rider termination conditions, and rider version/form number. Distinct from benefit_base which tracks the financial balance of living benefit guarantees; this entity tracks the contractual terms, eligibility rules, and charge structure of the rider.'
}

Table annuity.annuity_premium_payment {
  annuity_premium_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each annuity premium payment transaction record in the annuity payment receipt ledger.']
  aml_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the AML case record in the compliance domain when this payment has been escalated for AML investigation. Null if no AML case has been opened for this payment.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuity contract to which this premium payment is applied. Links the payment to the contract administration record in the Annuity Administration Platform.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Premium receipts require journal entries for cash receipt (debit), premium revenue recognition (credit), premium load (credit), and DAC capitalization (debit). Core accounting transaction for every pr']
  modal_premium_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the modal premium schedule that governs the payment frequency and amount for flexible premium deferred annuities. Null for single-premium or ad-hoc payments not tied to a scheduled billing cycle.']
  owner_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party record of the annuity contract owner who is making or authorizing the premium payment. Used for KYC/AML validation and tax reporting under IRC Section 72.']
  premium_bill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_bill. Business justification: Annuity premium reconciliation requires tracing each annuity_premium_payment to the premium_bill that triggered it. Supports billing statement reconciliation, audit trails, and NSF/grace period proces']
  sar_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sar_filing. Business justification: BSA/AML regulations require SAR filings for suspicious premium payments (e.g., large cash deposits, structuring). annuity_premium_payment needs a direct sar_filing_id for the SAR reporting workflow — ']
  suitability_review_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the suitability review record in the compliance domain associated with this premium payment, particularly for subsequent premium payments that may trigger re-suitability assessment under NAIC Annuity Suitability Model Regulation.']
  suspense_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the suspense account record where the payment funds are held pending NIGO resolution or contract identification. Null for payments applied directly without suspense.']
  uw_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Premium payments on annuities may reference the originating underwriting application for audit trail, especially for 1035 exchanges from life policies or initial annuity applications requiring underwr']
  ach_trace_number varchar [note: 'The ACH trace number assigned by the originating depository financial institution (ODFI) for ACH electronic payment transactions. Used for NACHA reconciliation and return item tracking when payment_source is \'ach\'.']
  aml_review_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this payment has been flagged for AML review due to large cash transaction reporting thresholds, structuring patterns, or other suspicious activity indicators per FinCEN BSA requirements.']
  applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net dollar amount actually credited to the annuity contract Account Value (AV) after deduction of any applicable premium load, surrender charges on exchanged contracts, or other fees. May equal gross_payment_amount if no deductions apply.']
  bonus_credited_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual dollar amount of premium bonus credited to the contract AV as a result of this payment. Calculated as bonus_eligible_amount multiplied by the applicable bonus rate per the contract terms.']
  bonus_eligible_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the gross payment amount that qualifies for a premium bonus credit under the annuity contract terms. Some annuity products offer a percentage bonus on qualifying premium payments. Null if the contract does not offer a premium bonus.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'The check number printed on the physical check when payment_source is \'check\'. Used for bank reconciliation and payment tracing. Null for non-check payment sources.']
  contract_year varchar [note: 'The contract year in which this payment falls, calculated from the contract issue date. Used for surrender charge schedule application, bonus crediting eligibility, and actuarial valuation purposes.']
  cost_basis_contribution decimal(18,2) [note: 'The after-tax cost basis amount contributed by this payment to the annuity contract\'s cumulative investment in the contract (IIC). For non-qualified annuities, this is the after-tax portion of the payment. For qualified annuities, this is typically zero. Critical for IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio calculations and IRS Form 1099-R reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment record was first created in the annuity administration system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount. Typically \'USD\' for domestic US annuity contracts. Required for multi-currency reporting and GAAP/SAP financial statements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The contract effective date on which the payment is credited to the annuity contract Account Value (AV). May differ from payment_date due to processing lag, back-dating rules, or 1035 exchange settlement timing.']
  exchange_cost_basis_received decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost basis (investment in the contract) transferred from the surrendered contract in a 1035 exchange. This amount carries over to the new annuity contract and is critical for IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio calculations. Null for non-exchange payments.']
  exchange_source_carrier varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance carrier or financial institution from which 1035 exchange proceeds were transferred. Required for 1035 exchange documentation and AML source-of-funds verification.']
  exchange_source_contract_number varchar [note: 'The contract or policy number of the surrendered/exchanged contract from which 1035 exchange proceeds originated. Required for IRS Form 1035 documentation and audit trail when is_1035_exchange = true. Null for non-exchange payments.']
  gross_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross dollar amount of the premium payment as received from the payor before any deductions, fees, or adjustments. This is the base monetary amount used for AV crediting and cost basis calculations.']
  is_1035_exchange boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this payment represents proceeds received from a tax-free 1035 exchange (IRC Section 1035 — Tax-Free Insurance Policy Exchange). When true, additional 1035 exchange tracking fields apply and the payment requires IRS Form 1035 documentation.']
  is_unscheduled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this payment is an unscheduled additional premium contribution not tied to the modal premium schedule. Common for flexible premium deferred annuities where owners make ad-hoc contributions.']
  nigo_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the NIGO deficiency reason when nigo_status is \'nigo_pending\' or \'nigo_rejected\'. Examples: missing suitability form, missing signature, missing source of funds documentation, incomplete beneficiary designation.']
  nigo_resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the NIGO deficiency was resolved and the payment was cleared for application to the annuity contract. Null if nigo_status is \'good_order\' or \'nigo_pending\'.']
  nigo_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the payment application was received in good order or flagged as Not In Good Order (NIGO). \'good_order\' = all required documentation and information received; \'nigo_pending\' = deficiency identified, awaiting resolution; \'nigo_resolved\' = NIGO deficiency resolved and payment applied; \'nigo_rejected\' = NIGO could not be resolved and payment returned.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The business date on which the premium payment was received by the company. This is the principal real-world event date used for contract crediting, cost basis tracking, and IRC Section 72 tax reporting.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'The modal frequency at which this payment is made under the contract\'s premium schedule. \'single\' = one-time single premium; \'annual\' = annual mode; \'semi_annual\' = semi-annual mode; \'quarterly\' = quarterly mode; \'monthly\' = monthly mode. Drives premium billing schedule and APE (Annual Premium Equivalent) calculations.']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to this premium payment transaction by the Annuity Administration Platform (e.g., Sapiens Annuity or FAST Annuity). Used for reconciliation, customer correspondence, and audit trails.']
  payment_source varchar [note: 'The remittance instrument or channel through which the premium payment funds were received. Drives reconciliation workflows and AML monitoring thresholds. \'check\' = personal or cashier\'s check; \'wire\' = wire transfer; \'ach\' = ACH electronic funds transfer; \'1035_exchange\' = proceeds from a 1035 tax-free exchange; \'rollover_check\' = qualified plan rollover check; \'credit_card\' = credit card payment.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the premium payment transaction. \'pending\' = received but not yet applied to contract AV; \'posted\' = successfully applied; \'reversed\' = payment reversed after posting; \'returned\' = payment returned by financial institution (e.g., NSF); \'suspended\' = held in suspense pending NIGO resolution; \'rejected\' = rejected and not applied.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the premium payment by its business purpose. \'initial_single_premium\' = first and only premium for SPIAs or single-premium deferred annuities; \'subsequent_flexible_premium\' = additional premium for flexible premium deferred annuities; \'1035_exchange_proceeds\' = funds received via a tax-free 1035 exchange; \'rollover\' = qualified plan rollover (IRA, 401k); \'transfer\' = direct trustee-to-trustee transfer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initial_single_premium|subsequent_flexible_premium|1035_exchange_proceeds|rollover|transfer|direct_transfer|trustee_transfer — promote to reference product]']
  payor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual or entity that remitted the premium payment. May differ from the contract owner when a third party remits payment. Required for AML/KYC source-of-funds verification and IRS tax reporting.']
  payor_relationship_to_owner varchar [note: 'Describes the relationship of the payor to the annuity contract owner when the payor is not the owner. Used for AML third-party payment monitoring and suitability review. \'self\' = owner is the payor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: self|spouse|parent|child|trust|employer|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  premium_load_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of premium load or front-end charge deducted from the gross payment before crediting to the contract AV. Represents the difference between gross_payment_amount and applied_amount attributable to premium loads.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment reversal was processed and the AV adjustment was made to the annuity contract. Null for non-reversed payments.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for payment reversal when payment_status is \'reversed\'. Examples: duplicate payment, incorrect contract application, owner request, NSF return. Null for non-reversed payments.']
  scheduled_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The contractually scheduled premium amount for this payment period as defined in the modal premium schedule. Compared against gross_payment_amount to identify over-payments, under-payments, or unscheduled additional premiums.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this payment record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. \'SAPIENS_ANNUITY\' = Sapiens Annuity platform; \'FAST_ANNUITY\' = FAST Annuity platform; \'POLICY_ADMIN\' = Policy Administration System; \'MANUAL\' = manually entered correction.']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'The native transaction identifier assigned by the originating operational system (e.g., Sapiens Annuity or FAST Annuity) for this payment record. Used for data lineage, reconciliation between Silver Layer and source systems, and audit trail.']
  tax_qualification_code varchar [note: 'Indicates the tax qualification status of the premium payment, which determines cost basis treatment and tax reporting obligations under IRC Section 72. \'qualified\' = pre-tax qualified plan funds (IRA, 401k, 403b); \'non_qualified\' = after-tax funds; \'roth_ira\' = Roth IRA funds; \'sep_ira\' = SEP-IRA; \'simple_ira\' = SIMPLE IRA; \'403b\' = 403(b) tax-sheltered annuity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: qualified|non_qualified|roth_ira|sep_ira|simple_ira|403b|457b|pension — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment record was last modified in the annuity administration system. Tracks status changes, NIGO resolution updates, and corrections.']
  wire_reference_number varchar [note: 'The wire transfer reference or IMAD (Input Message Accountability Data) number assigned by the sending financial institution when payment_source is \'wire\'. Used for Fedwire reconciliation and AML source-of-funds documentation.']

  Note: 'Records all premium and purchase payment transactions applied to annuity contracts, including initial single premiums, subsequent flexible premiums (for flexible premium deferred annuities), and 1035 exchange proceeds received. Captures payment date, payment amount, payment source (check, wire, ACH, 1035 exchange), tax qualification of payment (qualified/non-qualified), cost basis contribution, modal premium schedule reference, and NIGO resolution status. Distinct from billing domain premium billing records; this is the annuity-specific payment receipt ledger.'
}

Table annuity.cost_basis_ledger {
  cost_basis_ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each cost basis ledger record in the annuity domain. Primary key for the cost_basis_ledger data product.']
  annuitant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.annuitant. Business justification: IRC §72 exclusion ratio calculation requires the annuitant\'s age at annuity start date. cost_basis_ledger.owner_age_at_annuity_start is a denormalized value from annuitant; a direct FK enables exclusi']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuity contract for which this cost basis ledger record is maintained. Links to the annuity contract master.']
  contract_owner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract owner (policyholder) party record. The owner is the taxpayer responsible for IRC Section 72 cost basis tracking and 1099-R reporting.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Cost basis adjustments (1035 exchange basis transfers, partial surrender basis reductions, MEC reclassifications) generate journal entries for deferred gain/loss accounting. Finance teams link cost ba']
  mortality_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: Exclusion ratio calculations for annuity taxation use IRS mortality tables (IRC Section 72) to determine expected return. Required for accurate Form 1099-R reporting, tax compliance, and audit defense']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Tax qualification rules, IRC 72 compliance parameters, and exclusion ratio calculations are version-specific. Required for accurate 1099-R reporting, cost basis recovery calculations, and ensuring ver']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Cost basis ledgers directly support IRS Form 1099-R and Form 5498 regulatory filings. The cost_basis_ledger must reference the regulatory filing it supports for tax reporting compliance and audit trai']
  tax_free_exchange_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.tax_free_exchange. Business justification: cost_basis_ledger tracks investment in contract (cost basis) including exchange_basis_received and source_contract_number fields that indicate 1035 exchange origin. tax_free_exchange manages the 1035 ']
  annual_exclusion_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annual dollar amount of annuity payments that is excludable from gross income under the exclusion ratio. Equals the periodic payment amount times payment frequency times the exclusion ratio. Reported as the non-taxable amount on IRS Form 1099-R.']
  annuitization_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the annuity contract entered the payout (annuitization) phase. Marks the transition from accumulation to distribution phase and triggers the exclusion ratio calculation for periodic payments.']
  annuity_start_date_basis varchar [note: 'Indicates the event that established the annuity start date for IRC Section 72 purposes. Determines which date is used as the reference point for expected return and exclusion ratio calculations.']
  contract_issue_date date [note: 'The date the annuity contract was originally issued. Establishes the start of the investment-in-contract accumulation period for IRC Section 72 cost basis purposes.']
  cost_basis_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for the most recent adjustment to the investment in contract. Used for audit trail and tax reporting reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1035_exchange_in|1035_exchange_out|partial_surrender|correction|court_order|divorce_transfer|death_claim|annuitization|premium_addition — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this cost basis ledger record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  cumulative_premiums_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total after-tax premium contributions made to the annuity contract since inception or since the last 1035 exchange. Represents the gross cost basis before adjustments for tax-free distributions received.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this cost basis ledger record. Typically USD for domestic annuity contracts.']
  distribution_code varchar [note: 'The IRS distribution code to be reported in Box 7 of IRS Form 1099-R. Indicates the type of distribution for tax purposes (e.g., 1=early distribution no exception, 2=early distribution exception applies, 4=death, 6=1035 exchange, 7=normal distribution). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7|8|9|A|B|C|D|E|F|G|H|J|K|L|M|N|P|Q|R|S|T|U|W — promote to reference product]']
  early_withdrawal_penalty_applies boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the 10% additional tax on early distributions under IRC Section 72(q) applies to distributions from this contract. True if the owner is under age 59½ and no exception applies.']
  exchange_basis_received decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost basis transferred into this annuity contract from a prior contract via a tax-free 1035 exchange. Carries over the investment-in-contract from the surrendered contract per IRC Section 1035.']
  exchange_basis_transferred_out decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost basis transferred out of this annuity contract to a receiving contract via a tax-free 1035 exchange. Reduces the investment-in-contract of this contract.']
  exclusion_ratio decimal(10,8) [note: 'The ratio of the investment in contract to the expected return, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.35000000 = 35%). Applied to each annuity payment to determine the non-taxable portion under IRC Section 72(b). Calculated as remaining_investment_in_contract divided by expected_return.']
  exclusion_ratio_exhausted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investment in contract has been fully recovered through prior non-taxable distributions. When True, all future annuity payments are fully taxable with no exclusion allowed under IRC Section 72(b)(2).']
  exclusion_ratio_exhausted_date date [note: 'The date on which the investment in contract was fully recovered and the exclusion ratio was exhausted. Null if the exclusion ratio has not yet been exhausted.']
  expected_return decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount expected to be received under the annuity contract over the payment period, calculated using IRS actuarial tables (life expectancy multiples) per IRC Section 72(c). Used as the denominator in the exclusion ratio calculation.']
  expected_return_multiple decimal(8,1) [note: 'The life expectancy multiple from the applicable IRS actuarial table (IRS Publication 939) used to calculate the expected return. Expressed in years (e.g., 20.0 years). Multiplied by the annual payment amount to derive the expected return.']
  form_1099r_box1_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross distribution amount to be reported in Box 1 of IRS Form 1099-R for the applicable tax year. Represents total distributions paid from the contract during the tax year.']
  form_1099r_box2a_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The taxable amount to be reported in Box 2a of IRS Form 1099-R for the applicable tax year. Represents the portion of gross distributions that is includable in gross income after applying the exclusion ratio.']
  form_1099r_taxable_amount_not_determined boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the taxable amount cannot be determined by the insurance company and Box 2b \'Taxable amount not determined\' should be checked on IRS Form 1099-R. True when the company lacks sufficient cost basis information to compute the taxable amount.']
  investment_in_contract decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative after-tax investment in the annuity contract as defined under IRC Section 72(e)(6). Represents the total premiums paid less any amounts previously received tax-free. This is the primary cost basis figure used for exclusion ratio calculations and 1099-R reporting.']
  last_adjustment_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent adjustment to the investment in contract (cost basis). Used for audit trail and reconciliation with the annuity administration platform.']
  ledger_as_of_date date [note: 'The business date through which this cost basis ledger snapshot is current. Used for point-in-time reporting, year-end tax reporting, and 1099-R preparation.']
  ledger_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this cost basis ledger record became effective, typically the contract issue date or the date of a 1035 exchange that established the initial investment in contract.']
  ledger_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost basis ledger record. Active indicates ongoing tracking; closed indicates the contract has been fully surrendered or annuitized; suspended indicates a hold pending review or correction; pending_review indicates an audit or reconciliation is in progress.']
  mec_determination_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract was determined to be or not to be a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC). Null if MEC determination has not been triggered.']
  mec_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the annuity contract has been classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) under IRC Section 7702A. MEC status affects the tax treatment of distributions, subjecting them to LIFO (last-in, first-out) income recognition and potential 10% penalty tax.']
  nontaxable_distributions_received decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount of distributions that have been treated as non-taxable return of cost basis (investment in contract) under the IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio. Reduces the remaining investment in contract.']
  partial_surrender_basis_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative reduction to the investment in contract resulting from partial surrenders or withdrawals that returned cost basis to the owner. Tracks the pro-rata cost basis recovery applied to partial surrenders of non-qualified annuities.']
  payout_option varchar [note: 'The annuitization payout option elected by the contract owner. Determines the expected return calculation method and applicable IRS actuarial table (Table V, VI, or VII) for the exclusion ratio.']
  periodic_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The fixed periodic payment amount received under the annuitization option. Used to calculate the annual exclusion amount (periodic_payment_amount multiplied by payment frequency multiplied by exclusion_ratio).']
  remaining_investment_in_contract decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining unrecovered cost basis in the annuity contract as of the ledger_as_of_date. Calculated as investment_in_contract minus nontaxable_distributions_received. Drives the exclusion ratio for future annuity payments.']
  source_contract_number varchar [note: 'The contract number of the prior annuity or life insurance contract from which cost basis was received via a 1035 exchange. Null if no 1035 exchange occurred.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The calendar tax year for which this cost basis ledger snapshot is prepared. Used for annual 1099-R tax reporting and IRS compliance. Format: YYYY (e.g., 2024).']
  taxable_distributions_received decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount of distributions that have been treated as taxable income (earnings above cost basis) under IRC Section 72. Reported on IRS Form 1099-R Box 2a.']
  total_distributions_received decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative gross distributions (withdrawals, partial surrenders, annuity payments) received from the contract since inception. Used in conjunction with the exclusion ratio to determine the taxable and non-taxable portions of each distribution.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this cost basis ledger record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Tracks the investment in contract (cost basis) for each annuity contract for IRC Section 72 tax reporting purposes. Captures cumulative after-tax premiums paid, cost basis adjustments from 1035 exchanges, partial surrender cost basis recovery, exclusion ratio calculation inputs, total distributions received, remaining investment in contract, and MEC (Modified Endowment Contract) status flag. Essential for accurate 1099-R tax reporting and IRC Section 72 exclusion ratio calculations for non-qualified annuities.'
}

Table annuity.contract_valuation {
  contract_valuation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each contract valuation snapshot record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_value_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.account_value. Business justification: contract_valuation is a periodic valuation snapshot capturing the full financial position of an annuity contract. account_value is the authoritative periodic valuation ledger for the same contract. A ']
  assumption_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.assumption_set. Business justification: LDTI/GAAP reserve reporting requires tracing which actuarial assumption set (discount rate, lapse, mortality) produced each contract valuation snapshot. Actuaries and finance teams depend on this link']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annuity contract being valued. Links this valuation snapshot to the parent annuity contract master record.']
  gaap_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gaap_reserve. Business justification: Valuation outputs feed GAAP reserve calculations for financial statement liability reporting under LDTI. Actuarial cash flow projections and discount rates from valuation runs populate reserve balance']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract owner/policyholder party record. Supports policyholder-level aggregation and annual statement generation.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Contract valuation snapshots must reference the investment portfolio driving reserve calculations for ALM reporting, GAAP/STAT reserve attribution, and investment performance reporting. Actuaries and ']
  scenario_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.scenario_set. Business justification: Variable and FIA annuity contract valuations under VM-21 CTE70 stochastic reserving reference a specific economic scenario set. This link enables traceability of which scenario set drove a contract-le']
  statutory_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.statutory_reserve. Business justification: Valuation run results directly populate statutory reserve amounts for regulatory filings. Actuarial valuation outputs feed reserve balances reported in NAIC Annual Statement and state regulatory submi']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the actuarial valuation batch run that produced this record. Enables traceability of valuation results back to a specific run of the actuarial valuation system (MoSes, AXIS, or Prophet). Supports audit and restatement workflows.']
  account_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total Account Value (AV) of the annuity contract as of the valuation date. Represents the accumulated fund value including premiums paid, credited interest/index credits, and less withdrawals and charges. Core financial metric for annuity contract administration.']
  accrued_rider_charges decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total rider charges accrued but not yet deducted from the AV as of the valuation date. Includes charges for GMWB, GMIB, GMAB, GMDB, and other optional benefit riders. Used for accurate AV reconciliation and income statement accruals.']
  annuitization_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract transitioned from the accumulation phase to the annuitization/payout phase. Null if the contract has not yet been annuitized. Triggers shift from AV-based to income-stream-based valuation.']
  cash_surrender_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Cash Surrender Value (CSV) available to the contract owner upon full surrender as of the valuation date. Equals AV less applicable surrender charges and market value adjustments. Used for policyholder statements and surrender processing.']
  contract_phase varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle phase of the annuity contract at the valuation date. Accumulation phase tracks AV growth; annuitization/payout phase tracks income stream; surrender and death claim phases indicate terminal events.']
  cost_basis decimal(18,2) [note: 'The owner\'s investment in the contract (cost basis) as of the valuation date per IRC Section 72. Represents after-tax premiums paid less any tax-free distributions received. Used to determine the taxable gain upon surrender or annuitization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contract valuation record was first created in the Silver layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under SOX.']
  credited_interest_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The current credited interest rate applied to the contract AV for the valuation period. For fixed annuities, this is the declared rate. For FIA contracts, this reflects the effective rate after index crediting. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.045 = 4.5%).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this valuation record. Typically USD for domestic US annuity contracts.']
  death_benefit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Death Benefit (DB) payable to the beneficiary upon the death of the annuitant or contract owner as of the valuation date. May equal AV, a return-of-premium guarantee, or a stepped-up value depending on contract terms.']
  gaap_reserve decimal(18,2) [note: 'The GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) reserve for this contract as of the valuation date, calculated under FASB ASC 944 including LDTI requirements. Supports financial statement reporting and SOX compliance.']
  gmab_benefit_base decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit (GMAB) benefit base as of the valuation date. Represents the guaranteed minimum AV at the end of the accumulation period, protecting against investment losses.']
  gmab_rider_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the GMAB rider is currently active on this contract as of the valuation date. Drives inclusion of GMAB benefit base and maturity guarantee calculations.']
  gmdb_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit (GMDB) base or benefit amount as of the valuation date. Represents the minimum death benefit guaranteed regardless of investment performance. Applicable for variable and indexed annuities with GMDB riders.']
  gmib_benefit_base decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit (GMIB) benefit base as of the valuation date. Used to calculate the minimum annuitization income stream the contract owner is guaranteed upon exercising the GMIB rider.']
  gmib_rider_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the GMIB rider is currently active on this contract as of the valuation date. Drives inclusion of GMIB benefit base calculations and annuitization guarantee valuations.']
  gmwb_annual_withdrawal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum annual withdrawal amount guaranteed under the GMWB rider as of the valuation date. Calculated as the GMWB benefit base multiplied by the applicable withdrawal percentage. Used for RMD compliance checks and policyholder benefit illustrations.']
  gmwb_benefit_base decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit (GMWB) benefit base as of the valuation date. This is the notional amount used to calculate the guaranteed annual withdrawal amount, which may differ from AV due to step-ups, roll-ups, or bonus credits.']
  gmwb_rider_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the GMWB rider is currently active on this contract as of the valuation date. Drives inclusion of GMWB benefit base and NAR calculations in the valuation.']
  index_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The index credit amount applied to the contract AV during the current index crediting period as of the valuation date. Applicable for Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA) contracts. Reflects the gain linked to the selected index strategy.']
  is_1035_exchange boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract was funded via a 1035 tax-free exchange from a prior insurance or annuity contract. True if funded by 1035 exchange; False if funded by direct premium. Affects cost basis tracking and tax reporting.']
  is_mec boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the annuity contract has been classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) under IRC Section 7702A. MEC status affects the tax treatment of withdrawals and loans. True if MEC; False otherwise.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The original issue date of the annuity contract. Used to calculate contract duration, surrender charge schedule position, and benefit base roll-up periods.']
  market_value_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Market Value Adjustment (MVA) applicable to the contract as of the valuation date. Positive or negative adjustment applied to the surrender value based on changes in interest rates since contract issue. Applicable for MVA-type fixed annuities.']
  maturity_date date [note: 'The contractual maturity date of the annuity contract, after which the contract must be annuitized or surrendered per contract terms. Used for GMAB maturity benefit calculations and policyholder notifications.']
  nar_gmdb decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Net Amount at Risk (NAR) for the GMDB rider as of the valuation date. Calculated as the excess of the GMDB value over the current AV. Represents the insurer\'s economic exposure on the death benefit guarantee. Key input for actuarial reserving and reinsurance cession.']
  nar_gmwb decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Net Amount at Risk (NAR) for the GMWB rider as of the valuation date. Represents the present value of projected future guaranteed withdrawals in excess of projected AV. Key input for LDTI/IFRS 17 liability measurement.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific annuity product type (e.g., FIA, SPIA, variable annuity, fixed annuity) associated with this contract. Drives product-specific valuation logic and benefit calculations.']
  rmd_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) amount calculated for the contract for the current tax year as of the valuation date. Applicable for qualified annuity contracts (IRA, 401(k)). Ensures IRS compliance under IRC Section 401(a)(9).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational source system that originated this valuation data. Supports data lineage tracking across the lakehouse Silver layer.']
  statutory_reserve decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated statutory reserve for this contract as of the valuation date, calculated under SAP (Statutory Accounting Principles) per NAIC requirements. Includes Commissioners Annuity Reserve Valuation Method (CARVM) or Principle-Based Reserving (PBR) as applicable. Feeds state regulatory reserve filings.']
  surrender_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The contingent deferred sales charge (CDSC) or surrender charge applicable to the contract as of the valuation date. Deducted from AV to arrive at CSV. Typically declines over the surrender charge period.']
  surrender_charge_period_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the surrender charge period expires for this contract. After this date, the contract owner may surrender without incurring a surrender charge. Key date for policyholder communications and surrender processing.']
