  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Facultative submissions for risks in specific states must comply with state reinsurance regulations (authorized reinsurer requirements, credit for reinsurance rules). Reinsurance underwriters must ver']
  treaty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance treaty under which this facultative submission is being placed, if applicable. Nullable when the submission is entirely outside existing treaty arrangements.']
  underwriting_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: Facultative submissions are evaluated against underwriting classes to determine reinsurer terms and table ratings. The risk_classification plain attribute is a denormalized underwriting class referenc']
  approved_cession_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual cession amount approved by the reinsurer, which may differ from the requested cession amount if the reinsurer offered partial coverage or modified terms.']
  aps_received_date date [note: 'The date on which the Attending Physician Statement (APS) was received from the insured\'s physician and included in the facultative submission evidence package.']
  aps_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an Attending Physician Statement (APS) was required by the reinsurer as part of the facultative underwriting evidence package for this submission.']
  cession_type varchar [note: 'The reinsurance cession structure requested for this facultative submission, defining how risk and reserves are shared between the ceding company and the reinsurer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this facultative submission record was first created in the reinsurance management system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this facultative submission record (e.g., USD, CAD). Supports multi-currency reinsurance arrangements.']
  decision_date date [note: 'The date on which the reinsurer rendered its underwriting decision on the facultative submission. Used to measure turnaround time and track SLA compliance with reinsurance partners.']
  decision_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason for the reinsurer\'s underwriting decision, particularly for declined or postponed submissions (e.g., cardiovascular risk, BMI, hazardous occupation). Supports experience studies and underwriting analytics.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the facultative reinsurance coverage becomes effective upon acceptance of the submission. Typically aligned with the underlying policy issue date or a negotiated retroactive date.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date by which the ceding company must accept the reinsurer\'s facultative offer before it lapses. After this date, a new submission may be required.']
  face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total face amount (death benefit) of the underlying life insurance policy being submitted for facultative reinsurance. Used to determine the NAR and cession limits.']
  final_disposition varchar [note: 'The ultimate outcome of the facultative submission after all negotiations and decisions are complete. Indicates whether the risk was successfully placed with the reinsurer, partially placed, or not placed.']
  flat_extra_duration_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the flat extra premium applies from the policy issue date. A value of zero or null indicates a permanent flat extra. Used for temporary substandard rating administration.']
  flat_extra_premium decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional flat extra premium per thousand dollars of coverage charged by the reinsurer for temporary or permanent extra mortality risk not captured by table ratings (e.g., hazardous occupation, aviation risk).']
  mib_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Medical Information Bureau (MIB) check was performed as part of the underwriting evidence for this facultative submission. Required for compliance with MIB Group member obligations.']
  nar_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Amount at Risk (NAR) — the difference between the policy death benefit and the policy reserve or account value at the time of submission. The primary basis for facultative reinsurance premium calculation under YRT structures.']
  priority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this facultative submission has been flagged as a priority case requiring expedited review by the reinsurer, typically due to policy issuance deadlines or large face amounts.']
  reinsurance_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annual reinsurance premium amount payable to the reinsurer for the ceded risk under this facultative submission. Calculated from the premium rate applied to the ceded NAR or face amount.']
  reinsurance_premium_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The reinsurance premium rate per thousand dollars of NAR or face amount offered or agreed upon for this facultative submission. Used to calculate the reinsurance premium payable to the reinsurer.']
  reinsurer_reference_number varchar [note: 'The reference number assigned by the reinsurer to this facultative submission in their own system (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS or RGA AURA). Used for cross-system reconciliation and bordereaux reporting.']
  requested_cession_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The face amount or net amount at risk (NAR) the ceding company is requesting to cede to the reinsurer under this facultative submission. Expressed in USD.']
  requested_cession_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'The percentage of the total risk the ceding company is requesting to cede to the reinsurer. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9000 = 90%). Used alongside requested_cession_amount for proportional treaty structures.']
  retention_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of risk the ceding company retains on its own books after ceding the requested portion to the reinsurer. Retention amount plus cession amount should equal total face amount or NAR.']
  retrocession_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this facultative submission involves a retrocession arrangement, where the reinsurer further cedes a portion of the accepted risk to a retrocessionaire.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational source system from which this facultative submission record originated (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS, RGA AURA Reinsurance). Supports data lineage and ETL traceability in the lakehouse.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the facultative submission was transmitted to the reinsurer (e.g., electronic data interchange, reinsurer portal, manual paper submission). Supports operational efficiency analysis.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The business date on which the facultative submission was formally submitted to the reinsurer for individual underwriting review. This is the principal real-world event date for this transaction.']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business reference number assigned to the facultative submission, used in all correspondence with the reinsurer and in bordereaux reporting. Format: FAC-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the facultative submission within the placement workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through reinsurer decision. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|declined|withdrawn|expired — promote to reference product]']
  table_rating varchar [note: 'Substandard mortality table rating assigned by the reinsurer\'s underwriter, indicating the degree of extra mortality risk (e.g., Table B, Table 4). Standard indicates no extra mortality. Used to calculate rated-up reinsurance premiums.']
  underwriting_decision varchar [note: 'The formal underwriting decision rendered by the reinsurer on this facultative submission. Drives the final disposition and any rated terms offered. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: approved_standard|approved_rated|approved_modified|declined|postponed|more_info_required — promote to reference product]']
  underwriting_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing underwriting remarks, special conditions, exclusions, or other qualitative information relevant to the facultative submission and the reinsurer\'s decision. May include medical or financial underwriting commentary.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this facultative submission record. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data processing.']

  Note: 'Tracks individual facultative reinsurance submissions where a specific risk cannot be ceded automatically and requires individual underwriting review by the reinsurer. Captures submission date, risk details, requested cession amount, reinsurer assigned, underwriting decision, rated terms offered, and final disposition. Supports the facultative placement workflow distinct from automatic treaty cessions.'
}

Table reinsurance.premium {
  premium_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each reinsurance premium billing record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  cession_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance cession record that this premium billing is associated with. Links the premium to the specific risk ceded under a treaty or facultative arrangement.']
  coi_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.coi_rate_schedule. Business justification: YRT reinsurance premiums are calculated using COI rates from the product\'s COI rate schedule. The yrt_rate stored on premium is derived from this schedule; the FK enables rate traceability, experience']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying life insurance policy for which reinsurance premium is being billed. Links the reinsurance premium to the direct policy in the Policy Administration System.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Reinsurance premium accounting requires direct linkage to GL posting for monthly close reconciliation, audit trail, GAAP/SAP reporting, and SOX controls. Finance teams reconcile premium bordereaux to ']
  nar_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.nar_calculation. Business justification: YRT reinsurance premiums are directly driven by NAR calculations — the premium amount is computed as NAR × rate per thousand. Linking premium.nar_calculation_id → nar_calculation provides full audit t']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Reinsurance premiums are calculated per product plan. Critical for premium reconciliation, bordereaux preparation, financial reporting, and product profitability analysis. Plan_code is denormalized id']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Reinsurance premiums are calculated using rates from a specific plan version\'s rate tables. Premium reconciliation, experience refund calculations, and bordereaux reporting require knowing which plan ']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Under funds-withheld reinsurance treaties, ceded premiums are retained in the ceding company\'s investment portfolio rather than remitted. Investment accounting requires the portfolio reference on each']
  premium_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_adjustment. Business justification: When a billing premium_adjustment changes the gross premium (e.g., COLA, face change, rate revision), the corresponding reinsurance premium record must be recalculated and linked to the adjustment for']
  premium_rate_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.premium_rate_table. Business justification: Reinsurance premium amounts are derived from the product\'s premium rate table. Linking each reinsurance premium transaction to the rate table used enables rate audit trails, reinsurer rate reconciliat']
  premium_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_schedule. Business justification: Reinsurance premium calculations require premium schedule data for modal factors, billing frequency, and paid-to-date status. Actuaries and reinsurance accountants use this to validate ceded premium a']
  reinsurer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance counterparty (e.g., RGA, Swiss Re, Munich Re) to whom the premium is remitted. Identifies the reinsurer entity in the reinsurer master.']
  treaty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance treaty under which this premium is billed. Identifies the governing treaty agreement (YRT, coinsurance, modified coinsurance, etc.) between the ceding company and the reinsurer.']
  treaty_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.treaty_schedule. Business justification: Reinsurance premiums are calculated using rates defined in the treaty schedule (yrt_rate_per_thousand, flat_extra_premium, coinsurance_percentage). Linking premium.treaty_schedule_id → treaty_schedule']
  value_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.value. Business justification: YRT reinsurance premiums are calculated from NAR, which derives from the policy value record (death benefit minus account value). Premium reconciliation audits and reinsurance billing disputes require']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The monetary adjustment applied to the standard calculated reinsurance premium for this billing period. Positive values indicate additional premium; negative values indicate credits. Null if no adjustment applies.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for any adjustment to the reinsurance premium from the standard calculated amount. Captures retroactive corrections, rate table updates, NAR recalculations, or treaty amendments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rate_correction|nar_correction|retroactive_adjustment|treaty_amendment|data_correction|none — promote to reference product if additional reasons are needed]']
  allowance_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The ceding allowance or expense allowance credited back to the ceding company by the reinsurer, representing a portion of the gross premium returned to offset acquisition and administrative costs.']
  billing_mode varchar [note: 'The frequency at which reinsurance premiums are billed to the ceding company under the treaty terms. Determines the billing cycle and modal factor applied.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the billing period for which this reinsurance premium is charged. Defines the end of the coverage interval for the ceded risk in this billing cycle.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the billing period for which this reinsurance premium is charged. Defines the start of the coverage interval for the ceded risk in this billing cycle.']
  bordereaux_period varchar [note: 'The YYYY-MM formatted reporting period for which this premium is included in the bordereaux report submitted to the reinsurer. Supports monthly bordereaux reconciliation and reinsurer reporting obligations.']
  ceded_face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the policy face amount (death benefit) ceded to the reinsurer under this cession. Used in coinsurance arrangements and for bordereaux reporting of ceded exposure.']
  ceded_nar decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Net Amount at Risk (NAR) ceded to the reinsurer as of the billing date. Represents the reinsurer\'s exposure — the difference between the ceded death benefit and the ceded reserve or account value. Used as the basis for YRT premium calculation.']
  ceded_reserve_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actuarial reserve amount ceded to the reinsurer as of the billing period. Relevant for coinsurance and modified coinsurance (ModCo) treaties where reserves are shared. Supports RBC optimization and statutory reporting.']
  coinsurance_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of the risk ceded to the reinsurer under a coinsurance or modified coinsurance treaty. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9000 = 90%). Used to calculate the proportional share of premiums and reserves ceded.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this reinsurance premium record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this reinsurance premium record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency treaty arrangements.']
  due_date date [note: 'The contractual date by which the reinsurance premium must be remitted to the reinsurer per treaty terms. Used for overdue tracking and cash flow management.']
  experience_refund_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The experience refund or profit commission amount credited back to the ceding company based on favorable claims experience under the treaty. Applicable to treaties with experience refund provisions. Null if no experience refund applies for this period.']
  gross_premium_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total gross reinsurance premium due for the billing period before any allowances, credits, or adjustments. Represents the full ceded premium obligation to the reinsurer.']
  insured_attained_age varchar [note: 'The attained age of the insured as of the billing period start date. Used to look up the applicable YRT rate from the treaty rate table and for actuarial experience studies.']
  insured_gender varchar [note: 'The gender of the insured used for YRT rate table lookup. Unisex rates apply in jurisdictions or products where gender-distinct pricing is not permitted.']
  is_retrocession boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reinsurance premium relates to a retrocession arrangement (where the reinsurer further cedes risk to another reinsurer). True = retrocession; False = direct reinsurance cession.']
  modal_factor decimal(8,6) [note: 'The modal factor applied to convert the annual reinsurance premium to the billing frequency (e.g., monthly = 1/12 = 0.083333, quarterly = 0.25, semi-annual = 0.5, annual = 1.0). Reflects the billing mode agreed in the treaty.']
  net_premium_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net reinsurance premium payable to the reinsurer after deducting the ceding allowance from the gross premium. This is the actual cash amount remitted. Calculated as gross_premium_amount minus allowance_amount.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the reinsurance premium was remitted to the reinsurer. Null if payment has not yet been made. Used for reconciliation and bordereaux reporting.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reinsurance premium billing record. Tracks whether the premium is pending remittance, has been paid, is overdue, has been waived, reversed due to correction, or is under dispute with the reinsurer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|paid|overdue|waived|reversed|disputed — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]']
  policy_year varchar [note: 'The policy year in force at the time of this reinsurance premium billing. Used for YRT rate table lookup (rates often vary by policy duration) and for experience studies.']
  premium_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this reinsurance premium billing record, as assigned by the Reinsurance Management System (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS or RGA AURA). Used in bordereaux reporting and reinsurer remittance advice.']
  reinsurance_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year of the reinsurance premium billing. Used for annual bordereaux aggregation, statutory reporting, and reinsurance accounting period alignment.']
  reinsurer_policy_number varchar [note: 'The policy or certificate number assigned by the reinsurer to identify the ceded risk in their system. Used for bordereaux reconciliation and reinsurer correspondence.']
  remittance_reference varchar [note: 'The payment or wire transfer reference number associated with the actual remittance of this reinsurance premium to the reinsurer. Used for cash reconciliation and bordereaux matching.']
  risk_class varchar [note: 'The underwriting risk classification of the insured as applied to the reinsurance treaty rate table (e.g., preferred_plus, preferred, standard, substandard, table_rated). Determines the YRT rate applied. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: preferred_plus|preferred|standard|substandard|table_rated|smoker|non_smoker — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational source system from which this reinsurance premium record was extracted (e.g., SWISS_RE_IRIS, RGA_AURA). Supports data lineage and ETL reconciliation in the lakehouse Silver layer.']
  tax_withholding_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The amount of tax withheld from the reinsurance premium remittance, applicable for cross-border reinsurance arrangements subject to withholding tax treaties. Null for domestic arrangements with no withholding obligation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this reinsurance premium record was last modified. Supports audit trail, change tracking, and Silver layer incremental processing.']
  yrt_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The Yearly Renewable Term (YRT) rate per unit of Net Amount at Risk (NAR) applied to calculate the reinsurance premium for the billing period. Expressed as a rate per $1,000 of NAR. Sourced from the treaty rate table based on attained age, gender, and risk class.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of reinsurance premiums due and paid to reinsurers for ceded risk. Captures billing period, premium amount by cession, YRT rate applied, modal factor, ceded NAR at billing date, payment due date, payment status, and remittance reference. Supports bordereaux reporting and reinsurance premium accounting. Distinct from the billing domain which owns direct policyholder premiums.'
}

Table reinsurance.claim_recoverable {
  claim_recoverable_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each reinsurance claim recoverable record. Primary key for the claim_recoverable data product.']
  benefit_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.benefit_payment. Business justification: A benefit_payment (death benefit disbursement on annuity) directly triggers a reinsurance claim_recoverable. Linking claim_recoverable to the specific benefit_payment provides end-to-end traceability ']
  bordereaux_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.bordereaux. Business justification: Claim recoverables are formally submitted to reinsurers via bordereaux. Linking claim_recoverable to the specific bordereaux in which it was reported enables reinsurance accounting reconciliation of o']
  cession_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific cession record that governs the portion of risk ceded to the reinsurer for the underlying policy, linking the recoverable to the cession processing module.']
  claim_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating claim record in the Claims Management System (e.g., Guidewire ClaimCenter) from which this reinsurance recoverable arises. Links the recoverable to the underlying insured event.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Death benefit claims on annuity contracts with GMDB riders generate reinsurance claim recoverables. Current claim_recoverable only references in_force_policy_id. This FK enables annuity death claim re']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying life insurance policy associated with the ceded claim, enabling policy-level recoverable aggregation and RBC reporting.']
  insurance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Claim recoverable submissions to reinsurers require formal claim documentation (death certificates, proof of loss, claim forms). Linking recoverable to its submission document supports reinsurer dispu']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Claim recoverable booking must link to GL entry for balance sheet reconciliation, reserve adequacy testing, and external audit. Actuarial and finance teams reconcile recoverable balances to journal en']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Claim recoverables recognition is governed by specific regulatory obligations (e.g., credit for reinsurance rules under NAIC model law, RBC recoverable credit requirements). Statutory accountants must']
  reinsurance_recoverable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.reinsurance_recoverable. Business justification: Claim recoverable records are the operational source of the reinsurance recoverable balance sheet asset. Finance teams reconcile claim_recoverable.recoverable_amount against finance.reinsurance_recove']
  reinsurer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurer counterparty (e.g., RGA, Swiss Re) responsible for settling this recoverable. Used for counterparty exposure tracking and dispute management.']
  reserve_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.reserve_calculation. Business justification: Claim recoverables affect reserve positions and must be tracked at calculation level for reserve adequacy testing, statutory reporting (Schedule S Part 3), and RBC credit calculations.']
  treaty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance treaty under which this recoverable is ceded. Determines the applicable cession terms, retention limits, and recoverable percentage.']
  aging_bucket varchar [note: 'Aging classification of the outstanding recoverable balance based on days elapsed since the due_date. Used for statutory balance sheet aging schedules and reinsurer credit risk monitoring per NAIC Schedule F reporting.']
  allowance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Expense allowance or ceding commission received from the reinsurer as part of the coinsurance or modified coinsurance arrangement, offsetting the ceding company\'s acquisition and maintenance costs.']
  allowance_for_doubtful_recovery decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reserve amount established against this recoverable to reflect the risk that the reinsurer may not pay in full, particularly for disputed or aged recoverables. Impacts statutory surplus and RBC calculations per NAIC Schedule F.']
  amount_received decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual cash amount received from the reinsurer to date for this recoverable. May differ from recoverable_amount due to partial payments, disputes, or negotiated settlements.']
  bordereaux_period varchar [note: 'The reporting period (YYYY-MM) in which this recoverable is included in the bordereaux report submitted to the reinsurer. Aligns with monthly cession and recoverable reporting cycles.']
  ceded_claim_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the gross claim amount ceded to the reinsurer under the applicable treaty, representing the maximum recoverable before any adjustments or disputes.']
  claim_event_date date [note: 'Date of the insured event (e.g., date of death, date of disability onset) that triggered the underlying claim. The principal real-world business event timestamp for this recoverable.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the underlying claim driving the recoverable. Includes Death Benefit (DB), Accidental Death Benefit (ADB), Long-Term Care (LTC), disability, maturity, surrender, and annuity payout claims. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: death_benefit|adb|ltc|disability|maturity|surrender|annuity_payout — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reinsurance recoverable record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for SOX and data governance compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this recoverable record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency reinsurance treaty arrangements.']
  dispute_open_date date [note: 'Date on which the reinsurer formally raised the dispute. Starts the dispute resolution clock and triggers legal/reinsurance operations oversight. Null when is_disputed is False.']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the dispute was formally resolved and closed. Null if the dispute is still open. Used to measure dispute cycle time and reinsurer relationship performance.']
  dispute_resolution_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the dispute was or is being resolved. Negotiation = bilateral agreement; arbitration = binding third-party decision per treaty arbitration clause; litigation = court proceedings; mediation = non-binding facilitated resolution; withdrawal = reinsurer withdrew the dispute. Null when is_disputed is False.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the dispute lifecycle. \'open\' = newly raised; \'under_review\' = being evaluated; \'escalated\' = referred to legal or senior management; \'resolved\' = agreed outcome reached; \'withdrawn\' = reinsurer retracted the dispute. Null when is_disputed is False.']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of the reinsurer\'s dispute. Coverage interpretation disputes involve treaty scope; treaty terms disputes involve contractual language; claim validity disputes challenge the underlying claim; documentation disputes involve missing or insufficient evidence; calculation disputes involve amount computation. Null when is_disputed is False.']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The specific monetary amount contested by the reinsurer. May equal the full recoverable_amount or a partial portion. Null when is_disputed is False. Critical for legal reserve estimation.']
  due_date date [note: 'Contractual date by which the reinsurer is obligated to remit payment per the treaty terms. Used for aging bucket analysis and escalation of overdue recoverables.']
  gross_claim_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross claim payment made by Life Insurance to the claimant before any reinsurance recovery. Represents the full liability settled on the underlying policy.']
  is_disputed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reinsurer has formally disputed this recoverable. True when the recoverable_status is \'disputed\' or a dispute record is active. Drives legal and reinsurance operations workflow.']
  is_facultative boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recoverable is associated with a facultative (individually negotiated) reinsurance arrangement rather than an automatic treaty. Facultative recoverables require additional documentation and underwriting evidence.']
  is_retrocession boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recoverable arises from a retrocession arrangement, where the reinsurer has itself ceded a portion of the risk to another reinsurer. True for retrocession recoverables; False for direct treaty recoverables.']
  nar_at_cession decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Amount at Risk (NAR) on the underlying policy at the time of cession, representing the death benefit minus the policy account value. Used in YRT premium calculations and RBC optimization analysis.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on the recoverable, calculated as recoverable_amount minus amount_received. Drives statutory balance sheet reporting and RBC optimization analysis.']
  recoverable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated amount Life Insurance expects to recover from the reinsurer, derived from the ceded claim amount multiplied by the recoverable percentage. This is the primary balance sheet asset for statutory reporting.']
  recoverable_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The contractual percentage of the ceded claim amount recoverable from the reinsurer as defined in the treaty terms. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9000 = 90%). Used in NAR-based cession calculations.']
  recoverable_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier assigned to this reinsurance recoverable record, used in bordereaux reporting and reinsurer correspondence. Sourced from the Reinsurance Management System (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS, RGA AURA Reinsurance).']
  recoverable_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reinsurance recoverable. \'pending\' = identified but not yet submitted; \'submitted\' = sent to reinsurer awaiting payment; \'received\' = payment collected; \'disputed\' = reinsurer has contested the recoverable; \'closed\' = fully resolved; \'waived\' = recoverable forgone.']
  reinsurance_premium_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total reinsurance premium paid to the reinsurer for the ceded risk on the underlying policy up to the claim event date. Used in net cost-of-reinsurance analysis and profitability reporting.']
  reinsurer_response varchar [note: 'Free-text summary of the reinsurer\'s formal response or position on the recoverable or dispute, as communicated in writing. Captures treaty interpretation arguments, documentation requests, or payment confirmations.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the reinsurer completed payment of the recoverable amount. Null if the recoverable has not yet been settled. Used for cash flow and aging analysis.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational source system from which this recoverable record was extracted (e.g., SWISS_RE_IRIS, RGA_AURA). Supports data lineage and ETL audit traceability in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the recoverable claim was formally submitted to the reinsurer for payment. Triggers the reinsurer\'s response and payment obligation timeline per treaty terms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this reinsurance recoverable record. Tracks the last modification for change data capture and audit trail purposes in the Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Tracks reinsurance recoverables arising from ceded claims where Life Insurance is entitled to recover a portion of claim payments from reinsurers, including full dispute lifecycle management for contested recoverables, premium calculations, and treaty interpretations. Captures originating claim reference, ceded claim amount, recoverable percentage per treaty, recoverable status (pending, submitted, received, disputed), submission date, settlement date, and dispute details (dispute type, disputed amount, dispute open date, resolution method, resolution date, dispute status, reinsurer response). This is the single source of truth for all reinsurance recoverables and reinsurance disputes. Critical for RBC optimization, statutory balance sheet reporting, and reinsurance operations/legal oversight of counterparty disputes.'
}

Table reinsurance.bordereaux {
  bordereaux_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each bordereaux submission record in the reinsurance management system. Primary key for this entity.']
  insurance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Bordereaux are formal reporting documents submitted to reinsurers with supporting schedules, reconciliation reports, and reinsurer acknowledgment letters. Treaty administration and settlement processe']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Bordereaux settlement accounting requires GL posting reference for monthly close, reinsurer reconciliation, and cash flow tracking. Finance teams post bordereaux net settlements to GL and reconcile to']
  original_bordereaux_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original bordereaux record when this record represents a corrected resubmission. Null for original submissions. Enables audit trail linking between original and corrected bordereaux versions.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Bordereaux data feeds statutory annual statement filings, particularly Schedule S reporting of reinsurance ceded. Direct link required for audit trail, filing reconciliation, and regulatory examinatio']
  reinsurer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurer counterparty (e.g., RGA, Swiss Re, Munich Re) to whom this bordereaux is submitted. Links to the reinsurer party master record.']
  treaty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance treaty under which this bordereaux is submitted. Links to the treaty master record governing the cession terms (YRT, coinsurance, modified coinsurance, etc.).']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: Bordereaux submissions provide reinsurance data inputs for actuarial valuation runs. Critical for quarterly valuation data reconciliation, reserve calculation validation, and statutory filing preparat']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the bordereaux was formally approved by the authorized approver prior to submission to the reinsurer. Part of the internal control workflow for reinsurance reporting.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the authorized individual who approved this bordereaux submission prior to transmission to the reinsurer. Required for internal control and SOX compliance in the reinsurance reporting process.']
  bordereaux_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to the bordereaux submission, used for tracking and reconciliation with the reinsurer (e.g., Swiss Re, RGA). Corresponds to the submission reference in Swiss Re IRIS and RGA AURA bordereaux reporting modules.']
  bordereaux_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bordereaux submission indicating the category of reinsurance activity reported. Premium bordereaux report ceded premiums; claims bordereaux report reinsurance recoverables; in-force bordereaux report active ceded policies; lapse bordereaux report terminated cessions; new business bordereaux report newly ceded policies.']
  cession_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the cessions reported in this bordereaux are automatic (ceded under treaty automatic binding limits), facultative (individually underwritten and negotiated), or retrocession (reinsurer ceding risk to another reinsurer). Drives different processing workflows in the reinsurance management system.']
  claim_count varchar [note: 'The total number of reinsurance claim recoverable transactions included in this bordereaux submission for the reporting period. Applicable primarily to claims bordereaux type submissions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this bordereaux record was first created in the reinsurance management system. Provides the audit trail creation point for data governance and lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts reported in this bordereaux (e.g., USD for US Dollar). All ceded face amounts, reinsurance premiums, and recoverables are denominated in this currency.']
  due_date date [note: 'The contractually required deadline by which the bordereaux must be submitted to the reinsurer as specified in the reinsurance treaty terms. Used for monitoring compliance with reporting obligations.']
  is_retrocession boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bordereaux covers retrocession arrangements where the reinsurer is itself ceding risk to another reinsurer. True for retrocession bordereaux; False for standard cession bordereaux.']
  net_settlement_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'The net financial settlement amount for this bordereaux period, calculated as total reinsurance premium minus total recoverables minus total allowance amount. A positive value indicates a net payment due to the reinsurer; a negative value indicates a net recovery due to the ceding company.']
  new_cession_count varchar [note: 'The number of newly ceded policies included in this bordereaux submission during the reporting period. Represents new business (NB) ceded to the reinsurer and is used for new business value (NBV) analysis and treaty capacity monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional commentary, exceptions, or explanatory notes related to this bordereaux submission. Used by the reinsurance operations team to document special circumstances, adjustments, or communications with the reinsurer.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'The insurance product line classification for the policies covered in this bordereaux. Identifies whether the ceded business is term life, whole life (WL), universal life (UL), variable life (VUL/IUL), annuity, disability income (DI), or long-term care (LTC). Used for treaty-level product segmentation and experience analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: term_life|whole_life|universal_life|variable_life|annuity|disability_income|long_term_care — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reinsurer_acknowledgment_date date [note: 'The date on which the reinsurer formally acknowledged receipt of the bordereaux submission. Used to confirm delivery and initiate the reinsurer review and settlement process.']
  reinsurer_treaty_reference varchar [note: 'The reinsurer\'s own internal reference code or identifier for the treaty, as used in the reinsurer\'s system (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS treaty code or RGA AURA treaty identifier). Required for cross-system reconciliation and bordereaux matching at the reinsurer.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'The contractually agreed frequency at which bordereaux submissions are made to the reinsurer under the treaty terms. Determines the reporting period duration and submission schedule.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the reporting period covered by this bordereaux submission. Together with reporting_period_start_date, defines the complete activity window for cession, premium, and claims reporting.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the reporting period covered by this bordereaux submission. Defines the beginning of the activity window for which cessions, premiums, and claims are being reported to the reinsurer.']
  resubmission_number varchar [note: 'Sequential counter indicating how many times this bordereaux has been resubmitted to the reinsurer after an initial rejection or correction. A value of 0 indicates the original submission; values greater than 0 indicate corrected resubmissions.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the net financial settlement for this bordereaux was completed. Compared against settlement_due_date to identify late settlements and treaty compliance issues.']
  settlement_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the net financial settlement for this bordereaux must be completed between the ceding company and the reinsurer. Determined by treaty payment terms and used for cash flow management and accounts receivable/payable tracking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this bordereaux data was originated or extracted. Identifies whether the data came from Swiss Re IRIS, RGA AURA Reinsurance, the Policy Administration System, or was manually prepared. Used for data lineage and ETL audit purposes.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the bordereaux was formally submitted to the reinsurer. Used for tracking timeliness of reporting obligations and treaty compliance deadlines.']
  submission_format varchar [note: 'The file or data format in which the bordereaux was submitted to the reinsurer. ACORD XML is the industry standard; CSV and Excel are common for legacy systems; EDI is used for electronic data interchange with certain reinsurers.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'The channel or method used to transmit the bordereaux to the reinsurer. Electronic submissions via SFTP or API are standard for Swiss Re IRIS and RGA AURA integrations; portal submissions use the reinsurer\'s web portal; email is used for legacy or manual submissions.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the bordereaux submission in the workflow. Draft indicates preparation in progress; submitted indicates sent to reinsurer; acknowledged indicates receipt confirmed; accepted indicates reinsurer validated and approved; rejected indicates reinsurer identified discrepancies; under_review indicates reinsurer is processing.']
  terminated_cession_count varchar [note: 'The number of cessions terminated during the reporting period due to policy lapses, surrenders, deaths, or other termination events. Used for in-force reconciliation and treaty run-off analysis.']
  total_allowance_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'The aggregate expense allowance or ceding commission paid by the reinsurer to the ceding company for the business ceded in this bordereaux period. Offsets the ceding company\'s acquisition and maintenance costs under coinsurance and modified coinsurance treaties.']
  total_ceded_face_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'The aggregate face amount (death benefit) of all policies ceded to the reinsurer within the reporting period covered by this bordereaux. Represents the total net amount at risk (NAR) transferred to the reinsurer. Used for RBC optimization analysis and treaty capacity monitoring.']
  total_ceded_reserve decimal(20,2) [note: 'The aggregate actuarial reserve ceded to the reinsurer for all in-force cessions reported in this bordereaux. Relevant for coinsurance and modified coinsurance treaties where reserve credit is taken. Supports PBR compliance and RBC calculations.']
  total_cession_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual policy cession records included in this bordereaux submission for the reporting period. Used for reconciliation and completeness validation against the reinsurance management system cession records.']
  total_recoverables decimal(20,2) [note: 'The aggregate amount recoverable from the reinsurer for claims paid on ceded policies during the reporting period. Represents the reinsurer\'s share of claim settlements and is a key asset on the ceding company\'s balance sheet under SAP and GAAP.']
  total_reinsurance_premium decimal(20,2) [note: 'The aggregate reinsurance premium payable to the reinsurer for all cessions reported in this bordereaux during the reporting period. Represents the cost of risk transfer and is a key financial settlement figure in the bordereaux.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this bordereaux record was most recently modified in the reinsurance management system. Used for change tracking, data reconciliation, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The actuarial valuation date as of which reserves, net amount at risk (NAR), and in-force amounts are calculated for this bordereaux. Typically the last day of the reporting period. Aligns with actuarial valuation system (AXIS, MoSes, Prophet) run dates.']

  Note: 'Periodic summary reporting package submitted to reinsurers detailing all cessions, premiums, and claims activity within a reporting period. Captures bordereaux type (premium, claims, in-force), reporting period, reinsurer, treaty reference, submission date, total ceded face amount, total reinsurance premium, total recoverables, and submission status. Supports Swiss Re IRIS and RGA AURA bordereaux reporting module requirements.'
}

Table reinsurance.bordereaux_line {
  bordereaux_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual line item within a bordereaux submission. Serves as the primary key for this record in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  billing_reinstatement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_reinstatement. Business justification: Reinstated policies must be reported in the bordereaux as reactivated cessions. Each bordereaux_line for a reinstated policy references the billing_reinstatement to confirm back-premium collected and ']
  bordereaux_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent bordereaux submission header that this line belongs to. Establishes the header/detail relationship for reconciliation and reporting.']
  cession_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual cession record associated with this bordereaux line, linking to the specific risk ceded to the reinsurer under the applicable treaty.']
  claim_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated claim record in the Claims Management System when this bordereaux line represents a claim recoverable. Links the recoverable to the adjudicated claim for reconciliation.']
  claim_recoverable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.claim_recoverable. Business justification: bordereaux_line.claim_recoverable_amount is a snapshot value on a bordereaux line that represents a claim recoverable. Adding claim_recoverable_id creates a direct FK to the authoritative claim_recove']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Bordereaux lines report individual cession details to reinsurers. For annuity reinsurance, each line represents one annuity contract. The existing policy_number plain column is a denormalized referenc']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.in_force_policy. Business justification: Bordereaux lines report individual policy-level cession data to reinsurers. Direct FK to in_force_policy enables policy-level bordereaux reconciliation and regulatory reporting without traversing the ']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: Bordereaux lines report individual cession details to reinsurers; each line represents a specific insured\'s risk data. A direct FK to insured enables reconciliation between bordereaux submissions and ']
  original_line_bordereaux_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original bordereaux line that this record adjusts or corrects, populated only when adjustment_indicator is True. Enables audit trail for retroactive corrections.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Each bordereaux line reports a cession for a specific product plan. Required for bordereaux validation, reinsurer reporting, and product-level reconciliation. Plan_code is denormalized identifier; FK ']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Bordereaux lines report cession details at the plan version level — reinsurers require plan version information for rate verification and experience analysis. Regulatory bordereaux submissions and rei']
  premium_bill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_bill. Business justification: Bordereaux reporting to reinsurers includes premium billing status to validate cession eligibility and confirm premium payment. Reinsurance accountants reconcile bordereaux lines to specific bills to ']
  premium_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.premium. Business justification: bordereaux_line represents individual line items in a bordereaux submission, which include premium lines. bordereaux_line already has premium_bill_id (cross-domain to billing) but lacks a direct FK to']
  reinsurer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance counterparty (e.g., RGA, Swiss Re) to whom this risk or recoverable is reported on this bordereaux line.']
  treaty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance treaty under which this cession or claim recoverable is reported. Links the line to the governing treaty terms including YRT or coinsurance arrangements.']
  adjustment_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bordereaux line is an adjustment or correction to a previously submitted line. True when the line represents a retroactive correction to prior period amounts.']
  allowance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Ceding allowance or expense allowance paid by the reinsurer to the ceding company on this line, representing the reinsurer\'s contribution toward acquisition and maintenance expenses under coinsurance treaties.']
  attained_age varchar [note: 'Age of the insured at the time of the reporting period, used for YRT rate table lookups and NAR-based premium calculations. Typically calculated as age nearest or age last birthday per treaty terms.']
  ceded_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the policy face amount ceded to the reinsurer under the applicable treaty. Represents the reinsurer\'s share of the death benefit risk for this line.']
  claim_recoverable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount recoverable from the reinsurer for a paid or pending claim on this bordereaux line, representing the reinsurer\'s proportional share of the claim payment based on the ceded amount.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Type of insurance claim associated with this bordereaux line when reporting claim recoverables. Distinguishes death claims, disability income (DI) claims, waiver of premium, critical illness, and Accidental Death Benefit (ADB) claims.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bordereaux line record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and reconciliation processes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this bordereaux line (e.g., USD). Required for multi-currency treaty arrangements and financial reporting.']
  face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total face amount (death benefit) of the underlying life insurance policy. Represents the gross insurance in force before any reinsurance cession.']
  flat_extra_premium decimal(10,4) [note: 'Additional per-thousand flat extra premium charged for temporary or permanent extra mortality risk not captured by table ratings (e.g., hazardous occupation, aviation). Applied in reinsurance premium calculations.']
  line_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number of this record within the parent bordereaux submission, used for ordering, reconciliation, and identifying specific lines during dispute resolution.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of this bordereaux line item within the reinsurance settlement workflow. Tracks the lifecycle from initial submission through reinsurer acceptance, dispute resolution, or voiding.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of this bordereaux line item, distinguishing between reinsurance premium cessions, claim recoverables, reserve credits, experience refunds, and allowances. Drives downstream accounting and reconciliation logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: premium_cession|claim_recoverable|reserve_credit|experience_refund|allowance|retrocession_cession — promote to reference product]']
  mib_code varchar [note: 'Medical Information Bureau (MIB) code associated with the insured\'s underwriting file, if applicable. Used by reinsurers to verify underwriting integrity and detect potential anti-selection.']
  nar_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Amount at Risk representing the reinsurer\'s exposure on this cession, calculated as the ceded death benefit less the policy\'s reserve or account value. Drives YRT reinsurance premium calculations.']
  policy_duration_years varchar [note: 'Number of complete policy years elapsed since the policy issue date as of the reporting period. Used in select-and-ultimate mortality table lookups and experience studies.']
  policy_issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the underlying life insurance policy was originally issued. Used to determine policy duration, treaty applicability, and contestability period status.']
  reinsurance_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reinsurance premium due to the reinsurer for this bordereaux line for the reporting period. Calculated based on NAR, treaty rates, and risk class for YRT treaties, or as a proportional share for coinsurance treaties.']
  reinsurer_line_reference varchar [note: 'The reinsurer\'s own reference number or identifier for this bordereaux line as assigned by the reinsurer (e.g., RGA or Swiss Re internal reference). Used for cross-party reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the bordereaux reporting period covered by this line item. Defines the close of the interval for which premiums, recoverables, or other amounts are being reported.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the bordereaux reporting period covered by this line item. Defines the beginning of the interval for which premiums, recoverables, or other amounts are being reported.']
  retrocession_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bordereaux line relates to a retrocession arrangement, where the reinsurer has further ceded a portion of the risk to a retrocessionaire.']
  risk_class varchar [note: 'Underwriting risk classification assigned to the insured at policy issue, reflecting mortality risk level. Drives reinsurance premium rates and NAR-based cession calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: preferred_plus|preferred|standard_plus|standard|substandard|rated|uninsurable — promote to reference product]']
  smoker_status varchar [note: 'Tobacco use status of the insured as determined at underwriting. A key mortality risk factor that affects reinsurance premium rates and risk classification under YRT and coinsurance treaties.']
  state_of_issue varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code where the underlying policy was issued. Relevant for state-specific regulatory compliance, reserve requirements, and treaty applicability by jurisdiction.']
  table_rating varchar [note: 'Substandard mortality table rating applied to the insured (e.g., Table B, Table D, 150%), indicating the degree of extra mortality risk above standard. Affects reinsurance premium rates for rated policies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bordereaux line record was last modified in the Silver Layer data product, capturing status changes, adjustments, or dispute resolutions.']

  Note: 'Individual line-item detail within a bordereaux submission, representing one cession or claim recoverable record included in the periodic report. Captures policy reference, insured details, ceded amount, premium or recoverable amount, risk class, plan code, and line status. Enables granular reconciliation between bordereaux submissions and individual cession and claim recoverable records.'
}

Table reinsurance.nar_calculation {
  nar_calculation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each Net Amount at Risk (NAR) calculation record in the reinsurance domain. Primary key for the nar_calculation data product.']
  account_value_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.account_value. Business justification: NAR = death_benefit - account_value. The nar_calculation stores account_value_amount as a denormalized plain column. Replacing with FK to account_value provides audit traceability to the exact valuati']
  benefit_base_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.benefit_base. Business justification: For GMDB/GMWB riders, NAR is calculated against the benefit base (not just account value). Linking nar_calculation to benefit_base identifies which guaranteed benefit base was used, enabling accurate ']
  cession_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific reinsurance cession record under which this NAR calculation is performed. Links to the cession record in the Reinsurance Management System.']
  coi_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.coi_rate_schedule. Business justification: NAR calculations use COI rates from the product\'s COI rate schedule to determine the net amount at risk for reinsurance purposes. The coi_rate_per_thousand on nar_calculation is sourced from this sche']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: NAR for annuity reinsurance (death benefit minus account value) must be calculated per annuity contract. Current nar_calculation only references in_force_policy_id. This FK enables annuity-specific NA']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ceded life insurance policy for which the NAR is being calculated. Links to the policy master record in the Policy Administration System.']
  mortality_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: NAR calculation applies mortality table COI rates to determine net amount at risk (death benefit minus reserve). rate_table_version is a denormalized reference to the mortality table version used. A r']
  premium_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_schedule. Business justification: NAR calculations require premium schedule data including modal premium amount, billing frequency, and paid-to-date status to compute net amount at risk accurately. Actuaries use this for treaty settle']
  prior_nar_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous NAR calculation record for the same policy and cession, enabling version chaining for corrections and restatements. Null for the initial calculation.']
  reinsurer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.reinsurer. Business justification: NAR calculations are performed for specific reinsurers under treaty arrangements. The table currently has \'reinsurer_code\' STRING attribute which is a denormalized reference. Adding reinsurer_id FK pr']
  reserve_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.reserve_calculation. Business justification: NAR calculations feed directly into reserve calculations for reinsurance accounting, premium billing, and statutory reporting. NAR is the basis for YRT premium calculation and reserve credit.']
  rider_benefit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.rider_benefit. Business justification: NAR calculations for annuity reinsurance are rider-specific (GMDB NAR differs from GMWB NAR on the same contract). Linking nar_calculation to rider_benefit identifies which rider\'s risk is being measu']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: For variable life and VUL policies, the net amount at risk equals death benefit minus separate account value. The NAR calculation must reference the specific separate account to retrieve current fund ']
  treaty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance treaty (YRT or coinsurance) governing the cession for which NAR is calculated. Determines the applicable COI rates and premium computation rules.']
  treaty_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.treaty_schedule. Business justification: NAR calculations use treaty schedule parameters (yrt_rate_per_thousand, retention_limit, coinsurance_percentage, attained_age_basis) to compute the net amount at risk for each ceded policy. nar_calcul']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: NAR calculations are produced within specific actuarial valuation runs. Reserve reconciliation, LDTI/IFRS17 reporting, and RBC calculations require tracing NAR amounts to the valuation run that genera']
  value_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.value. Business justification: NAR calculations use the specific policy value snapshot (account value, death benefit) as direct actuarial input. Actuarial audit and reinsurance premium reconciliation require tracing which exact val']
  billing_period_type varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the reinsurance premium is billed to the reinsurer for this cession. Determines the proration of the annual NAR-based premium across billing cycles.']
  bordereaux_period varchar [note: 'The YYYY-MM formatted reporting period for which this NAR calculation is included in the bordereaux report submitted to the reinsurer. Used for reinsurance premium reconciliation and treaty compliance reporting.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'The accounting basis under which the NAR calculation is performed. Statutory (SAP) basis is used for regulatory reporting; GAAP basis for financial reporting under FASB ASC 944; IFRS basis for international reporting.']
  calculation_date date [note: 'The specific date on which the Net Amount at Risk was calculated for the ceded policy. Represents the principal real-world event date for this calculation record.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'The actuarial method used to compute the Net Amount at Risk. Determines how the death benefit and reserve or account value are combined to derive NAR. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_amount_at_risk_standard|interpolated_reserve|account_value_offset|modified_coinsurance|coinsurance — promote to reference product]']
  calculation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing any exceptions, manual overrides, or special circumstances associated with this NAR calculation, such as facultative underwriting adjustments or treaty amendment impacts.']
  calculation_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this NAR calculation record, used in bordereaux reporting and reinsurance premium reconciliation with reinsurance partners such as RGA and Swiss Re.']
  calculation_run_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the NAR calculation was produced as part of a scheduled periodic run, an ad hoc request, a correction to a prior calculation, or a restatement for prior-period adjustments.']
  calculation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the NAR calculation record. Indicates whether the calculation is pending review, completed and approved, revised due to data corrections, voided, or in error state.']
  ceded_death_benefit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the gross death benefit ceded to the reinsurer under the treaty. May differ from the gross death benefit when only a portion of the risk is ceded (e.g., automatic cession limits apply).']
  coi_rate_per_thousand decimal(10,6) [note: 'The YRT cost of insurance (COI) rate per $1,000 of Net Amount at Risk, sourced from the applicable treaty rate table based on attained age, gender, risk class, smoker status, and policy year. Directly used to compute the reinsurance premium.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this NAR calculation record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this NAR calculation record (e.g., USD for US Dollar). Supports multi-currency reinsurance treaties.']
  death_benefit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross death benefit (DB) payable under the ceded policy as of the calculation date. Represents the total face amount including any riders such as Accidental Death Benefit (ADB) that are included in the cession.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The start date of the period for which this NAR calculation is effective. Used to determine the applicable YRT reinsurance premium billing period.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The end date of the period for which this NAR calculation is effective. Defines the close of the billing period for YRT reinsurance premium computation.']
  insured_age_at_calculation varchar [note: 'The attained age of the insured as of the calculation date. Used in YRT rate table lookups to determine the applicable cost of insurance (COI) rate per unit of NAR for reinsurance premium billing.']
  insured_gender varchar [note: 'The gender of the insured as used in the YRT rate table lookup. Unisex rates apply where state regulations prohibit gender-distinct pricing.']
  is_retrocession boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NAR calculation pertains to a retrocession arrangement (i.e., the company is acting as a reinsurer ceding risk to a retrocessionaire). Distinguishes direct cession NAR from retrocession NAR in reporting.']
  nar_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The computed Net Amount at Risk (NAR), representing the difference between the ceded death benefit and the policy reserve or account value. This is the fundamental driver of YRT cost of insurance (COI) charges and reinsurance premium computation.']
  policy_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actuarial reserve held for the ceded policy as of the valuation date. Used in the NAR calculation as the offset against the death benefit for traditional life products. Derived from the Actuarial Valuation System (e.g., AXIS, Prophet).']
  policy_year varchar [note: 'The policy year in force as of the calculation date. Used in YRT rate table lookups where rates vary by policy duration, and for actuarial experience studies.']
  product_type_code varchar [note: 'The type of life insurance product for the ceded policy. Determines the applicable NAR calculation method (e.g., account value offset for UL/IUL/VUL vs. interpolated reserve for WL/Term). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wl|ul|iul|vul|term|yrt|ltc|di — promote to reference product]']
  rbc_credit_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reinsurance cession associated with this NAR calculation qualifies for Risk-Based Capital (RBC) credit under NAIC RBC rules. Supports RBC optimization reporting.']
  reinsurance_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The reinsurance premium payable to the reinsurer for the effective period, computed as (NAR / 1,000) × COI rate per thousand. This is the primary output of the NAR calculation used in YRT premium billing and bordereaux reporting.']
  reinsurance_treaty_type varchar [note: 'The type of reinsurance treaty under which the NAR is calculated. YRT (Yearly Renewable Term) treaties use NAR directly for premium billing; coinsurance and modified coinsurance treaties use NAR for risk monitoring and RBC optimization.']
  risk_class varchar [note: 'The underwriting risk classification assigned to the insured, which determines the applicable YRT COI rate tier. Preferred classes attract lower reinsurance premiums; substandard or rated classes attract higher rates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: preferred_plus|preferred|standard_plus|standard|substandard|rated — promote to reference product]']
  smoker_status varchar [note: 'Tobacco/smoker status of the insured as used in YRT rate table lookups. Smoker status significantly impacts the COI rate applied to the NAR for reinsurance premium computation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational source system from which the NAR calculation data was originated (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS, RGA AURA Reinsurance, AXIS Actuarial Valuation System). Supports data lineage and ETL traceability.']
  treaty_year varchar [note: 'The year of the reinsurance treaty in force at the time of the NAR calculation. Used to identify the applicable rate table version when treaty terms are renegotiated periodically.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this NAR calculation record was last modified. Tracks revisions, corrections, and restatements for audit and compliance purposes.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The actuarial valuation date as of which the policy reserve or account value is measured for NAR computation. Typically aligns with the beginning-of-year (BOY) or end-of-year (EOY) valuation cycle.']

  Note: 'Stores the periodic Net Amount at Risk (NAR) calculations for each ceded policy, which form the basis for YRT reinsurance premium billing. Captures calculation date, policy reference, death benefit, account value or reserve, NAR amount, calculation method, and effective period. NAR is the fundamental driver of YRT cost of insurance charges and reinsurance premium computation. Supports actuarial and reinsurance premium reconciliation.'
}

Table reinsurance.recapture_event {
  recapture_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each recapture event record in the reinsurance management system. Primary key for the recapture_event data product.']
  cession_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific cession record being recaptured. Identifies the original cession transaction from which risk is being reclaimed by Life Insurance.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: When an annuity contract surrenders or a rider terminates, the insurer may recapture previously ceded risk. Current recapture_event only references in_force_policy_id. This FK enables annuity-specific']
  experience_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.experience_study. Business justification: Recapture rights are triggered by adverse mortality experience documented in experience studies. recapture_event.mortality_experience_ratio is the key metric from the experience study. Regulatory and ']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying life insurance policy whose ceded risk is being recaptured. Links to the policy master record in the Policy Administration System.']
  insurance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Recapture events require formal notice documents, reinsurer acknowledgment letters, settlement agreements, penalty calculations, and regulatory notifications. Treaty administration and financial settl']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Recapture transactions require GL posting for reserve transfer, RBC impact tracking, and financial statement disclosure. Actuarial and finance teams post recapture entries for statutory reporting, cap']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: When a recapture occurs under coinsurance or funds-withheld treaties, ceded assets are returned to the ceding company\'s investment portfolio. Investment operations must identify the receiving portfoli']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Recapture events may be triggered by or must comply with regulatory obligations (e.g., credit for reinsurance rules requiring recapture when reinsurer loses authorized status, RBC-triggered recapture ']
  reinsurer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurer counterparty (e.g., RGA, Swiss Re) from whom risk is being recaptured. Identifies the party relationship in the reinsurance management system.']
  surrender_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.surrender. Business justification: Policy surrender is a primary trigger for reinsurance recapture events. The recapture settlement amount and timing depend on the surrender transaction details (net proceeds, effective date). Reinsuran']
  treaty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance treaty under which the recapture right is exercised. Links to the treaty master record governing the cession arrangement being recaptured.']
  treaty_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.treaty_schedule. Business justification: Recapture eligibility and terms (recapture_eligible, recapture_waiting_period_years, recapture_percentage) are defined at the treaty schedule level. A recapture event is triggered based on the specifi']
  approval_reference varchar [note: 'Internal approval reference number or authorization code issued by Life Insurance\'s reinsurance management or finance team authorizing the recapture event. Supports audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  bordereaux_period varchar [note: 'The bordereaux reporting period (e.g., 2024-Q3 or 2024-06) in which this recapture event is included for submission to the reinsurer. Supports ACORD bordereaux reporting and reinsurer reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recapture event record was first created in the reinsurance management system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the recapture takes legal and financial effect, which may differ from the recapture_date if retroactive adjustments are required under the treaty terms.']
  mortality_experience_ratio decimal(7,4) [note: 'The actual-to-expected (A/E) mortality ratio that served as the actuarial basis for a mortality_improvement recapture trigger. A ratio below 1.0000 indicates favorable mortality experience justifying recapture. Sourced from actuarial experience studies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional commentary, context, or operational notes related to the recapture event, entered by the reinsurance operations team. Supports treaty management and audit documentation.']
  notice_date date [note: 'Date on which Life Insurance formally notified the reinsurer of its intent to recapture, as required by treaty notice provisions. Used to validate compliance with contractual notice periods.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days of advance notice required by the treaty before the recapture can take effect. Used to validate that the notice_date and recapture_date are compliant with treaty terms.']
  partial_recapture_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recapture event represents a partial recapture of the ceded risk (True) or a full recapture of the entire cession (False). Partial recaptures are common in large facultative placements.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual penalty fee payable to the reinsurer upon exercise of the recapture right, as stipulated in the treaty terms. May be zero if the treaty permits penalty-free recapture after a specified duration or under qualifying trigger conditions.']
  penalty_waived_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractual recapture penalty has been waived by the reinsurer, typically through negotiation or as a result of a qualifying trigger condition such as reinsurer credit concern.']
  rbc_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated change in Risk-Based Capital (RBC) requirement resulting from the recapture of ceded risk. A positive value indicates increased capital requirement as retained risk rises. Supports RBC capital planning and ORSA reporting.']
  recapture_date date [note: 'The principal real-world business event date on which Life Insurance formally exercises its contractual right to recapture previously ceded risk from the reinsurer. This is the operative date for treaty and RBC calculations.']
  recapture_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'The proportion of the original ceded risk being recaptured, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.5000 = 50%). Applicable when partial_recapture_flag is True. Used to calculate pro-rata reserve and premium adjustments.']
  recapture_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier assigned to the recapture event, used in bordereaux reporting and reinsurer correspondence. Sourced from the Reinsurance Management System (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS or RGA AURA Reinsurance).']
  recapture_right_eligible_date date [note: 'The earliest date on which Life Insurance became contractually eligible to exercise the recapture right under the treaty terms, typically based on a minimum cession duration or treaty anniversary.']
  recapture_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recapture event workflow. Tracks progression from initiation through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|in_progress|completed|cancelled|disputed — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  recapture_trigger_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the business reason that triggered the recapture event. Supports treaty management analytics and RBC capital planning.
