[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mortality_improvement|treaty_renegotiation|reinsurer_credit_concern|treaty_expiry|voluntary|regulatory_requirement — promote to reference product if additional triggers are required]']
  recaptured_face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross face amount (death benefit) of the ceded risk being recaptured from the reinsurer. Represents the notional insurance coverage amount returned to Life Insurance\'s retained book.']
  recaptured_nar_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Net Amount at Risk (NAR) — the difference between the death benefit and the policy reserve — that is being recaptured from the reinsurer. This is the primary quantitative measure of risk reclaimed and is central to RBC capital planning calculations.']
  recaptured_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annualized or in-force reinsurance premium amount that will cease to be paid to the reinsurer as a result of the recapture. Used for premium billing reconciliation and financial impact assessment.']
  recaptured_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The statutory or GAAP reserve amount associated with the recaptured cession that must be transferred back to Life Insurance\'s balance sheet upon recapture. Relevant for coinsurance and modified coinsurance treaty types.']
  reinsurer_acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date on which the reinsurer formally acknowledged the recapture notice. Populated when reinsurer_acknowledgment_flag is True. Used to track treaty compliance timelines.']
  reinsurer_acknowledgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reinsurer has formally acknowledged receipt of the recapture notice. Required to confirm the recapture process is proceeding in accordance with treaty terms.']
  reinsurer_credit_rating varchar [note: 'The credit rating of the reinsurer at the time the recapture event was initiated, as assigned by a recognized rating agency (e.g., A.M. Best, S&P, Moody\'s). Captured to document the credit concern trigger basis and support ERM reporting.']
  reinsurer_credit_rating_agency varchar [note: 'The rating agency that issued the credit rating captured in reinsurer_credit_rating. Used to contextualize the rating value for ERM and credit risk reporting.']
  retrocession_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cession being recaptured was part of a retrocession arrangement (i.e., the reinsurer had further ceded the risk to a retrocessionaire). Flags potential downstream settlement complexity.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net financial settlement amount payable between Life Insurance and the reinsurer to finalize the recapture, accounting for reserve transfers, return premiums, and any applicable penalty. Represents the net cash flow of the recapture transaction.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this recapture event record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency treaty arrangements with international reinsurers.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date on which the financial settlement of the recapture event is completed, including reserve transfers and penalty payments. Used for cash flow reconciliation and bordereaux reporting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this recapture event record originated (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS, RGA AURA Reinsurance). Supports data lineage and ETL reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  treaty_anniversary_year varchar [note: 'The treaty year (number of years since treaty inception) at the time of recapture. Many treaties permit recapture only after a minimum number of treaty years, and this field documents compliance with that condition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recapture event record was most recently modified in the reinsurance management system. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.']

  Note: 'Tracks recapture events where Life Insurance exercises its contractual right to reclaim previously ceded risk from a reinsurer, typically triggered by improved mortality experience, treaty renegotiation, or reinsurer credit concerns. Captures recapture date, treaty reference, recaptured ceded amount, recapture penalty if applicable, effective date, and recapture status. Supports treaty management and RBC capital planning.'
}

Table reinsurance.reinsurer_settlement {
  reinsurer_settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each reinsurer settlement record in the lakehouse. Primary key for the reinsurer_settlement data product.']
  bordereaux_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.bordereaux. Business justification: Reinsurer settlements reconcile financial positions between Life Insurance and reinsurers, and they reference specific bordereaux submissions as supporting documentation. The table currently has \'bord']
  insurance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Reinsurer settlements generate formal remittance advices, settlement statements, and wire transfer confirmations that are retained as legal and accounting documents. Linking settlement to its document']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Settlement transactions must link to GL entries for cash reconciliation, intercompany elimination, and financial statement preparation. Treasury and accounting teams reconcile settlement payments to j']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Funds-withheld treaty settlements directly credit or debit specific investment portfolios holding the withheld assets. Investment accounting and ALM teams require this link to reconcile settlement cas']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Settlement amounts flow directly to statutory financial statements and must be disclosed in regulatory filings. Critical for Schedule S Part 3 reconciliation and quarterly/annual statement preparation']
  reinsurance_recoverable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.reinsurance_recoverable. Business justification: Cash application process: when a reinsurer settlement is received, it reduces the outstanding reinsurance_recoverable balance on the balance sheet. Finance teams must link settlement records to recove']
  reinsurer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurer entity (e.g., RGA, Swiss Re) that is the counterparty to this settlement. Links to the reinsurer master record.']
  reserve_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.reserve_calculation. Business justification: reinsurer_settlement.reserve_adjustment_amount is directly derived from actuarial reserve calculations. Financial close, settlement reconciliation, and STAT/GAAP reporting require tracing settlement r']
  treaty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance treaty (YRT, coinsurance, modified coinsurance, or facultative) under which this settlement is calculated.']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: Period-end reinsurer settlement reconciliation is performed against actuarial valuation run outputs for GAAP/STAT close. Settlement amounts must tie to the valuation run for LDTI/IFRS17 ceded reserve ']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The financial reporting period to which this settlement is attributed, expressed as YYYY-MM for monthly or YYYY-Q# for quarterly periods (e.g., \'2024-03\' or \'2024-Q1\'). Used for financial close alignment and statutory reporting.']
  allowance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Ceding allowance or expense allowance paid by the reinsurer to offset the ceding company\'s acquisition and administrative costs. Common in coinsurance and quota share treaties. Reduces the net settlement payable.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the settlement was formally approved by the authorized approver within Life Insurance prior to payment initiation. Supports SOX internal controls and dual-authorization requirements.']
  ceded_nar_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total Net Amount at Risk (NAR) ceded to the reinsurer across all policies in the settlement period. NAR is the death benefit minus the policy reserve and is the basis for YRT premium calculations.']
  ceded_policy_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual ceded policies or certificates included in this settlement period\'s bordereaux. Supports volume reconciliation and treaty capacity monitoring.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this settlement record was first created in the Reinsurance Management System. Used for audit trail and financial close reconciliation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this settlement record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency reinsurance arrangements with international reinsurers.']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which a disputed settlement was resolved and agreed upon by both parties. Null if no dispute exists or if the dispute is still open.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Foreign exchange rate applied to convert settlement amounts to the functional currency (USD) when the settlement currency differs. Rate is expressed as units of settlement currency per one USD.']
  experience_refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Experience refund or profit commission payable by the reinsurer to the ceding company when actual claims experience is more favorable than the treaty\'s expected loss ratio. Reduces the net settlement payable.']
  funds_withheld_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative balance of funds withheld by the ceding company from the reinsurer under a funds-withheld or modified coinsurance arrangement. The ceding company retains assets equal to the ceded reserve and pays interest to the reinsurer.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The General Ledger (GL) account code to which the net settlement amount is posted in the finance domain. Enables automated journal entry creation and financial close reconciliation under SAP and GAAP reporting.']
  gross_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total reinsurance premiums payable to the reinsurer for the settlement period before any offsets. Represents the ceded premium obligation under the treaty for the period.']
  gross_recoverable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total claim recoverables receivable from the reinsurer for the settlement period before any offsets. Represents the reinsurer\'s share of claims paid or incurred under the treaty.']
  ibnr_recoverable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reinsurer\'s estimated share of Incurred But Not Reported (IBNR) claims included in the settlement. Represents the actuarially estimated recoverable for claims that have occurred but have not yet been reported or fully adjudicated.']
  interest_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Interest credited or charged on funds withheld or late payments under modified coinsurance or funds-withheld arrangements. Positive values indicate interest owed to the ceding company; negative values indicate interest owed to the reinsurer.']
  net_settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net financial obligation after netting gross reinsurance premiums payable against gross claim recoverables receivable, experience refunds, allowances, and interest adjustments. Represents the final amount to be paid or received.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional commentary, special instructions, or contextual information related to this settlement. Used by reinsurance operations staff to document exceptions, adjustments, or communications with the reinsurer.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was initiated or the funds were transferred. May differ from settlement_date if there is a processing lag between settlement approval and actual funds movement.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Wire transfer reference, ACH trace number, or check number associated with the actual payment or receipt of the net settlement amount. Used for bank reconciliation and accounts payable/receivable matching in the finance domain.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date on which the settlement was successfully reconciled against the finance domain\'s accounts payable/receivable ledger. Null if reconciliation is not yet complete.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the accounts payable/receivable reconciliation process for this settlement with the finance domain. \'unreconciled\' indicates not yet matched; \'in_progress\' indicates active review; \'reconciled\' confirms match; \'exception\' indicates a discrepancy requiring resolution.']
  reserve_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Adjustment to ceded reserves transferred between the ceding company and reinsurer under coinsurance or modified coinsurance treaties. Reflects changes in policy reserves during the settlement period.']
  retrocession_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this settlement relates to a retrocession arrangement (True) where the reinsurer cedes risk to another reinsurer, or a standard cession from the primary insurer (False). Supports retrocession tracking and RBC optimization analysis.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the net settlement amount was paid or received, representing the principal real-world financial event date. Distinct from the settlement period end date and the record creation timestamp.']
  settlement_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the net settlement amount is payable by Life Insurance to the reinsurer (\'payable\') or receivable by Life Insurance from the reinsurer (\'receivable\'). Drives accounts payable/receivable classification in the finance domain.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'The agreed-upon frequency at which settlements are performed under the reinsurance treaty. Drives the settlement period dates and financial close calendar.']
  settlement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to this settlement by the Reinsurance Management System (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS or RGA AURA). Used in bordereaux reporting and accounts payable/receivable reconciliation with the finance domain.']
  settlement_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the accounting period covered by this settlement. Together with settlement_period_start_date, defines the bordereaux reporting window for premium and recoverable netting.']
  settlement_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the accounting period covered by this settlement (e.g., first day of the month or quarter). Defines the beginning of the bordereaux reporting window.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the settlement record. \'draft\' indicates initial calculation; \'pending\' awaits approval; \'approved\' is authorized for payment; \'settled\' confirms payment has been made or received; \'disputed\' indicates a discrepancy raised by either party; \'voided\' indicates cancellation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this settlement record originated (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS, RGA AURA Reinsurance). Supports data lineage tracking in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  source_system_settlement_reference varchar [note: 'The native settlement identifier from the originating Reinsurance Management System (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS or RGA AURA). Enables traceability from the lakehouse silver layer back to the operational system of record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this settlement record, supporting audit trail requirements and change tracking for financial close processes.']

  Note: 'Records periodic financial settlements between Life Insurance and reinsurers, netting reinsurance premiums payable against claim recoverables receivable and experience refunds. Captures settlement period, reinsurer, gross premium amount, gross recoverable amount, experience refund amount, net settlement amount, settlement direction (payable/receivable), settlement date, payment reference, and reconciliation status. Supports accounts payable/receivable reconciliation with the finance domain and provides the financial close record for each reinsurer relationship.'
}

Table reinsurance.reserve {
  reserve_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each reinsurance reserve calculation record. Primary key for the reinsurance_reserve data product.']
  cession_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific cession record (automatic or facultative) associated with this reserve calculation. Links to the cession processing record.']
  cohort_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.cohort_definition. Business justification: LDTI/IFRS17 ceded reserve measurement is organized by actuarial cohorts. reserve.ifrs17_csg_amount and ifrs17_ra_ceded_amount are cohort-based measures. A reinsurance actuary implementing LDTI/IFRS17 ']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Ceded reinsurance reserves must be tracked per annuity contract for statutory and GAAP reporting. Current reserve only references in_force_policy_id. This FK enables annuity-specific ceded reserve cal']
  gaap_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gaap_reserve. Business justification: LDTI/GAAP reserve reconciliation requires tracing ceded reserve components (dac_ceded_amount, ifrs17_ra_ceded_amount) from the reinsurance operational system to the GAAP reserve record. Finance teams ']
  ifrs17_contract_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ifrs17_contract_group. Business justification: IFRS17 requires reinsurance held contracts to be grouped into contract groups for measurement. Ceded reserve amounts (ifrs17_ra_ceded_amount, modco_reserve_adjustment) must be attributed to the correc']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying life insurance policy for which the ceded reserve is being calculated and reported.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Reserve movements require GL posting for statutory and GAAP reporting, RBC calculation, and external audit. Actuarial teams calculate ceded reserves; finance teams post reserve changes to GL for quart']
  nar_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.nar_calculation. Business justification: Reinsurance reserves for YRT and coinsurance treaties are computed using NAR as a key input — the ceded reserve is a function of the NAR and the applicable reserve factors. Linking reserve.nar_calcula']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Reinsurance reserves are held per product plan. Critical for statutory reporting, GAAP/IFRS reserve calculations, and product-level reserve adequacy analysis. Product_line is high-level category; FK e']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Ceded reserve calculations (statutory, GAAP, IFRS17) depend on the specific plan version\'s reserve basis, benefit structure, and actuarial assumptions. Actuarial valuation reports and regulatory filin']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Ceded reserves are backed by designated investment portfolios in ModCo and funds-withheld arrangements. Business process: reserve valuation, investment income allocation, interest crediting, and ALM g']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Ceded reserves must comply with specific regulatory obligations (credit for reinsurance reserve credit rules, Regulation XXX, LDTI/IFRS17 ceded reserve requirements). The reserve entity has rbc_credit']
  reinsurer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurer counterparty (e.g., RGA, Swiss Re) holding or crediting the ceded reserve under this treaty.']
  reserve_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.reserve_calculation. Business justification: Reinsurance reserves are calculated as part of overall reserve calculations for statutory valuation, RBC credit determination, and GAAP/IFRS17 reporting. Direct business process dependency.']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Ceded reserve calculations are governed by state-specific regulations (Regulation XXX, AG38, state credit-for-reinsurance rules). Statutory actuaries must reference the applicable state regulation whe']
  statutory_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.statutory_reserve. Business justification: Statutory reserve reconciliation process requires matching ceded reserve amounts in the reinsurance system (reinsurance.reserve.ceded_reserve_amount) against the statutory reserve exhibit (finance.sta']
  treaty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance treaty under which this ceded reserve is calculated. Links to the treaty master record governing the cession terms.']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: Reinsurance ceded reserves are produced within actuarial valuation runs. LDTI/IFRS17 ceded reserve reporting, reserve movement analysis, and actuarial opinion documentation require direct linkage of e']
  value_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.value. Business justification: Reinsurance ceded reserve calculations are based on the specific policy value snapshot (CSV, account value, reserve amount). Actuarial and statutory reporting require tracing which policy value record']
  actuarial_assumption_set varchar [note: 'The identifier or label of the actuarial assumption set used in the reserve calculation (e.g., \'PBR_2024_Q4\', \'LDTI_LOCKED_IN_2023\', \'IFRS17_CURRENT_EST\'). Enables traceability of reserve calculations to specific assumption vintages used in the Actuarial Valuation System.']
  basis varchar [note: 'The accounting or regulatory basis under which the ceded reserve is calculated. \'gaap\' = Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (FASB ASC 944/LDTI); \'sap\' = Statutory Accounting Principles (NAIC); \'ifrs17\' = International Financial Reporting Standards 17; \'pbr\' = Principle-Based Reserving (NAIC VM-20/VM-21); \'tax\' = IRS tax reserve basis.']
  bordereaux_period varchar [note: 'The period identifier for the bordereaux report in which this reserve record is included (e.g., Q12024, M032024, FY2023). Bordereaux reports are periodic data submissions to reinsurers summarizing ceded reserves, premiums, and claims. Supports Swiss Re IRIS and RGA AURA bordereaux reporting modules.']
  ceded_face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the policy face amount (death benefit) ceded to the reinsurer under this treaty. Represents the reinsurer\'s share of the total death benefit exposure. Used in automatic and facultative cession calculations.']
  ceded_nar_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Net Amount at Risk (NAR) ceded to the reinsurer under this treaty as of the valuation date. NAR represents the excess of the death benefit over the policy reserve, and is the basis for YRT reinsurance premium calculations. Critical for RBC optimization and NAR-based cession calculations.']
  ceded_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross ceded reserve amount transferred to or credited from the reinsurer under the treaty as of the valuation date. Represents the ceding company\'s share of policy reserves ceded to the reinsurer. Used in LDTI, PBR, and IFRS 17 ceded reserve reporting.']
  collateral_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of collateral (letters of credit, trust funds, or funds withheld) posted by an unauthorized reinsurer to support the ceded reserve credit. Required for statutory credit for reinsurance when the reinsurer is not authorized in the ceding company\'s domiciliary state.']
  collateral_type varchar [note: 'The type of collateral posted by the reinsurer to support the ceded reserve credit for unauthorized reinsurers. \'letter_of_credit\' = LOC from an approved bank; \'trust_fund\' = assets held in a qualifying trust; \'funds_withheld\' = assets retained by ceding company; \'cash_deposit\' = cash deposited with the ceding company.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this reinsurance reserve record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements for financial reporting records.']
  credited_interest_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The interest rate credited by the ceding company to the reinsurer on the funds withheld balance under a funds withheld or ModCo treaty. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.045000 = 4.5%). Drives the interest credited amount calculation and ALM coordination.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this reserve record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency reinsurance treaties with international reinsurers such as Swiss Re and RGA.']
  dac_ceded_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Deferred Acquisition Cost (DAC) amount ceded to the reinsurer under coinsurance treaties. Represents the reinsurer\'s proportional share of deferred acquisition costs, which offsets the ceding company\'s DAC asset. Required for GAAP and LDTI financial reporting.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this reinsurance reserve record is effective. Represents the start of the period covered by this reserve calculation, typically aligned with the treaty effective date or policy issue date.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this reinsurance reserve record expires or is superseded. Represents the end of the period covered by this reserve calculation. Null for open-ended or in-force records.']
  funds_withheld_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The balance of assets withheld by the ceding company on behalf of the reinsurer under a funds withheld coinsurance or ModCo treaty. Represents the liability owed to the reinsurer for assets retained. Critical for ALM coordination and investment domain integration.']
  ifrs17_csg_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The ceded Contractual Service Margin (CSM) amount under IFRS 17 for this reinsurance reserve record. Represents the reinsurer\'s share of unearned profit under IFRS 17 measurement. Required for IFRS 17 ceded reinsurance contract reporting.']
  ifrs17_ra_ceded_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The ceded Risk Adjustment (RA) for non-financial risk under IFRS 17 for this reinsurance reserve record. Represents the reinsurer\'s share of the risk adjustment, reflecting the compensation required for bearing uncertainty in insurance cash flows.']
  interest_credited_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of interest credited to the reinsurer on the funds withheld balance for the valuation period. Calculated as the funds withheld balance multiplied by the credited interest rate for the period. Supports investment domain integration and settlement calculations.']
  is_authorized_reinsurer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reinsurer is authorized (licensed) in the ceding company\'s state of domicile (True) or is an unauthorized/alien reinsurer requiring collateral (False). Affects statutory credit for reinsurance and RBC treatment under NAIC regulations.']
  is_retrocession boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reserve record relates to a retrocession arrangement (True) where the reinsurer further cedes risk to another reinsurer, or a direct reinsurance cession (False). Supports retrocession reserve tracking and RBC reporting.']
  modco_reserve_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Modified Coinsurance (ModCo) reserve adjustment amount representing the difference between the ceded reserve and the funds withheld balance. Captures the periodic settlement adjustment required under ModCo treaties to reconcile reserve movements with withheld asset performance.']
  movement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The change in ceded reserve between the prior valuation date and the current valuation date. Positive values indicate reserve increases (additional cession); negative values indicate reserve releases. Used in bordereaux reporting and reinsurance settlement calculations.']
  net_ceded_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net ceded reserve amount after applying any ModCo adjustments, experience refund reserves, or other treaty-specific offsets. Represents the final net reserve position ceded to the reinsurer for financial reporting purposes.']
  prior_period_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The ceded reserve amount as of the prior valuation date. Used to calculate the reserve movement (change in ceded reserve) for the current period, which drives reinsurance premium settlements and LDTI/IFRS 17 ceded reserve roll-forward reporting.']
  rbc_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Risk-Based Capital (RBC) credit recognized by the ceding company for this ceded reserve position. Represents the reduction in required capital resulting from the reinsurance arrangement. Supports RBC optimization reporting and ORSA (Own Risk and Solvency Assessment).']
  reinsurer_share_percentage decimal(8,6) [note: 'The percentage of the policy reserve ceded to this reinsurer under the treaty. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.900000 = 90%). Used in proportional coinsurance and quota share treaties to calculate the reinsurer\'s share of reserves and premiums.']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'The financial reporting period to which this reserve record belongs, expressed as quarter (e.g., Q12024, Q42023) or full year (e.g., FY2023). Used for LDTI, PBR, IFRS 17, and NAIC statutory reporting period alignment.']
  reserve_method varchar [note: 'The actuarial reserving method applied to calculate the ceded reserve. \'net_premium\' = Net Premium Reserve method; \'gross_premium\' = Gross Premium Reserve method (LDTI); \'commissioners_reserve\' = NAIC Commissioners Reserve Valuation Method (CRVM); \'pbr_deterministic\' = PBR deterministic scenario; \'pbr_stochastic\' = PBR stochastic scenario. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_premium|gross_premium|commissioners_reserve|pbr_deterministic|pbr_stochastic|crvm|net_single_premium — promote to reference product]']
  reserve_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this reinsurance reserve record, used in bordereaux reporting and reinsurer settlement statements. Typically assigned by the reinsurance management system (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS or RGA AURA).']
  reserve_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reinsurance reserve by structural arrangement. \'traditional_ceded\' = standard ceded reserve transferred to reinsurer; \'funds_withheld\' = reserve retained by ceding company under funds withheld coinsurance; \'modco_adjustment\' = Modified Coinsurance (ModCo) reserve adjustment; \'experience_refund_reserve\' = reserve held for experience refund obligations; \'retrocession_ceded\' = reserve ceded under retrocession arrangement.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date on which the reinsurance reserve balance was or is expected to be settled with the reinsurer. Used for cash flow planning, ALM coordination, and reinsurance recoverable tracking.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current settlement status of the reinsurance reserve balance with the reinsurer. \'pending\' = awaiting settlement; \'settled\' = fully settled in current period; \'disputed\' = under dispute with reinsurer; \'partially_settled\' = partially settled; \'voided\' = record voided due to error or treaty termination.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this reinsurance reserve record was sourced (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS, RGA AURA Reinsurance). Supports data lineage and ETL (Extract Transform Load) traceability in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this reinsurance reserve record was last modified. Supports audit trail, change tracking, and SOX compliance requirements for financial reporting records in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The as-of date for which the ceded reserve is calculated. Represents the actuarial valuation date used in reserve calculations (e.g., quarter-end or year-end). Aligns with LDTI, PBR, and IFRS 17 reporting periods.']
  valuation_system varchar [note: 'The actuarial valuation system used to calculate this ceded reserve (e.g., AXIS, Prophet, MoSes). Supports data lineage, audit traceability, and reconciliation between actuarial and reinsurance management systems.']

  Note: 'Stores reinsurance reserve calculations representing the ceded portion of policy reserves held by or credited from reinsurers under coinsurance, modified coinsurance (ModCo), and funds withheld coinsurance treaties. Captures valuation date, treaty reference, reinsurer, ceded reserve basis (GAAP/SAP/IFRS), ceded reserve amount, reserve type (traditional ceded/funds withheld/ModCo adjustment), funds withheld balance amount, credited interest rate, interest credited amount, modified coinsurance reserve adjustment, settlement status, and reserve method. This is the single source of truth for all ceded reserves and funds withheld balances. Supports LDTI, PBR, and IFRS 17 ceded reserve reporting, ALM coordination for withheld asset management, and investment domain integration for credited interest calculations.'
}

Table reinsurance.automatic_binding_limit {
  automatic_binding_limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each automatic binding limit record. Primary key for the automatic_binding_limit reference table. [_canonical_skip_reason: Entity role is REFERENCE_LOOKUP — defines maximum face amounts auto-ceded per treaty/plan/risk-class/age-band combination; exempt from per-role minimums.]']
  mortality_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: Automatic binding limits are calibrated by age band and risk class against specific mortality tables. Underwriting capacity management and RBC credit eligibility require tracing binding limit amounts ']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Automatic binding limits are defined per product plan. Essential for automatic vs. facultative decisioning in underwriting workflow, capacity management, and treaty compliance. Plan_code is denormaliz']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Automatic binding limits are negotiated for specific plan versions — when a plan version changes its benefit structure or underwriting requirements, binding limits must be renegotiated. Treaty adminis']
  pricing_basis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Automatic binding limits are set based on pricing bases, mortality assumptions, and risk assessment. Required for automatic capacity determination and treaty design.']
  prior_limit_automatic_binding_limit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous version of this binding limit record that was superseded by the current record. Enables traversal of the amendment history chain for audit and regulatory review purposes.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Automatic binding limits must comply with regulatory obligations (e.g., NAIC credit for reinsurance model law limits on automatic cessions, RBC automatic binding capacity rules). Compliance officers t']
  reinsurer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurer (e.g., RGA, Swiss Re) that is party to the treaty for which this binding limit is defined. Used to identify the counterparty in cession routing decisions.']
  retention_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.retention_limit. Business justification: Automatic binding limits and retention limits are complementary reference tables that together define the cession framework for a treaty. The ABL (how much can be automatically ceded) is defined in th']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Automatic binding limits must comply with state-specific regulations governing retention requirements, jumbo limit thresholds, and automatic coverage capacity. State regulations define minimum retenti']
  treaty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance treaty under which this automatic binding limit applies. Links the limit record to the governing treaty agreement between the ceding company and the reinsurer.']
  underwriting_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: Automatic binding limits differ by underwriting class — preferred risks have higher limits than substandard. The risk_class plain attribute is a denormalized underwriting class reference. Proper FK en']
  age_band_max varchar [note: 'The maximum attained age (inclusive) of the insured for which this automatic binding limit row applies. Together with age_band_min, defines the age band dimension of the cession routing matrix.']
  age_band_min varchar [note: 'The minimum attained age (inclusive) of the insured for which this automatic binding limit row applies. Age banding is a standard actuarial practice in reinsurance treaty design as mortality risk and NAR vary significantly by age.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the business reason for creating or amending this binding limit record (e.g., treaty renegotiation, capacity adjustment, regulatory change, experience-driven repricing). Supports audit trail and governance review.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this binding limit record was formally approved by the authorized approver. Supports governance audit trail and SOX compliance documentation.']
  automatic_capacity_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total aggregate face amount capacity available under the automatic treaty for new cessions. Once this capacity is exhausted, new risks must be submitted facultatively even if they fall within the per-policy binding limit. Null indicates unlimited capacity.']
  binding_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum face amount (death benefit) in the treaty currency that can be automatically ceded to the reinsurer under this treaty without requiring facultative submission. Risks with face amounts at or below this threshold qualify for automatic placement. This is the principal quantitative fact of this reference record.']
  cession_basis varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this limit record governs automatic cessions, facultative cessions, or an automatic-facultative arrangement. Drives the cession routing logic in the Reinsurance Management System to determine placement pathway.']
  cession_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of the eligible risk (face amount or NAR) that is ceded to the reinsurer under quota-share or coinsurance arrangements. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9000 = 90%). Applicable primarily for coinsurance and quota-share treaty types; may be null for YRT treaties where cession is based on NAR excess over retention.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this automatic binding limit record was first created in the data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the binding limit and jumbo limit amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, CAD). Required for multi-currency treaty arrangements with international reinsurers such as Swiss Re or RGA.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this automatic binding limit record becomes operative for cession routing decisions. New business applications with policy effective dates on or after this date are subject to this limit.']
  facultative_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The face amount above the automatic binding limit at which the ceding company must submit the risk facultatively to the reinsurer. Risks between the binding limit and this threshold may be eligible for automatic-facultative placement; risks above this threshold require full facultative submission.']
  flat_extra_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum flat extra premium per thousand of face amount (per mille) that is eligible for automatic cession under this binding limit. Risks with flat extras exceeding this threshold require facultative submission. Null indicates no flat extra restriction.']
  insured_gender varchar [note: 'The gender of the insured to which this binding limit applies. Some treaties define gender-specific binding limits due to mortality differentials. A value of Unisex indicates the limit applies regardless of gender.']
  is_aviation_excluded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether risks involving aviation hazards (private pilot, commercial pilot, or frequent flyer exclusions) are excluded from automatic cession eligibility under this binding limit. When True, aviation-rated risks must be submitted facultatively.']
  is_foreign_national_excluded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether foreign nationals (non-US citizens or non-permanent residents) are excluded from automatic cession eligibility under this binding limit. When True, policies on foreign nationals must be submitted facultatively regardless of face amount.']
  is_jumbo_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a jumbo limit check (aggregate in-force across all reinsurers on the same life) must be performed before automatic cession is permitted under this binding limit. When True, the cession routing system must query total in-force amounts across all treaties before confirming automatic placement.']
  is_mib_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Medical Information Bureau (MIB) check is required as a condition of automatic cession eligibility under this binding limit. When True, the underwriting workbench must confirm MIB clearance before the risk qualifies for automatic placement.']
  issue_age_basis varchar [note: 'The age calculation convention used to determine the insured\'s age for applying the age band (e.g., Age Last Birthday, Age Nearest Birthday). Must align with the age basis used in the Policy Administration System to ensure consistent cession routing.']
  jumbo_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum total in-force amount across all reinsurers on a single life (aggregate across all treaties) beyond which the risk is considered a jumbo risk and requires special handling or facultative placement regardless of individual treaty binding limits. Prevents over-concentration of risk on a single life.']
  limit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this automatic binding limit record. Active records are used in cession routing. Superseded records have been replaced by a newer version. Expired records are past their termination date. Suspended records are temporarily inactive pending treaty renegotiation.']
  nar_basis varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the binding limit is applied against the full face amount (death benefit) or the Net Amount at Risk (NAR), which is the death benefit less the policy reserve or account value. NAR-based limits are common in UL and IUL products where the NAR decreases as the account value grows.']
  reinsurer_treaty_reference varchar [note: 'The reinsurer\'s own internal reference number or identifier for this treaty and binding limit schedule. Used for reconciliation with reinsurer bordereaux reports and correspondence from partners such as RGA or Swiss Re.']
  retention_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum face amount the ceding company retains on its own books before ceding the excess to the reinsurer under this treaty. Used in conjunction with the binding limit to determine the ceded portion (face amount minus retention). Supports NAR-based cession calculations.']
  smoker_status varchar [note: 'The tobacco/smoker classification of the insured to which this binding limit applies. Smoker and non-smoker populations carry different mortality rates and may have distinct binding limits under the treaty.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational source system from which this binding limit record was extracted (e.g., SWISS_RE_IRIS, RGA_AURA, INTERNAL_TREATY_MGMT). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  source_system_limit_ref varchar [note: 'The natural key or record identifier assigned to this binding limit record in the originating source system (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS internal limit ID or RGA AURA schedule reference). Enables reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the source system.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code (USPS abbreviation) for which this binding limit applies, if the limit is state-specific. Null indicates the limit applies in all states. Some states have regulatory restrictions that may affect automatic binding limits.']
  table_rating_max varchar [note: 'The maximum substandard table rating (e.g., Table A through Table P, or Table 1 through Table 16) eligible for automatic cession under this binding limit. Risks with table ratings exceeding this threshold must be submitted facultatively. Null indicates no table rating restriction.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which this automatic binding limit record ceases to be operative. Null indicates the record is currently active with no defined end date. Used to support historical versioning of binding limits as treaties are amended.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this automatic binding limit record was last modified in the data platform. Used to detect changes and support incremental processing in the Databricks Silver Layer ETL pipeline.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for this binding limit record within the same treaty/plan/risk-class/age-band combination. Incremented each time the binding limit is amended. Supports historical audit trail and point-in-time cession routing reconstruction.']

  Note: 'Reference table defining the automatic binding limits per treaty, plan code, risk class, and age band — the maximum face amount that can be automatically ceded without facultative submission. Captures treaty reference, plan code, risk class, age band, automatic binding limit amount, jumbo limit, and effective date. Drives the cession routing logic determining whether a risk qualifies for automatic or facultative placement.'
}

Table reinsurance.retention_limit {
  retention_limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each retention limit record. Primary key for the retention_limit reference table. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — this is a reference/configuration record defining net retention and automatic binding limit thresholds used in cession routing decisions.']
  mortality_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: Retention limits are calibrated by age band, risk class, and table_rating_max against specific mortality tables. RBC credit eligibility and retention adequacy reviews require tracing retention limit a']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Retention limits vary by product plan. Required for cession amount calculation, treaty compliance validation, and retention strategy management. Plan_code and product_line are denormalized; FK enables']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Retention limits are set for specific plan versions — benefit structure changes (e.g., adding GMDB) affect the company\'s risk retention appetite. Reinsurance treaty negotiations and RBC capital manage']
  pricing_basis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Retention limits are established using pricing bases and profitability targets to optimize risk retention vs reinsurance cost. Essential for retention strategy and treaty design.']
  prior_limit_retention_limit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding version of this retention limit record that was superseded. Enables a linked history chain for audit trail and regulatory review of limit changes over time.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Retention limits are established in direct response to regulatory obligations (NAIC credit for reinsurance model law, RBC retention requirements). Compliance and actuarial teams must trace each retent']
  reinsurer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance counterparty (e.g., RGA, Swiss Re, Munich Re) for whom this automatic binding limit or retention schedule applies. Some treaties involve multiple reinsurers with different binding limits per reinsurer.']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Retention limits are directly governed by state regulations specifying minimum retention requirements for credit for reinsurance. retention_limit has \'state_code\' as a denormalized plain attribute. Co']
  treaty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reinsurance treaty under which this retention or automatic binding limit applies. Links the limit record to the governing treaty agreement with the reinsurer (e.g., RGA, Swiss Re).']
  underwriting_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: Retention limits vary by underwriting class — companies retain more preferred risk and cede more substandard risk. The risk_class plain attribute is a denormalized underwriting class reference. Proper']
  age_band_max varchar [note: 'The maximum attained age (inclusive) of the insured for which this retention limit record applies. Combined with age_band_min to define the age band. Null indicates no upper age restriction for this limit record.']
  age_band_min varchar [note: 'The minimum attained age (inclusive) of the insured for which this retention limit record applies. Combined with age_band_max to define the age band. Retention and automatic binding limits typically vary by age due to increasing mortality risk at older ages.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this retention limit record was formally approved by the authorized actuarial or reinsurance management authority. Required for SOX compliance and internal governance documentation of limit changes.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual (typically Chief Actuary, VP Reinsurance, or CRO) who authorized this retention limit record. Supports governance, audit trail, and SOX internal control requirements.']
  cession_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this limit record governs automatic cessions (risks ceded within treaty terms without individual submission), facultative cessions (risks requiring individual reinsurer approval), or facultative-obligatory arrangements. Automatic binding limits apply only to automatic cession type.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this retention limit record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  credit_for_reinsurance_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reinsurer qualifies for credit for reinsurance under applicable state insurance law, allowing the ceding company to take a reserve credit on its statutory balance sheet. Determined by reinsurer licensure status and collateral arrangements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the limit amount is denominated (e.g., USD). Required for multi-currency treaty arrangements and international reinsurance partners.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this retention or binding limit record becomes operative and governs cession routing decisions. Aligns with treaty effective dates and policy year boundaries.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this retention or binding limit record ceases to be operative. Null indicates an open-ended limit with no scheduled expiration. Used in conjunction with effective_date to determine the applicable limit for a given valuation or cession date.']
  flat_extra_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum flat extra premium per thousand of face amount (expressed in dollars per thousand) eligible for automatic cession under this limit. Risks with flat extras exceeding this threshold require facultative submission. Null indicates no flat extra restriction.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Gender classification band to which this retention limit applies. Some treaty structures differentiate retention limits by gender due to mortality table differences. Unisex indicates the limit applies to all genders. Null indicates no gender restriction.']
  jumbo_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The absolute maximum face amount threshold above which facultative reinsurance submission is required, regardless of whether the risk falls within the automatic binding limit. Risks exceeding the jumbo limit cannot be automatically ceded and must be individually underwritten by the reinsurer. Null if this record is not of type jumbo_limit.']
  limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The principal monetary threshold value for this limit record, expressed in the applicable currency. For retention_per_life and retention_per_policy, this is the maximum net face amount retained by the company. For automatic_binding_limit, this is the maximum face amount that can be auto-ceded. For jumbo_limit, this is the threshold above which facultative submission is mandatory. Drives the cession routing decision engine.']
  limit_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this retention or binding limit configuration within the reinsurance management system (e.g., RET-WL-NS-M-01). Used as the business key for cross-system reference and bordereaux reporting.']
  limit_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about this retention limit record, including any special conditions, exceptions, or treaty-specific notes that are not captured in structured fields. Used by reinsurance analysts and actuaries for reference.']
  limit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the retention limit record. Active records drive live cession routing decisions. Superseded records have been replaced by a newer version. Expired records are past their effective period. Pending records are approved but not yet in effect.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the limit record: retention_per_life (maximum net amount retained across all policies on a single life), retention_per_policy (maximum net amount retained on a single policy), automatic_binding_limit (maximum face amount that can be automatically ceded without facultative submission), or jumbo_limit (threshold above which facultative reinsurance submission is required regardless of treaty).']
  nar_basis varchar [note: 'Specifies the basis on which the retention limit amount is measured: face_amount (total death benefit face), NAR (Net Amount at Risk — death benefit minus reserve), account_value (for UL/IUL/FIA products), or death_benefit (total contractual death benefit including riders). Drives the NAR-based cession calculation methodology.']
  per_life_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum total net amount the company will retain across all policies insuring the same life (insured). When total in-force exposure on a single life exceeds this threshold, the excess must be ceded. Critical for managing concentration risk on high-value lives.']
  policy_year_max varchar [note: 'The maximum policy year (inclusive) for which this retention limit applies. Null indicates the limit applies through the end of the policy term. Used in conjunction with policy_year_min to define the policy year band.']
  policy_year_min varchar [note: 'The minimum policy year (inclusive) for which this retention limit applies. Some treaties define different retention schedules for early policy years versus later years, particularly for YRT (Yearly Renewable Term) treaties where NAR changes over time.']
  rate_table_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the actuarial rate table used to derive the retention limit amounts (e.g., 2017CSO, 2001CSO, company-specific mortality table). Ensures traceability between limit values and the underlying actuarial assumptions used in their derivation.']
  rbc_credit_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cessions made under this retention limit qualify for RBC (Risk-Based Capital) credit under NAIC RBC formula requirements. RBC-eligible cessions reduce the company\'s required capital, making this flag critical for capital optimization and ORSA reporting.']
  smoker_status varchar [note: 'Tobacco use classification band to which this retention limit applies. Smoker and non-smoker populations carry materially different mortality rates, often resulting in distinct retention schedules. Null indicates the limit applies regardless of smoker status.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this retention limit record originated (e.g., SWISS_RE_IRIS, RGA_AURA, INTERNAL_TREATY_MGR). Supports data lineage tracking and ETL reconciliation in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  source_system_limit_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier or reference number assigned to this retention limit record in the originating operational system (e.g., Swiss Re IRIS internal limit ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and traceability from the silver layer back to the source system.']
  table_rating_max varchar [note: 'The maximum substandard table rating (expressed as a letter A–P or numeric percentage, e.g., Table D or 150%) eligible for automatic cession under this limit. Risks with table ratings exceeding this threshold require facultative submission regardless of face amount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this retention limit record was last modified. Tracks changes to limit amounts, effective dates, or status. Supports audit trail and change management requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this retention limit record. Incremented each time the limit is revised. Enables tracking of limit changes over time and supports point-in-time queries for historical cession routing validation and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Consolidated reference table defining Life Insurance\'s net retention limits and automatic binding limits by treaty, plan code, risk class, age band, and policy year. Captures limit type (retention per life, retention per policy, automatic binding limit, jumbo limit), limit amount, effective date, and applicable treaty. Includes automatic binding limits per treaty — the maximum face amount that can be automatically ceded without facultative submission — with age/sex/risk class bands and jumbo thresholds. Drives the cession routing decision: risks within retention are kept, risks within automatic binding limits are auto-ceded, and risks exceeding jumbo limits require facultative submission. This is the single source of truth for all retention and automatic binding limit reference data. Supports NAR-based cession calculations and treaty compliance.'
}

Table actuarial.reserve_calculation {
  reserve_calculation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the reserve calculation record. Primary key for this entity.']
  annuitant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.annuitant. Business justification: Annuity reserve calculations require the annuitant\'s age, payout option, benefit base, and GMWB/GMIB indicators to compute longevity reserves. The existing annuity_contract_id does not provide direct ']
  assumption_set_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the actuarial assumption set (mortality, lapse, expense, interest) applied in this reserve calculation.']
  cohort_definition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policy cohort definition used for grouped reserve calculations. Cohorts typically group policies by issue year, product type, and underwriting class.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Statutory and GAAP reserves are calculated at the individual annuity contract level. Essential for financial reporting, regulatory filings (Annual Statement), PBR compliance, and reconciliation of agg']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual policy or contract for which reserves are calculated. May be null for cohort-level or model-segment-level calculations.']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured. Business justification: Reserve calculations require individual insured characteristics (issue age, attained age, gender, tobacco status, risk class) for accurate mortality and lapse assumptions. Actuaries calculate reserves']
  mortality_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.mortality_table. Business justification: reserve_calculation.mortality_assumption_table is a STRING column holding a denormalized reference to the mortality table used in the calculation. Replacing it with a FK mortality_table_id → actuarial']
  pbr_model_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the PBR model segment used for VM-20 deterministic and stochastic reserve calculations. Segments group policies by similar risk characteristics.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Reserve calculations require direct product plan reference for applying product-specific reserve basis rules (statutory/GAAP/IFRS17), PBR model segment assignment, and assumption set selection. Critic']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Reserves are calculated against a specific plan version — PBR applicability, benefit structures, and reserve basis change with plan version. Statutory reserve reporting and actuarial opinion require v']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: PBR (VM-20) and asset adequacy testing require identifying the investment portfolio backing each reserve calculation to apply correct earned rate assumptions and RBC C-3 interest rate risk charges. Ac']
  premium_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_schedule. Business justification: Net premium reserve calculations under SAP/GAAP require the gross premium, limited pay years, and premium paying period from the billing premium_schedule. Actuarial reserve systems must reference the ']
  scenario_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.scenario_set. Business justification: reserve_calculation captures stochastic_reserve_amount and stochastic_scenario_count, indicating it is computed against a set of stochastic scenarios. Adding scenario_set_id normalizes the reference t']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent actuarial valuation run that produced this reserve calculation. Links to the valuation cycle execution (e.g., Q4 2023 year-end valuation).']
  actuary_name varchar [note: 'The name of the qualified actuary responsible for this reserve calculation. Required for statutory opinion and regulatory compliance.']
  assumption_unlock_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The change in reserves due to updates or unlocking of actuarial assumptions (mortality, lapse, expense, interest). Required under LDTI for GAAP reporting.']
  boy_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The reserve balance at the beginning of the valuation period (typically January 1 for annual valuations). Used for reserve rollforward analysis.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'The actuarial method or formula used for this reserve calculation (e.g., Commissioners Reserve Valuation Method, Gross Premium Valuation, Seriatim Projection, Model Office). Free-text field for method documentation.']
  calculation_status varchar [note: 'The current status of this reserve calculation in the valuation workflow. Tracks progression from draft through final approval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this reserve calculation record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  cte_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'The CTE confidence level used for stochastic reserve calculations under VM-20 (e.g., 70 for CTE70, 98 for CTE98). Represents the average of the worst X% of scenarios.']
  deterministic_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The deterministic reserve calculated under VM-20 using prescribed scenarios. One of the three components (DR, SR, NPR) used to determine the final PBR reserve.']
  discount_rate decimal(8,5) [note: 'The interest rate used to discount future cash flows in the reserve calculation. May be a statutory rate, GAAP upper-medium grade rate, or IFRS discount curve rate depending on reporting basis.']
  eoy_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The reserve balance at the end of the valuation period (typically December 31 for annual valuations). Represents the closing liability position.']
  expense_assumption_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The per-policy or per-unit expense assumption used in the reserve calculation. Includes maintenance expenses and overhead allocation.']
  experience_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The change in reserves due to actual experience differing from expected (e.g., actual mortality vs. expected mortality). Component of reserve variance analysis.']
  interest_accretion_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The increase in reserves due to interest accretion at the valuation interest rate during the period. Component of the reserve rollforward.']
  lapse_assumption_rate decimal(8,5) [note: 'The annual lapse rate assumption applied in the reserve calculation. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5% lapse rate).']
  model_change_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The change in reserves due to changes in the actuarial valuation model, methodology, or system (e.g., migration from AXIS to Prophet). Tracked separately for audit and reconciliation.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the actuarial model used for this calculation. Critical for model governance, reproducibility, and audit.']
  net_amount_at_risk decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount at risk, calculated as death benefit minus account value or reserve. Represents the insurer\'s exposure to mortality risk.']
  net_premium_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net premium reserve calculated using traditional actuarial methods. One of the three components (DR, SR, NPR) used to determine the final PBR reserve, and serves as the floor.']
  new_business_reserve_change decimal(18,2) [note: 'The change in reserves attributable to new business issued during the valuation period. Component of the reserve rollforward.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for actuarial notes, assumptions, methodology details, or special considerations for this reserve calculation. Used for documentation and audit trail.']
  pbr_floor_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the NPR floor was applied in the final PBR reserve calculation. True if the NPR exceeded the greater of DR and SR.']
  reinsurance_ceded_reserve decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the reserve that has been ceded to reinsurers under reinsurance treaties. Reduces the net reserve liability for the direct writer.']
  reinsurance_recoverable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount recoverable from reinsurers for this reserve. Represents an asset on the balance sheet offsetting the gross reserve liability.']
  reporting_basis varchar [note: 'The accounting or regulatory basis under which this reserve is calculated: GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), SAP (Statutory Accounting Principles), IFRS 17 (International Financial Reporting Standards), PBR (Principle-Based Reserving per VM-20), or Tax basis.']
  reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated reserve amount in the reporting currency. This is the primary liability value for the policy, cohort, or model segment as of the valuation date.']
  reserve_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the reserve amount (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP).']
  reserve_per_unit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The reserve amount per unit of coverage (e.g., per $1,000 of face amount). Used for cohort-level and model-segment-level calculations.']
  reserve_type varchar [note: 'The specific type of reserve calculated: NPR (Net Premium Reserve), GPR (Gross Premium Reserve), IBNR (Incurred But Not Reported), DR (Deterministic Reserve per VM-20), SR (Stochastic Reserve per VM-20), LFPB (Liability for Future Policy Benefits under LDTI), CSV (Cash Surrender Value), DAC (Deferred Acquisition Cost), PVFB (Present Value of Future Benefits). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NPR|GPR|IBNR|DR|SR|LFPB|CSV|DAC|PVFB — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  review_date date [note: 'The date this reserve calculation was reviewed and approved by the qualified actuary or peer reviewer.']
  stochastic_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The stochastic reserve calculated under VM-20 using CTE methodology. One of the three components (DR, SR, NPR) used to determine the final PBR reserve.']
  stochastic_scenario_count varchar [note: 'The number of stochastic scenarios run for VM-20 stochastic reserve (SR) calculations. Typically 1,000 to 10,000 scenarios depending on model complexity.']
  termination_reserve_change decimal(18,2) [note: 'The change in reserves due to policy terminations (deaths, lapses, surrenders, maturities) during the valuation period. Typically a negative value.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this reserve calculation record was last modified. Audit trail field for tracking changes.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The as-of date for this reserve calculation. Typically end-of-year (EOY) or end-of-quarter (EOQ) for statutory and GAAP reporting.']

  Note: 'Core actuarial reserve calculation records capturing GAAP, SAP, and IFRS 17 reserve amounts computed per policy cohort or model segment. Stores net premium reserve (NPR), gross premium reserve (GPR), IBNR, PBR deterministic reserve (DR), stochastic reserve (SR), and LDTI liability for future policy benefits (LFPB). Includes point-in-time snapshot data: BOY and EOY reserve balances, net amount at risk (NAR), reserve per unit, and reserve change components (new business, terminations, interest accretion, assumption unlocking). Each record is linked to a parent valuation_run, assumption_set, cohort_definition, and pbr_model_segment. Sourced from Prophet/AXIS/MoSes valuation runs. Represents the primary output of the actuarial valuation cycle for statutory annual statement, GAAP financial close, and IFRS 17 reporting.'
}

Table actuarial.valuation_run {
  valuation_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each actuarial valuation model execution. Primary key for the valuation run record.']
  actuarial_opinion_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Valuation runs generate actuarial opinions, reserve certifications, and regulatory memoranda required for statutory filings and regulatory examinations. The signed opinion document is the primary deli']
  assumption_set_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific set of actuarial assumptions (mortality, lapse, expense, interest rates) applied in this valuation run.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Valuation runs are scheduled and mandated by specific regulatory obligations (e.g., annual statutory valuation, quarterly LDTI). Compliance tracks which regulatory_obligation triggers each valuation_r']
  scenario_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.scenario_set. Business justification: valuation_run.scenario_count (INT) is an aggregate count of scenarios used in the run, but there is no FK to the scenario_set that defines those scenarios. Adding scenario_set_id → actuarial.scenario_']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the valuation run results were formally approved by the appointed actuary.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this valuation run to detailed audit logs, change control records, and regulatory filing documentation.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for actuaries to document special considerations, assumption changes, methodology adjustments, or other relevant notes about this valuation run.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this valuation run record was first created in the actuarial data warehouse.']
  dac_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total Deferred Acquisition Cost asset balance calculated in this valuation run, representing unamortized acquisition expenses under GAAP.']
  data_extract_date date [note: 'The date when in-force policy data was extracted from the policy administration system for use in this valuation run.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The policy administration or data warehouse system from which in-force policy data was sourced (e.g., \'FAST\', \'Sapiens LifePro\', \'Oracle OIPA\', \'Enterprise Data Lake\').']
  discount_rate_method varchar [note: 'The method used to determine discount rates for present value calculations (e.g., \'Net Portfolio Rate\', \'Risk-Free Rate + Spread\', \'Prescribed Statutory Rate\').']
  error_log_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of errors, warnings, or data quality issues encountered during the valuation run execution, used for troubleshooting and audit trails.']
  in_force_policy_count varchar [note: 'Total number of in-force policies included in the valuation run as of the valuation date.']
  ldti_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GAAP valuation run follows the Long Duration Targeted Improvements standard (ASU 2018-12) effective for fiscal years beginning after December 15, 2022.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the actuarial model used for this run, enabling traceability and reproducibility of valuation results.']
  mortality_table varchar [note: 'The mortality table applied in this valuation (e.g., \'2017 CSO\', \'2012 IAM Basic\', company-specific experience table).']
  output_file_path varchar [note: 'File system or cloud storage path where detailed valuation output files (cash flows, seriatim results, summary reports) are stored.']
  pbr_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this valuation run follows Principle-Based Reserving methodology per NAIC Valuation Manual VM-20 and VM-21 (True) or uses formula-based reserves (False).']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the reconciliation process between valuation results and general ledger or prior period reserves.']
  reinsurance_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether reinsurance ceded and assumed treaties are reflected in the reserve calculations (True) or if this is a gross reserve run (False).']
  run_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in minutes for the valuation model execution, used for performance monitoring and capacity planning.']
  run_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the valuation model execution completed (successfully or with errors).']
  run_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name assigned to the valuation run for identification purposes, often including valuation basis and period (e.g., \'Q4 2023 GAAP Statutory Reserve Calculation\').']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing identifier for the valuation run, typically formatted as YYYY-MM-RUN### for external reference and audit trails.']
  run_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the valuation model execution was initiated.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current execution and approval status of the valuation run in its lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Initiated|Running|Completed|Failed|Cancelled|Under Review|Approved — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the valuation run purpose: Production (official reserves), Test (validation), Pricing (product development), Sensitivity (scenario analysis), Experience Study (assumption review), or Ad Hoc (special analysis).']
  scenario_count varchar [note: 'Number of stochastic economic scenarios executed in this valuation run for PBR or IFRS calculations (typically 1 for deterministic, 1000+ for stochastic).']
  total_account_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate account value across all universal life and annuity contracts included in the valuation, representing policyholder fund balances.']
  total_face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate death benefit face amount across all policies included in the valuation, expressed in the reporting currency.']
  total_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate reserve liability calculated by this valuation run, representing the present value of future policy benefits and expenses.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this valuation run record was last modified, supporting audit trails and data lineage.']
  valuation_basis varchar [note: 'The accounting or regulatory framework under which reserves are calculated: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP), International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), Tax basis, Principle-Based Reserving (PBR), or Embedded Value.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The financial reporting date for which reserves and liabilities are being calculated (e.g., December 31, 2023 for year-end valuation).']
  valuation_period_type varchar [note: 'The reporting period classification: Beginning of Year (BOY), End of Year (EOY), Quarterly, Monthly, or Interim valuation snapshot.']
  variance_from_prior_period decimal(18,2) [note: 'The change in total reserve amount compared to the previous valuation period, used for reserve movement analysis and financial reporting.']
  voba_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total Value of Business Acquired intangible asset balance for acquired blocks of business, amortized over the life of the policies.']

  Note: 'Master record for each actuarial valuation model execution in Prophet, AXIS, or MoSes. Captures run date, valuation basis (BOY/EOY/interim), model version, assumption set used, run type (production/test/pricing), and completion status. Serves as the parent record linking all reserve calculations, cash flow projections, and experience study outputs to a specific model run.'
}

Table actuarial.assumption_set {
  assumption_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the actuarial assumption set. Primary key.']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Assumption sets require formal peer review documentation and actuarial sign-off per ASOP standards. The existing documentation_url is a denormalized text reference; replacing it with a proper FK to do']
