  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Mortgage loans are Schedule B assets owned by specific legal entities, required for NAIC Annual Statement reporting and RBC C1 asset risk calculations.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment portfolio that holds this mortgage loan asset. Links to the general account portfolio structure.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when the insurer acquired the mortgage loan investment. May differ from origination date if purchased on secondary market.']
  acquisition_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Purchase price paid by the insurer to acquire the mortgage loan. Used for premium/discount amortization and return calculations.']
  allowance_for_credit_losses decimal(18,2) [note: 'GAAP allowance for expected credit losses under ASC 326 CECL. Reduces the carrying value of the mortgage loan on the balance sheet.']
  amortization_type varchar [note: 'Structure of principal repayment over the loan term. Fully amortizing loans pay down to zero; balloon loans require lump sum at maturity; interest-only defer principal.']
  amortized_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amortized cost basis of the mortgage loan under Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP). Reflects purchase price adjusted for premium/discount amortization and credit losses.']
  appraisal_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent property appraisal. Regulatory guidelines require periodic reappraisals for commercial mortgages.']
  appraisal_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Most recent appraised Fair Market Value (FMV) of the property securing the mortgage loan. Used for LTV ratio calculations and impairment testing.']
  book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Carrying value of the mortgage loan on the general account balance sheet under Statutory Accounting Principles (SAP). May differ from outstanding principal due to premium/discount amortization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this mortgage loan record was first created in the investment management system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts. Supports international mortgage investments and foreign exchange risk management.']
  dscr decimal(5,2) [note: 'Debt Service Coverage Ratio for commercial mortgage loans. Measures the property\'s net operating income relative to debt service obligations. Key underwriting and risk metric.']
  impairment_status varchar [note: 'Current impairment classification under GAAP ASC 326 Current Expected Credit Loss (CECL) or SAP guidance. Drives allowance for credit losses and income recognition.']
  interest_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Current annual interest rate on the mortgage loan expressed as a decimal percentage. Used for income accrual and Asset Liability Management (ALM) duration calculations.']
  interest_rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the interest rate structure. Fixed rates remain constant; variable/adjustable rates change based on index; hybrid combines both.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received from the borrower. Used to calculate delinquency status and days past due.']
  loan_number varchar [note: 'External loan number or identifier assigned by the originator or servicer. Business identifier for the mortgage loan.']
  loan_status varchar [note: 'Current payment and performance status of the mortgage loan. Delinquency stages track days past due. Real Estate Owned (REO) indicates foreclosed property. Drives reserve requirements and RBC charges. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: current|delinquent_30|delinquent_60|delinquent_90|in_foreclosure|reo|paid_off — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ltv_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'Loan-to-Value ratio expressed as a percentage. Calculated as outstanding principal balance divided by current appraised property value. Critical risk metric for NAIC Schedule B classification and RBC charges.']
  maturity_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the mortgage loan principal is due in full. Critical for Asset Liability Management (ALM) duration matching and cash flow projections.']
  naic_designation varchar [note: 'NAIC credit quality designation for the mortgage loan ranging from 1 (highest quality) to 6 (in or near default). Determines RBC C-1 risk charge factors.']
  naic_schedule_b_line varchar [note: 'Specific line item classification on NAIC Schedule B annual statement where this mortgage loan is reported. Supports regulatory financial reporting.']
  next_payment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next principal and interest payment. Used for cash flow forecasting and delinquency monitoring.']
  original_loan_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Principal amount of the mortgage loan at origination. Used for Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio calculations and historical tracking.']
  origination_date date [note: 'Date when the mortgage loan was originally funded and the note was executed. Used for age-based risk analysis and amortization calculations.']
  outstanding_principal_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current unpaid principal balance of the mortgage loan. Key metric for Risk-Based Capital (RBC) C-1 mortgage risk charge calculations.']
  prepayment_penalty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the mortgage loan includes prepayment penalties. True if penalties apply; False if borrower can prepay without penalty. Impacts ALM prepayment risk modeling.']
  property_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address for the property securing the mortgage loan.']
  property_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address for the property securing the mortgage loan (suite, unit, building).']
  property_city varchar [note: 'City where the property securing the mortgage loan is located.']
  property_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the property location. Supports international mortgage investments.']
  property_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code or ZIP code for the property securing the mortgage loan.']
  property_state varchar [note: 'State or province where the property securing the mortgage loan is located. Two-letter state code.']
  property_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the property securing the mortgage loan. Determines risk assessment and regulatory capital treatment.']
  rbc_c1_charge decimal(18,2) [note: 'Risk-Based Capital charge for asset default risk (C-1 component) calculated for this mortgage loan. Based on NAIC designation and property type. Critical for solvency monitoring.']
  rbc_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'RBC factor percentage applied to the outstanding balance to calculate the C-1 risk charge. Varies by NAIC designation and property type.']
  recourse_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the loan is recourse or non-recourse. True if lender has recourse to borrower assets beyond collateral; False if limited to property collateral only. Affects credit risk assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this mortgage loan record. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record for commercial and residential mortgage loans held as invested assets in the general account. Captures loan number, property type (commercial, residential, agricultural), property address, original loan amount, outstanding balance, interest rate, loan-to-value (LTV) ratio, debt service coverage ratio (DSCR), origination date, maturity date, amortization type, loan status (current, delinquent, in foreclosure, REO), NAIC Schedule B classification, and appraisal value. Supports NAIC Schedule B reporting and RBC C-1 mortgage risk charges.'
}

Table investment.derivative_contract {
  derivative_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the derivative contract record. Primary key.']
  benefit_structure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.benefit_structure. Business justification: Derivative contracts hedge specific guaranteed benefit structures (GMDB, GMWB, GMIB). derivative_contract already has gmdb_hedge_flag and gmwb_hedge_flag but no FK to the benefit structure being hedge']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Derivatives (Schedule DB) are held by specific legal entities for regulatory reporting, collateral management, RBC calculations, and hedge accounting documentation.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Derivatives hedge product-specific risks: equity index options for GMDB/GMWB guarantees on variable annuities, interest rate swaps for fixed annuity crediting rates, and currency forwards for internat']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment portfolio that holds this derivative position.']
  primary_derivative_broker_dealer_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broker-dealer that facilitated the derivative transaction, if applicable.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Derivative contracts for GMDB/GMWB hedging are subject to specific regulatory obligations: NAIC Schedule DB reporting, state insurance derivative use plan requirements, and Dodd-Frank clearing obligat']
  trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.trade. Business justification: Derivative contracts are initiated and executed through the trade lifecycle. A derivative_contract should reference the originating trade record that captured the order, execution, and settlement deta']
  underlying_security_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.security. Business justification: Derivative contracts reference underlying securities (stocks, bonds, indices). The existing underlying_cusip attribute identifies the underlying but lacks FK to security master. Adding underlying_secu']
  alm_segment varchar [note: 'Asset Liability Management segment or bucket to which this derivative is assigned for duration matching and interest rate risk management purposes.']
  collateral_posted decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of collateral posted by the company to the counterparty to secure the derivative obligation.']
  collateral_received decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of collateral received from the counterparty to secure their derivative obligation to the company.']
  collateral_type varchar [note: 'Type of collateral used to secure the derivative contract obligations.']
  contract_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special terms related to the derivative contract.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the derivative contract, typically assigned by the investment management system or counterparty.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the derivative contract indicating whether it is active, matured, terminated, cancelled, or pending execution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this derivative contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the notional amount and contract payments.']
  current_fmv decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current fair market value of the derivative contract as of the most recent valuation date, representing the amount that would be received or paid to terminate the contract.']
  derivative_subtype varchar [note: 'Further classification of the derivative instrument providing additional detail beyond the primary type (e.g., payer swap, receiver swap, call option, put option).']
  derivative_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the derivative instrument type used for hedging or replication purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: interest_rate_swap|swaption|cap|floor|collar|credit_default_swap|equity_option|currency_forward|futures — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the derivative contract becomes effective and obligations begin.']
  fair_value_hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the fair value measurement input level under GAAP (Level 1: quoted prices, Level 2: observable inputs, Level 3: unobservable inputs).']
  floating_rate_index varchar [note: 'The benchmark interest rate index used for floating rate calculations (e.g., SOFR, LIBOR, Fed Funds Rate).']
  gmdb_hedge_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this derivative is used to hedge Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit exposure on variable annuity contracts.']
  gmwb_hedge_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this derivative is used to hedge Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit exposure on variable annuity contracts.']
  hedge_designation varchar [note: 'Accounting classification of the hedge relationship under GAAP or SAP, indicating whether the derivative qualifies for hedge accounting treatment.']
  hedge_effectiveness_test_result varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent hedge effectiveness assessment, determining whether the derivative continues to qualify for hedge accounting.']
  isda_master_agreement_date date [note: 'The effective date of the ISDA Master Agreement governing the derivative contract terms and conditions.']
  isda_master_agreement_version varchar [note: 'Version of the ISDA Master Agreement under which the derivative contract is documented (e.g., 1992, 2002).']
  maturity_date date [note: 'The date on which the derivative contract expires or reaches final settlement.']
  naic_designation varchar [note: 'NAIC quality rating designation for the derivative contract or underlying reference entity, used for regulatory capital calculations.']
  notional_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The principal amount upon which derivative payments are calculated. This amount is not exchanged but serves as the basis for calculating cash flows.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which cash flows or settlements occur under the derivative contract.']
  rbc_category varchar [note: 'Risk-Based Capital category classification for the derivative contract used in calculating C-1, C-2, or C-3 risk charges.']
  rbc_charge decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated Risk-Based Capital charge amount for this derivative position, contributing to the company\'s total RBC requirement.']
  schedule_db_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this derivative contract must be reported on NAIC Schedule DB (Derivative Instruments) in the Annual Statement.']
  separate_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this derivative is held in a separate account (variable product account) versus the general account.']
  strike_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The fixed interest rate, strike price, or reference rate specified in the derivative contract (e.g., fixed rate in an interest rate swap, strike price in an option).']
  termination_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the derivative contract was terminated or closed out prior to maturity, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for early termination of the derivative contract (e.g., hedge no longer needed, counterparty credit concerns, portfolio rebalancing).']
  trade_date date [note: 'The date on which the derivative contract was executed and agreed upon by both parties.']
  underlying_reference varchar [note: 'Description or identifier of the underlying asset, index, rate, or reference entity upon which the derivative value is based (e.g., LIBOR, S&P 500, specific bond CUSIP).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this derivative contract record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master record for derivative instruments used for hedging or replication purposes including interest rate swaps, swaptions, caps/floors, credit default swaps, equity options, and currency forwards. Captures derivative type, notional amount, counterparty, trade date, effective date, maturity date, hedge designation (fair value hedge, cash flow hedge, economic hedge), underlying reference, strike/fixed rate, current FMV, collateral posted/received, and ISDA master agreement reference. Supports ALM hedging programs, GMDB/GMWB hedge accounting, and NAIC derivative reporting.'
}

Table investment.counterparty {
  counterparty_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the investment counterparty. Primary key for the counterparty master record.']
  affiliated_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Investment counterparties may be affiliated legal entities requiring intercompany elimination, related-party disclosure, and consolidation adjustments for financial statement preparation.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the counterparty\'s registered business address. Used for legal correspondence and regulatory reporting.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the counterparty\'s registered business address (suite, floor, building name).']
  aml_kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of AML and KYC due diligence for this counterparty. Must be compliant before executing transactions.']
  approved_asset_classes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of asset classes approved for trading with this counterparty (e.g., fixed_income, equity, derivatives, alternatives). Used for pre-trade compliance checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fixed_income|equity|derivatives|alternatives|real_estate|commodities|structured_products — promote to reference product]']
  approved_trading_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate exposure amount approved for trading with this counterparty. Enforced by pre-trade compliance checks to manage counterparty concentration risk.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the counterparty\'s registered business address.']
  counterparty_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the counterparty relationship. Controls whether new trades and transactions can be executed with this counterparty.']
  counterparty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the counterparty role in investment operations. Determines applicable compliance rules, credit limits, and operational workflows.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the counterparty\'s domicile. Used for regulatory reporting and cross-border risk assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this counterparty record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Current credit rating assigned by external rating agencies (e.g., Moody\'s, S&P, Fitch). Used for counterparty risk assessment and RBC calculations.']
  csa_type varchar [note: 'Type of Credit Support Annex governing collateral arrangements for derivative transactions. Bilateral requires both parties to post collateral; unilateral requires only one party.']
  custodian_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number assigned by the custodian bank for holding securities. Applicable when counterparty_type is custodian_bank or sub_custodian.']
  fatca_status varchar [note: 'FATCA classification status for foreign counterparties. Determines withholding and reporting obligations under US tax law.']
  finra_registration_number varchar [note: 'FINRA Central Registration Depository (CRD) number for broker-dealers. Required for broker-dealer counterparties.']
  form_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the W-8 or W-9 form on file. W-8 forms typically expire after three years; W-9 forms do not expire but require updates when information changes.']
  isda_agreement_date date [note: 'The date when the ISDA Master Agreement was executed. Null if no ISDA agreement exists.']
  isda_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an ISDA Master Agreement is in place with this counterparty. Required for derivative and swap transactions.']
  kyc_next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next KYC review. Typically annual or risk-based frequency.']
  kyc_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent KYC review. Used to trigger periodic re-verification workflows per regulatory requirements.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the counterparty as registered with regulatory authorities. Used for legal documentation, contracts, and regulatory reporting.']
  lei varchar [note: 'ISO 17442 standard 20-character Legal Entity Identifier used for global counterparty identification in financial transactions and regulatory reporting.']
  limit_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the approved trading limit. Typically USD for US-domiciled insurers.']
  naic_designation_override varchar [note: 'Manual NAIC designation override applied to securities issued by this counterparty. Used when SVO designation differs from external ratings.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional information about the counterparty, special handling instructions, or historical context.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the counterparty was approved and onboarded for trading. Marks the start of the business relationship.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the counterparty\'s registered business address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person. Used for trade confirmations, settlement instructions, and operational communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the counterparty organization for operational and trading matters.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact person for urgent operational matters and trade execution.']
  rating_agency varchar [note: 'The rating agency that provided the credit rating. Multiple agencies may rate the same counterparty; this field captures the primary rating source used for risk decisions.']
  rating_date date [note: 'The date when the current credit rating became effective. Used to track rating staleness and trigger review workflows.']
  rbc_concentration_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'RBC concentration adjustment factor applied to positions with this counterparty. Higher factors increase capital charges for concentrated exposures.']
  sec_registration_number varchar [note: 'SEC registration number for investment advisers, broker-dealers, or fund managers. Used to verify regulatory standing.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or common name used for operational purposes and system displays.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the counterparty\'s registered business address. Two-letter state code for US addresses.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (EIN for US entities, equivalent for foreign entities). Used for tax reporting and IRS Form 1099 generation.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the counterparty relationship was terminated. Null for active relationships.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for terminating the counterparty relationship (e.g., credit downgrade, regulatory action, business decision, counterparty request).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this counterparty record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  w8_w9_form_on_file_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a valid IRS Form W-8 (for foreign entities) or W-9 (for US entities) is on file. Required for tax withholding compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record for all investment counterparties including broker-dealers, swap counterparties, custodian banks, fund managers, securities lending agents, and sub-custodians. Captures counterparty legal name, LEI (Legal Entity Identifier), counterparty type, credit rating, ISDA agreement reference, approved trading limits, collateral agreement type (CSA), custodian account details, AML/KYC status, regulatory registration (FINRA, SEC), and relationship manager. SSOT for investment counterparty identity within the investment domain; distinct from policyholder or agent counterparties in other domains.'
}

Table agent.producer {
  producer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the producer. Primary key for the producer entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Producers generate commission expenses and overhead costs that must be allocated to cost centers for distribution channel profitability analysis, expense management, and producer P&L reporting—standar']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Producers are contracted to specific legal entities for commission payment processing, 1099 tax reporting, state licensing compliance, and statutory financial reporting—fundamental requirement for pro']
  parent_producer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent producer in a multi-level distribution hierarchy. Used to model upline relationships for MGAs, GAs, and agency hierarchies.']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.schedule. Business justification: Producer compensation setup requires knowing which commission schedule governs their payments. The existing commission_schedule_code is a denormalized reference; a proper FK to commission.schedule ena']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the producer\'s primary business or residential address.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the producer\'s address for suite, apartment, or unit information.']
  annual_production_target decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target annual premium production amount assigned to the producer for performance tracking and incentive qualification.']
  background_check_date date [note: 'Date the producer\'s background check was completed.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the producer\'s background check during onboarding. Used for compliance and risk assessment.']
  business_name varchar [note: 'Legal business name or DBA name for agency entities such as BGAs, MGAs, GAs, or broker-dealer firms.']
  city varchar [note: 'City name for the producer\'s primary address.']
  contracting_status varchar [note: 'Status of the producer\'s contractual agreement with the carrier. Indicates whether the producer has a valid, signed contract in place.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the producer\'s primary address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the producer record was first created in the system.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth for individual producers. Used for identity verification and regulatory compliance.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for producer communication, commission statements, and regulatory notices.']
  eo_coverage_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the producer has active Errors and Omissions insurance coverage. Required for compliance and risk management.']
  eo_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the producer\'s Errors and Omissions insurance policy. Monitored for compliance.']
  eo_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number for the producer\'s Errors and Omissions insurance coverage.']
  finra_crd_number varchar [note: 'FINRA-assigned identifier for broker-dealers and registered representatives authorized to sell variable life insurance and annuity products.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the producer if the producer is an individual person.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the distribution hierarchy. Level 1 is top-level (e.g., MGA), higher numbers represent downline agents.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the producer was hired or appointed by the carrier. Marks the start of the producer\'s relationship with the company.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Know Your Customer verification process for the producer. Required for AML compliance.']
  kyc_verification_date date [note: 'Date the producer\'s KYC verification was completed.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name or surname of the producer if the producer is an individual person.']
  lifetime_production_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative total premium production amount for the producer since hire date.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the producer if the producer is an individual person.']
  npn varchar [note: 'Unique 10-digit identifier assigned by the NAIC to licensed insurance producers. Used for multi-state licensing and regulatory tracking.']
  onboarding_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the producer onboarding workflow. Tracks progress from initial application through final approval. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: application_submitted|background_check|licensing_verification|contract_review|training|approved|rejected — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the producer.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code or ZIP+4 code for the producer\'s primary address.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO language code for the producer\'s preferred communication language.']
  producer_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the producer for operational use. Externally-known unique code used in commission systems and policy administration.']
  producer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the producer\'s relationship with the carrier. Active (currently writing business), Inactive (not writing but relationship intact), Suspended (temporarily restricted), Terminated (relationship ended), Pending (onboarding in progress).']
  producer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the producer\'s distribution channel role. Captive Agent (exclusive to one carrier), Independent Agent (represents multiple carriers), BGA (Brokerage General Agency), MGA (Managing General Agent), GA (General Agent), Broker-Dealer (securities-licensed for variable products).']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or territory code for the producer\'s primary address.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Federal tax identification number for the producer. May be an Employer Identification Number (EIN) for entities or Social Security Number (SSN) for individuals. Used for IRS Form 1099 reporting.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the producer\'s appointment or contract with the carrier was terminated. Null if the producer is still active.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the producer\'s termination. Required for regulatory reporting and compliance tracking.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date the producer completed required onboarding and product training.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the producer record was last modified.']
  ytd_production_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total premium production amount for the producer in the current calendar year. Updated as new business is issued.']

  Note: 'Master record for all distribution channel participants — captive agents, independent agents, BGAs, MGAs, GAs, and broker-dealers. Serves as the SSOT for producer identity, demographic profile, tax identification (TIN/SSN), producer type classification, contracting status, onboarding stage, and channel affiliation. Sourced from the Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission / Annocera).'
}

Table agent.license {
  license_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the insurance producer license record. Primary key.']
  certificate_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: State-issued license certificates and renewal confirmations must be retained for regulatory compliance. Appointment processing and DOI audits require production of license certificates. Links license ']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent or producer who holds this license.']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Producer license CE requirements, renewal cycles, and line-of-authority rules are defined by state_regulation. Compliance teams validate license CE compliance and renewal eligibility against state reg']
  appointment_date date [note: 'Date when the producer was appointed by the insurance carrier to sell products in the issuing state.']
  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the producer\'s appointment with the insurance carrier in the issuing state.']
  background_check_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent background check was completed for the producer as part of licensing or renewal requirements.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent background check for the producer.']
  ce_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the producer has met the continuing education requirements for the current renewal cycle.']
  ce_hours_completed decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of continuing education hours completed by the producer toward the current renewal cycle.']
  ce_hours_required decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of continuing education hours required by the issuing state for license renewal.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this license record was first created in the system.']
  doi_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation number issued by the state Department of Insurance (DOI) upon successful renewal submission.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the license became active and the producer was authorized to begin selling insurance products in the issuing state.']
  eo_coverage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Errors and Omissions (E&O) insurance coverage is required for this license by the issuing state.']
  eo_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the producer\'s Errors and Omissions (E&O) insurance coverage.']
  eo_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number of the Errors and Omissions (E&O) insurance coverage maintained by the producer.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the license expires and must be renewed to maintain active status. Nullable for licenses with indefinite terms.']
  finra_crd_number varchar [note: 'FINRA Central Registration Depository (CRD) number for producers authorized to sell variable products.']
  finra_registration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FINRA registration is required for this license to sell variable life insurance or variable annuity products.']
  issuing_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or territory code of the Department of Insurance (DOI) that issued this license. Supports all 50 US states plus territories.']
  last_renewal_date date [note: 'Date when the license was most recently renewed by the producer.']
  late_penalty_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty amount assessed for late license renewal submission.']
  late_renewal_penalty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a late renewal penalty was assessed due to renewal submission after the due date.']
  license_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the license based on the producer\'s residency status in the issuing state.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued insurance producer license number assigned by the Department of Insurance (DOI). This is the externally-known unique identifier for the license.']
  license_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the license indicating whether the producer is authorized to sell insurance products.']
  line_of_authority varchar [note: 'The insurance product line(s) the producer is authorized to sell under this license (e.g., life, health, variable products).']
  next_renewal_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the producer must submit renewal application and fees to maintain active license status.']
  nipr_number varchar [note: 'National Insurance Producer Registry (NIPR) number used for multi-state license management and reciprocity.']
  original_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the license was first issued to the producer in the issuing state, prior to any renewals.']
  reciprocal_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code of the producer\'s home state when this is a non-resident reciprocal license.']
  renewal_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months in the license renewal cycle (typically 12, 24, or 36 months depending on state requirements).']
  renewal_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee amount required by the issuing state Department of Insurance (DOI) for license renewal.']
  renewal_fee_paid_date date [note: 'Date when the renewal fee was paid to the issuing state Department of Insurance (DOI).']
  renewal_reminder_sent_date date [note: 'Date when the automated renewal reminder notification was sent to the producer.']
  renewal_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the license renewal application was submitted to the issuing state Department of Insurance (DOI).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the license or appointment was terminated, suspended, or revoked.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for license or appointment termination, suspension, or revocation (e.g., voluntary surrender, regulatory action, non-renewal).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this license record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'State-issued insurance producer license records tracking license number, line of authority (life, health, variable), issuing state DOI, license class, effective date, expiration date, and active/inactive status. Includes renewal lifecycle tracking: CE hours completed vs. required, renewal submission dates, fee payment status, state DOI submission confirmation, renewed expiration dates, and late renewal penalty flags. Supports multi-state licensing across all 50 US states and territories per NAIC Producer Licensing Model Act requirements. Provides automated renewal reminders and CE compliance monitoring across multi-state license portfolios.'
}

Table agent.appointment {
  appointment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier appointment record.']
  appointment_producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed producer being appointed.']
  appointment_upline_agent_producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervising or sponsoring agent in the distribution hierarchy, if applicable. Used for commission rollup and override calculations.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Appointment filing fees and compliance costs are tracked against cost centers for expense allocation and DAC capitalization of agent acquisition costs. Finance requires cost center attribution for all']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Appointment letters, state filing confirmations, and termination notices are regulatory documents requiring retention. State DOI audits and producer disputes require production of appointment document']
  jurisdiction_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_license. Business justification: An appointment in a state is only valid if the carrier holds a jurisdiction_license for that state. Compliance teams validate appointment eligibility against carrier jurisdiction licenses — a mandator']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Appointments are filed with state regulators on behalf of a specific legal entity (appointing carrier). Required for NAIC statutory filings, RBC reporting by entity, and commission expense attribution']
  license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.license. Business justification: Appointment records authorize a producer to sell specific product lines based on an underlying state license. The appointment.state_of_appointment and appointment.line_of_authority should correspond t']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.schedule. Business justification: Each carrier appointment specifies the commission schedule under which the producer is compensated for that carrier/state/line combination. The denormalized commission_schedule_code should be replaced']
  anti_money_laundering_training_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the producer has completed required AML training as mandated by FinCEN and internal compliance policies.']
  anti_money_laundering_training_date date [note: 'Date on which the producer completed required AML training. Must be refreshed periodically per compliance requirements.']
  appointing_carrier_code varchar [note: 'NAIC company code or internal identifier of the insurance carrier granting the appointment.']
  appointment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the appointment record, often used in state DOI filings and external correspondence.']
  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the appointment. Active indicates the producer is authorized to sell; pending indicates awaiting state approval; terminated indicates appointment has ended.']
  appointment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the appointment action: new appointment, renewal of existing appointment, reinstatement after lapse, termination, or amendment to existing terms.']
  background_check_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required background check was completed prior to appointment approval, per carrier onboarding requirements.']
  background_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the background check was completed for the producer.']
  broker_dealer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the broker-dealer firm through which the producer is registered for variable product sales, if applicable.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying agent contract or agreement that governs the commercial relationship between the carrier and producer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this appointment record was first created in the system.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution channel through which the producer operates (captive agent, independent agent, BGA, MGA, broker-dealer, bank, worksite, direct). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: captive|independent|bga|mga|broker_dealer|bank|worksite|direct — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the producer is authorized to begin selling products on behalf of the carrier in the specified state and line of authority.']
  errors_and_omissions_coverage_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier requires the producer to maintain errors and omissions insurance as a condition of appointment.']
  finra_registration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FINRA registration is required for this appointment due to variable product sales authority. True if line of authority includes variable life or variable annuities.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Position of the producer within the distribution hierarchy (writing agent, general agent, managing general agent, brokerage general agent, regional director, national director).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this appointment record was last updated in the system.']
  line_of_authority varchar [note: 'Product line or lines the producer is authorized to sell under this appointment (e.g., Life, Accident and Health, Variable Contracts, Fixed Annuities). May be comma-separated for multi-line appointments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: life|accident_and_health|variable_life|variable_annuity|fixed_annuity|long_term_care|disability_income — promote to reference product]']
  nipr_appointment_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by NIPR for appointments filed through the Producer Database (PDB) system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special conditions, or restrictions associated with the appointment.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date on which the appointment is scheduled for renewal review. Some states require periodic renewal filings.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system from which this appointment record originated (e.g., Sapiens Commission, Annocera, NIPR PDB).']
  state_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the state Department of Insurance approved the appointment. May differ from effective date in some jurisdictions.']
  state_filing_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation or tracking number provided by the state DOI upon successful appointment filing submission.']
  state_filing_date date [note: 'Date on which the appointment was filed with the state Department of Insurance. Most states require filing within 15-30 days of appointment.']
  state_of_appointment varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or territory code where the appointment is filed and effective. Each state requires separate appointment filing.']
  suitability_training_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the producer has completed required product suitability and best interest training per state and NAIC requirements.']
  suitability_training_date date [note: 'Date on which the producer completed required suitability and best interest training.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the appointment ended or will end. Null for active appointments. Required for terminated appointments per state DOI notification requirements.']
  termination_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for appointment termination. For-cause terminations must be reported to state DOI within specified timeframes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary|involuntary|for_cause|non_renewal|license_lapse|death|retirement|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  termination_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the termination reason, especially for for-cause terminations that require state DOI reporting.']

  Note: 'Formal carrier appointment records authorizing a licensed producer to sell specific product lines on behalf of the insurer. Tracks appointment status, appointing carrier, line of authority, state of appointment, effective and termination dates, and appointment type (new, renewal, termination). Required for regulatory compliance with state DOI appointment filing obligations.'
}

Table agent.hierarchy_node {
  hierarchy_node_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the hierarchy node. Primary key.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.agency. Business justification: Hierarchy nodes can represent either individual producers or agency entities in the distribution hierarchy. When a node represents an agency (BGA, MGA, GA), this FK links to the agency master record. ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Hierarchy nodes map to cost centers for distribution expense allocation, override commission accounting, and production reporting. Finance requires cost center attribution at the hierarchy node level ']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Distribution hierarchy nodes in multi-entity insurance groups are scoped to a legal entity for commission override calculations, production roll-up reporting, and statutory expense allocation by entit']
  parent_node_hierarchy_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent node in the distribution hierarchy. Self-referencing foreign key enabling tree traversal and roll-up reporting. Null for top-level nodes.']
  primary_hierarchy_producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent or distribution participant represented by this hierarchy node. Links to the agent master record.']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.schedule. Business justification: Hierarchy nodes drive override commission calculations; each node is associated with a commission schedule that determines override rates for that level. Replacing the denormalized commission_schedule']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Distribution channel classification for this hierarchy node. Career represents captive career agents. Independent represents independent agents and agencies. Worksite represents employer-sponsored distribution. Financial institution represents bank and credit union channels. Broker-dealer represents securities-licensed distribution for variable products.']
  child_node_count varchar [note: 'Number of direct child nodes reporting to this hierarchy node. Used for span-of-control analysis and organizational structure metrics. Zero for leaf nodes.']
  commission_hierarchy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node participates in commission calculation and override chains. True for nodes eligible for commission processing. False for reporting-only or inactive nodes.']
  commission_split_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of commission allocated to this hierarchy node when multiple nodes share commission on the same policy. Used in split-commission scenarios and joint-writing arrangements. Value between 0.00 and 100.00.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Legal relationship type between the company and this hierarchy node. W2 employee represents captive career agents. 1099 contractor represents independent agents. Corporate entity represents agency corporations. Partnership represents multi-owner agencies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hierarchy node record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  depth_level varchar [note: 'Calculated depth of this node from the root of the hierarchy tree. Root nodes have depth 0. Used for tree traversal algorithms and organizational chart rendering.']
  descendant_node_count varchar [note: 'Total number of descendant nodes (children, grandchildren, etc.) under this hierarchy node. Used for organizational size metrics and production roll-up calculations. Zero for leaf nodes.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this hierarchy node became active and operational. Used for temporal queries and commission eligibility determination.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the distribution hierarchy tree. Level 1 represents top-level nodes (e.g., Brokerage General Agency (BGA)), higher numbers represent deeper levels (e.g., writing agents). Enables depth-based queries and organizational charting.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Materialized path representing the full lineage from root to this node, typically formatted as slash-delimited node IDs (e.g., /1/45/203/). Enables efficient ancestor and descendant queries without recursive joins.']
  hierarchy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution hierarchy structure. Captive represents company-owned distribution, independent represents non-exclusive agents, brokerage represents broker-dealer channels, hybrid represents mixed models, direct represents company-direct sales, reinsurance represents reinsurance intermediary chains.']
  hierarchy_version varchar [note: 'Version number for this hierarchy node configuration. Incremented when node attributes change. Supports temporal hierarchy analysis and historical commission recalculations.']
  leaf_node_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node is a leaf node (has no children) in the hierarchy tree. True for individual writing agents. False for supervisory and management levels with downstream agents.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code for this hierarchy node. Used for reporting, commission processing, and system integration. May align with legacy system codes or external partner identifiers.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Business name or label for this hierarchy node. May represent the agent name, agency name, Managing General Agent (MGA) name, General Agent (GA) name, or Brokerage General Agency (BGA) name depending on hierarchy level.']
  node_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the hierarchy node. Active indicates the node is operational and eligible for commission processing. Inactive indicates temporary suspension. Terminated indicates permanent closure. Pending indicates awaiting approval or activation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text notes and comments about this hierarchy node. Used for documenting special arrangements, historical context, or operational considerations.']
  organizational_unit varchar [note: 'Business division, region, or organizational unit to which this hierarchy node belongs. Used for management reporting, territory assignment, and organizational charting.']
  override_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hierarchy node is eligible to receive override commissions from downstream production. True for supervisory and management levels (Managing General Agent (MGA), General Agent (GA), Brokerage General Agency (BGA)). False for individual writing agents.']
  production_credit_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of production volume credited to this hierarchy node for quota and performance tracking. Used in split-credit scenarios and joint-production arrangements. Value between 0.00 and 100.00.']
  reporting_hierarchy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node participates in management reporting hierarchies. True for nodes used in executive dashboards and performance reporting. False for commission-only or administrative nodes.']
  roll_up_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether production from this node should roll up to parent nodes for reporting and commission calculations. Typically true for all nodes except top-level organizational units.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Display sequence for this node among siblings in the hierarchy tree. Used for consistent organizational chart rendering and report ordering.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when this hierarchy node was terminated or deactivated. Null for active nodes. Used for historical reporting and commission cutoff calculations.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for hierarchy node termination. Voluntary indicates agent-initiated departure. Involuntary indicates company-initiated termination. Restructure indicates organizational change. Compliance indicates regulatory or policy violation. Performance indicates failure to meet production targets. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary|involuntary|restructure|merger|acquisition|compliance|performance|retirement — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Geographic or market territory assigned to this hierarchy node. Used for territory management, lead assignment, and market penetration analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hierarchy node record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']

  Note: 'Represents a single node in the multi-level distribution hierarchy — from individual writing agents up through supervisory levels, GAs, MGAs, BGAs, and broker-dealers. Each node captures the participant, their hierarchy level, parent node reference (enabling tree traversal), effective and termination dates, override eligibility flag, and hierarchy type (captive, independent, brokerage). Self-referencing parent-child structure enables roll-up reporting, commission override chain calculations, and organizational charting across distribution tiers.'
}

Table agent.contracting {
  contracting_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the producer contracting record. Primary key for the contracting entity.']
  contract_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Executed producer contracts are legal documents requiring retention per regulatory schedules. Termination processing, commission disputes, and regulatory audits require access to signed contract docum']
  contracting_producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent or producer who is party to this contract. Links to the agent master record.']
  contracting_upline_agent_producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the upline agent or agency in the distribution hierarchy. Null for top-level producers who report directly to the carrier.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Contracting costs (background checks, E&O validation, onboarding expenses) are allocated to cost centers for expense management and DAC capitalization of acquisition costs. Finance requires cost cente']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Contracting records establish which legal entity (carrier) a producer is contracted with — required for statutory reporting, NAIC filings, and commission accounting by entity. A compliance officer wou']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.schedule. Business justification: The contracting record establishes the producer\'s compensation terms including the applicable commission schedule. Replacing the denormalized commission_schedule_code with a FK enables direct schedule']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Contracting is subject to state-specific regulatory requirements (state notification obligations, filing deadlines, termination notice rules). contracting already has state_notification_required_flag ']
  workflow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.workflow. Business justification: Contracting generates onboarding document workflows including NIGO resolution and background check processing. Compliance teams track contracting workflow status to manage producer activation timeline']
  background_check_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent background check was completed for the producer as part of the contracting process.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the background check conducted during producer onboarding. Passed indicates no issues found; failed indicates disqualifying findings; pending indicates check in progress; waived indicates check was not required.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for the producer contract. Used for business reference and correspondence.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the producer contract. Pending indicates onboarding in progress; active indicates the producer is authorized to sell; suspended indicates temporary hold; terminated indicates contract has ended.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the producer contract. Captive agents represent only the carrier; independent agents represent multiple carriers; brokerage agreements are for broker-dealers; MGA (Managing General Agent), BGA (Brokerage General Agency), and GA (General Agent) represent hierarchical distribution models.']
  contracting_date date [note: 'Date when the producer contract was executed and signed by both parties. Marks the formal agreement date.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contracting record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the producer contract becomes active and the producer is authorized to sell products on behalf of the carrier.']
  eo_coverage_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the producer is required to maintain Errors and Omissions insurance as a condition of the contract.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the producer contract expires or is scheduled to end. Null for open-ended contracts.']
  finra_registration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the producer must be FINRA-registered to sell variable products under this contract. True for contracts authorizing variable life or variable annuity sales.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the distribution hierarchy. Level 1 typically represents direct agents; higher levels represent MGAs, BGAs, or GAs who manage downline producers.']
  nigo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contracting application is Not In Good Order due to missing documentation, incomplete information, or compliance issues. True means the contract is incomplete and requires remediation.']
  nigo_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the contracting application is flagged as NIGO. Describes missing documents, compliance gaps, or information deficiencies.']
  nigo_resolution_date date [note: 'Date when all NIGO issues were resolved and the contract moved to good order status.']
  onboarding_completion_date date [note: 'Date when all onboarding requirements were completed and the producer was fully activated in the system.']
  product_line_authorization varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product lines the producer is authorized to sell under this contract (e.g., life, annuity, variable products, long-term care). Defines the scope of selling authority.']
  regulatory_reporting_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory reporting obligations for this contract termination. Tracks whether required notifications to NAIC, state DOI, and FINRA have been completed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the Agent and Commission Management System module that originated this contracting record (e.g., Sapiens Commission Onboarding, Annocera Contracting).']
  state_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier notified the state Department of Insurance about the contract termination.']
  state_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier is required to notify state Departments of Insurance about this contract termination per state regulatory requirements.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Effective date when the producer contract was terminated and the producer lost selling authority.']
  termination_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for contract termination (e.g., performance issues, compliance violation, business closure, retirement, voluntary resignation).']
  termination_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the circumstances and reasons for contract termination.']
  termination_type varchar [note: 'Classification of contract termination. Voluntary indicates producer-initiated termination; carrier-initiated indicates the carrier ended the contract; regulatory indicates termination due to regulatory action; mutual indicates both parties agreed to end the contract.']
  u5_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'FINRA-issued confirmation number for the U5 filing, serving as proof of submission.']
  u5_filing_date date [note: 'Date when the FINRA Form U5 was filed to report the termination of the producer\'s securities registration.']
  u5_filing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a FINRA Form U5 filing is required for this termination. Required when the producer was registered to sell variable products and the contract is terminated.']
  unearned_commission_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount of unearned commissions that must be returned by the producer upon termination.']
  unearned_commission_return_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the producer is required to return unearned commissions upon contract termination. Typically applies when policies lapse or are cancelled shortly after issue.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contracting record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Producer contracting records capturing the formal agreement between the insurer and a producer or agency. Tracks contract type (captive, independent, brokerage), contract status (pending, active, suspended, terminated), product line authorizations, contracting date, and NIGO (Not In Good Order) flags. Includes full termination lifecycle: termination type (voluntary, carrier-initiated, regulatory), effective date, reason code, state notification requirements, U5 filing obligation flag, return of unearned commission flag, regulatory reporting status, and termination_event_id for historical reference. Supports NAIC termination reporting and FINRA U5 filing obligations. Sourced from the Agent and Commission Management System onboarding module.'
}

Table agent.onboarding_case {
  onboarding_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the producer onboarding case. Primary key for tracking the complete onboarding workflow from initial application through contracting and system provisioning.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.agency. Business justification: Onboarding cases track the producer onboarding workflow, which often involves joining a specific agency. This FK links the onboarding case to the agency the producer is joining. No columns removed bec']
  appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.appointment. Business justification: The onboarding_case tracks the end-to-end producer onboarding workflow from initial application through contracting, licensing, and ultimately appointment. The case already links to contracting_id and']
  compensation_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.compensation_contract. Business justification: Onboarding completion requires establishing a compensation contract before a producer can submit business. Linking onboarding_case to compensation_contract enables onboarding status validation (confir']
  contracting_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.contracting. Business justification: Onboarding cases track the end-to-end producer onboarding workflow, which culminates in a contracting record. This FK links the onboarding case to the resulting contracting record. Populated when onbo']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Onboarding activities incur recruiting costs (background checks, training, licensing fees, system provisioning) that must be capitalized or expensed to cost centers for producer acquisition cost analy']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Onboarding cases are processed for a specific legal entity (appointing carrier). Required for multi-entity expense allocation, DAC capitalization of onboarding costs, and regulatory reporting of new p']
  license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.license. Business justification: Onboarding cases track license verification status. Currently onboarding_case has nipr_number (STRING) but no FK to the license table. Adding license_id FK links the onboarding case to the specific li']
  onboarding_document_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_package. Business justification: Producer onboarding requires tracking complete documentation packages (licenses, background checks, E&O certificates, training records). Onboarding workflow depends on document package completeness ve']
  primary_onboarding_producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent or producer being onboarded. Links to the agent master record once created during the onboarding process.']
  workflow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.workflow. Business justification: Onboarding cases drive document processing workflows (NIGO resolution, background check document review, license verification). Operations teams track onboarding workflow status to manage SLA complian']
  adverse_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an adverse action (denial or termination based on background check results) was taken or is being considered. True if adverse action applies, False otherwise.']
  aml_training_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the producer has completed required Anti-Money Laundering training. True if completed, False otherwise. Required for compliance with Bank Secrecy Act and USA PATRIOT Act.']
  aml_training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the producer completed AML training. Used for compliance tracking and periodic re-training requirements.']
  application_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the producer submitted the initial onboarding application. Marks the start of the onboarding workflow and is used for SLA tracking.']
  application_type varchar [note: 'Type of onboarding application: new agent appointment, transfer from another carrier, reappointment after termination, or upgrade to higher authority level.']
  assigned_reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the internal staff member assigned to review this onboarding case. Provides quick reference without requiring a join to the employee table.']
  background_check_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the background check results were received from the screening vendor. Marks completion of the screening phase.']
  background_check_result_status varchar [note: 'Overall result status of the background screening: pending (not yet complete), clear (no issues found), flagged (issues requiring review), or adverse action (disqualifying issues found).']
  background_check_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the background check request was submitted to the screening vendor. Used for tracking turnaround time and SLA compliance.']
  background_check_type varchar [note: 'Type of background check performed: criminal history only, credit check, regulatory history (state DOI and FINRA), or comprehensive (all checks combined).']
  background_screening_vendor varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party vendor used to conduct the background screening. Examples include LexisNexis, Sterling, or Checkr.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique case number assigned to the onboarding workflow. Used for tracking and communication with the applicant and internal stakeholders.']
  contracting_entity_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the legal entity or carrier with which the producer is contracting. Supports multi-carrier and multi-entity operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the onboarding case record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  eo_coverage_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Errors and Omissions insurance coverage is required for this producer based on their distribution channel and product authority. True if required, False otherwise.']
  eo_validation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the E&O insurance validation. Indicates whether the producers E&O coverage has been verified to meet carrier requirements.']
  finra_registration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FINRA registration is required for this producer based on their intended product authority (variable life, variable annuities). True if required, False otherwise.']
  finra_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of FINRA registration verification. Indicates whether the producers FINRA registration has been validated through BrokerCheck.']
  license_verification_date date [note: 'Date when the producers licenses were successfully verified. Used for compliance tracking and audit purposes.']
  license_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the license verification process. Indicates whether the producers state licenses have been validated through NIPR or state DOI systems.']
  mib_inquiry_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for any MIB inquiry conducted as part of the background screening. Used for producers who will sell life insurance products requiring underwriting knowledge.']
  nigo_date date [note: 'Date when the application was marked as Not In Good Order. Used for tracking resolution time and applicant communication.']
  nigo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the application has been flagged as Not In Good Order due to missing or incomplete documentation. True if NIGO, False otherwise.']
  nigo_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the specific reason the application was marked NIGO. Examples include missing license copy, incomplete background check authorization, or missing E&O certificate.']
  nigo_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the application was marked NIGO. Provides specific guidance to the applicant on what is needed to move forward.']
  onboarding_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the entire onboarding process was completed and the producer was fully appointed and ready to transact business. Used for time-to-productivity metrics and SLA tracking.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the onboarding case in the workflow. Tracks progression from initial application through final approval or rejection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initiated|nigo|under_review|pending_background|pending_licensing|approved|rejected|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_disqualification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the producer has any regulatory disqualifications that would prevent appointment. Examples include state DOI sanctions, FINRA bars, or federal convictions. True if disqualified, False otherwise.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date when the onboarding application was formally rejected. Used for tracking and adverse action notification timing.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for onboarding rejection. Examples include failed background check, license issues, or regulatory disqualification.']
  rejection_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the onboarding application was rejected. Provides context for the rejection decision and supports adverse action communication.']
  suitability_training_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the producer has completed required suitability and best interest training for annuity sales. True if completed, False otherwise. Required for NAIC Model Regulation 275 compliance.']
  suitability_training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the producer completed suitability training. Used for compliance tracking and state reporting requirements.']
  system_provisioning_date date [note: 'Date when system access was successfully provisioned for the producer. Marks readiness for production activity.']
  system_provisioning_status varchar [note: 'Status of system access provisioning for the producer. Indicates whether user accounts, POS system access, and portal credentials have been created.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the onboarding case record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Tracks the end-to-end producer onboarding workflow from initial application through contracting, licensing verification, E&O validation, background screening, and system provisioning. Captures onboarding stage, NIGO reason codes, assigned reviewer, AML training completion, and overall onboarding completion date. Includes embedded background check tracking: screening vendor, check type (criminal, credit, regulatory history), submission date, result status (clear, flagged, pending), adverse action flags, MIB inquiry reference, and regulatory disqualification indicators. Supports periodic re-screening workflows and maintains historical screening records with unique background_check_id references. Satisfies NAIC background check requirements and FINRA suitability obligations. Sourced from the Agent and Commission Management System onboarding module.'
}

Finance requires cost center at']

  producer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the producer who completed the training. Links to the producer master record.']

State regulators require signed training acknowledgments for CE credit. Existing certificate_document_id links to the certifi']

Business justification: CE training credit validity is governed by state_regulation (approved course types, CE hours required, renewal cycles per state). Compliance teams validate producer CE training records against state r']
  attempts_count varchar [note: 'Number of attempts the producer made to complete or pass the training course.']

  certificate_issued_date date [note: 'Date the training certificate or credential was issued to the producer.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Unique certification or credential number issued upon successful completion of the training.']
  completion_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score or percentage achieved by the producer upon completion of the training course.']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: aml_bsa|dol_fiduciary|finra_continuing_education|state_ce_mandate|carrier_certification|suitability|ethics|product_authorization — promote to reference product]']
