  agency_producer_affiliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the agency-producer affiliation relationship record.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agency organization in this affiliation relationship.']
  contracting_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.contracting. Business justification: Agency-producer affiliations reference formal contracting records. Currently affiliation has contract_number (STRING) and contract dates but no FK to contracting table. Adding contracting_id FK links ']
  primary_agency_producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual producer in this affiliation relationship.']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.schedule. Business justification: The agency-producer affiliation record captures the specific commission schedule under which a producer operates within an agency, governing split arrangements and agency override compensation. A FK r']
  affiliation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for capturing additional information, special circumstances, or administrative notes related to this agency-producer affiliation. Used for internal documentation and context.']
  affiliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the agency-producer affiliation. Active indicates the relationship is in force, inactive indicates temporarily not producing, suspended indicates regulatory or compliance hold, pending indicates awaiting approval or onboarding completion, terminated indicates relationship has ended.']
  affiliation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the relationship between the producer and agency. Employed indicates W-2 employee status, contracted indicates 1099 independent contractor, sub-agent indicates hierarchical sub-relationship, independent indicates non-exclusive arrangement, captive indicates exclusive arrangement, broker indicates broker-dealer relationship.']
  annual_production_target decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target annual production volume in Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) or premium dollars assigned to this producer within this agency affiliation. Used for performance management and incentive qualification.']
  anti_money_laundering_training_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the producer has completed required Anti-Money Laundering training for this agency affiliation. True if completed, false if not. Required under Bank Secrecy Act and USA PATRIOT Act.']
  anti_money_laundering_training_date date [note: 'Date when the producer completed Anti-Money Laundering training for this agency affiliation. Used for compliance tracking and renewal scheduling.']
  background_check_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a background check was completed for this producer as part of the agency affiliation onboarding process. True if completed, false if not.']
  background_check_date date [note: 'Date when the background check was completed for this producer within this agency affiliation.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Result status of the background check. Passed indicates cleared for affiliation, failed indicates issues identified, pending indicates in progress, waived indicates not required or exempted.']
  broker_dealer_crd_number varchar [note: 'FINRA CRD number of the broker-dealer firm associated with this agency affiliation. Used for regulatory reporting and compliance verification.']
  broker_dealer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the broker-dealer firm through which the producer is registered for variable product sales within this agency affiliation. Required when variable_products_authorized_flag is true.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this agency-producer affiliation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution channel through which this producer operates within the agency. Career indicates captive career agents, independent indicates independent agents, BGA indicates Brokerage General Agency, MGA indicates Managing General Agent, broker_dealer indicates registered representatives, bank indicates bank channel, worksite indicates worksite marketing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: career|independent|bga|mga|broker_dealer|bank|worksite — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the agency-producer affiliation relationship became active and the producer was authorized to represent the agency.']
  errors_and_omissions_coverage_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether errors and omissions professional liability insurance coverage is required for this producer within this agency affiliation. True if required, false if not. Requirements vary by state and agency policy.']
  finra_crd_number varchar [note: 'The producer\'s FINRA Central Registration Depository number associated with this agency affiliation. Required for producers selling variable products. Links to FINRA BrokerCheck system.']
  finra_registration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FINRA registration is required for this producer within this agency affiliation based on the products they are authorized to sell. True if FINRA registration required, false if not.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric indicator of the producer\'s position within the agency\'s organizational hierarchy. Level 1 typically represents direct agency producers, higher numbers indicate sub-agent or downline positions. Used for commission override calculations and reporting rollups.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this agency-producer affiliation record was last updated in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  lines_of_authority varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of insurance lines of authority the producer is authorized to sell through this agency affiliation. Examples include life, accident and health, variable products, annuities. Must align with producer\'s active licenses.']
  onboarding_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the producer completed all required onboarding activities for this agency affiliation, including training, compliance certifications, system access setup, and contract execution.']
  override_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the producer is eligible to receive override commissions on production from downline producers in the hierarchy. True if eligible for overrides, false if not. Typically true for Managing General Agents (MGA), General Agents (GA), and Brokerage General Agents (BGA).']
  primary_agency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agency is the producer\'s primary or home agency affiliation. True for primary agency, false for secondary or additional affiliations. Used in multi-agency affiliation scenarios common in independent distribution channels.']
  production_credit_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of production volume credited to this agency for this producer\'s sales. Used in split-credit scenarios where a producer has multiple agency affiliations. Value between 0.00 and 100.00.']
  suitability_training_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the producer has completed required suitability and best interest training for this agency affiliation. True if completed, false if not. Required under NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation and state insurance regulations.']
  suitability_training_date date [note: 'Date when the producer completed suitability and best interest training for this agency affiliation. Used for compliance tracking and renewal scheduling.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the agency-producer affiliation relationship ended. Null for active affiliations.']
  termination_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for affiliation termination. Voluntary indicates producer-initiated separation, involuntary indicates agency-initiated separation, retirement indicates producer retirement, relocation indicates geographic move, performance indicates production or quality issues, compliance indicates regulatory or policy violations, license_revocation indicates loss of required licensing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary|involuntary|retirement|relocation|performance|compliance|license_revocation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  termination_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the circumstances and reasons for affiliation termination. Used for internal documentation and regulatory reporting.']
  variable_products_authorized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the producer is authorized to sell variable life insurance and variable annuity products through this agency affiliation. True if authorized, false if not. Requires active FINRA registration and broker-dealer affiliation.']

  Note: 'Association table capturing the relationship between individual producers and the agencies they are affiliated with. Tracks affiliation type (employed, contracted, sub-agent), effective and termination dates, primary agency flag, override eligibility, and hierarchy level within the agency. Supports multi-agency affiliation scenarios common in independent distribution channels.'
}

Table agent.termination_event {
  termination_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for termination_event']
  appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.appointment. Business justification: Termination events may terminate specific appointments. Currently termination_event does not have appointment_id. Adding this FK allows tracking which appointment is being terminated. No columns to re']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Severance amounts and unearned commission return amounts on termination events must be charged to specific cost centers for expense management and financial close. Finance requires cost center attribu']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Termination events trigger regulatory filings (FINRA U5, state notifications) and financial settlements (unearned commission clawbacks, severance) that must be attributed to a specific legal entity fo']
  primary_termination_producer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the agent whose contract or appointment is being terminated.']
  reinstated_termination_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on termination_event (reinstated_termination_event_id)']
  signature_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.signature. Business justification: FINRA U5 filings and severance agreements require producer signatures. Regulatory compliance requires linking the termination event to the producer\'s executed signature record. No existing signature F']
  termination_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Termination events generate regulatory documents including FINRA U5 filings, state termination notices, and severance agreements. Regulators and compliance teams require direct linkage between the ter']
  workflow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.workflow. Business justification: Termination events trigger document processing workflows including U5 filing workflows and book-of-business transfer document workflows. Compliance teams track termination workflow completion status f']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the termination was formally approved by management or compliance.']
  book_of_business_transfer_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the agent\'s book of business (client policies) is being transferred to another agent.']
  book_transfer_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the book of business transfer to the successor agent becomes effective.']
  commission_payout_status varchar [note: 'Status of outstanding commission payments owed to the agent at the time of termination.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'The final date of the agent\'s contract or appointment, which may differ from the termination effective date in cases of notice periods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this termination event record was first created in the system.']
  exit_interview_completed_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether an exit interview was conducted with the agent as part of the termination process.']
  exit_interview_date date [note: 'The date on which the exit interview with the terminated agent was conducted.']
  final_commission_payout_date date [note: 'The date on which the final commission payment was or will be issued to the terminated agent.']
  finra_form_u5_filed_date date [note: 'The date on which FINRA Form U5 (Uniform Termination Notice for Securities Industry Registration) was filed for this agent termination.']
  finra_reportable_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this termination must be reported to FINRA due to the agent\'s registration for variable product sales.']
  for_cause_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the termination was for cause (due to misconduct, policy violation, or performance failure) versus without cause.']
  initiated_by varchar [note: 'Indicates which party initiated the termination: the agent, the company, mutual agreement, or a regulatory authority.']
  initiating_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific individual or department that initiated the termination process.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this termination event record was most recently updated or modified.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of notice provided or required between termination notification and effective date, as specified in the agent contract.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or state insurance department to which the termination was reported.']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'The date on which the termination was reported to the relevant regulatory authority, if applicable.']
  regulatory_reportable_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this termination event must be reported to state insurance departments or regulatory authorities.']
  rehire_eligible_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the terminated agent is eligible for future rehire or reappointment with the company.']
  severance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary severance payment provided to the agent upon termination, if applicable.']
  severance_eligible_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the agent is eligible for severance pay or benefits as part of the termination.']
  termination_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the agent termination becomes legally and operationally effective, marking the end of the agent\'s active status.']
  termination_notes varchar [note: 'Internal confidential notes and comments regarding the termination event, including any special circumstances or follow-up actions required.']
  termination_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the agent was formally notified of the termination decision.']
  termination_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the termination event, used in correspondence and reporting.']
  termination_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for termination (e.g., performance, compliance violation, business restructure, personal reasons).']
  termination_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the reason for the agent termination, providing context beyond the reason code.']
  termination_request_date date [note: 'The date on which the termination was initially requested or initiated, either by the agent or the company.']
  termination_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the termination event indicating its stage in the termination process.']
  termination_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the termination event indicating whether it was voluntary, involuntary, mutual agreement, contract expiration, regulatory action, death, or retirement.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for termination_event. Referenced by termination_event_id.'
}

Table commission.schedule {
  schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the commission schedule record. Primary key for the commission_schedule data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Commission schedules are mapped to cost centers for expense allocation, product line P&L, and budget management. Distribution channel and product-specific commission structures require cost center ass']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Commission schedules with requires_state_filing=true must link to the DOI-filed approval document. State insurance regulators require filed commission schedules to reference their approved filing docu']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Multi-entity insurers maintain entity-specific commission schedules for statutory reporting, intercompany allocation, and state regulatory filings. Linking schedule to legal_entity enables entity-leve']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan. Business justification: Commission schedules are defined per product plan (whole life, term, annuity). Schedule lookup and commission calculation require knowing which plan a schedule applies to. Insurance operations always ']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Commission schedules are version-specific — when a plan_version is superseded, the applicable schedule must be traceable to that version for producer compensation audits and DAC amortization reporting']
  primary_superseded_by_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commission_schedule_id of the successor schedule that replaced this schedule. Populated when schedule_status = \'superseded\'. Enables schedule version lineage tracking and ensures continuity of commission governance.']
  rider_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.rider_definition. Business justification: Riders have distinct commission schedules separate from base products. Rider_code may exist in product_code but explicit rider_definition_id FK enables rider-specific commission rate lookup and rider ']
  source_schedule_id varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this commission schedule record in the originating source system (e.g., Sapiens Commission internal schedule ID). Enables reconciliation between the Silver Layer lakehouse record and the operational system of record.']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Commission schedules are filed and approved on a per-state basis per state insurance regulations. The schedule has state_code (plain attr, denormalized) and requires_state_filing flag. Direct FK to st']
  agent_tier varchar [note: 'The agent classification tier or hierarchy level to which this schedule applies (e.g., \'Tier 1\', \'Senior Producer\', \'GA\', \'MGA\', \'BGA\'). Supports multi-level payout calculations tied to New Business Value (NBV) and production thresholds. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tier_1|tier_2|tier_3|ga|mga|bga|override — promote to reference product]']
  ape_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) production threshold an agent must achieve to qualify for this commission schedule or bonus tier. Expressed in USD. Used in production-based tiered compensation structures. Null if no APE threshold applies.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this commission schedule was formally approved by the designated business authority. Distinct from the effective_date (when the schedule becomes operative). Required for regulatory audit trails and SOX compliance.']
  bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether policies written under this commission schedule are eligible for bonus and incentive programs (e.g., production bonuses, persistency bonuses, Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) bonuses). True = eligible; False = not eligible.']
  chargeback_period_months varchar [note: 'The number of months during which a commission chargeback may be assessed if the policy lapses or is surrendered. Defines the chargeback window for FYC recovery. After this period, no chargeback is applied. Typically ranges from 12 to 24 months for life products.']
  chargeback_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of previously paid commission subject to recovery (chargeback) upon policy lapse or surrender within the chargeback period. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 100.0000 = full chargeback, 50.0000 = partial). Governs chargeback rules for policy changes and lapses.']
  compensation_basis varchar [note: 'Defines how the commission amount is calculated. \'percent_of_premium\' applies the rate to the premium paid; \'flat_dollar\' pays a fixed dollar amount per policy; \'percent_of_account_value\' applies to Account Value (AV) for UL/annuity products; \'percent_of_nav\' applies to Net Asset Value (NAV) for VUL sub-accounts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this commission schedule record was first created in the system of record. Expressed in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and SOX compliance requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which commission rates and amounts under this schedule are denominated (e.g., \'USD\'). Supports multi-currency operations for international or cross-border distribution arrangements.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The distribution channel for which this commission schedule is applicable. Differentiates compensation structures across career agents, independent agents, Brokerage General Agencies (BGA), Managing General Agents (MGA), bank distribution, direct-to-consumer, Third Party Administrators (TPA), and worksite/employer channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: career_agent|independent|bga|mga|bank|direct|tpa|worksite — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this commission schedule becomes effective and applicable to new policy sales. Policies issued on or after this date are subject to the rates defined in this schedule. Part of the effective date range governing schedule applicability.']
  excess_interest_credit_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The excess interest credit rate applicable to UL and annuity products, representing additional compensation tied to credited interest rates above the guaranteed minimum. Expressed as a percentage. Null for products where excess interest credits do not apply.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this commission schedule ceases to apply to new policy sales (inclusive). Null for open-ended schedules with no defined end date. Policies issued after this date will be governed by a successor schedule. Part of the effective date range.']
  finra_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commission schedule applies to variable products (VUL, variable annuities) subject to FINRA oversight and compensation disclosure requirements. True = FINRA reporting obligations apply; False = not subject to FINRA reporting.']
  fyc_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The First-Year Commission (FYC) rate expressed as a percentage (e.g., 55.0000 = 55%) or flat-dollar amount per the compensation_basis. Represents the commission payable in the first policy year. Core field for heaped and hybrid compensation structures across WL, UL, IUL, and annuity product lines.']
  modal_factor_annual decimal(7,6) [note: 'The modal factor applied when premiums are paid on an annual basis. Used to adjust the commission base for modal premium payments. Typically 1.000000 for annual mode. Part of the modal factor set used in commission calculations.']
  modal_factor_monthly decimal(7,6) [note: 'The modal factor applied when premiums are paid on a monthly basis (e.g., 0.950000). Adjusts the commission base for monthly modal premium payments. Monthly mode is the most common payment frequency and often carries the lowest modal factor.']
  modal_factor_quarterly decimal(7,6) [note: 'The modal factor applied when premiums are paid on a quarterly basis (e.g., 0.970000). Adjusts the commission base for quarterly modal premium payments in commission calculations.']
  modal_factor_semi_annual decimal(7,6) [note: 'The modal factor applied when premiums are paid on a semi-annual basis. Adjusts the commission base for semi-annual modal premium payments (e.g., 0.980000). Used in commission calculations to account for the timing difference in premium collection.']
  override_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The override commission rate payable to a supervising agent, GA, MGA, or BGA in the hierarchy above the writing agent. Expressed as a percentage of premium or AV per the compensation_basis. Supports multi-level hierarchy-based payout calculations tied to NBV and policy sales.']
  persistency_bonus_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Additional commission rate payable as a persistency bonus when the policy remains in force beyond a defined duration threshold. Expressed as a percentage of premium or AV. Incentivizes agents to write quality, long-duration business. Null if no persistency bonus applies.']
  policy_year_from varchar [note: 'The starting policy year (inclusive) for which this commission rate row is applicable. Used to define rate tiers by policy duration (e.g., policy year 1 for FYC, years 2-10 for renewal, year 11+ for trail). Enables granular multi-year commission schedules.']
  policy_year_to varchar [note: 'The ending policy year (inclusive) for which this commission rate row is applicable. Null indicates the rate applies indefinitely beyond the policy_year_from. Paired with policy_year_from to define the policy duration band for this rate tier.']
  premium_band_max decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum annualized premium amount (exclusive) defining the upper bound of the premium band for this rate tier. Null indicates no upper limit (open-ended band). Paired with premium_band_min to define the premium volume tier.']
  premium_band_min decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum annualized premium amount (inclusive) defining the lower bound of the premium band for this rate tier. Enables tiered commission rates based on premium volume. Expressed in USD. Null if no premium banding applies.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'The insurance or annuity product line to which this commission schedule applies (e.g., WL — Whole Life, UL — Universal Life, IUL — Indexed Universal Life, VUL — Variable Universal Life, FIA — Fixed Indexed Annuity, SPIA — Single Premium Immediate Annuity, Term, DI — Disability Income, LTC — Long-Term Care). Drives which policies are eligible for this schedule. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WL|UL|IUL|VUL|FIA|SPIA|Term|DI|LTC|Annuity — promote to reference product]']
  renewal_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The commission rate applicable to renewal years (policy year 2 and beyond) expressed as a percentage or flat-dollar amount per the compensation_basis. Used in level and hybrid compensation structures. May vary by policy year band as defined in rate tier rows.']
  requires_state_filing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commission schedule requires regulatory filing with one or more State Departments of Insurance prior to use. True = state filing required; False = no filing required. Supports compliance tracking for state-specific commission rate approvals.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this commission schedule within the Agent and Commission Management System (e.g., \'WL-FYC-TIER1-2024\'). Used in bordereaux reporting, agent statements, and system integrations.']
  schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the commission schedule, including its business purpose, applicable product lines, special conditions, and any notes relevant to commission calculation or agent communication. Used in commission statements and internal documentation.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the commission schedule (e.g., \'Whole Life First-Year Heaped Schedule 2024\'). Used in agent-facing commission statements and internal reporting.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the commission schedule. \'active\' schedules are applied to new and in-force policies; \'superseded\' schedules have been replaced by a newer version but may still govern existing policies; \'draft\' schedules are under review and not yet applied.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the compensation structure of the schedule. \'heaped\' concentrates commission in the first year (FYC); \'level\' spreads commission evenly across policy years; \'hybrid\' combines both; \'override\' applies to hierarchy-based override rates; \'bonus\' covers incentive/bonus programs; \'trailer\' applies to ongoing trail commissions on annuity and investment products.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational source system from which this commission schedule record originated (e.g., \'SAPIENS_COMM\', \'ANNOCERA\'). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer and ETL reconciliation.']
  tax_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether commissions paid under this schedule are subject to IRS 1099 tax reporting requirements. True = commissions are reportable on Form 1099-MISC or 1099-NEC; False = not subject to 1099 reporting (e.g., W-2 employee agents). Governs 1099 tax reporting obligations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this commission schedule record was most recently modified. Expressed in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection in ETL pipelines and audit trail maintenance.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version number for this commission schedule, incremented each time the schedule is revised or superseded. Enables version history tracking and audit trail for regulatory compliance. Version 1 is the original schedule.']
  vested_after_months varchar [note: 'The number of months after policy issue after which the agent\'s commission is fully vested and no longer subject to chargeback. Supports partial vesting schedules where chargeback_rate decreases over time. Null if no vesting schedule applies.']

  Note: 'Master reference entity defining commission rate structures, granular rate tiers, and compensation schedules for life insurance and annuity products. Each schedule contains one or more rate rows specifying exact percentage or flat-dollar commission payable per policy year, premium band, product code, agent tier, and distribution channel. Stores FYC (First-Year Commission) rates, renewal rates, excess interest credits, override rate tables, and modal factors with effective date ranges. Supports heaped, level, and hybrid compensation structures across WL, UL, IUL, VUL, FIA, SPIA, and annuity product lines. Consolidates both schedule-level metadata and individual rate-row detail as a single authoritative rate source. Owned by the Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission / Annocera).'
}

Table commission.rate {
  rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a commission rate row within a commission schedule. Primary key for this entity.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Commission rates are product-specific; product_code exists but plan_id FK enables proper rate table joins for commission calculation, product eligibility validation, and commission schedule management']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Commission rates are version-specific — a new plan_version (e.g., updated benefit structure, new state filing) triggers new commission rates. Linking rate → plan_version enables version-level commissi']
  premium_rate_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.premium_rate_table. Business justification: Commission rates are calculated as a percentage of premium; the premium rate table defines the premium basis for FYC and renewal commission calculation. Insurance operations require direct traceabilit']
  pricing_basis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Commission rates are a direct output of the actuarial pricing process — the pricing_basis contains acquisition expense rates, profit margin targets, and commission loads that determine sustainable com']
  rider_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.rider_definition. Business justification: Rider-specific commission rates differ from base product rates. Explicit rider_definition_id FK enables accurate rider commission calculation, rider rate table joins, and rider-specific commission rul']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent commission schedule that owns this rate row. Links the rate to its governing schedule definition.']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable annuity commission rates differ by separate account fund type (equity vs. fixed, proprietary vs. third-party). Linking rate to separate_account enables fund-specific commission rate lookup du']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Commission rates are state-regulated in life insurance. The rate product has state_code (plain attr, denormalized). Linking to state_regulation enables compliance validation that commission rates conf']
  underwriting_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.underwriting_class. Business justification: Life insurance commission rates vary by underwriting class (preferred, standard, substandard table ratings). Commission rate lookup during policy issue requires matching the underwriting class to the ']
  agent_tier_code varchar [note: 'Agent or broker compensation tier designation (e.g., GA, MGA, BGA, career agent, broker) that determines which rate row applies. Supports multi-level hierarchy payout calculations.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this commission rate row was formally approved by the designated business authority. Supports regulatory audit trails and SOX compliance documentation.']
  basis varchar [note: 'The base upon which the commission rate or flat amount is calculated: target_premium = target/planned premium for UL/IUL; modal_premium = actual modal premium paid; annualized_premium = APE basis; account_value = AV for trailer commissions on annuities; face_amount = death benefit face; deposit = single premium deposit for SPIAs/FIAs.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Distribution channel for which this rate applies: career = captive career agents; broker = independent brokers; bga = Brokerage General Agency (BGA); mga = Managing General Agent (MGA); ga = General Agent (GA); bank = bank distribution; direct = direct-to-consumer; tpa = Third Party Administrator (TPA). Supports channel-specific compensation strategies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: career|broker|bga|mga|ga|bank|direct|tpa — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  chargeback_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months during which a commission chargeback applies if the policy lapses or is surrendered. After this period, the commission is considered fully earned. Critical for lapse administration and commission recovery.']
  chargeback_rate_percent decimal(8,5) [note: 'Percentage of the original commission subject to chargeback upon policy lapse or surrender within the chargeback period. May be a full 100% recapture or a graded percentage depending on the schedule. Stored as a decimal fraction.']
  compensation_structure varchar [note: 'Describes the commission compensation design: heaped = large FYC with low renewals; level = uniform rate across all policy years; hybrid = combination of heaped and level; graded = rate steps down over defined policy years.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commission rate row was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the commission rate (e.g., USD). Required for flat_dollar_amount rows and for multi-currency operations.']
  dac_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether commissions under this rate row qualify as Deferred Acquisition Costs (DAC) for GAAP accounting purposes under FASB ASC 944 (LDTI). True = commission is deferrable as DAC; False = expensed immediately. Critical for actuarial reserving and financial reporting.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this commission rate row becomes active and applicable to new business or in-force policy calculations. Part of the rate versioning mechanism.']
  excess_premium_rate_percent decimal(8,5) [note: 'Commission rate applicable to premium paid above the target premium level for UL/IUL products. Typically lower than the target premium rate. Stored as a decimal fraction. Null if not applicable to this product type.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this commission rate row is no longer applicable to new calculations. Null indicates the rate is open-ended with no scheduled expiry. Supports rate versioning and schedule amendments.']
  flat_dollar_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar commission amount payable per policy or per unit, used when compensation is not percentage-based. Mutually exclusive with rate_percent; one must be populated and the other null. Common in certain annuity and group products.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level within the agent distribution hierarchy at which this rate applies (e.g., 1 = writing agent, 2 = GA, 3 = MGA, 4 = BGA). Supports multi-level override and upline commission calculations tied to New Business Value (NBV).']
  issue_age_max varchar [note: 'Maximum insured issue age (in years) for this commission rate row to apply. Used with issue_age_min to define age-banded commission structures. Null if no upper age restriction applies.']
  issue_age_min varchar [note: 'Minimum insured issue age (in years) for this commission rate row to apply. Some products offer differentiated commission rates by age band (e.g., juvenile policies, senior market). Null if no age restriction applies.']
  max_face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum policy face amount (death benefit) for which this commission rate row applies. Null indicates no upper cap. Used in conjunction with min_face_amount to define face amount bands.']
  min_face_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum policy face amount (death benefit) required for this commission rate row to apply. Used to differentiate rates for jumbo or high-face-amount policies. Null if no minimum face amount restriction applies.']
  nbv_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether commissions calculated under this rate row are included in New Business Value (NBV) reporting and multi-level hierarchy payout calculations. True = eligible for NBV-linked override calculations; False = excluded.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional business context, exceptions, or conditions associated with this commission rate row (e.g., \'Applies only to 1035 Exchange business\', \'Excludes MEC policies\', \'Subject to STOLI review\'). Not used for calculations.']
  override_rate_percent decimal(8,5) [note: 'Additional override commission rate percentage payable to upline hierarchy levels (GA, MGA, BGA) on top of the writing agent\'s base rate. Stored as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.02000 = 2%). Null if this row does not carry an override component.']
  payment_mode varchar [note: 'Premium payment frequency mode for which this rate applies: annual, semi_annual, quarterly, or monthly. Commission rates may be adjusted by payment mode to account for modal loading factors in the Billing and Premium Collection System.']
  percent decimal(8,5) [note: 'Commission rate expressed as a percentage of the applicable premium or account value (AV). Stored as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.05500 = 5.5%). Mutually exclusive with flat_dollar_amount; one must be populated and the other null.']
  persistency_bonus_rate_percent decimal(8,5) [note: 'Additional commission rate awarded as a persistency bonus when a policy remains in force beyond a defined duration. Incentivizes agents to place quality, long-duration business. Stored as a decimal fraction. Null if no persistency bonus applies.']
  policy_year_from varchar [note: 'Starting policy year (inclusive) for which this rate row is applicable. Policy year 1 typically carries the FYC rate; subsequent years carry renewal rates. Used in conjunction with policy_year_to to define the rate band.']
  policy_year_to varchar [note: 'Ending policy year (inclusive) for which this rate row is applicable. A null value indicates the rate applies indefinitely beyond policy_year_from. Supports graded and level structures.']
  premium_band_max decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum annualized premium amount (in USD) for this rate row to apply. Null indicates no upper cap on the premium band. Used with premium_band_min to define the qualifying premium range.']
  premium_band_min decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum annualized premium amount (in USD) for this rate row to apply. Enables banded commission schedules where higher premium volumes attract different rates. Null if no lower band threshold applies.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Insurance product code to which this commission rate applies (e.g., WL, UL, IUL, VUL, FIA, SPIA, Term). Aligns with the product catalog in the Policy Administration System.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Broad product line classification for this rate row: life = individual life insurance (WL, UL, IUL, VUL, Term); annuity = fixed, variable, or indexed annuity (FIA, SPIA, GMWB); disability = DI products; ltc = Long-Term Care (LTC) riders or standalone; group = group life or group annuity.']
  product_subtype varchar [note: 'Granular product subtype classification within the product line (e.g., WL = Whole Life, UL = Universal Life, IUL = Indexed Universal Life, VUL = Variable Universal Life, FIA = Fixed Indexed Annuity, SPIA = Single Premium Immediate Annuity, YRT = Yearly Renewable Term). Uses standard industry abbreviations from common business jargon. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WL|UL|IUL|VUL|FIA|SPIA|YRT|GMWB|GMIB|GMAB|DI|LTC — promote to reference product]']
  rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this commission rate row: active = currently in use for calculations; inactive = no longer applied; pending = approved but not yet effective; superseded = replaced by a newer rate version; draft = under review and not yet approved.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the commission rate row: fyc = First-Year Commission (FYC) paid on new business premium; renewal = ongoing renewal commission; override = hierarchy override paid to upline; excess_interest = credit on interest above guaranteed rate; bonus = incentive bonus rate; trailer = trail commission on annuity account value (AV). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fyc|renewal|override|excess_interest|bonus|trailer|chargebacks|persistency — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  target_premium_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate row applies to the target (planned) premium portion of a Universal Life (UL) or Indexed Universal Life (IUL) policy. True = rate applies to target premium only; False = rate applies to excess or total premium. Critical for heaped UL commission structures.']
  tax_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether commissions calculated under this rate row are subject to IRS 1099 tax reporting. True = commissions are reportable on Form 1099-MISC or 1099-NEC; False = not reportable (e.g., salary-based or excluded compensation).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this commission rate row. Used for change tracking, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.']
  version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for this commission rate row within its schedule. Incremented each time the rate is amended. Supports rate versioning, audit trails, and historical rate lookups for in-force policy calculations.']
  vesting_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months an agent must remain contracted before commission is fully vested and not subject to chargeback. Supports deferred compensation and retention incentive structures.']

  Note: 'Granular rate rows within a commission schedule, specifying the exact percentage or flat-dollar commission payable per policy year, premium band, product code, and agent tier. Captures FYC rates, renewal rates, excess interest credits, and override rates. Supports heaped, level, and hybrid compensation structures used across WL, UL, IUL, VUL, FIA, and annuity product lines.'
}

Table commission.transaction {
  transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each commission transaction record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the commission_transaction data product.']
  annuity_premium_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_premium_payment. Business justification: Premium payments on annuity contracts directly trigger FYC commission transactions. Linking commission_transaction to the specific annuity_premium_payment enables premium-to-commission reconciliation,']
  assumption_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.assumption_set. Business justification: Commission calculations for DAC accounting under LDTI require tracing to the actuarial assumption set used in product pricing to ensure consistency between pricing assumptions and commission expense r']
  cession_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.cession. Business justification: Commission calculations on reinsured policies require cession reference to determine net retained premium basis (face amount minus ceded amount) for accurate commission calculation. Essential for prod']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Commission transactions are earned on specific annuity contracts. Commission processing, chargeback calculations, and producer compensation reporting all require linking transactions to the originatin']
  dac_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.dac_asset. Business justification: First-year commissions are the primary DAC-capitalized acquisition cost under GAAP/LDTI. Linking commission_transaction to dac_asset enables the DAC capitalization workflow to trace which transactions']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the life insurance policy or annuity contract that generated this commission earning event. Links the commission transaction to the underlying policy record.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Commission transactions generate GL entries for expense recognition, cash accrual, and financial reporting. Every commission earning event must be journalized for GAAP/SAP compliance and SOX controls.']
  ownership_transfer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.ownership_transfer. Business justification: Transfer-for-value commission event tracking: IRC 101(a)(2) transfer-for-value rules require insurers to track commission transactions generated by ownership transfers. Direct FK enables regulatory re']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the commission payment run batch in which this transaction was processed and disbursed. Used for reconciliation, statement generation, and payment audit trails.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Transactions must link to the product sold to calculate correct commission rates, validate product-specific eligibility rules, and support commission reconciliation. Product_type alone is insufficient']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Commission expense allocation to investment portfolios is required for DAC amortization and investment income attribution on general account products. Actuarial and finance teams report commission cos']
  premium_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_allocation. Business justification: Commission calculation basis (FYC, target premium) for UL/VUL products depends on premium allocation breakdown across base, COI, and rider components. Linking commission_transaction to premium_allocat']
  premium_bill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_bill. Business justification: Commission earnings are triggered by premium billing events. Reconciliation and audit processes require direct traceability from commission transaction to the specific premium bill that generated the ']
  primary_commission_producer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the licensed agent or broker who earned this commission transaction. Used for multi-level hierarchy payout calculations and 1099 tax reporting.']
  reinstatement_billing_reinstatement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_reinstatement. Business justification: Reinstatement events generate new commission transactions subject to state-specific reinstatement commission rules. Linking commission_transaction to billing_reinstatement provides the audit trail req']
  rider_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.rider_definition. Business justification: Transactions for rider sales require rider-specific commission calculation separate from base product. Rider_definition_id FK enables accurate rider commission rate lookup, rider eligibility validatio']
  rider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.policy_rider. Business justification: Rider premiums generate separate commission transactions with rider-specific rates and chargeback rules. Rider-level commission reporting, chargeback calculations on rider terminations, and FINRA comp']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the commission schedule (rate table) applied to calculate this transaction, covering first-year, renewal, trailer, and override rate structures.']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable annuity and variable life products generate trail commissions based on separate account fund values. Commission systems must track which separate account fund underlies each commission transa']
  statement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the commission statement on which this transaction appears. Links the transaction to the agent-facing statement document for dispute resolution and 1099 reconciliation.']
  suitability_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: Variable annuity and life commission transactions require suitability clearance before payment per FINRA Reg BI and state suitability rules. Linking commission_transaction to suitability_review enable']
  tax_free_exchange_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.tax_free_exchange. Business justification: 1035 exchanges generate FYC commission transactions on the receiving annuity contract. Regulators and compliance teams require traceability from commission earned to the specific exchange event for re']
  tertiary_commission_co_agent_producer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the co-agent sharing the commission in a split commission arrangement. Populated only when transaction_type is split_commission. References the agent domain for the secondary writing agent.']
  transaction_commission_producer_id varchar [note: 'FK to agent.producer.producer_id — Commission-to-producer tracing is fundamental for compensation accounting, 1099 tax reporting, and commission dispute resolution. Without this FK, commission runs cannot be reconciled to the producing']
  advance_basis varchar [note: 'The method used to calculate the advance commission amount disbursed to the agent prior to premium collection. Populated only when transaction_type is advance_commission.']
  advance_outstanding_balance decimal(18,4) [note: 'The remaining unrecovered balance of an advance commission disbursement. Decremented as renewal commissions are earned and applied against the advance. Populated only when transaction_type is advance_commission.']
  advance_repayment_status varchar [note: 'Current recovery status of an advance commission disbursement, tracking whether the advance has been fully, partially, or not yet recovered through earned renewals. Populated only when transaction_type is advance_commission.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'The financial base upon which the commission rate is applied. Determines how the gross commission amount is derived: Annual Premium Equivalent (APE), modal premium, Account Value (AV), Net Asset Value (NAV), face amount, or annualized premium.']
  calculation_basis_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual dollar value of the calculation basis (APE, modal premium, AV, NAV, etc.) used to compute the gross commission amount for this transaction. Expressed in USD.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The dollar amount recovered or debited from the agent due to policy lapse, surrender, or cancellation within the chargeback period. Reduces the net payable commission.']
  chargeback_period_end_date date [note: 'The date after which the agent is no longer subject to chargeback recovery for this commission transaction. Determined by the commission schedule chargeback rules and policy year.']
  chargeback_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason triggering a commission chargeback or recovery: policy lapse, surrender, cancellation, NSF payment, or free-look period cancellation. Populated when chargeback_amount is non-zero.']
  commission_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The percentage rate applied to the calculation basis to derive the gross commission amount. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 = 5%). Sourced from the applicable commission schedule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commission transaction record was first created in the commission calculation system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this transaction (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international business.']
  earning_date date [note: 'The real-world business date on which the commission was earned, typically aligned to the premium due date, policy issue date, or annuity contract anniversary. Principal business event date for this transaction.']
  fee_basis varchar [note: 'The financial base upon which the service or trail fee rate is applied: account value, NAV, flat fee, or per-policy basis. Populated only when transaction_type is service_fee.']
  fee_calculation_period_end date [note: 'The end date of the period over which the service or trail fee is calculated. Defines the billing window for asset-based compensation. Populated only when transaction_type is service_fee.']
  fee_calculation_period_start date [note: 'The start date of the period over which the service or trail fee is calculated. Defines the billing window for asset-based compensation. Populated only when transaction_type is service_fee.']
  fee_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The annualized percentage rate applied to the fee basis to calculate the service or trail fee amount. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0025 = 0.25% basis points). Populated only when transaction_type is service_fee.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total commission amount calculated before any chargebacks, adjustments, or deductions. Represents the full earned compensation prior to netting. Expressed in USD.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The level within the agent distribution hierarchy at which this commission transaction is earned: writing agent, General Agent (GA), Managing General Agent (MGA), Brokerage General Agency (BGA), or carrier override level.']
  is_1099_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commission transaction must be included in IRS Form 1099-MISC reporting for the agent. False for internal adjustments, reversals, or transactions below the IRS reporting threshold.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net commission payable after subtracting chargebacks and adjustments from the gross amount. This is the actual disbursement amount to the agent. Expressed in USD.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the commission was or is scheduled to be disbursed to the agent or broker. Used for cash flow reporting and 1099 tax year assignment.']
  persistency_credit_type varchar [note: 'The form of persistency credit awarded at the milestone: cash bonus, commission rate enhancement, or points-based credit. Populated only when transaction_type is persistency_credit.']
  persistency_milestone varchar [note: 'The policy persistency milestone (in months in force) that triggered this persistency credit transaction. Populated only when transaction_type is persistency_credit.']
  policy_year varchar [note: 'The year of the policy in force at the time of this commission transaction. Determines whether first-year (FYC) or renewal commission rates apply, and governs chargeback eligibility periods.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'The type of insurance or annuity product associated with the policy generating this commission. Determines applicable commission schedule and regulatory disclosure requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_life|term_life|universal_life|variable_universal_life|indexed_universal_life|fixed_annuity|variable_annuity|fixed_indexed_annuity|disability_income|long_term_care — promote to reference product]']
  service_fee_type varchar [note: 'The category of service or trail fee earned by an RIA, TPA, or broker-dealer for ongoing policy or annuity servicing. Populated only when transaction_type is service_fee.']
  source_system_transaction_code varchar [note: 'The native transaction identifier assigned by the originating commission calculation system (Sapiens Commission or Annocera). Enables traceability back to the operational system of record for audit and reconciliation.']
  split_approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for split commission arrangements requiring supervisory or compliance sign-off before disbursement. Populated only when transaction_type is split_commission.']
  split_percentage decimal(6,4) [note: 'The percentage of the total commission allocated to this agent in a split commission arrangement. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.50 = 50%). Populated only when transaction_type is split_commission.']
  split_type varchar [note: 'The method by which the commission is divided among co-agents in a split commission arrangement. Populated only when transaction_type is split_commission.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year to which this commission transaction is attributed for IRS 1099-MISC tax reporting purposes. May differ from the earning date year for advance commissions.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for this commission transaction as assigned by the commission calculation system (e.g., Sapiens Commission or Annocera). Used on commission statements, dispute references, and 1099 reporting.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the commission transaction within the payment workflow. Drives payable processing, dispute resolution, and audit trail management.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator classifying the nature of the commission earning event. Drives type-specific attribute interpretation: FYC (First-Year Commission), renewal, trailer, override, service/trail fee, advance, split, persistency credit, chargeback, or adjustment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: first_year_commission|renewal_commission|override_commission|trailer_commission|service_fee|advance_commission|split_commission|persistency_credit|chargeback|adjustment — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this commission transaction record. Used for incremental ETL processing and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record capturing every individual commission and compensation earning event tied to a policy or annuity contract. Records all transaction types via discriminator: standard commissions (FYC, renewal, trailer), override commissions, persistency credits (with milestone and credit type), service/trail fees (with fee type, fee basis, fee rate, and calculation period for RIA/TPA/broker-dealer asset-based compensation), advance commissions (with advance basis, repayment schedule, outstanding balance, and repayment status), and split commissions (with split percentage, co-agent references, split type, and approval status). Captures gross amount, net-of-chargeback amount, calculation basis (APE, modal premium, account value, NAV), policy year, and payment status. Primary audit trail for all commission payables. Sourced from Sapiens Commission / Annocera commission calculation module.'
}

Table commission.payment {
  payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each commission payment disbursement record in the commission ledger. Primary key for the commission_payment data product.']
  bonus_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bonus program definition under which this bonus payment was earned. Populated only when payment_type is \'bonus\' or \'incentive\'. Null for standard commission, persistency, and service fee payments.']
  bonus_qualification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bonus_qualification record when payment_type is \'bonus\'. Null for non-bonus payment types. Links the disbursement to the qualifying bonus program and period.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Commission payments are made for production on specific annuity contracts. Payment reconciliation, tax reporting (1099), and dispute resolution require tracing payments back to the originating annuity']
  dac_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.dac_asset. Business justification: Commission payments drive DAC capitalization entries. Linking commission_payment to dac_asset allows the DAC capitalization process to identify which payments were capitalized, supporting GAAP DAC ass']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the insurance policy or annuity contract that generated the commission earning underlying this payment. Null for bonus, incentive, or service fee payments not tied to a specific policy.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Commission payments are cash disbursements requiring GL posting for expense recognition, tax withholding liability, and cash account reconciliation. Payment runs generate batch journal entries for fin']
  payee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.payee. Business justification: commission_payment is a disbursement made TO a payee. payee is the commission-domain master entity representing all commission recipients (agents, brokers, BGAs, MGAs, GAs). Linking commission_payment']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Payments need product context for 1099 tax reporting (product categorization for IRS compliance), commission reconciliation by product, and product-specific payment rule application in payment process']
  premium_bill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_bill. Business justification: Commission payments reconcile against specific premium bills for audit and financial reporting. Auditors and finance teams trace commission payments to originating premium bills to validate payment ac']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent, broker, BGA, MGA, GA, or corporate entity receiving this compensation disbursement. Links to the agent hierarchy for multi-level payout calculations.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Commission payments are reported in regulatory filings (Form 1099-MISC to IRS, state producer compensation reports to DOI). Direct operational link: every 1099-reportable payment must be traceable to ']
  run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the batch payment run that generated this disbursement. Groups all payments processed together in a single payroll-cycle execution within Sapiens Commission / Annocera.']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commission schedule (first-year or renewal rate table) applied to calculate the earned commission amount included in this payment. Supports audit traceability of rate application.']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Trail/asset-based commission payments for variable annuity products are calculated from separate account NAV. Linking commission_payment to separate_account enables NAV-based fee payment reconciliatio']
  statement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commission statement on which this payment line item appears. Links the disbursement to the agent-facing remittance advice document generated by the commission system.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying commission_transaction record that this payment settles. Supports reconciliation between earned commission and disbursed payment amounts.']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: DAC accounting under LDTI/IFRS17 requires commission payments to be tied to specific valuation runs for proper acquisition cost amortization. Essential for quarterly financial close, DAC balance recon']
  ach_trace_number varchar [note: '15-digit NACHA ACH trace number assigned by the originating depository financial institution (ODFI) for ACH payment transactions. Used for payment tracing, return item processing, and bank reconciliation. Null for check or wire payments.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for any manual adjustment applied to this payment amount. Populated when the payment differs from the system-calculated amount due to dispute resolution, rate correction, retroactive schedule change, or manual override by commission operations.']
  annualized_premium_equivalent decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) of the policy or policies underlying this commission payment. Calculated as 100% of regular annual premiums plus 10% of single premiums. Used for production credit, commission schedule tier determination, and sales performance reporting.']
  backup_withholding_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IRS backup withholding at the statutory rate (currently 24%) was applied to this payment due to missing, incorrect, or IRS-notified TIN. True = backup withholding applied; False = no backup withholding.']
  bank_account_reference varchar [note: 'Masked or tokenized reference to the payee\'s bank account on file used for ACH or wire disbursement. Stores a non-sensitive token or last-4 digits only; full account details are held in the secure payment vault. Null for check payments.']
  bonus_qualification_period_end date [note: 'End date of the performance measurement period during which the agent accumulated qualifying production to earn this bonus payment. Populated only when payment_type is \'bonus\' or \'incentive\'.']
  bonus_qualification_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the performance measurement period during which the agent accumulated qualifying production to earn this bonus payment. Populated only when payment_type is \'bonus\' or \'incentive\'.']
  chargeback_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment record represents a chargeback (recovery of previously paid commission due to policy lapse, surrender, or cancellation within the chargeback period). True = chargeback debit; False = standard credit payment.']
  chargeback_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why a commission chargeback was applied: \'lapse\' (policy lapsed for non-payment), \'surrender\' (policyholder surrendered the policy), \'cancellation\' (policy cancelled within free-look or chargeback period), \'not_in_good_order\' (NIGO — application rejected), \'rescission\' (policy rescinded), \'other\'. Null when chargeback_flag is False. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lapse|surrender|cancellation|not_in_good_order|rescission|other — promote to reference product]']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Sequential check number printed on the physical commission check issued to the payee. Used for check reconciliation, stop-payment processing, and positive pay bank file matching. Null for ACH or wire payments.']
  commission_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the commission was earned (not necessarily the payment year). Used for 1099 tax year assignment, DAC amortization schedules, and annual production reporting. Four-digit year (e.g., 2024).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commission payment record was first created in the source commission system. Supports audit trail, SOX compliance, and data lineage tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the payment denomination (e.g., \'USD\' for US Dollar). Supports multi-currency operations for international reinsurance intermediaries or cross-border distribution arrangements.']
  federal_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of federal income tax withheld from the gross payment under backup withholding rules (IRS 24% backup withholding rate) or voluntary withholding agreements. Zero if no federal withholding applies.']
  gross_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total compensation amount before any tax withholding deductions. Represents the full earned commission, bonus, incentive, persistency credit, or service fee amount prior to federal and state withholding. Denominated in USD.']
  nbv_basis_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The New Business Value (NBV) amount used as the basis for calculating commission on new policy sales. Represents the present value of future profits attributable to new business production tied to this payment. Used for production reporting and commission schedule application.']
  net_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual amount disbursed to the payee after deducting federal and state tax withholding from the gross payment amount. This is the amount that appears on the remittance advice and is transferred via ACH, check, or wire.']
  override_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchy level at which this payment is being made within the agent distribution hierarchy: \'direct\' (writing agent), \'ga\' (General Agent override), \'mga\' (Managing General Agent override), \'bga\' (Brokerage General Agent override), \'corporate\' (corporate entity or home office override). Supports multi-level commission payout calculations.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the commission disbursement was released to the payee. Used for 1099 tax year assignment, cash flow reporting, and payment reconciliation.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The disbursement instrument used to transfer funds to the payee: \'ACH\' (Automated Clearing House electronic transfer), \'check\' (physical check mailed to payee), \'wire\' (domestic or international wire transfer for large or international payments).']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable payment reference number assigned by the commission system (e.g., PAY-0000123456). Used on remittance advices, commission statements, and 1099 tax reporting reconciliation.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the commission payment disbursement: \'pending\' (scheduled but not yet released), \'processed\' (submitted to payment processor), \'paid\' (confirmed settled to payee), \'reversed\' (payment recalled or reversed), \'voided\' (cancelled before processing), \'on_hold\' (suspended pending dispute or compliance review).']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator classifying the nature of the compensation disbursement: \'commission\' for standard first-year (FYC) or renewal commissions, \'bonus\' for program-based bonus awards, \'incentive\' for sales contest or incentive awards, \'persistency\' for persistency credits on in-force policies, \'service_fee\' for administrative service fee payments.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the commission earning period covered by this payment. Together with payment_period_start_date, defines the full earning window for reconciliation against commission_transaction records.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the commission earning period covered by this payment. Defines the start of the calculation window for commissions, persistency credits, or service fees included in this disbursement.']
  source_system_payment_ref varchar [note: 'Native payment identifier assigned by the originating commission system (Sapiens Commission or Annocera) before ingestion into the lakehouse. Preserves the source system key for lineage tracing, reconciliation, and re-processing scenarios.']
  state_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of state income tax withheld from the gross payment per applicable state withholding requirements. Zero if no state withholding applies. State jurisdiction determined by agent\'s state of domicile or state of licensure.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'IRS tax reporting year to which this payment is attributed for 1099-NEC reporting purposes. May differ from commission_year for payments made in January covering prior-year earnings. Four-digit year (e.g., 2024).']
  tin_on_file varchar [note: 'The IRS Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) — either SSN for individual agents or EIN for corporate entities — on file at time of payment. Used for 1099-NEC tax reporting and IRS backup withholding compliance. Stored as masked or tokenized value per data security policy.']
  tin_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the TIN on file: \'SSN\' (Social Security Number for individual agents), \'EIN\' (Employer Identification Number for corporate entities, BGAs, MGAs), \'ITIN\' (Individual Taxpayer Identification Number for non-resident alien agents).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this commission payment record in the source system. Tracks reversals, status changes, adjustments, and dispute resolutions. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  wire_reference_number varchar [note: 'Bank-assigned reference number for wire transfer payments. Used for wire confirmation, reconciliation, and tracing with the receiving financial institution. Null for ACH or check payments.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of all compensation disbursements made to agents, brokers, BGAs, MGAs, GAs, and corporate entities. Serves as the single disbursement ledger for all commission-domain payments including standard commissions, bonuses, persistency credits, incentive awards, and service fee payments. Captures payment type discriminator (commission, bonus, incentive, persistency, service_fee), payment date, payment method (ACH, check, wire), gross payment amount, tax withholding (federal, state), net payment amount, payment run identifier, and bank account reference. For bonus payments: includes bonus program reference, qualification period, and bonus amount. Supports 1099 tax reporting, payment reconciliation against commission_transaction and bonus_qualification records. Sourced from Sapiens Commission / Annocera payment processing module.'
}

Table commission.chargeback {
  chargeback_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each chargeback transaction or rule record within the commission chargeback module.']
  chargeback_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chargeback rule record that governs the recapture parameters applied to this transaction. Null for rule records themselves.']
  claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claims.claim. Business justification: In Life Insurance, death claims, surrenders, or lapses within the chargeback period trigger commission recapture. The chargeback record must reference the triggering claim for audit, regulatory report']
  compensation_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.compensation_contract. Business justification: Chargebacks are governed by both chargeback_rule (already linked) and the specific compensation_contract under which the original commission was paid. The compensation_contract defines chargeback_peri']
  contestability_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claims.contestability_review. Business justification: When a contestability review results in policy rescission, the original first-year commission must be recaptured. The chargeback record must reference the contestability_review driving rescission for ']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Chargebacks occur when annuity contracts lapse, surrender, or fail to persist. Chargeback processing requires linking to the specific annuity contract to determine surrender date, contract duration, a']
  dac_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.dac_asset. Business justification: chargeback already carries dac_adjustment_amount, confirming chargebacks directly affect DAC asset balances. Linking to dac_asset enables the DAC impairment/adjustment process to identify which DAC as']
  death_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.death_verification. Direct FK to death_verific']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Chargeback events are triggered by policy lapse, cancellation, or termination notices — documents that must be retained for audit and regulatory review. Life insurance compliance requires linking each']
  grace_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.grace_period. Business justification: Chargebacks initiated during grace periods require tracking which grace period triggered the recapture for regulatory compliance and producer communication. Grace period duration and resolution outcom']
  hierarchy_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.hierarchy_node. Business justification: Override chargebacks flow up the hierarchy — when a downline producer\'s policy lapses, chargebacks are assessed against upline hierarchy nodes. Linking chargeback to hierarchy_node enables hierarchy-l']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance policy that triggered the chargeback event (lapse, surrender, not-taken, or rescission). Links the chargeback to the underlying insured contract.']
  insured_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.insured.
