Business justification: Death-claim chargeback exemption processing: when an insured dies, chargeback_rule.death_claim_exempt may waive recapture. Operations must link the chargeback directly to the insured to apply exemptio']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Chargebacks reverse commission expense and trigger DAC/NBV adjustments that must be journalized. Financial statements require offsetting entries for previously recognized costs. Critical for accurate ']
  lapse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.lapse_event. Business justification: Chargebacks are directly triggered by policy lapses. Linking chargeback to lapse_event enables chargeback dispute resolution, producer reconciliation reports, and audit trails tracing each chargeback ']
  original_commission_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original commission payment transaction that is being recaptured. Provides the audit trail linking the chargeback back to the specific FYC or renewal commission disbursement.']
  ownership_transfer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policyholder.ownership_transfer. Business justification: Transfer-for-value chargeback audit trail: ownership transfers (especially transfer-for-value) are a primary chargeback trigger in life insurance. Linking chargeback directly to the triggering ownersh']
  payee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.payee. Business justification: A chargeback is a commission recapture event directed at a specific payee (the agent/broker from whom commission is being recaptured). chargeback currently references producer_id (cross-domain to agen']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Chargebacks are product-specific with different rules per product; product_type exists but plan_id FK enables proper chargeback rule application, product-specific recapture percentage lookup, and accu']
  premium_bill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_bill. Business justification: Chargebacks are triggered by billing failures (NSF, lapse, non-payment). Operations require direct reference to the failed or reversed premium bill to calculate recapture amounts and validate chargeba']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the writing agent or broker whose first-year commission (FYC) or renewal commission is subject to recapture. Central party reference for multi-level payout calculations.']
  recapture_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.recapture_event. Business justification: Reinsurance recapture events trigger commission chargebacks on previously ceded policies — a named process in life reinsurance operations. When risk is recaptured, commissions paid on that ceded face ']
  reinstatement_billing_reinstatement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_reinstatement. Business justification: When a lapsed policy is reinstated, previously triggered chargebacks may be reversed or waived. Linking chargeback to billing_reinstatement supports chargeback reversal processing and waiver decisions']
  reinstatement_policy_reinstatement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.policy_reinstatement. Business justification: When a lapsed policy is reinstated, chargebacks triggered by the lapse must be reversed or adjusted. Chargeback reversal processing and producer dispute resolution require direct linkage from the char']
  suitability_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: Chargebacks triggered by unsuitable replacement sales or policy lapses must be linked to the suitability review that flagged the sale. Compliance and commission teams jointly track suitability-driven ']
  surrender_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.surrender. Business justification: Surrender events are the primary trigger for commission chargebacks. Chargeback dispute resolution, audit trails, and regulatory reporting require tracing each chargeback to the specific surrender tra']
  triggering_claim_investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claims.claim_investigation. Business justification: When a claim investigation confirms fraud or STOLI, the writing agent\'s commission is subject to chargeback. Linking chargeback to the triggering claim_investigation supports SIU reporting, regulatory']
  triggering_premium_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_adjustment. Business justification: Premium adjustments (face amount changes, rate revisions) reduce the commission basis and trigger chargebacks on previously paid commission. Linking chargeback directly to the triggering premium_adjus']
  withdrawal_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.withdrawal_transaction. Business justification: Annuity surrenders and full withdrawals are the primary chargeback trigger events. Linking chargeback directly to the withdrawal_transaction that caused it supports chargeback initiation workflows, tr']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net dollar amount to be recaptured from the agent based on the applicable recapture percentage schedule and the policy duration at time of lapse or surrender. This is the primary monetary fact of the chargeback transaction.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or employee identifier of the commission operations manager or supervisor who approved the chargeback initiation, waiver, or write-off decision. Supports SOX audit trail requirements.']
  chargeback_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to the chargeback event or rule by the commission system. Used in agent statements, dispute correspondence, and 1099 reconciliation.']
  chargeback_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the chargeback record: pending (initiated, not yet in recovery), in_recovery (deductions or repayments in progress), recovered (fully recaptured), waived (management decision to forgive), disputed (agent has filed a dispute), written_off (deemed unrecoverable).']
  commission_paid_date date [note: 'The date on which the original commission subject to chargeback was disbursed to the agent. Used for aging analysis and DAC (Deferred Acquisition Cost) accounting.']
  commission_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the type of commission being recaptured: FYC (First-Year Commission), renewal, override (hierarchy-level override commission), or bonus. Critical for FYC recapture tracking on early-lapse policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this chargeback record was first created in the commission system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this chargeback record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international distribution.']
  dac_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of the Deferred Acquisition Cost (DAC) asset adjustment triggered by the chargeback event. When a policy lapses and commission is recaptured, the corresponding DAC balance must be reversed for GAAP and SAP reporting.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agent has formally disputed the chargeback. When True, the chargeback is placed in a disputed state pending review and resolution by the commission operations team.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded description of the agent\'s stated reason for disputing the chargeback (e.g., policy reinstated, incorrect trigger date, rule misapplication). Populated only when dispute_flag is True.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The distribution channel through which the policy was sold (e.g., career agent, brokerage, BGA, MGA, direct, bank). Chargeback period length and recapture percentage schedules vary by channel.']
  distribution_tier varchar [note: 'The distribution tier classification of the agent or agency (e.g., Tier 1 top producer, Tier 4 standard). Chargeback rules and recapture schedules may vary by distribution tier as part of producer incentive agreements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the chargeback rule or transaction record is effective. For rule records, this is the date the rule parameters became active. For transaction records, this aligns with the trigger event date.']
  expected_recovery_date date [note: 'The projected date by which the full chargeback amount is expected to be recovered, based on the recovery method and scheduled deduction plan. Used for cash flow forecasting and accounts receivable aging.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the chargeback rule record expires and is no longer applicable to new transactions. Null for open-ended rules. Supports rule versioning and temporal validity management.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The level in the agent distribution hierarchy at which the chargeback applies: writing agent, GA (General Agent), MGA (Managing General Agent), BGA (Brokerage General Agency), or carrier. Supports multi-level chargeback payout calculations.']
  initiated_date date [note: 'The date on which the chargeback was formally initiated in the commission system following detection of the trigger event. Marks the start of the recovery process.']
  minimum_premium_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum annualized or modal premium amount required for the chargeback rule to apply. Policies with premiums below this threshold may be exempt from chargeback. Applies primarily to rule records.']
  nbv_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated impact of the chargeback on the New Business Value (NBV) metric for the associated policy sale. Used in sales performance reporting and agent scorecard analytics tied to NBV and policy sales.']
  original_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross commission amount originally paid to the agent for the policy that is now subject to chargeback. Denominated in USD. Serves as the basis for calculating the recapture amount.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining dollar amount of the chargeback not yet recovered (chargeback_amount minus recovered_amount). Drives accounts receivable reporting and agent balance management.']
  period_months varchar [note: 'The total number of months during which a commission is subject to chargeback recapture per the applicable rule. Typically 12–24 months for FYC on life products. Applies to rule records; populated on transaction records from the governing rule.']
  period_remaining_months varchar [note: 'The number of months remaining in the chargeback period at the time the trigger event occurred. Used to calculate the recapture amount and schedule recovery deductions.']
  policy_duration_months varchar [note: 'The number of months the policy had been in force at the time the chargeback trigger event occurred. Used to look up the applicable recapture percentage from the chargeback rule schedule.']
  policy_issue_date date [note: 'The date the underlying insurance policy was issued and placed in force. Used to calculate policy duration at trigger and validate chargeback period applicability.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the insurance or annuity product associated with the chargeback (e.g., Whole Life, Term, Universal Life, Fixed Indexed Annuity). Determines which chargeback rule schedule applies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WL|Term|UL|IUL|VUL|FIA|SPIA|DI|LTC|Annuity — promote to reference product]']
  recapture_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of the original commission to be recaptured, determined by the chargeback rule schedule based on the policy duration month at time of trigger event. For example, a policy lapsing in month 6 may have a 75% recapture rate.']
  recapture_schedule varchar [note: 'Serialized representation of the month-by-month recapture percentage schedule (e.g., \'100,100,75,75,50,50,25,25,0,0,0,0\' for a 12-month schedule). Applies to rule records. Defines the declining recapture rate as policy duration increases.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator field distinguishing between a chargeback transaction event (recapture of previously paid commission) and a chargeback rule record (parameters governing recapture logic). Drives downstream processing and reporting branching.']
  recovered_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative dollar amount of the chargeback that has been successfully recaptured to date through commission deductions or direct repayment. Used to track recovery progress against the total chargeback amount.']
  recovery_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism by which the chargeback amount will be recaptured: commission_deduction (withheld from future commission payments), direct_repayment (agent remits payment directly), offset (netted against other payables), or write_off (deemed unrecoverable and expensed).']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the chargeback was fully resolved — either through complete recovery, waiver, write-off, or dispute settlement. Null if still open.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this chargeback record was sourced (e.g., Sapiens Commission, Annocera, manual entry, policy administration system). Supports data lineage and ETL audit.']
  trigger_event_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the triggering business event (lapse, surrender, NTO, rescission) occurred on the policy. This is the principal real-world event date driving the chargeback calculation.']
  trigger_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the business event that triggered the chargeback: lapse (policy lapsed for non-payment), surrender (policyholder surrendered the policy), NTO (not-taken, policy returned during free-look), rescission (policy voided), death (death claim within chargeback period), or maturity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent update to this chargeback record (e.g., status change, recovery posting, dispute filing). Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chargeback has been formally waived by management (e.g., due to agent hardship, policy reinstatement, or contractual exception). When True, no further recovery action is taken.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the business justification for waiving the chargeback (e.g., policy reinstated within grace period, agent deceased, contractual exception). Populated only when waiver_flag is True.']

  Note: 'Transactional and reference entity managing commission recapture events and the rules governing them. Transaction records capture chargebacks triggered by policy lapse, surrender, not-taken, or early termination — including original commission transaction reference, chargeback amount, reason code (lapse, surrender, NTO, rescission), chargeback period remaining, recovery method (deduction from future commissions or direct repayment), and resolution status. Rule records define chargeback parameters by product type, distribution channel, and policy duration — specifying chargeback period length (months), recapture percentage schedule by policy month, minimum premium threshold, and applicable distribution tiers. Uses record_type discriminator to distinguish transaction vs rule entries. Critical for managing FYC recapture on early-lapse policies. Sourced from Sapiens Commission / Annocera rules engine and chargeback processing module.'
}

Table commission.chargeback_rule {
  chargeback_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the chargeback rule record within the commission management system (Sapiens Commission / Annocera rules engine).']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Chargeback rules are defined per product with product-specific recapture percentages and chargeback periods. Product_type_code exists but plan_id FK enables precise rule-to-product mapping and accurat']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Chargeback rules are version-specific — policies issued under a superseded plan_version retain the chargeback rules of that version. Regulatory audits and producer dispute resolution require tracing c']
  pricing_basis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Chargeback periods and recapture schedules are actuarially derived from the pricing basis — specifically the DAC amortization schedule and profit emergence pattern. Actuaries and product managers must']
  rule_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Chargeback rules with state_jurisdiction scope require DOI-filed policy documents and internal approval documentation. The chargeback_rule has approval_status and approval_date fields indicating forma']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Chargeback rules are state-specific — states regulate minimum free-look periods, death claim exemptions, and chargeback recapture limits. The chargeback_rule has state_jurisdiction (plain attr, denorm']
  superseded_by_rule_chargeback_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chargeback_rule_id of the newer rule that replaced this rule when it was superseded. Enables forward-chaining of rule version history for audit and compliance purposes. NULL if this rule has not been superseded.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this chargeback rule received final business approval and was authorized for activation in the commission rules engine.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status of this chargeback rule within the commission governance process. Rules must be approved before activation. \'under_review\' indicates the rule is being evaluated by compliance or actuarial teams.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the business owner or compliance officer who approved this chargeback rule for activation. Required for SOX audit trail and commission governance documentation.']
  chargeback_period_months varchar [note: 'Total number of policy months during which a commission chargeback may be triggered upon early policy termination or lapse. After this period expires, no commission recapture is applied. Typically ranges from 12 to 36 months for life products.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this chargeback rule record was first created in the commission management system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and NAIC market conduct standards.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary thresholds and amounts defined in this chargeback rule (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international life insurance products.']
  dac_impact_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether commission chargebacks under this rule affect the Deferred Acquisition Cost (DAC) balance recorded in the general ledger. True = DAC amortization schedule is adjusted upon chargeback processing.']
  death_claim_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether policies terminated due to insured death are exempt from commission chargeback under this rule. True = death-triggered terminations do not trigger recapture. Standard industry practice to exempt death claims.']
  disability_waiver_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether policies terminated due to activation of a disability waiver of premium (AWL) rider are exempt from commission chargeback. True = AWL-triggered terminations are excluded from recapture.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel to which this chargeback rule applies. Channels include career agents, Brokerage General Agencies (BGA), Managing General Agents (MGA), General Agents (GA), direct-to-consumer, Third Party Administrators (TPA), bank/financial institution, and worksite/employer-sponsored. Different channels carry distinct commission and chargeback structures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: career_agent|bga|mga|ga|direct|tpa|bank|worksite — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  distribution_tier varchar [note: 'Specific tier or level within the distribution channel hierarchy to which this rule applies (e.g., Tier 1 BGA, Tier 2 MGA, Standard Agent). Enables differentiated chargeback schedules for high-volume or preferred distribution partners. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tier_1|tier_2|tier_3|standard|preferred|elite — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this chargeback rule is effective and applicable to new policy terminations. Policies issued on or after this date are subject to this rule\'s recapture schedule.']
  exchange_1035_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether policies terminated via a tax-free 1035 Exchange to another policy within the same carrier are exempt from commission chargeback. True = internal 1035 exchanges do not trigger recapture.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this chargeback rule is no longer applicable to new policy terminations. NULL indicates the rule has no defined end date and remains active until explicitly superseded or deactivated.']
  free_look_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether policies terminated during the state-mandated free-look period (typically 10–30 days) are exempt from commission chargeback under this rule. True = free-look terminations are excluded from recapture.']
  fyc_recapture_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Maximum percentage of First-Year Commission (FYC) that can be recaptured upon policy termination within the chargeback period. Represents the starting recapture rate at policy month 1 before any earned-down schedule is applied.']
  hierarchy_level_scope varchar [note: 'Specifies which levels of the agent commission hierarchy are subject to chargeback under this rule. \'writing_agent\' recaptures only from the producing agent; \'all_levels\' recaptures proportionally across the full hierarchy including GA, MGA, and BGA overrides.']
  installment_recapture_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chargeback amount may be recovered from the agent in installments (deducted from future commission payments) rather than as a single lump-sum deduction. True = installment recovery is permitted under this rule.']
  max_chargeback_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Absolute dollar cap on the commission amount that can be recaptured under this rule for a single policy termination event. Prevents disproportionate recapture on high-face-value policies. NULL indicates no cap applies.']
  max_installment_months varchar [note: 'Maximum number of monthly commission payment cycles over which a chargeback amount may be recovered via installment deductions from future commissions. Applicable only when installment_recapture_allowed is True.']
  min_premium_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum annualized premium amount (in USD) that a policy must have paid before this chargeback rule is applicable. Policies with premiums below this threshold may be exempt from chargeback or subject to a different rule.']
  nbv_adjustment_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether chargebacks under this rule trigger a corresponding adjustment to the New Business Value (NBV) metric reported for the originating sales period. True = NBV is restated when chargeback is applied.']
  override_recapture_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of hierarchy override commissions (paid to GA, MGA, BGA levels) that are recaptured upon early policy termination. May differ from the writing agent FYC recapture rate.']
  policy_month_12_recapture_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Commission recapture percentage applicable when a policy terminates at or around policy month 12 (end of first policy year). A key milestone in the chargeback schedule as FYC is typically fully earned after year one.']
  policy_month_1_recapture_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Commission recapture percentage applicable when a policy terminates in its first policy month. Typically the highest recapture rate in the schedule, often 100% of FYC paid.']
  policy_month_24_recapture_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Commission recapture percentage applicable when a policy terminates at or around policy month 24. Represents the renewal commission recapture rate at the end of the second policy year.']
  policy_month_36_recapture_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Commission recapture percentage applicable when a policy terminates at or around policy month 36. Typically the final step in a 36-month chargeback schedule; after this point no recapture applies.']
  policy_month_6_recapture_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Commission recapture percentage applicable when a policy terminates at or around policy month 6. Represents an intermediate point in the earned-down schedule.']
  product_type_code varchar [note: 'Insurance product type to which this chargeback rule applies (e.g., WL — Whole Life, UL — Universal Life, IUL — Indexed Universal Life, VUL — Variable Universal Life, Term, FIA — Fixed Indexed Annuity, SPIA — Single Premium Immediate Annuity, DI — Disability Income, LTC — Long-Term Care). Drives rule applicability during commission recapture processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WL|UL|IUL|VUL|TERM|FIA|SPIA|DI|LTC|ANNUITY — promote to reference product]']
  recapture_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the declining recapture percentage over the chargeback period. \'straight_line\' reduces recapture evenly each month; \'step_down\' applies tiered reductions at defined intervals; \'pro_rata\' is proportional to remaining period; \'flat\' applies a constant rate; \'custom_schedule\' references a separate recapture schedule table.']
  renewal_recapture_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Maximum percentage of renewal commissions that can be recaptured upon early policy termination within the chargeback period. Renewal recapture schedules are typically less aggressive than FYC recapture.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this chargeback rule within the commission rules engine. Used in bordereaux reporting and commission statements to reference the applicable recapture schedule.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explaining the business rationale, applicability conditions, and recapture mechanics of this chargeback rule. Supports compliance documentation and agent dispute resolution.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the chargeback rule (e.g., \'Term Life 24-Month FYC Recapture — BGA Tier\'). Used in commission statements and agent communications.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the chargeback rule within the rules engine. \'active\' rules are applied to new policy terminations; \'superseded\' rules remain for historical recapture calculations on in-force policies.']
  source_rule_reference varchar [note: 'Native rule identifier or reference key from the originating commission management system (Sapiens Commission / Annocera). Enables traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer record and the operational system rule definition.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this chargeback rule was sourced (e.g., SAPIENS_COMMISSION, ANNOCERA). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  termination_reason_scope varchar [note: 'Defines which policy termination reasons trigger this chargeback rule. \'lapse_only\' applies only to policies lapsed for non-payment; \'surrender_only\' applies to voluntary surrenders; \'all\' applies to any early termination regardless of reason.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this chargeback rule record. Used to detect rule changes and trigger downstream recalculation of in-flight chargeback obligations.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this chargeback rule, incremented each time the rule is modified and re-approved. Enables historical tracking of rule changes and ensures correct rule version is applied to policies issued under prior schedules.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining chargeback rules by product type, distribution channel, and policy duration. Specifies chargeback period length (months), recapture percentage schedule by policy month, minimum premium threshold, and applicable distribution tiers. Governs when and how much commission is recaptured upon early policy termination. Managed within Sapiens Commission / Annocera rules engine.'
}

Table commission.override_commission {
  override_commission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each override commission record. Serves as the primary key for both transaction and rule records within this discriminated entity.']
  compensation_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.compensation_contract. Business justification: Override commissions are earned under the terms of a compensation_contract between the insurer and the upline agent/BGA/MGA. The compensation_contract defines override eligibility, override rates, and']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Override commissions are earned by upline producers on downline annuity production. Override calculations, hierarchy compensation reports, and producer performance tracking require linking overrides t']
  hierarchy_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.hierarchy_node. Business justification: Override commissions are calculated based on a producer\'s position in the hierarchy — the hierarchy node determines override eligibility and rate. The existing hierarchy_level_number and hierarchy_pat']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the insurance policy or annuity contract that generated the production activity triggering this override commission.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Override commissions are hierarchical compensation expenses requiring separate GL posting for multi-level distribution accounting. DAC capitalization rules differ for overrides vs. base commission, re']
  payee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.payee. Business justification: override_commission records hierarchy-based override commissions earned by upline agents. The recipient of the override is a payee in the commission domain. override_commission currently references pr']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Override calculations depend on product-specific override schedules, eligibility rules, and override rate structures. Product_type exists but plan_id FK enables accurate override rate lookup and produ']
  pricing_basis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.pricing_basis. Business justification: Override commissions must be validated against the pricing basis to confirm total distribution costs (base plus override) remain within the expense load assumed at pricing. Profitability monitoring an']
  primary_override_producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the upline agent, BGA, MGA, or GA entity in the agent hierarchy who is the recipient of this override commission. Used for multi-level payout calculations.']
  run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commission processing run batch that calculated and generated this override commission record. Enables traceability and reprocessing of commission calculation cycles.']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.schedule. Business justification: Override commission rates are defined within commission schedules. override_commission records the override_percentage and override_level, which are governed by a specific commission schedule. Adding ']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Variable annuity override (trail) commissions are calculated as a percentage of AUM held in separate accounts. Linking override_commission to separate_account supports AUM-based override calculation, ']
  suitability_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: Supervisory override commissions on variable products require that the underlying sale passed suitability review. Reg BI supervisory obligations require managers to confirm suitability before receivin']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying first-year or renewal commission transaction upon which this override commission is calculated. Links override earnings to the originating commission event.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The financial reporting period (YYYY-MM) to which this override commission is attributed for general ledger posting, statutory accounting (SAP), and GAAP reporting under FASB ASC 944.']
  annualized_premium decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Annualized Premium Equivalent (APE) of the policy generating this override commission. Used as the production volume basis for override eligibility thresholds and minimum downline production requirements.']
  base_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross base commission amount earned by the producing agent upon which the override percentage is applied. Denominated in USD. Provides the calculation basis for the override amount.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of previously paid override commission subject to chargeback due to policy lapse, surrender, or cancellation within the chargeback period. Reduces net override commission payable.']
  chargeback_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for the override commission chargeback, such as policy lapse, surrender, cancellation, non-payment of premium, or rescission. Required for chargeback dispute resolution and audit trails.']
  commission_event_date date [note: 'The real-world business date on which the override commission was earned, corresponding to the premium payment, policy issuance, or renewal event that triggered the base commission. Used for period-end accruals and DAC amortization.']
  commission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the override commission record. Tracks progression from initial calculation through approval, payment, and potential reversal or dispute. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|calculated|approved|paid|reversed|disputed|voided — promote to reference product]']
  commission_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the override commission: First-Year Commission (FYC), renewal, bonus, incentive, or standard override. Determines the applicable schedule and tax treatment for 1099 reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this override commission record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this record. Typically USD for domestic life insurance operations. Required for multi-currency reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason provided by the upline agent or agency for disputing the override commission calculation or payment amount. Used in dispute adjudication and audit trails.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Tracks the dispute resolution status for this override commission record. Populated when the upline agent or agency contests the calculated or paid override amount.']
  effective_date date [note: 'For rule records: the date from which the override commission rule parameters (percentage, cap, eligibility criteria) become effective. For transaction records: the date from which the override commission obligation is recognized.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'For rule records: the date after which the override commission rule parameters are no longer applicable. Null indicates an open-ended rule with no defined end date. Supports versioned rule management.']
  is_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the upline agent or agency met all eligibility criteria (minimum downline production, active licensing, hierarchy position) to receive this override commission at the time of calculation.']
  licensing_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the upline agent\'s or agency\'s state insurance license was verified as active and in good standing at the time the override commission was calculated. Regulatory compliance requirement for commission payment.']
  min_downline_production decimal(18,2) [note: 'For rule records: the minimum aggregate downline production volume (in APE or premium) required for the upline agent or agency to qualify for this override commission tier. Eligibility criterion defined in the override rule.']
  nbv_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The New Business Value (NBV) attributed to the producing agent\'s policy sale that underlies this override commission. NBV is a key performance metric used to determine override eligibility and tier qualification in the agent hierarchy.']
  net_override_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final net override commission amount payable after applying any cap, chargeback adjustments, or reversals. This is the amount included in the commission payment run and reflected on the commission statement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, manual adjustment explanations, or exception handling comments related to this override commission record. Used by commission administrators for audit documentation.']
  override_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross override commission amount earned by the upline agent or agency, calculated as base_commission_amount multiplied by override_percentage. Denominated in USD. Subject to maximum override cap rules.']
  override_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum override commission amount payable to the upline agent or agency for a given period or transaction, as defined in the override rule record. Prevents excessive payouts beyond contractual limits.']
  override_commission_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the override commission record, used in commission statements, dispute resolution, and 1099 tax reporting. Assigned by the Agent and Commission Management System.']
  override_level varchar [note: 'The hierarchy tier of the upline agent or agency receiving the override commission. Values include Brokerage General Agency (BGA), Managing General Agency (MGA), General Agent (GA), regional, or national. Determines applicable override percentage schedules.']
  override_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage rate applied to the base commission amount to calculate the override commission earned by the upline agent or agency. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0500 = 5%). Sourced from the applicable override rule record.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the override commission was or is scheduled to be disbursed to the upline agent or agency. Used for cash flow management, commission statement generation, and 1099 tax reporting.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The disbursement method used to pay the override commission to the upline agent or agency. Options include ACH direct deposit, check, wire transfer, or credit memo applied against a balance.']
  policy_year varchar [note: 'The year of the policy in force at the time the override commission is earned. Override percentage schedules typically differ between first-year (FYC) and renewal years, making this a critical determinant of the applicable override rate.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'The insurance or annuity product type associated with the policy generating the override commission. Used to apply the correct override schedule. Values include Whole Life (WL), Universal Life (UL), Indexed Universal Life (IUL), Variable Universal Life (VUL), Fixed Indexed Annuity (FIA), Single Premium Immediate Annuity (SPIA), Disability Income (DI), Long-Term Care (LTC), etc. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WL|UL|IUL|VUL|term|FIA|SPIA|annuity|DI|LTC — promote to reference product]']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator field distinguishing between a transaction record (an actual override commission earned event) and a rule record (a configuration defining override parameters by product type and policy year). Drives downstream processing logic.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system of record that originated this override commission record (e.g., Sapiens Commission, Annocera). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  source_system_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this override commission record in the originating source system (e.g., Sapiens Commission internal transaction ID). Enables reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational system of record.']
  tax_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this override commission amount is subject to IRS 1099-MISC tax reporting requirements. Drives inclusion in annual 1099 statement generation for the upline agent or agency.']
  tin varchar [note: 'The Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) of the upline agent or agency receiving the override commission. Required for IRS 1099-MISC reporting and AML/KYC compliance. May be an SSN or EIN.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this override commission record was last modified. Tracks adjustments, status changes, dispute updates, and chargeback processing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Transactional and reference entity for hierarchy-based override commissions earned by upline agents, BGAs, MGAs, and GAs on the production of their downline agents. Transaction records capture override earnings including override level (BGA, MGA, GA, regional), override percentage, base commission reference, producing agent reference, and override amount. Rule records define hierarchy override parameters by product type and policy year — specifying override percentages, maximum override caps, eligibility criteria (minimum downline production, licensing requirements), and effective date ranges. Uses record_type discriminator to distinguish transaction vs rule entries. Supports multi-level payout calculations tied to NBV and policy sales as required by the agent hierarchy from the agent domain.'
}

Table commission.bonus_program {
  bonus_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the agent or broker bonus and incentive program record within the Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission / Annocera).']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Bonus programs have budget_amount and gl_account_code attributes, confirming they are tracked as financial expense items. Linking to cost_center enables bonus expense budget allocation, variance repor']
  hierarchy_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.hierarchy_node. Business justification: Bonus programs are scoped to specific hierarchy nodes (e.g., regional manager programs, territory-specific incentives). Linking bonus_program to hierarchy_node enables eligibility determination, terri']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Bonus programs target specific products; target_product_line exists but plan_id FK enables precise product eligibility validation, product-specific threshold application, and accurate bonus qualificat']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Bonus programs are tied to specific product versions — a bonus incentive for a new UL plan_version differs from prior versions. Linking enables version-specific bonus eligibility determination and sup']
  program_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Bonus program terms and conditions must be documented for producer disclosure, compliance verification, and audit defense. Real business process: program rule publication, agent communication, regulat']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Producer bonus programs trigger regulatory obligations including FINRA incentive compensation reporting, state producer compensation disclosure requirements, and DOL fiduciary rules. Compliance teams ']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the bonus program received formal business approval to proceed. Supports SOX audit trail and compensation governance requirements.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Internal approval workflow status for the bonus program: pending (awaiting review), approved (authorized for distribution), rejected (not approved), withdrawn (pulled back after initial submission). Supports SOX compliance controls over compensation program authorization.']
  award_form varchar [note: 'The form in which the bonus is delivered to qualifying agents: cash (direct payment), non_cash (gift cards, merchandise), points (redeemable reward points), travel (conference or trip award), merchandise (physical goods). Affects 1099 tax reporting obligations.']
  bonus_rate_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to the qualifying base (APE or NBV) to calculate the bonus payout when bonus_structure_type is \'ape_percentage\' or \'nbv_percentage\'. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 2.5000 = 2.5%).']
  bonus_structure_type varchar [note: 'Defines the calculation methodology for the bonus payout: flat (fixed dollar amount), tiered (escalating rates by production band), ape_percentage (percentage of Annual Premium Equivalent), nbv_percentage (percentage of New Business Value), points_based (points redeemable for awards).']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total financial budget allocated for bonus payouts under this program across all qualifying agents. Used for financial planning, DAC (Deferred Acquisition Cost) tracking, and expense management. Expressed in the program\'s currency.']
  chargeback_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bonus amounts paid under this program are subject to chargeback (recoupment) if qualifying policies lapse or are surrendered within a defined period after the bonus payment date.']
  chargeback_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months following bonus payment during which a policy lapse or surrender triggers a chargeback of the bonus amount. Applicable only when chargeback_applicable is True.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bonus program record was first created in the system of record. Supports audit trail, SOX compliance, and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts defined in this bonus program (e.g., \'USD\'). Supports multi-currency operations for international distribution channels.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the bonus program becomes active and agents begin accumulating qualifying production or activity toward the program\'s thresholds.']
  eligible_agent_type varchar [note: 'Category of agent or distribution channel eligible to participate in this bonus program: captive_agent, independent_agent, mga (Managing General Agent), bga (Brokerage General Agency), broker, ga (General Agent). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: captive_agent|independent_agent|mga|bga|broker|ga — promote to reference product]']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the bonus program ends and no further production qualifies toward the program. Null for open-ended programs.']
  flat_bonus_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount awarded to qualifying agents when the bonus_structure_type is \'flat\'. Null for non-flat structures. Expressed in USD.']
  hierarchy_level_eligibility varchar [note: 'Specifies which level(s) of the agent hierarchy are eligible to earn bonuses under this program: writing_agent (direct producer), supervisor, manager, regional_director, or all levels. Drives multi-level payout calculations in the commission hierarchy.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bonuses earned under this program can be combined (stacked) with bonuses from other active programs for the same agent during the same qualification period. False means the program is mutually exclusive with other programs.']
  is_tiered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bonus program uses a tiered payout structure where higher production levels earn progressively higher bonus rates or amounts. When True, tier details are stored in the associated bonus_program_tier child entity.']
  max_bonus_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total bonus payout amount an individual agent or broker may earn under this program during the qualification period, regardless of production achieved. Null if no cap applies.']
  min_agent_tenure_months varchar [note: 'Minimum number of months an agent must have been actively contracted with the company to be eligible to participate in this bonus program. Zero means no tenure requirement.']
  min_production_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum qualifying production amount (in APE, NBV, or premium dollars) an agent must achieve during the qualification period to become eligible for any bonus payout under this program.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date on which earned bonus amounts are disbursed to qualifying agents or brokers following the close of the qualification period.']
  persistency_measurement_months varchar [note: 'Number of months over which policy persistency is measured to determine eligibility for a persistency bonus. Typically 12 or 13 months per NAIC persistency reporting conventions.']
  persistency_rate_required decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum policy persistency rate (as a percentage) an agent must maintain during the qualification period to remain eligible for a persistency bonus. Applicable when program_type is \'persistency_bonus\'. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 85.00 = 85%).']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the bonus program, used in commission statements, bordereaux reporting, and agent communications (e.g., \'PROD-BONUS-2024\', \'PERSIST-Q1-2024\').']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the bonus program\'s objectives, eligibility rules, qualification criteria, and payout mechanics as communicated to agents and distribution management.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the bonus or incentive program as displayed to agents, brokers, and distribution management (e.g., \'Q1 2024 Production Bonus\', \'Annual Persistency Award\', \'NBV Champions Contest\').']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the bonus program: draft (being configured), active (open for qualification), suspended (temporarily halted), closed (no new qualifications accepted), expired (past effective period).']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bonus program by its incentive purpose: production_bonus (volume-based), persistency_bonus (policy retention), nbv_bonus (New Business Value target), sales_contest (time-limited competition), recruiting_bonus (agent recruitment), conservation_bonus (lapse prevention). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: production_bonus|persistency_bonus|nbv_bonus|sales_contest|recruiting_bonus|conservation_bonus — promote to reference product]']
  program_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the bonus program definition, incremented each time material changes are made to eligibility criteria, payout structure, or thresholds. Supports audit trail and historical comparison of program terms.']
  qualification_end_date date [note: 'End date of the measurement window during which agent production, persistency, or NBV activity is tracked for bonus qualification purposes.']
  qualification_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the measurement window during which agent production, persistency, or NBV activity is tracked for bonus qualification purposes. May differ from the program effective date.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this bonus program record was sourced (e.g., \'SAPIENS_COMM\', \'ANNOCERA\'). Supports data lineage and ETL traceability in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  source_system_program_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this bonus program record in the originating operational system (Sapiens Commission or Annocera). Enables reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the system of record.']
  state_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of US state codes (e.g., \'CA,TX,NY,FL\') where this bonus program is available to agents. \'ALL\' indicates nationwide availability. Supports state-level regulatory compliance for producer compensation disclosures.']
  target_product_line varchar [note: 'The insurance product line(s) whose sales count toward qualification for this bonus program (e.g., \'WL\', \'UL\', \'IUL\', \'FIA\', \'Term\', \'Annuity\', \'All\'). Multiple values may be stored as a delimited string. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WL|UL|IUL|VUL|FIA|SPIA|Term|DI|LTC|Annuity|All — promote to reference product]']
  tax_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bonus payments under this program must be reported on IRS Form 1099-MISC or 1099-NEC for independent contractor agents. True for cash bonuses; may be False for non-cash awards below IRS de minimis thresholds.']
  threshold_basis varchar [note: 'The unit of measure used to evaluate whether an agent has met the minimum production threshold: ape (Annual Premium Equivalent), nbv (New Business Value), premium (collected premium dollars), policy_count (number of issued policies), face_amount (total face amount of issued policies).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this bonus program record in the system of record. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining agent and broker bonus and incentive programs including production bonuses, persistency bonuses, new business value (NBV) bonuses, and sales contest awards. Stores program name, eligibility criteria, qualification period, bonus structure (flat, tiered, percentage of APE), target product lines, minimum production thresholds, and program effective dates. Managed within Sapiens Commission / Annocera incentive tracking module.'
}

Table commission.bonus_qualification {
  bonus_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each bonus qualification tracking record. Primary key for the bonus_qualification data product.']
  bonus_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific bonus or incentive program for which qualification is being tracked (e.g., annual sales contest, quarterly production bonus, convention qualifier).']
  compensation_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.compensation_contract. Business justification: Bonus qualification tracking is tied to the specific compensation_contract governing an agent\'s or broker\'s bonus eligibility. The compensation_contract defines bonus_eligible status, ape_threshold, a']
  hierarchy_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent\'s position within the distribution hierarchy (e.g., agent, GA, MGA, BGA) at the time of qualification tracking, which may determine applicable bonus tier.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Qualifications are measured against product-specific thresholds (APE, NBV vary by product). Product_line exists but plan_id FK enables accurate product-specific threshold lookup and qualification meas']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent or broker whose progress toward bonus qualification is being tracked. Links to the agent master record in the agent domain.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for any manual adjustment applied to this qualification record. Required when manual_adjustment_flag is True. Supports audit and dispute resolution.']
  all_thresholds_met boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the agent has simultaneously satisfied all mandatory qualification thresholds (APE, policy count, NBV, and persistency where applicable) as of the as_of_date. True triggers downstream bonus payment processing.']
  ape_attainment_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The ratio of cumulative APE to the APE threshold, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.7500 = 75% attainment). Used for real-time progress tracking and agent motivation dashboards.']
  ape_gap_to_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining APE production the agent must generate to reach the APE threshold by the end of the qualification period (ape_threshold minus cumulative_ape). Negative values indicate the threshold has been exceeded. Used in agent coaching and motivation reporting.']
  ape_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum APE production level the agent must achieve within the qualification period to satisfy the APE component of the bonus program. Sourced from the bonus program\'s qualification schedule.']
  as_of_date date [note: 'The business date through which cumulative production metrics in this record have been calculated. Enables point-in-time snapshots for real-time incentive tracking and agent motivation reporting.']
  bonus_tier varchar [note: 'The incentive tier level the agent is currently tracking toward or has achieved within the bonus program (e.g., bronze, silver, gold, platinum, diamond). Higher tiers correspond to greater production thresholds and larger bonus payouts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bronze|silver|gold|platinum|diamond — promote to reference product if tiers vary significantly by program]']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'The distribution channel through which the agent operates (e.g., career, independent, BGA, MGA, bank, direct). Bonus program eligibility and thresholds may vary by channel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this bonus qualification record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  cumulative_ape decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) accumulated by the agent within the qualification period as of the as_of_date. APE normalizes single and regular premium business to an annualized basis (single premium / 10 + regular premium) and is the primary production metric for bonus qualification in life insurance.']
  cumulative_nbv decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total New Business Value (NBV) generated by the agent within the qualification period as of the as_of_date. NBV represents the present value of future profits from new policies and is used in value-based bonus programs tied to profitability rather than volume.']
  cumulative_policy_count varchar [note: 'Total number of new business (NB) policies issued and in-force that the agent has placed within the qualification period as of the as_of_date. Used as a secondary qualification metric alongside APE.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international distribution channels.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agent has formally disputed the qualification status or production metrics recorded in this record. When True, the record is placed under review and bonus payment may be held pending resolution.']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which a formal dispute regarding this qualification record was resolved. Null if no dispute has been filed or if the dispute remains open.']
  disqualification_date date [note: 'The date on which the agent was determined to have failed to meet one or more mandatory qualification thresholds, resulting in disqualification from the bonus program for the period.']
  disqualification_reason varchar [note: 'The primary reason the agent was disqualified from the bonus program. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: persistency_below_threshold|ape_shortfall|policy_count_shortfall|compliance_violation|license_lapse|e_o_violation|voluntary_withdrawal — promote to reference product]']
  estimated_bonus_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The projected bonus payout amount the agent would receive if current qualification status is maintained through the end of the period. Calculated based on current tier attainment and the bonus program\'s payout schedule. Used for agent motivation and financial accrual purposes.']
  manual_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any production metrics or qualification status in this record have been manually adjusted by a commission administrator (e.g., to correct a data error or apply a business exception). Triggers audit trail requirements.']
  nbv_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum NBV the agent must generate within the qualification period to satisfy the NBV component of the bonus program. Applicable only to programs that include a value-based qualification criterion.']
  override_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agent is eligible to receive hierarchy-based override bonuses in addition to direct production bonuses, based on their position in the distribution hierarchy (e.g., GA, MGA, BGA overrides on downline production).']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the bonus qualification measurement window. Production activity after this date is not counted toward the current period\'s thresholds.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the bonus qualification measurement window during which the agent\'s production activity is counted toward threshold attainment.']
  persistency_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The proportion of the agent\'s in-force policies that have remained active (not lapsed or surrendered) during the qualification period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9200 = 92%). Persistency is a key quality-of-business metric used in bonus qualification to discourage churning.']
  persistency_threshold decimal(7,4) [note: 'The minimum persistency rate the agent must maintain during the qualification period to satisfy the persistency component of the bonus program. Falling below this threshold may result in disqualification.']
  policy_count_gap varchar [note: 'The remaining number of new business policies the agent must place to reach the policy count threshold (policy_count_threshold minus cumulative_policy_count). Negative values indicate the threshold has been exceeded.']
  policy_count_threshold varchar [note: 'The minimum number of new business policies the agent must place within the qualification period to satisfy the policy count component of the bonus program.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'The insurance product line(s) whose new business production counts toward this bonus qualification (e.g., life, annuity, disability, long_term_care, all). Some bonus programs are product-line specific.']
  projected_qualification_date date [note: 'Estimated date by which the agent is projected to meet all bonus qualification thresholds based on current production pace. Used in agent motivation reporting and coaching conversations.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the agent satisfied all required thresholds and achieved qualified status for the bonus program. Null if qualification has not yet been achieved.']
  qualification_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for this bonus qualification record, used in agent statements, incentive reports, and dispute resolution (e.g., BQ-2024-00123456).']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the agent\'s bonus qualification for this program and period. \'in_progress\' indicates the period is active and thresholds not yet met or missed; \'qualified\' indicates all thresholds have been satisfied; \'disqualified\' indicates a threshold was permanently missed or a compliance violation occurred; \'pending_review\' indicates manual review is required; \'forfeited\' indicates the agent voluntarily or contractually relinquished eligibility.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this bonus qualification record originated (e.g., SAPIENS_COMMISSION, ANNOCERA). Supports data lineage and ETL reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this bonus qualification record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated on every recalculation of cumulative metrics or status change.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking an agent\'s progress toward bonus program qualification thresholds during a qualification period. Captures cumulative APE, policy count, NBV, persistency rate, and qualification status (in-progress, qualified, disqualified) per bonus program per agent per period. Enables real-time incentive tracking and agent motivation reporting.'
}

Table commission.bonus_payment {
  bonus_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each bonus payment disbursement record. Primary key for the bonus_payment data product.']
  bonus_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bonus or incentive program under which this payment was awarded, linking to the program definition including eligibility rules, tiers, and payout schedules.']
  bonus_qualification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bonus_qualification record that triggered this payment, enabling reconciliation between qualification events and actual disbursements.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Bonus payments are compensation expenses requiring GL entries for accrual accounting, tax withholding, and cash disbursement. Incentive compensation must be journalized separately from base commission']
  payee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.payee. Business justification: bonus_payment is a bonus/incentive award disbursement to an agent or broker. The recipient is a payee in the commission domain. bonus_payment currently references primary_bonus_producer_id (cross-doma']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment run batch in which this bonus disbursement was processed, enabling batch-level reconciliation and audit.']
  primary_bonus_producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent or broker receiving this bonus payment. Used for multi-level hierarchy payout calculations and 1099 tax reporting.']
  reversed_payment_bonus_payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original bonus_payment record that this record reverses or corrects. Populated only for reversal or adjustment transactions, enabling full audit chain of payment corrections.']
  statement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commission statement on which this bonus payment appears. Links the disbursement to the agent-facing statement for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  ach_trace_number varchar [note: 'The unique trace number assigned by the originating financial institution for ACH payment transactions. Used for payment tracing, reconciliation, and dispute resolution with the agent\'s bank.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the bonus payment was formally approved for disbursement by the authorized approver.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the bonus payment prior to disbursement. Payments above defined thresholds may require manager or finance approval before processing.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager or finance officer who approved this bonus payment for disbursement. Supports SOX audit trail requirements for compensation approvals.']
  backup_withholding_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IRS backup withholding was applied to this bonus payment due to missing or incorrect TIN (Taxpayer Identification Number) on file for the agent.']
  bank_account_last4 varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the agent\'s bank account number used for ACH or direct deposit disbursement. Stored for payment verification and audit purposes without retaining full account details.']
  basis_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The qualifying production amount (e.g., APE, NBV, or premium volume) upon which the bonus calculation was applied. Represents the agent\'s measured performance metric for the qualification period.']
  bonus_basis varchar [note: 'The metric upon which the bonus amount was calculated. Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) and New Business Value (NBV) are common bases for life insurance production bonuses; policy_count and points are used for contest-based programs.']
  bonus_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The percentage or multiplier applied to the basis amount to calculate the gross bonus. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 = 5%). Derived from the applicable bonus program tier.']
  bonus_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bonus award by its business purpose. Production bonuses reward new business volume (NBV); persistency bonuses reward policy retention; recruiting bonuses reward agent growth; contest bonuses are time-limited incentive campaigns; override bonuses are hierarchy-based; special bonuses are discretionary awards.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Check number assigned when the bonus payment is disbursed via physical check. Used for check reconciliation, stop-payment processing, and audit trail.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bonus payment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the bonus payment amount (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international distribution channels.']
  federal_tax_withheld decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of federal income tax withheld from the gross bonus payment, applied when backup withholding is required or elected. Reported on IRS Form 1099-NEC for the applicable tax year.']
  gross_bonus_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total bonus amount earned before any tax withholding, adjustments, or deductions. Represents the full incentive award as calculated by the bonus program rules.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The agent\'s position in the distribution hierarchy at the time of this bonus payment. Determines override bonus eligibility and multi-level payout calculations. MGA = Managing General Agent, GA = General Agent, BGA = Brokerage General Agency.']
  is_1099_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bonus payment must be included in the agent\'s IRS Form 1099-NEC filing. Payments to incorporated entities (corporations) may be exempt from 1099 reporting under IRS rules.']
  net_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual amount disbursed to the agent after deducting federal and state tax withholding from the gross bonus amount. This is the amount reflected on the agent\'s commission statement and payment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or context related to this bonus payment. Used by commission administrators for exception handling and dispute documentation.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the bonus payment was disbursed to the agent or broker. Used for 1099 tax year assignment and cash flow reporting.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The disbursement method used to deliver the bonus payment to the agent. ACH and direct deposit are most common for recurring payments; wire for large awards; check for agents without electronic banking setup.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to this bonus payment disbursement, used for reconciliation, agent statements, and 1099 tax reporting. Sourced from Sapiens Commission / Annocera incentive tracking module.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the bonus payment disbursement. Tracks progression from pending approval through paid or cancelled/reversed states.']
  qualification_period_end date [note: 'End date of the performance measurement period during which the agent qualified for this bonus. Together with qualification_period_start, defines the full evaluation window.']
  qualification_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the performance measurement period during which the agent qualified for this bonus. Defines the window of production activity evaluated for eligibility.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for reversing or cancelling a previously issued bonus payment. Common reasons include policy lapse, qualification recalculation, agent termination, or data correction.']
  scheduled_payment_date date [note: 'The originally scheduled date for disbursement of the bonus payment, before any processing delays or holds. Enables variance analysis between scheduled and actual payment dates.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this bonus payment record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  source_system_payment_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this bonus payment record in the originating operational system (Sapiens Commission or Annocera). Enables traceability from the Silver Layer back to the system of record.']
  state_tax_withheld decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of state income tax withheld from the gross bonus payment, based on the agent\'s state of residence or the state\'s withholding requirements. Supports state-level 1099 reporting.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The calendar tax year to which this bonus payment is attributed for IRS 1099-NEC reporting purposes. Determines the tax reporting period for the agent\'s compensation.']
  tin_on_file varchar [note: 'The Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) — either SSN or EIN — on file for the agent at the time of payment. Masked for display (e.g., XXXXX1234). Required for IRS 1099-NEC reporting and backup withholding determination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bonus payment record was last modified. Tracks changes to payment status, amounts, or approval state for audit and reconciliation purposes.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of bonus and incentive award disbursements to agents and brokers upon successful qualification. Captures bonus program reference, qualification period, bonus amount, payment date, payment method, tax withholding, and net payment. Feeds 1099 tax reporting and is reconciled against bonus_qualification records. Sourced from Sapiens Commission / Annocera incentive tracking module.'
}

Table commission.statement {
  statement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the commission statement record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.agency. Business justification: Agency-level commission statements are issued to agencies summarizing all producer activity within the agency. This is a standard life insurance operational requirement for agency compensation reporti']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Commission statements are regulatory documents requiring retention, e-delivery tracking, and audit trails. Real business process: statement generation, electronic delivery consent verification, DOI co']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Commission statements are issued by a specific legal entity, determining the 1099 reporting entity, state tax withholding jurisdiction, and intercompany payable attribution. Multi-entity insurers requ']
  payee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.payee. Business justification: A commission statement is issued to a specific payee. payee is the commission-domain master for all statement recipients. statement currently references producer_id (cross-domain to agent.producer) bu']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment run batch that generated and funded this commission statement. Links the statement to the disbursement cycle.']
  privacy_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_incident. Business justification: Commission statements contain sensitive producer financial data (SSN, earnings, bank details); breaches must be tracked as privacy incidents. Business process: incident response for statement data exp']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent, broker, BGA, MGA, or GA for whom this commission statement is issued. Drives multi-level hierarchy payout calculations.']
  advance_recoupment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount withheld from this statement to recoup previously advanced commissions that have not yet been earned. Reduces net payable amount. Tracks outstanding advance balances per agent.']
  agent_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payee entity type: Agent (captive or independent), Broker, Brokerage General Agency (BGA), Managing General Agent (MGA), or General Agent (GA). Determines applicable commission schedule and hierarchy override rules.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the commission operations manager or system process that approved this statement for payment. Required for SOX audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the commission statement was formally approved for payment processing. Null if not yet approved. Part of the SOX-compliant approval audit trail.']
  backup_withholding_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IRS backup withholding at the statutory rate (24%) was applied to this statement due to a missing, incorrect, or flagged TIN. True = backup withholding applied; False = no withholding required.']
  bonus_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total bonus and incentive compensation earned during the statement period, including production bonuses, persistency bonuses, and sales contest awards. Tracked separately from base commissions for incentive program reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this commission statement record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field used for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this statement (e.g., USD). Life insurance operations are predominantly USD; multi-currency support required for international business.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the commission statement is delivered to the agent: email, agent portal (self-service), physical mail, EDI transmission to BGA/MGA systems, or suppressed (no delivery). Tracks statement distribution compliance.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current delivery status of the commission statement to the agent: Pending (not yet sent), Delivered (successfully transmitted), Failed (delivery attempt unsuccessful), Acknowledged (agent confirmed receipt via portal).']
