  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the commission statement was successfully delivered to the agent via the designated delivery channel. Null if delivery is pending or failed.']
  dispute_deadline_date date [note: 'Last date by which the agent may file a formal dispute against the commission amounts on this statement, per the contractual dispute window (typically 30–90 days from statement date). Enforces dispute eligibility rules.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agent has initiated a formal dispute against any commission amounts on this statement. True = dispute filed; False = no dispute. Triggers dispute workflow in the commission management system.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'Periodicity at which this statement is generated for the agent. Determines the cadence of commission payment cycles (e.g., monthly for renewal commissions, bi-weekly for FYC).']
  fyc_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of gross commissions attributable to first-year commission (FYC) earnings on new business (NB) policies issued during the statement period. Key metric for new business value (NBV) tracking and DAC amortization.']
  gross_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross commission earned by the agent during the statement period before any chargebacks, adjustments, or withholdings. Includes first-year commissions (FYC), renewal commissions, and override commissions.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of the agent within the distribution hierarchy (e.g., 1 = writing agent, 2 = GA, 3 = MGA, 4 = BGA). Used to apply tiered override commission rates in multi-level payout calculations.']
  is_1099_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the net paid amount on this statement contributes to the agent\'s IRS Form 1099-NEC reportable compensation for the tax year. True if the agent is a non-employee and cumulative YTD payments meet or exceed the IRS reporting threshold ($600).']
  net_payable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net amount payable to the agent after applying all chargebacks, adjustments, advance recoupments, and withholdings to gross commissions. This is the actual disbursement amount. Formula: gross_commission_amount + bonus_amount + service_fee_amount - total_chargeback_amount +/- total_adjustment_amount - advance_recoupment_amount - withholding_amount.']
  override_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Commission earned by this agent as a hierarchy override on production written by downline agents. Applicable to GA, MGA, and BGA levels in multi-level distribution structures.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date on which the net payable amount was or will be disbursed to the agent. Used for cash flow management and agent payment tracking.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the net payable amount is disbursed to the agent: ACH (direct deposit), check, wire transfer, offset against a debit balance, or held pending resolution. Drives payment processing routing.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the commission earning period covered by this statement. Defines the inclusive upper bound of the period for which commissions are summarized.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the commission earning period covered by this statement. Defines the inclusive lower bound of the period for which commissions are summarized.']
  policy_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct policies contributing to commission earnings on this statement. Used for production reporting and persistency analysis.']
  renewal_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of gross commissions attributable to renewal-year commissions earned on in-force policies during the statement period. Reflects persistency-based compensation.']
  service_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Service fees earned by the agent or broker for policy servicing activities during the statement period, distinct from sales-based commissions. Common in annuity and UL/IUL product lines.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Native statement identifier from the originating commission management system (e.g., Sapiens Commission or Annocera statement ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  statement_date date [note: 'The official date on which the commission statement was generated and issued to the agent or broker. Used as the reference date for dispute windows and 1099 tax reporting.']
  statement_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable statement reference number assigned by the commission management system (e.g., CS-2024-03-000142). Used by agents for reconciliation and dispute initiation.']
  statement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the commission statement. Draft: being assembled; Generated: finalized but not yet approved; Approved: cleared for payment; Paid: disbursement completed; Disputed: agent has raised a formal dispute; Voided: cancelled and replaced.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Calendar tax year to which the commission earnings on this statement are attributed for IRS 1099-NEC reporting purposes. Typically matches the statement period year but may differ for year-end adjustments.']
  tin varchar [note: 'IRS Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) of the agent or agency entity as recorded at statement generation time. May be an SSN (individual) or EIN (agency). Required for 1099 tax reporting and AML/KYC compliance. Stored as masked or tokenized value per data security policy.']
  tin_type varchar [note: 'Type of TIN associated with the agent payee: SSN (Social Security Number for individual agents), EIN (Employer Identification Number for agency entities), or ITIN (Individual Taxpayer Identification Number for non-resident aliens).']
  total_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net total of all manual and system-generated commission adjustments applied during the statement period (positive or negative). Includes corrections for prior-period errors, rate changes, and policy endorsements.']
  total_chargeback_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total commission chargebacks applied during the statement period due to policy lapses, surrenders, or early terminations within the chargeback recapture window. Reduces net payable amount.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual commission transactions (FYC, renewals, overrides, chargebacks, adjustments) included in this statement. Used for statement reconciliation and audit verification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this commission statement record was most recently modified. Updated on any status change, adjustment, dispute filing, or delivery status update. Used for incremental ETL processing and audit trails.']
  withholding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount withheld from the commission payment for backup withholding or other tax withholding obligations (e.g., IRS backup withholding at 24% for missing TIN). Required for 1099 tax reporting compliance.']
  ytd_gross_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative gross commission earned by the agent from the beginning of the calendar year through the end of this statement period. Required for IRS 1099-NEC annual reporting threshold monitoring.']
  ytd_net_paid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative net commission amount actually paid to the agent from the beginning of the calendar year through the end of this statement period. Used for 1099 tax form preparation and agent reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Periodic commission statement issued to agents, brokers, BGAs, MGAs, and GAs summarizing all commission earnings, chargebacks, adjustments, bonuses, and net payment for a statement period. Captures statement period start/end dates, total gross commissions, total chargebacks, total adjustments, total bonuses, net payable amount, and statement delivery status. Serves as the official compensation record for agent reconciliation and dispute initiation.'
}

Table commission.adjustment {
  adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each commission adjustment record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the commission_adjustment data product.']
  adjustment_override_agent_producer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supervising agent or manager entitled to an override commission on this adjustment. Populated when the adjustment cascades up the distribution hierarchy to affect override compensation.']
  adjustment_producer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the licensed agent or broker whose commission is being adjusted. Used for multi-level hierarchy payout recalculations and 1099 tax reporting reconciliation.']
  cession_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reinsurance.cession. Business justification: Reinsurance premium adjustments (NAR recalculations, treaty amendments, recapture events) trigger corresponding commission adjustments on the retained portion. This link ensures commission adjustments']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to annuity.annuity_contract. Business justification: Commission adjustments correct errors or apply retroactive changes to commissions on specific annuity contracts. Adjustment processing, audit trails, and producer dispute resolution require linking ad']
  dac_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.dac_asset. Business justification: adjustment carries dac_adjustment_amount, confirming commission adjustments affect DAC. Linking to dac_asset enables the DAC adjustment workflow to trace which asset is impacted by commission correcti']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Material commission adjustments require supporting documentation (audit findings, error correction memos, dispute resolution letters) for SOX compliance and FINRA recordkeeping. Insurance operations r']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the life insurance policy or annuity contract associated with this commission adjustment. Links the adjustment to the underlying insured risk for NBV impact tracking.']
  original_commission_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source commission transaction record that this adjustment corrects or modifies. Establishes the audit trail linking the adjustment back to the originating commission calculation event.']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commission payment run batch in which this adjustment was included for disbursement. Null if the adjustment has not yet been included in a payment run.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Adjustments require product context to recalculate correct commission amounts using product-specific rates, validate adjustment eligibility, and apply product-specific adjustment rules in commission c']
  premium_bill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_bill. Business justification: Commission adjustments frequently result from billing corrections, premium adjustments, or billing disputes. Operations require direct link to the corrected premium bill to recalculate commission basi']
  reinstatement_policy_reinstatement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.policy_reinstatement. Business justification: Policy reinstatements trigger commission adjustments to reverse lapse-period chargebacks and reinstate renewal commissions. Commission adjustment processing and producer statement reconciliation requi']
  reversed_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original commission_adjustment record that this entry reverses. Populated only when reversal_flag = True. Enables full reversal chain tracing.']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commission schedule (FYC or renewal rate table) applicable after the adjustment. Used to validate that the corrected rate aligns with the contractual schedule in effect.']
  statement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commission statement on which this adjustment appears. Links the adjustment to the agent-facing statement document for transparency and dispute resolution.']
  suitability_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.suitability_review. Business justification: Commission adjustments triggered by suitability failures — such as reversals on unsuitable replacement sales — must reference the suitability review that initiated the adjustment. This link supports c']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The financial reporting period (YYYY-MM) to which this commission adjustment is posted for GAAP and SAP statutory accounting purposes. Determines the general ledger period impacted and supports period-close reconciliation.']
  adjusted_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The resulting commission amount after applying this adjustment (original_commission_amount + adjustment_amount). Represents the corrected payable amount for the referenced transaction.']
  adjusted_commission_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The corrected commission rate (expressed as a decimal) applied after the adjustment. Populated for retroactive_rate_change adjustment types to document the new contractual rate.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for the adjustment record (e.g., ADJ-2024-00012345). Used in commission statements, dispute correspondence, and audit documentation.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the adjustment record. Drives processing eligibility: only \'approved\' adjustments are included in payment runs; \'reversed\' indicates a previously processed adjustment that was subsequently undone.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the reason driving the adjustment. Correction = data entry error fix; retroactive_rate_change = schedule amendment applied retroactively; policy_change = endorsement or rider modification; reinstatement = lapsed policy restored; 1035_exchange_credit = tax-free exchange credit per IRC Section 1035; chargeback_reversal = reversal of a previously applied chargeback.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the adjustment before any tax withholding. Positive values increase the agent\'s commission; negative values represent reductions or recoveries. Expressed in the currency indicated by currency_code.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the commission adjustment was formally approved by the designated approver. Used for SLA tracking and audit trail completeness.']
  calculation_basis_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The premium, annualized premium equivalent (APE), or account value amount used as the base for recalculating the adjusted commission. May differ from the original basis if the policy change altered the premium.']
  commission_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commission category being adjusted. FYC (First-Year Commission) applies to new business; renewal applies to in-force policy years; override applies to hierarchy-level supervisory commissions; bonus applies to incentive programs; service_fee applies to ongoing policy servicing fees; advance_recovery applies to recapture of previously advanced commissions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when this commission adjustment record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this adjustment record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international distribution channels.']
  dac_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The corresponding DAC (Deferred Acquisition Cost) balance adjustment triggered by this commission correction. Required for GAAP ASC 944 and LDTI compliance to ensure commission capitalization records remain accurate.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment increases (positive) or decreases (negative) the agent\'s commission. Supports sign-agnostic reporting and reconciliation without relying on the sign of the adjustment_amount field alone.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The sales and distribution channel through which the underlying policy was sold. Affects applicable commission schedule rules, override eligibility, and regulatory reporting requirements by channel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: career_agent|independent_agent|brokerage|bank_channel|direct|worksite|tpa — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'The business date on which the commission adjustment takes effect for accounting and payment purposes. For retroactive adjustments, this may precede the record creation date and determines the accounting period impacted.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The distribution hierarchy tier of the agent receiving the adjusted commission. Determines override commission eligibility and multi-level payout recalculation scope. BGA (Brokerage General Agency) and MGA (Managing General Agent) levels may trigger cascading override adjustments.']
  initiated_by varchar [note: 'Identifies the initiating party or system that created the adjustment request. \'system\' indicates an automated rule-engine trigger (e.g., policy lapse detection); other values indicate human-initiated corrections from specific business functions.']
  nbv_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated change in NBV (New Business Value) attributable to this commission adjustment. Used by actuarial and finance teams to assess the profitability impact of commission corrections on new business metrics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text commentary entered by the approver or initiator providing supplemental context for the adjustment. Supports dispute resolution and internal audit review.']
  original_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The commission amount as originally calculated and recorded on the source commission transaction prior to this adjustment. Retained for audit trail and variance analysis.']
  original_commission_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The commission rate (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.055000 = 5.5%) that was applied in the original commission transaction. Retained for rate variance analysis and retroactive rate change audits.']
  policy_year varchar [note: 'The policy duration year (1 = first year, 2 = renewal year 2, etc.) to which the adjusted commission applies. Determines whether FYC (First-Year Commission) or renewal commission rates govern the adjustment.']
  processed_date date [note: 'The date on which the approved adjustment was executed in the commission system and included in a payment run or ledger posting. Null for adjustments not yet processed.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'The insurance or annuity product category associated with the policy on which the commission adjustment is based. Drives applicable commission schedule rules and DAC (Deferred Acquisition Cost) accounting treatment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_life|term_life|universal_life|variable_universal_life|indexed_universal_life|fixed_annuity|fixed_indexed_annuity|variable_annuity|long_term_care|disability_income — promote to reference product]']
  reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured narrative describing the specific business reason for the commission adjustment. Required for audit trail compliance and commission dispute resolution. Examples: \'Rate table corrected per MGA contract amendment effective 2024-01-01\', \'Policy reinstated after lapse — FYC recalculated\'.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code classifying the root cause of the adjustment for systematic reporting and trend analysis. Complements the free-text adjustment_reason field. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data_entry_error|rate_table_update|policy_endorsement|policy_lapse_reinstatement|1035_exchange|chargeback_waiver|hierarchy_correction|split_correction|advance_reconciliation — promote to reference product]']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment record is itself a reversal of a prior adjustment (True = reversal entry). Supports double-entry audit trail for commission ledger integrity.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this commission adjustment record. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and upstream source systems.']
  split_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'The proportion of the adjusted commission allocated to the primary agent when a co-agent split arrangement applies (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.6000 = 60%). Used to recalculate split amounts after adjustment.']
  tax_withholding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Federal or state income tax withheld from the adjusted commission payment, if applicable. Relevant for agents subject to backup withholding per IRS regulations. Impacts 1099-MISC / 1099-NEC reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when this commission adjustment record was last modified. Updated on every status transition, approval action, or data correction.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of manual or system-generated corrections to commission amounts outside of normal calculation cycles. Captures adjustment type (correction, retroactive rate change, policy change, reinstatement, 1035 exchange credit), original commission transaction reference, adjustment amount (positive or negative), adjustment reason, approver, and effective date. Supports audit trail for all post-calculation commission modifications.'
}

Table commission.run {
  run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each commission calculation and payment processing cycle record in the Agent and Commission Management System (Sapiens Commission / Annocera).']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Commission runs generate expense totals (gross_commission_amount, override_commission_amount, bonus_incentive_amount) that must be allocated to cost centers for financial reporting and budget variance']
  hierarchy_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.hierarchy_node. Business justification: Commission runs are often scoped to specific hierarchy nodes for territory-based or regional processing. Linking run to hierarchy_node enables targeted commission processing, hierarchy-scoped reconcil']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Commission runs are executed by specific legal entities for regulatory reporting (NAIC company code), tax filing (EIN), and consolidated financial statements. Multi-entity insurers require entity-leve']
  prior_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding commission run that this run supersedes, corrects, or is linked to (e.g., for adjustment or reversal run types). Enables run lineage tracing and prior-period reconciliation.']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: Period-end financial close requires reconciling the commission payment run against the actuarial valuation run for DAC amortization entries, LDTI compliance, and reserve-to-commission expense reconcil']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The financial accounting period (YYYY-MM) to which this commission run\'s payables are posted in the general ledger. Aligns commission expense recognition with GAAP ASC 944 and SAP statutory reporting requirements.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the commission calculation engine began processing this run. Used for performance monitoring, SLA compliance, and audit trail of processing timelines.']
  advance_recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount recovered in this run against outstanding commission advances previously paid to agents. Deducted from gross commission before net payable calculation. Tracks advance repayment progress across run cycles.']
  agents_processed_count varchar [note: 'The total number of distinct agent and broker records for which commission calculations were performed in this run. Used for run completeness validation and agent payment reconciliation.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the commission run was formally approved for payment release by an authorized manager. Null until approval is granted. Supports SOX audit trail and dual-control documentation.']
  bonus_incentive_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total bonus and incentive compensation calculated in this run, including production bonuses, persistency bonuses, and contest awards. Separate from base commission for expense categorization and 1099 reporting.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the commission run finished processing, whether successfully completed or failed. Null if the run has not yet concluded. Used for SLA measurement and downstream payment release gating.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this commission run record was first created in the system. Serves as the immutable audit creation marker for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this commission run (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international distribution channels.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The agent distribution channel segment processed in this commission run. Determines applicable commission schedules, hierarchy override rules, and regulatory disclosure requirements. BGA = Brokerage General Agency; MGA = Managing General Agent; TPA = Third Party Administrator. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: career_agent|independent_agent|bga|mga|broker_dealer|direct|tpa|bank — promote to reference product]']
  error_count varchar [note: 'The total number of error conditions encountered during this commission run that prevented individual transaction calculations from completing successfully. A non-zero value requires investigation before payment release.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Summary description of the primary error or failure reason when the run status is \'failed\'. Provides operational context for troubleshooting and reprocessing decisions. Null for successful runs.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'The scheduled recurrence cadence for this commission run type. Drives the run calendar configuration in Sapiens Commission / Annocera and determines payment cycle expectations for agents and brokers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weekly|bi_weekly|semi_monthly|monthly|quarterly|annual|ad_hoc — promote to reference product]']
  fyc_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total first-year commission (FYC) component of gross commission calculated in this run, representing commissions earned on new business policies issued within their first policy year. Key metric for new business value (NBV) reporting and DAC amortization.']
  gross_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross commission calculated across all agents and policies in this run before chargebacks, adjustments, or withholdings are applied. Represents the sum of all first-year (FYC), renewal, override, and bonus commissions earned in the period.']
  is_test_run boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commission run was executed as a simulation or test cycle (True) rather than a production run (False). Test runs calculate commissions but do not generate actual payment transactions or 1099 reportable amounts.']
  net_payable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final net amount payable to agents and brokers in this commission run, calculated as gross commission minus chargebacks plus/minus adjustments. This is the amount released to the payment processing system for disbursement.']
  override_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total hierarchy-based override commission calculated in this run, representing additional compensation paid to upline agents (GA, MGA, BGA) based on the production of their downline agents. Supports multi-level payout calculations.']
  payment_release_date date [note: 'The date on which commission payments from this run were approved and released to the payment processing system for disbursement to agents and brokers. Null until payments are released.']
  payment_release_status varchar [note: 'The approval and release status of commission payments generated by this run. Payments move from pending (run completed) to approved (management sign-off) to released (sent to payment system). Held or rejected statuses require remediation.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the commission earning period covered by this run. Defines the inclusive end boundary for policy transactions, premiums, and events eligible for commission calculation in this cycle.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the commission earning period covered by this run. Defines the inclusive start boundary for policy transactions, premiums, and events eligible for commission calculation in this cycle.']
  policies_processed_count varchar [note: 'The total number of policy records evaluated and processed during this commission run. Includes all policies with commission-eligible events within the run period. Used for run reconciliation and volume reporting.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'The insurance product line segment included in this commission run. Determines applicable commission rate schedules and regulatory reporting requirements. LTC = Long-Term Care. \'all\' indicates a cross-product run.']
  renewal_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total renewal commission component of gross commission calculated in this run, representing commissions earned on in-force policies beyond their first policy year. Distinct from FYC for DAC and expense ratio analysis.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the commission run, formatted as CR-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN. Used in commission statements, audit trails, and dispute references.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the commission run within the processing workflow. Transitions: scheduled → in_progress → completed or failed. Cancelled and on_hold are terminal or paused states requiring manual intervention. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|in_progress|completed|failed|cancelled|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commission processing cycle indicating its business purpose. Regular runs are scheduled periodic cycles; off-cycle runs are unscheduled; adjustment runs correct prior period errors; reversal runs undo a prior run; bonus runs process incentive payouts; supplemental runs handle additional payments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular|off_cycle|adjustment|reversal|bonus|supplemental — promote to reference product]']
  scheduled_run_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this commission run was originally scheduled to execute. Used for SLA tracking, run calendar management, and reconciliation against actual execution date.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this commission run record. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and reconciliation between source systems.']
  source_system_run_ref varchar [note: 'The native run identifier or batch reference number assigned by the originating source system (Sapiens Commission or Annocera). Enables direct traceability back to the operational system record for audit and reconciliation.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The IRS tax year (YYYY) to which commission payments in this run are attributed for 1099-NEC reporting purposes. Critical for year-end tax reporting reconciliation and FinCEN AML compliance.']
  total_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net sum of all manual corrections, dispute resolutions, and prior-period adjustments applied in this commission run. May be positive (additional payment) or negative (reduction). Distinct from chargebacks which are rule-driven recaptures.']
  total_chargeback_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate amount of commission chargebacks applied in this run due to policy lapses, surrenders, cancellations, or other recapture events. Reduces gross commission to arrive at net payable.']
  total_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate federal and state income tax withheld from agent commission payments in this run, applicable for agents subject to backup withholding. Required for IRS 1099 tax reporting reconciliation.']
  transactions_generated_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual commission transaction records created by this run, including first-year commissions (FYC), renewals, overrides, and service fees. Used for run output validation and downstream reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this commission run record was most recently modified. Tracks status transitions, corrections, and any updates to run metadata for audit and change management purposes.']
  warning_count varchar [note: 'The total number of warning conditions flagged during this commission run that did not prevent processing but require review (e.g., missing licensing data, rate schedule gaps, hierarchy mismatches).']

  Note: 'Operational master record representing a scheduled commission calculation and payment processing cycle. Captures run type (regular, off-cycle, adjustment), run period start/end dates, run status (scheduled, in-progress, completed, failed), total policies processed, total commission calculated, total chargebacks applied, total net payable, and run completion timestamp. Provides the operational control record for each commission processing cycle in Sapiens Commission / Annocera.'
}

Table commission.payee {
  payee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the commission payee record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the payee master entity.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.agency. Business justification: In life insurance, commissions are frequently paid to agency entities rather than individual producers. A payee can represent an agency (not just a producer). Linking payee to agency enables agency-le']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier linking this payee to the corresponding agent record in the agent domain. Enables operational linkage between the financial disbursement identity (payee) and the licensing/appointment/hierarchy record (agent). Nullable for non-agent payees such as corporate BGAs or TPAs with no direct agent record.']
  w9_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Payee has w9_certified and w9_certification_date fields requiring the actual W-9/W-8 document on file. IRS backup withholding rules and FINRA recordkeeping require insurers to retain and reference the']
  ach_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number at the payee\'s financial institution for ACH direct deposit of commission payments. Required when payment_method is ACH. May be checking or savings account. Stored in encrypted/tokenized form per PCI DSS requirements. Only the last four digits are displayed in operational interfaces.']
  ach_account_type varchar [note: 'Type of bank account used for ACH commission payment deposits. Checking accounts are most common for business payees; savings accounts may be used by individual agents. Required for ACH payment file generation per NACHA formatting standards.']
  ach_routing_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit ABA routing transit number identifying the payee\'s financial institution for ACH direct deposit of commission payments. Required when payment_method is ACH. Validated against the Federal Reserve\'s routing number database. Stored in encrypted form per PCI DSS requirements.']
  advance_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payee is eligible to receive commission advances — upfront payment of future expected commissions against new business production. Eligibility is typically based on production history, chargeback record, and contractual agreement. When True, the commission system may process advance payments subject to advance repayment tracking.']
  advance_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding commission advance balance permitted for this payee at any point in time. Expressed in the payee\'s payment currency. Enforced by the commission management system to limit financial exposure from advance chargebacks. Null if advance_eligible is False.']
  backup_withholding_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payee is subject to mandatory IRS backup withholding on commission payments. Set to True when the IRS notifies the company of a TIN mismatch, when the payee fails to certify their TIN on Form W-9, or when the payee has underreported income. When True, the payment processing system must withhold the statutory backup withholding rate from all reportable payments.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution where the payee holds the account designated for commission payment deposits. Used for payment reconciliation, remittance advice generation, and ACH return resolution. Stored for operational reference alongside the routing and account numbers.']
  commission_statement_delivery varchar [note: 'Payee\'s preferred delivery method for periodic commission statements. Electronic = PDF delivered via secure portal or email; Paper = printed statement mailed to mailing address; Both = electronic and paper. Drives statement generation and distribution workflows in the commission management system.']
  crd_number varchar [note: 'FINRA-assigned Central Registration Depository (CRD) number for payees who are registered representatives or broker-dealers selling variable life insurance or variable annuity products. Required for FINRA regulatory reporting and suitability compliance for variable product commissions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payee record was first created in the commission management system. Represents the business onboarding event time, not the ETL load time. Used for audit trail, compliance reporting, and payee lifecycle analytics.']
  doing_business_as_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or DBA name under which the payee operates, if different from the legal name. Common for BGAs, MGAs, and GAs operating under a brand name distinct from their legal entity name. Used on commission statements and correspondence.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the payee record became active and eligible to receive commission payments. Typically corresponds to the date the payee\'s contract with the company was executed and all onboarding requirements (W-9, KYC, banking) were satisfied.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the payee used for electronic commission statement delivery, payment notifications, W-9 re-certification requests, and compliance communications. For entity payees, this is typically the accounts payable or finance contact email.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Position of the payee within the multi-level commission hierarchy used for override commission calculations. Determines eligibility for first-year commission (FYC), renewal commissions, and hierarchy-based override payments. Writing Agent = direct producer; Field Manager = first-level override; Regional Manager = second-level override; BGA/MGA = wholesale distribution override levels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: writing_agent|field_manager|regional_manager|bga|mga|home_office|other — promote to reference product]']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current Know Your Customer (KYC) verification status for the payee as required by FinCEN AML/BSA regulations. Verified = identity confirmed and AML screening passed; Pending = verification in progress; Failed = identity verification or AML screening failed, payments blocked; Exempt = entity type exempt from KYC requirements (e.g., publicly traded corporations). Payments must not be processed to payees with Failed KYC status.']
  kyc_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the payee\'s KYC identity verification and AML screening was most recently completed and approved. Used to determine KYC currency and trigger periodic re-verification workflows per the company\'s AML compliance program.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the payee as registered with the IRS and state authorities. For individuals, this is the name on the W-9 form. For entities, this is the registered business name. Used for 1099 tax reporting, ACH payment files, and check issuance. Must match the name associated with the TIN on file.']
  license_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation for the state in which the payee holds their primary insurance producer license. Used to validate appointment status and commission payment eligibility per state-specific insurance regulations.']
  mailing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for mailing commission checks, 1099 tax forms, and commission statements to the payee. For individuals, this is typically a home or office address. For entities, this is the registered business address.']
  mailing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, apartment, unit, floor, or PO Box information for the payee\'s mailing address. Optional field used when the primary address line is insufficient to fully specify the delivery location.']
  mailing_city varchar [note: 'City component of the payee\'s mailing address. Used for check mailing, 1099 form delivery, and commission statement distribution.']
  mailing_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the payee\'s mailing address. Defaults to USA for domestic payees. Required for international wire transfer payees and cross-border distribution arrangements. Drives FATCA and FBAR reporting obligations for foreign payees.']
  mailing_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the payee\'s mailing address. Used for check delivery, 1099 form mailing, and geographic analytics on distribution channel coverage.']
  mailing_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation for the payee\'s mailing address. Used for state income tax withholding determination, state-level 1099 reporting, and compliance with state Department of Insurance requirements.']
  npn varchar [note: 'NAIC-assigned National Producer Number (NPN) uniquely identifying the payee as an insurance producer across all US states. Used for cross-state license verification via the NAIC NIPR database, appointment validation, and regulatory reporting. Mandatory for all licensed producer payees.']
  ofac_last_screened_date date [note: 'Date on which the payee was most recently screened against OFAC sanctions lists. Used to enforce periodic re-screening requirements and ensure ongoing compliance with US sanctions regulations before each payment run.']
  ofac_screened boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payee has been screened against the OFAC (Office of Foreign Assets Control) Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list and other sanctions lists. Must be True before any commission payment is disbursed. Payments to OFAC-listed entities are prohibited by US law.']
  payee_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier assigned to the payee by the Agent and Commission Management System (e.g., Sapiens Commission, Annocera). Used on commission statements, 1099 forms, and payment remittance advices to uniquely identify the payee across operational systems.']
  payee_role varchar [note: 'Distribution channel role of the payee within the life insurance and annuity sales ecosystem. Drives commission schedule selection, hierarchy override eligibility, and regulatory reporting. BGA = Brokerage General Agency; MGA = Managing General Agent; GA = General Agent; RIA = Registered Investment Advisor; TPA = Third Party Administrator. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: agent|broker|bga|mga|ga|ria|tpa|broker_dealer|other — promote to reference product]']
  payee_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payee record in the commission management system. Active = eligible to receive commission payments; Inactive = no current production but record retained; Suspended = payments on hold pending investigation or compliance review; Pending = onboarding in progress, W-9 or banking not yet verified; Terminated = permanently closed, no further payments.']
  payee_type varchar [note: 'Legal entity classification of the payee for tax reporting and payment processing purposes. Determines W-9 federal tax classification, 1099 reporting obligations, and backup withholding rules. Individual = natural person agent/broker; Corporation = incorporated entity; Partnership = general or limited partnership; LLC = limited liability company; Trust = ILIT or other trust entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: individual|corporation|partnership|llc|trust|other — promote to reference product]']
  payment_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for commission payment disbursements to this payee. Defaults to USD for domestic US payees. May be CAD or other currencies for cross-border distribution arrangements. Drives currency conversion logic in the commission payment run.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for disbursing commission payments to this payee. ACH = Automated Clearing House electronic funds transfer (most common); Check = paper check mailed to payee address; Wire = domestic or international wire transfer for large or international payees. Drives payment run processing logic in the commission management system.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the payee. Used for payment issue resolution, W-9 certification follow-up, and commission dispute communications. For entity payees, this is the main business phone number.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this payee record was sourced (e.g., Sapiens Commission, Annocera, Salesforce Financial Services Cloud). Used for data lineage tracking, ETL reconciliation, and issue resolution in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  source_system_ref varchar [note: 'Native primary key or unique identifier of this payee record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Sapiens Commission internal payee ID, Annocera agent code). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the source system for reconciliation and issue resolution.']
  state_license_number varchar [note: 'Primary state insurance producer license number for the payee. For individual agents and brokers, this is the license number in their primary state of appointment. For entity payees (BGAs, MGAs), this is the entity license number. Used to validate commission payment eligibility — commissions may only be paid to licensed producers per state insurance regulations.']
  tax_classification_1099 varchar [note: 'IRS 1099 form type classification for annual tax reporting of commission and fee payments to this payee. 1099-NEC = non-employee compensation for individual agents/brokers; 1099-MISC = miscellaneous income for certain entity payees; 1099-R = distributions from retirement/annuity contracts; Exempt = corporations exempt from 1099 reporting per IRS rules; Not Reportable = payments below IRS reporting threshold or otherwise excluded.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the payee\'s commission payment eligibility was terminated. Null for active payees. After this date, no new commission payments are processed except for renewal commissions or chargebacks governed by the payee\'s contract terms. Used in conjunction with payee_status to manage the full payee lifecycle.']
  tin varchar [note: 'IRS-issued Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) used for 1099 tax reporting and backup withholding determination. For individuals, this is the Social Security Number (SSN); for entities, this is the Employer Identification Number (EIN). Stored in masked or encrypted form per PCI/PII data protection requirements. Must match the name on the W-9 certification.']
  tin_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the TIN on file is a Social Security Number (SSN) for individual payees or an Employer Identification Number (EIN) for entity payees. Drives IRS 1099 form type selection (1099-NEC vs 1099-MISC) and backup withholding logic.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payee record in the commission management system. Tracks changes to banking details, W-9 status, KYC status, payment preferences, or any other payee attributes. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the lakehouse ETL pipeline and audit trail compliance.']
  w9_certification_date date [note: 'Date on which the payee\'s most recent IRS Form W-9 was signed and certified. Used to determine W-9 currency and trigger re-certification workflows when the form is stale or when payee information changes (e.g., name change, TIN change).']
  w9_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payee has submitted a valid, signed IRS Form W-9 certifying their TIN and federal tax classification. Payments to payees without a certified W-9 are subject to mandatory backup withholding at the IRS-prescribed rate. Required before any commission disbursement can be processed.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing all commission payees — individual agents, brokers, BGAs, MGAs, GAs, RIAs, TPAs, broker-dealers, and corporate entities — eligible to receive commission and fee payments. Captures payee legal name, payee type (individual, corporation, partnership, LLC), TIN/SSN, banking details (ACH routing/account number), payment preference (ACH, check, wire), W-9 certification status, backup withholding flag, 1099 reporting classification, and payee active/inactive status. This is the financial disbursement identity — distinct from the agent domain\'s agent record which owns licensing, appointment, and hierarchy data. Cross-referenced to agent domain via agent identifier for operational linkage.'
}

Table commission.compensation_contract {
  compensation_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the compensation contract record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.agency. Business justification: Agencies have their own compensation contracts with carriers governing override rates and agency-level compensation terms. A compensation_contract can belong to an agency entity (not just an individua']
  agency_producer_affiliation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.agency_producer_affiliation. Business justification: Agency-producer affiliation arrangements often have distinct compensation contracts governing split percentages and agency override terms. Linking compensation_contract to agency_producer_affiliation ']
  bonus_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific bonus or incentive program applicable to this compensation contract. Governs production thresholds, qualification periods, and bonus payout structures.']
  chargeback_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chargeback rule set governing recapture of commissions paid under this contract in the event of policy lapse, surrender, or early termination.']
  contract_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Compensation contracts are legal agreements requiring signature workflow, version control, legal hold capability, and long-term retention. Real business process: contract execution, e-signature captur']
  contracting_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.contracting. Business justification: Compensation contracts define commission terms for producer contracting agreements. Business requires linking contract_number across domains for commission dispute resolution, contract amendment track']
  jurisdiction_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_license. Business justification: Compensation contracts are state-specific and can only be executed in jurisdictions where the insurer holds a valid license. Compliance teams validate compensation contracts against jurisdiction licen']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Compensation contracts are legal agreements between a producer and a specific issuing legal entity, determining the tax reporting entity for 1099 issuance, intercompany commission allocation, and regu']
  payee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.payee. Business justification: A compensation_contract is a formal agreement between the insurer and a distribution entity. payee is the commission-domain master representing all such distribution entities as payees. compensation_c']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Contracts specify product line eligibility; product_line exists but plan_id FK enables granular product authorization validation, product-specific contract term application, and agent product appointm']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Compensation contracts specify commission terms for specific product versions. When a plan_version is released or retired, new compensation contracts are executed. Linking enables producer compensatio']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution entity (agent, broker, BGA, MGA, GA, or broker-dealer) that is the primary counterparty to this compensation contract.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Compensation contracts are subject to specific regulatory obligations including FINRA disclosure rules, state producer compensation disclosure requirements, and DOL fiduciary standards. Compliance tea']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commission schedule that governs the base compensation rates applicable under this contract.']
  separate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.separate_account. Business justification: Compensation contracts for variable annuity producers are scoped to eligible separate account fund families (e.g., proprietary funds only). This link supports producer eligibility validation, FINRA ov']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Compensation contracts must comply with state-specific commission regulations including disclosure requirements and compensation caps. The state_code plain attribute is a denormalization of state_regu']
  superseded_by_contract_compensation_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the replacement compensation contract that supersedes this record upon amendment or renegotiation. Null if this is the current active version.']
  advance_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contracted entity is eligible to receive commission advances (pre-payment of future earned commissions) under this contract.']
  advance_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of earned first-year commission that may be advanced to the contracted entity prior to full earning. Governs the advance provision under this contract.']
  advance_repayment_months varchar [note: 'The number of months over which commission advances must be repaid through earned commission offsets under this contract.']
  ape_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) production threshold that the contracted entity must achieve to qualify for enhanced commission rates, bonus programs, or override entitlements under this contract.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the compensation contract received final internal approval and was authorized for commission processing.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The internal approval workflow status of the compensation contract. Contracts must be approved before commission calculations can be initiated.']
  backup_withholding_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contracted entity is subject to IRS backup withholding on commission payments due to TIN mismatch, IRS notification, or failure to certify TIN.']
  bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contracted entity is eligible to participate in bonus and incentive programs (e.g., production bonuses, persistency bonuses, Annual Premium Equivalent (APE) thresholds) under this contract.']
  chargeback_period_months varchar [note: 'The number of months from policy issue date during which commissions paid under this contract are subject to chargeback recapture upon policy lapse or surrender.']
  compensation_basis varchar [note: 'The primary basis on which compensation is calculated under this contract. FYC (First-Year Commission) applies to new business; renewal applies to in-force policies; fee-based applies to service arrangements; hybrid combines multiple bases.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the compensation contract as assigned by the Agent and Commission Management System. Used in correspondence, statements, and regulatory filings.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the compensation contract. Drives eligibility for commission calculation, payment processing, and chargeback enforcement.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution entity type that is party to this contract. Determines the applicable compensation structure and regulatory requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: agent|bga|mga|ga|broker_dealer|tpa — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compensation contract record was first created in the lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary compensation amounts under this contract are denominated.']
  dac_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether commissions paid under this contract qualify as Deferred Acquisition Costs (DAC) for GAAP and statutory accounting purposes under FASB ASC 944.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The sales and distribution channel through which the contracted entity operates. Determines applicable commission schedules, override structures, and regulatory requirements.']
  e_o_coverage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contracted distribution entity is required to maintain active Errors and Omissions (E&O) professional liability insurance as a condition of this compensation contract.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the compensation contract becomes legally binding and commission obligations commence. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.']
  finra_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compensation paid under this contract is subject to FINRA reporting requirements, applicable for variable product (VUL, variable annuity) distribution arrangements.']
  fyc_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The contracted First-Year Commission (FYC) rate expressed as a percentage of target premium for new business written under this contract. Represents the primary compensation incentive for new policy sales.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The level of the contracted entity within the agent distribution hierarchy. Governs override commission entitlements and multi-level payout calculations tied to New Business Value (NBV).']
  nbv_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether production under this compensation contract is included in New Business Value (NBV) calculations for actuarial and financial reporting purposes.']
  override_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compensation contract entitles the distribution entity to receive override commissions on downline production. True for BGA, MGA, GA, and supervising agent contracts.']
  override_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The contracted override commission rate entitlement for this entity on production generated by downline agents. Applicable for BGA, MGA, GA, and supervising agent contracts.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'The contracted frequency at which commission payments are processed and disbursed to the distribution entity under this agreement.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The contracted method by which commission payments are disbursed to the distribution entity: ACH direct deposit, paper check, wire transfer, or offset against outstanding advances.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'The insurance product line(s) covered by this compensation contract. Determines which policy types generate commissions under this agreement.']
  renewal_commission_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The contracted renewal commission rate expressed as a percentage of renewal premium for in-force policies beyond the first policy year. Incentivizes persistency and ongoing policy servicing.']
  source_contract_ref varchar [note: 'The native contract identifier as recorded in the originating Agent and Commission Management System (e.g., Sapiens Commission or Annocera). Enables traceability back to the system of record.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational source system from which this compensation contract record was ingested (e.g., SAPIENS_COMMISSION, ANNOCERA). Supports data lineage and ETL reconciliation.']
  tax_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compensation paid under this contract is subject to IRS 1099-MISC or 1099-NEC reporting requirements. Drives annual tax reporting obligations.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the compensation contract ends and commission obligations cease. Nullable for open-ended contracts. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for contract termination when the contract_status is terminated or expired. Drives chargeback enforcement, vesting forfeiture, and regulatory notification requirements.']
  tin varchar [note: 'The Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) of the contracted distribution entity as required for IRS 1099 tax reporting. May be an SSN for individual agents or EIN for business entities.']
  tin_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the type of Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) on file for the contracted entity: SSN (Social Security Number) for individuals, EIN for business entities, or ITIN for foreign individuals.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compensation contract record was last modified in the lakehouse. Supports change detection, audit trail, and incremental ETL processing.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the compensation contract, incremented each time material terms are amended. Supports contract amendment history and audit trail.']
  vesting_period_months varchar [note: 'The number of months of continuous contract tenure required before renewal commissions and override entitlements become fully vested for the contracted entity.']

  Note: 'Master record of the formal compensation agreement between the insurer and a distribution entity (agent, BGA, MGA, GA, broker-dealer). Captures contract type, contracted commission schedule references, override entitlements, bonus program eligibility, chargeback terms, advance provisions, effective date, termination date, and contract status. Serves as the authoritative agreement governing all commission and compensation obligations for a distribution relationship.'
}

Table product.plan {
  plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product plan. Primary key.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Products map to specific GL accounts for premium revenue recognition, reserve liability booking, and DAC asset capitalization. Required for automated journal entry generation, financial statement prep']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Products are issued by specific insurance carriers (legal entities). Essential for statutory reporting by NAIC company code, RBC calculations, and state regulatory filings. Every product must be owned']
  cash_value_option boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this product plan includes a cash value accumulation component. True for permanent life insurance and annuities; false for term life and pure protection products.']
  contestability_period_years varchar [note: 'The number of years from policy issue during which the insurer can contest claims based on material misrepresentation in the application. Typically 2 years per state regulation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this product plan record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  dac_amortization_method varchar [note: 'The accounting method used to amortize deferred acquisition costs for this product plan under GAAP and LDTI standards. Used in actuarial valuation and financial reporting.']
  distribution_channel_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of distribution channels through which this product plan can be sold (e.g., career_agent, independent_broker, bank_channel, direct_to_consumer, worksite). Used in agent authorization and commission calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: career_agent|independent_broker|bank_channel|direct_to_consumer|worksite|bga|mga — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this product plan becomes available for sale and new business issuance. Used in policy administration and new business processing.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this product plan is no longer available for new business. Nullable for plans with no planned retirement date. Existing policies remain in force after this date.']
  free_look_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of days after policy delivery during which the policyholder can cancel the policy for a full refund. Varies by state regulation, typically 10-30 days.']
  gpt_corridor_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'The corridor factor used in the Guideline Premium Test calculation to ensure IRC 7702 compliance. Defines the minimum death benefit relative to cash value at each attained age.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of days after a premium due date during which the policy remains in force without penalty. Typically 30-31 days per state regulation.']
  guaranteed_issue boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this product plan is available on a guaranteed issue basis without medical underwriting. Used in underwriting workbench and new business processing.']
  illustration_software_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the illustration software configuration used to generate policy illustrations for this product plan. Used in point-of-sale systems and agent tools.']
  insurance_type varchar [note: 'The specific insurance product type code. WL (Whole Life), UL (Universal Life), IUL (Indexed Universal Life), VUL (Variable Universal Life), YRT (Yearly Renewable Term), FIA (Fixed Indexed Annuity), SPIA (Single Premium Immediate Annuity), GMAB (Guaranteed Minimum Accumulation Benefit), GMDB (Guaranteed Minimum Death Benefit), GMIB (Guaranteed Minimum Income Benefit), GMWB (Guaranteed Minimum Withdrawal Benefit), DI (Disability Income). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WL|UL|IUL|VUL|YRT|FIA|SPIA|GMAB|GMDB|GMIB|GMWB|DI — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  irc_7702_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product plan is designed to comply with IRC Section 7702 life insurance tax qualification requirements including cash value accumulation test and guideline premium test.']
  loan_provision boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether policyholders can take loans against the cash value of this product plan. Used in policy servicing and customer service operations.']
  maximum_face_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum death benefit or coverage amount that can be issued under this product plan without special underwriting approval. Used in underwriting and new business validation.']
  maximum_issue_age varchar [note: 'The maximum age at which an applicant is eligible to purchase this product plan. Used in underwriting rules and new business processing.']
  maximum_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum premium amount allowed for this product plan without triggering MEC status or requiring special approval. Used in new business validation and IRC 7702 compliance testing.']
  minimum_face_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum death benefit or coverage amount that can be issued under this product plan. Used in underwriting and new business validation.']
  minimum_issue_age varchar [note: 'The minimum age at which an applicant is eligible to purchase this product plan. Used in underwriting rules and new business processing.']
  minimum_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum premium amount required for this product plan, typically expressed as annual premium equivalent. Used in new business validation and underwriting.']
  naic_product_type_code varchar [note: 'The three-digit NAIC product type code used for statutory reporting and regulatory filings. Required for Annual Statement Schedule T reporting.']
  participating_product boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this product plan is a participating policy eligible to receive dividends based on company experience. Used in actuarial valuation and policyholder communications.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying this product plan across all systems and distribution channels. Used in policy administration, underwriting, and billing systems.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the product plan including key features, benefits, and target market. Used for agent training and customer education materials.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'The full marketing and legal name of the product plan as it appears in policy documents, illustrations, and customer communications.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the product plan. Active plans are available for new business; filed plans are pending regulatory approval; approved plans are cleared but not yet active; suspended plans are temporarily unavailable; retired plans are closed to new business; withdrawn plans have been removed from filing.']
  premium_mode_options varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of premium payment frequency options available for this product plan (e.g., annual, semi_annual, quarterly, monthly, single). Used in billing system configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual|semi_annual|quarterly|monthly|single|flexible — promote to reference product]']
  product_family varchar [note: 'The product family grouping used for portfolio management, profitability analysis, and strategic planning. Examples include term life family, universal life family, fixed annuity family.']
  profitability_target_irr decimal(5,4) [note: 'The target internal rate of return for this product plan used in pricing and profitability analysis. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1200 for 12.00%). Business-confidential metric.']
  reinstatement_period_months varchar [note: 'The number of months after policy lapse during which the policyholder can reinstate the policy without new underwriting. Typically 3-5 years per state regulation.']
  reinsurance_treaty_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the reinsurance treaty under which this product plan is ceded. Used in reinsurance management system for cession processing and bordereaux reporting.']
  reserve_basis varchar [note: 'The primary reserve calculation basis for this product plan. Statutory (SAP), GAAP, IFRS 17, or Principle-Based Reserving (PBR). Used in actuarial valuation system.']
  retirement_reason varchar [note: 'The business or regulatory reason for retiring this product plan. Used in product lifecycle management and strategic planning.']
  simplified_issue boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this product plan uses simplified underwriting with limited health questions and no medical exam. Used in underwriting workbench and new business processing.']
  state_approval_count varchar [note: 'The number of US states and territories in which this product plan has received regulatory approval for sale. Used in distribution planning and compliance monitoring.']
  suicide_exclusion_period_years varchar [note: 'The number of years from policy issue during which death by suicide results in limited or no death benefit payment. Typically 2 years per state regulation.']
  surrender_charge_schedule varchar [note: 'Reference to the surrender charge schedule identifier that defines the penalty structure for early policy termination. Used in policy servicing and cash surrender value calculations.']
  tax_qualification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the product is tax-qualified under IRC Section 7702 and whether it is classified as a Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) under IRC Section 7702A. Critical for tax reporting and compliance.']
  underwriting_class_eligibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of underwriting risk classes eligible for this product plan (e.g., preferred_plus, preferred, standard, substandard). Used in underwriting workbench and rate table selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: preferred_plus|preferred|standard|substandard|table_rated|declined — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this product plan record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all life insurance and annuity product plans offered by the company — WL, UL, IUL, VUL, YRT, FIA, SPIA, GMAB/GMDB/GMIB/GMWB riders, and DI products. Defines the canonical product identity including plan code, product line, product family, insurance type, tax qualification status (IRC 7702 / MEC), minimum and maximum issue ages, face amount limits, underwriting class eligibility, distribution channel availability, product status (active, filed, retired), effective and expiration dates, and NAIC product type code. This is the SSOT for all product definitions across the enterprise.'
}

Table product.plan_version {
  plan_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the plan version record. Primary key.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Plan versions define product economics (expected_nbv_per_policy, profitability_target_irr) used to establish DAC capitalization accounts and GAAP reserve GL mappings at new business setup. Finance tea']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Plan versions track which legal entity issues them for state approval tracking and financial reporting segregation. State approvals and filings are entity-specific; version-level tracking enables accu']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent product plan. Links this version to the overarching product plan entity.']
  replacement_plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the successor plan version that replaces this retired version. Supports product migration and agent guidance. Null if no direct replacement exists.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the state insurance department granted approval for this plan version. May differ from effective date based on filing type and state requirements.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of regulatory approval for this plan version. Tracks progression through state insurance department review and approval workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|conditionally_approved|rejected|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approved_by_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority that approved this version (e.g., New York Department of Financial Services, California Department of Insurance). Supports multi-state product tracking.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for creating this version. Documents the driver for the version change such as regulatory mandate, competitive positioning, profitability improvement, actuarial assumption update, or product redesign.']
  closed_block_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this plan version is part of a closed block for statutory accounting and reserve segregation. True when the version is closed to new business and subject to closed block reporting requirements.']
  closed_block_transition_date date [note: 'Date when this plan version was formally designated as a closed block for statutory accounting purposes. Triggers specific reserve calculation and reporting requirements under SAP (Statutory Accounting Principles).']
  communication_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or description of the stakeholder communication strategy for this version change. Documents agent bulletins, policyholder notices, and internal training materials associated with the version transition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this plan version record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and version history tracking.']
  distribution_channel_eligibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of authorized distribution channels for this plan version. Examples: career_agent, independent_broker, bank_channel, direct_to_consumer, worksite, brokerage_general_agency. Governs product availability across sales channels.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this plan version becomes active and available for new business or in-force policy administration. Aligns with state insurance department approval effective dates.']
  expected_nbv_per_policy decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected new business value per policy for this plan version based on actuarial pricing assumptions. Represents the present value of future profits at issue. Confidential metric used for product portfolio management and sales incentive design.']
  filing_reference_number varchar [note: 'State insurance department filing identifier or SERFF (System for Electronic Rate and Form Filing) tracking number. Links this version to the regulatory approval documentation.']
  gpt_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plan version satisfies the Guideline Premium Test under IRC Section 7702. One of two tests (GPT or Cash Value Accumulation Test) required for life insurance tax qualification.']
  inforce_continuation_policy varchar [note: 'Strategy for managing existing policies after version retirement. Full servicing maintains all policy features; limited servicing restricts certain transactions; closed block segregates reserves; reinsured transfers risk; sold indicates block sale to another carrier.']
  irc_7702_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plan version meets IRC Section 7702 definition of life insurance for federal tax qualification. Critical for ensuring death benefit tax-free treatment and cash value accumulation tax deferral.']
