  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Name of the internal department or business unit primarily responsible for fulfilling this obligation (e.g., \'Actuarial\', \'Legal & Compliance\', \'Finance\', \'Tax\', \'Operations\', \'Investment Management\'). Used for workload assignment and escalation routing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this obligation record originates or is primarily managed (e.g., \'Compliance Calendar System\', \'Policy Administration System\', \'Actuarial Valuation System\'). Supports data lineage and ETL traceability in the lakehouse.']
  statutory_due_date date [note: 'The legally mandated deadline by which this obligation must be fulfilled as specified by the governing body or regulation. For recurring obligations, this represents the due date for the current or next filing period. Failure to meet this date may result in regulatory penalties.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'The required or preferred method by which this obligation must be submitted to the governing body. \'electronic\' = NAIC SERFF or similar; \'portal\' = regulator-specific web portal (e.g., SEC EDGAR, IRS e-file); \'api\' = machine-to-machine submission; \'paper\' = physical mailing.']
  submission_format varchar [note: 'The required data format or form number for the filing submission (e.g., \'NAIC Annual Statement Blank\', \'Form N-CEN\', \'Form 5500\', \'SAR FinCEN 111\', \'ACORD XML\'). Ensures the compliance team prepares the filing in the correct format required by the governing body.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all regulatory obligations, filing deadlines, and compliance calendar entries applicable to Life Insurance operations across all jurisdictions. Each obligation captures the governing body, regulation citation, effective date, applicability scope (product lines, states), compliance owner, filing frequency (annual, quarterly, monthly, ad hoc), statutory due date, internal preparation deadline, grace period, escalation thresholds, responsible department, and renewal cycle. Serves as the single authoritative source for what must be done, by whom, and by when — functioning as both the obligation registry and the enterprise compliance calendar for proactive deadline management.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_filing {
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory filing record in the compliance data product. Primary key for the regulatory_filing table.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.agency. Business justification: Some state filings are agency-specific: agency appointment/termination notifications, agency E&O coverage reporting, agency license renewals. Regulatory_filing needs agency_id to track these agency-le']
  amended_filing_regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original regulatory_filing_id that this record amends or corrects. Null for original filings. Enables lineage tracking across filing versions and amendment chains.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Regulatory filing preparation costs (legal, actuarial, compliance staff time) are allocated to cost centers for expense tracking, budgeting, and SOX control over compliance expenditures. Standard fina']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.in_force_policy. Business justification: Some regulatory filings are policy-specific (e.g., variable product registrations, individual policy filings for large cases). Linking enables tracking of policy-level regulatory submissions and suppo']
  insurance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Regulatory filings generate supporting documents (actuarial memos, board resolutions, exhibits, submission confirmations) that must be retained per regulatory requirements. Essential for audit trail a']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Filing fees, late filing penalties, and associated costs are journalized. Standard accounting practice for regulatory compliance expenses and penalty accruals.']
  jurisdiction_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_license. Business justification: Regulatory filings (e.g., annual statements, license renewal filings) are submitted for a specific jurisdiction license. Adding jurisdiction_license_id to regulatory_filing enables linking filing reco']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Regulatory filings are submitted by specific legal entities (insurance companies within a holding company structure). The regulatory_filing product currently lacks a direct legal entity FK. Adding leg']
  market_conduct_exam_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.market_conduct_exam. Business justification: regulatory_filing.examination_reference is a denormalized string reference to a market conduct exam. Replacing it with a proper FK market_conduct_exam_id normalizes the relationship, enabling joins to']
  policy_form_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval. Adding policy_form_approval_id to regulatory_filing enabl']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Investment-related regulatory filings (NAIC Schedule D, RBC reports, annual statement) reference specific portfolios. Real business process: statutory reporting, investment schedule preparation, portf']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.producer. Business justification: State DOIs require filings for producer terminations for cause, E&O coverage lapses, and license revocations. Regulatory_filing tracks these producer-specific state notifications required by insurance']

Adding rate_filing_id to regulatory_filing enables linking the transa']
  rbc_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.rbc_calculation. Business justification: Annual statement filings include or reference RBC calculations. Direct regulatory reporting relationship - RBC filing is often part of broader annual statement filing package to state regulators.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory filings execute specific regulatory obligations. Each filing fulfills an obligation from the master registry. The obligation defines the filing type, governing body, and jurisdiction, makin']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: A regulatory filing is submitted under a specific state regulatory framework. Adding state_regulation_id normalizes the governing regulation reference. lead_state_code is retained as it captures the o']
  statutory_reserve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.statutory_reserve. Business justification: Annual statement regulatory filings (NAIC blank filings) directly report statutory reserves. The regulatory_filing record for an annual statement submission must reference the statutory_reserve calcul']
  termination_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.termination_event. Business justification: FINRA Form U5 filings and state termination notices are regulatory filings directly triggered by producer termination events. Compliance teams must link each termination-related regulatory_filing to t']
  uw_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.decision. Business justification: Certain underwriting decisions (STOLI determinations, declinations, replacement decisions) directly trigger mandatory regulatory filings. Regulators require traceability from the filing back to the sp']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'The date on which the governing body formally accepted or acknowledged the filing as complete and compliant. Null if the filing has not yet been accepted. Distinguishes submission date from regulatory acceptance, which may differ for filings requiring review.']
  aml_transaction_type varchar [note: 'For FinCEN filings, identifies whether this is a Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) or Currency Transaction Report (CTR). Applicable only to FinCEN/BSA filings. Null for non-AML filing types. Supports AML/BSA compliance tracking.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the designated approver formally signed off on the filing prior to submission. Required for SOX internal control documentation and NAIC officer certification.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the officer or compliance official who approved the filing. Retained for audit trail and regulatory examination purposes.']
  board_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the company\'s Board of Directors formally approved the filing prior to submission. Required for ORSA reports and certain NAIC annual statement certifications. Null for filings that do not require board-level approval.']
  capital_adequacy_conclusion varchar [note: 'The conclusion reached in the ORSA or RBC filing regarding the insurer\'s capital adequacy relative to its risk profile (e.g., adequate, inadequate, marginal). Applicable to ORSA and RBC-related filings. Null for non-capital filings.']
  confirmation_reference varchar [note: 'The acknowledgment or confirmation number returned by the governing body\'s filing system upon successful receipt of the submission (e.g., NAIC confirmation number, SEC accession number, DOL EFAST2 confirmation, FinCEN BSA tracking number). Serves as the authoritative proof of submission.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this regulatory filing record was first created in the compliance system. Provides the audit trail entry point for the filing lifecycle. Aligns with SOX internal control documentation requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for any monetary amounts reported in this filing (e.g., \'USD\'). Primarily USD for domestic US regulatory filings. Required when penalty_amount or other monetary fields are populated.']
  days_late varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days by which the filing was submitted after the statutory due date. Zero or negative if filed on time. Used to assess late-filing penalties and regulatory exposure. Calculated as submission_date minus due_date.']
  due_date date [note: 'The statutory or regulatory deadline by which this filing must be submitted to the governing body. Used to calculate timeliness, trigger escalation workflows, and assess late-filing penalties. Derived from the regulatory calendar for the applicable filing type and jurisdiction.']
  erisa_plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ERISA-covered plan reported in this DOL Form 5500 filing (e.g., group life, 401k annuity, pension, welfare). Applicable only to ERISA filings. Null for non-ERISA filing types.']
  fiduciary_designation varchar [note: 'Name or role of the designated fiduciary responsible for the ERISA plan covered by this filing. Required for DOL Form 5500 and DOL fiduciary compliance under ERISA. Null for non-ERISA filing types.']
  filing_channel varchar [note: 'The transmission method used to submit the filing to the governing body (e.g., electronic via NAIC SERFF, SEC EDGAR, DOL EFAST2, FinCEN BSA E-Filing portal, or paper submission). Relevant for audit trail and submission confirmation tracking.']
  filing_form_name varchar [note: 'The official name or designation of the regulatory form used for this filing (e.g., \'NAIC Life/Health Annual Statement\', \'SEC Form N-4\', \'IRS Form 1099-R\', \'FinCEN Form 112 CTR\', \'DOL Form 5500\', \'ORSA Summary Report\'). Provides precise identification of the statutory instrument.']
  filing_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the filing — either generated internally or assigned by the receiving regulatory body upon submission (e.g., NAIC filing reference, SEC accession number, DOL confirmation number). Used for tracking and correspondence with regulators.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the regulatory filing submission. Tracks progression from internal preparation (draft) through submission to the regulator and final disposition (accepted, rejected, withdrawn). Drives compliance workflow and remediation tracking.']
  filing_system_source varchar [note: 'Name of the internal operational system or platform from which the filing data was sourced or generated (e.g., \'Policy Administration System\', \'Actuarial Valuation System\', \'Claims Management System\'). Supports data lineage and ETL traceability.']
  filing_version varchar [note: 'Version or amendment number of this filing submission (e.g., \'original\', \'amendment_1\', \'corrected\'). Tracks whether this is the initial submission or a subsequent amendment/correction filed with the governing body.']
  is_amended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filing is an amendment or correction to a previously submitted filing. True if this is an amended submission; False if this is the original filing. Used to distinguish original from corrected statutory submissions.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this regulatory filing record was most recently modified in the compliance system. Tracks the most recent status change, amendment, or data correction. Supports audit trail and SOX change management controls.']
  late_filing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filing was submitted after the statutory due date. True if submission_date exceeds due_date; False if filed on time. Used for compliance monitoring, penalty assessment, and regulatory examination preparation.']
  lead_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-character US state code identifying the lead state regulator for ORSA filings and multi-state regulatory submissions. The lead state coordinates review among all states where the insurer is licensed. Applicable primarily to ORSA and group-level filings.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers to record supplemental context, internal commentary, regulator correspondence notes, or remediation instructions related to this filing. Not transmitted to the regulator; for internal compliance workflow use only.']
  orsa_risk_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of risk categories assessed in the ORSA filing (e.g., \'insurance_risk, market_risk, credit_risk, operational_risk, liquidity_risk\'). Applicable only to ORSA filings. Null for non-ORSA filing types. Supports ERM and ORSA reporting requirements.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty assessed by the governing body for late or deficient filing, expressed in USD. Null if no penalty was assessed. Used for financial reporting, regulatory expense tracking, and SOX control monitoring.']
  plan_sponsor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the employer or organization sponsoring the retirement or group benefit plan covered by this ERISA filing (DOL Form 5500). Applicable only to ERISA filings. Null for non-ERISA filing types.']
  preparer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual responsible for preparing the regulatory filing. Retained for audit trail and regulatory examination purposes even if the employee record changes.']
  pte_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for any Prohibited Transaction Exemption (PTE) cited in the ERISA filing (e.g., PTE 84-24, PTE 2020-02). Applicable to ERISA filings where prohibited transaction exemptions are claimed. Null for non-ERISA filing types.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'The date on which the governing body formally rejected the filing. Populated only when filing_status is \'rejected\'. Triggers remediation workflow and resubmission tracking.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Textual description or regulator-provided code explaining why the filing was rejected. Populated when filing_status is \'rejected\'. Used to drive remediation actions and resubmission preparation.']
  stress_test_scenarios varchar [note: 'Description or comma-separated list of stress test scenarios included in the ORSA or actuarial filing (e.g., \'interest_rate_shock, pandemic_mortality, equity_market_decline\'). Applicable to ORSA and actuarial valuation filings. Null for non-ORSA filing types.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the filing was transmitted to the governing body. Compared against due_date to determine timeliness and potential late-filing exposure. Populated upon successful transmission.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the filing was electronically transmitted to the governing body. Provides audit-grade evidence of timely submission, particularly for filings with intraday deadlines (e.g., FinCEN CTR 15-day rule).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every regulatory filing submitted to governing bodies — NAIC annual/quarterly statements, state DOI market conduct filings, SEC Form N-4/N-6 for variable products, FINRA filings, IRS Form 1099-R/5498, FinCEN SAR/CTR filings, ORSA reports, ERISA Form 5500 filings, and all other statutory submissions. Captures filing type, jurisdiction, submission date, due date, filing status (draft, submitted, accepted, rejected), filing period, preparer, approver, and submission confirmation reference. For ORSA filings: includes risk categories assessed, capital adequacy conclusion, stress test scenarios, board approval date, and lead state designation. For ERISA filings: includes plan sponsor, plan type (group life, 401k annuity, pension), fiduciary designation, prohibited transaction exemption (PTE) references, and DOL filing date. Serves as the single transactional record for all regulatory submissions regardless of filing type or governing body.'
}

Business justification: Exam response costs (legal counsel, document production, staff time, remediation) are significant and tracked by cost center for budget variance analysis and management accountability. Required for fi']
  exam_report_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Market conduct exams produce official exam reports (draft and final) stored in the document system. Linking exam to sampled policies enables tracking which policies were exam']

Business justification: Market conduct exams result in monetary penalties and restitution amounts that must be accrued as journal entries. Insurance finance teams book penalty accruals and restitution liabilities when exam o']
  jurisdiction_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_license. Business justification: A market conduct exam is conducted within a specific jurisdiction where the company holds a Certificate of Authority. Linking market_conduct_exam to jurisdiction_license enables tracking which license']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Market conduct exams are conducted against specific legal entities. Exam scope, findings, and penalties are entity-specific for regulatory accountability and financial statement impact.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: Market conduct exams review investment practices, variable product sales, separate account management, and portfolio compliance. Real business process: regulatory examination, investment compliance re']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.producer. Business justification: Market conduct exams are frequently initiated due to specific producer conduct complaints (churning, misrepresentation, unsuitable sales). Regulators and compliance teams need to link exams to the imp']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: A market conduct examination is triggered by or conducted in fulfillment of a specific regulatory obligation (e.g., NAIC market conduct annual statement, state DOI examination cycle). Linking market_c']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: A market conduct exam is conducted under the authority of specific state insurance regulations. Adding state_regulation_id normalizes the governing regulatory framework. initiating_state_code is retai']
  appeal_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the company has filed a formal appeal or objection to the examination findings or enforcement action. True if an appeal has been filed; False otherwise. Triggers legal hold and appeal tracking workflow.']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date on which the company formally filed its appeal or objection with the state DOI or administrative tribunal. Null if no appeal has been filed. Used to track appeal timelines and regulatory response deadlines.']
  appeal_resolution varchar [note: 'Outcome of the company\'s formal appeal of examination findings or enforcement action. Null if no appeal was filed or appeal is still pending. Values: pending (appeal filed, awaiting decision), upheld (original findings confirmed), partially_upheld (some findings modified), overturned (findings reversed), withdrawn (company withdrew appeal).']
  cap_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the state DOI formally approved the company\'s submitted Corrective Action Plan (CAP). Null if the CAP has not yet been approved or is not required. Marks the start of the formal remediation execution period.']
  cap_submission_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the company submitted its Corrective Action Plan (CAP) to the state DOI. Null if the CAP has not yet been submitted. Used to track timeliness of regulatory response and compliance with DOI deadlines.']
  cap_submission_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the company must submit its Corrective Action Plan (CAP) to the state DOI. Null if no CAP is required. Triggers internal compliance workflow and executive escalation if approaching without submission.']
  company_exam_coordinator varchar [note: 'Full name of the internal company employee designated as the primary point of contact and coordinator for this market conduct examination. Responsible for document production, examiner liaison, and internal response coordination.']
  company_legal_counsel varchar [note: 'Name of the internal or external legal counsel assigned to advise the company during the market conduct examination. Responsible for legal strategy, response drafting, and appeal proceedings if applicable.']
  company_response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the company must submit its formal written response to the draft examination report findings. Calculated from the draft report issuance date per state DOI requirements. Failure to respond by this date may result in findings being finalized without company input.']
  corrective_action_plan_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state DOI has required the company to submit a formal Corrective Action Plan (CAP) as a result of examination findings. True if a CAP is required; False if no formal plan is mandated. Drives the corrective action tracking workflow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this market conduct examination record was first created in the compliance management system. Represents the system-of-record audit trail creation event. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of examination findings classified as critical severity — representing systemic violations, significant consumer harm, or material regulatory non-compliance requiring immediate corrective action. Supports executive reporting and regulatory escalation tracking.']
  draft_report_date date [note: 'Date on which the state DOI issued the preliminary draft examination report to the company for review and response. Initiates the company\'s formal response period, which is typically 30 days under NAIC standards.']
  exam_close_date date [note: 'Date on which the state DOI formally closed the examination after confirming all findings have been remediated or resolved. Represents the end of the examination lifecycle. Null if the examination is still open.']
  exam_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal compliance team notes regarding the examination, including context about regulatory relationships, strategic considerations, unusual circumstances, or coordination with other regulatory matters. Not shared with the DOI.']
  exam_number varchar [note: 'Externally-assigned examination reference number issued by the initiating state Department of Insurance (DOI). Used for correspondence, filings, and cross-referencing with the regulator. Format varies by state DOI.']
  exam_outcome varchar [note: 'Regulatory outcome or enforcement action resulting from the completed examination. Captures the most severe action taken: no_action (no violations found or all minor), corrective_action_plan (formal CAP required), consent_order (negotiated regulatory agreement), cease_and_desist (order to stop specific practices), license_suspension (temporary license action), monetary_penalty (fine assessed), restitution_ordered (consumer repayment required), multi_action (multiple enforcement actions). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: no_action|corrective_action_plan|consent_order|cease_and_desist|license_suspension|monetary_penalty|restitution_ordered|multi_action — promote to reference product]']
  exam_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the business activity review period covered by the examination. Defines the latest date of company records and transactions subject to examiner review.']
  exam_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the business activity review period covered by the examination. Defines the earliest date of company records and transactions subject to examiner review. Typically spans 3-5 years of prior business activity.']
  exam_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the business areas, product lines, and practices subject to review in this examination. May reference specific lines of business (life, annuity, disability), functional areas (claims, underwriting, sales, billing), or regulatory concerns (suitability, AML, HIPAA). Derived from the DOI examination notice.']
  exam_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the market conduct examination from initial notification through final closure. Tracks the examination workflow stage: notification_received (DOI notice received), in_progress (active examination underway), draft_report (preliminary findings issued), response_pending (company response to draft due), final_report (final examination report issued), closed (all findings resolved), appealed (company has filed a formal appeal). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: notification_received|in_progress|draft_report|response_pending|final_report|closed|appealed — promote to reference product]']
  exam_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the market conduct examination by its scope and trigger. Routine examinations are scheduled periodic reviews; targeted examinations focus on specific business practices; multi-state examinations are coordinated across multiple state DOIs; for-cause examinations are triggered by complaints or regulatory concerns; desk examinations are conducted remotely; market analysis examinations are data-driven reviews.']
  final_report_date date [note: 'Date on which the state DOI issued the final examination report incorporating company responses and examiner determinations. This is the official regulatory record of examination findings and required corrective actions.']
  functional_areas_examined varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of business functional areas reviewed during the examination (e.g., \'claims_adjudication, policy_servicing, sales_practices, underwriting, billing, complaint_handling, anti_money_laundering\'). Supports gap analysis and remediation planning.']
  initiating_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation identifying the lead state Department of Insurance that initiated and is conducting the market conduct examination. For multi-state examinations, this is the lead state coordinating the review.']
  lead_examiner_contact varchar [note: 'Email address or phone number of the state DOI lead examiner for official examination correspondence. Used by the company\'s compliance team to coordinate document requests, schedule interviews, and submit responses.']
  lead_examiner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the state DOI lead examiner assigned to conduct and oversee the market conduct examination. Used for correspondence, scheduling, and examination record attribution. This is a regulatory contact, not a company employee.']
  lines_of_business varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of insurance product lines included in the examination scope (e.g., \'whole_life, term_life, annuity, disability_income, long_term_care\'). Aligns with NAIC line-of-business classifications. Supports product-level finding analysis and trend reporting.']
  major_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of examination findings classified as major severity — representing significant but non-systemic violations requiring formal corrective action plans. Supports prioritization of remediation resources.']
  minor_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of examination findings classified as minor severity — representing isolated or technical violations with limited consumer impact. Typically addressed through process improvements rather than formal corrective action plans.']
  monetary_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary penalty or fine assessed by the state DOI as a result of this examination, expressed in US dollars. Null if no monetary penalty was assessed. Supports financial exposure tracking, SOX controls, and regulatory liability reporting. Classified as confidential due to business-sensitive financial exposure.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the company received official written notification from the state Department of Insurance of the upcoming or initiated market conduct examination. Triggers internal response timelines and document preservation obligations.']
  onsite_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the active on-site (or remote desk) examination phase concluded and examiners departed or ceased active record review. Marks the transition to report drafting phase.']
  onsite_start_date date [note: 'Date on which examiners physically arrived at company premises (or commenced remote access for desk examinations) to begin active review of records and interviews. Null for market analysis examinations that are entirely data-driven.']
  participating_states varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of two-letter USPS state abbreviations for all states participating in a multi-state market conduct examination, excluding the initiating state. Null or empty for single-state examinations. Supports multi-state coordination tracking per NAIC protocols.']
  prior_exam_reference varchar [note: 'Examination number of the prior market conduct examination that this examination follows up on, if applicable. Null if this is not a repeat examination. Enables linkage between examination cycles for repeat-finding trend analysis.']
  repeat_exam_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this examination is a follow-up or repeat examination triggered by unresolved findings from a prior examination cycle. True if this is a repeat examination; False if it is an initial examination. Critical for repeat-finding trend analysis and regulatory escalation risk assessment.']
  restitution_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of consumer restitution ordered by the state DOI as a result of examination findings, expressed in US dollars. Represents payments owed to policyholders harmed by identified violations. Null if no restitution was ordered. Classified as confidential due to business-sensitive financial exposure.']
  total_findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct examination findings identified across all severity levels in this examination. Provides a high-level measure of examination outcome severity and supports trend analysis across examination cycles.']
  total_policies_examined varchar [note: 'Total number of individual policy or contract records reviewed by examiners during the examination. Represents the full sample size across all functional areas and lines of business examined. Used to calculate error rates and assess statistical significance of findings.']
  total_policies_with_findings varchar [note: 'Total number of policy or contract records in which at least one examination finding was identified. Used to calculate the overall error rate (policies with findings / total policies examined) and assess the breadth of non-compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this market conduct examination record was most recently modified in the compliance management system. Supports audit trail, change detection, and incremental ETL processing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Comprehensive record of state DOI market conduct examinations, their associated findings, and finding-level detail. Captures examination type (routine, targeted, multi-state), initiating state DOI, scope, lead examiner, examination period, status, and outcome. Includes embedded finding records: finding description, regulatory citation violated, severity classification (critical, major, minor), affected policy counts, examiner recommendations, company response, finding status (open, disputed, remediated, closed), and corrective action deadlines. Supports the full examination lifecycle from notification through finding closure and repeat-finding trend analysis across examination cycles.'
}

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: policy_underwriting|new_business_application|premium_billing|claims_adjudication|annuity_administration|policy_servicing|reinsurance_treaty|actuarial_valuation|commission_management|investment_portfolio — promote to reference product]']

  examiner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead examiner or auditor from the regulatory authority or internal compliance team who identified and documented this finding.']
  examiner_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or agency the examiner represents (e.g., \'Texas Department of Insurance\', \'FINRA District 6\', \'Internal Audit Division\').']
  examiner_recommendation varchar [note: 'Formal recommendation or corrective action prescribed by the examiner or auditor to resolve the finding, as stated in the examination report.']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the finding, deficiency, or violation as documented by the examiner or internal compliance reviewer, including factual basis and observed conduct.']
  finding_identified_date date [note: 'The real-world date on which the examiner or auditor first identified and documented this finding during the examination or review period.']
  finding_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to this finding by the examining authority or internal compliance team, used in official correspondence and remediation tracking (e.g., NAIC-2024-F00123).']
  finding_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the finding in the remediation lifecycle: open (active deficiency), disputed (company contests the finding), remediated (corrective action completed), closed (examiner accepted resolution), pending_verification (awaiting examiner confirmation).']
  finding_title varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title summarizing the nature of the finding or deficiency (e.g., \'Failure to Provide Required Policy Disclosure Within Statutory Timeframe\').']
  finding_type varchar [note: 'Category of examination or review that produced this finding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: market_conduct|financial_examination|internal_audit|regulatory_audit|sox_control|aml_bsa|hipaa_privacy|finra_review|dol_erisa — promote to reference product]']
  hipaa_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding involves a HIPAA privacy or security rule violation related to protected health information (PHI) accessed or processed during underwriting, claims, or policy servicing.']
  impacted_policy_count varchar [note: 'Number of insurance policies or annuity contracts directly affected by the deficiency identified in this finding, used to assess consumer harm scope and remediation effort.']
  impacted_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual transactions (e.g., premium payments, claims, disbursements) directly affected by the deficiency, used alongside impacted_policy_count for full scope assessment.']
  is_repeat_finding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding is a recurrence of a previously identified deficiency from a prior examination cycle, used for repeat-finding analysis and escalation prioritization.']
  jurisdiction_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or territory code identifying the jurisdiction in which the finding applies or was raised by a State Department of Insurance (DOI) or other state-level regulator.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this exam finding record, used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'The governing body or regulatory authority that identified or has jurisdiction over this finding (e.g., NAIC, state DOI, SEC, FINRA, IRS, DOL, FinCEN). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NAIC|state_doi|SEC|FINRA|IRS|DOL|FASB|FinCEN|IAIS|ACORD — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Specific statute, regulation, rule, or code section violated or at issue (e.g., \'NAIC Model Regulation 641\', \'IRC Section 7702\', \'HIPAA 45 CFR §164.502\', \'SOX Section 404\', \'FINRA Rule 2111 Suitability\').']
  remediation_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal business owner or department responsible for executing the remediation plan and driving the finding to closure.']
  remediation_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action plan developed by the company to address the finding, including process changes, system updates, training, or policy revisions.']
  severity_classification varchar [note: 'Risk-based severity level assigned to the finding: critical (systemic violation with significant consumer harm or regulatory penalty risk), major (material deficiency requiring prompt remediation), minor (isolated or low-impact deficiency), informational (observation without formal violation).']
  sox_control_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SOX internal control identifier or control objective that this finding relates to, used for SOX Section 302/404 compliance reporting and control deficiency tracking.']
  suitability_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding requires a suitability review of affected transactions or policies under FINRA Rule 2111, DOL fiduciary standards, or NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation.']
  target_remediation_date date [note: 'Committed target date by which the company plans to complete all corrective actions and submit evidence of remediation to the examining authority.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mandatory compliance training has been identified as part of the corrective action plan for this finding, applicable to affected business units or agents.']

  Note: 'Individual finding or deficiency identified during a market conduct examination, regulatory audit, or internal compliance review. Captures finding description, regulatory citation violated, severity classification (critical, major, minor), affected business process, number of impacted policies or transactions, examiner recommendation, company response, and finding status (open, disputed, remediated, closed). Supports remediation tracking and repeat-finding analysis.'
}

  escalation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this remediation plan requires escalation to senior management, the Board of Directors, or regulatory authorities due to severity, financial exposure, or regulatory mandate. Triggers escalation workflow in the compliance management system.']

Includes potential regulatory fines, restitution to policyholders, and remediation costs. Used for risk quantification and ERM reporting.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Two- or three-character code identifying the regulatory jurisdiction (US state, territory, or federal) under which the remediation obligation arises. Uses standard USPS state abbreviations or \'FED\' for federal-level requirements.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this remediation plan record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail maintenance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  plan_open_date date [note: 'The business date on which the remediation plan was formally opened and accepted as a compliance obligation. Represents the start of the remediation lifecycle and is used to calculate aging and regulatory deadline compliance.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the remediation plan. Tracks progression from initial planning through corrective action execution to regulatory acceptance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|in_progress|completed|validated|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  plan_title varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title summarizing the nature of the compliance deficiency and the corrective action being taken. Used in dashboards, reports, and regulatory submissions.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the remediation plan by the source of the triggering deficiency. Distinguishes between plans arising from regulatory examinations, internal audits, SOX control failures, market conduct reviews, or self-identified gaps. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regulatory_exam|internal_audit|self_identified|sox_control|market_conduct|aml_bsa|hipaa|erisa — promote to reference product]']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to the remediation plan, which may differ from severity level based on regulatory deadlines, resource constraints, or strategic risk appetite. Drives scheduling and resource allocation decisions.']
  recurrence_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance deficiency is a repeat finding from a prior examination or audit cycle. Repeat findings typically receive heightened regulatory scrutiny and may result in escalated enforcement actions under NAIC and state DOI standards.']
  regulator_reference_number varchar [note: 'The reference number or docket number assigned by the regulatory authority (state DOI, NAIC, SEC, FinCEN, etc.) to track this remediation obligation in their examination or enforcement records. Used for cross-referencing regulatory correspondence.']
  regulatory_acceptance_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulator or examining authority formally accepted the remediation plan as satisfactorily completed. Null until regulatory acceptance is received. Marks the end of the regulatory obligation lifecycle.']
  regulatory_acceptance_status varchar [note: 'Status of the regulator\'s or examining authority\'s formal acceptance of the remediation plan closure. Tracks whether the state DOI, NAIC, SEC, FinCEN, or other governing body has acknowledged that the deficiency has been satisfactorily resolved.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory or compliance framework under which this remediation plan is required. Identifies the governing body or standard driving the corrective action obligation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NAIC|SOX|HIPAA|AML_BSA|ERISA|SEC_FINRA|IRS_7702|ORSA|STATE_DOI|IFRS — promote to reference product]']
  remediation_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual or estimated cost incurred to implement the corrective actions under this remediation plan, expressed in USD. Includes internal labor, system changes, third-party consulting, and training costs. Used for compliance budget management.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Name of the business unit or department accountable for executing the remediation plan. Used for enterprise-wide compliance gap analysis and departmental accountability reporting.']
  responsible_owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual accountable for executing the remediation plan. Retained as a denormalized field for regulatory reporting and audit trail purposes where the employee record may change over time.']
  revised_target_date date [note: 'Updated target completion date when the original target date has been formally extended due to scope changes, resource constraints, or regulatory negotiation. Null if no revision has occurred.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the root cause to enable enterprise-wide trend analysis and systemic issue identification across remediation plans. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: process_gap|system_failure|training_deficiency|control_weakness|data_quality|staffing|vendor_failure|policy_gap — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Formal root cause analysis narrative identifying the underlying process, system, control, or organizational failure that led to the compliance deficiency. Essential for preventing recurrence and demonstrating thoroughness to regulators.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Risk-based severity rating of the underlying compliance deficiency, used to prioritize remediation efforts and escalation thresholds. Critical findings typically require immediate regulatory notification under NAIC and state DOI standards.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'The committed date by which all corrective actions under this remediation plan are expected to be fully implemented and validated. May be set by regulatory mandate or internal commitment to the examining authority.']
  validation_date date [note: 'The date on which the validation of corrective action effectiveness was completed and documented. Null until validation is performed. Distinct from actual completion date, as validation may occur after implementation.']
  validation_method varchar [note: 'The approach used to verify that corrective actions have been effectively implemented and the compliance deficiency has been resolved. Determines the rigor and independence of the closure evidence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal_testing|independent_review|regulatory_inspection|third_party_audit|management_attestation|continuous_monitoring — promote to reference product]']
  validation_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the validation testing or review confirming whether the corrective actions have been effectively implemented and the deficiency has been resolved. A failed or partially passed outcome requires additional remediation.']

  Note: 'Formal remediation plan created in response to exam findings, audit observations, or self-identified compliance deficiencies. Tracks root cause analysis, corrective action description, responsible owner, target completion date, actual completion date, remediation status (planned, in-progress, completed, validated), validation method, and regulatory acceptance status. Enables enterprise-wide tracking of compliance gap closure.'
}

Table compliance.suitability_review {
  suitability_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for suitability_review']
  appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.appointment. Business justification: Suitability reviews are conducted under a producer\'s specific appointment authority (the appointment defines the state and product line authorization). Linking suitability_review to appointment enable']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Suitability review programs are conducted by specific compliance departments whose costs are allocated to cost centers for budgeting and regulatory expense reporting. Insurance companies track suitabi']
  license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.license. Business justification: Suitability reviews must verify producer held valid license in jurisdiction at time of sale, with correct line of authority for product sold. License_id links review to specific license record for com']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder or annuity contract owner whose financial profile and suitability is being assessed at the time of sale.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the insurance or annuity product recommended and sold, used to assess product-specific suitability criteria.']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Suitability reviews are conducted for the specific plan version sold. The plan_version determines charges, surrender schedules, and disclosures that must be reviewed. Reg BI and NAIC Suitability Model']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed agent or broker who conducted the sale and is responsible for the suitability determination.']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commission.commission_schedule. Business justification: Suitability reviews evaluate whether commission structure incentivized unsuitable sales (excessive FYC rates, high bonuses for specific products). Real business process: compliance review links produc']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Suitability reviews must comply with state-specific suitability and best-interest regulations. The review describes the assessment, while the regulation describes the state-specific rules.']
  suitability_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.suitability_rule. Business justification: Each suitability review is conducted against the applicable product suitability_rule defining minimum income, net worth, and risk tolerance thresholds. Compliance officers must link each review to the']
  uw_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Suitability reviews are performed ON applications during underwriting. Regulatory requirement to validate product suitability before issue. Natural 1:N (one application may have multiple suitability r']
  aml_kyc_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether AML/KYC verification was completed for this transaction, confirming the client\'s identity and source of funds in compliance with FinCEN BSA requirements.']
  annual_income decimal(18,2) [note: 'Client\'s total annual income in USD at the time of the suitability review, as self-reported on the suitability questionnaire. Used to assess affordability of premiums and appropriateness of product.']
  client_acknowledgment_date date [note: 'The date on which the client signed or electronically acknowledged the suitability questionnaire and disclosure documents.']
  client_acknowledgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client has signed or electronically acknowledged the suitability questionnaire and associated disclosures, confirming the accuracy of the financial profile information provided.']
  client_age varchar [note: 'Age of the policyholder in whole years at the time of the suitability review. Critical factor in determining product appropriateness, particularly for annuity surrender periods relative to life expectancy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this suitability review record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and SOX compliance tracking.']
  determination_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation provided by the agent or compliance reviewer documenting the basis for the suitability determination, including how the product aligns with the client\'s financial profile, objectives, and risk tolerance.']
  disclosure_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required regulatory disclosures (e.g., NAIC Buyer\'s Guide, product prospectus for variable products, fee disclosure) were provided to the client prior to or at the time of sale.']
  existing_insurance_coverage boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client has existing life insurance or annuity coverage at the time of the suitability review, relevant for replacement analysis and concentration assessment.']
  finra_reg_bi_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SEC Regulation Best Interest (Reg BI) applies to this transaction, typically for variable product sales through registered broker-dealers.']
  investment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The premium or investment amount in USD placed into the recommended product at the time of sale. Used to assess concentration risk relative to the client\'s total liquid net worth.']
  investment_objective varchar [note: 'Client\'s primary investment objective as stated on the suitability questionnaire. Drives product recommendation alignment analysis.']
  investment_time_horizon varchar [note: 'Client\'s stated investment time horizon: short_term (under 3 years), medium_term (3–10 years), long_term (over 10 years). Must be compatible with product surrender charge period.']
  is_1035_exchange boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction involves a tax-free 1035 exchange of an existing insurance or annuity contract, requiring additional suitability analysis of surrender charges, tax implications, and benefit comparisons.']
  is_replacement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sale involves the replacement of an existing life insurance policy or annuity contract, triggering additional disclosure and suitability requirements under NAIC Model Regulation #613.']
  jurisdiction_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code of the jurisdiction in which the sale transaction occurred, determining applicable state DOI suitability rules and disclosure requirements.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this suitability review record was most recently modified, supporting audit trail requirements and change tracking for regulatory examination purposes.']
  liquid_net_worth decimal(18,2) [note: 'Client\'s liquid net worth in USD (readily accessible assets excluding illiquid holdings such as real estate and retirement accounts) at the time of review. Critical for assessing ability to meet surrender charges and liquidity needs.']
  net_worth decimal(18,2) [note: 'Client\'s total net worth in USD (assets minus liabilities) at the time of the suitability review, as self-reported. Used to evaluate financial capacity and product suitability relative to overall wealth.']
  override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client elected to proceed with the transaction despite a \'not_suitable\' or \'conditionally_suitable\' determination, exercising their right to override the recommendation. Requires documented client acknowledgment.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason provided by the client for proceeding with the transaction despite a non-suitable or conditional determination. Required when override_flag is True.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'The type of insurance or annuity product recommended. Uses industry-standard abbreviations: FIA (Fixed Indexed Annuity), VUL (Variable Universal Life), UL (Universal Life), WL (Whole Life), IUL (Indexed Universal Life), SPIA (Single Premium Immediate Annuity), DI (Disability Income), LTC (Long-Term Care), term (Term Life). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FIA|VUL|UL|WL|IUL|SPIA|DI|LTC|term|GMWB|GMIB|GMAB — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory framework governing this suitability review. NAIC_275 for annuity suitability; DOL_ERISA for employer-sponsored plan sales; SEC_FINRA for variable product sales; STATE_DOI for state-specific requirements; BEST_INTEREST for Reg BI compliance.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which required remediation actions must be completed. Populated when remediation_required_flag is True.']
  remediation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a compliance remediation action is required as a result of this suitability review, such as policy rescission, refund, or corrective disclosure.']
  review_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the suitability review was conducted, corresponding to the point-of-sale (POS) transaction date.']
  review_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this suitability review at initiation, used for correspondence, audit trails, and regulatory filings. Format: SR-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the suitability review. \'suitable\' indicates the product is appropriate for the client; \'not_suitable\' triggers mandatory remediation; \'needs_review\' requires supervisory escalation; \'escalated\' indicates referral to compliance; \'withdrawn\' indicates the transaction was cancelled.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review type. \'initial\' for new sales; \'replacement\' for policy replacements per NAIC Model #613; \'exchange\' for 1035 tax-free exchanges; \'periodic\' for ongoing best-interest reviews; \'supervisory\' for principal-level reviews.']
  risk_tolerance varchar [note: 'Client\'s self-reported investment risk tolerance as captured on the suitability questionnaire. Determines appropriate product risk profile alignment.']
  source_of_funds varchar [note: 'The origin of the funds used to purchase the product. Required for AML/BSA compliance and suitability analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: savings|retirement_account|inheritance|proceeds_from_sale|existing_policy|business_income|gift|other — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this suitability review record originated (e.g., CRM for Salesforce Financial Services Cloud, POLICY_ADMIN for Policy Administration System, COMPLIANCE_WB for compliance workbench).']
  suitability_determination varchar [note: 'The formal suitability determination made by the reviewing agent: \'suitable\' confirms the product meets the client\'s needs; \'not_suitable\' prohibits the sale; \'conditionally_suitable\' requires additional disclosures or client acknowledgment.']
  supervisory_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the supervising principal documenting the basis for the supervisory review outcome, including any conditions, concerns, or required follow-up actions.']
  supervisory_review_date date [note: 'The date on which the supervisory or principal review of the suitability determination was completed.']
  supervisory_review_outcome varchar [note: 'The outcome of the principal or supervisory compliance review of the suitability determination. \'approved\' confirms the sale may proceed; \'rejected\' halts the transaction; \'returned_for_revision\' requires agent to update the analysis; \'escalated\' refers to senior compliance.']
  tax_status varchar [note: 'Tax qualification status of the funds used to purchase the product. \'qualified\' indicates pre-tax retirement funds (IRA, 401k); \'non_qualified\' indicates after-tax funds; \'roth\' indicates Roth IRA funds. Affects IRC Section 72 tax treatment and suitability of tax-deferred products.']
  variable_product_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recommended product is a variable insurance or annuity product (e.g., VUL, variable annuity), triggering additional SEC/FINRA oversight, prospectus delivery, and suitability requirements.']

  Note: 'Record of suitability and best-interest review conducted for annuity and life insurance sales transactions per NAIC Suitability in Annuity Transactions Model Regulation (#275) and DOL fiduciary standards. Captures policyholder financial profile at time of sale, product recommended, suitability determination (suitable, not suitable, needs review), reviewing agent, supervisory review outcome, and any required disclosures provided. Critical for FINRA and SEC oversight of variable product sales.'
}

Table compliance.aml_case {
  aml_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the AML/BSA investigation case record. Primary key for this entity.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.agency. Business justification: AML investigations may reveal agency-level patterns: multiple producers within same agency involved in premium financing schemes, STOLI rings, or structured transactions. Agency context is critical fo']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AML investigation programs have dedicated compliance cost centers. Investigation costs, external counsel fees, and FinCEN filing costs must be allocated to cost centers for BSA/AML program expense rep']
  insurance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: AML cases require documented investigation evidence, transaction records, and narrative support for SAR filings. Essential for AML case management, FinCEN reporting, and regulatory examination defense']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: AML cases resulting in asset seizure, restitution payments, or regulatory penalties generate journal entries. Finance must track financial impact of AML cases for regulatory reporting and financial st']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: AML cases are investigated and SAR filings made by specific legal entities. FinCEN filing requirement is entity-specific - each entity must have own AML program and file own SARs.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder record associated with this AML/BSA case, when the subject is a policyholder or the suspicious activity is linked to a specific policy contract.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan. Business justification: AML cases in life insurance are triggered by suspicious transactions on specific insurance plans (e.g., single-premium whole life used for structuring). FinCEN and ACLI guidance requires product-level']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed agent or broker associated with this AML/BSA case, when the subject or a contributing party is an agent. Used for producer conduct investigations and STOLI case tracking.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: AML/BSA cases are governed by specific regulatory obligations (BSA Section 314(a)/(b), FinCEN CDD Rule, OFAC compliance). Adding regulatory_obligation_id to aml_case normalizes the governing regulator']
  trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.trade. Business justification: AML cases investigate suspicious trading activity (premium financing, STOLI indicators, rapid surrenders, unusual investment patterns). Real business process: suspicious activity investigation, SAR fi']
  activity_end_date date [note: 'Latest date of the suspicious activity or transaction pattern under investigation. Together with activity_start_date, defines the full activity period reported on FinCEN SAR filings.']
  activity_start_date date [note: 'Earliest date of the suspicious activity or transaction pattern under investigation. Used to define the activity period reported on FinCEN SAR filings and to scope the investigation lookback window.']
  case_closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the AML/BSA investigation case was formally closed, either after FinCEN filing, no-filing determination, or referral to law enforcement. Null for open or in-progress cases.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the AML/BSA case, used in correspondence, FinCEN filings, and regulatory communications. Format: AML-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  case_opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the AML/BSA investigation case was formally opened and entered into the case management system. This is the principal business event timestamp for the case lifecycle and is used to calculate FinCEN filing deadline compliance (SAR must be filed within 30 days of detection).']
  case_priority varchar [note: 'Risk-based priority level assigned to the case, driving investigator workload management and escalation timelines. CRITICAL cases typically involve OFAC hits or law enforcement referrals.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle status of the AML/BSA investigation case. OPEN = newly initiated; UNDER_REVIEW = active investigation in progress; PENDING_FILING = investigation complete, awaiting FinCEN submission; FILED = SAR/CTR submitted to FinCEN; CLOSED = case resolved with no filing or post-filing closure; ESCALATED = referred to law enforcement or senior compliance.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the AML/BSA investigation case by regulatory obligation or suspicious activity pattern. SAR = Suspicious Activity Report; CTR = Currency Transaction Report; STRUCTURING = deliberate transaction structuring to evade reporting thresholds; STOLI = Stranger-Originated Life Insurance fraud; OFAC = Office of Foreign Assets Control screening hit; KYC_REMEDIATION = Know Your Customer remediation case. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAR|CTR|STRUCTURING|STOLI|OFAC|KYC_REMEDIATION|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this AML case record was first created in the compliance case management system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking per SOX controls.']
  detection_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the suspicious activity or transaction was first detected or identified, which may precede the formal case opening date. Critical for calculating the 30-day SAR filing deadline from the date of initial detection per FinCEN requirements.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final or current disposition outcome of the AML/BSA investigation case. SAR_FILED = Suspicious Activity Report submitted to FinCEN; CTR_FILED = Currency Transaction Report submitted; NO_ACTION = investigation concluded with no suspicious activity confirmed; LAW_ENFORCEMENT_REFERRED = case escalated to law enforcement; POLICY_TERMINATED = associated policy cancelled due to AML findings; CLAIM_DENIED = associated claim denied due to AML findings; PENDING = disposition not yet determined. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAR_FILED|CTR_FILED|NO_ACTION|LAW_ENFORCEMENT_REFERRED|POLICY_TERMINATED|CLAIM_DENIED|PENDING — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disposition_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting the rationale for the case disposition, investigative findings summary, and any actions taken. Required for regulatory examination evidence and internal audit trail. Subject to SAR confidentiality provisions — must not be disclosed to the subject.']
  fincen_filed_date date [note: 'Date on which the SAR or CTR was successfully submitted to FinCEN via the BSA E-Filing System. Used to verify compliance with the 30-day (or 60-day) SAR filing deadline and for regulatory examination evidence.']
  fincen_filing_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory report filed with FinCEN as a result of this AML/BSA case. SAR = Suspicious Activity Report (FinCEN Form 111); CTR = Currency Transaction Report (FinCEN Form 112); FBAR = Foreign Bank Account Report; NONE = case closed with no filing required after investigation.']
  fincen_reference_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation reference number assigned by FinCEN upon successful submission of a SAR or CTR filing. Used to track filing status, respond to law enforcement requests, and demonstrate regulatory compliance. Null until filing is submitted and acknowledged.']
  initiation_source varchar [note: 'Origin channel that triggered the creation of this AML/BSA case. AUTOMATED_ALERT = transaction monitoring system rule fired; MANUAL_REFERRAL = compliance officer or business unit referral; LAW_ENFORCEMENT = subpoena or law enforcement request; REGULATORY_EXAM = identified during state DOI or FinCEN examination; INTERNAL_AUDIT = identified during SOX or internal audit review; AGENT_REFERRAL = agent or broker flagged suspicious activity.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Two or three-letter code identifying the primary regulatory jurisdiction governing this AML case (e.g., US state code or federal jurisdiction code). Determines applicable state DOI reporting requirements and law enforcement referral protocols.']
  kyc_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this AML case has triggered a mandatory KYC enhanced due diligence (EDD) review for the subject policyholder or agent. True = KYC/EDD review required; False = standard KYC sufficient.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to this AML case record. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking for regulatory examination purposes.']
  law_enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the law enforcement agency to which this case was referred, if applicable (e.g., FBI, DEA, IRS-CI, State Attorney General). Null when law_enforcement_referral is False.']
  law_enforcement_referral boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this AML/BSA case has been referred to law enforcement agencies (e.g., FBI, DEA, IRS Criminal Investigation, state law enforcement). True = referral made; False = no referral.']
  ofac_match_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or SDN entry name from the OFAC screening system when a potential or confirmed match is identified. Used for escalation documentation and regulatory reporting. Null when screening status is CLEAR or NOT_SCREENED.']
  ofac_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) and sanctions list screening for the case subject. CONFIRMED_MATCH requires immediate case escalation and potential transaction blocking per OFAC compliance obligations.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score assigned to this AML case by the transaction monitoring system or compliance analyst, typically on a scale of 0-100. Higher scores indicate greater money laundering risk and drive case prioritization. Used for risk-based AML program management.']
  sar_confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this case is subject to SAR confidentiality provisions under the BSA, prohibiting disclosure of the SAR filing or its existence to the subject of the report. True = SAR filed and confidentiality applies; False = no SAR filed or confidentiality not applicable.']
  sar_filing_deadline date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the SAR must be submitted to FinCEN, calculated as 30 days from the detection date (or 60 days if no subject is identified). Drives compliance SLA monitoring and escalation workflows.']
  subject_date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the individual subject under investigation, required for identity verification, OFAC screening, and FinCEN SAR filing. Null for entity (non-individual) subjects.']
  subject_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the primary individual or entity under investigation in this AML/BSA case, as reported on FinCEN SAR/CTR filings. Required for regulatory filing and OFAC screening.']
  subject_tax_number varchar [note: 'Social Security Number (SSN) or Employer Identification Number (EIN) / Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) of the AML case subject, used for FinCEN SAR/CTR filing and OFAC screening. Stored encrypted per HIPAA and IRS data security requirements.']
  subject_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary subject under investigation. Identifies whether the suspicious activity is associated with a policyholder, licensed agent/broker, policy beneficiary, unrelated third party, or a legal entity (trust, corporation).']
  suspicious_activity_type varchar [note: 'Specific category of suspicious activity identified in the investigation, aligned with FinCEN SAR activity type classifications. Examples include: CASH_TRANSACTION, STRUCTURING, MONEY_LAUNDERING, FRAUD, TERRORIST_FINANCING, INSURANCE_FRAUD, STOLI, IDENTITY_THEFT, ELDER_FINANCIAL_ABUSE. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CASH_TRANSACTION|STRUCTURING|MONEY_LAUNDERING|FRAUD|TERRORIST_FINANCING|INSURANCE_FRAUD|STOLI|IDENTITY_THEFT|ELDER_FINANCIAL_ABUSE — promote to reference product]']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount of the suspicious transaction(s) under investigation. For CTR cases, this is the cash transaction amount exceeding the $10,000 reporting threshold. For SAR cases, this is the aggregate amount of suspicious activity. Reported on FinCEN filings.']
  transaction_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the suspicious transaction amount. Typically USD for domestic cases; foreign currency cases may indicate additional money laundering risk.']

  Note: 'Operational record of an Anti-Money Laundering (AML) or Bank Secrecy Act (BSA) investigation case initiated by automated transaction monitoring alerts or manual referrals. Captures case type (SAR, CTR, structuring, STOLI), subject policyholder or agent, triggering transaction details, case status (open, under review, filed, closed), assigned investigator, FinCEN filing reference, and disposition. Supports FinCEN SAR/CTR filing obligations and OFAC screening outcomes.'
}

Table compliance.privacy_incident {
  privacy_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the privacy incident record. Primary key for this entity.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.agency. Business justification: Privacy incidents can originate from agency-level data handling failures (agency staff accessing or exposing policyholder PHI/PII). Linking privacy_incident to agency enables breach investigation, age']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: HIPAA breach remediation, individual notification, and legal response costs must be allocated to cost centers for financial tracking and regulatory expense reporting. Insurance companies track privacy']
  exam_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.exam_finding. Business justification: A privacy incident may be identified through a specific exam finding (e.g., an examiner identifies improper PHI handling during a market conduct exam). Adding exam_finding_id to privacy_incident estab']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to policy.in_force_policy. Business justification: Privacy incidents often involve specific policy records (e.g., unauthorized access, data breach). Linking enables tracking which policies were affected, supports breach notification requirements under']
  insurance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Privacy incidents generate incident reports, breach notification letters, HHS notifications, and remediation documentation required by HIPAA and state breach laws. Critical for breach response workflo']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Privacy breaches result in penalties, legal costs, notification costs, credit monitoring expenses that must be journalized. Finance needs to track actual financial impact for reserve adequacy and disc']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Privacy incidents are reported by the legal entity responsible for the data. HIPAA and state breach notification laws require entity-specific reporting and accountability.']
  market_conduct_exam_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.market_conduct_exam. Business justification: Privacy incidents may be identified during a market conduct examination (e.g., DOI examination of data handling practices). Adding market_conduct_exam_id to privacy_incident enables tracking of exam-i']
  mib_inquiry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.mib_inquiry. Business justification: MIB inquiries involve consumer report data; unauthorized access or disclosure triggers privacy incident reporting. FCRA/privacy requirement: breach tracking for MIB data. Links privacy incident to spe']
  notification_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.template. Business justification: HIPAA and state breach notification laws require specific notification letter templates for affected individuals, HHS, and state AGs. Privacy incident response teams must track which approved notifica']
  paramedical_exam_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.paramedical_exam. Business justification: Paramedical exam results contain PHI; breaches (lost specimens, unauthorized access to lab results) require incident tracking. HIPAA requirement: breach notification for medical examination data. Link']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary policyholder whose Protected Health Information (PHI) or Personally Identifiable Information (PII) was involved in the incident. Links to the policyholder master record.']
  producer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to agent.producer. Business justification: Privacy incidents often involve producer actions: unauthorized PHI access, lost/stolen devices with customer data, email breaches. HIPAA breach investigations require tracking which producer caused or']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: HIPAA privacy incidents are governed by specific regulatory obligations (HIPAA Privacy Rule, HITECH Act, state breach notification laws). Adding regulatory_obligation_id to privacy_incident normalizes']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Privacy incidents trigger state-specific breach notification obligations that vary by jurisdiction. Adding state_regulation_id to privacy_incident normalizes the governing state regulation, enabling s']
  underwriting_aps_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.aps_record. Business justification: APS records contain highly sensitive PHI; breaches involving APS documents (lost records, unauthorized disclosure) must be tracked. HIPAA requirement: breach notification for attending physician state']
  uw_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to underwriting.application. Business justification: Privacy breaches during underwriting (unauthorized PHI access, lost medical records) must be tracked against the application. HIPAA requirement: breach notification tied to affected individual\'s appli']
  affected_individuals_count varchar [note: 'Number of individuals whose PHI or PII was involved in the privacy incident. Determines notification thresholds: incidents affecting 500+ individuals in a state require immediate HHS OCR notification; 500+ total triggers media notification.']
  breach_determination_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal breach determination was made following completion of the four-factor risk assessment. Relevant for calculating notification deadlines under HIPAA and state breach notification laws.']
