  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the privacy incident record was first created in the compliance management system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  data_format varchar [note: 'Format of the data involved in the privacy incident — electronic (digital files, databases, emails), paper (physical documents), verbal (oral disclosure), or mixed. Influences remediation approach and risk assessment scoring.']
  discovery_date date [note: 'The date on which the covered entity or business associate first became aware of the potential privacy incident. Starts the 60-day HIPAA breach notification clock under 45 CFR § 164.412.']
  doi_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which notification was submitted to the State Department of Insurance. Under the NAIC model law, notification is generally required within 72 hours of determining a cybersecurity event.']
  doi_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether notification to the State Department of Insurance is required under the NAIC Insurance Data Security Model Law or state-specific insurance data security regulations.']
  hhs_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which notification was submitted to HHS OCR via the breach reporting portal. Must occur within 60 days of discovery for breaches affecting 500+ individuals, or by March 1 of the following year for smaller breaches.']
  hhs_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether notification to the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services Office for Civil Rights (HHS OCR) is required. True for all confirmed HIPAA breaches; breaches affecting 500+ individuals require immediate notification, others are reported annually.']
  incident_date date [note: 'The date on which the privacy incident actually occurred or is estimated to have begun. May differ from discovery_date when incidents are discovered after the fact. Required for HHS OCR annual breach report.']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the privacy incident — what happened, how it was discovered, what data was involved, and the circumstances of the unauthorized access or disclosure. Core documentation for regulatory submissions and internal investigations.']
  incident_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the privacy incident ceased or was contained. Used to calculate exposure duration and assess scope of harm for breach risk assessment.']
  incident_location varchar [note: 'Physical or logical location where the privacy incident occurred (e.g., home office, branch office name, cloud environment, third-party vendor site). Used for root cause analysis and remediation targeting.']
  incident_reference_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable identifier assigned to the privacy incident at intake (e.g., PI-2024-000123). Used in regulatory correspondence, HHS OCR submissions, and state AG notifications.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the privacy incident through its lifecycle — from initial intake through investigation, breach determination, notification, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|under_investigation|breach_confirmed|no_breach_determined|notified|closed|reopened — promote to reference product]']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of the privacy incident as defined by HHS OCR breach reporting categories. Drives notification obligations and remediation workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: unauthorized_access|unauthorized_disclosure|lost_device|stolen_device|hacking_it_incident|improper_disposal|mailing_error|other — promote to reference product]']
  includes_financial_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether financial account numbers, payment card data, or banking information were among the data elements exposed. Triggers PCI DSS notification obligations and heightened state breach law requirements.']
  includes_health_information boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Protected Health Information (PHI) as defined under HIPAA was among the data elements exposed. Determines applicability of HIPAA Breach Notification Rule and state health privacy laws.']
  includes_ssn boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Social Security Numbers (SSNs) were among the data elements exposed in the incident. SSN exposure triggers mandatory notification under virtually all 50 state data breach notification laws and heightens identity theft risk assessment.']
  individual_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which written notification was sent to affected individuals. Must occur within 60 days of discovery of a confirmed breach. Supports HIPAA compliance deadline tracking.']
  individual_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether written notification to affected individuals is required under the HIPAA Breach Notification Rule. Required for all confirmed breaches; must be sent within 60 days of discovery.']
  involved_business_associate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a HIPAA Business Associate (BA) — such as a TPA, vendor, or service provider — was involved in or responsible for the privacy incident. Business associates have independent HIPAA breach notification obligations to the covered entity.']
  is_breach_determined boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the privacy incident has been formally determined to constitute a reportable breach under the HIPAA Breach Notification Rule following completion of the four-factor risk assessment. True = breach confirmed; False = not a breach (low probability of compromise).']
  jurisdiction_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code of the primary jurisdiction governing notification obligations for this incident. Determines which state data breach notification law applies and the applicable notification deadline.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the privacy incident record. Supports audit trail integrity and change management tracking.']
  media_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which notification was provided to prominent media outlets in the affected state(s). Required within 60 days of discovery for breaches affecting 500+ residents of a state.']
  media_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether notification to prominent media outlets is required. Triggered when a confirmed breach affects 500 or more residents of a single state or jurisdiction.']
  multi_state_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident involves affected individuals across multiple states, triggering notification obligations under multiple state data breach notification laws simultaneously.']
  no_breach_rationale varchar [note: 'Documented justification when the incident is determined NOT to constitute a reportable breach, referencing the four-factor risk assessment outcome (nature/extent of PHI, unauthorized person, whether PHI was actually acquired/viewed, extent of risk mitigation). Required for HIPAA documentation obligations.']
  notification_deadline_date date [note: 'Calculated deadline by which all required notifications (HHS OCR, individuals, media, state AG, DOI) must be completed. Derived from discovery_date plus the applicable regulatory notification window (e.g., 60 days under HIPAA, 30–72 hours under NAIC model law). Stored for deadline monitoring and escalation.']
  phi_data_elements_exposed varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of PHI or PII data element categories involved in the incident (e.g., name, SSN, date_of_birth, diagnosis, policy_number, financial_account, biometric). Required for HHS OCR breach report and state AG notifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: name|ssn|date_of_birth|diagnosis|treatment|policy_number|financial_account|biometric|address|email|phone — promote to reference product]']
  remediation_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Narrative description of corrective and remediation actions taken in response to the privacy incident (e.g., system access revoked, encryption implemented, workforce retrained, BA agreement updated, policy revised). Required for HIPAA documentation and regulatory examination.']
  remediation_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which all remediation actions were confirmed as completed. Supports closure of the incident record and regulatory documentation of corrective action.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which all remediation actions must be completed. Used for compliance tracking and escalation management.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the privacy incident was formally reported to the compliance or privacy office. Establishes the internal reporting timeline for SOX controls and HIPAA workforce training obligations.']
  risk_assessment_score varchar [note: 'Overall risk level determined by the four-factor HIPAA breach risk assessment: (1) nature and extent of PHI involved, (2) identity of unauthorized person, (3) whether PHI was actually acquired or viewed, (4) extent to which risk has been mitigated. Drives breach determination and notification decisions.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the root cause of the privacy incident. Used for trend analysis, remediation prioritization, and regulatory reporting to HHS OCR and state DOIs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: human_error|malicious_insider|external_attack|system_failure|vendor_error|process_failure|other — promote to reference product]']
  state_ag_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which notification was submitted to the State Attorney General. Deadline varies by state (e.g., 30 days in some states, 60 days in others).']
  state_ag_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether notification to the State Attorney General is required under applicable state data breach notification law. Requirements vary by state.']

  Note: 'Record of a HIPAA privacy incident, data breach, or unauthorized disclosure of Protected Health Information (PHI) or Personally Identifiable Information (PII). Captures incident type, discovery date, affected individuals count, data elements exposed, breach determination (yes/no), notification obligations triggered (HHS, state AG, affected individuals), notification dates, and remediation actions taken. Supports HIPAA Breach Notification Rule compliance and state data breach law reporting.'
}

Table compliance.policy_form_approval {
  policy_form_approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each policy form or rate filing approval record. Primary key for the policy_form_approval data product.']
  acord_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.acord_form. Business justification: Policy form approvals are filed for specific ACORD forms or company-proprietary forms. Linking policy_form_approval directly to acord_form enables compliance teams to determine which ACORD form versio']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Form filing costs (actuarial review, legal counsel, state filing fees) are tracked by product development cost center for product profitability analysis and budget management.']
  form_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Form approvals require the actual policy form documents, rate manuals, actuarial certifications, and DOI approval letters. Essential for state form filing workflow and approved form library management']
  form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.form. Business justification: A policy_form_approval is the DOI regulatory approval of a specific product form. The compliance record must reference the product.form entity it approves. This is the most fundamental link in form fi']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Form approvals are entity-specific (each legal entity must file for approval in each jurisdiction where it operates). Fundamental regulatory filing requirement for product distribution.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_plan. Business justification: Form approvals are state-specific approvals of product plans for sale in each jurisdiction. Each approval record ties to the plan being approved. Critical for determining which plans can be sold in wh']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Form approvals are version-specific - when a plan version changes (benefits, rates, terms), new state approvals are required. More granular than plan-level linking. Essential for version control, stat']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Policy form approvals are regulatory filings that fulfill specific state DOI obligations. The obligation defines the filing type and requirements.']
  rider_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.rider_definition. Business justification: Rider forms require separate DOI form approvals in each state. A policy_form_approval covering a rider must reference the rider_definition it approves. This is standard DOI filing practice — rider app']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Policy form approvals must comply with state-specific insurance regulations. The approval describes the filing, while the regulation describes the state-specific rules governing form approval.']
  actuarial_justification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the actuarial memorandum or actuarial certification document that supports the rate filing, including loss ratio analysis, experience data, and rate adequacy demonstration. Required for rate filings per state DOI requirements.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the state DOI formally approved the form or rate filing. Null if the filing is pending, disapproved, or withdrawn.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the form or rate filing with the state DOI. Drives whether the form or rate may be used in the jurisdiction. Deemed approved applies when the DOI has not acted within the statutory review period.']
  company_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Internal company-assigned reference number used to track the filing within the company\'s compliance and policy administration systems, independent of the SERFF or DOI reference numbers.']
  company_response_date date [note: 'The date on which the company submitted its formal response to DOI objections or requests for additional information. Tracks responsiveness within the regulatory review cycle.']
  conditional_approval_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of conditions or modifications required by the DOI as part of a conditional approval. The company must satisfy these conditions before or concurrent with use of the form or rate. Populated only when approval_status is \'conditionally_approved\'.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this policy form approval record was first created in the compliance data product, capturing the initial data entry or system ingestion event.']
  deemed_approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the filing is deemed approved by operation of law because the DOI did not act within the statutory review period. Calculated as submission_date plus review_period_days. Null if the DOI acted before the deadline.']
  disapproval_reason varchar [note: 'The stated reason provided by the state DOI for disapproving the form or rate filing. Populated only when approval_status is \'disapproved\'. Used to guide remediation and refiling efforts.']
  doi_approval_reference varchar [note: 'The official approval reference number, certificate number, or acknowledgment identifier issued by the state Department of Insurance (DOI) upon approval of the filing. Used to evidence regulatory compliance during market conduct examinations.']
  doi_objection_details varchar [note: 'Narrative description of objections, deficiency letters, or requests for additional information issued by the state DOI during the review process. Captures the substance of regulatory concerns requiring company response.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the approved form or rate schedule becomes effective for use in new policy issuance or in-force policy administration within the jurisdiction. For rate filings, this is the proposed or approved rate effective date.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the form or rate approval expires or is superseded, after which the form or rate may no longer be used for new business. Null for approvals with no defined expiration.']
  filing_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the purpose and scope of the filing, including key changes from prior versions, new product features, benefit modifications, or regulatory compliance updates driving the submission.']
  filing_method varchar [note: 'The regulatory filing method required by the jurisdiction for this form or rate filing. Prior approval requires DOI approval before use; file-and-use allows use upon filing; use-and-file allows use before filing; informational is for notice only.']
  filing_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal compliance officer, actuary, or product manager responsible for managing this filing through the regulatory approval process.']
  filing_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to the filing submission, as issued by the state Department of Insurance (DOI) or the company\'s internal filing tracking system (e.g., SERFF tracking number or company-assigned filing ID).']
  irc_7702_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filed policy form has been reviewed and confirmed to comply with IRC Section 7702 life insurance tax qualification requirements, including the Cash Value Accumulation Test (CVAT) or Guideline Premium Test (GPT). Critical for life insurance tax treatment.']
  is_currently_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the form or rate is currently approved and authorized for use in the jurisdiction as of the current date. Derived from approval_status and effective/expiration dates; serves as the operational compliance gate for policy issuance systems.']
  is_in_use boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the form or rate schedule is actively being used in new business issuance or in-force policy administration within the jurisdiction. Distinguishes approved-but-not-deployed forms from actively deployed ones.']
  jurisdiction_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or territory code identifying the jurisdiction in which this form or rate filing was submitted to the Department of Insurance. Ensures only approved forms and rates are used in each specific jurisdiction.']
  ltc_rate_increase_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'For Long-Term Care (LTC) insurance rate increase filings, the specific percentage increase in LTC premiums requested from the DOI. Subject to heightened regulatory scrutiny and actuarial justification requirements under NAIC LTC model regulations.']
  mec_test_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the IRC Section 7702 compliance test methodology applied to the policy form: Cash Value Accumulation Test (CVAT) or Guideline Premium Test (GPT). Determines Modified Endowment Contract (MEC) status thresholds. Not applicable for annuity or non-life products.']
  naic_product_code varchar [note: 'The NAIC standardized product code assigned to the insurance product type associated with this filing. Used for regulatory reporting, market conduct analysis, and cross-state filing coordination.']
  outside_counsel_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the external legal counsel or regulatory affairs firm engaged to support the filing, if applicable. Used for complex or contested filings requiring specialized regulatory legal expertise.']
  prior_form_number varchar [note: 'The form number of the previously approved version that this filing supersedes or replaces. Enables traceability of form revision history and supports grandfathering analysis for in-force policies.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'The insurance product line to which the filed form or rate schedule applies (e.g., Term Life, Whole Life (WL), Universal Life (UL), Variable Universal Life (VUL), Annuity, Long-Term Care (LTC), Disability Income (DI), Group Life). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: term_life|whole_life|universal_life|variable_life|indexed_universal_life|fixed_indexed_annuity|variable_annuity|ltc|disability_income|group_life|spia — promote to reference product]']
  rate_change_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'For rate filings, the percentage change in premium rates proposed or approved relative to the current in-force rates. Positive values indicate a rate increase; negative values indicate a rate decrease. Null for form-only filings.']
  rate_filing_type varchar [note: 'For rate filings, classifies the nature of the rate action: new rate schedule introduction, revision to existing rates, Long-Term Care (LTC) rate increase filing, or withdrawal of a rate schedule. Null for pure form filings.']
  review_period_days varchar [note: 'The statutory or regulatory maximum number of days the DOI has to review and act on the filing before it is deemed approved (for prior approval states). Varies by state and product line.']
  sec_registration_number varchar [note: 'For variable product filings, the SEC registration number (e.g., Form S-1, Form N-4 registration) associated with the product. Required for variable life and variable annuity products subject to SEC registration. Null for non-variable products.']
  serff_tracking_number varchar [note: 'The unique tracking number assigned by the NAIC\'s System for Electronic Rate and Form Filing (SERFF) platform when the filing is submitted electronically. Primary cross-reference identifier for SERFF-based filings.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the form or rate filing was formally submitted to the state Department of Insurance. Marks the start of the regulatory review clock for prior approval filings.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this policy form approval record was most recently updated, reflecting any changes to filing status, approval dates, DOI responses, or other attributes.']
  variable_product_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filed form or rate applies to a variable insurance or annuity product subject to SEC and FINRA oversight in addition to state DOI regulation. Triggers additional compliance requirements for variable product filings.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'The date on which the company voluntarily withdrew the filing from DOI review. Populated only when approval_status is \'withdrawn\'.']

  Note: 'Master record of state DOI filings for policy forms, rider forms, endorsement forms, and premium rate schedules. For form filings: captures form number, form type (base policy, rider, endorsement, application), product line, filing method (prior approval, file-and-use, use-and-file), state, submission/approval dates, approval reference, and form status. For rate filings: captures filing type (new rate, rate revision, LTC rate increase), actuarial justification reference, proposed effective date, approval status, rate change percentage, and DOI response details. Ensures only approved forms and rates are used in each jurisdiction, supports state rate regulation compliance, and serves as the single source of truth for all form and rate approval status across all jurisdictions.'
}

Table compliance.rate_filing {
  rate_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each rate filing record in the compliance domain. Primary key for the rate_filing data product.']
  coi_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.coi_rate_schedule. Business justification: COI rate schedule changes for in-force UL/IUL policies require state rate filings. The compliance rate_filing that approves a COI rate increase must reference the specific coi_rate_schedule it governs']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Rate filing preparation costs (actuarial analysis, regulatory filings, legal review) are significant and tracked by cost center for product line profitability and pricing adequacy analysis.']
  ifrs17_contract_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ifrs17_contract_group. Business justification: Rate filings affect the profitability classification and CSM of IFRS17 contract groups. When rates change, the onerous contract assessment and CSM release pattern for the affected contract group must ']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Rate filings are made by specific legal entities in specific jurisdictions. Fundamental regulatory filing requirement - rates must be approved for each entity in each state.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the insurance product (e.g., term life, whole life, LTC, annuity) for which the rate filing is submitted. Links the filing to the specific product line subject to rate regulation.']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan_version. Business justification: Rate filings are version-specific regulatory submissions. Actuaries and state DOI filings reference the exact plan version whose rates are being changed. rate_filing already has plan_id but lacks plan']
  policy_form_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_form_approval. Business justification: Rate filings reference specific approved policy forms (rate changes apply to approved form numbers). Links rate filing to the form approval for product launch coordination and regulatory compliance va']
  premium_rate_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.premium_rate_table. Business justification: A compliance rate_filing is the regulatory approval that activates a new premium_rate_table. Actuarial and compliance teams must link the approved filing to the rate table it governs. This is the core']
  rbc_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.rbc_calculation. Business justification: Rate filings, particularly LTC rate increase filings, are directly tied to RBC calculations demonstrating capital adequacy supporting the rate change. Regulators require actuarial justification linkin']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Rate filings fulfill regulatory obligations for premium rate approval. The obligation defines the filing type and jurisdiction requirements.']
  state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_regulation. Business justification: Rate filings must comply with state-specific rate filing regulations. The filing describes the submission, while the regulation describes the state-specific rules governing rate approval.']
  affected_policy_count varchar [note: 'Number of in-force policies in the filing jurisdiction that will be affected by the proposed rate change. Required for LTC rate increase filings and used by the DOI to assess consumer impact. Derived from policy administration system counts at time of filing.']
  annual_premium_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total annual premium impact (in USD) of the proposed rate change across all affected in-force policies in the filing jurisdiction. Used by the DOI to assess the financial impact on policyholders and by the company for financial planning.']
  approved_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the DOI-approved rates become effective for use. May differ from the proposed effective date if the DOI modifies the requested effective date as a condition of approval. Null until the filing reaches approved status.']
  approved_rate_change_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The rate change percentage as finally approved by the state DOI, which may differ from the originally proposed rate_change_percentage if the DOI required modifications as a condition of approval. Null until the filing is approved.']
  certifying_actuary_credential varchar [note: 'Professional actuarial credential of the certifying actuary (e.g., FSA — Fellow of the Society of Actuaries, MAAA — Member of the American Academy of Actuaries). Validates the actuary\'s qualification to certify the rate filing.']
  certifying_actuary_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the qualified actuary (Fellow of the Society of Actuaries or equivalent) who certified the actuarial memorandum and rate justification supporting this filing. Required by most state DOIs as part of the rate filing package.']
  consumer_notice_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rate filing requires individual policyholder notification of the rate change (e.g., LTC rate increases, in-force rate revisions). Triggers the consumer notification workflow in the policy administration system.']
  consumer_notice_sent_date date [note: 'Date on which policyholder notification letters were sent for the rate change. Required for LTC rate increases and other in-force rate revisions requiring consumer notice. Null if consumer_notice_required_flag is false.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rate filing record was first created in the compliance system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking.']
  disapproval_date date [note: 'Date on which the state Department of Insurance (DOI) formally disapproved the rate filing. Null for filings that are approved, pending, or withdrawn. Triggers the company\'s obligation to respond or refile.']
  disapproval_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the state DOI\'s stated reason(s) for disapproving the rate filing. Null for approved or pending filings. Used to guide refiling strategy and actuarial revisions.']
  doi_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the state Department of Insurance (DOI) formally approved the rate filing. Null for filings that are pending, disapproved, or withdrawn. Distinct from the approved effective date, which is when rates go into use.']
  doi_objection_count varchar [note: 'Number of formal objections or deficiency letters issued by the state DOI during the review of this rate filing. Each objection requires a company response and may extend the review timeline. Tracks regulatory engagement complexity.']
  doi_objection_response_date date [note: 'Date on which the company submitted its response to the DOI\'s most recent objection or deficiency letter. Used to track compliance with DOI response deadlines and filing timeline management.']
  doi_objection_response_due_date date [note: 'Due date for the company\'s response to the most recent DOI objection or deficiency letter. Failure to respond by this date may result in automatic disapproval or withdrawal of the filing.']
  experience_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the actuarial experience period used to develop the loss ratios, claim costs, and other actuarial assumptions supporting the rate filing. Together with the start date, defines the historical data window underlying the actuarial justification.']
  experience_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the actuarial experience period used to develop the loss ratios, claim costs, and other actuarial assumptions supporting the rate filing. Defines the historical data window underlying the actuarial justification.']
  external_counsel_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or name of external legal counsel engaged to support the rate filing, if applicable. Relevant for contested filings, LTC rate increase hearings, or filings requiring regulatory negotiation.']
  filing_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the rate filing, summarizing the nature of the rate change, the affected policy forms, the actuarial basis, and any special circumstances (e.g., LTC rate stabilization, experience deterioration, regulatory mandate). Used for internal compliance tracking and DOI correspondence.']
  filing_method varchar [note: 'Regulatory filing method required by the state for this rate filing. Prior approval requires DOI approval before use; file-and-use allows immediate use upon filing; use-and-file requires filing after use; no-filing-required states are exempt. Determines the compliance timeline and approval workflow.']
  filing_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to the rate filing, typically generated by the company or the state DOI system (e.g., SERFF tracking number or company-assigned filing reference). Used for cross-referencing with state DOI portals and internal compliance tracking.']
  filing_preparer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal compliance or actuarial staff member responsible for preparing and submitting the rate filing. Used for accountability tracking and DOI correspondence.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rate filing with the state DOI. Tracks the filing from initial draft through submission, regulatory review, and final disposition (approved, disapproved, or withdrawn). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|pending_review|approved|disapproved|withdrawn|conditionally_approved — promote to reference product]']
  hearing_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date of the DOI public or administrative hearing for this rate filing. Null if hearing_required_flag is false. Tracks the regulatory hearing calendar for compliance management.']
  hearing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state DOI has required a public hearing or administrative hearing as part of the rate filing review process. Hearings are common for large LTC rate increases and may significantly extend the approval timeline.']
  jurisdiction_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or territory code identifying the state Department of Insurance (DOI) to which this rate filing is submitted. Each state has independent rate regulation authority; a single product rate change may require separate filings per state.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Regulatory line of business classification for the rate filing as defined by NAIC and state DOI reporting standards. Determines the applicable rate regulation statutes and actuarial certification requirements.']
  loss_ratio_current decimal(7,4) [note: 'The current (pre-filing) loss ratio for the affected product line and policy forms, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.85 = 85%). A key actuarial metric supporting the rate change justification; high loss ratios indicate inadequate current rates.']
  loss_ratio_projected decimal(7,4) [note: 'The projected loss ratio following implementation of the proposed rate change, expressed as a decimal. Demonstrates to the DOI that the revised rates will achieve actuarial adequacy and rate stability.']
  ltc_rate_increase_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filing is a Long-Term Care (LTC) rate increase filing, which is subject to heightened regulatory scrutiny, consumer notification requirements, and actuarial certification standards under the NAIC LTC Insurance Model Regulation.']
  policy_form_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of policy form numbers (as approved by the DOI) to which the rate filing applies. Policy forms are the standardized contract templates approved by the state DOI; rate filings are tied to specific approved forms.']
  prior_filing_number varchar [note: 'Filing number of the immediately preceding approved rate filing for the same product line and jurisdiction. Establishes the rate revision history chain and allows comparison of current vs. prior approved rates.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Broad insurance product line classification for the rate filing (e.g., Term Life, Whole Life (WL), Universal Life (UL), Long-Term Care (LTC), Annuity). Determines applicable regulatory framework and actuarial standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: term_life|whole_life|universal_life|variable_life|ltc|annuity|disability_income|accidental_death — promote to reference product]']
  proposed_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the company proposes the new or revised rates to become effective for new business or in-force policies. Subject to DOI approval for prior approval states; may differ from the approved effective date.']
  rate_change_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage change in premium rates proposed in this filing relative to the currently approved rates. Positive values indicate a rate increase; negative values indicate a rate decrease. Critical metric for actuarial justification and DOI review. For LTC rate increase filings, subject to heightened regulatory scrutiny.']
  rate_change_type varchar [note: 'Directional classification of the rate change proposed in this filing. Distinguishes between rate increases, decreases, no-change filings (e.g., non-forfeiture value updates), and new rate filings for newly introduced products.']
  rate_stability_certification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filing includes an actuarial certification of rate stability as required by the NAIC LTC Insurance Model Regulation. Rate stability certifications attest that the proposed rates are not expected to require future increases.']
  serff_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Tracking number assigned by the NAIC System for Electronic Rate and Form Filing (SERFF) upon submission of the rate filing to the state DOI. Serves as the authoritative cross-reference between the company\'s internal records and the state regulatory portal.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the rate filing was formally submitted to the state Department of Insurance (DOI). Marks the start of the regulatory review clock for prior approval states and establishes the compliance timeline for file-and-use states.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rate filing record was most recently updated in the compliance system. Supports audit trail requirements, change tracking, and data lineage for the Silver layer.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the company voluntarily withdrew the rate filing from DOI review. Null for filings not withdrawn. Withdrawal may occur due to actuarial revisions, regulatory feedback, or business strategy changes.']

  Note: 'Record of premium rate and non-forfeiture value filings submitted to state DOIs for approval or notification. Captures product line, filing type (new rate, rate revision, LTC rate increase), filing method (prior approval, file-and-use), state, actuarial justification reference, proposed effective date, DOI submission date, approval status, approved effective date, and rate change percentage. Supports compliance with state rate regulation requirements.'
}

Table compliance.jurisdiction_license {
  jurisdiction_license_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each jurisdiction license record in the Silver layer. Primary key for the jurisdiction_license data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: License renewal fees, statutory deposit requirements, and state compliance costs are allocated to cost centers by jurisdiction. Insurance companies track licensing costs by state/cost center for expen']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Licenses are issued to legal entities. Fundamental regulatory relationship - each legal entity must be licensed in jurisdictions where it operates. Required for regulatory compliance tracking.']
  license_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: Jurisdiction licenses have associated certificate documents, renewal notices, and DOI correspondence stored in the document system. Compliance teams need to retrieve the actual license certificate doc']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Jurisdiction licenses are maintained to satisfy regulatory obligations for authority to transact insurance business. The license describes the authority granted, while the obligation describes the req']
  admitted_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the company is admitted (licensed and subject to state guaranty fund protection), non-admitted, or writing on a surplus lines basis in the jurisdiction. Affects policyholder protections and regulatory requirements.']
  annual_statement_filing_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the company must file its NAIC Annual Statement (statutory financial statement) with the DOI for this jurisdiction. Typically March 1 for most states but may vary. Drives the regulatory filing calendar.']
  authorized_lines_of_business varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of the specific lines of business authorized under this license (e.g., Life, Annuity, Variable Life, Variable Annuity, Accident and Health). Governs which product types may be sold in the jurisdiction.']
  compliance_officer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company\'s designated compliance officer or regulatory contact responsible for managing the license and DOI relationship in this jurisdiction. Used for regulatory correspondence and examination coordination.']
  domicile_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS code for the state in which the company is domiciled (chartered). Distinguishes the home state license from foreign state licenses and determines which state\'s insurance laws govern the company\'s primary regulatory oversight.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the Certificate of Authority or license became effective and the company was first authorized to transact the specified line of business in the jurisdiction. Marks the start of the binding authorization period.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the current license term expires and renewal is required to maintain authorization. Null for perpetual licenses that do not require periodic renewal. Drives the renewal calendar and compliance alerts.']
  fein varchar [note: 'The Federal Employer Identification Number (FEIN) of the company as registered with the IRS. Required on certain state DOI filings and tax-related regulatory submissions. Also known as Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN).']
  guaranty_fund_member_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the company is a member of the state life and health guaranty association for this jurisdiction. Admitted carriers are typically required to be members. Relevant for policyholder protection disclosures and NAIC reporting.']
  is_domestic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this license is a domestic (home state) license (True) or a foreign state license (False). Domestic licenses are issued by the state of domicile; foreign licenses are issued by other states where the company is admitted to transact business.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body that issued the license (e.g., California Department of Insurance, New York State Department of Financial Services). Identifies the specific DOI or equivalent authority responsible for the license.']
  issuing_authority_contact varchar [note: 'Contact information (address, phone, or email) for the issuing DOI or regulatory authority. Used for correspondence, renewal submissions, and regulatory inquiries.']
  jurisdiction_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the state or territory jurisdiction (e.g., California, New York, Puerto Rico, District of Columbia). Complements the state code for display and reporting purposes.']
  jurisdiction_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state or territory code identifying the jurisdiction in which the license is held (e.g., CA, NY, TX, PR, DC). Used to validate that the company is authorized to write business in the state before policy issuance.']
  last_examination_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent market conduct or financial examination conducted by the DOI in this jurisdiction. Used to track examination cycles and anticipate upcoming examinations per NAIC examination standards.']
  license_condition_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DOI has imposed any conditions, restrictions, or limitations on this license. When True, the license_conditions field contains the details. Conditions may restrict product types, distribution channels, or require enhanced reporting.']
  license_conditions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any conditions, restrictions, or limitations imposed by the DOI on this license. Examples include restrictions on writing new business, requirements for enhanced consumer disclosures, or limitations on specific product features. Populated only when license_condition_flag is True.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'The official license or Certificate of Authority (COA) number assigned by the state Department of Insurance (DOI) to the company for the given jurisdiction. This is the externally-known business identifier used in regulatory filings and correspondence with the DOI.']
  license_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the jurisdiction license as maintained by the state DOI. Active indicates the company is authorized to transact business. Suspended or revoked statuses trigger immediate cessation of new business writing in the jurisdiction.']
  license_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the insurance license by product line authorized. Determines which lines of business the company is permitted to transact in the jurisdiction. Variable product types are subject to additional SEC/FINRA oversight. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: life|annuity|variable_life|variable_annuity|accident_health|disability_income — promote to reference product if additional lines are added]']
  minimum_capital_requirement decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum capital and surplus amount required by the jurisdiction\'s DOI for the company to maintain its license, denominated in USD. Linked to Risk-Based Capital (RBC) requirements and solvency monitoring.']
  naic_company_code varchar [note: 'The five-digit NAIC company code assigned to the insurer. Used in all NAIC regulatory filings, state financial examinations, and market conduct reporting to uniquely identify the company across all jurisdictions.']
  next_examination_due_date date [note: 'The anticipated or scheduled date for the next DOI examination in this jurisdiction. Typically every 3-5 years per NAIC standards. Used for compliance planning and resource allocation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional compliance notes, DOI correspondence summaries, or internal observations related to the license. Used by the compliance team to document material events not captured in structured fields.']
  original_issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the company was first granted a license in this jurisdiction for this license type, regardless of subsequent renewals. Establishes the historical tenure of authorization in the jurisdiction.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this jurisdiction license record was first created in the compliance data product. Provides audit trail for record provenance in the Silver layer.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this jurisdiction license record was most recently updated in the compliance data product. Supports change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  registered_agent_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the registered agent for service of process in the jurisdiction. Required for legal and regulatory correspondence. Maintained as confidential business contact information.']
  registered_agent_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company\'s registered agent for service of process in the jurisdiction. Required by most states as a condition of licensure. The registered agent receives legal notices and regulatory correspondence on behalf of the company.']
  reinstatement_date date [note: 'The date on which a previously suspended or lapsed license was reinstated by the DOI. Null if the license has never been reinstated. Marks the resumption of authorization to transact business.']
  renewal_application_date date [note: 'The date on which the company submitted the renewal application to the state DOI. Used to confirm timely filing and to calculate days-before-expiration for compliance reporting.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date by which the renewal application and associated fees must be submitted to the DOI to avoid lapse of authorization. May differ from expiration_date where the DOI requires advance filing.']
  renewal_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The monetary amount paid or payable to the state DOI for license renewal, denominated in USD. Tracked for budgeting, accounts payable, and regulatory expense reporting.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the renewal process for the license. Tracks whether the renewal application has been submitted, is under DOI review, approved, or has lapsed. Critical for compliance monitoring to ensure continuous authorization.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the type of restriction or condition imposed on the license by the DOI. Enables structured reporting and monitoring of restricted licenses across jurisdictions.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'The date on which the DOI permanently revoked the license, if applicable. Null for active or suspended licenses. A revoked license requires a new application process to re-enter the jurisdiction.']
  statutory_deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount of securities or cash deposited with the state treasury or DOI as a condition of licensure in the jurisdiction, denominated in USD. Required by certain states as a financial security requirement for admitted carriers.']
  statutory_deposit_type varchar [note: 'Type of asset held on deposit with the state as a condition of licensure. Determines the nature of the financial security arrangement and applicable valuation methods under SAP.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'The date on which the DOI suspended the license, if applicable. Null for licenses that have never been suspended. Triggers immediate cessation of new business writing and notification to the Policy Administration System.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason provided by the DOI for suspending the license. Populated only when suspension_date is not null. Used for regulatory response planning and remediation tracking.']
  variable_product_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this license authorizes the sale of variable insurance products (variable life or variable annuity) that are subject to SEC and FINRA oversight in addition to state DOI regulation. True triggers additional compliance checks.']

  Note: 'Master record of the company\'s Certificate of Authority (COA) and licenses to transact insurance business in each state and territory. Captures jurisdiction, license type (life, annuity, variable life, variable annuity), license number, effective date, renewal date, renewal status, authorized lines of business, and any license conditions or restrictions imposed by the DOI. Ensures the company only writes business in jurisdictions where it is properly licensed.'
}

Table compliance.state_regulation {
  state_regulation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the state-specific insurance regulation record. Primary key.']
  primary_superseded_by_regulation_state_regulation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer state regulation that replaces this regulation, if applicable. Null if not superseded.']
  accelerated_death_benefit_regulation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state has specific regulations governing accelerated death benefit riders. True if ADB regulations apply, False if not.']
  best_interest_standard_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state has adopted a best interest standard for annuity sales (similar to Regulation Best Interest). True if best interest standard applies, False if not.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes documenting compliance considerations, implementation guidance, or special handling requirements for this state regulation.']
  contestability_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months during which the insurer may contest a life insurance policy for material misrepresentation, as mandated by state law. Typically 24 months but may vary by state.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this state regulation record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the state-specific regulation becomes enforceable and applicable to insurance operations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the state regulation ceases to be in effect, if applicable. Null for regulations with no defined end date.']
  form_filing_method varchar [note: 'State-mandated method for submitting and approving policy forms (prior approval required before use, file and use after DOI review, use and file with post-use notification, no filing required).']
  free_look_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the policyholder has to review and cancel a newly issued policy without penalty, as mandated by state regulation. Varies by state and product type.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after premium due date during which coverage remains in force without lapse, as required by state regulation. Typically 30-31 days but varies by state.']
  illustration_regulation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state has specific regulations governing life insurance policy illustrations. True if illustration rules apply, False if not.']
  insurable_interest_requirement varchar [note: 'State-specific definition and requirements for establishing insurable interest in life insurance transactions. Describes when insurable interest must exist (at inception, at death, etc.).']
  interest_rate_guarantee_minimum decimal(5,4) [note: 'State-mandated minimum guaranteed interest rate for cash value accumulation in life insurance and annuity products. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0200 for 2%).']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the state regulatory body that issued or enforces this regulation (e.g., California Department of Insurance, New York Department of Financial Services).']
  jurisdiction_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the state or territory regulatory jurisdiction (e.g., California, New York, Texas).']
  jurisdiction_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code identifying the regulatory jurisdiction (e.g., CA, NY, TX). Aligns with NAIC state codes.']
  last_amended_date date [note: 'Date when the state regulation was last amended or revised. Null if no amendments have been made since original enactment.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this state regulation record was last modified in the system.']
  long_term_care_partnership_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state participates in the Long-Term Care Partnership Program, allowing policyholders to protect assets while qualifying for Medicaid. True if partnership state, False if not.']
  minimum_cash_surrender_value_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'State-mandated minimum cash surrender value as a percentage of premiums paid or reserves, as required by nonforfeiture laws. Null if no state-specific requirement.']
  minimum_death_benefit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'State-mandated minimum death benefit amount for life insurance policies issued in the jurisdiction. Null if no state-specific minimum applies.']
  nonforfeiture_method varchar [note: 'State-mandated method for calculating nonforfeiture values (Commissioners Reserve Valuation Method, Commissioners Extended Term Method, Cash Surrender Value Method).']
  nonforfeiture_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state mandates nonforfeiture benefits (cash surrender value, reduced paid-up insurance, extended term insurance) for this product type. True if required, False if not.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Insurance product line(s) to which this state regulation applies (e.g., Life, Annuity, Variable Life, Variable Annuity, Long-Term Care). May contain multiple values separated by semicolons.']
  rate_filing_method varchar [note: 'State-mandated method for submitting and approving insurance rates (prior approval required before use, file and use after DOI review, use and file with post-use notification, no filing required).']
  regulation_citation varchar [note: 'Official citation reference to the state statute, regulation, or administrative code section that establishes this requirement (e.g., California Insurance Code Section 10113.71).']
  regulation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the state-specific regulatory requirement, including key provisions, applicability conditions, and compliance obligations.']
  regulation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the state regulation (active, superseded by newer regulation, pending approval, withdrawn).']
  regulation_type varchar [note: 'Category of state-specific insurance regulation (policy form approval, rate filing requirements, nonforfeiture standards, free look period, grace period, replacement regulations).']
  replacement_notice_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state requires written notice to the existing insurer when a policy replacement is proposed. True if notice required, False if not.']
  replacement_regulation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state has specific regulations governing policy replacement transactions (1035 exchanges, internal replacements). True if state has replacement rules, False if not.']
  serff_filing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state requires rate and form filings to be submitted through the NAIC SERFF system. True if SERFF required, False if alternative methods allowed.']
  stoli_prohibition_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state has enacted specific prohibitions against stranger-originated life insurance transactions. True if STOLI prohibited, False if not.']
  suicide_exclusion_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months during which death by suicide is excluded from life insurance coverage, as mandated by state law. Typically 24 months but may vary by state.']
  suitability_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state mandates suitability reviews and documentation for annuity sales. True if suitability review required, False if not.']
  variable_product_registration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state requires separate registration or approval for variable life and variable annuity products beyond standard form filing. True if additional registration required, False if not.']

  Note: 'Reference table of state-specific insurance regulatory requirements, variations, and effective dates by jurisdiction. Captures state DOI-specific rules for policy forms, rate filings, nonforfeiture requirements, free look periods, grace periods, and replacement regulations that vary by state.'
}

Table compliance.sar_filing {
  sar_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SAR filing record. Primary key.']
  aml_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the AML case investigation that resulted in this SAR filing.']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the life insurance or annuity policy involved in the suspicious activity, if applicable.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the policyholder who is the subject of the suspicious activity report.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.plan. Business justification: SAR filings reference the specific insurance plan involved in suspicious activity. FinCEN BSA reporting for insurance requires product-level identification. product_type on sar_filing is a denormalize']
  prior_sar_filing_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sar_filing (prior_sar_filing_id)']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: SAR filings fulfill FinCEN AML/BSA regulatory obligations. The obligation defines the filing deadline and requirements.']
  sar_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document. Business justification: SAR filings are formal FinCEN documents with narrative descriptions and supporting evidence required by BSA/AML regulations. Critical for AML regulatory reporting and FinCEN submission tracking in Lif']
  trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.trade. Business justification: SAR filings report suspicious trading activity (structuring, unusual patterns, STOLI indicators). Real business process: suspicious transaction reporting, FinCEN filing, AML compliance. Required for i']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date when FinCEN acknowledged receipt of the SAR filing. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  activity_end_date date [note: 'Date when the suspicious activity ended or was last observed. May be null for ongoing activity. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  activity_start_date date [note: 'Date when the suspicious activity began or was first observed. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the SAR filing was approved for submission to FinCEN. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the senior compliance officer or manager who approved the SAR filing for submission.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates that this SAR filing is subject to strict confidentiality requirements under the Bank Secrecy Act. Always True for SAR filings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SAR filing record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the suspicious activity was detected: automated monitoring system, manual review, customer complaint, law enforcement inquiry, regulatory examination, or other.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date when the SAR was filed with FinCEN. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  filing_reference_number varchar [note: 'Internal reference number assigned to this SAR filing for tracking and audit purposes.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the SAR filing in the internal workflow: draft (being prepared), pending_review (awaiting compliance review), approved (ready for submission), submitted (sent to FinCEN), acknowledged (FinCEN confirmation received), rejected (FinCEN rejected submission).']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'Type of SAR filing: initial (first report of suspicious activity), continuing (follow-up to prior SAR), corrected (correction to previously filed SAR), or amended (substantive change to prior SAR).']
  fincen_bsa_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by FinCEN upon successful submission of the SAR. Also known as the BSA Identifier or acknowledgment number.']
  jurisdiction_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code where the suspicious activity occurred or where the policy was issued.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SAR filing record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  law_enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the law enforcement agency contacted regarding this suspicious activity, if applicable.']
  law_enforcement_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether law enforcement has been contacted regarding this suspicious activity. True if contacted, False otherwise.']
  narrative_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the suspicious activity, including facts, circumstances, and reasons for suspicion. This is the core content of the SAR filing.']
  preparer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the compliance officer or employee who prepared the SAR filing.']
  prior_sar_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to a prior SAR filing if this is a continuing, corrected, or amended SAR. Contains the FinCEN BSA ID of the prior SAR.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date when FinCEN rejected the SAR filing due to errors or incomplete information. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by FinCEN for rejecting the SAR filing, if applicable.']
  subject_address varchar [note: 'Full address of the subject involved in the suspicious activity.']
  subject_date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the individual subject, if applicable. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  subject_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual or entity that is the subject of the suspicious activity report.']
  subject_tax_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number of the subject: Social Security Number (SSN) for individuals or Employer Identification Number (EIN) for entities.']
  subject_type varchar [note: 'Type of subject involved in the suspicious activity: individual (natural person), entity (organization/business), or unknown.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the SAR was electronically submitted to FinCEN. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  suspicious_activity_type varchar [note: 'Category or type of suspicious activity being reported (e.g., structuring, money laundering, terrorist financing, fraud, identity theft). May contain multiple types separated by semicolons. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: structuring|money_laundering|terrorist_financing|fraud|identity_theft|elder_financial_exploitation|insurance_fraud|premium_financing_abuse|stoli|tax_evasion|bribery|embezzlement — promote to reference product]']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount of the transaction(s) involved in the suspicious activity. Aggregate amount if multiple transactions.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of Suspicious Activity Reports (SARs) filed with FinCEN under the Bank Secrecy Act (BSA) for life insurance and annuity transactions. Captures SAR type (initial/continuing), filing date, FinCEN BSA ID, suspicious activity date range, transaction amounts, subject information, narrative description, filing status, and acknowledgment receipt. Required for AML/BSA compliance.'
}

Table finance.general_ledger {
  general_ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the general ledger entry. Primary key for the general ledger data product.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: general_ledger IS the instance of a chart_of_accounts account with balances. All master data attributes (account_name, account_type, normal_balance, hierarchy, classifications, flags, etc.) should com']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the cost center associated with this account. Used for expense allocation, departmental reporting, and management accounting. Null for balance sheet accounts.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the legal entity to which this general ledger entry belongs. Links to the legal entity master for multi-entity consolidation and statutory reporting.']
  account_code varchar [note: 'The unique account code from the chart of accounts (COA). Serves as the primary business identifier for the GL account. Typically 4-10 digit numeric code following the enterprise COA structure.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the general ledger account. Inactive or closed accounts cannot accept new postings but retain historical balances.']
  cohort_code varchar [note: 'The IFRS 17 cohort identifier for insurance contract grouping. Used for Contractual Service Margin (CSM) tracking and IFRS 17 measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this general ledger account record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for this account. Supports multi-currency accounting and foreign exchange reporting.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this account becomes inactive and no longer accepts new postings. Null for currently active accounts. Supports historical reporting and COA governance.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this account becomes active and available for posting. Supports chart of accounts change management and version control.']
  functional_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 functional currency code for the legal entity. Used for currency translation and consolidation under ASC 830.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier that last modified this general ledger account record. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this general ledger account record was last modified. Critical for change tracking and SOX compliance.']
  product_line_code varchar [note: 'The insurance product line associated with this account (e.g., \'WL\' for Whole Life, \'UL\' for Universal Life, \'SPIA\' for Single Premium Immediate Annuity). Enables product-level profitability analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the source system from which this general ledger account originated. Supports data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  source_system_key varchar [note: 'The natural key or unique identifier for this account in the source system. Enables traceability back to the originating system of record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier that created this general ledger account record. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'SSOT for all enterprise general ledger accounts, balances, and chart of accounts (COA) hierarchy. Owns account codes, account names, account type classifications (asset, liability, equity, revenue, expense, contra), normal balance direction (debit/credit), financial statement line mapping, SAP and GAAP dual-basis account mappings, IFRS 17 cohort account linkages, statutory vs. GAAP account designations, RBC line mapping, and account active/inactive governance with effective date ranges. Includes COA change management, account hierarchy (parent-child), account group definitions, and COA governance processes. Manages period open/close governance, close milestone tracking, and SOX-controlled financial close processes. Serves as the authoritative financial position record and chart of accounts master for all legal entities. Supports external audit support and regulatory filing account mapping.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the journal entry record. Primary key for the journal entry entity.']
  in_force_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific insurance policy or annuity contract associated with this journal entry. Populated for policy-level transactions such as premium revenue recognition, claim payments, surrender processing, and policy-specific reserve adjustments.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: journal_entry has legal_entity_code (STRING) and should FK to legal_entity. Journal entries are posted within a legal entity; FK is essential for legal entity financial statements and consolidation.']
  reversed_entry_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original journal entry that this entry reverses. Populated when this entry is a reversing entry, creating an audit trail back to the original transaction.']
  valuation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to actuarial.valuation_run. Business justification: Reserve-related journal entries (pbr_indicator, ldti_indicator flagged) must be traceable to their actuarial valuation run. This supports the reserve-to-GL reconciliation process — a key SOX control i']
  accounting_basis varchar [note: 'The accounting framework under which this journal entry is recorded. SAP = Statutory Accounting Principles (regulatory), GAAP = Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (US GAAP), IFRS17 = International Financial Reporting Standards 17 (insurance contracts), TAX = Tax basis reporting.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period to which this journal entry is assigned, typically in YYYY-MM format (e.g., 2024-03 for March 2024). Used for period-end close processes and financial reporting.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the journal entry was approved for posting. Used to track approval workflow timing and ensure timely financial close processes.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who approved the journal entry. Used for financial close workflow tracking and audit documentation.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch or group of journal entries processed together. Used for bulk posting operations, system-generated entries from upstream processes, and batch-level reconciliation.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the organizational cost center responsible for this journal entry. Used for management reporting, budget vs. actual variance analysis, and expense allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the journal entry record was first created in the database. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total credit amount for this journal entry header. In double-entry accounting, this must equal debit_amount to maintain balance. Represents increases to liability, equity, and revenue accounts or decreases to asset and expense accounts.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the journal entry amounts. Typically USD for US-domiciled life insurance companies, but may include other currencies for international operations or foreign investments.']
  debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total debit amount for this journal entry header. In double-entry accounting, this must equal credit_amount to maintain balance. Represents increases to asset and expense accounts or decreases to liability, equity, and revenue accounts.']
  entry_category varchar [note: 'High-level business classification of the journal entry purpose. Examples: Premium Revenue, DAC Amortization, Reserve Movement, Claim Payment, Commission Accrual, Investment Income, Intercompany Settlement, Reinsurance Cession, Policy Acquisition Cost, Benefit Payment, Surrender Processing, LDTI Adjustment, PBR Reserve Entry.']
  entry_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the journal entry was created or recorded in the system. May differ from posting_date for adjusting or backdated entries.']
  entry_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique journal entry number assigned by the general ledger system. Used for external audit trails and financial close documentation.']
  entry_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the journal entry. Draft = in preparation, Pending Approval = submitted for review, Approved = authorized but not yet posted, Posted = committed to general ledger, Reversed = entry has been reversed, Rejected = not approved for posting.']
  entry_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the journal entry based on its purpose in the accounting cycle. Standard = routine operational entries, Adjusting = period-end accruals and deferrals, Reversing = automatic reversal of prior period adjusting entries, Closing = period-end closing entries, Opening = beginning of period entries, Reclassification = reallocation between accounts.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert transaction currency amounts to functional currency. Populated only for entries involving foreign currency transactions.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this journal entry belongs. Used for annual financial statement preparation and multi-year comparative reporting.']
  functional_currency_credit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The credit amount converted to the company\'s functional currency (typically USD) using the applicable exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting when original entry is in a foreign currency.']
  functional_currency_debit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The debit amount converted to the company\'s functional currency (typically USD) using the applicable exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting when original entry is in a foreign currency.']
  ifrs17_measurement_model varchar [note: 'The IFRS 17 measurement model applied to this journal entry for insurance contract accounting. GMM = General Measurement Model (building block approach), PAA = Premium Allocation Approach (simplified for short-duration contracts), VFA = Variable Fee Approach (for participating contracts with investment components).']
  intercompany_entity_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the counterparty legal entity for intercompany transactions. Used for intercompany reconciliation and consolidated financial statement elimination entries. Populated only when intercompany_indicator is True.']
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this journal entry involves intercompany transactions between legal entities within the insurance group. True = entry requires intercompany elimination for consolidated reporting, False = intra-entity entry.']
  journal_entry_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the journal entry purpose, business rationale, and supporting documentation references. Used for audit trails, financial close documentation, and regulatory examinations.']
  ldti_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this journal entry is related to FASB ASU 2018-12 Long Duration Targeted Improvements (LDTI) accounting changes. True = entry is part of LDTI implementation (e.g., transition adjustments, updated DAC amortization, market risk benefit remeasurement), False = standard entry not impacted by LDTI.']
  pbr_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this journal entry relates to Principle-Based Reserving (PBR) calculations under VM-20 and VM-21. True = entry records PBR reserves, stochastic reserve adjustments, or deterministic reserve calculations, False = entry not related to PBR.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The system timestamp when the journal entry was committed to the general ledger. Used for audit trails and to track financial close process timing.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger. This is the effective date for financial statement impact and determines the accounting period assignment.']
  preparer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who prepared the journal entry. Used for financial close workflow tracking and audit documentation.']
  product_line_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the insurance product line to which this journal entry relates. Examples: Term Life, Whole Life, Universal Life, Variable Universal Life, Fixed Annuity, Variable Annuity, Indexed Annuity. Used for product-level profitability analysis and segment reporting.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which this journal entry will be or was automatically reversed. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this journal entry is configured to automatically reverse in the subsequent accounting period. True = entry will auto-reverse, False = entry is permanent. Commonly used for accruals and deferrals.']
